text: this starbucks is always quick and efficient. the baristas are so friendly here. they always make my drink perfect and if i'm looking for something new they always have the perfect suggestion.

this is not a starbucks that i frequent because it's not normally on my way to where i'm going. however i have been here a few times and it's pretty consistent. the staff is friendly and i appreciate that. the drive thru is kind of a cluster because of the limited space, but so are most starbucks drive thrus!

i love this little starbucks. the baristas are very friendly and my order is always accurately made. the drive thru service is also very good. there is not a lot of room for a car line so a sign indicates that you can't be waiting in line past it. this is a for safety reasons so try not to get too bent out of shape :)

horrible service. they no longer have or try to cater to allergies. manager tried to refuse me service. even when i tried to explain i'm ordering for my husband her attitude was horrible and her voice extremely condescending when she responded ' well, it's probably not safe for you because we have almond milk on everything, it's everywhere. to which i repeated that it was for my husband. if you have almond milk like, every where as you say, maybe you need a health inspector come by and see why you aren't cleaning up your products.

wow. worst starbucks ever? i had them remake a drink and they gave me an empty drink! i wanted to go back and get my money refunded but it's inconvenient for me as a customer and they are probably one of the only starbucks that does not have a drive thru store.

located on the intersection at magazine and washington is this great starbucks location. being on the intersection, there are plenty of parking options available through out your visit there. the line is always 10+ people long, no matter what time i go but they usually manage to go through it pretty quickly, which is nice. on my last visit, i wanted to try the gingerbread latte and i had a free birthday drink, so when i placed my order i requested both of those, the woman who took my order "okayed" it and along i went. i paid with a different guy who ended up charging me for the drink which was supposed to free, i explained that i told the other woman it was meant to be the free birthday special but he just laughed and said "you have to tell me, not her". well, she's the one that took my order so she should've been the one to put me through the register too. so that was annoying, they just had no communication between the staff members and how he shrugged it off was irritating. anyways, i got my gingerbread latte and took a seat. now this place is always busy so don't expect your ideal seating if you come too late in the day. i ended up moving three times as new, more preferred places to sit became available. the downside to the seating is that there aren't many powerpoint available and starbucks has to know that people come to work here while they drink their coffee. not a clever move. the gingerbread latte was just okay. it didn't taste like gingerbread at all so i was pretty disappointed but i managed to finish it off despite the lack of taste. there is a wide variety of seating inside with wall seating, tabled seating, and a few couch seating, which is nice. there is also outdoor seating but during this weather, it isn't recommended but would give you views of the street which is a nice distraction from the hustle and bustle from inside. overall, a beautiful location but the lack of communication and consideration the staff have between themselves and the customers made the visit spoiled.

after hastily driving to work on morning, i realized i left my breakfast sitting on my counter. i quickly placed an online order for pickup for a bagel here. i luckily had rewards for use so it was free! (cha ching!) i pulled up to a huge line (obviously starbucks is packed in the morning). but i parked and went inside. my bagel was ready shortly after. i realized i didn't have cream cheese (silly me, i didn't realize cream cheese was extra!) i asked the lady behind the counter and she said "don't worry about paying this time, everyone's just tryin to make it to work on time" and gave me some cream cheese. simple gesture, but i just thought it was really nice of her. great customer service starbucks!

this starbucks is usually top notch. i'm not sure if it was just a bad day or if they don't have the hang of drivethru service yet. we had to give our order three times to three separate people, and the order was still wrong. my chai was so sweet i couldn't drink it, it was only filled up part of the way, and it lacked the three shots of espresso that i asked for three times. if i go back here anytime soon i am not using the drivethru because it was chaos.

comfortable leather (looking) chairs. it made a good stop for an after movie drink and snack. the croissant was not very good, especially after having a excellent one a couple days before. the oatmeal raisin cookie was pretty good. i am not a starbucks acolyte, and it always seems strange that everyone around me seems to be in this highly efficient and exact routine when i go to a starbucks. they have tazo tea, and the chai wasn't bad.

this is a really good location for a coffee shop. the seating area is large and very comfortable with free wifi. there is also a large table with 10 chairs around it and i often see people come in for informal meetings. the staff is always very pleasant - true rarity. a great place to hang out!!!

not very nice service. the young woman wanted to close and not allow me to check out even though i was in line. i was just buying a prepacked food item. there were three people working. they couldn't help bc she had a tab open and was also making their drinks. it took approx 2 seconds to check me out with my sbux app after she closed her tab. she turned away other customers as well.

you'd think with six employees it wouldn't take 10 minutes to get an iced coffee!starbucks has been added to my regular morning routine which usually means a long line in the drive thru. my logic says park and you'll beat a lot of the cars in line. well my reasoning is incorrect. this location obviously caters to the guests in their drive thru. i'll admit the product is always good but you'd think patrons that go inside would receive the same service as those in their car.
great service, full cup coffee. justin and fabl are working amazing! good location and good coffee

i've been to this starbucks location couple of times and the experience of getting coffee here always been the same, horrible! minus the dirty counters and grumpy baristas who visibly hate their job including the coffee they made you they also will make you wait for it. the tipping point for me was when i ordered a simple drink (tall pike) went to get half & half at the dirty counter but found it to be empty, skim milk - empty, whole milk - empty, i asked barista if i could get more half & half, she looked at me and said sure! then ignored me for good 10 minutes until i left, other baristas also ignored me while being busy hating on their job. i went to the near by gas station to get me half & half for the coffee i purchased at the starbucks. (anyone else see anything wrong with this?) so if you still decide to come here, get in line for slow crevice and free humiliation. next time i'll come here, but who am i kidding i will never come here again.

this is definitely my 2nd home in philly. i live just one block away, so it's not unusual for me to spend 3 hours a day here doing work. most of the staff are totally cool - one person definitely irks me though. she just isn't friendly and likes to make a ton of noise an hour before closing to make people leave. i love that there are lots of places to sit, lots of outlets, and most of the sitting area is away from the noisy counter. the only thing i wish was that they made better chai lattes. i'll have to say that if you want your drink done right, go to the 12th and walnut starbucks. that was my go to place until my wallet was stolen there 2 years ago. i have been there once or twice and savor the chai when i go there, but that whole experience turned me off to that location. thanks 10th and chestnut for being my home away from home!

now i know why starbucks does not mix the frappuccinos all the way! it's so the drinks can look pretty waiting for customers to pick up their orders! my favorites are the matcha green tea and chai fraps!

ordered 4 coffees size grande. received 2 size tall in grande cups and 2 grande that were actually grande. very disappointed. was taking to my fellow nurses working 12 hour shifts at a pediatric hospital. will never go to starbucks again. $17 for 4 coffees that weren't even full when i could have went to wawa and paid $4 for 4 full coffees.

what an absolute pleasure going through this drive-thru this morning! the gentleman passing our coffee out the window and taking payment was such a sweetheart. you know it only takes a moment to be really nice, make a joke or two and completely make someone's morning. this guy knows it. plus it's just a short jaunt from the ridiculously busy drive-thru at the speedway/wilmot store where i sometimes worry i might run out of gas waiting in line because the line is so. freakin'. long. not an issue at broadway/kolb plus they're super nice and speedy!
pricey, but overall the best place for a tasty venti vanilla latte. love the lemon pound cake w/white glaze frosting poured on top, soothes the sweet tooth and goes well with your java! : )
you guys are the best! and reallly held it together well during covid with that drive thru madness... i come almost every day and rarely is my order ever messed up!! thank you!

the problem is now, the same as it's always been, the manager natalie. i worked for her for about 7 months back in 2010 and the same thing i hear from customers today is the same thing as it was back then. profits above care. as long as you could sell bulk coffee to teenagers for only came in at night to steal wi-fi (yes, let the logic wash over you) you were good. she's driven numerous good employees from the store, or cut down their hours to bare minimal (a barista frank went from 35 per week to 7) the parking for this place will never get better because it can't get better, there is no way of expanding the lot, so you have to deal with the privileged parking mommy and daddy's bmw any way they see fit. brit is the only reason i'd ever walk back into that place. one great employee though can't save the sinking ship.
new location is now open - avoid it. it's dirty inside. service is downright pissy. and their drinks aren't made right.
i went in here to order a beverage but the line was so long and the employee was moving at snail speed so i walked out. will not be returning.

they coffee here is good, like at all starbucks. the offerings to eat is quite limited. what is astounding is how indifferent the people who work here are to how loud the music is. at times it is impossible to talk to someone on the phone without using the speaker phone. i have had to talk to people who work at various starbucks about this, requesting that they turn the music down, mostly with bad results. they say they will gladly will turn the music down, then they don't. and they will not turn it off. i can only surmise what the reason is for this is: they think they are a music venue, and the music they are playing is simply too important for people not to be exposed to it.

today, i had a horrible experience at this starbucks. i'm going to call out the particular shift because i think managers should know what their barista's are doing. i was in around 11am on sunday. the barista started my drink, and another customers drink. during this time she decided to walk to the back..get a mop..proceed to mop the floor...and than put the mop back...finally getting back to finish off our drinks...really????? horrible customer service starbucks....while i will be back...i hope to get a barista who actually cares about their customers.
thomas was awesome! busy line and he was able to get it moving quick so i was quick to get my drink and go to work on time. love all of the staff here. my favorite starbucks!
a great place to get some work done! the plugs are plentiful. it's located just off the hwy and... most importantly... the baristas are fast and friendly.

this starbucks seems to stay pretty busy. lots of traffic but they seem on top of things. i have a certain way that i like my coffee doing keto. i've only had one barista to make it where it tastes different and not in a good way. everyone else that's ever made it tastes the same. if that barista is making coffees and i happen to go in there i will just wait to get my coffee.

i am so sick and the only card i have was on my cell phone and it doesn't scan. it has the numbers and the security code on the back. update your system it's 2019 i am so sick and needed a medicine ball. thanks for making me wait 29 minutes to get to the register and then tell me i can't order because your computer doesn't allow you to type in a credit card. horrible

the ladies behind the counter were on it today. i walked up to a 10 deep line and they cranked through it like pros. not the most friendly bunch, but efficient, and sometimes that is just good enough. star off for the sitting area being a bit messy.

the staff that work at this starbucks are amazing. a review mentioning "loud and obnoxious employees" is totally off base. i've never had a bad experience here and i am a daily regular. you will be greeted with a smile and a great beverage every time you visit!

honestly, reviewing a starbucks is like reviewing a mcdonalds. you know what you're getting, and the quality may vary depending on just how little the employees care at the precise moment you're there, but it will never be the best culinary experience of your life, nor will it be the worst. that said, the staff were friendly, seemed competent, and made their way through a fairly long line of customers in a reasonable amount of time.

boyfriend and i went to grab some iced coffee drink for him and water for both of us. spent about 10-12 minutes in drive-thru with only 3 cars in front of us. we come from a congested area in the suburbs of dc where drive-thrus are usually 10-15 cars long and the wait won't be much longer than experienced today. when we pulled up to the window, we saw 5 employees standing around chatting and were told that someone will be right with us. boyfriend gets his drink and a minute later, we get one water after ordering two. no receipt, no straw for coffee drink, and no second water. if we weren't in a rush, we would've come in the store and demanded to speak with the shift manager. but for some reason, i feel that wouldn't have resolved or changed anything. stay away. go to dunkin for quick coffee fix, or small local coffee shops for small business support and better service.

this starbucks is always busy and packed, but mostly because it's the size of a large closet. there are only three tables in the whole joint, and i'm sure that's on purpose. with the number of people shuffling in and out, it's tough to be productive. fortunately they have more tables outside. so if the weather is nice, you can enjoy some great sites of indianapolis' downtown circle.
one of the best staffed starbucks in the area!!! friendly and always feel welcomed management is awesome place is clean

i love this starbucks! i agree with some other reviewers about the decor not being as fancy or modern as other starbucks, but i don't really care about the decorations as much as the customer service. we all know that starbucks isn't cheap, but when you have great service it feels worth it. consequently - i've had horrible service at the one across the street in la cumbre plaza....literally the barista called out "venti iced coffee sweetened for mary" and i stood up and apologetically gestured "oh, i said nosweetener actually" and she glared at me while dumping out my iced coffee (continuously glaring at me) and then refilled it and pushed it towards me. her coworker was standing next to her and i smiled at her and said "wow, what great customer service, huh?" she just smiled and looked away. i mean, if you hate your job it definitely shows through to your customers. i've never had a bad experience here at this starbucks, but i avoid the other like the plague. i come for the coffee but i come back for the excellent service. in fact, at this one in particular i've had baristas tell me that if i'm not happy with my drink choice to please let them know so they can remake it.

let me start this review by saying fishers and the greater indianapolis area needs more late night coffee spots! i've bounced around several college towns over the last 16yrs and it baffles me that when i'm feeling a late night need go study, write, and just get out with a friend and talk over coffee, my only consistent late night option is ihop . that being said, this is a...drumroll please...starbucks!!! you know the menu. it's consistent. however, why i give this specific starbucks 4* is because i love the vibe/atmosphere and is it open until 11pm on friday and saturday night! most of my study time happens in the evening once my kids go down and it's nice to have a place in fishers to go. the baristas are really nice, the menu is consistent. there are plenty of tables and outlets to go around. and it's open late(r)!

stopped on a very rainy saturday for a nice hot tea and a coffee. i was greeted right when i walked in the door and the barista who took my order was very friendly. this location was very clean and seemed to be doing a brisk business. the drinks were prepared quickly and tasted exactly right. i had even asked at the counter what coffee they would recommend for the average coffee drinker, since i do not drink black coffee and he was glad to help and recommended the pike roast. my mom approved when she tasted. if i am near this location i would return.

well, they tried. the employees were nice. they keep the location clean, which i'd imagine might be a challenge with a bunch of i-95 drivers passing through. but, i waited a bit longer than i expected for my drink only to get a grande when i'd ordered a venti. i noted the issue, they offered to fix it, i waited another five minutes or so before telling them that i'd rather take the grande for the venti price and get back on the road. they apologized again and offered to make my drink immediately... had i cared more, i believe they would have gotten me the right thing right away. none of the shortcomings were willful, but i still opted to hit the road with my grande. my memory is a bit hazy on the details, but i remember having a similar issue on a past trip. the staff members seem to mean well, and the experience could be a lot worse, but "meh. i've experienced better."

recently given a nip/tuck.. this starbucks brings the new single-serving clover brewing system and starbuck's new reserve beans. not to mention the great new starbucks decor and look! back to to clover system. it's so great! especially for an iced coffee lover. i now have so many more options for iced coffee instead of just the one typical pre-made "iced coffee blend". they can now make any bean they have in store into an iced coffee. it's fantastic! try them all! my favorite right now is an iced indivisible blend coffee! and the people are nice too. very friendly. something else that makes this unique is the outdoor walk-up window. very cool. my go-to starbucks!
the espresso shots weren't as strong as they usually are in other starbucks locations. i found this disappointing, since i really needed the shots to wake myself up for a long drive.

the staff here is super sweet and helpful, i haven't had an issue with customer service at all. however, i recently have started doing my daily mobile order because my job moved locations, and 4/5 times my order is either wrong or late. it seems like this store is incredibly busy, which is totally understandable, but they also seem to have some very serious staffing/management issues. it's definitely not the employees fault, they are just trying their best, but when i give them 20 minutes to get my drink done via the mobile app and then i show up and it's still not done, it's clear that this store is operational deficient.

i feel the manager of this location has a unprofessional manner when dealing with the staff. maybe she needs more training in leadership and how it directly affects staff and customer service! she has the tenancy to belittle them with "problems the entire staff is doing wrong". not leading by example and giving them "written guide instructions" needed for shift work. this is a crazy busy position for young adults trying to introduce themselves in the workforce! they need good leadership, encouragement and defined instruction of what is needed to do the job well! not bullying and negative attitude by their manager! this always correlates to customer service! i hope this helps in some way. this could be a great place if management could change.
starbucks always has good customer service! the best variety menu! comfortable and never disappoints#

i was happy to see a new starbucks open just off the highway which in convenient on my work route. it's essentially a small drive thru only store. it does have a small walk up window but there is no inside option. this works out well for me as i'm coming here on my drive to work. the times i've been here the line has moved very quickly and the service was friendly. i usually go to the richmond heights store but even though it's a nice location and has a lot of staff they are often painfully slow and make me late to work. ill definitely be using this location as my go to workday stop from now on.
i'm just wondering why i got stuck in the drive thru for 30+ minutes with a total of 5 cars in front of me. i so wish i was exaggerating.

we always get friendly and professional service here plus properly made drinks. we buy a hot chocolate for my wife's manicurist each month and sometimes an espresso for myself. thanks for being on point! life is good with espresso!

pretty disappointed in this starbucks. i never go to starbucks because they are always overly packed and i could easily go to dd. well decided to treat myself and well the drink they gave me was disgusting mind you i order the same thing for probably 10 years now and know what it should taste like. they also didn't put the carmel that i paid extra for in it which says on the side of the cup. so i turned around and decided to get it fixed i get it mess ups happen, they go ahead and tell me they are remaking it. i spent fifteen minutes waiting for my new drink which at first i was like ok they are obviously going to get the couple before me...... no they got about 5 cars and 7 orders before i got mine which i have already waited patiently for my first one. will now go eat my cold breakfast sandwich with my ok remake drink on my way to work. will save my time and money next time i want to treat myself.

super slow!!! but super friendly. there were only two cars in front of me and they didn't have a massive order or anything but it still took 15 mins to get my drink. that's a bit excessive for a drive thru. sure, i could have mobile order (which i usually do) but this location said they were out of what i wanted on mobile app but really they were not out. my frap was melted and my tea looked like it was sitting around for awhile. i'm sure they made the drinks right away when i ordered. they just took a long time through the drive thru. anyways, i'm sure i'll be back to this location if i'm in the area.

you have a very rude (continuously) female barista who must hate her job, or hate life in general in the morning hours before 9am on the weekdays. i've experienced this and have seen her be rude to others as well. she either needs a cup of coffee or a reality check that she's making other people's mornings not so pleasant. makes me want to go to another coffee shop.

usually all is well here at this store, and i usually frequent it everyday on my way home. today was different. i stopped in around 6pm and i guess there was training going on where the store cleanliness was neglected, even though there was very little traffic (since the drive thru was closed too). i got my coffee then went for a refill, where i was told that coffee doesn't get refills. this is the first time i've heard this "company policy" since it could "give me a heart attack." the barista really didn't care that everyone else but her has done refills and have me a similar drink without asking if that's what i wanted. it not like i'll never come back, but i'll avoid that barista for sure. so much for the third place.

absolutely the worst starbucks in new orleans . store is dirty, unkempt and not stocked with basic supplies i.e. straws and napkins. baristas are rude and can't even get a mobile order correct or complete . save your money and aggravation and go somewhere else , anywhere else. not interested in guest satisfaction at all. avoid avoid avoid .ps french truck coffee just opened down magazine , go there , i know i will.

this is by far my favorite starbucks. the baristas get inundated with crowds from the dome, from the met, from downtown generally, and they still take time to make sure everyone gets everything and they always get it right. the baristas know my name and i've only been in town for a few months. they're very kind, ask how things are going, and are very hard working. i especially like dealing with cam, sam, desiree, michale, shelton, lydia, mac, and another fellow whose name i just blanked out on (sorry!) love this store and this team.

i met a friend here once a week and the service is always quick but not very personal. there are so many people here is hard to find a place to sit. not a good place to work but a great location and fast service.

this is an small starbucks in odessa but the close one to the lutz area. i am very excited about the service and attention today. my coffee was prepared to perfection. joe, the manager was talking with the customers today about the remodeling of the store and what we will expecting at the end of may. he graciously passed out some samples of sconces to all the guests. i can see how hard he is working to make sure that are employees are nice and attentive to customers. keep the hard work!!!

the worst customer service ever. waited while people who were behind me, got their drinks. saw 5 orders made that were wrong and had to be redone. one three times! starbucks area manager - you had best get over here and do some training. unorganized.. unmotivated. unhappy...

if i could give zero stars i would once again, another terrible experience at this location. the staff is so unprofessional it's a shame. i started to say my order and before i could even get out what i wanted the lady was walking away starting the order. i said "can i have a small hot coffee? caramel drizzle, extra..." she walks away and begins making it before i could complete my sentence. then she returns to the register with a black coffee. i said "i wasn't finish". i continue to say my order and the lady said "we don't put half and half in, we let the customer do it" i continued to tell her i wanted whipped cream. then she magically was able to add half and half. pure laziness. i asked for a banana nut bread and she said "we ain't got none ". the level of ignorance and irritation that they display is disgusting !!! i will never return

i have to say, starbucks as a whole, i thoroughly enjoy. and even after trying multiple coffee shops, i always go back. but i always find myself stuck stopping at this one on my way to visit family in hernando county, and i hate it. they are always out of almost half their menu items by 10:30am and the staff are incredibly unfriendly. i cringe when i have to go in, and find myself usually annoyed when i leave. to the staff, if you hate your job or can't be nice, maybe the hospitality business isn't for you, and quit. to the manager, please figure out your quotas and stock, running out of something three weeks in a row is pretty bad. sincerely, annoyed customer.

for many years we suffered without a starbucks drive-thru on the west side of town. all of the starbucks were closer to work and had spectacularly long lines. suddenly, like a mirage in the desert the starbucks on a hill appears! conveniently located next to the interstate, this starbucks is perfectly located for a frappuccino or latte on the way to work! it hasn't been open long, but in all my visits so far, i've been greeted with friendly baristas who promptly delivered my coffee beverage. i look forward to many more visits to this starbucks in the years to come.
so slow, 25 mins for a cup of coffee, app doesn't work, shorted on coffee, not even especially nice...not a good way to start the day

i have a couple complaints about this starbucks. my boyfriend and i live nearby, so this is the starbucks we frequent, especially when we need a pick-me-up on our way to phoenix. we usually get a coffee and a breakfast sandwich. my favorite is the spinach feta wrap, which is always perfection but twice now my boyfriend has gotten the sausage & egg sandwich and it's been cold in the middle. another time, we frequented this place was to get an iced tea and a latte. i ordered an unsweetened passion fruit and the bf got a vanilla latte. his came out at least 5 minutes before mine and when i finally got mine and took a sip, i could tell it had sweetener in it. i checked the cup label to see if it said unsweetened, and it did. i stood there for another couple minutes before an employee approached me and made me a new drink. this all wouldn't have bothered me so much if i didn't have to wait so long in between erik's drink and mine.
good danish. fresh, buttery and soft. but $4.00 for a medium iced chai tea latte? really! and it wasn't even that good. ummm half bucks wasted here.
the absolutely worse starbucks experience that one can have. it is actually a starbucks franchise. wouldn't recommend it to my worse enemy.. or even to donald trump .

i like this place. have come here for years. they moved it a bit up tbe street and added a drive thru. unfortunately, they haven't figured out how to manage the traffic from drivethru and walkin yet. always slow.
worst starbucks i have ever visited. they're have a 7 people behind the counter that do not know what they're doing-the line just grows. i've never waited this long for a drink and food.

this is really embarrassing. starbucks subjects it's customer to boring national standards for product while providing none of the large scale benefits of employee support and training. the "baristas" at both this and the harbor island store are painfully slow and lack understanding of the product. combine this with one more stained shirt from the lid opening being put on the seam and i am going elsewhere. starbucks said "it was all about the experience". if this is what they deliver they better improve the coffee!

for the most part this location does a decent job in delivering good, temperature appropriate food/beverages. on many occasions they've forgotten to give napkins, condiments, and/or handed items out of the window one by one rather than putting everything into a bag. i know there's a pandemic going on and shortages on supply but let's think logically and give the customers what they need.
love starbucks but the egyptian taking all over the place and the fight in it wallah... i come here everyday because i love seeing them fighting lol
stopped by this morning. my drink was alright but the place smelled like burnt coffee like usual. there were actually seats open today. most of the time there is barely enough room to wait.

probably still the worst starbucks i've been too. they make you wait a 20min long line just to be told that they're out of what you want to get regardless of what time of day. manager is rude and no help either.

i went here to get my salted cold foam cold brew, and the barista said they were all out of salted cold foam cold brew. what the heck do you mean you're out of salted cold foam cold brew, y'all are advertising it everywhere. so, they gave me an iced coffee instead of my salted cold foam cold brew- and thankfully they put some salted cold foam on top, but the salted cold foam wasn't even salted!? wtf is this? why would i go to get a salted cold foam cold brew if the salted cold foam isn't even salted? unacceptable. this is why people regularly die in the bathrooms.

wanted a quick starbucks stop for coffee and treats and this location was great!! fresh coffee, yummy bakery items, and very prompt service!! definitely recommend if you're driving through or in the area!

i think a little t depends on the time of the day for this location. i've been here several times and mostly good experiences. i've used the drive through and when i got home i had the wrong order and was upset but too far to drive back to fix the issue. i have my usual order at any starbucks and it's an easy drink and 95% of the time it's on point but today my drink was a bit off, but not enough to ask them to remake it. i come here because it's a convenient location. this is basically a free standing location but there is a chase bank connected. plenty of parking and a large seat area albeit full most of the time. they also offer an outdoor patio which is nice when the florida weather cooperates! i'd recommend this location as much as any other. the baristas aren't overly friendly but they do what is expected - making a coffe drink or a tea beverage.

as others has said this is by far one of the slowest locations i've ever been at. one person working taking orders and working as a cashier. the majority of my time in the drive thru was listening to her taking orders. a definite need for more help or training. my frappe was very good but melted a little while i waited to be served.

another starbucks. typical drinks, typical snacks, typical decoration. so what makes this one different from the rest? i can point out 2 pros and 1 con: pro #1: location. this is located on a busy corner of one of the busiest foot traffic streets in edmonton (whyte ave; also known as 82nd ave). so the location makes this starbucks a great place to meet up with friends while enjoying an afternoon out and about. another great aspect about the location is the ability to people watch. whether you sit inside, or at a table on the sidewalk, this is a great location to soak in your surroundings. pro #2: i like the bar space set up inside. this is something i haven't seen at any other starbucks. just to the left of the barista brewing centre there is a bar with about 4 stools set up. it is fun to watch the barista's work behind the counter and i love the space available. the round tables at typical starbucks' are quite small if you are going there to do work but this particular bar is very wide which is ideal to spread out books and papers. con #1: i guess this is related to the first pro. the fact that it is located on a busy street means lots of street noise from vehicles driving by and also can make it difficult to find parking. hopefully this encourages people to take public transit but i know that option is not always feasible. overall i do really like this starbucks, especially in the summer while the city is abuzz with all of the festivals taking place.

this place hasn't been reviewed in ages. i've come here quite a bit and they are fabulous. some starbucks lack quick service and pleasantries. not this one. the staff are stupid fast and very nice. the location itself is well maintained and has tons of seating to accommodate people watching, eavesdropping, networking, coffee breaks and working. this is one of my fave stops on the north side of yeg. the only issue you may find is parking. the lot can get really busy with the mall patrons. so be on the look out for db parkers and clueless drivers. it gets hairy in this location.

i have never had such rude service at a starbucks before. quite shocking for the area of nashville. i walked in used the restroom and went to the counter where i was the only person in line and waited while being ignore for at least 4 minutes while the lady carried on a conversation with her co worker all while counting her tips. once she finally finished and announced her grand total of tips she finally recognized that i was standing there patiently waiting to order. she was not very friendly nor apologetic for the wait. i order my drinks and my brothers (all the same drinks) and once received walked outside and took a sip of my drink and recognized that it didn't taste like it normally does. so i walked back into the store where the lady who made the drink basically told me that it's not normally a sweet drink and do i want her to add sweetener? my brother also at this time tried his drink and verified his wasn't sweet either and came in behind me as i'm speaking to the lady. the other lady jumps into the conversation and basically says that his normal starbucks is not making his drink following the recipe. so they are not normally sweet. however they can add vanilla sweetener to our drinks if we would like. instead of arguing with them we accepted the vanilla sweetener and went on our way. i felt this duo are just lazy and argumentative and did not want to remake the drinks properly.
drive-thru wait time a little longer than should be expected. walk-in much quicker. service is great, though! nice people.

we stopped in for a iced tea on a very warm day. customer service was good. my tea was good as well. the store felt very warm inside, and we ended up going out and sitting in the car with air on to cool off.

i always forget this starbucks is there. there is always seating when you go at the right time of the day. i get my hair done at circa 79 so it's a great location to grab something sweet before getting beautified!
super unfriendly baristas, two bathrooms but they had one closed and said the other one was employee only.

thank you. doth such a place share such an idea with a complement reader? seeks a very refined, polite, female acquaintance who may further an incomplete introduction to society, though discreetly. background...
go off-peak hours or plan to wait in line. if you're not in a rush, this is a great place to meet, read, work. their staff is friendly.
stopped here to get some coffee real quick. i was happy they had a drive through. the guy at the drive through made my day with his sense of humor.

i've really had enough of this starbucks. i stop at it every once in a while on my way between lafayette and indianapolis because it breaks up the drive and i like a little caffeine boost. invariably, whether i walk in or use the drive-through, they screw up my order. usually it's something like whipped cream when i didn't want it, but this time they put regular milk in my drink when i asked for soy. i asked for soy because i'm lactose intolerant. the pain i experienced from their mistake has finally convinced me to never stop at this location again.

one of my favorite starbucks for on-the-go coffee, certainly not for doing work. not sure if there is any seating at all. however, because it's a little hidden in the bellevue there's never a line and they've never messed up my order or anything. one of my free drink voucher thingies was expired and she was nice enough to still take it! taking a star away though because coffee shops should have some area to meet up and chit chat or do work i think.

i wouldn't recommend going to this starbucks location. they have consistently gotten my iced coffee wrong, which i think is a simple order, just milk and a flavor, probably 4 of the past 5 visits i had. they are also slightly more expensive and don't usually have as many food items as other starbucks in the area. although the location is convenient, it's worth it to drive a little farther out of my way to go to a different starbucks that will have what i need & get my order correct.

walked in before they closed at 6:00 pm, there at 5:55 pm and they said they were closed and wouldn't serve me. stopped at this exit just to go to starbucks. need to have a talk with their employees about closing early!
super quick when you're in need of some caffeine and breakfast first thing in the am!

my girls love starbucks every time we pass a starbucks they want to stop. yesterday we were celebrating my daughter's birthday weekend, passed starbucks and had to stop in the drive up to get their favorites. well, to our surprise and disappointment. the cookie and cake pop was delicious as usual, but the 3 drinks were not good at all. they lacked flavor, half the drink was liquid, and the rest taste like slushy ice, with lumpy deflated whipped cream. there was only 1 car at the window, which left pretty quick. we were next, but waited at the window for a very long time. it appeared the barista was making several orders while we waited. now there is a line around the building ‍‍so so disappointed. a total waste of my $22, then they kept my bank card and ruined my daughter's birthday shopping spree because we had to leave the mall to go back to get my bank card in order to shop. i don't recommend 2705 veterans blvd.
reliably good coffee with mediocre service. pastries and sandwiches are made off-site, so quality would be the same as all other starbucks concessions. average cleanliness.
this place is extremely busy but the staff does a great job keeping the lines moving! coffee is always good!

this is an above average starbucks mostly for its location and its indoor and outdoor seating area. parking can be tricky around here especially at night and weekends. this is a popular area. this coffee shop can get crowded but i have never had to wait very long in a line here nor have i waited long for my drinks. enjoying starbucks outside at this location usually provides some great people watching.
can't go wrong with starbucks just get to this location early because they get crowded in the mornings.
friendly cashier, not good coffee. definitely not a starbucks filled with employees taking any pride in their work or their creations as baristas. not impressed.

this is the starbucks closest to my work and i'd come out here even if it wasn't. it tends to be pretty busy and attracts all kinds of characters wandering the mall, but the staff is always friendly and i've never had a bad coffee fix here. some of the workers do make an effort to remember customer names which is a nice touch. it can be a bit of a wait sometimes, but it seems to be because of sheer customer volume, as the place has all the staff that can fit in the small location and it's run well. 5 shots of espresso out of 5.

convenient and hut like with a drive thru. the outside seating is sparse but the drive thru is quick. i go here often wheni am crunched for time and they have always been speedy. bonus, have yet to get a messed up order here :)

lady spilled her venti drink and no employee helped her and when asked in regards to an order barista was rude!! i am a regular at starbucks, in new england i have never experienced this type of service. i hope supervisor reads this to address customer service skills with their employees.

lol, great experience at this location. i was claiming my free coffee from playing a starbucks game and had no clue how to order with the starbucks lingo. the cashier, coffee man or whatever you wanna call him, lol was super helpful and helped me order a "large" coffee. even though i'm you have to add you own sugar and cream it was still a pleasant visit. polite folks here and i'll probably stop in here if i win another coffee!
going down hill. huge line again in drive through bc the new kid is in the window. why aren't the employees and managers realizing this!!! u guys rocked but now... sad

when i came in they were short staffed and incredibly busy, when i went to get my drink another person took it and ran off before i could stop them, but the barista saw and immediately remade it for me. a long wait should not negate human decency.
this is a pretty decent place to come to and do homework. free wi-fi. can't complain!

this is my favorite starbucks ever thanks to the barista (her name starts with a c, i believe) who made my drink, a double shot on ice, perfectly and without a second thought or even an eye roll. she seemed happy to be there! the manager at the counter, however, named jeff, was absolutely rude to me. he acted visibly annoyed with me while taking my order, and had a mocking tone to his voice. i could tell he didn't want to "deal" with me. i was polite as can be but he definitely woke up on the wrong side of the bed. seems to me like the barista and manager should switch positions. she is much more customer friendly than he is and served me with sweet hospitality and a warm smile. she is the only reason i will return.

to be honest.. this place is beyond slow, and the drive thru is horrendously long. if you have a place to be go somewhere else. i waited twenty min for one small drink, a strawberry refresher for my daughter. i was really disappointed.

this starbucks is hit or miss when it comes to making drinks right. i go here often as it is the closest starbucks and i always order the same thing for my wife and i, but two out of three times they tend to get the order wrong and completely botch the drinks entirely. it's especially terrible if you order by drive thru, and it's only slightly better if you order inside. on the plus side, the employees are always friendly and welcoming.

this is my favorite time of the year since all the holiday flavors come back. it makes it harder to decide what to order. the employees were super friendly and patient. i kept making edits to my order and the girl helping me was more than happy to fix it. yes, i was that person today. service was super fast even though the store was busy. to drink, i ordered a lite, half caff, salted caramel mocha with no whip. the drink was perfect. i ordered a premade bacon chicken sandwich with my drink. the sandwich tasted really good but was a little on the drier side, even though it stated there was a mustard sauce on it.

smallish starbucks located on a corner street in old city. it's right down the way from the neighborhood ups store. they take starbucks cards here and have the full menu so come get your favorite coffee flavored treats! quick in and out, but few seating areas here.

customer service is alive and well at this starbucks location. i had a problem with my starbucks reward card and went to speak to the manager on site, felicia. she took care of me very kindly and graciously. apologized for the mistake. wow... this doesn't happen now a days. love their service and coffee. they will have a loyal customer here

this starbucks is my favorite one in the nashville area. most of the staff here are extremely friendly and even remember my name, despite being one of the busiest starbucks i've ever been to. it isn't unusual to have a long line inside and out and to have difficulty finding parking, but everything moves through fairly quickly, despite the volume of customers their serving.

hey starbucks - when it's slow - customer service is even more important. the woman on drive thru duty (identified by the headset) waited on me when i walked in. the silence between us was awkward for me because i never know if they are listening to someone outside. i waited for her que. i ordered my cocoa. she made it personally. two of her coworkers were busy behind the counter. no one said thank you. i loved the way my order was announced - just like when they are busy. i'm a grande hot chocolate. there were no other customers anywhere - inside or outside. went home across the street. one full inch of room i noted upon arrival - within 3 minutes. thought it would have whip? called to ask if it comes standard? yes indeed the reply. well, not in mine. woman with attitude at this store. and no team to help her out. baby it's cold inside. i am more sure than ever i must cut the cord to you starbucks. good-bye.

coming in here just now reminded me why i stopped coming to this location ...... lack of customer service that in some cases as today almost borders on rudeness ..... i should have taken my card back and driven 2 miles down the road to another location ...... hopefully i will not give in to "i hope it's better this time" when shopping up here. what separates one starbucks from another is the customer service ..... not the drinks .......
slowest starbucks i've ever been to. don't use the drive through. you'll be stuck for an hour.
not the starbucks quality i have become used to. my cup got pushed to the back of the line. at least 10 peoples drinks were made before mine that were behind me in line.

barista crew is efficient and polite. if you come here during the day, expect tables to be taken up by mcewan students and business meetings. not a lot of seating, but a nice space none-the-less. large windows let in a lot of natural light, which makes this location welcoming.

the barista on register this weekend needs lots of training!!!!!! she didn't know how to redeem my birthday reward, which was so annoying. i just received it at 12am and she just kept saying can't use it until your bday. the sbux app on my phone, says it's available and it expires on the 28th. she got everyone's order wrong. her manager came behind the counter eating with food in his hand. he was talking with a mouth full of food and started scolding her for taking food from the back because it was for the next business day. overall customer service sucked so bad. the manager on duty made it worse. i'll just wait till my weekday crew is back in effect:/

this is the starbucks located in the lobby of the hotel we were staying at for our annual convention. it has a large seating area in the back with many tables for meetings or just comfy chairs to sit in. during my convention, i would duck down here to use their wifi and get some work done while i was on lunch break. this place has the same great drinks that you have grown to love and expect from a starbucks. everytime i came in here the place was spotless and the staff was extremely helpful and friendly. they even remembered me from the days prior. it was very welcoming.
has a lot of room to sit an relax. it is very clean and has a friendly staff.

this starbucks is soooooo disorganized. the staff behind the counter talk to each other instead of making drinks and leave you waiting at the pick up counter with your foot tapping. i asked what teas i could choose from and the girl just said we have green tea that's all i know. i asked for earl gray and i was the only one customer waiting for drink besides the drive thru line which had 2 cars.... needless to say, i waited for an uncomfortable amount of time to the point where other folks walked in, ordered and got their drink before me. i just asked for tea!! they definitely forgot my order. not cool! i had to grab the girl's attention to ask where my tea was. i said, "do you know how much longer on the earl gray?" and she says, "no" and i wanted to say, "well then who should i ask to get my tea?" but i held my tongue. then i see her drop a random tea bag into a cup and quickly fill with water and push towards me. i thought they typically sweeten with some kind of syrup but i guess not for me or not today. i seriously think they need to hire and train folks not to be so disorganized even when things are slow. just tried the tea and wow it is bland and tastes all wrong. i looked at the tag and it says "english breakfast" so i'm definitely not happy with the service but i already drove away bc i was so ready to get out of there. next time, get the order right and don't forget customers.

not the worst, but they definitely need to work on some things. this starbucks is already located in a target store, so i feel like it should lose points for that. i don't like having to wade through a sea of middle-aged, oblivious moms in yoga pants who would rather chit chat than let you get your coffee (this actually happened, i'm not stereotyping). there was one woman working when i was here and she was being run ragged. she was hustling getting coffee, food and everything else that the mams were demanding of her. i seriously think she was thrilled when i just ordered a large iced coffee. at other stores such a simple order comes out right away, but because there was only one person working, it took a while. seriously target, get that girl some help. the coffee was mediocre, as expected, and i only stopped here in the first place because someone gave me a gift card for christmas because they obviously hate me or something. anyway, if you need coffee during your target trip, then this is the spot. otherwise, you should probably avoid.
these employees are amazing!! i live coming here. no matter how busy they are, each employee is keeping their cool. always amazing service and coffees, no matter how ridiculous my order is

although they've moved (they used to be next to the apple store), they've still kept their quality service intact. pretty much every time i go here, i get fast and friendly service. i did have an issue once with an unfriendly worker, but i'm going to consider that a one-off. definitely gets busy quick, but they do their best to get you in and out with the right drink and a smile!

i don't know who is in charge of this place. they want to charge you a premium price and yet always have ti substitute items on your order. it's a simple fix, you know how much you sell a week, so order what you need. really is no excuse to be out of things. not that complicated. example. let's give you the smallest straw since we are out. even though the cup is bigger then the straw. while still charging you full price.
excellent location. i visit starbucks from manhattan down to this location weekly. drinks are always ready for me when i arrive, made perfectly, and the staff is very friendly.
the worst starbucks ever! i have no choice but to come to this one, but how have these people not gotten it together! awful!

the best in town. love the clover brewed coffee. i can get any coffee either hot or iced. they take time to get to know you when you come in often. if you are so inclined, you can have a beer or some wine.

the service/staff were great; friendly and quick at getting my order. i love starbucks. the pastries make me smile, the oatmeal is comforting, and the breakfast sandwiches are cheesy goodness. my daughter is a big fan of the turkey pesto panini. if i love the star so much why only 4 stars?..the disappointment of the holiday panini. it was underwhelming. the sandwich was a barely a resemblance of what is pictured on the starbucks site. ***** site description: a generous portion of sliced turkey breast is at the heart of this holiday-inspired panini. it's topped with cranberry-herb stuffing and turkey gravy then served warm on toasted ciabatta bread. it's a handheld turkey dinner.***** generous is a subjective term. the panini was slim..it actually looked as though there wasn't anything inside. the stuffing must've soaked up all the gravy creating a thick paste that birthed a mushy texture. thankfully the bread was toasted; it provided some crunch. this experience has me doubtful on trying something new at starbucks. no worries though, i have plenty of favorites to keep me going back. *the oatmeal options are so good i often order one for lunch or dinner :d
this new location is notoriously slow in preparing mobile orders. they also served my wife burnt coffee beans recently in her eggnog latte. friendly, but certainly lacking in the production side.
it's clean and organized this early in the morning. wifi is strong, limited electrical plug outlets availability and restrooms require a code to enter them.

i placed an order for a iced breakfast tea with 5 pumps of sugar via the mobile app. it took me a few minutes to get to the store and i walked up to see if my order was ready, however i did not see it. i have gotten this drink many times, so i know what cup to look for. i stood waiting for my drink for 10 minutes. i didn't say something immediately because i did see they were busy and perhaps they were brewing tea. i finally decided to ask if maybe i am at the wrong store. i was asked for my name, and i was advised it was there but maybe they made it wrong... and they did. no one apologized, no one seemed to be worried why i was just standing there. i watched them make my drink sloppy and in a hurry and of course did not do the right amount of sugar pumps. when my drink was ready, it was just handed to me..no words, no apology. i left feeling extremely disappointed.

consistently good drinks and one of the best in the area with getting mobile orders out on time. if you're complaining about this location you haven't been to blue bell or springhouse, clearly, not even close to these baristas!

this starbucks has a great location, right by jefferson and near rothman and in the washington square area. i've often stopped by when i've been at a medical appointment in the area or at the jefferson station which is also close by. they move the line somewhat quickly here, usually are pretty well staffed with baristas, so they're pretty efficient here. the baristas seem nice here and helpful, i don't go here as much as other starbucks near me but i'm glad this starbucks is in the area! good starbucks! i think the seating area could be kept a little cleaner but again great location and friendly staff.

as far as starbucks chain coffee shops go, this one is well managed, maintained, very clean, has friendly and knowledgeable staff, and make your order for a drink fast. of course when i have gone to this coffee shop i do it after i make my purchases at target, not before, and just drink the coffee while driving home.

not only are the beverages and food exceptional, but the baristas make this place. always patient, friendly, remembering your drink orders. they make a bright spot in every day i visit. it is a bit crowded at this store in newtown borough. would love to see them expand -- maybe upstairs with some outside seating!
only reason i didn't give 1 star is because the young man at the front was so kind to us! and our chocolate croissants were great. i simply ordered a matcha latte and it tasted awful :( sad

this is the worst starbucks ever! i've ordered twice from the app. both times i had to wait at least ten minutes to drink my drink even though it was already made and sitting at the side. each time at least three people made eye contact with me, but didn't offer to help and i had to be really assertive to eventually get my drink. this morning my order was wrong, but i didn't have time to ask them to make it again because i had waited so long to get and was on my way to work. what a bummer they hired such sucky people. usually the people at starbucks are great, but at this one their terrible.

i witnessed an appalling display of customer service. a male employee treated a customer terribly rudely even though the customer was in no way acting badly. the starbucks jerk got the man's order wrong and the customer actually apologized to the employee. rather than respond like all employees are taught, this moron actually insults the customer under his breath. the old man couldn't hear and asked the employee what he had said. the employee said, 'nothing', and then muttered another insult. i was too shocked to say anything. the old guy never knew anything was wrong and left just as happy as when he came in. shameful.

i am spending a few days at this starbucks this week because of the free wifi.... great staff and super friendly... i am originally from orange county, ca and i have to say, i was not used to nice, and friendly staff at starbucks. a big 2 thumbs up for all the staff here.

very friendly staff that were efficient and hardworking. solid consistent starbucks drinks. great assortment of pastries/snacks. i found a spot to sit and relax with my drink and book for a bit. the parking lot can be quite the nightmare during sporadic busier times of day though.
it's starbucks. it's on tucson's southside. there is wifi. the staff are friendlier than most other starbucks. there is rarely a long wait inside.
stupid starbucks, won't sell the food in the display because its the next day's inventory. what idiot would think up that one. a sale is a sale, but a sale denied is an annoyed ex-customer.

wonderful location of starbucks. friendly staff , consistent drinks, clean lobby and service may be slow at times but staff work with great sense of urgency and awareness. all in all seattle would be proud.
this location has very slow service every time i go. luckily there are faster ones on the way to work.

i go here all the time and there is literally never the same employees... very confusing... not sure if it's a training store or what. sadly my cloud macchiato was completely wrong today ... even more sad i expected it because my orders have been messed up in the past. every employee i've come in contact with is super nice though.

the drink and food were on point and came out quickly despite the line of customers in front of me. it is a long store, so it's pretty skinny and doesn't have a lot of places to sit. luckily most people were getting their items to go so we snagged a seat at their bar area and ate there. outside customers were smoking (which is not allowed at the starbucks in our hometown), so we had to stay inside to avoid the grossness. the counter wasn't clean, but they were busy, so i get it. you need a code for their single bathroom, so be sure to ask when you are in line so you don't have to go back up and ask!

this is the slowest starbucks ever!!! second time trying and orders always slow waiting 5,6,10 minutes in line at the drive thru is absurd!! don't bother going here just go across the street to the dd

not too long of a wait and the baristas were friendly for the most part. my hazelnut frappucino was perfect, added a shot of espresso which they didn't let die and put the right amount of torani pumps in it. they serve great drinks here.

if no stars was possible that's what they would get....this starbucks has the most incompetent staff in town. i consistently get the worst drinks from them as they can not follow directions. not once have they gotten my drink order correct. i have even ordered simple drinks they should not mess up like a green tea latte and they do not add enough of the mixture and i end up with a milk drink essentially. so incompetent!

starbucks is starbucks, but this one always seems to have a line, and the retail area is hard to get to when there are ten people standing in line...it makes you feel bad that you want to browse the mugs and gifts. still, they are a friendly bunch behind the counter, and it's always nice and clean in here, unlike some other starbucks locations where there never seems to be a clean table. lovin' my peppermint mochas this holiday season as always...i'm limiting myself to one per week!

nothing but good things to say. i am constantly here, and i have never had a bad experience with the staff or the food/drinks. sometimes i come in after a very long day at work, and the simple kindness of one of the staff members asking me how i am doing is enough to make me feel better. love the clover...not sure what i'd do without my iced willow!!! bottom line: this is the best starbucks in the city!

if you like the clover brewed coffee, this location has a clover machine. ask the barista for a coffee that is not already brewed and the barista will use the clover machine to brew it. i ask for a decaf pike; the barista brews the coffee using the clover and i do not pay extra for getting a clover brewed coffee. the location was recently remodeled with new wall murals and furniture. there are convenient electrical ports right below the seating. i like this location for their service, informed baristas and cleanliness. thank you starbucks!

i do not need to tell you the coffee is horrible, you already know that. i'm here to stop you from wasting your money on there breakfast or lunch sandwiches. overpriced garbage! the chicken sandwich had a teespoon of chicken. nothing like the one on display. the tomato sandwich had 2 pieces of tomato. the sandwiches are pre-made at who the f**k knows. they taste like sh*t! no more!

this starbucks has earned my 5th star!! my wife and i mobile order frequently. now that they know us they are super friendly and give us exceptional service. when the covid crisis first started we did a mobile order. when i approached the store, there was a line out onto 202. i am guessing it would have taken 25 minutes before i would have made it to the drive up window. i called and asked to confirm that i wasn't able to come in to pick up our drinks. they confirmed that was correct, and then offered to bring my drinks out to me!! about thirty seconds later a super nice barista walked my drinks outside for me. this was above and beyond and i love this location for saving me 15-20 minutes of my time!!
always very friendly and fast with drink orders. seems to be in an area that doesn't get to busy. at least the times that i go.

i know. i know. everyone thinks it's cool to hate starbucks because they're a big fat corporate target, but i'd rank this store way above any indy shop any day. i've tried most of them in town, and they've got nothing on starbucks's quality or service.

every time i come here, this place always messes up my order. also, i am continuously baffled how long it takes to just pour a black coffee out to make an iced americano. a tall house coffee shouldn't require a 10-minute wait.

i love starbucks and this is two miles from my home, but the parking has become impossible. last two times, i've been--no parking. drove across the street to an independent, equally good coffee shop. i want to be a loyal customer, but this location is making it hard. don't do business coffee here anymore either. no quiet tables for two. the staff here is great. the space is clean. but it needs a bigger space and parking lot.

watch this starbucks when you order. three times in the past few months i have ordered a trenta drink and they have given me a venti. i am sure that helps with their profit margin but not the best customer experience. the first time i didn't notice. the second time a friend picked it up for me so it was too late by the time i received it. today they handed it to me like i wouldn't notice. when i pointed it out to the barista, she said they were out of trenta cups so she clearly knew the order wasn't what i had ordered. i use the order ahead feature in the mobile app so even the label clearly states the the correct size. as others have pointed out - management and customer service training seem to be in order for this location.

disappointed when i got my vanilla bean frap, i asked for extra carmel and i didn't get one drop of carmel. the person in drive thru really needs to listen to the customers order. and they didn't have enough green tea!

i stop into this starbucks from time to time, usually doing a mobile order so i can pop in and out without having to wait in the line at the drive-thru. every time i've been inside, i've been pleased with how friendly the baristas are, how clean the place has been, and how quick they are to get my order to the mobile bar. they always make it a point to say hello when i come in and thank me for my business. certainly an a+ experience!

on sunday, august 19th, my husband and i, along with another couple, were returning to west lafayette, in from the indiana state fair in indianapolis, in. we were tired after a long day, and it's about an hour drive, so we decided to stop at the first starbucks we could find that was just outside of indy (to avoid local traffic), to get a little caffeine. according to google maps, the starbucks located at 6378 crane drive in whitestown, in would be open until 10 p.m., and it was 9:12 p.m. when we got off on exit 130; so we weren't worried, even though from a distance we could see a line of cars. we considered getting gas at the gas station next door, but decided not to. when i pulled into the drive-thru, there weren't any more cars in line, so i was expecting a fast, efficient crew. instead, when i spoke our order into the drive-thru's speaker, i got no response. i repeated it, but still, no response. i then said "hello" a few times, but again, no response. i thought that maybe their speaker was broken, that's been the case before at a starbucks, so no big deal. i drove forward to speak with someone at the window. i saw a girl at the register and tried to get her attention by waving my arm around, this did nothing. i lightly honked my horn once, she turned her head and looked at me. i smiled and waved, so that she would understand that i just wanted to get her attention, not to be rude or anything like that. she looked at us, almost thru us, with a blank stare, and turned away. we sat there, puzzled. i was about to honk and wave again, when she walked away. i was hoping this was to go get someone else or something like that. after a short while, she came back to the register area, but did not face us, and did not look at us again. i was stunned at the way she was ignoring us, but i tried to remain calm and reserved, i didn't want to get upset over a simple misunderstanding. as my patience began to wear thin, another girl walked by, i took the opportunity, and honked. she stopped walking, and turned to look at us. i smiled and waved again, but she too just stared at us. then, she seemed to huff as she also walked away from us, like we weren't there, like we didn't exist. now i was upset. we started to wonder if google maps was wrong, and if the store was actually closed. but the lighted sign was still on, and the lights inside were still on. we continued thru the drive-thru and neared the front of the store. we were going to get out and check to see if the hours of operation were posted on the door, but at that point, we didn't want to give them our money anyway. not after that rude display of customer service. even if the store was closed, the only thing the employees needed to do, was tell us that; that would have made us slightly disappointed, but we would've been informed, and on our way. that would have been the decent thing to do. but no, they couldn't even be bothered to give us that, simple human courtesy. as we drove away, we saw a few people getting out of their cars and walking up to the store. we were going to warn them away from the bad customer service, but frankly i was too annoyed to be advising anyone, and didn't want to waste even one more second on that store. we have never received such rude service, actually, non-service, from a starbucks before. it was truly unbelievable. horrible customer service, we will never go here again! the next starbucks we came across had great customer service, thankfully.

awful experience here today. employees are rude and inconsiderate, and lost their minds over my toddler not wearing a mask while sipping his drink as we waited over 20 minutes for my order (that they forgot). you guys, he isn't old enough for a mask period. don't waste your time here!
love this starbucks! i often stop by here on my way to work, they are always fast, friendly, and my drinks are always perfect and delicious!

while a starbucks is usually an as-to-be-expected starbucks - this one is ranking a little lower in my books. yes, this location has all the coffee, tea, and snacks you'd expect, but the customer service here is a bit lacking - and the recycling options were nil. there actually is a big black bin with a recycling symbol on it - but it's obvious that the management of this store doesn't comply - and instead the bin is a platform for a basket of items on sale. (aren't stores like starbucks required to recycle these days?) if i'm in need of a pick-me-up, i'd reconsider going to a different starbucks in the area, or better yet, a local coffee shop!

the staff will do literally anything to make your day and be personal. if they ask if you want something made off of their clover press do not say no! so worth it. every penny. i love these people. shantel is the store manager and i wish she was my mom. tony is hilarious and personal, phil is knowledgable and up for conversation, christine is like your best friend, sakinah is sweet and adorable, evan is a polite model, mary knows her stuff and you can ask her anything, darcy makes the place feel like home, she loves the popcorn and you will too. eli knows the best way to add the right flavor or spice to any coffee, let him help you! he helped me find my soul coffee -- verona with cinnamon. come here and be merry!
they always make drinks wrong and sloppy. i go there and order frappes and 9/10 they are always made wrong. too lose or too thick. not recommended.

i have been coming to this starbucks at least three days a week for the past three years. what i have walked away with, besides an exorbitantly large coffee bill and a caffeine addiction, is the gratification of consistency. the staff here is great & super friendly -- it's awfully nice to walk in, be greeted by your name, and to not have to order your drink half of the time since they've got it memorized (i work nearby and most everyone in my office frequents this location and i am not the only one who has this experience. it's always amazing to me how well they keep track of their customers' wants). the other great thing about this location is how quickly they move. the line can be backed up to the door (as it often is...) and they can plow through it in a matter of minutes. a starbucks is a starbucks is a starbucks.... but the staff at this location just makes this particular one stand out for me (and the update to the decor that they did a few months ago doesn't hurt either).

left here laughing and irritated. laughing because i couldn't believe how ridiculous the barista that waited on me was. she was so dramatic and inappropriate. making comments like "i can't take this anymore" loud enough for us to hear. it wasn't even that busy. 3 of us in line inside and a couple cars at drive thru. i needed to order 3 drinks. got through the first and started the second and she just walked away while i was talking. didn't say excuse me or anything. just walked away. i had to repeat my first drink order 3 times and it wasn't complicated. basic off the menu item. she was just rude and didn't know how to act properly in front of customers. i was a little embarrassed for her.
we waited for half an hour and every single part of our order was messed up. literally every drink.
stopped by for a cup of coffee. couldn't wait for pike coffee to brew so i chose the other type. a sip as soon as i drove off, coffee is warm to cool. so disappointed. thanks for ruining my morning

they are constantly out of product. the staff is slow and don't seem to understand that running out of the same thing every day by 9 am means that you should make more of it. they can't complete simple tasks like putting mobile order labels on the correct order - then acting incensed when questioned about whose food is whose.

we ordered several items, they all came, except one drink. a family of about 8 ordered after us, they alllll got their orders before my missing drink. waited probably 15 mins total, as orders passed. i finally asked the girl, who saw me standing there (i even said "just waiting on my drink" when a guy asked if i was in line and she looked right at me, didn't bother to look into it), and she said "ummmm what size was it?" (does it matter?) then she looks all over the counter top, whispers to another barista, then comes to me and goes "it'll be ready in like a minute"... i said "has it even been started? we're going to be late for our appointment if i wait." and she goes "she's starting it next" so i was like ok well, i gotta go. not worth the $4 coffee, and she said "ok bye." it's one thing to miss an order, people are human. don't bs me about it though when i'm watching you guys not even making the drink.
my absolute favorite starbucks. the baristas are always friendly and very personable. i love all of them and they always make the experience a great one.

the stars are great workers and very friendly. because the area inside is so small, they should probably start asking patrons to leave larger strollers outside. at one point recently i was 22nd in line and it went fairly quickly.

when starbucks is really busy and they only have 1 cashier it's a lot for that 1 person to do. i reccomend that they hire 1 additional or two to only man the cashiers only. nothing negative, just an idea

sure, "starbuck's is starbuck's", but it's all about the customer service. one time i ordered a venti iced soy chai tea latte and got an "are you effing kidding me?" look from the guy behind the counter. what's wrong with a chai latte...iced...with soy. i don't think i'm being that high maintenance. i did have to smile to myself, however, when he spilled it just as he was putting the lid on and had to re-make it. also, for some reason this location does not make iced coffee in winter. when i ordered it i was told they don't make it in winter, but she could make it another way for me if i liked, so i said yes - and i don't know what she did, but it was about 100 times better than their regular iced coffee. (ok, so this is a plus, but i still hate the place). also, i've witnessed who i assume is the manager berating an employee on the phone for being late once again, right in front of the customers, which i thought was extremely unprofessional. overall poor service... it's never speedy and i'm usually worried they are going to get some aspect of the drink wrong. saw a guy wait so long for his misto he had to leave before it was ready. lame.

every time i come here, i plan to wait. this is by far the slowest starbucks in the area. today i skipped drive through and went inside to order my coffee, and discovered the source of the slowness. staff are moving at snail speed! they show no energy and perform every task in slow motion. so frustrating. i get it...food service isn't fun, but take some pride in your service. i have 2 stars because the drink tasted great...15 minutes after i ordered it.

it's starbucks. they all look alike to some degree, all have the exact same menu. ones such as this one always has a plethora of hipsters on their macs doing some graphic design project or blog post.... little coffee trivia i found out there : cold brew takes 20 hours to prepare and is smoother than regular iced coffee.

standard issue starbucks. coffee tasted about the same as most of the other starbucks i've been to. the seating area inside and outside is relatively large. no drive thru, prices were about the same, and the friendliness factor at starbucks is generally bordering weird as it is.
decent latte..great quick drive thru....what else can you say...
