cockroaches! hello! i tell the staff at the counter every other time i order coffee at this location. after a month, cockroaches! maybe it's the old building or maybe it's all the homeless people that hang out in there. this is likely the last time i visit here until they advertise a remodel or put up a sign on the front doors reading, "no homeless welcome". it's a business not a shelter. buy something or don't enter. it's not a personal shower room either, it's called a rest room.

i don't know what it is with this starbucks but there is always a line of at least 7 cars in the drive through. many cars leave. since i did a mobile order, these orders can't be cancelled. so i waited well over 15 minutes to get my very basic order. one time, they made the wrong drink; i could tell by the color. this is a new starbucks; i hope it gets better.

they ran out of ice. not much more to say besides the rude customer service to go with it. i asked if they had anymore double smoked bacon sandwiches or if they were out too & was given the response, "well does that have ice?"

went to this starbucks today, we got a grande caramel macchiatto and a tall skinny vanilla latte in the drive thru. when we got home we found out our coffees not full, they didnt fill it up to the rim, there was only about 3/4 of coffee with the foam in them. we were disappointed.

this starbucks is near my office, so i've gone a bunch of times for lunch. i can tell you that both their pre-packaged lunches and their frappacinos and teas are up to par, and the service is prompt and efficient. i usually come mid to late afternoons, and even then it is busy but i can always find a spot. it seems to be a favorite for tutors and highpowered business people in particular. i can't vouch for early mornings waits, but from what i've seen this location is a good one.

i always receive fast and friendly service at this location. today hannah took my order and served me my coffee. she was very pleasant. i noticed she was helping the person in line ahead of me to receive the best value for her request. not only is the staff friendly, the store and restrooms are clean. these aspects make this my favorite starbucks location.
not a lot of seating but they get my orders out in a timely manner. service is good and it is clean.
this location sucks because of the parking. however, the store inside is pretty big. it looks very small from the outside.

food: 4 stars service: 5 stars this new starbucks at the corner of olive and n. woodsmill is not only conveniently located, but it's fast! (zippidy doo-dah!) the number 1 thing i look for when there's a drive-thru is speed. how quickly can you place your order and have it made/ready for you at the next window? most starbucks take around 5 minutes, with the longest i've ever waited taking 17 minutes. yes. 17 minutes in a drive-thru. :| we received our drinks immediately after we paid. super fast, and super friendly! the starbucks at this location does have a walk-up window as well and a small dining area outside, but does not allow for customers to come in. for people like me who are often on the go, this is perfect! my favorite drinks: chai tea latte & vanilla bean frappuccinos!

my love affair with starbucks began in college, when a budgeted weekend-only soy latte remedied any hangover, amped me up, and helped forge my way to a 4.0. now, 5 years later, my latte love-turned-lifeline has spiraled and my discretionary income takes a hit more mornings than not. there's something very special about a beverage that is a symbol for industriousness and also the reason why i hike out in two feet of snow, in freezing weather, sometimes with the flu, to get a beloved boisson. as an aside, this location is the best s-bux ever! the team always manage to exceed my expectations.

i ordered 3 different food items and they were all out including the egg whites and tomato mozzarella panini. this starbucks used to be on top of it all but i don't think i'll come to this location anymore.

starbucks is starbucks, that much we know. so what sets each cafe apart is how well they make their drinks, and how good their service is. for being a busy cafe in the heart of clearwater beach, the crew here sure do know how to pump out their orders quickly and accurately while being courteous simultaneously. and whenever i have issues with my orders, they're quick to fix - of course, i'm courteous to them which, really, is all they ask for. because when you're on the beach, it seems like anyone who isn't a local are always rude.

beautiful remodel! the washrooms are beautiful. nice new selection of sandwiches too!!! one of our fav places to go after church. still like the old set up better though. not crazy about sitting on a stool with no back on it :-( staff is always pleasant no matter how busy they are.

this absolutely sucked my a$$ i ordered a venti and paid for a venti and got a hot damn grande! then i was like at least i still got this burrito and i tasted it and just my luck it was just booty hole another cherry on top was the drive thru window lady.... let me tell you her breath was appalling that's it that's all i have to say.

i would have to say this is my favorite starbucks. i work across the street so i frequent this establishment quite often and i cannot recall one bad experience. the baristas are always so nice and if i've ever had a problem with my order they always offer to fix it and are very nice about it. i have been known to have an order of up to 10+ drinks on more than one occasion during for hospital starbucks runs and the speed that they pump out those drinks is amazing. keep it up south meadows starbucks!

i took my dad to starbucks for a coffee however there was not a place to sit together because everyone in there had a computer and took up 4 seats per person with their backpacks books and crap all over the tables. wtf!!! this crap is out of control real customers do no have a place to sit and enjoy coffee that's crazy that they have to take over the entire seating sections.
staff is friendly and quick to fill orders. ambience is nice and there seems to be plenty of space to work in the main area, attached room, or outside. there's a drive thru, too.
lilah, and the entire crew are the best. always great service and great attitude jay , elora, emily, allison, amber

this starbucks is closest to my house, so i always come before i have classes at school. at night it does get a little crowded, but everyone is very nice, there is plenty of seating both indoors and outdoors, and there are a lot of parking spaces. sam, dylan, michael, and this girl with pigtails (i forgot her name) are the best! they're very nice and actually talk to you while they're making your drink. i appreciate that, because sometimes they tell you about new things you should try next time you come in, or make little suggestions that could help make your drink better.

just went through the drive thru and the young man at the window literally deserves a raise. the small 30 second interaction we had made my day with his positive, go get 'em attitude. a plus costumer service!!!!
though long overdue, the recent renovation makes this a great coffee shop with a variety of indoor seating options to satisfy all. also excellent outdoor area. as always, the coffee is the best.

usually this location is really awesome and they have been open much earlier and more often than some branches at this time. but i arrived this morning and was allowed to place a mobile order right at 6 o'clock this morning. i got to the front door and it was dark and no one was even there. i could only wait 10 minutes before i had to leave and go to work so i left empty-handed. super disappointed with this location this was always my go to spot. i hope they figure out their stuff.

i went in here this morning and i was already running late. there were 7 people in line in front of me so i felt pretty nervous. ahh, i needed my coffee so i sucked it up and waited in line. the line moved quickly and soon i ordered my favourite beverage: a skinny vanilla latte (and a tea latte for my boyfriend). as the barista was finishing up the drinks in front of mine, the woman in front of me spilled her drink everywhere and it held up the line. the barista was really friendly about it, told her not to apologize over and over again and she quickly cleaned it up and continued on with her drinks. i really liked this location: it had the speediness of downtown cafes but the friendliness of a neighbourhood coffee shop!
bad service. 15 min wait mid day for a mocha. a hassled barrister. sweating. with attidude. maybe just a bad day.

customer service wise this starbucks is good. fast drive-through, great customer service and clean restrooms, like almost every other starbucks i've been to. so why just two stars? the parking situation is horrible! the flow of traffic is really bad, in fact dangerous if you see how the patterns move. i would be very surprised if there haven't been any accidents or fender-benders here due to the design of the parking lot. having said that, it's a block away from my office and convenient as heck, so i roll the dice to get my tall cup of pikes...every single day.

i can't say that i ran into the same issue as the previous person that reviewed this starbucks. in fact, i had a rather positive experience. driving down the road, i had a hankering for a soy chai tea latte right about the time i passed this starbucks. i swung in and realized i had to go inside because there isn't a drive thru. i also realized after i parked by the door there was a big sign with an arrow that said "use other door" right where everyone else was parked. there were a couple of people in line ahead of me, but the cashier kept the pace moving and before i knew it, i was ordering and swiping my app to pay. there was only one barista crafting the drinks, so there was a little bit of a delay in getting my beverage, but it was perfectly prepared (i ordered it extra hot, and this was the perfect temp) and tasted wonderful. a solid 4 star review.. i just can't give a 5-star to a starbucks without a drive thru. i debated on sitting out front and honking until someone came out and gave them my order, but that sounded rude.
ordered online but the workers were super unhelpful and basically ignored me. supremely rude staff. looked at me like i was an alien for ordering an extra drink after passing past drive thru.

my colleague and i were in need of a washroom and a great iced coffee so what better place to stop at than starbucks to order 2 coffee frappuccinos. they make the best! it was refreshing and needed in a friday afternoon. i get mine made with coconut milk and it's got just the perfect balance of sweetness. i'm always pleased to stop at a starbucks to use their facilities. they are typically very clean. today was no exception the washrooms were very clean and our order took no time at all. this location is pretty nice great big windows and a nice patio.

this is consistently the slowest starbucks i have visited. if you're looking to grab something quickly in the morning, do not visit this store. there always seems to be 10 people behind the counter but only one at the register and the line moves very slow. the store is aesthetically appealing inside and some of the employees are very friendly but i find it frustrating how consistently slow the service is.
i love it here! the staff is all super sweet- especially martha! great atmosphere and plenty of space to sit. my only gripe is that parking can be a pain. still- this is my favorite starbucks by far

i used to work right on 4th & market and parked around this starbucks, so obviously on my walk to work, i would randomly stop in for a morning latte. i love this staff. i have been to many-a-starbucks and they are top notch and my absolute favorite. they were always so friendly and though i'd only stop in once a week, they remembered me and always had a warm welcome. i seriously miss this group and every time i am in old city, i make sure i stop in because i love dealing with them. just thought i would let yelpers know how wonderful they are! p.s. - like another guy wrote, be very careful with parking. the ppa will nab your car within 3 seconds. also, peak hours, it can be a little tight in there.
bad service. the regulars are taken care of before you regardless of who is next in line. and i was on my way to work and the "regular" was just hanging out. not cool.

friendly staff and quick service. i appreciate that they still have breakfast sandwiches near noon. today is black friday and they're keeping everything moving smooth. they have a unique selection of starbucks coffee cups that i haven't noticed in others. very clean as well.

sure this place has its flaws, but trust me when i say this: they are trying their best here! each and every partner at this store comes to work in hopes of a better day, they are trying to make every customer happy. even the ones that are complete trash to them. give them a break! instead of complaining about the wait take a minute to look around you, see that line of customers out the door? did you also see that drive thru line wrapped around the building? did you know that this is the only starbucks with a drive thru in this area? did you notice how this starbucks is located right off the highway? expect it to be busy. now remember at the end of the day it's just coffee and those baristas are humans with feelings so be kind to them. 5 stars to those hard working baristas and 1.5 stars to the many customers that come here.
i went into this starbucks recently and was impressed by the friendly staff and quick service! definitely my favorite starbucks location in town!
the only thing that could make starbucks better would be if a coffee/green tea didn't cost almost five dollars. they always have the best staff and great drinks

went out of my way to come here. sat in the drive-through for 8 minutes. when i get up to the window, i sit for another five or six minutes. then they tell me the espresso machine is not working. then asked if i wanted something else. i said no, i went out of my way to come here to get a latte. she said sorry about that and gave me my money back. i just sat there for 14 minutes and my time was not acknowledged. they didn't offer me a free drink, free food. a gift card. nothing. justa "sorry about that". just left me super annoyed that i wasted so much time for nothing. i know many other starbucks would try to compensate for some thing so serious but not this one i suppose.

i often stop by here when i am at work as they are only a block away. the baristas know me by name but i never really order the same drinks 2 days in a row. like to keep them guessing. lol. they have pretty good with service and try to be efficient and get people through. i love their patios in the summer time and actually it's probably my favorite patio for a coffee shop in the city.

i am a regular at this starbucks and found all the staff to be very nice and friendly except i had an awful experience with an employee today. she was rude and kept interrupting me when i was trying to order, and lied to me about not having any blueberry muffins despite there being 3 on display, and didn't even bother to check. i even called in to check if they had muffins two minutes after i ordered and they did have blueberry muffins! i will not be returning to this starbucks again, thanks isabel, you just lost a customer. :(

i stopped in at what was the original delaware county starbucks. it has been updated a few times since it first opened. they stuck with only tables on the last two renovations. i hadn't been here in a while but stopped in while waiting for a friend to finish their appointment a few doors down. it was a friday evening and i was a bit disappointed. there were several people working the counter and i got good service and good coffee. why then was i unhappy, you are thinking? as i am waiting, cruising the internet, i am in the company of 6 or so pubescent tweeners who are talking drugs and private parts to the extent that in any other location, this would be sexual harassment. thank goodness it only lasted 15 minutes, but i was surprised that the staff just ignored it and let it go on. after they left, i was inundated by two more sets of teenagers. not a place for adult drinkers like the havertown location....
these directions led us to a cow field, no word on whether his name is starbucks, but he didn't have any coffee.

overcharge me yet again, this time they refused to fix the issue and since the drive through had already taken 10min for 4 cars i left. i'm sorry but kids cups of whipped cream are free at every other store not $2.95 for a short cup of nothing but whipped cream. last time they told me i ordered two mochas not one and tried to make me pay for their whoops. will be avoiding them from now on.. other starbucks around town who don't treat customers so rudely.

this is the slowest starbucks i've ever seen. the line in the drive through is often backed up almost to the interstate and i can't tell you how many times they have messed up my order. unfortunately it's the only starbucks in plant city so i have no choice but to suck it up and wait wait wait! i used to visit 3-4 times a week but now i barely make it twice a month. if it weren't for the gift cards i receive i probably would never go!!

nice run of the mill starbuck. cashier let me try the nitro coffee before i purchased it. i never had it before and wanted to try something new. less bitter than any of the other coffee from starbucks. i ordered it with sweet cream in it. yum!!! the employees were attentive and great and treated everyone equitable while i was there.

i go to this starbucks only because i work right around the corner, so it's based on convenience. i never have an issue with the service or the wait times etc, i just can never seem to get the exact same order twice. i, much like thousands of other coffee drinkers, usually have a regular go-to coffee order, so i basically order the same thing every time, but rarely do i ever get the same quality. often times i'll order coffee at 7-7:30am and it's room temperature. i'll ask for cream and sugar and get either barely any sweetness or super sugary, this morning i asked for cream and sugar and it looked like they put no cream in it. guys- i understand the struggles, i work in the service industry too, but you're making coffee not building spaceships. i'm sure you have a great training program but please do better.

this is a standard starbucks location. i got a beverage here and got the exact experience that i expected from my visit (especially as i ordered on the app). i don't really have much else to say about this location as i've only visited it once or twice ever.
worst starbucks i've ever been to. trash was all over the floor yesterday. drink was made with waaaaaaay too much cream.

kim is not a very nice barista, in my opinion, & seemingly they have a high turnover of employees, but everyone else i've dealt with at this location is okay. if you like dark coffee, don't stop here after 4pm, as they don't brew it in the afternoon & they apparently don't offer pour overs.
the place is dirty and small. too bad for the most important plaza in indianapolis. the cashier was ill-mannered. she didn't say a word to me at all.
starbucks needs to retrain employees. today again they couldn't even make an iced green tea correctly as i received an iced black tea. listen up starbucks you have a problem in this store!

was super excited to have this location here since i could hit the drive by before work to grab coffee. i did go inside and it's very small. the entrance is shared with the bank. however the first time going the service was super slow and there were only 3 customers in line. the one barista messed up my friends drink (it was remade and she loved it... so she didn't complain) but my friend had to remind the women to give her a cookie she ordered. my 2nd time going was very pleasant and i used the drive-thru... very convenient and look forward to coming back.

horrible horrible service!! i walked in to order a decaf black. the cashier wanted to know if it was a "decaf this" or a "decaf that". i just want a decaf. "well no one ever orders decafs. that's going to take a while. you'll just have to wait! is that ok with you?" well, i guess i have no choice. so i sat down and waited and waited. finally the cashier ( same one who took my order for the black decaf) calls out, "do you want room for cream?" ugh, no, black means no room for cream. then the barista called out several minutes later, "vente decaf!" i walk up, no decaf. i stand there for five minutes. still no decaf. i go back and sit down for five minutes then finally i walk back to the counter and ask about the decaf they called me up for. " oh i haven't started it yet". i kid you not, 20 minutes from placing my order to getting my coffee. that's ridiculous. the barista apparently knew i was unhappy so she got me back. she put the lid on so the drinking hole was on the crack and it would drip on my dress. thank you. that was thoughtful. had to drive home to change before going to work.

don't go to this starbucks, one of the workers stole my info off my cc & starbucks card & took 200.00 out of my account. according to starbucks headquarters this isn't the first time it happened here.
worst service of any starbucks i have been to across the country. staff was indifferent to the needs or time of the customers present. i will spend my money at another place.
i've been to many of starbucks. this one is great and haters gonna hate. keep up the good work and thank you for your friendly service!

it took twenty minutes for them to make me my egg bites. they kept saying "sorry she's in training." i don't understand how that makes it take twenty minutes when there is no line. when i got the egg bites they threw it in a bag and smushed it, no small flat tray like they usually come in. i will never come back to this location, holy shit i am so annoyed.
they need to hire more than three people to work. i should not have to wait 10 minutes to have my order taken. only go to starbucks because someone gave me a gift card - over priced slow service.

this starbucks is really great. i guess it's a pretty high volume store because of the intersection it sits on. i recently went in because i heard they had a renovation (within the past year according to other yelp photos) and man is it beautiful in here! i love all the tufted leather, wood paneling, wall paper, and gorgeous art! not to mention the edison bulbs in the bathroom. i'm not sure if they just redid it or expanded the size as well because it looks much larger than i remember it. this location was already nicer than other locations and the remodel just tipped the scales. as for coffee they have the same usual starbucks menu. they did appear to have a large wall with other snacks and gifting items though. i got a venti psl and it tasted delicious although they don't fill the cup nearly as high as you'd think because of all the whip they add. my drink did take awhile to make. i feel like i was waiting much longer than other patrons. maybe they cater to the drive thru first? there were a lot of people working here though. all of them running back and forth. if you're not in a hurry this is a great starbucks to sit and stay awhile.
whoa! definitely my favorite starbucks. i drive out of my way just to see mckenzie. she always greets me with a smile and most of the time remembers my name and what i like to order.
the absolute worse starbucks i have ever been to. none of the ac outlets work so you can not charge your laptop. i will never come here again. good bye starbucks and hello dunkin donuts!!!!
i'm so exhausted of this place. it's the only one closest to me that's safe but their lattes are always so short.

someone gave a one star because they didn't like the remodel....hmmmm...ok. this is an excellent location with fantastic employees who provide outstanding customer service. they are quick even during the morning rush. the windows allow a refreshing amount of light in without being blinded. i particularly like the long tables that promote a friendly feeling instead of being segregated tables that clutter up the room. this also makes the location feel more roomy. great job in this location.

better than average starbucks.. great design great fast service, very friendly much faster than nj and what i'm used to waiting in long lines. i miss the homemade starbucks yogurt not sure why the only sell oikos.. i love my starbucks version
the staff is rude and the location has no power outlets. how does a coffee shop not be equipped for tele commuters? do not visit this location!

although i do not drink starbucks often (way too pricey for me), i do like it here. the customer service is top notch! i noticed that they really try to get to know each and every customer there. they are huge on teamwork back there. when one person gets extremely busy, i noticed that they don't even need to ask for help. other team members just step up and do their part. they tend to always butcher my name, but i know they are trying. :-) maybe if i came more often than once ever 2-3 weeks, they would get it right. plus, i give them a thumbs up for knowing so many people's names since the place is always packed to its max.
nice that we have a starbucks in fishhawk but the service is not so great! drive through wait times a horrific and service is not so friendly.

there is always the longest line at this starbucks. plan on being in the drive through for over 10 mins, and probably closer to 15 mins. the employees are extremely kind and personable, but the service is extraordinarily slow. also, they seem to be randomly closed at times for no particular reason.

we went here today mostly for the coolness of having a starbucks at the zoo and having a chance to try out the paper straws. all was good! this starbucks functions like any other starbucks, including the breakfast sandwich options, but they serve iced coffee with a paper straw. i've never used a paper straw before today but i'm happy to report it worked just like a plastic one. definitely recommend this starbucks.

this starbucks has been open throughout the pandemic with altered hours 6am-6pm. when they are open they have a really great dining room and a private room for studying in the back! i've been through the drive thru quite a lot lately and my drinks are always made correctly here. also everyone here is very friendly despite the times. if this starbucks was closer to me then this would definitely be my go-to!

spacious, clean starbucks located next to st. joe's. baristas were friendly but a little inefficient. 2-3 people in line and 10 minutes to get my drink. hate to see what would happen if the line really started to back up. not sure how long this store has been around, but it seems pretty new. strange hours as it closed at 5 on the day that i visited. let's not stay open too late now; we might pull in too many college kids in need of a study break.
the slowest, and i mean crazy slowest, starbucks i have ever been into in my entire life. fire everyone please and let's start from scratch?

this starbucks was a quick pit-stop on the way out of philadelphia to head back to dc. not only is this a gorgeous location if you're considering going for the wifi and to do work, but the employees here are quick and friendly. we were very happy with our experience here, and i'd absolutely go back the next time i'm in philadelphia. also, parking was easy. let's be honest, in any major city parking has to factor into the rating of an establishment. well, way to go this starbucks! you're great with that. street parking nearby, and we were leaving on a monday morning at like 10. so there should have been lots of people trying to park, or so i'd expected. but the parking gods were on our side! all in all, a beautiful and accessible starbucks location in a fun, vibrant part of the city.

wow, unhelpful baristas with seriously unsanitary habits. this location is always packed, and there must've been seven employees working, but watching them work was unsettling. the work space is a mess, and the cloths used to wipe the appliances are placed on dirty counters then used to wipe steam nozzles used to froth your milk. a gentleman returned his sandwich, saying he requested it with just egg, no meat, and there was bacon grease all over it. turns out they cooked the pre-prepared sandwich (which includes bacon), then pulled it open, removed the bacon, and gave him the sandwich. i waited 22 minutes for my order, and at no point did i see a single employee put on gloves. so i can imagine how they pulled the bacon off the sandwich. (ew.) on a pickier note, my standard order is a vanilla latte with honey, and this is the first location i've encountered that "wasn't able" to add the honey to my drink. so i asked where the honey packets are, and the barista pointed to the end of the counter. of course, when i checked, there were no packets. i asked two different employees if they had any more. one ignored me and the other said "maybe in the back" and just stood there. when i asked him if he could check, he disappeared and didn't return. so, yeah, i'll be taking a hard pass on this location on my next business trip.

disappointed with the service of this location. ordered ahead through the app so i wouldn't have to stand in line. my coffee was ready but the food wasn't. i asked about the food; the response i got was "i just do drinks." okay, but what about the rest of my order...she walked away. finally i see a food item put into the pickup section. it was mine...but where was the rest of it. sat back down and continued to wait. finally went back to ask and they said they're working on it. once the guy came over with my oatmeal he had the wrong kind. ordered blueberry but got the classic. guess they figured i didn't want or need agave syrup and the nut medley. at least he tossed in the berries. needless to say, i won't be going back if i'm ordering food. service was slow and my order was wrong.

called at 10:15 p.m. last night to make sure they would be open until 11p.m. as posted. the gentleman confirmed the closing time was 11p.m. so i get there at 10:30 and every do you know? they are closed.

i have nothing against the drinks or food, but the way they conduct business. the baristas are super sweet, but the lack of attention to all of the different ways of ordering is bewildering. if you are going to offer curbside then make sure you get it done. after waiting 30 min, finally i go in to be told that my order wasn't even noticed by anyone. waited another however long and still didn't get my sandwich so i left. the physical location already makes this place trash.

if you're driving here with no hands, then perhaps you should steer-bucks. i really like this mall but i do not really like this starbucks. the employees were okay but i could not believe how much they were charging my girl for a frozen blended drink. inevitably, she gave me some, and it really just tasted like ice with a little sugar and milk in it. i could have sworn these drinks were better a few years ago. maybe it's just this location. one extra star for the friendly employees.

eew. l just paid $17 for: a triple macchiato that tastes like nothing but milk (no espresso in sight despite paying for extra espresso), a mocha frappuccino that is basically ice and chocolate syrup (you can even see the ice visibly separated from the syrup) not in the least appetizing, and a mocha that tastes like chocolate water. first and last visit.
this sounds silly, but i love this starbucks! the people that work there are super nice and when i open the door to leave i always think to myself, "well, what a nice day."

starbucks as far as drinks go is... well.... starbucks. they're always exactly the same. i know what to expect and that's what i get. so that's the reason for the solid three stars. what baffles me about this location is that i have been going there consistently since i moved to this side of town about three years ago. and by consistently, i mean at least three times a week, every single week. they still do not know my name. every time i go in, they act like its the first time they have ever seen me. they're never rude, but they're not really that nice and even when they're nice- they definitely don't recognize me. the qt employees next door recognize me more and they have only been seeing me for about 4 months... step it up starbucks. please.

people think i'm crazy for reviewing starbucks since they're a chain, but let's be real, there are some good ones and bad ones and you just never know what you're getting. the store is bright and new and has a great outdoor patio for sitting. there is a mix of comfy chairs and tables so you can choose what you'd like and there are enough spaces that i haven't had a problem finding a place to sit. the employees here are really friendly. i feel like they actually go above and beyond in customer service. if you ask a question, they genuinely talk to you. i've seen them make kiddos frappuccinos and hand them out with big smiles. they also have taken my drink to my table for me while i was in the restroom, and i see them bring people their pastries all the time. they take the time to make everyone feel like they're a real guest in the store. the store is clean, they keep the floor tidy, and the music isn't overly loud. bathrooms are well stocked, and they always have plenty of food available. definitely recommend you check out this location. it's also close enough to the eagle greenbelt you can head over for a walk, which is a bonus.

this is my morning go to coffee spot before the gym! absolutely love james, he always has great customer service! however, the past few times i've been my coffee is half full! i always order a grande and it feels so light. i didn't take a single sip from this coffee and finally checked it this time. i don't want to sound rude at the drive thru asking for it to be a full cup like i know there's space for whip, but my goodness!

i like indie coffee places, but sometimes i want a weird frappa-something-i-can't-pronounce. when i want something more than tea or black coffee, i head up here. this place is clean, the staff are nice and friendly, the food is just like any other starbucks, so you know what you are getting.

i'd give this particular starbucks a pass, if i were you. the wait is generally quite long. i've waited up to 10 minutes at the drive through where, unfortunately, you can't get away once you're stuck there. today, with the drive through line of cars hitting about 8, i went inside. there was no one in front of me in line so i thought this would be good. however, i waited at the counter for about 2 minutes with no one coming to take my order. after that i just left, thinking that if it is more than 2 minutes to take my order, how long would it be to actually get a cup of coffee. i wish i could cash in the starbucks gift card i got - so not worth it. also, i've gotten an inedible breakfast sandwich here with my coffee once in the drive through. the muffin-and-ham sandwich was still ice cold in the middle. so, give this place a pass and hit one of the non-chain coffee shops nearby. i'm definitely pretty annoyed with this store and won't bother going back.
cute and cozy. love the wood decor. drinks are fine, and the staff is friendly. would return for more hot beverages!

this is my daily go to coffee spot, the staff are friendly and fast. every starbucks is basically the same, this one is no worse than any other. except the location is ideal for me and the many downtown office workers. the bathroom can only be opened by asking at the cash register, this is due to the central downtown location.

i live nowhere near this starbucks and i legit will drive all the way downtown just to go to this one some days. they always do my right! my drink has never been messed up and the drive thru line is typically quick even during the "pandemic." i appreciate y'all! don't change or start messing up people's orders because this is my favorite starbucks in indiana. :)
standard starbucks but with drive-thru, free wifi, and an amazing, well-spaced outdoor patio. it's a great place to do work outside the house during covid

this is a good location and the folks here do a great job. i've been here when it's busy and when it's not. (of course, it's difficult to greet and chat with every customer when the line is 15 deep and there are 10 cars in the drive-thru. i say this because i've read other reviews for this location where people were kvetching that they didn't get a back-rub when they walked in...) this location was recently remodeled. it's more open, seems more spacious, with more natural light and has a simpler layout. the new furniture is not comfortable. it looks like somebody went crazy shopping at ikea. gone are the comfy chairs and couches. doesn't all-powerful starbucks want customers to "hang out at starbucks?"

was my first time at this starbucks my wife's favorite coffee place since the others are closed she came to this one was nice fast friendly and order was correct then we went to another place that was open now just so you all know lol i prefer coffee bean

yummy but the staff refused to make a frappacino i have ordered at least three times before at the same location and at least fifty times before in my lifetime at other locations. also she didn't get half my order then while we waited for it she lectured me about how old my request was . oh come on. i just want to go to home. i won't be back. but i will say once i got my order to where she would be willing to make it it was very good
i am not a fan of the coffee.i usually get latte ir capaccino..i am a fan of the bakery items..just personal preference.they also have a small gift area.

rating starbucks is like rating mcdonald's: what's the point? yep, it's a starbucks on market street attached to the huge marriott hotel. about what you'd expect from a starbucks. no better, no worse.
it's as good as any starbucks..mediocre at best

sometimes you just need coffee. i'd much prefer to give my money to a mom and pop coffee joint, but honestly, $bux will have to do. the service was excellent, the product was perfect. and the company was great.

i would have given this place a 5 star but everytime i order the green tea frap it taste like a vanilla bean frap. but its still pretty good and can't complain. the service here is amazing. its always busy and so hard to get parking if you have to drive around a couple of times. wish they had drive thru... lol. the spinach wraps also very good. love love it.

i know everyone is saying this is the worst starbucks, due in large part to the extended wait times. well, my experience was different. yes, the wait was long. in fact, it was absurdly long. we spent 40 minutes in the drive thru line (and there were not that many cars to start with!). however, my in-laws are in town, so i enjoyed the time away. sadly, this is pretty consistent at this location.

first let me say i'm giving this 5 stars just compared to other starbucks not compared to other coffee shops on general. this was one of the best starbucks i've been too. the customer service was fantastic and though i didn't stay to sit down it seemed to have more seats than the usual store. i am used to getting a slightly different spelling or different name but here i got the worst starbucks name fail i ever experienced. my name is kristin and when i got to the car i realized she wrote "griffin" on both my cups! oh well the drinks were perfect and my mom and i had a good laugh!

this is one of the easier locations to get to except during morning hours. go after 1030am. the drinks still do not come out consistent. i came here for 3 days straight and ordered the same green tea frap.. all tasted different.

it's starbucks...mostly consistent factory product delivery with variation in the accessibility, architecture, staff and local management. this is a relatively new spot (so new that google maps doesn't fully know about it as of this post.) kudos to the team as staff are pleasant and on point. your mileage is going to vary as "disappointing" is the best i can say overall, though you can still satisfy your buzz crave. accessibility gets low marks due to poor design of the strip center near the ovation property that may or may not ever fully materialize. parking is a challenge and will only become worse as the area expands to more fully suck. lanes are narrow, few, and full even during off-peak hours. architecture and design earn fails for both the garage band harshness of what should feel like an invitation to someone's bonus room and lack of convenient access to amenities. decor is comfy and open, but noise reduction is either missing altogether or substantially neglected by the designers. forget conversation, but no one talks these days. right? convenience also seems to have missed the design requirements. customers must swim 10-15 yards upstream from the door through the line or circumnavigate the tables into the rear of the space to order. add to that a lack of trash cans; none may be found in view if they actually exist in the general customer space at all. two small (not much larger than a grande cup) orifices exist built-in at the pickup counter. wade through all waiting customers (as well as those grazing over condiments and napkins) and you'll arrive at the too-small trash holes whose bins are usually filled to the wood of the counter top. it scores two thumbs way, way down as they help your fingers cram refuse into a wet lack of space.
pretty empty for a starbucks at 8am. but a larger sitting area than most and they have outdoor seating.
as one of the only coffee places in the area, i was excited to try it and i was not let down. the coffee was great, the little cafe itself was cute and well-equipped, and the staff was friendly.

i rate this starbucks 3 stars instead of 5, because: -as another reviewer stated, this starbucks tends to be consistently busy and loud on the inside. i would not recommend having a meeting, interview or phone call here. -the drive thru is very busy as well, and is one of the slower drive thrus i have used at a starbucks -the staff is below average, and there seems to be a fair amount of attrition. i have received more wrong orders here than at other starbucks it is clean on the inside, and has nice outdoor seating. the large windows to the south make the afternoon sunshine coming in nice.

place had 3 customers in there and it still took 20 minutes to get my drink which was terrible. they had 5 people working but only 2 seemed to be doing anything. very poorly managed location. this is not the first bad experience i've had at this location...but it will be the last as i will not be returning. there is a dunkin donuts right down the street.

so, i've finally ventured into this south street during the weekend. nice staff. just wish i could figure out where the trash was and that there was a little more seating. it was a little cramped once it got to be later in the day as i did my work.

great location, clean, friendly staff, and a really nice outdoor seating area. this is one of my favorite starbucks around. it's newer than most, so it has a modern feel with the wood fence outside, fresh coat of beige paint, and comfortable inside seats. they know how to personalize the drinks you order, so no need to walk inside to get your drink remade. yay! enjoy! xo

this location is the third busiest store in the entire midwest. the baristas here are humans. they all may have a bad day from time to time, due to angry customers constantly interrogating them on when their drink will come out. them looking for your order will only delay yours and everybody else's. please be understanding. there are only three machines to run and hundreds of drinks to be made. please be patient with them. if you are running late, you should never stop for coffee. do not blame baristas. you couldn't possibly expect to get your drinks in five minutes, when there were 15+ people in front of you (most likely with 2+ items each - this includes mobile order & pay) in line, right? remember golden rule, "do unto others as you would have them do unto you". i love the o'fallon team! you guys rock!

this starbucks location is probably the worst i have been to customer service wise. they continually over charge for their coffee. it's run like a disorganized mom and pop shop rather than a company store with standards. here are two examples with the first being from this morning: my wife and i went into starbucks to get a couple of wraps and some coffee. i ordered a regular coffee and my wife ordered a macchiato. she asked for it to be made decaf and wet (what she orders at all starbucks locations), which is a regular order at all the other starbucks. the server asks if she would prefer a cappucino. my wife said no very nicely. we were provided our total and paid. upon reviewing the receipt the first line item was for a capuccino which was strange. so my wife asked why there's capuccino and not macchiato. she was told that a wet macchiato is a short capuccino. now remember, she was just asked if she wanted a cappucino and she said no. yet, the server took it upon herself to charge for a cappucino any way and didn't offer to say, 'next time mam i will have to charge you for a cap'. nope, just went ahead and did it without asking or informing. terrible customer service experience. we're only talking an extra dollar, but the way it was done and handled was very bad. we both did not enjoy our breakfast and left with a very disappointing experience. so this is where the story gets better about this specific location. i drink decaf coffee and love to have a cup after lunch. so when i am having lunch i usually stop by a near starbucks to get a cup of decaf. for some strange reason this same starbucks location always tells me how they don't make decaf in the afternoon (as they all do) and that they will have to make me a cup on a drip machine or clover machine. what's different about this location is that they give me a song and dance every time because they have to make the cup separately, they have to charge more because they have to make decaf on demand in the afternoon since they don't brew big pots. the staff taxes you for buying decaf in the afternoon. so if i by a small cup they charge the price of a medium cup and if i buy a medium cup they charge for a large cup. huh? no other starbucks in town does this and it baffles me how this ones does that. the price displayed says one price and they decide to jack it up because they don't make it in the afternoon. their excuse, 'it requires more labor to make a cup of decaf'. this is a company that specializes in making coffee!! they're good at it and efficient at it. this is a definite way to drive away customers. you would not expect this from a company with company wide standards. this is what i mean when i say that this location is run like a disorganized mom and pop shop. i would prefer to give my money to a local family down the street running their own coffee shop. again not talking about a lot of money here, but when you go into a place and there's a price listed for something, you would feel weird when they over charge you for that thing. in the case of the decaf, i am not asking for extras or something different, i am just asking for what is on the menu regularly. can you tell i'm not happy with this specific starbucks location? i hope this post will get that store manager's attention and train the employees to provide better customer service and proper pricing. they get two stars instead of one because at least the coffee is up to standards. the rest of the starbucks in santa barbara are very well run, have great teams, and i will continue to frequent them.
extremely long wait but grateful for the drive thru. staff are pleasant. got my drinks right.
piss poor service not coming back anytime soon would rather go to a different starbucks and get better service with how much i buy starbucks
i really enjoy going to this starbucks. the folks here remember your name and are always friendly. never have to wait too long. keep it up y'all!
this starbucks has to be the worst service i've ever seen. spare yourself the agony and go to a different location. they had to remake my order 3 times and it still didn't taste right slow service...
i ordered a steamed milk for my child with vanilla. $3.56? really? won't be back for a while. don't appreciate being taken advantage of for the sake of a starbucks cup.

very slow, unfriendly workers with unimpressive barista skills. plan to spend a good part of your morning in their drive thru and when you get to the window you will realize they can't queue drinks to save their lives. and good luck not getting your order messed up.

this is just like any other starbucks you've ever been to, minus comfortable seating. the place is always busy and the staff is pretty efficient, but very business oriented. even rethink is focused on speed and less on hospitality. the coffee is starbucks, so nothing fancy. it's the same fare you can get at any location anywhere. the work areas are clean but the merchandise is covered in a layer of dust. just goes to show that i'm not the only one who thinks a 12oz tumbler should cost less than $25. but it's a moot point because i wanted a better cup of coffee but i had to very much settle for starbucks.

any ol starbucks, but super busy with jefferson students and doctors coming in and out all day long. not really a place to study because people are always moving around, but a nice comfortable place to have coffee with a friend.

well i don't know where to start. one thing this place is always packed!! this is my first time having starbucks...yeah it's true why you ask well probably because i hate the smell of coffee so if it wasn't for a gift card i would have never tried them. like i mentioned its always packed so we walked in the service was perfect i ordered a blueberry muffin and a apple juice it came out nice and warm and was damn good. i can honestly see why folks are crazy about this place i'm just not into all the coffee and lattes it's just not my thing and for the record i almost died from the coffee smell while waiting for the order lol.

rude and efficient employees. it's an airport. everyone is on a timeline. there were four employees, two of them basically doing nothing, and the one at the espresso machine in between drinks because they're moving customers through so slowly. meanwhile, the only cashier taking orders, takes time to go actually fix a drink. no one steps up to the cash register to take an order for the wait on someone else. so the long lessons around the corner. plus every single one of the employee individuals had an attitude. i'm also quite sure that starbucks could give a hoot

from 7am to 12:30pm this is the best starbucks in all of reno, the coffee is hot the service is fast and pleasent and the drinks are made correct..... any time after 12:30pm the service go hell in a hand bag! i have stopped by for a cup of coffee and they have no fresh coffee, they have to brew it... give me a break, what coffee place at 3pm doesn't have coffee.............. this place!

this is probably one of the worst starbucks i've been to. sat in the drive through line for at least 20 minutes (no exaggeration) on a saturday afternoon when starbucks generally isn't crowded. if you're looking for your starbucks fix in any sort of a timely fashion, do yourself a favor and choose one of the other locations nearby.

i frequently visit this starbucks location and the establishment is always clean, shelves orderly, and the staff is extremely nice. today when i went into starbucks with my husband, the staff went out of their way to make sure everything was great. my husband ordered his usual and i ordered a water and a white mocha. he received his coffee and my water pretty quickly and we sat down waiting for my mocha. we waited for a few minutes and when he went to check on the status, an employee had already made it and placed it on the counter and someone picked it up. realizing this, they apologized (totally wasn't their fault) and quickly made me another. i really appreciated the fact that they immediately made me another drink and apologized- even though the apology wasn't necessary.
love my skinny vanilla latte. always have quick, friendly and accurate service. i also love the outside seating. one of the better starbucks locations. the new decor is nice.
predictable, solid service. nice that your drive-thru drink orders are served to you on a tray to minimize touches.

was here for a meeting and this was closest to my hotel. can't believe it is attached to a beautiful marriott and the service was so poor. food did not look appetizing at all. the 3 women working were not friendly. got a snide laugh from one and an eye roll from the cashier when i checked to make sure i had ordered a grande. then a deadpan "yes it's a grande" with eye roll. the two were so rude. had asked for no foam and it came with foam but wasn't about to take it back to them for fear they would spit in my drink. hire better baristas that are more friendly and accommodating to the customers. will be walking to the farther starbucks as i don't care to go back to this one.

been to this starbucks thousands of times probably in the past 6 years that i've lived in clearwater. it always has friendly staff, they are fast, never mess up orders, mostly clean...what more can you ask for?
best service ever? i've been to a few hundred starbucks from work travels (worldwide) and this one stood out with it's great level of service, clean store and modern furniture. very impressed.

perfect location co-located near the mall near plenty of other shopping areas. service is always great, greeted at the door when walking in and always friendly smiles when taking your order. i ordered the triple (3) shot americana for my morning boost! can't go wrong with their bagels either. i routinely get them toasted, with butter and creamcheese. when not on bagel kick, the banana bread it my jam! :) the parking can be crowded during peek hours, so keep that in mind. as with most all sbux stores, the inside is very clean and offers a great meeting place for business or just to catch up with friends and family. cheers! rob

don't come here unless you are using the drive-thru! i have been here twice in the last 3 days and both times i had to wait over 20 minutes for a toasted bagel! terrible in-store service. coffee also inconsistent in quality and flavor. i observed the staff frantically getting orders out to drive-thru customers while ignoring in-store clients. bad form , bux!

fantastic drive thru experiences! friendly staff make that early morning coffee run that much better. parking lot area can be confusing the first time you navigate it so please be mindful of your surroundings so ya don't spill that latte!

i stopped here june 24 while heading to my gate. the line was super long. when i finally got to the counter, there was only 1 register being used. omg!!!! i figured, ok my caramel waffle cone frappuccino would be worth the wait. not!!! it took a while for them to make it because i believe they didn't know how. it was watery; hardly any flavor. surprised!!!! i never experienced that at a starbucks. very disappointed. i will not go back. if the line wasn't so long i would have returned it; that's how horrible it was.

i walk in they see me, i go pay and then pick my coffee up in two minutes. can't ask for more. one of the guys who is been working there for a while, and is been making my coffee all the time almost, u will be missed buddy. thanks for making my frap always delicious

ummm can't really expect much from airport starbucks but some will surprise you. i think hawaii and cancun annnnd seattle have done a number on me so i'm so picky now lol. got ice green tea latte, no need for syrup or extras but i do ask for light ice and some that notice that will add extra matcha but.... not here. it seemed like i was being a bother when i mentioned the little things i didn't want and want, coconut milk, light ice and no syrup. ( the look was priceless with light ice) i have a venti cup of iced green tea coconut milk flavored latte. i will definitely drink it and just note not to come to this location unless i want a basic all the bells and whistles drink young kids get. something told me to get a grande just in case but i have a 3 hr flight.

in the interest of full disclosure: i'm from seattle, and starbucks was my local place, so they really can do no wrong. this is my "home base" starbuck's. it's like cheers when i walk in, but without the bitter mailman. the line moves quickly and the baristas are consistent and learn your drink (and your name, apparently). one thing i love about this starbucks is that they're always having charity drives- either for the gospel rescue mission, the syann kindness project, or a local animal shelter. they are always giving back locally, even though they're now a worldwide chain. extra props for that.

if anyone knows me they know i love my starbucks...so much so i got over $150 in starbucks gift cards for my birthday...i love starbucks. i take care of my team there too...they know me and they know i'm loyal and i'm a super easy customer. soooo why did i experience the worst customer service of all at the starbucks at lambert airport? main terminal first one you run into on the left hand side...yes i'm talking about you, you know who you are. for some reason they believe that by calling you "mam" it doesn't make them rude as hell. your cashier was obviously new cause she needed help, but got annoyed with me when she had to have help with "my" order and her boss with those long ass extension in her head was no better cause she seemed annoyed to have to help. no "mam" not here that's on the street not in this airport 'mam" with the nastiest tone and demeanor. and then they say thank you cause they have to not because they mean it, followed by "mam"...rude asses need to learn where there tips come from and that's not from calling me "mam" in a condescending tone. if you can't do the job the right way and that means with great customer service hop yo ass over to one of those gates and catch a flight to need to get your life together and land in a different career choice.

wallet was stolen today at this starbucks. the workers there were not helpful at all. they wanted me to "file a report" to corporate and would call if it showed up. meanwhile someone was having fun with my credit card in cherry hill, new jersey. and they made it seem like it was my fault that it was stolen, but it was stolen when i was sitting with my items. not helpful or sympathetic.

yes, i hate to admit it but these baristas here- sure know my order by now. living from 1600 walnut to s. philly, i still come back every night to order my paleo version'd coffee black with a stick of butter. they get it right every time. why wouldn't they? many people look at me like i'm nuts but this starbucks makes me feel at home. my location at best! great workers, in fact i'm friends with a few now! drinks expensive? no way. same price as dd. i'm such a fan of this starbucks!!

i love starbucks. forget what people say about dunkin' donuts--that's bilge water! i don't even drink coffee anymore, but they offer an array of herbal teas iced and hot, and lately i've been treating myself to dark chocolate-covered graham crackers. don't judge. that being said, i must concur with many yelp reviewers in noting that this branch is the most inefficient location i've ever frequented. first, although the line is routinely interminably long, only one register is open. employees appear to be milling about in the background doing who knows what. true, other baristas are busy filling ridiculously complicated drink orders ("ummmm...i'll have a half-caf iced americano with a splash of coconut milk, a smidge of soy, and the tears of the woman behind me in line who's screaming and pulling her hair out."), but still. the other day, two middle-aged women conferred with the cashier for 15 minutes as i waited (uncharacteristically) my turn patiently. were they opening an offshore bank account, transferring money from their hedge fund to checking? no; for the life of me, i have no clue what the heck was happening, despite my earnest attempts at eavesdropping. variations of this scenario are repeated daily, but this is the location closest to me. the workers here are very friendly, and i usually tip, but i take serious umbrage with the extremely poor service. i kind of enjoy getting worked up about stuff, so i'll totally continue to patronize this starbucks, but still...

this starbucks location is so busy (especially in the mornings) as it is the only north valleys location. it is conveniently located right next to a freeway exit and has a drive thru. going through the drive thru is definitely quicker than going inside. i usually mobile order ahead, though. and this nearly always works for me. but when the sticker machine was broken or my drink was wrong, the employees at this location always made things right.
this is the filthiest starbucks i have ever been in. hard to enjoy a cup of coffee when your surroundings are just gross!
best starbucks i have been to yet. excellent layout with lots of natural lighting/windows and the coffee is great. will definitely be going to this location from now on!
does this look like light ice wtf for the price of these drinks we don't need cups full of ice

it's 40 degrees in philadelphia. why do you have the air conditioning on now? is that really what starbucks corporate wants? what a waste of energy. not very good as a corporate citizen. besides the fact that you guys barely recycle (correct me if i'm wrong). i guess you want to freeze us out of your location. what's up with that?

just a regular starbucks. pretty good service and a weird trendy atmosphere. you can watch them make your order which kind of sucks because i see them using wal-mart milk and generic ingredients which beats the purpose of getting expensive coffee!

going through drive-through today. the girl on the microphone was very polite and friendly sounding. there was one car ahead of me. the wait was extremely long. i have no idea why. i sat behind the car at the window, and the car behind me stuck for over 10 minutes. when the person came to the window to give the guy in front of me his order, there were two drinks. there were three girls inside talking, and i could only see one customer. i had to wait about seven minutes for my coffee. i decided to take a picture of the lobby to show there was no one in it, when the manager came up and told me i was not allowed to take pictures. i let her know that i did not take a picture of any people, as well as i would never put someone's picture on yelp without their permission. she seemed quite distressed, maybe she had a feeling that the review was not going to go well... who knows? it seems kind of ridiculous to wait in line for nearly 20 minutes for one coffee. so yeah, i'll give this place another shot sometime and see if it's consistent or not but as for now... might want to avoid this place seeing that the workers prefer to talk over customer service...

the customer service is great and what can i say....starbucks has the best coffee, cake pops aroumd! i love halloween time, my favorite pumpkin spice frap is available. i wish they'd bring back the unicorn frap. i was unable to get the sour sauce in mine when i had it since everyone ran out of it but it was still delicious. the insides cozy and they have two lounge chairs by the door.

great drinks, but the service here is so slow. i have gone inside and through the drive-thru and there's never any sense of urgency from the baristas. i feel like they take their time and don't consider the customers they are serving. it's just a bit frustrating when you're trying to get to work.
great customer service these guys make the best frappes ever shit was bombbbb af on my way home
great people here! they always remember me and what i order. super nice and friendly, every time.

incredibly busy constantly. i came here a few times during a trip to tulane and visited during the week and on a weekend and it seemed like there was always a line out the door. i think because of this the service wasn't the friendliest but i can understand given the sheer volume of orders. the outside patio is really nice and would be a great place to work or study! i just wish there would have been space the few times i was there.
extremely slow service. staff lacks product knowledge. they were also put off several products, and claimed that they could not brew a blonde roast coffee due to the time of day.
uhm no. coffee tasted burnt and chemical laden. i actually dumped it out and have never gone back. support your local coffeehouses!

this place is kind of crap. first of all, the line moves incredibly slowly, in part because they make everyone sign for every transaction, even if it's just for $2. second, they seemed to be out of a lot of things, including basics like light-roast filter coffee, whole milk, and unbleached sugar. it took me 10 minutes to get a cup of drip coffee, even though there were only four people in front of me. there were two people working behind the counter.
we were in the neighborhood & stopped by for coffee. we ended up getting a mocha frappe & vanilla bean frappe they were both excellent!!customer service was great also.

we stopped by here after spending some time at city park which was pretty much a hop, skip, and a jump away. this starbucks is located right over the little bridge in the lakeside area. we came around 4 pm for the meet for macchiatos event, and apparently so did everyone else because they were pretty swamped. anyhow, the service was still prompt, and the barista cat knows how to make a mean frappe. our drinks were served up ice cold, tasty and near perfect. we ordered the coconutmilk mocha macchiato, and the iced cinnamon almondmilk macchiato. i preferred the iced cinnamon almond milk one and i had this made with sf dolce syrup. the coconut milk mocha we added extra splenda and stirred it more and it was better after that. overall our coffee was pretty yummy, especially for the buy 1, share 1 event.
didn't warm my banana bread or my mothers and gave me a half full venti coffee and a lukewarm chai latte.. extremely disappointed. wish i could get my money back.

not bad, not superb. just starbucks. i love that there's a drive-thru but the location is off of the street so it's kind of hard to get here. yesterday morning, the lid of my frappuccino was not properly applied to the cup, so it created a whole mess after i pulled off. not cool baristas, not cool. but i've been here several times before then and had no problem.

starbucks is my favorite holiday stop! i always look forward to their new holiday flavors and it doesn't seem like the holidays without stopping at starbucks to both get one of the holiday flavors as well as look for christmas stocking stuffers.
most unprofessional starbucks i've ever been too. the 5 girls in the back were listening to their own music and not helping people.

i wish they had more seating. this place has a great warm & cozy vibe. i'm pretty certain if they had comfy seats it would be hard to get people (me!) to leave. i'm guessing it caters to the millions of people who work here in the comcast building (which totally makes sense) vs the occasional passerby who wants to sit & relax for a while. otherwise, it's a starbucks. just like any of their other places as far as drinks & food goes.

a starbucks like any other starbucks. this is the one i visit when i work in the mall, and there isn't anything special about it, but there's nothing i have found particularly awful, either. i mostly visit here as oppossed to tim hortons or seond cup because of the my starbucks rewards program. the lineups can get pretty ridiculous, obviously, since it's in a mall. they also tend to have only one person on till, even when it does get crazy, which is a bit annoying, especially if you're going there on your break and don't exactly have 15 minutes to spend standing in line to order and again waiting for that order. but most of the servers i've had have been very friendly and good at their jobs. there are one or two there that i dread getting, but that's the same anywhere. my coffee usually tastes just as i expect it to, and only once or twice have they gotten anything on my order wrong (and they are always happy to fix it). a good option if you're in the mall and want a drink or snack. (though don't hold out hope of them having the new-ish bistro boxes in stock - they're sold out more often than not!)

this starbucks in the tampa international airport is a great sanctuary after being poked, prodded, groped, and wanded by the friendly folks at homeland security. after putting my shoes back on and stuffing my pockets with everything i had in them, grabbing my carry-on bag and shaking off the feeling that a male security guard really likes his job a little too much, this starbucks sits at the end of the line. most people who feel the wrath of a burly homeland security guard seem to head here first. i always get my black coffee and a slice of key lime pound cake ( they don't always have it) or a couple key lime cookies.

so i'm going to throw 4 stars their way. i know there is quite a divided community on yelp about starbucks but here is my reasoning. 1) this location is quick, has decent hours, and the staff are extra-friendly. 2) this place is a quick walk from my office and has a ton of outdoor sunny seating that is perfect for zoning out and catching up on the vitamin d. 3) there is a little more seating than the one up on whyte ave, so i always find a place to sit and do work. 4) top-notch people watching. i've seen some hilarious pick-up-artists, awkward first dates, super-keen "pyramid err umm... triangle scheme" sellers and gorgeous athletes of both genders looking for a pick-up after their run over at strathcona field. 4 good points. 4 stars.

this starbucks takes forever to serve people and get them through the drive thru. 10-15 minutes is the amount of time i'm usually sitting there. needless to say i no longer frequent this location like i used to. it's faster and easier to order on the mobile app and just go to the closest store and walk in and walk right back out.

excellent starbucks with an excellent staff. this is an extremely busy 'bucks that manages to maintain starbucks quality at an incredible pace. order on your starbucks app, park on the far side, and walk in to get your order. it will save you 10-20 minutes of waiting compared to sitting in the drive through line.

every time i have been in this starbucks there's no more than a few people in front of me and at least one table free. i'm sure it's a busy location but i've been fortunate it hasn't been crazy busy. there can be a few others by the milk area but it clears up quickly. i usually get the refresh tea for $2 and like the mint taste. the staff are friendly and efficient. the last time i came, most people were seating on the patio so there was available indoor seating. the locations makes this one of my favourite starbucks. it's close to fun shops to check out and great to stop in for a mid shopping drink. there are benches outside if it's too busy inside. parking lot can get busy but there's residential streets that you can likely find a spot and enjoy a little walk.
everyone is so friendly here and starbucks team is great and friendly free tall lattes cold or hot for the pop up 1-2 today at this location long lines but everyone seems friendly
sure..... i'll wait and wait and wait for my drink. sure...... i'll be fine if you forgot my order. sure i'll be fine having to use my fingers to spread the cream cheese on my bagel. sure

ok, not a whole lot to say about starbucks other than it's starbucks. i go here about three times a week for coffee and i do that because it's consistent. no, i don't order the mocha skinny latte with a shot of anything. i order pikes peak because that's all i want. coffee. and because i only order coffee i find that their price is consistent with other coffee shops. if you do go to starbucks on a regular basis i would highly recommend doing the star points system. after 12 stars (1 star for each purchase) you can choose a free anything. sandwich, custom coffee, dessert. anything. that's kinda neat and makes it a better value.

some of the friendliest baristas i've encountered. i've been to lots of starbucks, but these gals was super nice and very people friendly. if i'm around here again i'll be sure to stop in for my drink and the smiling faces
always good service. with no attitude, unlike the starbucks at garden state park on rt 38.

it's convenient when you have a starbucks on base and you don't have to drive far, but the management aspect is struggle busing. the manager is slow as molasses, i literally just walk out if i see her at the register or making drinks. i will be late for work guaranteed despite getting there 15-20 minutes early. they consistently end up being too watery and...just not good. i will say the most of the baristas are very sweet and accommodating. some are better than others with modifications with drinks, i absolutely adore desirae (i think that's how her name is spelled). she's so bubbly and sweet and is the only one that consistently makes my drinks spot on. so, i'm on cloud 9 with my elixir of life. two stars for me on the quality of the drinks is inconsistent and slow service. not always the case, but it occurs enough to be noticed. however, if you don't do modifications to your drinks, you should be safe to order! it wasn't always this way, just went a little downhill progressively over a 20 month time span.
breakfast sandwich served cold in the middle! disappointing considering the price. after a long line and a high price i expect a warm sandwich.

has a pleasant ambience, a great place to come talk with a friend or do homework. the variety of drinks offered is impressive, there is always something to get for everyone. drinks are made quick and always taste delicious. definitely worth your time to stop in and grab a coffee.

don't go to this starbucks. it sucks. the decor is nice but it's always super packed and the baristas do a very crappy job with your drink. not only that but they don't have some of their advertised drinks in stock -_- i ordered a grande caramel macchiato and i opened the lid because it felt lighter than usual and yes it was not a "grande" probably just a tall in a grande cup...to top it off it looked really gross. go across to the street to beyond bread or anywhere else

i go through this starbucks on occasion when i'm driving through brentwood. every single time they mess up my order today they didn't give us the food we ordered and to re ring me up and pay more .. and the latte tastes like water - this happens absurd single time the lattes are completely watered down and service is shit and slow - never again

it is 8:15am and the store isn't that packed. the parking spaces are very few and people have to park illegally just for a fix of their starbucks. the staff is also few, and sllllooo why starbucks? why?! even when i come at other times of the day, parking is awful. wifi is slow.

this location is always absurdly busy in the mornings. this is the main reason i avoid it - even though it's on my way to work. typically i'll stop off at a local coffee shop on the westbank, or the marrero starbucks, if i need a caffeine fix before work. it would actually better for me if i purchased my coffee on the eastbank, so that i would still have some left upon arriving at work... but here's why it's not going to happen: if you wait in the drive thru, you're probably looking at a 10-15 minute wait on any given weekday morning during rush hour. go inside? add on at least another 5 minutes. i waited 20 mins from the time i walked in the door to the time i got my latte on friday (needed some extra caffeine after muses the night before). it's not that the baristas are slow. they actually crank out a lot of espresso at a fair pace. they're friendly, too, which is why this gets 2 stars instead of 1. if it weren't for the service, this would defintiely be a 1 star experience. i can't see recommending a 10-20 minute wait for the same beverage you can get at any of their other approximately 28196291 locations worldwide. granted, i'm sure the wait time is significantly reduced at any other time during the day... but the only time i ever *want* a $5 coffee is first thing in the morning.

i've been here plenty of times and had mixed service. frankly, i'm sick of it. yeah, it's a starbucks but: 1) when you walk in and nobody is behind the counter, that's bullcrap 2) when you discover the person who is supposed to be behind the counter is talking to somebody in the little lounge area, that's a "it should be ok to shoplift something as reparations" scenario 3) when a second person magically appears to ring you up and asks "do you want something to nibble on?" that's falling into "it should be legal for me to punch you in the face" territory 4) when you order an iced drink for your beautiful wife and it comes out hot, that's "eff them, i'm finally writing a review on yelp." and here we are. service (at least on weekdays) is on par for starbucks found in hotels and airports -- ok product, crappy service. and for the love of everything good, don't eat their food. i've had bars of soap that offered better flavor and texture.
i love the friendly baristas at this location! there isn't a lot of room for socializing here though-- it is a grab and go spot for sure.

1:30 friday feb 8 the worst service ever in any starbucks i have ever been in! staff very friendly, happy but ordered a sandwich and drink. finished sandwich before drink arrived. the drink came after 7 folks after my order and the store does not have a drive up. needs to have trash emptied. i will say over several years i have been in and out of this store but they are a mess today !

i ordered a carmel macchiato with an extra shot of expreso and and extra pump of syrup, when i went up to the window and drove away it was a nasty peppermint drink. i hate peppermint and ended up paying for a crappy drink. i know their busy but come on pay attention to your job.

waited for my drink for about 20 minutes and it was at the cash register, but she did not say my name. had a green tea frappe the other day and it was more milk than green tea. service has gotten worse over the years.
this starbucks is in a great location, but don't be surprised when you go here and wait upwards of 20-30 minutes in a (short) drive thru line. the wait times are always awful!

always busy due to their location in suburban square but this was my first ever starbucks location many moons ago and holds a special place in my heart. staff are always friendly and drinks are fast. i got a triple cap and a cup with a heart over the "i" in kim that made my morning. small selection, even smaller space but a job well done on the drinks
