i don't drink coffee but occasionally visit starbucks for other items. i paid more than six bucks for a strawberry acai lemonade refresher at this location and after a few sips through the straw, i started getting air. it turned out that the glass was about 80% ice (see photo). what a rip-off! i had a better experience when i ordered hot chocolate.

incredibly slow. i always regret it as soon as i pull into the drive-through line or go inside, having forgotten how long this is going to take. when i finally get the coffee it's fine, but the service here leaves a lot to be desired.
hanging with friends at one of my favorite places. you can't beat starbucks because they rock the house and they are always nice when you call about questions.
do not go here on saturdays earlier afternoon because they have only one person making drinks and it took forever!!!! i couldn't believe how long it took to get a frappuccino.

we stopped by this downtown starbucks location early on a sunday morning before heading out of town. the cashier was super friendly and personable, which is always nice. the shop was clean and our orders were quickly served up. there is a drive-thru here if you don't want to park. great visit!

this starbucks really needs help no matter what time of the day i come here i end up waiting ten minutes or more for a drink the only time they were fast was when a couple people were standing with a clipboard watching the person in the window... hopefully they get the help they need one day
the coffee is standard. the employees at this location are overworked and poorly managed.

so as someone who travels the country for work, i've been to at least 100 starbuckseseseses but this one is my new favorite. had a totally minor mishap about which i wasn't pressed at all, but the staff practically jumped through flaming hoops to help me out. and i should know from flaming. side bar one of the fellas working here is the cutest cub in the entire world. like, i would never hit on a service worker because that's a fucked up position to put someone in, and i don't come out to this area ever so i'll probably never see him again... but dude, if you see this, you are as gorgeous as you are kind. :p

it's worth mentioning that the employees at this starbucks location are all fantastic. management has really established a great team of individuals and they all function like a well oiled-machine, getting drinks out quickly while still making customers feel welcome. the drive-thru is often way too long (the only negative)... so i'd recommend going inside and ordering. you'll get your drink faster and the folks inside are great.
every time i come to this location my wait time is always over 10 minutes. if the customer volume is high add more people to your payroll.

this used to be my favorite location , but it seems like management may have changed. the employees don't seem to have that same personable feel like those in the past. i miss seeing the same people i did for years that actually knew me would actually ask how i am and we would laugh and chat while waiting for my drink. now it's pretty much in and out. i know no one nor do i think the people there really will take time to get to know me. otherwise , it's clean and in a good location.
horrible horrible horrible customer service. this location is more worried about pushing customers in and out that they don't care about customer service.

this is my second time coming here ,and the barista ( ash or ashley - i really don't remember the name ) already remembered exactly how i want my drink to be .the staffs here are very friendly , and they always greeting you in with a big smile no matter how busy they are .

hands down the best customer service in the industry right here. my wife and i have been coming to this location for years and always have an incredible experience with the employees when we come in. huge shout out to parker for always being friendly and always making the best drinks! this will always be our coffee spot.

of course starbucks is known for its coffee and amazing blends! however, today was my first time trying the breakfast. i ordered the sausage chicken biscuit and it was certainly a treat. i was shockingly surprised at how amazing this breakfast sandwich was. it was piping hot, sweetness from the biscuit and spicy, from the gravy. this's sandwich melted in my mouth. better than mcdonalds, chic-file, and burger kings sausage biscuit. i would rate this sandwich 5 stars hands down

not my preferred starbucks but it's a quick in and out thing. location sucks personally, high traffic area, small parking lot. sweet staff, quick and on top of things. everything ordered is always made perfect. they always have my daughters cake pops in stock unlike the irvington location.

this starbucks gets my order correct 9 times out of 10 which is great, but, this is by far the slowest starbucks i have ever been to. i've gone to this one fairly regularly since they opened and my experience is always the same. when the drive through is busy you could wait as long as 30 minutes from ordering to actually getting to the window. going inside does not make too much of a difference either. this is not to say that the staff is slow, they are always working and moving as fast as possible. the problem is that they do not have enough staff or kitchen space to keep up with the volume of patrons. my recommendation: put in a mobile order before you leave your house and it might be ready by the time you get there.

i have really bad anxiety in public places and its extremely hard interacting with people and placing orders, the guy working that day was patient and nice and that doesn't happen often so shout out to that guy i wish i would have gotten his name to put on here.

i stopped in here while i had an hour to burn. the cappuccino was pretty good and service was noticeably faster than the service at other local starbucks. there were many people working here, snuggled up with their laptops or phones facilitating employees or scheduling meetings. not a particularly relaxing venue. table area is long and narrow so it's hard to escape the conversations.

i wonder if i was having an off day in my personality, or if the store was having there own off day. i am a huge starbucks fan, but i really wasn't impressed with my one visit to this place. i don't like to be negative to my bucks, but this one and i didn't seem to mix well. didn't get the drink i ordered and staff just seemed to be distant. place was very busy, so they obviously do well by others.

this is my local starbucks and i must give them props because i've been going to this one for years as it was moved recently to a closer place by my work! it is very convenient i will say the parking lot is a war zone and i get scared for my life by all the drivers who ignore stop signs here! other than that there is no better starbucks location in the montgomeryville area! i literally will go out of my way to go to this one as opposed to many others in the are because i know my coffee will be perfect here as i've had bad experiences at other starbucks in this area! please look no further for the best starbucks cup of coffee in the area!

eh merry xmas first off.. it sucks this is the only starbucks in this area. we come to this one when we are in town visiting family. every time they seem to mess up our order. it's really not that hard i have huge issues with stores that can't do and make something so damn simple. so we did a mobile order ... and our drinks were wrong but they were right on our order on the phone(i took a picture of our order). my iced coffee had no classic no vanilla just cream and coffee when tho the little sticker had the right order.. along with my husbands drink he had a cinnamon dolce no whip and literally it was all milk.. i get this store is busy and the only one but still follow simple instructions . it's not that difficult to make a drink when it tells you how they want it made. sucks because we will still have to continue coming to the starbucks every time we come to town. i just hope their staff gets better and knows how to read and listen when it comes to making a drink.

coffee is usual starbucks quality and wifi is ok. but i give it 2 stars because it is, by far, the coldest starbucks ( or for that matter, any coffee shop) i've ever been too. don't know why senior management has dropped the ball here since its been like this for years. perhaps a marketing ploy...buy more coffee or leave because its too cold to stay! note: don't bring a sweater, bring a winter jacket!

love all the employee's here. i always do drive-thru because my dog gets a puppachino! they started this little delight for finn. a few of the girls have taken pic's of him, are always happy to see him, & know his name. service & coffee are always great :)

i'm not a starbucks fan, but if you're in the willow grove mall looking for coffee, this it. baristas were very efficiently working their way through a fairly long line. place is clean, and it's in the "food court" with convenient seating, pleasant lighting under skylights, clean seating area. very strong dark roast, almost with a burnt flavor. i don't drink it, but shockingly they were out of the cold brew, which must be a bestseller at a place like this.

the promise of starbucks drive through is that they get your order right every time. out of my 5-6 visits here so far my order gets messed up twice. this location is logistically great for me to and from work but i think i will go back to quik trip if i have to break for coffee. yesterday there was a mumbler at the drive through. i could barely hear him and he did not confirm anything i was saying to him. of course the drink order is wrong, i asked for soy milk and got regular milk and when i asked him if it was soy milk, i was told i did not tell him that i wanted soy milk. yes, he did offer to make me another drink and asked me to wait in front of the starbucks instead of the drive through line. that's fair. and 10 minutes later i had to walk into the place to ask for my replacement drink. i give up on this place. there are so many other coffee shops in the area with better service. sorry ferguson starbucks, you need to up your customer service game.

bad management makes this usually good chain terrible. over the last six months, every time i have gone to this store they have either (1) been closed when their hours state they should be open or (2) closed while i was there. today i was in line at the drive thru when the cashier told me they wouldn't take my order because they were closing without notice. if this happened once or twice i would let it slide. but when it becomes standard practice it is just unacceptable. i would rather go to dunkin (where the coffee isn't as good) or drive out of my way to go to a place with fewer options and no drive thru than ever go back to this location.
very friendly and nice people working there. the place is clean and busy most of the time, yet not loud.

my usual starbucks is the greyhound pass location which i love, but i used the atm outside of this location and decided to pop in quickly for an iced espresso. my drink is super simple and quick so i'm typically in and out. i walked into this location and waited behind two women who were together. there was a barista making drinks and a cashier. the two women in front of me who were ordering clearly knew/were friends with the cashier. the cashier was letting them sample cold brew, discussing food, going over literally the whole menu, etc. i was never acknowledged as someone waiting to order. the barista kept looking at me like she was feeling my pain or something. i was in a rush and i kid you not - i waited behind these ladies talking to their cashier friend for fifteen entire minutes. i almost walked out several times but didn't have the guts because it would've been so awkward. the ladies kept walking back and forth to the food display and just "couldn't decide". and just chatted away with the chatty kathy cashier. like... i hate stereotypes. especially the carmel one. but wow. this was the first time since moving here that i over and over again thought how much these ladies fit that stereotype. rich older women taking their sweet time with no care about anyone else. it was disappointing. anyway - when it was finally my turn, i expected a "sorry for your wait" comment of some sort. or just some genuine friendliness since she was so overly friendly to her friends. lol nope. the cashier acted annoyed that i killed her friend time. at work. in those fifteen minutes the line behind me drastically grew and there were several waiting. it was just an awkward experience. noooooot a fan.

i think i may have found my favorite starbucks in the city. i had only been in here once before, and the guy named jeremy remembered what i like in my coffee! i wasn't really thinking about what i was saying when i ordered, and instead of 3 raw sugar, i said 1. he asked if i was sure, because yesterday i has 3. i really appreciate the fact that they remember me, even though i had only been there once. good customer service, the employees interacted with their customers.
everything great here and it shows. good crowd on an early sunday afternoon, drive thru was easy, my wife's customized latte perfect and so were the crispy sandwiches. very friendly crew!
always have friendly staff when i'm here. nice environment in the bywater. nice lighting in the building by the way. nice play for a little meeting.
most of these employees make up for the occasional bad experience, they're so nice! always friendly and super complimentary.

starbucks is always going to be a starbucks. i like it enough; i'm a fan of their seasonal espresso drinks and an occasional iced latte. still, i am normally more local-business oriented, but starbucks is the only coffee shop in new orleans that is open when i leave for work at 5:40....so i am there every time i forget to set my coffee pot the night before. however, this location has gotten my drink order wrong far too many times for it to be alright. every other time i am there in the morning, i get in the car and realize that this is not what i wanted. even when my drink order is exactly what i would like, i discover that it is luke-warm at best. if i order extra-hot, i get burnt milk. i understand that it's early in the morning, but there are always other customers and i never order anything needlessly complicated (today it was a skim carmel latte and i discovered a full-fat mocha in my cup once i'd gotten on i-10). that being said, it is a nice coffee shop to go sit at; there's a patio and enough seating if you are going during prime-tulane time. i've never had problem with the pastries. the service is always friendly and awake when i get there. the holiday red cups make me feel warm and fuzzy.

this is by far my favorite location. the staff there is wonderful. they get excited with me when a new coffee comes out. they pay attention to which bag i buy, and then ask me on my next visit for a review. they suggest new coffees to me, and so far haven't let me down with my home brew. they make my latte perfect every day. when i say "thank you" they always respond. many other locations don't pay attention when you talk to them, but not this one, they always engage and respond. the store is always clean too! i come in pretty often so it feels like my neighborhood cheers (i don't drink alcohol but i have a strong coffee habit). there is a group of older people who come in every day and sit at the same table. we chat now while i wait for my coffee. it's just a fantastic environment, with exceptional employees who always have time to talk about coffee with me.
closes early without warning. extremely dark outside. lights are never on outside. logo light is never on when open...very rude staff

rude service. we pay serious $$$ for school here and the service is horrible. the worst starbucks in stl. what a shame. went in a few times and every time it seems like it's an inconvenience for them to make a drink for me. i can't believe they have such poor attitudes. i can't believe that a company like starbucks employs such trashy examples of society.

this is usually my favorite starbucks to go to but today i noticed there were two new faces and while i was ordering through the drive thru i started ordering and as soon as i would start ordering i heard one of the baristas mocking me and making a small a noxious noise and it was really annoying. i stoped talking and it would stop but as a started to talk again the noises continued. i hurried up and just ordered my drink. annoyed at this point i was about to just drive off and not even pay for my drink cause of how rude they were. i pull up and decide to just pay for my drink and i see it's a light skinned black male and a white girl with super long hair in a ponytail and as i'm waiting i hear the guy taking someone's order and the white girl is the one making the annoying sounds i was hearing while ordering. these people are not professional whatsoever. and after i got my drink i looked in through the drive thru window and the white girl gave me the dirtiest look. for no reason! maybe cause i'm cuter than her? haha and also my strawberry refresher tasted like straight water...starbucks please fire these kids

i've always known about this location but didn't come here until a week or so ago. it's easily become one of my favorites. i mainly go to starbucks since it's a good place to study. but because of where i live it can be hard to find one with free parking and enough seating. i love this one since there is always parking and it's free, which is huge. the dining room doesn't have a ton of seating but most people who come here get orders to go so you can pretty much always find a large table. the baristas are super friendly and i've never had a problem with my order. will definitely be a regular!

i really like this place. i don't care about the aesthetics. i'm just getting coffee and waiting for my daughter, for one reason or another.i sat in here off and on for hours ,some months ago, when my daughter was auditioning and kept getting callbacks. eventually, and happily, she got the job. yay!! the staff was as anxious as me as i sat there waiting. they are so friendly and upbeat. i'd rather deal w a floor that needs sweeping and has nice, cordial, funny people than vice versa. and the caramel brûlée was pretty good- though i will have to take out a second mortgage to afford another one. starbucks' prices are too crazy, so this is a pure treat. and one more thing. why is the smallest drink a "tall"? it boggles my little mind!
i love starbucks, but parking at this location is horrendous. i wanted to run in for an iced coffee this morning, but after circling the parking lot several times, i gave up and left.

after waiting several minutes to even be acknowledged, polly tried to charge me double for a tea without ice "because it's like getting two drinks." $7.50-something is what she tried to charge me, but in the app with mobile order it's $3.22. i might have laughed in surprise, explained that i know that's not how that works since i'm a partner at a corporate store, and told her there was no way i was paying for that and walked out. extremely disappointed and will not be returning. i also will be telling anyone who asks not to go there and to go to a real corporate starbucks, not this licensed store. this isn't the first time i've received as service here, but this is the first time it was so bad i walked out empty handed.

on 07/22/15 around 5:00 pm i was there. not busy, my latte took forever, the 2 baristas didn't bother making eye contact, vanilla shaker was empty, a fly and a fruit fly landed on my table and table was wobbly! is this a starbucks?
service was great and my drink is delicious. i order a java chip. probably the best one i've had. the man with the beard working the drive-thru was very friendly. overall great experience.

if you want to be chastised by eve the barista about your clothing, go here. i was having a tough day and stopped in to get a latte pick me up. instead what i got were questions from the barista about how i was dressed and it made me feel dumb. i was embarrassed and it was really uncalled for. starbucks may need to work on their customer service training. i will never go back to this location again.

i place an online order. it was a lunch box, so there wasn't much to do but have it ready. i went there and one of the girls told me your order isn't ready yet, so i told her it's just a lunch box, so she said oooh ok go ahead and grab it. they didn't have the box i ordered, so i chose a different one, and the cashier charged me $3 for it. i was so upset because i used my rewards to pay for the previous box, so i felt deceived and also pay extra for a $6 box. i basically end up paying $9. i am upset not just for the poor customer service but for the extra fee. she acted like if i was trying to steal it. the first girl i understand she was busy making the drinks, so she didn't bother me at all. i mean i can grab a box. however, the cashier displayed such poor customer service. she didn't even attempted to look in the back to make sure they didn't have the other lunch box. she also wasn't even busy. i wasted in line to have her full attention. just upsetting. also, the bread is old, and the box has bacon which i don't eat, that is why i ordered the egg box. $9 to eat half of an apple. great!
this location only fills their drinks up 2/3 the way. this has happened both times i've visited. what a rip off. i'll take the extra min to go to the one on rt 42 where you get a better value.

long wait times and staff is horrible and moody. everyone that works there is slow and always seems to not pay attention to my order, which is usually a cup of hot tea and a petite vanilla scone. (easy but they can't seem to get it right)
very nice baristas extremely friendly and considerate .clean ,always has my special cake
friendly staff but filthy. orders often wrong. few comfy chairs. the best part are the people who camp out in the ladies room.

pissed! i went in today to get a caramel apple spice and a double bacon breakfast sandwich. they were out of the sandwich as it seems they always are, and i received the wrong drink! i got a apple crisp macchiato, and even that had zero apple flavor! i didn't notice until i got home relaxed and ready to enjoy my breakfast. it was a total disappointment.
best starbucks ever!!! love the partners and the drinks are of excellent quality! thanks for a great experience!

this is my favorite starbucks in the westshore area because it is a larger locations and away from the mall crowds. there is outdoor seating, a drive-thru and parking in the back. there are plenty of outlets along the long bench seat and also outlets along the walls. although starbucks is always busy and a little noisy, it is my favorite starbucks to work at because there are a lot of tables.

stopped here and found a lot places to sit! that's about all that's good here. the employees were way too loud. i don't think i've ever been to a starbucks where the employees annoy me more than the people sitting around me. singly loudly and yelling behind the counter. but mind you, this was just my experience on one day of the week. and like i said, there are a lot of places to sit and an area outside as well.

free parking! highly convenient location right in the centre of edmonton's most busy area. reasonable prices and free parking is provided right at the starbucks. my family and i went here and enjoyed the service. the whole outdoor mall seems well managed. you can get groceries, supper, coffee, ice cream and everything else you need in one strip mall.

you would think that starbucks is a starbucks is a starbucks and that since they are a chain, you could pretty much walk into any starbucks and receive a relatively similar experience. that said, i've been to hundreds of starbucks in numerous states and this location is consistently the worst. i've had issues with this starbucks going back to 2007. for years i refused to frequent this particular location even though it was very close to my former office because for some reason their coffee tasted bitter and they frequently messed up my and/or my boss's order. that said, i recently moved temporarily into a rental in the mira loma area as my husband and i go through the home buying process to move back to our neighborhood in damonte and unfortunately this is the only starbucks on my route from my home to the freeway in the morning. i figured that since so many years have passed, certainly they have had ample time to correct any inconsistency issues, but i was oh so wrong. on at least 3 separate occasions in the last few months i either got the wrong coffee or they forgot to put any flavored syrup in the coffee at all, that's fine, i called they fixed it. it didn't really fix my crappy morning without my caffeine fix, but at least they fixed the issue. today i went through the drive through as i do most friday's and lo and behold, once again one of the drinks had no flavored syrup. it was bitter tasting coffee and milk. i tried to call and the first time they answered the phone and then set it down so i could hear the cashier taking orders and the chatter of customers and staff etc. i sat there for a while thinking someone would get on the line, nope, they just wanted to silence the phone, but heaven forbid they speak to the customer who just paid $5 for a cup of crap coffee i could have made better in my own kitchen. i called back twice afterward and they just let the phone ring and ring. from this point on i will either wait until i get to carson city or go out of my way to go to the damonte ranch starbucks who are always consistent. it's not about getting a replacement cup of coffee, i have the funds to pay for another cup of coffee, its the disappointment of waiting in line, paying $5 for a cup of coffee to brighten up my friday mornings and then getting swill and being inconvenienced by having to call and go back. i don't want a replacement, i'd like for your baristas to pay attention to what they are doing and to be consistent. if they have messed up that many times with just my orders, i wonder how many other customers are consistently disappointed by the lack of accuracy and/or poorly made drinks. sorry for ranting, i just wanted to give the back story on why this is so frustrating, but for one location to have continually screwed up for years is just incredibly annoying and i feel that management should be aware that they have the worst location in south reno or any starbucks i've been through throughout the u.s.
i am by no means a fan of starbucks, however the employees working this morning made my visit excellent, hence the 5 stars! they were all very friendly, smiled and seemed genuinely happy!

i absolutely hate going to this starbucks, but i go occasionally because it's on my university campus. it is literally awful. the line is always very long. then, when you get to the register they are always out of everything. the staff is so rude!!! today when i went, they were out of all alternative options of milk, which i am allergic to. they did not have almond, coconut or soy milk. this is a terrible place!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
slow service. be prepared to wait 30 minutes when line is 10+. absolutely ridiculous. employees need training with registers and a kick of urgency.

this starbucks is small but the staff are so friendly and the location is always clean. this starbucks has a far busier drive through so if you are looking for a place to sit down and chat, this is a pretty good option. the location is convenient as it is along one of the main roads that enters and exits the shopping center and i'm definitely a fan of their drinks.

42 people in line and 3 employees. i'm not complaining if it's a $2 coffee. but if i'm paying $7 for my venti iced nonfat upside down caramel macchiato, i expect to have a half decent customer experience. little help here?

my service was fine but there's a pet store next to it causing the lobby to smell like dog. no one seemed to have a problem with the smell but i don't have a dog which made me nauseous to the smell. again service was fine.
love this place! i am a regular here and the staff always so pleasant and kind and really do their best! thank you guys for going above and beyond!!
unfriendly staff. asked a question when i ordered. no response. said thank you, no response. no eye contact. the most hostile starbucks i've ever visited.

this location is poorly operated and the service is always slow! yes, i understand it takes longer to make some of the specialty drinks but the service here is very slow! this morning there was a long line and a woman complaining that her mobile drink was not ready for pick up! again, forget the sensitivity training and train your employees how to actually provide service at a reasonable pace!!!! the 3 star rating is for the decent size parking lot and decent size indoor/outdoor seating! other than that not much else i can say!!
the lady who doesn't brush her hair was being so rude and did not smile , was not polite. made a nasty face because of my ethnic name. rude. definitely will not return.

no lines unless you go during peak hours like 7am-9am. they accept starbucks gift cards which is a plus. when they take your gift card (you can use your phone), they need to enter a bunch of numbers in first and you need to sign a receipt. weird but not a problem.

shtarbucks. guys, i was waiting for my order for some time and had to hurry up with eating because of the flight boarding on my nose. i dropped some food on my pants because of that. i understand some inconveniences might happen in any kind of job. but there were not that many people and still i had to wait more than it usually takes. please take it into the consideration.
big fan. this place was busy. they were fast, so the wait went fast. the employees were friendly. and as busy as they were, the was very clean.

this starbucks is trying it's best. it's drive-thru is probably the busiest drive-thru not connected to a mcdonald's of chik-fil-a in greenwood. they work hard to get your order correct, fresh, and to you and if they are achieving that they are successful in my eyes. they've been of consistent quality as long as i can remember and the pandemic hasn't put changed that from experiences!
max did a great job redoing my drink after coming from another starbucks where i was unhappy with what i got. thank you max!!!

started out my morning with a few minutes to spare so thought i'd grab a chai on way to work. with one cashman truck in front of me ordering i thought "great this will be quick." not!!! the guy took forever to order, then he and a blond flirtatious woman chatted for a good ten minutes while he was very slowly handed just 2 drinks!!! with my extra time shot, plus the recognition the place must not know service i declined my order and had to blow my horn to get the guy to even drive out of the lane! needless to say i won't be back!

they weren't the fastest but weren't the slowest either! . the one on terry parkway is really slow... much slower than this one. my boyfriend says they weren't that slow and i am impatient in the mornings without caffeine lol. he's probably right.
always great service... drinks are always made perfectly. ..and the drive through i'm the morning is super fast. keeps me on time for work!!

this starbucks is located very close to where i live, it's only a block or two off rittenhouse square. the baristas are friendly and they know my coffee order which they give me exactly as i like, very nice! they also have given me advice on how to brew coffee at home with the correct water/coffee ratio. i'll definitely keep coming here, the convenience is great.

this has to be the slowest starbucks in all of reno. regardless if you go inside of the the store or go through the drive thru, expect at least a 15 to 20 minute wait. the staff moves so slow and it seems like they are just taking their sweet time even when they're is about 5-7 of them. they also always make your drink wrong 75% of the time.

i don't usually have coffee at starbucks but when i do, i make sure it's reserve beans on a clover machine. stay thirsty, my friend! (apparently this is the only starbucks in the state with a clover machine. google it and try it, you will be addicted. columbia beans are great and a good value)

my boyfriend and i stopped by this starbucks location on saturday evening. it was pretty busy, but we found a table near the door we could sit at. i ordered a chamomille tea, whie he ordered a passion tea lemonade. we sat down and waited patiently for our drinks to arrive. after over 5 minutes of waiting, i decided to go up to the barista counter to see if our drinks were there. nothing. so i walked back up to the girl who took our order and she said pointed at three paper bags with pastries in them and said my order was there. i was confused but she was busy with a customer. so i waited until she was done with the customer and asked her where our order was. she snapped back that they'd be waiting at the barista counter. so i walked back to the counter again to make sure i didn't miss it and still nothing was there. i was getting annoyed by now. meanwhile, both girls working the barista machine couldn't be bothered to even look up to see why i was walking back and forth looking frustrated. i walked back to the girl at the register and told her our drinks were there nor were they called out. she asked me what we ordered again, rolled her eyes and yelled at another girl behind the counter to make the passion tea lemonade. so i walked back to the barista side and waited for the drinks. the passion tea lemonade wasn't even shaken like it was supposed to be. all she did was pour the pre-made mix from the carton over a glass of ice and roughly put it on the counter. so i took it, annoyed, and brought it over to my boyfriend. no sooner had i sat down that i heard the girl at the register call out that my chamomille tea was ready, so i walked back again, and shehad placed it on the higher ledge (vs. the lower one where the barista girls put them). as i brought it down, i ended up spilling the tea all over my hand since it was so high. i was sooooo mad. why did she put it up there in the first place? i was able to finally put a lid on it and walk back to the table where my boyfriend immediately asked me what happened. he felt so bad he used his cold iced drink to relieve the pain and throbbing from my hand. this incidence was the second starbucks in the city i've had horrible service from. i am beyond done with this chain. their sister chain, teavana is way better in terms of customer service and product quality. i will never visit this store again,

been here a few times in the past. the store seems clean and organized. i feel like they are short staffed but the staff that is there put alot of effort into their job. today, rachel is fast and efficient at taking orders, always pleasant and starbucks is lucky to have her as an employee.

this was my favorite go to starbucks when stephanie managed it. when she left to open her own business it went down hill. the garbage cans are always full. my drink is usually wrong and the staff just isn't in sync. i hear the staff complaining about something most of the time, be it personal or about work. doesn't make for a very positive experience. the drive thru is slower than molasses in november and most of the time, after that long wait, the drink is wrong. the store is not neat and tidy any longer and just lacks that warmth it had when stephanie was manager. sorry to give such a negative review but, unfortunately after having tried to stick it out through the transition of management, i just don't go there except for ground coffee and only if i'm i'm the area. very sad to see this store go down hill.

brand new. clean as can be. updated and modern looking. excellent service and coffee drinks. probably the fastest i've ever seen my drinks made and drive thru line move. and one fancy drive thru order board to confirm your order. not to mention a speaker that sounds like the person taking order is standing at your window, unlike the muffled and crackling experience you get at most drive thrus. this specific starbucks just became my go to spot. and super convenient located on 301 and a short jump over to the i-75 on ramp means to can get any direction fast after your espresso fuel up!

pros: this starbucks is clean, has lots of tables, huge outdoor area, fast and friendly service. cons: as with all starbucks, it's freezing, and it could use more power outlets. very good place to have meetings or study all day.

ok, so this may be a real shocker... but just in case you weren't officially aware; my name is christy and i love sbux. now that the formalities are out of the way...i'm sure we can agree that all locations are not created equal. especially this new ferguson jewel. totally in a class of it's own. plenty of seating options from high tops to a private meeting area equipped with floor to ceiling industrial style pocket doors. there is also faux leather and patio seating perfect for close-quarter intimate conversations over a nice hot cup of joe. i want all locations to mimic this one. i seriously haven't been as excited as i was when i experienced their virtual drive-thru ordering system since i don't know when! this sbux is a breath of fresh air for ferguson and i'm ecstatic to see it here. ~christy

some people may hate chain retail coffee shops, but this is as good as it gets in arnold! i particularly enjoy starbucks because the service is almost always enjoyable and there is free wi-fi in the place. there are a lot of nice cozy chairs and i sometimes manage to plop here with a good book instead of the local library. (the library does tend to be a bit loud, but that is ok.) i can order a variety of drinks that can be syled according to my preferences. i always order the skinny version of an iced caramel machiatto. i always get my drink pretty quickly. i agree with other reviewers, they do need to add some more parking spots, but this is not a huge negative, because the store had to do with what was given to them.

best starbucks around!! even if im at giant down the street that has starbucks inside, i make the effort to stop here instead because the staff is amazing and makes the best coffee! i come here all the time and they all know my name, my favorite drink, and even know if i want decaf/caf depending on what time i come in! they always have bright smiles on their faces, and ask about my day, i can say a few of them are practically my friends i talk to them so much when im there. they will always make the drink exactly how you like and are happy to fix it if not to your liking. best baristas around! that is for sure!

not sure what has happened at this location. twice this week they have run out of cinnamon. kind of a first world problem, i suppose, but when paying starbucks prices, strange that manager can't go over to albertsons and get some cinnamon if they forgot how to otherwise order. that and morning service has slowed to a crawl. went to broadway as alternative and good latte but lid was covered in servers perfume. grrr. messing with a guys caffeine fix.

why does the worst starbucks in the city also have to be the one closest to my job? in general i don't think the issues of this place are directly related to the staff. they are always friendly when i'm there. i think the problem is that this place is always busy because it's in the marriott and there never seems to be enough staff. it doesn't matter if there's only 2 people in line or 30, i always seem to wait at least 15 minutes for my iced coffee. yet, i keep coming back thanks to my relentless caffeine addiction.
always friendly, fast service and never a problem with my order - even though there's regularly a line for the drive-through. it's the one starbucks location i know a line shouldn't mean a long wait!
clean and efficient starbucks. service was quick, friendly staff. nice large area to sit and work or just to have some coffee or tea and hang out with friends.
it is starbucks. coffee is just okay but we don't have many other options in town for a cup of caffeine. five stars for this location because the workers are always super friendly.

worst starbucks location i've been to so far. i tried it 3 days in a row. different people working each day. each day the people here seemed borderline hostile. the coffee was awful on top of it and not what i typically get at a starbucks. next time if i have a choice between this place and a plus, i'll go to a plus.

a lovely place to kill some time and listen to high character folk music, perfect for our situation yesterday, waiting for a flight. a nice little starbucks, well equipped with power and wifi, a little slice o heaven.

this location is good if you need to go somewhere on campus with work space and outlets, and that's about it. the good thing about starbucks generally is that it is so standardized you know exactly what you're getting every time. not so with this location- the bar is a mess, the milk area is often out of supplies, and the baristas are tired of dealing with students. this location is right next to a dorm so the ransacked supplies are likely due to that. it is clear the baristas don't enjoy dealing with the students but wind up delivering terrible customer service to everyone as a consequence. having worked in a dorm when i was younger i completely understand how old serving spoiled kids their dry cappuccinos gets, but the complete lack of acknowledgement when you approach the register is unfortunate. overall: decent place to work but bring your coffee.

did a mobile order. got there a few minutes after they were supposed to be ready. the drinks were, but we waited 12 minutes for our breakfast sandwiches before checking on them. the worker was rude and said "it will be over there when it's ready'. we waited 5 more minutes, then had to cancel because we had to leave. so...15-20 minutes after we got our drinks i told the girl to cancel. she actually looked up the order and said she "didn't get it". umm...it was a mobile order and our drinks were there. had to leave, no apology from staff, no breakfast. paid for two sandwiches but didn't get them. do not go here!!! i have never had a bad experience like this at starbucks!
i go to this location on a regular basis, the staff is consistently friendly and helpful! definitely recommend this location

awful parking and one of the entrances is one way only. really difficult access. a few years ago they changed all the seating and instead of comfy chairs and small tables they went to a large center table with community seating (e.g. no privacy) and hard bench seating. maybe that's purposeful to get people to not stay as long.

worse customer service !! this morning i went into the langhorne, pa starbucks and was treated very badly. as i stood there where for someone to ask for my order one of the workers walked pass me behind the counter to go wipe down the tables. i continued to stand there waiting for someone to take my order. while standing there another customer came in and we both stood there waiting. then the girl wiping the tables walked back around the counter and asked for my order. i could not believe it!!!! while i was standing there she was wiping down tables and when i said something she said"i had to wipe down the tables". she didn't say sorry or i apologize nothing. i think she should be fired. i was so happy to see this starbucks open but it is horrible!!!! starbucks suppose to be more than a fast food coffee shop. i thought they card about customer service and their customers. i guess only the starbucks in certain neighborhoods get the employees who care about their customers. i am so upset by this. i called the district manager so we will see what happens if he cares. if you just want cheap fast food service go to the langhorne, pa starbucks but if you want good service and coffee go to yardley or even the giant supermarket starbucks in langhorne, pa or lawrenceville, nj.

super friendly staff and being from philly i definitely love and appreciate that...but when you make my drink wrong and try to justify it, that just irritates me to no end. vanessa clearly needs more training or to be pulled off the morning crew

what a great location! i recently started working in the area and have been through here every day for a week and am never disappointed. my coffee is always perfect and everyone is super friendly :) oh and if you play pokémon, you can battle on the gym located right next to the drive thru while you wait!

i come to this at starbucks drive thru pretty religiously since it's close to work and they have the friendliest baristas. i usually see the same guy and thought man he's really friendly, but then i've had a few other people mixed in where i drove away delighted at how friendly and outgoing they are.

tried to stop in for some iced coffee before my ride back home because according to the sign on the door it says closes at 9 but it actually closes at 8:30pm. this is why it pays to check yelp beforehand.

i enjoy starbucks and this location. it's right in the middle of town and you can sit outside and enjoy your coffee and the weather. my only complaint is the long wait and it's a bit dirty. the tables and chairs always have some kind of spill marks and crumbs and the bathrooms are just filthy. i'm sure if they fixed those things up it would be much more appealing. getting my grande white chocolate mocha now!

i really like this starbucks location. it's definitely a neighborhood spot for east franklin. if you love nearby it's quite likely you'll run into a friend. typically it's easy to get in and out within 5 minutes. it does get very busy during the early morning hours. it's a great location to meet a friend or colleague unless they are full which happens more often than i'd like. this is a criticism i have have most starbucks. there's never enough seating. the staff is always very friendly and courteous. if you like starbucks coffee, i would recommend this location.

this is a pretty typical starbucks. their service is always better than their coffee in my opinion. i come in because they have a consistently average coffee and i know i'll be able to get wifi to get some work done. lot of people here with the same idea as far as laptops and getting work done. my pour over took about 10+ min. which they apologized for bringing out late. music is always a tad loud in my opinion at all starbucks so don't plan on taking any phone calls here with any semblance of quiet in the background. to make up for the long wait, i was provided $4 gift card. many thanks for that. like i said earlier, service (5 stars) is better than their coffee (3 stars.)
very slow service in drive-thru between 8:45 -9:00 am. i'm been waiting 15min already
the staff is always friendly and helpful. even though my order is pretty simple people don't always get it how i like it all the time ...today it was perfect which so made my day!

disappointed in this sb. took a long time to get drinks, even with what appears to be sufficient baristas behind the counter. and dirty with trash cans overflowing and tables dirty. it's mid afternoon on a weekend that is slow period. disappointed in this sb.

this starbucks is great! the employees are so friendly and attentive and very competent. you get your drink fast and perfect every time. maddie, stephen, drew, stacy, and brad are the best!! this store is a neighborhood staple. we have been waiting for it forever. yay!
finally: a starbucks that does everything right. i'm so used to sub-par starbucks places messing up my order. they were nice & everything was extra good.

my favorite starbucks. friendly baristas. my drinks are always well made and staff makes an attempt to know you're name and make friendly conversation. have experienced the "pay it forward" drive thru train a couple of times here which leads me to believe we have some kind customers and neighbors in this area. i love that even when the line looks crazy it never takes long.
been here before quiet friendly service many tables clean breakfast sandwich plus coffee = happiness

one of the lady was wonderful! seem like she says "you can make it anyway you want"...something like that phrase, customizing the drinks. i was having a disappointing day, but she brighten it back up. =d . one person can change a bumming day to a great day.

so i've come here on several occasions and they always seem to be "out" of something. so this time i'm here and i go to order a cloud macchiato and the girl ringing me up says they're out of the cloud powder that they make the drink with. so i said to her, "it seems like you guys are always out of something." so then she proceeds to tell me that all the starbucks in brandon are out. i knew that was absolute bs the minute she said it bc how would she know that? did you call all the stores to find that out? no. i didn't appreciate being lied to, especially since i went to the starbucks in the mall minutes later and they made it for me. sad.

this place probably has the worst customer service you will ever see. i ordered 2 drinks, expecting to get them together. instead i waited 15 minutes for my first drink, and never got my second one. i complained twice, and was not listening too, the employees repeatedly ignored my several requests. is was not until i raised my voice and made a scene, then i got my second drink. they have crappy service, and i am certian that i will never order there again.

really a no star rating. ordered an iced green tea latte and was made a green tea frap. when i asked to get the drink i really ordered, the barista made sure to let me know how irritated she was with me. seriously! it's not my fault the individual at the register is incompetent and i had to step away to the restroom while you blended my drink. i didn't even ask for a refund of the price difference, but was still treated like i personally attacked her. next time the amazing well coffee place is closed i will bypass getting coffee in fishers.

this was my go-to starbucks. this morning i got a rude barista who chastised me very publicly for trying to get my own drink carrier. i assume it's a policy that when you order multiple drinks on a mobile order, you should be expected to wait in line and try to flag down a busy barista to get a drink carrier so that you can actually carry your drinks out of the store. you know, waiting in line to get a drink carrier almost defeats the idea of putting in a mobile order, doesn't it? i get the idea of not wasting drink carriers, but common sense and a little customer service would be appreciated. in any event, there are lots of starbucks and other coffee shops around town where you can get better customer service and you don't get a side of attitude with the $5 cup of coffee. i'll gladly go there instead.
lots of room to work. not very busy at 8:54 am. friendly staff. loved my first dirty chai!

starbucks has the worst coffee ever! seriously, why would anyone want to waste their time and money on a place like starbucks? their coffee tastes nothing like coffee. don't bother going there for a cup. bob evans has better coffee than this place.

this is the single handed slowest starbucks location i've ever been to. i've come here multiple times and always wait 20 min plus. i've tried different times of day, the drive through, going inside, etc. i hate to leave a negative review because i am not that kind of person. i am very patient. but from a business standing point of view... something absolutely needs to change here. i pulled up to the drive through at 1030 and then got half way by 10:55. i was just about to round the corner into the part of the drive through that i would not be able to leave from . after 30 min of sitting i looked at the clock and realized the 8 cars in front of me would take at least another 15. so i left without anything. this area is busy yes. but i have 15 years of bartending and barista experience and this entire staff moves very slow. they are in need of some all star baristas added to their team for a reformation of speed and multi tasking. sorry for the negative review, but something needs to change at this place as it is beyond making excuses for at this point.

this starbucks is convenient and like most of the other establishments delivers a similar experience. i find it kind of funny that it's right next door to a dunkin donuts, but maybe that's a benefit for those of us who don't want to pay $6 for a scone made from organic power berries harvested from the heart of the jungles of expensivecoffeebeanland.

quick service. very clean. i ordered a blonde cappuccino, and after the foam, i had less then half a drink, that's was really disappointing. flavor was good, just wish i could have had a full cup for the price of starbucks drinks

great staff and ultra lively atmosphere. always crowded with nice people on the weekends. plus, it's in the perfect spot so i always have a reason to stop in for my favorite beverage. my favorite coffee place in tucson by far.
major shout out to joe (barista). always an inviting smile and welcoming attitude. i pass up 2 other locations just to get my drink made here because i know it will be done carefully and correctly.
do not go to this harrison location. they give you drinks that are all ice and when you bring it to their attention they just tell you...."everything is measured." i payed $5 for ice.
tried the cristal ball frappuccino. there were sugar on the cream. fruit flavor? quite familiar.

service has gone through the floor here, and to be fare most locations have as well. 20-30 min waits are not uncommon at busy times. wrong orders happen more then they should. i've gone from going daily to maybe once a month. and may not bother anymore.

after spin class, nine of us went to starbuck's first ever evening hours wine night. the weather was perfect; we enjoyed sitting outside . everyone ordered wine. the service was a little slow, maybe because this was the first day to serve wine? the staff was friendly and helpful. a pleasant lady brought our drinks to us and asked if we needed anything else. later she came back and cleared our dirty dishes. the wine is served in glasses. nice. and much to our surprise, a snack of seasoned pumpkin seeds was included. everyone seemed pleased about that. the wine was a good quality, mine was delicious. i heard several comments about how good it was. however, everyone seemed to agree that the pour was extremely small. and for the price- seemed a little chintzy. starbuck's, please reconsider the pour size and make it comparable to the wine quality and the price. do that and i will come back with a five star rating! we were all excited to know you are now having wine night.

my favorite starbucks! almost wish they could have a drive thru:/ and need a little more staffing in the mornings! otherwise super personal and friendly! they know the names of all their guests and are super friendly!t

i just moved to the area, and i've gone to this location twice. both of the times i went there i waited for 25 minutes in the drive-through when there was only five cars in front of me both times. i don't understand why they are so slow here at this location, i've never experienced service like this at any other starbucks location.

even though i like coffee from other places more, preferably a non-franchise place, coffee is coffee. they have new items on the menu including choices of vanilla blonde. the barista made vanilla blonde latte with soy milk and it was delish!

i love each and every starbucks, and this one i can remember pretty well. the inside isn't as big as preferred, it's actually to small i would say. this could easily be a problem for some study groups or students. they did get my order right and every drink received from them tasted amazing i should add. i will be going back because this location is very close to my house, but i wish the store was bigger.
my go to starbucks in tampa! the staff is always friendly and accommodating even when it gets overwhelmingly busy.

on my travels on canal street i'd pass the downtown sheraton starbuck's sign. today, i gave it a try. it is located at the canal street entrance. i came at a busy time. they were fast and too busy to be friendly. i had a choice of plush chairs, tables, and bar chairs overlooking canal street with the marriott. i chose the bar chairs. i got plenty of sunlight, which probably kept my coffee warmer, and did some great people watching.

i know you never expect good service in an airport, but this was terrible! the staff were unprofessional arguing with each other and then talking poorly about the customer in front of us while she was standing right there.

this starbucks is just okay. there isn't really anything special about it. sometimes they mess up my orders here when i do the online mobile ordering. they have added the wrong milk to my coffee and also gave me the wrong food once. everytime i order in store my order doesn't come out wrong only when i order on the app. there is always a huge line here hence why i do the online ordering. sometimes the staff here is in a good mood but other times they aren't. you can tell by the way they are talking to the customers if they are or not. overall i will continue to come to this starbucks because its convent for me but i hope that their customer service improves and they stop messing up my order.

traveling to see my husband who started a new position locally, i found myself at this starbucks. i currently have a special mug that allows for free coffee for the month and upon presenting this mug, the barista at the register offered a new type of brewing called clover. i agreed to try it. she was very informative and helpful. a true asset. then while seated at the bar awaiting my new drink, another barista, royce, gave me some interesting insight into starbucks,blends and home brew. he was funny and very helpful. even during an obviously busy time, he made my visit quite enjoyable. the icing on the cake, the coffee was amazing! well done!
this place is a homeless shelter, just happy my local starbucks is not like this or i would never go again.
convenient location, however, there are always flies swarming in the pastry display case and around the store. does not make for a very appetizing experience.

so tired of walking into places and people bring their dogs which are obviously not service animals. why can't people do the right thing? and why can't starbucks have the nuts to tell people to take their dogs outside?

i visited this location today to do a little homework and i had a great experience. i ordered a breakfast sammy and latte (which was made perfectly) and sat down to do school work. (i wish i remembered the girls name who waited on me) the staff was efficient, working hard and at the same time having fun. i think have an establishment where your team members work hard but are also able to enjoy their time there is important. the work place morale seemed to be positive, which means that management is doing a great job. the positive energy really resonated with me, and it makes me want to come back and be a regular here. for my second round of coffee i was waited/served by i think jordan and larry. they both had smiles on their face and were truly the epitome of great customer service.

one photo is from the starbucks on jefferson and walnut. the photo with the holiday cup is at loughborough commons! starbucks baristas are the best in customer service! they deserve more tips as they provide a needed service for a lot of us. tip: order hot drinks including lattes, short, or tall for the best flavor and longer-lasting heat! tip: frappuccinos have the best flavor as a grande! tip: order short brewed coffee or tea in the app. (get 2 stars per dollar) to build up your stars daily

came here late on the drive home from norcal. employees were friendly and our drinks were made perfectly to order. line went quickly and drinks were made quickly. granted it was pretty late at night however some starbucks are slow even with a short line but these baristas made our drinks quickly even though the line was short. great job!

it's interesting to see some rough reviews on here but i just went for the first time and enjoyed it! the woman working the register was very friendly in an auntie or mom kind of way, saying "you're welcome, baby" and stuff. that made me feel a bit more at home. i ordered the sumatra but they ran out halfway through the the cup. she asked if i wanted to get topped off with something else and i said the veranda, a bit sad since i couldn't get a full cup of what i wanted. then she asked if i just wanted all veranda, i said yes and she dumped out that sumatra and told me that the next time i come she'll give me a free cup. so, yay!

this is a very good starbucks. the parking is not the best in the world but it is located right next to the interstate and walmart. the staff here is always extremely friendly and the drinks are consistent with all other starbucks locations.
always have friendly and fast service! the drive thru is convenient during the cold months when you don't want to leave your car.
in line right now for drive thru. it's going to clear 30 minutes fir a coffee. going to be late to work. crazy.....

came to the same location at a different time and the experience was 100% better! patrick, the barista, was super helpful and top notch customer service. i got a vanilla roobois, which is really good and even better with a little bit of milk.
always happy with the coffee and service here! the staff is friendly and my wait has always been really short.

got a strawberry frappuccino. delicious as always. i also found the staff to be friendly to my kids. it gets kind of crowded in this starbucks, but it wasn't too bad this evening. great place for. usi was meetings over coffee or tea. especially if you can sit outside.

went here yesterday for a pink drink. very good service and no wait time or line. parking was easy to find but the space is a bit tight but overall good experience and friendly and very helpful staff.
a good convenient place for coffee but workers there are not clean at all. trash was strewn on the floor for the entire day, no one cared. i went there on the eve of christmas.

usually come there often, but service was beyond rude didn't even greet me, be honest didn't recognize the girl serving too, spend most of the time talking to coworker so just left. time is money and if i'm not getting proper service definitely not giving my money.

stopped in to get a coffee after a canceled meeting. figured i'd grab a gift card while i was there. ordered a dark roast coffee and paid for my gift card. stood and waited, and waited, and waited. it's a dark roast coffee that's already brewed. nothing needed to be made. watched over 20 people get their orders. could not even get any staff's attention to let them know. left an unhappy customer and will not be going back. another negative starbucks experience

starbucks seems out of place in an academic library. and there is nowhere to sit, the drinks are on par with any other starbucks - but with two tim hortons just outside the library on the same floor, this seems redundant.

i really like that this starbucks opened in richmond heights to give me an option not to have to fight the parking lot situation at the brentwood location. however it's been open for quite a while now and there seem to still be quite a few kinks. one time they forgot to make my food and i had to interrupt them talking behind the counter to ask for it since i had been waiting awhile. another time one employee was working the window and also training someone at the bar who was taking a long time to make my drink by stopping after each step to wait for more instruction. i waited 10 minutes for an iced drink and it wasn't correct. this past time i was in the drive thru with a lady in front of me that they couldn't answer her questions so someone else had to get on and answer them. then she sent her drink back at the window that they had to remake. after all that wait they told me the food i ordered was out and i had to wait again for something else. this location is gorgeous inside and convenient so i will keep giving them a chance but i hope their training picks up soon and they get more efficient.

all i have to say is yum!! starbucks is delicious. their coffee is warm,tasty, and how coffee should be. their passion tea lemonade is sweet and very good. i also enjoy their pumpkin and banana walnut slices of bread. the location is great. i'm fond of starbucks.

great drinks like any starbucks and great customer service, but the bugs flying around are so annoying!!! i can't study/do work in the store, i have to sit outside, because the gnats/flies are so present.

the two star is solely for my drink not starbucks as a whole: i ordered the iced guava white tea lemonade and i got a strawberry tasting drink that reflected more of a pink lemonade taste and zero guava taste. such a shame that nothing out of this drink screamed guava to me. good customer service by staff. starbucks need to omit this drink out of their menu unless they find a real guava tasting syrup or juice to make this drink a reality.
waited 15 minutes in the drive thru. got a smores and a waffle cone. they tasted nothing like they were supposed too and by the point in time we got them we just wanted to throw them away.

great "neighbourhood" starbucks. some of the sb shops have become kind of cold and sterile. this one located in north st. pete is warm and welcoming, cozy with a neighbourhood coffee shop "feeling" as the original owners intended.

so this is the starbucks in tampa's f terminal, which happens to be a starbucks reserve! they have the reserve clover-brewed coffee. ultimately it was a little disappointing because i mistakenly thought all reserve locations also had the nitro cold brew. i didn't snap a picture but it seems like they've also significantly downsized from their renovation a year ago? there wasn't any starbucks-specific seating and i felt squished waiting in line for my drink. other than that it was on-par with any other location.

this evening i had a splendid time networking with like minded individuals on my bumblebizz meeting. we enjoyed in depth conversations on current events and our campus culture. it's so nice being able to sit outside at the starbucks on the university of tampa campus!

the drive through is incredibly too long every time i'm here.. this time i was in line 30 min. it's just coffee what takes so long? normally it takes anywhere from 15-20 min in line and that's a good day....

let's face it: starbucks are like walgreen's - one on just about every corner in town. for the coffee lover, that's a good thing because you know you are never far from a hot cup of java. the thing that sets this particular starbucks apart is the congenial, helpful staff. often i stop in not for coffee but because i'm having a bit of a down day and need a lift in spirit. with very few exceptions, the baristas here always seem to enjoy helping their customers...and that's something you don't see every day! i know that no matter what the situation or time of day i can count on sandy, shelly, emily, heidy, patrick and the rest of the staff to not only put a smile on my face and a tasty drink in my hand, but to make me feel valued as a customer and a friend. a tip: for value and the best tasting cup of coffee you can get at starbucks, treat yourself to a french press. to me, that is the only way to discover the true flavor of the many coffees available. be sure and ask your barista about food pairings to go with your coffee too. it is amazing the flavors you can experience when food and coffee are properly matched. now for the drawbacks. my past couple of visits here i've noticed a disturbing trend: one of the managers (at least he acts like a manager...i can't be positive) has a bad habit of talking down to the rest of the staff and tends to be a bit of a tool. from some of the comments i've heard it seems like he may have some unresolved anger issues. (if he weren't of above-average stature, i'd say it was a napoleon complex.) not sure what his deal is but it makes me wonder about what kind of people skills starbucks requires managerial candidates to have. while it hasn't been enough (yet) to make me not return, it's getting really close.

i'm not usually a starbucks guy. i wanted a coffee and was on the way to visit friends; if i showed up with one coffee, i'd have to put up with sad puppy dog eyes, so i stopped at the local starbucks. there really aren't that many other options in the suburbs of west edmonton. i actually think the coffee and the espresso are pretty average and certainly not worth what they charge for it. but, this stuff is way popular and well branded. i mean walk into a real italian café and try that tall, grande, vente thing and watch the retarded child look you're going to get. only tall means something to a european barista and it ain't what you get a starbucks. a "tall" coffee is a single expresso made with twice the normal amount of milk as a "short". it's not a "size"; it's more volume because you're asking for more milk versus the more common "short". similarly at a bar, you can ask for your highball in a "short" or "tall" glass; same drink, more mix. if they get that much wrong, how good can the coffee really be? the really annoying thing is that this location has a drive-thru window. who's brainwave was that? they have customers both inside and outside and one barista. so, guess what? there is a long suffering line in front of the counter and one snaking around the building. additionally, you have no real sense of where you are in line or when you can reasonably expect your order to come up. the staff look stressed out; the bartista definitely is and there isn't a customer with a smile on their face for a good thirty paces. oh, what a good time.
highly disappointed it's 38 outside and this guy is telling me they don't make medicine balls and people have colds and it's one of the coldest days in history.wow i'm not coming back here .

i am truly disappointed in this starbucks location. i have only been here a few times, but today was my last. first off, they didn't have any of the sandwiches that i wanted to order, so i had to settle for something else. then the cashier definitely got my order wrong... i asked for two of the sandwiches we settled on and two waters. i got one of each. i didn't have time to stand around and correct the issue after i realized what happened because we were on our way to church and they move pretty slow at this location. overall, the customer service was mediocre at best. there are just too many other options to tolerate this level of disappointment.

been going to this starbucks for quite some time now. i order the same drink each time and i don't find the drink that i order to be that difficult to understand/make (green tea lemonade no classic syrup). without a doubt each time i go my drink is always made wrong whether it's not the right size cup i ordered or half the time they don't add the lemonade when i specify it 3-4 times prior to paying. i think starbucks needs to hire more attentive people than what they currently have on staff at this location. ridiculous.

i love this starbucks so much! i go every weekday morning and sometimes on my lunches as well. i always use the mobile ordering because it is so convenient. my drink/food is always perfectly prepared for me and ready as soon as i get there. the staff are all super friendly and they are super efficient. i used to wait in line before i got the app and they would just pump through the line. you don't have to wait 5+ minutes here just to put your order in and i love that. this is definitely my favourite one in the city!

mistake stopping here! never check reviews before stopping at a starbucks...but i will now! die hard starbucks fans on vacation, stopped on a sunday before noon. should have realized that the flies and i mean a lot of flies inside (um can you say "dump") was a bad sign. one person in line who took forever to order her tall iced coffee. ordering was easy - passion ice tea and a decaf iced with cream and pumpkin. simple drinks. tea came out in decent time...coffee try 10 minutes (mind you there was a drive thru, but two baristas running drinks). didn't tell us they had no ice decaf and only because we were watching and overheard convo where they were like just make a latte...usually they sub decaf americano in any other cafe...and they let you know. one person starts it after we correct them and then it sits while they go do other things...a second person picks it up and completes, not before squeezing caramel on top! we question what's on top and while she was nice, after we said we didn't order that way she said "do you want it with it?" - ah we just told you we didn't order it that way! took another 5 minutes to get it right...meanwhile a father and daughter enter and loudly say "look no line for once!" clearly there is an issue at this location.

this is a nice big clean starbucks on canal around a lot of busy hotels. there was only one person in line in front of me. the girl started ringing me up, stopped in the middle and got someone else since she was going on break. that person started ringing me up then stopped halfway and said someone else needed to train on the register. during this time two people behind me were rang up on the other register which was frustrating since i had my toddler with me and needed to get in and get out. i finally got my drink and it wasn't very good. tasted like their shots are burnt and there wasn't enough flavoring. definitely didn't stop by any more on the rest of my trip.

worst starbucks experience i've had. i was the only person in line and wasn't acknowledged for quite a while. till person was sub par, and made me feel weird. bar person had a headset on the entire time - which i would understand if she was talking to drive through customers - but i could tell quickly that she was talking to her coworker through it (the till girl). she made no contact with me. i've worked for sbux before and this was the worst experience i've had.
worst starbucks store, ever. if you want to wait for a cup of coffee for 15 minutes and not able to enjoy, please stop by.!!!!!

i usually come here on a coffee run for my family while they are at work, so i usually have a large order. i have never had any trouble with my order. it has always been right. i've only had 'burnt' coffee once and that wasn't even with the big orders. everyone is super friendly. i love this starbucks over the others in the area. they're nice, they get my order right, and they get it to me quickly.
this newly remodeled starbucks is great. the barista's are always very pleasant and the coffee taste great. it's kind of expensive but it's starbucks right.

worst one in tucson, went once had horrible experience, tried again a year later, still went bad, asked for a no water extra ice green tea no sweetener in a venti cup.....instead i got a black tea in a grande that was overly sweet.... don't hire kids, there stupid....

i have to update my review based on the experience i had on a double star thursday. obviously it was a busy morning which is why i placed my order with the mobile app. i arrived 5 minutes after the eta i was provided. there were at least 7-8 baristas running around. i stood right in front of the mobile order plastic window protector for about 10 minutes without being acknowledged by anyone. then another lady came around me and one barista finally came over to ask her first if she's been helped. i highly doubt you didn't see my broad shoulders in my large black hoodie sis. i just don't know what it is. sometimes this location provides outstanding service and then other times you get barely mediocre service. at least my eggnogg latte and roasted ham and swiss sandwhich tasted good. i asked for 3 shots of espresso in my eggnogg latte but it didn't taste extra charged like i was hoping.

this is an awful starbucks. it's consistently terrible service with rude employees and the drinks are never correct. i was in there today and they didn't have any blonde espresso. really? i also asked for whipped cream and when i got my drink there was no whipped cream. so, i sent the drink back asking for whipped cream. the barista said "well, it does not say that on the cup". and i was like i know, that's why am telling you now. because they weren't listening to me up there. and you can lose the attitude. why?!! why are all of the employees here so rude? why can't they also just get a drink correct? the line is also always way too long for the drive-through. it's pretty bad when there is a starbucks inside the target next-door and the employees there tell me they have people bringing drinks to them to be fixed that they got from this starbucks.

good, friendly service. quiet with ample seating, although i would have preferred some nicer and more comfortable leather seats. not a bad starbucks. my iced white mocha-cinnamon dulce hybrid latte was superb.

slow service, crappy staff, not what it use to be. asking for a name just goes to show how much the staff doesn't remember their day to day heavy tipping customers, well, fixed that pretty fast, don't mind if i do keep the extra two dollars everyday.
close to the freeway, good for travelers, good promt service, nice drinks. like any starbucks. mostly close to the freeway.

this is probably my favorite starbucks to go to in philly--and not just because they are directly across the street from my favorite fast food restaurant. the service and the drinks have always been on point and today was no different! i am not a fan of cold coffee but they offered me a sample of my favorite hot flavor cold. i tried it, but i didn't like it. somehow, willingly drinking cold coffee seems sacrilegious. in any event, the barista had made a small one in case i liked it. not wanting either of us to waste it, he suggested that i "bless someone who would appreciate it" and i managed to do just that. there tend to be homeless people hanging around across the street at my favorite place to eat. so, i went across the street and approached a man with a sign and asked him if he likes iced coffee. keep in mind, it's late july and the heat and humidity have been a bit much this year. i explained to him that they made it for me and that cold coffee just isn't my thing and offered it to him. he said he loved iced coffee and was more than willing to drink it. i gave it to him. i felt much better knowing that it wasn't going to waste, because, well, wasting starbucks is just wrong. i also feel good knowing that we did something good for someone in need. this was a good trip!

they be slow. even for regular coffee. they put their priorities on the drive through rather than the people who take the time to come inside, which i think is pretty fucked up. the place is clean tho, which is nice.
