this starbucks has probably one of the rudest barista's ever. slamming the oven, throwing our food down. i get that it's early but if you hate your job change it. three stars because the coffee is good.

this is a large, cozy location with ample variety of seating. there is a fireplace with cozy armchairs, perfect for a casual coffee date or chat. there are long shared tables that are perfectly suitable for both groups or individuals. if you'd like some more privacy to focus on your work, single tables are available, as are booths. there is also a enclave-type space for maximum privacy, partially separated from the rest of the room by a frosted wall. in the several hours i was at my table completing a ridiculous amount of work, this location became very busy and rather noisy. if you don't mind the bustle, then you'll be fine (particularly if you choose a table by the window and away from the masses, as i did). i ordered a fine cup of earl grey tea, and a rather delicious almond croissant.
quick drive thru service

this starbucks fucked up my order. i ordered a matcha latte w almond milk. they put way too much freaking powder. like chill. it shouldnt be that hard to make a matcha latte. it's literally powder and liquid. how do you mess that up. i understand that these employees r new but why did you hire them if they cant make a simple drink. fire them. i paid 7 dollars for the most horrendous piece of shit ever. u guys ruined my day, and my appetite.

it's unbelievable. my wife came here 10 min ago and asked for a cup of warm water for baby formula. they said 54 cents. she said i left the wallet with my husband on the first floor and starbucks is on the third. they said no. i don't know what's wrong with this particular location when the refuse mother with a baby a warm cup of water. we will follow up with a complain.

just had a 25 minute waiting experience inside. we went inside because the drive-thru line was backed up onto westshore. we waited so long and were going to be late for work that we decided to get a refund. we waited in line for that and then at the last minute thought our drinks were next to be made so we decided to wait for our drinks. ended up being inside just over 25 minutes. what's wrong with you, starbucks? hire more people. expand your facilities. open up a second location across the street or down the block. or have a sign that says "your anticipated wait time is 25 minutes."

my wife and our three little ones have been stuck in the minivan since michigan go visit my parents in oregon. we have stopped at many starbucks and fast food establishments. all have fallen flat in the hospitality and engagement realm. let's talk about the gem of hospitality in a 2 minute experience. her name is kara and i'm sure her face is as lovely as her spirit l, however under a black mask to protect herself and our family. she asked us how our weekend was and was very polite and understanding how amazing it is to drive 30 hours across the country with 3 little kids. sarcasm. haha. who offers water to their patrons on top of the amazing service?! kara! this establishment is more than lucky to have such a dedicated and lovely person working here.

the location and atmosphere are fine. it's a mall location. i just feel bad for the girl rosie. she is miserable at her job and tries to make the feeling mutual. u pick ur job love. u don't have to hate life n people don't want that vibe. #notrosie
nice people. don't bother with the egg nog latte because they have the flavor of warm milk. the pumpkin spice latte misses the mark as well.

nothing like sitting in the drive they for 30 minutes to find you missed the teeny tiny order screen and instead of them helping you at the window anyway- they tell you to drive back thru to the end of the line and order! poor service. i will now become a dunkin donuts patron for life!

timing is everything? yup! of course had to stop today to get the free holiday cups! of course you need to get the grande specialty drinks. as we were waiting, the line started to increase.. four cars behind me and three cars in front. hubby uses the app and says we needed to spend $20 to get 75 stars... our purchase total was $10.25... but he always falls for those gimmicks.. like buy one get one free etc.. when i got to the window and showed her his phone, she said the car in front paid for ours!!! perfect timing! thank you to the car in front!
this by far my favorite starbucks drive thru, even during busy hours the baristas are able to get you in and out without jeopardizing the quality of your order.

this is my favorite starbucks to go to in santa barbara!!! the staff is what makes this particular starbucks so great. this location can definitely get pretty busy at times, but the staff are always so friendly and provide wonderful customer service regardless of how busy they are.

not the closest starbucks to my work but i still go here every morning. lisa and carol are awesome, remember my drink every time and even got me started on a starbucks card so i can get free stuff since i am in so much! my 5 stars goes to them. the other staff members are also very friendly and usually have a line out of there in no time, with no complaints.

i come to this starbucks several times a week, mostly using the drive through but also coming inside often to work. the staff is friendly and knowledgeable. they seem to keep their staff--there's not a lot of turnover and constant training. the drive through is really quick. the amount of seating is pretty good for a smaller sized starbucks. there's a public table in the middle where you can sit and plug in your laptop. it's pretty quiet most of the time. i would honesty give it five stars, but two things made me hesitate: -this starbucks has floor to ceiling windows on two sides, which makes it really bright and inviting. but it is so cold in the winter. it's difficult to work with your coat on. -there are a couple of regulars who come in, don't purchase anything, and sit and sleep in the chairs. they smell like they haven't showered in quite a while and the entire place starts to smell like that. i feel for them and i wouldn't want them to be put out in the cold, but sometimes it becomes really unbearable. i don't have an answer for it. it's really sad and unfortunate. occasionally i'll buy them coffee. they're super sweet and kind. but it keeps me from holding business meetings here in the mornings. i'll choose another coffee shop nearby. overall, great, fast, awesome starbucks. one of my favorites.

i have recently been going to this starbuck's more due to the fact all my meetings are very close to this location. i was having a bad day and they asked how i was doing and i said terrible and they gave me a free drink. it was really great and kind. i went there the following day and the barista asked if i was having a better day. it was great to know they remembered me and cared enough to ask. i am really impressed by this location!
this location deserves 5 stars, but it is always so busy that it takes 15 minutes to get your order. the baristas are always fantastic and on point so if you can spare the time, treat yo self!

ever since i moved to bordentown this was one of my favorite things. the starbucks is less than 5 minutes away from my apartment. (can be a bad thing too haha) usually the service is always great; however, the only huge complaint i have is they don't check their mobile orders. on multiple occasions i've placed a mobile order and it won't be ready when i get there, nor did they start it. today, i arrived 10 minutes after i placed the order and they hadn't checked. i can understand if it's busy, but it wasn't today. this won't make me stop coming here, but maybe they should have a different system to know to check mobile orders. a lot of times i'm in a rush and then have to wait another 5-10minutes.

not a great starbucks location - exceptionally slow. and "exceptionally slow" doesn't even capture just how slow it is. we waited in line for 10 min behind a single person ordering a coffee (drip) and breakfast sandwich - and 3 people were "working" at the time. all i wanted was a simple drip coffee. you'll find this starbucks in the harrah's casino, next to the canal street entrance. make sure you go in that side or you'll have to traverse the entire casino floor to find it. and if you do that, you'll see lots of pitiful fools gambling half-asleep at 6:30 am. two stars because they are a real starbucks and can scan your phone app

this starbucks location is located in/next to the national constitution center which is a plus. no free/easy parking in the area so this spot is great for walking traffic. the staff was nice. they did not have cookies available so my son was disappointed. i'll definitely be back.

of course we all love starbucks! but lately i have found that my caramel mochiato doesn't taste the same as sometimes they don't have all the ingredients. also, the location that i go to is the one on town center. i would order food in advance, go pick it up to find out that they didn't have what i ordered and i had to find some kind of alternative; then wait for them to prepare it which is what i wanted to avoid in the first place. i wish that they would have called me in advance to let me know what they don't have in my order to spare me the wait. i learned my lesson not to order in advance, but on site or drive-thru.

since all starbucks are pretty similar, i'm going to review this based on atmosphere and location. this starbucks has a great outdoor seating area. it is pretty massive (being more than 5 tables) and is great for sitting outside in the few months that it is nice to do so. inside there are a few comfy chairs closest to the parking lot and some tables. there are actually two handicap tables that are rather large. that means, unlike other tables, two people can actually comfortably work at it. it is a pretty small starbucks, but small tables that fit two (or really one comfortably when working) seem readily available. the parking can be a little tricky. normally there is enough, but the empanada place next door has some reserved parking, which limits the parking spaces. i got the last one when i got there at about 1 p.m. on a monday. however, by the time i left around 3:30 p.m. there were plenty of spots. there is no take out at this starbucks. however, it is on a main road (4th street n) so it is pretty easy to swing by and get in get out.
my once-a-month place while waiting for my wife's meeting to end. always busy, a bit noisy but if i xan sit in the comfy chair. im happy. friendly baristas.

there is only one word that comes to mind when i think of this starbucks, and that's finally!!!!!!! oh my gosh i cannot explain how long i have been waiting for a starbucks in algiers and i am in loveeeee with this one since it has opened. my very first impression was how nice and big the building was. the interior is especially appealing with very nice architecture, decor, and plenty of seating. the staff are friendly and helpful and on my first visit one of the baristas offered me a free drink because she had accidentally made an extra one. i have been here 3 times since it has opened and have gotten multiple drinks at each visit--all of which were made to my liking. so far i have zero complaints and i'm sure this place will live up to its name. i will definitely continue to heavily support this starbucks!
great location, fast service (even at their peak hours), and friendly employees :) this is usually my go to sb & i'm never disappointed.

i love starbucks but this is the single worst location i have ever encountered by far. between 300 and 330 on monday mar 2 i was trapped in their drive thru line unable to get out during the half hour it took to get to the window and be able to escape. people were actually leaving their cars and clustering to talk while we waited, unable to escape. when we went in to plead just to be able to leave the line, forget the coffee, we were told "they got busy" - but hey looked fully staffed, not abnormally busy, and not edit silly hurried. awful awful awful.

absolutely the worst starbucks i've ever been to! i give it two stars for the two good baristas it has. to give a glimpse of what i frequently go through: i always ask for soy milk because i don't drink normal milk since it makes me sick but apparently someone thought it was ok (or didn't think at all about the drink they were making) and put whole milk in my drink let me repeat that whole milk do you know what whole milk does to someone who does not drink milk!! none the less that was not the first and last time they have messed up my order. my tummy, toilet and wallet still haven't forgiven them for their service and honestly the only reason i go is because it's inside the library but even then i rather walk a few minutes to get cheaper coffee at the student center.

should have stopped anywhere before here. stopped for a smoothie. thought i'd get one with protein and skip a full lunch. ordered the strawberry/banana and a banana nut bread. twenty mins later after i watch him pick up and put down my cup and make coffees for people that came in several minutes after i came, barista hands me some coffee drink. that's a smoothie? oh i read it wrong. then he goes to make to come back and tell me they don't have the right protein and asked if i want the new protein. sure as long as there is no dairy. he proceeds to dump in 2 scoops then before mixing it turns to tell me that oh it does have milk in it. uhm. yeah. that's a problem. so now he's on his third remake of my drink. so no joke and less exaggeration it took nearly 20 min for one drink in w store with two people in front of me and one behind me (who walked out before me). $9. i could have gotten a full meal at panera way faster. next time.

this is the worst starbucks i have ever been to. they always suck no matter the time of the day or the day of the week. they will 100% mess up your coffee. do not trust them. they are always in a bad mood and will rush you. absolutely terrible. last resort.

hate to say it but all starbucks are pretty much the same. this one gets an extra star because it's clean, the bathrooms are stocked and the baristas aren't rude. they aren't overly friendly, but i don't expect too much! they get the drinks right, they seem to be well trained and the app makes the purchase process go smoothly! be careful backing out of the parking spaces in the front lot!! there are bollards at the end of the spaces and i've seen quite a few people hit them backing out!

more than half of the people here were waiting for their coffee. i put in a mobile order, and my breakfast sandwhiches were done by the time i arrived. however, my brewed coffee and green tea took a total of 20 minutes (exactly). they're down to one espresso machine today, but it seemed like there were plenty of staff... one person was being trained... maybe have someone pour drip coffee and make tea? otherwise, place seems nice. plenty of seating and not too loud.

worst starbucks ever, staff ignored me when i had a problem with my order. in fact worse looked at me at the register and turned their back on me and continued to talk. all i wanted was to buy the banana nut bread i had asked for and they did not add to my order.

if you want something from this starbucks you need to go through the drive-through. the wait times are extremely long. you go inside hoping to get your drink and as you watch them serve every car in the drive-through before they get to your order it is frustrating. i've given this place about six tries now but it has consistent customer service. i think i'll take a pass.

if you have tried one starbucks it can definitely seem like you have tried them all but in reviewing this one i looked at the fact that of the three in the mall here in sherwood park (yes you are reading that right....there is infact three in this small mall) this location is the standout. for one if you work in the mall or somewhere nearby it is very easy to get in and out of this one quickly. all three times that i went last week around 8:30 there was nobody ahead of me in line and the only thing i really had a wait for was for the barista to put out the containers of milk and cream as she had not finished setting up for the day yet. secondly if you need a pastry with your morning java fix they seem to have the best selection here. i had tried the safeway starbucks as well last week and by the time i got there at 8:30 about half of the pastry case had already been cleared out. i don't think alot of people associate starbucks with target yet in their mind like how many do with safeway and starbucks. so to sum it all up while there was nothing stand out about this particular starbucks location when it comes to the franchise as a whole when it comes to convenience and selection of product for those in the area it really can't be beat.
big and spacious. not small like typical locations. and plenty of seating outside for you to enjoy the weather and views.

guess what? there's a good starbucks at rocky point! who knew? it's convenient to access from the causeway, just a right turn and you're in. as typical with all starbucks, it's kept clean. they have full service, so you can order a breakfast (i could live off the sausage egg and cheese) or have them heat up a lunch sandwich. the staff is friendly and the service is fast. i only wish they had a larger dinning area. there's only about 3-4 small tables. i know they don't want you to hang around but sometimes i just want to read in a nice comfy leather chair, read the news and sip my coffee. that's not happening here.

settle down everybody. it's a starbucks. we get it. you hate corporate coffee. as far as starbucks goes, this place is now part of my morning routine. it's pretty simple. i want coffee. they have good coffee. and the morning crew knows their regulars, knows their usual orders, are pleasant and attentive to everyone, and manage to crack a few jokes in the process.
it's like a mcdonald's, but coffee. expect average american coffee and service and you'll be ok.

a community a starbucks as you'll probably get. if you catch them at the right time you might even walk away with a free sample. that said they do get busy and you can tell the staff gets a bit overloaded. try to come by at off hours and it will improve the experience.

i love starbucks but i avoid this one. it has some of the slowest service i have ever experienced at a starbucks. i was there 2 days ago...the guy who was doing the drinks loves to talk to the costumers and his co-workers, which is fine...i just noticed he does a lot of talking and no working too. he will stop working and just start talking and talking. there was only one person ahead of me and it still took almost 10 minutes to get my drink. i also got over-charged at one point. the price on the menu was lower then the price i was charged. i added nothing special to my drink...i was going to ask for an explanation or my money back but there was a big line at that point so i let it go. small coffee shop, not much sitting. music can be too loud sometimes. on the plus side there is a sweet cece's and a chipotle right next door, that's a bonus.

the staff is incredibly friendly and even at the most hectic times keep things moving steadily and consistently. sometimes i appreciate being able to just take a break from everything and sit there and relax with something to eat and drink.
this is a large starbucks with plenty of good seating. good neighborhood with good restaurants for a lunchtime break.

(4.5) ~ very good overall food & service! ** my wish finally came true...a starbucks with a "clover brewing system" close to home!:-) read about the clover brewing system online and order your favorite coffee bean either hot -or- iced. my fav is their aged sumatra:-)

i would give no stars. i never do this,write a remark. but this was bad service. simple order two coffees and three breakfast sandwiches. waited and waited. got my wife's coffee waiting on mine. customer behind me got his three coffees and breakfast sandwich. still waiting and not a word from the employees. second customer behind me got his two coffees and food. still waiting, wife's coffee is now cold so i asked what's going on ? they said they are just making the other coffees now. so i asked them to make my wife's coffee again. third customer got their order. still waiting. i figured it out at the end, they saw my badge when i paid got to my car and the coffees they made weren't even hot. i love starbucks but i think i'm done. i'll find a mom and pop coffee shop.

this place have kicked up there game recently. the lines are moving much faster now! i'm basically in here everyday now. the workers are amazing. they all finally got my name right! yay!!! so many drinks that i love but can't get all the time bc of the crazy calories: salted caramel frap & mocha- delicious. i love the salitness and sweetness double chocolate frap with the choc whipped cream-yum white choc mocha-mmm hahaha...i could prob list the whole menu, but i won't. oh, the breakfast sandwiches and pastries are wonderful as well. give those a try!

i stop by this starbucks nearly every day. the staff are super friendly, quick, and i have yet to have a bad experience here. i usually order ahead on the app, but even when i am in line the service is quick. not to mention the fact that i have a custom order that i love and they always make it right. i have been to pretty much every starbucks in our area, this one is my absolute favorite.

this isn't my first choice for starbucks in the area, but it's up there on the list! they seem to stay pretty busy, but any time i've gone in to work for awhile, i've been able to find a seat somewhere - though it's been close once or twice. sometimes the drive thru is slow. sometimes my order's not quite right when i get it, though it's more a matter of how they make it rather than receiving the wrong item. the parking lot is currently hard to navigate between the pot holes and tight lot space. but despite these few "issues", i enjoy this starbucks location and keep coming back again and again. the friendly baristas and chill work space make the occasional hiccups worth it.

one of the largest starbucks in town. therefore has more space for added extras in food and coffee extras and seating. service has always been friendly. seating at tables, round tables, lounge like chairs, at a bar looking out to west end/harding and outdoor seating. many meetings have been held in this starbucks. parking is shared with the strip mall but always a place to be found. early am coffee drinkers are moved along nicely, so one doesn't feel like, "oh snap, i should have skipped coffee this am". the milk and sugar station remains filled and tidy and the tables and bar are clean. one of the top starbucks in town due to size and hospitality combined. others also have great quality service, but not the space. close to many other places, grocery, liguor store, mexican, sushi, sandwich place, office store and drug store and close to 40 and 440. looking for a meeting coffee place close to everything with great service and clean, bazinga, here it is!

this starbucks is still just a solid 3 stars. they get the job done, but without any extra pizzaz in customer service. i stopped in to pick up a grande cold brew before my class over at the apple store. the cashier was very dry, to be honest i do not remember her even thanking me or ordering. the wait for my beverage was minimal, considering two others were ahead of me in line. the young lady who presented me my drink, did thank me for coming in. so i figured, it was her job to say, "thanks". i wish starbucks would go to paper straws or the new lids that are like a travel mug and do away with the plastic straws. will i return? - only if i am taking a class at the apple store. when: february 27, 2020 why: liquid refreshment for class

zero sense of urgency in a place where you need it the most....at an airport gate. focus should be on expediting orders as quickly as possible. line of about 8 took me about 10 minutes to arrive at the cashier then another 10 minutes waiting for my food. totally unacceptable and happens way too often at starbucks. if the coffee wasn't so good i would never come here. you're hanging on by a thread starbucks! improve your ordering system and now!!

they were nice and gave my sweet, little, 18lb baby a "puppachino" he looked like scaly wag when he got it all over his little pug face. they were very polite, however dogs are not allowed. we had to eat it in the parking lot. while walking my sweet little pug friend, i couldn't help but notice the amount of cancer sticks thrown behind starbucks landscaping. shame on you starbucks employees!!! keep southport beautiful!

always super sweet and actually makes drinks in reusable cup and doesn't make it in plastic then dumps it into reusable one also never had a drink made incorrectly, but a great place to spend your time studying or working!!!

this starbucks has a very friendly staff! and to those who complain about the drive through line- i have no sympathy. people cram into the drive through line even though they know it takes a long time and blocks traffic. park and go in. it's simple.

knowledgeable barristas who don't hesitate to give you good service; its what you really are paying for at starbucks - not the coffee :) they are always busy, but being so close to the movies and off the highway has that effect.

this is the worst starbucks in this area! the drinks taste nothing like the other starbucks. the lines a very slow. i know how a triple shot should taste but they charge for triple and only use 1 or two. the drinks taste watered down so we try ristretto but i don't think they know how so that is horrible too. this is by far the worst starbucks i've ever been to and i have traveled around the world. i've given them many chances. i even called corporate. this store can't be helped and needs new management or should just shut down. if you really want to know how to make coffee go to the tampa (gandy) location. they are friendly and know how to make starbucks coffee. this store is no different than going to a gas station. the only difference is the gas station coffee is cheaper and taste better.

this is my favorite starbucks in the entire city. everyone is friendly and they offer outside seating with outlets and it is covered. i usually sit outside. there's also the cutest neighborhood black cat that would walk past. everytime i went , there was a good amount of people there but it was not loud or hectic unlike some other starbucks i have went to.
convenient location, friendly service and all menu options are sold here. i like the iced teas and iced coffee.

honestly, one of the best starbucks i've visited, in a while. professional staff. quiet place to work. my coffee has been perfect and prepared quickly, every day. i'm visiting for the holidays and this has been my go to for a little quiet time every morning.

this is the cafe that i usually go. employees are super nice and remember most of their regular's name. they also have strong customer connection! i always enjoy talking with every single employee who works here. it never feels like they're talking to you because they have to; you actually feel close to them. also for mobile orders there haven't been a single time that i've ordered and they didn't have it ready when i got there. the cafe is always clean! and washrooms always have the needed supplies! i absolutely love this café!
just went to the drive thru for a cappuccino. i think the person at the windows name was bryce. all i have to say is ---keep that energy booboo! so friendly.

slowest starbucks!!! i waited 29 minutes for an oatmeal that should have taken one minute to pour. other mobile orders and drinks were more important apparently. even standing there, i was not acknowledged or apologized to for the long wait.

the staff here doesn't understand the concept using one's "indoor voice." they could have their loud conversations a block apart from each other. if this location wasn't so small, the noise would not be so painful.

wrong order, burnt tasting espresso, very very rude barista. my expectations are not very high for a place with a drive thru but i went in having a nice day and i left completely irritable. also this is one of the busiest starbucks i have ever seen, and from waiting and seeing the people in line ahead of me it seems like i'm not the only person who didn't like their coffee...!
slowest starbucks drive through i've ever experienced. i will happily drive 3 miles out of my way to go to another starbucks. i save time and sanity.

finally visited this location after passing it when it first opened. my sister always used it so i wanted to see it and plus the dunkin fresh decaf tasted too strong. pretty busy on a sunday morning . customer service good but was disappointed that my london fog had no sweetener as it should .

very impressed. i met a friend here and when we got up to the counter, i ordered just a blonde roast with soy. well, they were out of blonde and instead of me having to make a different choice, they made me a pour over blonde. delicious! since i had to wait, they even gave me a coupon to get my next drink at any starbucks on them. great customer service!
this is the store that banned a customer for life for confronting dbags who were not handicapped but parking in the handicapped spots. never reward this kind of stupidity.

i should have left a review prior to this but after a good moment of thinking, this store has never done anything exceptional for my to even receive a star rating of high get them a 3 and this current rating is still generous to the wait for today. as a former partner, i completely understand the rush, the intensity, the hustle, and the stress of morning coffee, especially if you haven't consumed your own coffee yet. this store definitely beats to its own drum though. i mobile order approximately the same time multiple times throughout the week, give or take a few minutes. when the app says it's gunna take 6mins to prep and my drive will be 6 minutes, one would kind of expect their order to be read and or near ready upon arrival. well this store is never consistent. i can be half way their when i receive notification my order is ready or wait up to 20 minutes (like today 9/11/19) for my order. when i have to wait longer than 5 minutes for my order i'm slightly annoyed considering i put it in at least 6 mins prior to arrival. not only am i waiting longer but also i'm now late for work. this is the most convenient store in my way to work and when on time it's perfect, but days like these are rough and i contemplating eating the cost of my coffee and leaving. will the partners of this store please develop a better routine to ensure efficiency of the workflow and timeless of orders!!!!!

my favorite starbucks of all! it is such a beautiful building inside and out. what they did to the inside of the place is amazing. it makes me want to hang out there and just people watch even though i have to get back to work. the staff there is super friends and they seem like they really take pride in preparing great beverages, even when the place has a line almost out of the door. i love the wall of coffee and coffee drinking accessories when you walk in. it's hard to resist buying some of that stuff when you are waiting in line. the case that the pastries is in is very pleasing to the eye. thank god they have the calories posted next to each type of goodie or i might put some weight on from my frequency of visitation. it's nice to look at, not nice to my waist line. although i would like to suggest to the owner of that location that it would be awesome if they had some kind of deal with a nearby bakery that provided them with king cake, but i don't know, starbucks probably has a conglomerate code that would interfere with that kind of thing.

my go to starbucks. staff handles it all! never met with anything but a friendly face (even with the masking policy in place). they pay attention to your order and have gotten it right every time! "jello" along with a few others have gotten to recognize my order (venti apple macchiato hot) and it has been nice that they say hello to me by name. love having a starbucks closer to home.. it's clean, convenient, and as i mentioned, inviting. -holly

very friendly baristas at this location. it's in the bellevue so the architecture is beautiful. there's a seating area in the back corner separated by a wall. the first time i was here i didn't see the seating. there's also outlets underneath the bench seating so it's a good place to charge your phone. it's very clean too.
starbucks is a "treat" coffee for me cuz i usually do dd but when i want my coffee sweet with a lil kick of extra i go here. love it. and he threw in a cake pop for free

tried to order online to speed process up but cant do it. so go to the store lobby closed and 14 cars in line. you can never trust that lobby will be open and even if store will be open on time. employees are rude most of the time. this store really gives starbucks a bad name guess start going to human bean or db because major disappointment.

cashier was very pleasant. smiley and very nice. the barista however was more interested in paying attention to the latest gossip from the three other baristas that were just standing around laughing it up. she set a drink on the counter where myself and 2 others were waiting for their drinks but didn't call a name or say the name of the drink, nor did the cup have any letters on it indicating what drink it was. so i asked if it was the peppermint mocha and she turned to me and quickly said "yeah i think so" (as if i'd interrupted her) and went back to laughing and gossiping. so, i asked another barista if this was the peppermint mocha and she said "i'm not sure, probably." i was completely dumb founded. not sure i'll be returning to this particular starbucks.

service was fast and friendly enough. but we ordered five drinks - four simple iced coffees - that were all not filled up (about a half inch or more below the lid). the iced cloud macchiato was like 3 inches down and barely cold within a couple minutes. the picture i posted has not been drunken out of. the foam didn't last at all. that was a $6 drink. i've ordered these drinks before and they aren't like this. the cloud macchiato does have the foam go down, but only after you start drinking. not so fast on it's own. both of these issues aren't normal so i wasn't impressed.

this is my favorite starbucks around butler university's campus radius. the baristas are always so friendly and are great at making suggestions. the line typically isn't long and they accept online orders so your stuff is ready when you get there. great neighborhood starbucks.

had to head out one evening to get a fair amount of work done and found this spot packed wall-to-wall with students working on projects and studying. find your spot, get you coffee, and get working :-)
this place can be either a hit or a miss depending who makes the drink and whether or not the person is new. i do like the friendliness of the staff and i always leave good tips for their hard work.

those of you who follow my reviews know that i am not a big fan of coffee. in fact the thought of knocking back a mug of percolated, roasted columbian bean juice pretty much turns my stomach. no matter how much i might like that early morning blast of caffeine to clear the cobwebs after a night of sharing a bottle of caymus special selection cab during dinner, i just can't handle the taste of coffee. nope, sorry, it ain't gonna happen! now that we have that out of the way i'll continue with my review.... so there i was, innocently minding my own business, as my friend and i strolled the sidewalks of soho one afternoon, when she suddenly perked up and gushed, "oh look.....a starbucks!". she used her feminine trickery to convince me to stop at the store so that she could order a mega-cup of grande mocha triple-shot soy milk salted caramel flan latte blah blah blah while i ordered a simple hot green tea. (how in the hell does anyone remember the name of a drink that must take 5 minutes to write down all of the ingredients?) oh, and she also wanted a low-fat blueberry whole-grain yogurt muffin with honey ("because it sounded yummy"). we found a quiet table in a corner of the store, and sat down to chat while waiting for the barista to call the name on our order. (i had momentarily pondered giving my name as 'beelzebub', but i decided to behave.) perhaps i was captivated by my date's lovely smile, or the friendliness of the folks behind the counter, but i began to calm down and enjoy my time inside of this coffee shop. the place was jam-packed with people typing away on their laptops and conversing on their cellphones. there was a group in the corner playing some sort of word game, and every so often they would shout out a random off-the-wall, and generally inappropriate, word and the whole shop would bust out laughing. another group was playing a board game, and so the mood was very festive. as i sipped my tea i took in the décor of the shop which included vaulted high ceilings, huge plate-glass windows, tables and chairs which could easily be pushed together to accommodate any-sized group, a large outdoor patio where the locals sat with their four-footed furry friends, super-friendly folks behind the counter, and everyone laughing as they worked or played games or simply chatted away. the smells of the various drinks and food items was pleasing, and i am told that the muffin was divine, though i was threatened with a fork stab to the back of my hand if i even thought about reaching across the table to try a bite. the folks from starbucks love to say, "wake up and smell the coffee -- starbucks is everywhere," and rightly so as the world's #1 specialty coffee retailer has more than 16,000 coffee shops in more than 35 countries. they've built a massively-successful brand, and you have to admire all of that work and the quality of their products. you'll find this particular starbucks located along south howard just south of swann avenue, and in easy walking distance of several popular restaurants and neighborhood watering holes. the shop is open at 5am each morning, and stays open till 11pm on weeknights, and then till 12 midnight on friday and saturday nights for your late-night java fix. there's a huge parking lot out back, so plenty of room to c'mon down and meet your friends. i'll never be a coffee drinker, but i am a huge fan of this neighborhood gathering spot and the service that they provide to the local community. oh, and they have hot tea and cocoa to keep me happy!

the absolute worst! i unfortunately have to rely on this starbucks because i work at the mall. with holiday season approaching it would be nice to know i can rely on starbucks caffeine to get me through the day with all the chaos. but no! constantly under staffed. a mobile order takes at least 20 minutes. why? this place needs to be completely re-staffed. why on weekends is their only two baristas? they're not even moving fast , they are taking their time. i'm really hoping some management changes are made because it would be such a huge inconvenience to have to start going to another starbucks. literally the worst starbucks i've been to in my life.

the good: their service was fast. the bad: the staff was rude, ignored me for a few minutes as i stood there waiting to order. one of the very fews times that i didn't have a good/welcoming customer service experience at a starbucks. tip: they do take your starbucks card here.

this will be brief, mostly because my visit was brief but my experience was consistent with previous times so i wanted to rate... pros: fast service, drinks are fine. cons: poor service, careless manners.
i was unaware that it is apparently bad manners to call and order a box of coffee the day you want it. the staff was unbelievably rude.
extremely slow service!! they need to hire some more help because their drive threw line takes over 30 minutes to get through.
great service and the coffee and pastry hit the spot for an early wake up to fly home. the line is long. may be time for a larger starbucks!

10 people working at 2 45pm on a thursday one of whom is training. we went inside to order our two drinks. a honey almondmilk flat white and a mocha frappuccino. it took at least 10 minutes to get our drinks!. now, they do have a drive through, they had some pre-orders, and 2 of those 10 people were training. but the trainee made our drinks. and there were only 2 drinks picked up while we were there. it's obvious their priority is with the drive through customers and this is something all of the starbucks seem to do, at least in my recent experience. their specialty drinks are tasty but, overall, starbucks brewed coffee is horribly over-roasted (read 'burned'). still, when i can't locate a local coffeehouse and there is a starbucks nearby when i need a coffee fix, i'll go.
drinks are fine, and the employees are nice, but the outside area is consistently dirty--trash surrounding the sitting areas. kinda gross unfortunately.

the usual! no complaints. got the coffee. although the person taking my order must have been new because she got it wrong. the barista had no problem taking care of it. a typical starbucks and layout!

the location of this starbucks is not the greatest. you'd think with it being right off i-10 it would be but it's very difficult to get out, especially if you have to make a right hand turn onto ina. the staff is very friendly, though, and that makes up for the traffic problems.

ordered a cold brew. handed me a warm one with zero ice. asked if they could add some ice so that it was at least somewhat cold (since you know, it's a cold brew). she poured out 80% of the coffee and stuffed the rest full of ice. awesome. thanks. i'll enjoy this $4.91 cup of ice. hopefully it helps me get through the rest of my day!

love these guys. especially considering how swamped they can be, they're always very professional and pleasant. i'm in there a lot, and whenever they actually get a chance to breathe, they definitely recognize you and acknowledge you in one way or another. very friendly and personable. they're also very on top of their customer service element. i've been in there a couple times when i've asked for a food item that they were currently out of, and whoever was helping me was quick to suggest some similar items i might have an interest in and also offered to discount a more expensive replacement item to the price of the item i originally asked for. the store is also very clean (as are the restrooms) and they have a very nice outdoor patio area. definitely my favorite local coffee stop!

i never realized there was a starbucks here until i had a meeting scheduled here a few months ago. this is one of the starbucks that is on the smaller side, and located around so many striip malls and stores, i somehow hadn't noticed it before. i went inside, ordered a cold pressed juice and sat outside. the outside - that's the best part. they have outdoor patio seating that made our meeting much more fun. it was great to be in the sun and the wifi also reached out there, allowing me to get a little work done before leaving. solid starbs.

adequate, describes this location perfectly. the barista said they were unable to accept starbucks rewards because they are affiliated with the hyatt hotel and not starbucks. i'm not sure why they can scan my starbucks card for payment but not scan for rewards? service was quick and lots of seating areas. but tables and chairs were filled with crumbs and a bit challenging to find a clean spot. only came here because we needed a cool drink after wandering around the arch park.
i owe starbuck's coffee shops for my undergraduate degree. i just moved to town and i really like this one. i love the post-it board and everyone is very friendly. i will going back for sure!

i have been to this starbucks several times since it has opened and am really pleased by the friendly staff and the coffee. i wouldn't recommend always going into the drive-thru since it's packed often with cars. therefore, i will share a small secret with you.... behind the starbucks building is more parking (that's where i go). you can park your car, walk inside, order and be out faster than those waiting in the drive-thru. i have saved so much time doing this. i'm serious! i get my iced white chocolate mocha (and a sugar treat) far faster than those waiting in line in their car. it saves me serious time. i think the staff have a good multi-tasking system, but i have never had to wait at the counter in-store to have my order taken and it only took the barista less than two minutes to have my drink ready for me. each starbucks is different, i have been to other locations where it was ok waiting in the drive-thru. plus, there is very comfy seating outdoors (like a super large couch). my bum doesn't always like sitting in hard metal chairs so the outdoor couch is a nice change.
chai latte tasted horrible, like the syrup was rancid. asked for a replacement, same thing. lots of flies on the ceiling. i give two stars because they were helpful in trying to remake the drink.

14 minutes for a mobile order of sous vide egg bites because none of the workers bothered to check the mobile orders is unacceptable. on top of that, my order was wrong once i received it. oof. the workers seem to move at a glacial pace here. i'll stick to my park blvd store from here on out--they're much more efficient and won't get my order wrong.

the jw starbucks is rude and unprofessional. ordered a cappuccino and venti water, it was $6, come to find out they charge for water in a cup. i said i don't want the water. after the third time of telling me they need to scan my phone app, i said why don't you train your people to warn about charging for water so i don't have to scan my phone three times. i went back to sit down and some sjw employee says in front of my seven year old that she's going to call security on me. why is a low brain sjw scaring my kid? i contacted starbucks and if they don't make this right, i'm done with starbucks and their sjw nonsense. i spend $200 month in starsucks and they'll let homeless people use their toilet water for free but charge a good customer for a cup of water? i've had enough of this sjw company treating me worse than a homeless person. this jw marriott has nearly convinced me that i'm done with starsucks and their new bonus system sucks as well. i'm really starting to hate starsucks

now it's taken me quite some time to actually write this review because i wasn't quite sure what i wanted to say..but here goes......... don't get me wrong, i do like starbucks but i finally came to the realization that it's overpriced and overrated. i was a gold status for quite a few years as my favorite drink was the hot or iced chai tea and more recently, hot green tea. i began boycotting them when they had the holiday red cup release in 2018 for the fact that i received by red cup but with no lid since the store was out --- the barista @ that time advised me to just visit another location or call and ask starbucks and they should be able to give me one because they sometimes just ship to few to the individual stores. i went around and asked other locations who were sold out of the cups and didn't have any extra lids so i proceeded to call starbucks as well as post an inquiry online. i was not given any help or support for this...just basically "oh well" and they couldn't do anything about it. now my concern is how can starbucks send a store a certain number of red cups but not enough lids for the cups. is the expectation for guests to have their hot drinks in lid-less cups!? i could spill my drink on myself and it's not cool to have my starbucks hot drink in a lid-less cup because most of the time you're on the go when enjoying your overpriced drink. based on the level of service i've had at other establishments when there are shortfalls or issues w/things when being a committed customer, i was surprised by the level of service and just lack of caring i received when i spoke to someone at corporate about the issue. when i look at starbucks now, not being intrigued or care to be a gold star status anymore, i don't know why consumers are so crazy about this place! it can't be that the drinks are that amazing that people are willing to pay so much for their iced coffee. when i used to get the chai tea --- it was never made fresh --- the chai comes out of a cardboard container, heated or iced. yes, i used to be one of these people but not anymore .... and now i would rather save that $5 that i used to spend on a drink and get a smoothie from a place that tastes much better and is not overly sweetened and unhealthy. i'm always amazed at how any starbucks establishment always has a crowd --- you almost never see one that's empty or not busy in some capacity. and as i look at other reviews on yelp, i can see that mine is clearly one of the very few that isn't 4-5 stars.

horrible service. walked in as the only customer with one order ahead of me. stood there for 15 minutes while others got served first. interaction from employees was less than friendly or understanding. walked out with one drink instead of try two i ordered. at least i asked for a refund. will not be back to this location.

mobile order not ready. they said it wasn't received. they asked if i sent it to the correct location. i showed them my receipt with the correct location. then they said maybe someone took it. i mobile ordered because i was in a hurry. employees did not offer anything to make this inconvenience right.

the goods cannot function without a latte. leboulangerie is the new bakery and it is much better than before ambiance 1. i like the faint lights in the ceiling kind of rustic chic service 1. these people know me all the time!!! remember me by name!! mojo 1. anything drink, i love them all but sugar free coffee is my fav

it makes me so happy that there's a starbucks in this location. super convenient to a neighborhood that's largely interested in organic food and granola-type coffee shops. thank you, u sciences, for bringing my beloved big-business espresso to the 'hood.

doesn't open until 6:30 most days, fyi. i've gotten good drinks here and bad drinks here. i've also gotten food warmed up fully and food that's still frozen in the middle. seems like a hit or miss location regarding quality.

smelled like paint and glue? why? there are 4 local coffee shops within walking distance with far superior product, atmosphere and community contribution. i kid you not... four! spend your money here and watch it leave louisiana faster then you can say "predatory placement practices".

usually this starbucks is very good! but my past experience changed my whole mindset. i ordered a grande hot chocolate at kids temperature. when i got to the window the lady gave me a kids sized hot chocolate. i told her i ordered a grande but at kids temperature, not the kids size. and she was so rude, she said she could remake it but i paid for a kids. that's not my fault because i know that i asked for a grande. she gave me the rudest attitude and remade it, she made me feel like i was being so difficult but she is the one who screwed up my order, not me.
staff very nice. smile, friendly, and very customer service driven. place is a little dirty most of the time...but beyond that...i will go again for sure!!
the service is beyond slow! was thinking they were preparing my pastry... no they completely forgot about it after reminding them twice.

they just opened last year and every time we pass by they are alway super super busy. the line is always at least 5 people. but they actually move pretty fast because they have enough people working so that's good. if you like starbucks then make sure stopping by when you are shopping.
this place has the a/c on freezer cold. bring a sweater. everything else seems normal.

so i'm doing the keto thang! and what other way to try to stick to a meal plan than buy trying different sugar free drinks! i've tried it several locations and this location was one of the worst at making it look like diet crap! they did not mix the heavy cream and it made it look like spoiled milk was added to my drink! i asked the barista and she brushed me off by saying that's what you ordered. but did you have to make it look so nasty? next visit they got it right!

this starbucks location inside the greenwood target had some very friendly baristas working yesterday. through the target app; target has been offering 20% off drinks. i took advantage of that discount several times before the coupon expires. they were unfortunately out of my favorite breakfast sandwiches; however that is something i encounter frequently at many starbucks locations. the barista made a delicious iced americano cold foam irish creme for me and i got one of my favorite treats - a brownie.

every single time i've been here the people were really friendly and made my drinks correctly! i cannot tell you how many times i've ordered my drink at other starbies and it's made wrong. if you go through the drive thru id go when i have extra time.

so far, so good. it's now open as of september 1st. the configuration is kinda weird- seating areas are long and narrow, so plenty (or all?) seats near a view outside to market or new jersey streets. always amazes me how many of gess they can pack into such a small geography! one more spot for the die-hands to get their psl. no drive-thru. that would be what i'd like to see in a downtown location, though i guess traffic would be an immediate issue.

excellent store, fantastic location. this spot is one of the better starbucks i've been to. the baristas are extremely friendly and if you are a regular they remember your drink and your name. they are quick and efficient and you are in-and-out. hands down best store in the area.

this is a smallish starbuck's compared to some in town but it is my local one and is perfect for the slightly out-of-the way, family oriented, slightly rural feeling mesa neighborhood of santa barbara. there are a few chairs and small tables on the "pet friendly" front patio that is in an alcove sheltered from the weather. the atmosphere inside is cozy on a cold day and, being near sb city college, is often quiet and studious but is friendly and comfortable. there are two (quite well maintained) bathrooms which is unusual for a small coffeehouse, and the staff is quite efficient and friendly though the place does occaisionally get very busy in the morning rush. this is a "big chain" coffee with a distinctly friendly, local ambience!
really nice people. just wish they could get my order right. i am a daily to double daily and my order is only right maybe half the time. and it is the same order every time

this place is okay, but what starbuck's has no venti-sized straws. i literally have to go to jamba juice to get a fucking large straw. just be warned, if you buy a trenta or venti or any large sized drink, they will leave you hanging. better work on the customer service.
drive thru is super convenient, but can be slow, especially during the weekday mornings. seems understaffed at times, but so far havent had an issue with an order

this was the worst starbucks service i've ever had. i waited for five minutes in line for someone to even acknowledge me and take my order. the barista on the cash register was unable to pay attention to my order, and thus put it in wrong. he argued with me over the contents of the drink, which i ended up being right about after confirming with a second barista. then i requested to use my birthday reward, but again he wasn't listening and proceeded to tell me i didn't have enough money on my card. after i repeated myself 2 more times he understood. then my drink was made wrong because he didn't put it in correct in the cash register. my drink was remade by the second barista and even then the drink was incorrect and tasted horrible. will never return.

seriously awful service - literally the worst experience i've ever had at a starbucks, a company that (at least i thought) prided itelf on positive customer engagement and friendly service. i went to this location to pickup just a basic dark roast ordered in advance through the app, and after i tried to walk in at 11:50 am on a saturday to make the pickup in store, i was abruptly and rudely confronted by a mind-numbingly unhelpful "worker" who greeted me with "we're closed!" when asked "what time do you close?" (for some reason this location closes at noon - random, but ok still doesn't explained why i can't go inside for 2 freaking seconds...what if i decided i just wanted to take a walk to starbucks and didn't have a my car to make this pickup? doesn't make sense why only the drive-thru is only option) the worker didn't offer any assistance whatsoever and instead insisted i "get in the drive-thru" line, which was easily 10+ cars deep at this point. i asked if i could just step inside briefly and pick up my single item, since with 10 minutes to spare before they actually should be closed it would have taken me all of only 10 seconds to pickup my item off the counter (as usual) and then this would all be over, but allegedly this worker "can't get back inside" (she was confronting me in the parking like, repeating over and over again "sir, we're closed", when in fact as i pointed out they were not closed yet) i thought this was a totally ridiculous and obvious lie that she couldn't get back inside, so i decided to just wait for one of the other customers still inside waiting to just let me in as they left - yes other customers were still inside, so clearly i think it would have been no problem if i just stepped inside for literally maybe 7 seconds flat just to pick up my coffee sitting there on the counter right there which i could still see through the front door (the door was locked to outside at this point of course). eventually, a customer came out but immediately a starbucks employee from inside closed the door and locked it again before i could get to the door or ask for any assistance from him! i thought: this is the absolute worst customer service i think i've ever experienced, maybe ever! so at this point i'm force to utterly waste my time and get at the back of the round-the-building drive-thru line while multiple cars ahead of me continued to place orders in front of mine well after my simple coffee was ready and getting cold by this point no doubt. by the time i finally got my coffee, after it was cold of course, i didn't even want it anymore and didn't drink it - i ended up just throwing it away! what an utterly absurd waste time and money. easily the worst starbucks experience i've ever had in my life. i will not be back.

so we have come to know and expect a standard level of service from starbucks no matter where we are in the world... unfortunately this one does not meet the standard. as this is a good location to meet up with friends (somewhat in the middle between north side edmonton and st. albert), i frequent it about once a month. every single time there has been some level of let down... either the baristas plain ignore me standing at the counter (for a full 6 min), or they don't listen close enough to my order and get it wrong (twice), to the bathrooms not clean on a regular basis, and the cramped feeling inside. generally i find the employees disrespectful towards patrons and i have even had the manager make me feel uncomfortable in a situation. inside is small and there are not many tables, the decor is standard, and the lighting is dim. the one bright spot in this review is that i have never had issues with the drive thru. overall i would not recommend this location for your starbucks fix unless absolutely necessary. and definitely not a good place for studying or relaxing due to the cramped space and lack of lighting.

i've only been in here once but the baristas are super friendly and chatty. i really appreciate it because a lot of people in the university area seem to hate the students. it closes late at midnight, and it's got two floors! i love starbucks. you always know what you're going to get.

employee named riple was very disrespectful, worst starbucks i ever been too. slow service , employee riple was arguing and was very disrespectful. terrible service and experience at this starbucks location. wont be back to this location.
one of the best starbucks in delaware. the only downside is like many others i'll say it could use a good cleaning.

i happen to love this location of starbucks - the people who run it make the difference. there are usually several people sitting at the barstools chatting with the barristas. it's alot of fun and makes dropping $4.72 on an iced tea or coffee that much easier.
not as good as the other starbucks i have visited. the prices were also higher here. guess the higher prices fit based on where it is.
ordered grande cafe americano and server poured me regular coffee from pre made bin. guess i just should have ordered coffee and saved some $$.
rude as hell the worst service didn't shake drink didn't even use manners i work in service i understand how hard it can be but the experience i had was ridiculous rude
so, i was pushed to pay for my coffee by the cashier before i was able to order food and then the line was 20 people long. while i loved my coffee it seems like she could have switched registers?

as always starbucks is great but expensive. i tried the new maple pecan in a venti size, not impressed:( it has a very weird taste...taste like coffee mixed with peanut butter. not a good combination. i personally won't order this drink again but it was a double star day! next time i will stick with my peppermint mocha.

the employees work really hard. they are friendly. coffee always seems better than other locations. i see them keeping the building clean. when there isn't many customers in the store. i love the caramel apple spice cider yummy

i opened this review like oh this will be an easy up as i thought of my quick trip but actually the layout of this location is (can i cuss??!?!) ..it's enough to not go back actually. i've been inside this location at least 3x and the service, cleanliness, speed, and atmosphere were fine. during peak hours you can't even get into the parking lot because it's a narrow angled one-way drive around. there's not enough room inside of their parking lot to accommodate the waiting cars so they sit in the street causing a traffic jam. there's other traffic from the other businesses in the suite. it's the most frustrating thing. you can't even get inside of the lot to park to order inside or grab your pickup items. i had actually mobile ordered and was almost involved in an accident trying to get inside. my order was sitting at the station waiting for me

consistently great service . clean bathrooms and cafe area. they do seem to run out of bottled water and pastries pretty often ,though. i had a meeting and was starving so imagine my delight when i noticed they had a vegan(minus dressing) veggie and brown rice salad bowl! it was layers of yummy , well cooked brown rice, butternut squash , broccoli , world's tinniest(but tasty)peas, toasted beets, kale, red cabbage , and roasted tomatoes. the side tahini dress is not vegan ,unfortunately, it contains honey. . otherwise, it's a great meal if you're in a pinch for a healthy, delicious and well portioned dinner .

i stopped in the day after that snow storm because this was the only starbucks open. martha took great care of me. she was super friendly and had a great personality and we chatted about her friend whom i kno at another starbucks. i again returned the next night and i had allison take care of me. she was also super sweet and friendly. she was very tentative to my needs. both made my drink perfect! thanks ladies for all your hard work and keep it up!!!!

best place to grab a coffee in chestnut hill. im not sure why butt hurt drama queen liberal from good old flint,mi is so upset about @erica a. but she needs to go back to her home state. the staff is super friendly and show they do care about their customers. would highly recomend stopping buy while you window shop in lovely little chestnut hill area. #phillylove #starbucksrocks
this store has a great staff and a good location near i-580. it is in the same shopping area as home depot. the staff works hard for quality in their product and it shows.
this is not a starbucks. if this were a starbucks, then i could pay for my coffee with my starbucks stars. as i can't, it's rather a waste of time and effort, and false advertising.

this been a regular stop on the way to work for me going on 5+ years now. up front, the three stars are for the staff however, a couple drag the rest down, one with a regularly flippant attitude. another common occurrence is at the point of sale when hot food items are ordered. the patron's name is not asked and when the order comes, a weak callout is made with only the name of the food and consequently not heard in restaurant over the often loud music and sometimes the sound level of the staff having a good time at work... i love seeing happy employees so perhaps this is a fair trade-off. however, a half warm breakfast sandwich tastes like hell. often, they do not have in stock breakfast items like the heated sandwiches. who does the ordering and for that matter, is there a manager even present seeing to these and the following glaring indiscretions? as for the condition of the restaurant, i'd have to say it's a bit dirty and unattended. the window exteriors appear to not have been cleaned since the remodel, the window interiors are not far behind. the tables don't get wiped on a regular basis through the morning as well as the floor swept and debris remaining throughout the morning, it doesn't appear that it's mopped nightly either. the supply station is often out of items as i have observed often on my numerous visits at 6 am, the condition of the floors as described herein, unswept and still showing the signs of the previous business day. i did ask about this once and was told "oh, we got hammered this morning". again, 6 am and i look around seeing that i was one of three people in the place and but two cars in line outside, the staff kids partying it up... really? as said, there is good people working there that take a personal pride in the job they do but all that goes out the door given the aforementioned issues. a manager that isn't busy behind the counter preparing orders to oversee that the place is taken care of would be a start. a good hard cleaning of the entire restaurant, inside and out including the filthy paved areas outside, including the furniture is more than overdue. i suggest steam cleaning and sterilization. can anyone say "reset"?

starbucks needs to rein in the jackass employees at this franchise. we've been in nashville for months and we keep giving these bitchy baristas another chance. they suck! the service, the attitudes and the orders always suck. they actually gave me crap for ordering too much in the drive through when their was no parking and no other options. i'm sorry to inconvenience you with my $50.00 order at your establishment. these guys are aweful, rude and snippy.
ahh much better (unlike the 39th street). swift, efficient, clean. the youthberry granita is delicious!

absolutely terrible! don't know how this location is still open. waited 40 minutes for a simple iced coffee and ended up no being able to drink it due to how horrific it tasted between the inch of sugar and coffee grounds that were in it.
same as every other but south philly stores are so much cheaper for some reason.

as far as starbucks go, this location used to be okay. friendly service, upbeat and somewhat comedic baristas and always orderly. well, over the past 6 years, something odd has happened. kids... young ass children are now hipstering it up and drinking freaking coffee. how this happened, i don't know. maybe it was the suburban soccer moms constantly rolling thru to grab an over-priced cup of joe with their tots in the baby seat, and now those tots have grown up a wee bit. who knows? but i stopped in at this location at the request of my aunt who was in town from st. louis, and it was a disaster. the line was a mile long with kids under the age of 12 who couldn't make up their damn minds on what to order. it took a good 10-15 minutes just to get up to the counter. and as if that wasn't enough, they were all loud as hell. i couldn't even hear myself think, let alone have a conversation with their silly school chatter bouncing off of the tile flooring. when i think of starbucks, or any coffee shop for that matter, i think of a nice, relaxing joint to sip some coffee and get my ponder on. good luck with that at this location. have to use the bathroom? get in line, it's gonna be a while. these kids must hold their piss all day, and then queue up here or some shit. the lines to get into the restroom were a kin to what one might see at a night club, only with children. there is an elementary and middle school in close proximity, and apparently the kids all scramble to starbucks when they get out. maybe they should put a play area out front with slides and swings. when the hell did elementary and middle school kids start drinking coffee anyways?

i love starbucks but this is not one that i would recommend if you can avoid it. the one good thing is that there is a drive through, not many people like getting out to get coffee so it's nice that it has one. there is plenty of seating indoors and i think when it's nice outside there is also outdoor seating. they don't really make my coffee that great, they never have. if you get frapps or lattes only then it's not that bad.. you can't mess those up. like in my other reviews- caramel iced coffee with soy and it sucked almost every time. i stopped going here when the last 3 times they gave me the soy to put in myself, um, somethings wrong there. i was really unhappy with that. the people aren't that great or memorable so wouldn't stop by here again.
bryce (i believe his name was) was such a sweet barista while i was running late for the day. deff enjoy coming to this location!
unfriendly. second time visiting this starbucks....different employees but same unwelcoming environment.

i have had nothing but satisfaction at this new location. quick, efficient, never messed up an order. today however, is a different story. the drinks were satisfactory, sure. but what came through the straw of one of our drinks was appalling. what i thought was maybe a remnant of blueberry was in fact a piece of rubber. it was the most unsanitary thing i have seen from a starbucks. i sincerely hope this was a one off and nobody else at this store encountered such. we will not be coming back to this particular starbucks that is just minutes from my home.

arrived @ 5:20 pm. happy the wifi works in the parking lot on this cold day. seats outside are not very socially distanced. all inside closed down. understandable. very friendly ladies behind the counter. not sure if one is in training because two don't need to be standing around just chatting it up with each other ignoring customers. these two behind the counter just laughing it up, talking about up north and "some other girl the other day" and i can hear all these personal conversations over the music that i assume was playing. i was greeted by them so i'm acknowledged but they don't trace where i've been or how long i was there. one speedy worker making all the drinks - no help and they may like it like that and then the 1 barista working drive thru. i stood in the store for almost 20minutes waiting on my order. i asked the ladies, who ignored me since the initial hello, where my order was because i have been standing over there waiting. i was pacing after my order said ready but i watched the barista making all the drinks. i never received an apology just laughter- more like giggles and reply of "it got mixed up with drive thru, haha". i didn't find it funny that over 10minutes after the app said my order was ready and no one asked me why i'm waiting around. very disappointing. most other starbucks bend over backwards to make sure customers are happy. if one is in training then she is not learning the starbucks way from her trainer. luckily this is not my normal starbucks because if so you just made we wanna walk into sip85 instead of this because that experience was so stressful. i wish management good luck because those two employees earn the giggle and gossip award but not hard working. most displeased

stopped in harrah's casino for quick a gamble and popped in starbucks for a quick drink. staff very nice and patient as my mom could not remember her order and kept switching it up on the guy. i got a sugar cookie which was pretty good for a packaged item. mom and cheryl got teas and a caramel frappuccino which were to their liking. i got a vente aqua polar freeze which is the best drinks they make :-) a.k.a. iced water.

sigh.....for such a great location this starbucks borders on mediocre. the wifi signal kept stopping and starting, by far the worst wifi i've encountered in any starbucks. staff was somewhat friendly, could use some more classes on proper public service, other than that nothing outstanding at all. if you're in this part of the city and want a drink, sure stop in. if you're looking for a place to do some surfing or working online while you relax with a coffee, stay home. worst wifi ever.

starbucks is (supposed) to be starbucks. i'm not sure why they can't seem to follow the recipe that they were trained to follow. i don't order anything extra or special and about 50% of the time, it seems that they've added 1 too many pumps to the drink. i should just stick with my neighborhood starbucks.
very busy location in the glen eagle shopping center. great barista who knows how to make a cappuccino.

i have to comment on the drive-thru service folks... i continue to have the unfortunate pleasure of being served by 3 ladies who seem to dislike working at starbucks. never been to a starbucks where customer service was just disappointing! and boring. i might as well been served at a dmv. many of us enjoy the idea of ordering from starbucks and having us a fancy drink being served by a friendly upbeat employee. i continue to be robbed of such experience. please switch out the drive thru ladies!

wonderful location. the baristas are super friendly and go out of their way to help you. the space is really open and bright and the whole place is always spotless. the bathrooms have always been in good shape when i've visited, which they get bonus points for. they're efficient at taking orders and making drinks and there's never a long wait, even if the drive thru is busy too. definitely love the patio, which is big and has seating and even offers an outdoor electrical outlet, if you want to get work done outside. that's pretty unheard of. i seriously haven't met an unfriendly barista here, they're always stocked up on food and have plenty of merch in case you need a last minute hostess gift. i would go out of my way to choose this location over others. starbucks same quality drinks with the added benefit of a super friendly vibe.

i am not a starbucks aficionado but i've been to this location a couple of times. the baristas are very friendly and the noise level isn't too bad -- probably my favorite thing compared to the typical starbucks.
quick drive through always a great coffee or lattes! only thing i see is that they need to keep their outside seating clean!!!

i love starbucks, i love what they do, i love their mission, vision, and values, and this starbucks is no exception, super friendly, clean, efficient i'm outside here sitting in the lite rain in the morning enjoying the day, and a young lady from starbucks comes out and wipes down all the tables to make it a more user-friendly experience......... way to think about the customer experience.

went there with 4 friends and ordered 5 espresso. each one served in two cups with lids resulting in the waste of 10 none recyclable cups and 5 plastic lids! starbucks definitely gives zero f*** about the environment! thank you from the next generations!
very nice place with great service. definitely one of my favorite starbucks stores in center city philly.
great starbucks. not much more to say then that. plenty of seating to get work done, very very friendly staff, and drinks are always made to perfection.

this starbucks is a hit or miss with staff. some can be super rude and don't listen. for example, just left and asked for light ice.. pretty sure they put extra. but sometimes when you get the right barista, they make it perfect. joshua, i think is his name, is pretty awesome and one other younger guy, but i can't remember his name. :/

by far one of the more people friendly and welcoming starbucks i've been to. i'm not a consecutive customer but when i have some extra change or i can't stand another day of trying to choke down office kitchen coffee i'll make a stop in. what really stands out about this place other than the delicious coffee is the gentleman who manages the establishment unfortunately i haven't caught his name but i appreciate the fact he remembers how to spell my name (it's a bit off from how it's pronounced) especially since i'm not an every day customer. it's those little insignificant personal touches that go a long way and are truly appreciated.

i have been in starbucks all over the country. media pennsylvania is a funky little fair trade town nestled into the county seat with its courthouse and lawyer class. the starbucks lacks an identity. it is on the outskirts of the town center. it feels less like a coffee house than a strip mall fast food coffee shop. plus the sour smell of old bitter coffee hanging in the air here is killing me. i get the feeling that this place is open only because it can, and no other reason. too bad. starbucks is such a great brand.

due to their south street location, this starbucks is usually more crowded than dead. it's not the biggest starbucks ever, but there's space upstairs, and the staff are fine. the place is clean enough - including the bathrooms - at least when i was there. it'll do if you have a hankering for starbucks, otherwise i recommend hitting up a local coffee shop instead.

a buch of racist ppl work here. walked in no one greeted me. a white lady walkes in every one (baristas)told her welcome. i was next in line the barista/cashier finished the order with the customer told them thank you, turned around and start3d to make them their order. i wad standing there for 5 min made eye contact with two other baristas and felt like they were looking right thru me. the barista/cashier that was making the order to the two customers that had just paid turned around looked at me and loked away. damn never coming to this starbucks again. been a gold member since 2014
