favorite place to get coffee from. but yesterday this place left a bad taste in my mouth. as i was downing my coffee there was a large piece of plastic in coffee that i almost choked on. didn't go back or bring up to management because i do not like confrontation.

*no drive through, the closest starbucks with one is a few miles south at 10002 n dale mabry hwy. i frequent this starbucks to sit and get work done. the staff is always friendly. it may take a bit to get a table inside on which to work, they only have six tables: 2 four-seater high tops, 2 four-seater regular height tables and 2 two-seater regular height tables (i'm here right now lol.). they have outdoor seating on two sides of the building and all of it is under an awning. i also put in a fair amount of orders here through the mobile app. unfortunately they don't have a drive through and since this location is in a strip plaza parking may be a tad challenging if you're trying to just run in and grab a mobile order drink from the designated counter area for them. it's not that bad though. the bathrooms have been clean here every time i've used them. well, the mens'. can't speak for the womens' (would i tell you if i could?). overall a solid 4/5 starbucks. a drive through would probably make it 5/5 but short of a location change it ain't gonna happen.

my experience is that they have always been friendly. usually crowded which makes it rough to get drinks in a timely fashion. i do find it crowded in the store, but again, that comes with the territory of being in a "coffee shop" setting. it definitely is nice and comfy if that is the type of setting you want. there is outdoor seating, but with fl you can only use that every so often - when it is not super hot, not raining, and not filled with some type of bug flying around! overall, this place is really good. they make good coffee and other beverages and the staff is nice :)

this starbucks location is a franchisee located in the bridgeton target. i frequent this starbucks when i am on a target run and unfortunately it's the only one located on this long busy business strip. there is an obvious difference between this franchisee starbucks and other company owned starbucks. they never have all the same pastries, wraps and salads that you'll find at a company owned starbucks. they also receive limited quantities for seasonal drink additives so when they run out, they run out and they aren't able to restock. getting a consistent drink here is really hit or miss. the associates here are not trained like starbucks baristas; so one day they may be stocking, one day they may be cashiers and the next day they may be here at the starbucks counter. over the past year i've become familiar with at least two ladies that work here often and when i come for an afternoon run and they are extremely helpful. i no longer have to state how many pumps of toffee-nut or mocha i would like in my drink (keep in mind you never have to do that at a company owned store because they are trained on proper usage and only deviate per customer requests). this location has lots of the regular starbucks swag and seasonal coffee. now you can get the 10-cent discount on your drink purchase when you bring in a travel coffee mug. all in all, in a pinch and needing a legal addictive stimulant - this place will do.

the furniture looks like it came out of an old college dorm. not uncomfortable, worn out, uninviting. environment is cold and warehouse like. wifi doesn't work. i ask starbucks person and they say it doesn't work for them either. have you tried fixing it? i ended up going to one on 96th street. much better.
slowest airport starbucks. i dont know if they're shorthanded.. but they are slow. if your baristas cant keep up in a high volume location, get new people or more help. pathetic.

this is my cheers! the place where everybody knows my name and is happy that i came. the place where my caffeine fix is handled promptly and with a smile. i have been coming to this particular location for about four years now. although i change up my drink order daily, the baristas are fast. many times the line is long but they work through orders fast and with a smile. i do tend to avoid the starbucks with drive thru, it always seems the cars take priority, so this location works for me! a big shout out to danyelle, tress, jamie and jeremy. without you, my good mornings would just be plain ordinary mornings.
great service during covid! drive though was super fast and easy. i ordered like normal, at the first stop. then i paid and got my drink at the second stop. girl was friendly.

we went to this starbucks on a tuesday around 9 am. while they were busy we waited a long time for sandwiches that were just reheated. my husband ordered coffee and sandwiches. the coffee was ready right away. he then ordered a couple of more sandwiches. the first order of sandwiches took 10 minutes. the second order took 30 minutes! ridiculous!

they cater to what you want. i typically order a skinny vanilla latte but with only one pump of vanilla syrup... so it won't be too sweet. i really like the ham and cheese croissant square when i need breakfast on the go and their green tea is like no other! the only aspect i don't like are the long lines. it doesn't matter what time of day it is there is going to be a line. they're really not that bad though... i'm just spoiled from living in tucson my whole life.

speaking from a first hand pov, the coffee specialist lisa a. is not to be trusted. she has stolen from multiple starbucks locations here in long island, ny. she has spat in customers drinks if they appear "rude" or "cheap." not to mention, she's a tad looney ..... you don't know everything, lady. please hire somebody who is not only trustworthy, but will keep the good name to the town of zionsville.

their matcha latte with coconut milk was overly sugary and sweet. i asked the barista to add hot water to help dilute the sweetness. she obliged and also informed me that all the sugar is in the matcha powder. her recommendation is to ask for one less scoop of matcha for next time. the downside is that you'll also lose flavor. my recommendation is to find a coffee shop that does not have sugar in their matcha powder. i wish they had oat or cashew milk!
love the starbucks, the staff is super friendly they always know what i want. green tea latte soy no classic yum!!!!!!

majority of the time i am going to starbucks is on the way to work in the morning...it's early...not always the best mood...the people at starbucks truly do brighten your day. from the moment you order in the drive through with the excitement in their voice and even the small talk they make at the window!

drove to this location hoping to get my coffee at the beginning of our trip, and they decided not to show up to work! online it said they open at 5am, here it was close to 7 with no indication they'd be in anytime soon.

there were two registers. when it was my turn, i walked to the second one and he was preoccupied with something else. so now, i was in the other line, with no cashier. the other cashier didn't bother to ask me anything as 3 people skipped over me to order. i would've spoken up if i wasn't sick and lost my voice. i work in customer service/food. speak up and do better.
never get an order right. hot not cold big not small not sweet too sweet. never fail to get it wrong. remind me not to come back. cute inside but coffee is a disaster.

discourteous: lacking courtesy. this starbucks has mastered the ability to hire discourteous employees. unless you're a hipster with skinny jeans and sporting black rimmed glasses, your chances of getting good service are very slim to none! i've been to this particular starbucks a handful of times. it's in the old 'hood of lockerbie, and i like to reminisce the fun times i had there before the neighborhood changed into hipster central. {frown face} this starbucks is conveniently located on my way outta town, and will admit that it is one of the nicer (non cookie cutter) stores. the food is nicely displayed, and the coffee has always been above average. please note: i am not a huge fan of starbucks coffee. i only buy coffee here because of the convenience factor. this morning was the final straw. i hate foo foo coffee that has whipped cream, sprinkles, and all of the useless garbage on top of it. i ordered a seasonal coffee, and politely requested that they withold the foo foo stuff. the disgruntled employee rolled her eyes, and shrugged when i asked for a copy of my credit card receipt. shame on me for being detailed with my finances. when i was waiting for my coffee and banana bread, the mule of a woman, who on a dark night could be mistaken for a man, literally threw my straw on the counter, and slid my coffee, in which it almost fell off the counter, if i didn't react swiftly and catch it. she responded {deep voice} with not a thank you and have a nice day, but with "oops." won't return!

okay, so currently this starbucks has low ratings... forget the bad reviews because they have definitely made enough change for me to be granting them 5 stars! first off all i'm here and it's about to close and there is not one open seat!... college students dig hanging here, which if you don't know common sense means it's a rad spot! i was greated with the cutest and most fun baristas who totally infected me with the laughter and happy vibe they both exuded so well! my night is so happy now and i feel like i will be coming back specifically to this starbucks because of the exceptional customer service skills that lexi (super blonde babe!) and angela (sexy dark hair babe!) they go above and beyond and have the best time ever when they are at work which is so contagious and bam! this is the starbucks to go to! located next to other cool shops too! and i only mentioned two employees because they specifically stood out for me... all the employees are great from my perspective. happy starbucks!!!
after dark, starbucks corporate's new policies have turned this place into the mos eisley catina .... (look it up) my kinda place ...

had a very nice experience here this morning. we were pleasantly surprised they were open at 5 am. great coffee, nice breakfast sandwich of egg white and turkey bacon on an english muffin. very sweet young lady answered my menu questions and was quite charming. you will find them by gate e 16.

honestly the worst service ever. does it make sense to take someone else's order when they're behind you? i dont care if they are a regular at that location, its first come first serve. not first serve regulars then others.
free ice water, it's got my vote. sure, i know that's a policy of all starbucks but this is a valuable thing whilst traveling -- where bottles of water are often charged at disneyland prices.

fatima jackson, fatima jackson... the supervisor at this starbucks is the most inconsiderate supervisor there is .. i ordered a strawberry açaí drink for my daughter and asked for it to be blended and as if i asked her for her soul, she just got defensive , saying we don't do blended here.. i dont know what tipped her , is it my skin color, my accent or my arizona shirt !!... the lady before me in the line ordered a drink and substituted everything for everything and that was ok but she had the correct skin color and accent.. unfortunately for me i did not... fatima jackson you shoukd not be a supervisor

i go to this starbucks at least 4 times a week! they never are very happy! they are always out of drink trays and drink stoppers! today they told me they don't get drink stoppers anymore! the coffee was running down both cups and they didn't offer to clean off! i will not being spending my money at this starbucks anymore! very rude and not fully stocks! at $4.80 a drink they need to have the supplies!

the mcmullen booth starbucks is my primetime chill out spots. it's one of the most spacious and least crowded that i've been to, which is why it is close to heaven for readers. the interior is decorated in the rich, warm colors that starbucks is known for, but unlike most of the cafes this one has two long, wooden tables for multiple patrons to sit and enjoy their java. this is perfect for groups, and personally my husband & i love coming here as a date night for some drinks and board games. if you're looking for comfort, they also have leather chairs that are soft enough to sink into and stay for a while. when i'm not bringing the hubby for date night, you can find me with a kindle in one hand and a tall caramel macchiato in the other(which i order with nonfat milk to make it less than 200 calories. yum!). the onnnnnly gripe i have, is that sometimes the sounds of their appliances can be quite noisy, but who's ever heard of a quiet blender? all in all, this is a wonderful place to come sip a tasty drink and kick back. the environment is calm & cool, the baristas are quirky & perky, and of course the drinks are always delish.
i don't like the borritos because mcdonalds has better donuts

horrible experience at this starbucks. i came in during a time when they were carting inventory in from a truck, but the space to do that was in between where the line starts. i was in line to make my order when the employee harshly demanded that i move out of the way. i would have been fine if he had politely told me the situation, but he literally hollered at me from behind the counter and was walking around multiple times (cleaning counters? replacing cups?) before even taking my order. really? it was a slow evening which is why i expected it to not take too long to receive my drink. took a total of 2 minutes being hollered at and being told to move away while being stared at, 5 minutes just to place my order and 3 minutes for my drink to be ready. the lag time because this employee was slow and rude was just not worth it.

dang it - deleted my last review and this one is simply not going to have the panache that the last one did. basically, i love sbux but i don't like it when the baristas are stuffy and flippant - these guys are so incredibly nice. i love them all. they are also completey on point in the morning, and travel at warp speed to deliver my iced coffee lt. cream with a smile and a wink. i attached a map to show the best way to get in and out of this parking lot, without being interupted by the drive thru - basically you need to drive around the block (head north on martin ave off of broadway) and you won't get blocked by the drive thru traffic on the broadway street-side, if you are trying to park... thank you smiley happy people i see each day, you guys are awesome.

i've been to several starbucks locations since recently moving to the area, & hands down, i'm giving this one an automatic 5 stars simply due to being the speediest, fastest, quickest - however you want to put it - starbucks drive-thru that i've ever been to. there was a long line to the road even, & i was in & out in 5 minutes from start to finish, including drive-thru time!!

everyday i drive past river and campbell and drive the extra distance to both this location at wetmore or the sbux on stone for quality service. the morning crew here is probably the most cohesive team i've seen, completing beverages quickly and accurately, and it only took the team a few weeks to memorize my drink and my name. impressive, especially considering that at a majority of the other locations the baristas still look at me blankly while holding their sharpies, even after 6-7 years. i really feel valued as a customer at this location, and that's what makes all the difference.

this starbucks is on point when the manager is there, when he's not there it's crap. my drink is consistently made wrong every single time! i've even began asking them to make it right while i'm placing my order so i don't have the line backed up. but the moment i don't mention it, it's back to being made wrong! but this time today i was beyond mad. i made my order for the white chocolate mocha frapp. it was once again made wrong and i told the women in the drive through that it was made wrong and i've had this convo multiple times with her about my drink being wrong. so she had the guy make a new one. mean while she opened the window back up and asked me if i wanted the messed up one as well as the new one. so i said sure i'll take it home. i took it and waited for my drink. then the nasty attitude girl ( not sure her name) came to the window and asked for the drink back so she could dump it even though i was just given it by another employee. she is always nasty no matter if i go inside or out. personally don't think she's a good fit for starbucks! i only come to this starbucks cause it's on my way home or to work. i'm gonna have to find another place to go, i'm am disappointed in starbucks employees! the morning staff are better than the night crew!

super slow service regardless whether it's 6:15 am on a tuesday morning or 10 am on a saturday morning. 15 minutes in the drive thru is completely unacceptable! i come here because it's right by my house but it's so inconvenient because they're always so slow!

by far the worst service i've seen at any starbucks in the world!.. i travel the world for work and go to europe, latin america, even asia, and get starbucks at locations that are 5-6 times busier than this one. but don't ever recall waiting more than 3 minutes. at this location though... they make sure you spend a few minutes in line before you order your drink and approximately 15 minutes on a slow day! what do i usually order? just a trenta very berry hibiscus refresher... awful! just awful.
amazing staff, great view in the outside seating area. i love the people and pet watching here, it's like my second home when visiting my parents in the area :)

this starbucks location is my favorite in all of tucson. after moving here, i've been testing out different ones to see where i like best for all day studying. all i needed was 3 things: opens late, check! open tables, check! outlets available, check! there are usually always tables available and plenty of outlets for usage. it is also one of the later closing starbucks around here, closing at 10pm mon-sat and 9:30pm on sundays. the patrons who go here are also very pleasant. dock one star because baristas fail to ignore me and other customers at the register when they are tied up on many occasions. it is somewhat understandable because they have to juggle in-store customers and drive-thru. however, employees should be trained to acknowledge the customer for waiting.

extremely rude older lady with blonde hair running the drive thru. argued with me over making my drink wrong after she made it wrong twice, and i watched her fill my pink drink half with water. won't be coming back here again. management you need to take a look a your employee.

i've been here several times. ice cold air conditioning. clean restrooms. ample outdoor seating in the shade. appears to be brisk and efficient with the drive-thru. full compliment of indoor seating. efficient and friendly baristas. they get my highest bucks rating for treating me well. i ordered a drink that they made correctly--changed my mind (way to sweet), asked them for a new one, and they made me a different drink with no fuss. yes, yes, bucks is supposed to do this. but the execution here was very professional.

today i went for my usual coffee and when i went up to the register to order, i found that i had left my debit card at home. the cashier was kind enough to say "don't worry about it we'll take care of it. " and gave me my drink for free... she totally made my day! and because of her i will always recommend this place. :) thank you

the service at this starbucks is very poor. they care more about their hair than providing good service. they forgot my order after taking my money and after 10 min wait made it like it was my problem.

so they got my size wrong and the drink wrong and no espresso shot when i paid for it and not double blended when that's what i asked for. really annoying and no receipt...? very rude and seemed unorganized. won't be going back anytime soon, dutch bros it is.

rude and bad service. left my drink on the table and never called out my name. even when i stood in front of the girl she continued to ignore and failed to mention my drink was waiting. i hated spending a single cent at *$ but got a gift card to burn. would not go back to this store.

my friends like starbucks food. i am neutral. they got me cheese bacon egg croissant: it was hot and every single item was good quality. i am giving five stars based on my food, not the physical location. nb: previous reviews would have made me turn away.

i have been to this starbucks on several occasions and have not had very good customer service. maybe it is because i am going thru the drive thru? i always feels like i am annoying them or they are doing me some huge favor. my drink was consistent with other starbucks and service was done in a timely manner.
while i usually prefer the drive up locations, it's nice to stop in and chat with the barista's.

slow drive through service. sunday morning - wanted ti stop for a coffee before church. drive through set up won't let you leave - we've been stuck here for 21 min. please fix this! finally got to the window and the order was wrong. drinks, food, everything was wrong. had to pull up and wait for them to bring out.
this starbucks was ok. small, cramped and people lounge everywhere so there was no where to sit. the staff seemed gossipy and distracted. not my favorite one! i will stop here on an as needed basis.

this starbucks location is the gold standard. i have been to many others in nashville and in small towns and far bigger cities, and this west end location provides the friendliest and speediest service and most consistent quality. have i gotten a wrong order once or twice in my history of going there at least 2x/week for years and years... yes, maybe so, but really... what do we expect, fellow nashvillians, if our vehicles are lined up for a block and half on west end and we get thru the line in ten minutes.

horrible customer service!! literally stood there for a minute while 5 employees all talked to each other and the cashier lady just stared right through me! i wasn't sure wether to order or leave. then she finally came back to earth and asked me if i wanted to order. pretty sure that's why i'm standing there?! got my drink and the barista made zero eye contact and didn't thank me. as a past starbucks employee myself, they didn't do anything to standard and was the worst customer service i've encountered in a long time! will not be back!

you always expect it to be bad at an on-campus starbucks but this one might just give others a run for their money. i have had extensive waits whether it be 30 mins for a cold brew that i have preordered through tapingo (without cream, might i add) or for some hot tea which i don't get until the five people in line after me have already received their lattes and frappes. the staff is also very rude and have a lack of understanding. do yourself a favor and skip this location.. order ahead to the library location or take a walk to university instead. anything. is. better. than. this!!!

starbucks has been my favorite coffee place since its first invention. i love this store and it is probably my favorite one though i've never hit a bad starbucks. try it if you love starbucks coffee, and i promise you that you will not be disappointed!
very poor experience at this location. no menu, staff unfamiliar with tea options. all items i requested were unavailable at 11am on monday such as teas and croissants.

i always mobile order for this location. my drink is always ready when i arrive which is a huge plus. sometimes even when you order the same drink at starbucks it will vary in taste slightly each time. however this location has always been consistent and my drinks are always great. today my barista wrote "have an amazing day!" i have never had anyone write a message on my cup but i have had a rough week so it was very inspiring. great location for mobile orders!

the staff was obnoxious. every time i come in here they are extremely loud and have no regard for any of their customers. i do not feel like listening to the staff sing. it seems as if they have no managers on duty. it's unprofessional. very annoying to try to concentrate while the employees are yelling at the top of their lungs. if it were other customers being loud around me i would understand. but the staff.......

not sure what is going on lately, but since the new manager and new weekend staff it is a hot mess here!!! soooo slow and uninterested ... night and say difference from the excellence if pat, mel and the weekday staff....hate coming on on weekends :(

i truly wanted to give a higher rating, because i love starbucks, but this location definitely needs some extra care. when visiting the inside of the store, it's quaint size is comfortable, but the issue is, i don't think the employees are comfortable. i've never seen an establishment so unsanitary. there were miscellaneous liquids across their entire workspace, and not one person stopped to wipe anything down. i was pretty disgusted. not to mention, i waited over 15 minutes for a single white chocolate mocha. i love starbucks, but if you like imagining your beverage being prepared in a clean workspace, please go through the drive thru only. mind you, the only reason i went inside this time, was because my order is always incorrect when i order via the drive through. this will be my last visit here... i rather get a coffee from mcdonald's, two storefronts down.

excellent starbucks, i really enjoy the atmosphere and my drink was made with care and is fantastic. great place to get work done and have a cup of coffee! the staff was particularly attentive and welcomed me with a smile.

i liked this starbucks! you can't beat the convenience of the location if you're staying at the gsr - i like that it's even an option! they have the covid social distancing stickers on the floor to enforce guidelines, all employees were wearing their masks. i definitely didn't have any issues with this place! our drinks came out quickly and just right. my one complaint is that since it's so loud, and with masks and plexi glass between you and the barista, it's really hard to hear them when you're giving your order and probably vice versa. other than that, great experience.
nice roomy starbucks. plenty of seating. friendly service. consistent drinks. clean and tidy.

store is supposed to be open till 8 pm. i came in for coffee at 7:25 pm only to find that they stop brewing coffee at 7. i've never had a starbucks tell me i couldn't get any coffee before. they didn't even offer a pour over. i've been to starbucks at 7:50 pm and was still able to get a coffee but not at this location. i'm on my way to the one a couple of blocks away.
i love how friendly the staff is. went here multiple times already and the experience is always good. they serve fast and accurate. though parking lot is quite small.

we come to this starbucks every time we are in town it's always a great atmosphere but, today it was not. i'm assuming it's the store manager not wearing her apron and has the nastiest attitude i've ever seen. she's giving the most horrible customer service to every single customer apparently because the morning shift did not stock prior to them leaving. she's letting out her frustrations at the customers. i ordered my drink at 1.31p and just now got it at 1.54p and it was made wrong. when i mentioned it to her all she said was "my bad" no i'm sorry no i'll remake for you, nothing she just turned around threw the drink away and had another associate make my drink. this is not what you should expect when walking into any starbucks.

the nicest starbucks i've ever been to. also the only starbucks with wine and been. the cashier told me there are only 10 starbucks with beer and wine license in florida. also the cashier sarah was extremely nice and cheerful. and the barista got my name right

this place is always clean. they just added new tables and chairs and makes it easier to camp out than in the past. the new seating is great for small groups or for students who don't mind sharing a table. the employees here are always friendly and their do great work. i think the "old" reviews are outdated as it seems that this staff has addressed the issues. the best part...they open before the other stores in the area which is a big help to me.

worst customer service from rude employees. we waited in the drive-thru for the three cars in front of us to order their coffees and when we got to the speaker we were rudely told by a girl on the other end that "we're closed, and we can't take your order". not only were we not served, but we were made to wait in the drive-thru behind all the other cars getting their orders because there's no way out of the drive-thru once you're in it!!!!
i love starbucks!!! i think that's all i have to say about it.. the location, however, is very convenient and the service is great, friendly and fast. definitely recommend.

just left starbucks on hazelwood... the barista greeted me with "what you need?"... i said, "excuse me?" and he repeated it... i'm not necessarily tripping on that, but where was the greeting? i'm sorry, but if i'm paying 5 cents, or $5... greeting the customer should always come first...not going to stop me from my hot beverage run... but i'm not ya homeboy, i'm your customer....

truly the best starbucks i have been to. they are always so sweet and understanding in there. you can tell they really care about the community with all of the programs they have going on in store. they give their grounds out for free for those who compost and have a sanitary product donation drive too. the store is always decorated for the season and has a pride flag year round. i love you market street starbucks!
the flies! please please please put up a fly trap or something inside. love this place, but it's annoying.

nice drive-thru which was great when staying in the area on vacation with our dog. also, looks like this location has nice outdoor seating, but we didn't get to use it since we were on the run to see family each time we stopped in here.

terrible cold brew. i ordered a trente because i needed the boost to get stuff done this morning. i watched them fill the cup 1/3 of the way and they handed it back to me severely watered down with melted ice less than 30 seconds later, which leads me to believe they filled the rest with water... super upsetting considering i spent $5 on a coffee.

depends on the person who is making coffee, it can be super nice or it can be to sweet. i usually order my caramel frappuccino from people whom i know there. but it's very chill place for chatting and enjoy your coffee
huge starbucks!! nice and cool inside. clean and very pleasant workers! fast service!!

least favorite sbux. the staff looks at me like i'm crazy/dirty/cheap when i ask for a refill on my iced coffee. i never know if they will be charging me for the cream in my americano. i no longer order bar drinks here because it totally depends who is making them. my order is right half the time i go thru the drive up window. i'm ignored for a few mins at the counter when i walk in b/c everyone is focused on the drive up. yup. i think that's about it.

omg. this place keeps getting worse. truth. i have been waiting in line for 10 minutes. i am serious. if my eyes weren't burning with fire bc i only had 4 hrs of sleep i would have pulled off. this is ridiculous. officially done with this location

this a great starbucks location! i have been through the drive thru several times, and the service is always friendly and efficient. today i had a rare opportunity to enjoy my drink in store, and the staff was just as friendly, including answering a few questions i had about my drink, and still very efficient. i also appreciated that there is a lot of seating in this location, which made it a great place to enjoy my drink. a nice location all around!

i don't mind the long lines, but starbucks is known for friendly service and this starbucks was anything but! the starbucks where i live (new york city) are always friendly and helpful. i would expect more out of a starbucks in a city that is supposed to be the friendliest. my order was wrong and the woman (ecasia) was really rude. i was shocked. try a different starbucks.
typical starbucks, nice location and nice enough staff. drinks are overpriced so i don't come here often but i love the passion fruit lemonade.

liberal party rejects! well below standards in customer service all served up w/ a side of attitude that puts patrons off. the conversations the employees share about other customers is such a sad thing to hear.

absolute great staff here at this starbucks location. they were extremely busy and got everyone in and out. i have been turned on recently to their cold drew coffee. great staff, location, and timeliness to get you in and out all adds up to a perfect score in my mind. looking forward to traveling back this location and sitting outside next time.
needs more staff but otherwise handles the customer flow very well. very small store so it gets crowded. would opt for dunkin' donuts if you don't want to deal with the line (i did)
i have gotten my usual vente americano here they did a fine job. makes me want to grab some every time before i shop.

i am just about done with starbucks. i don't drink coffee& i love all the drink options that they offer but the price per quality served are not adding up. i did order ahead& while they were busy you would think that 20 minutes would be enough time so just be able to walk in& get my drinks. that was not the case. then when i did get my drinks there was a lap in my first and second drink being made. 3 other customers were helped in this time. the drink i got was a cinnamon dolce crème, just steamed milk with cinnamon dolce syrup, whipped cream& a little cinnamon on top. the last 3 times i got it my cup has been half full when paying 450 for a 16 oz drink you want all of those 16 ozs. also & this is nothing of the staff but the parking situation& getting in& out of the lot is a total nightmare.

i walked into this place and it smelled gross like spoiled milk, and i couldn't help but notice the flies flying around inside by the counter. i ordered my latte with soy and they said they did not have soy milk. i realize they probably have the market cornered, but i don't think that makes it okay to drop the ball. customers have a certain level of expectation for the starbucks brand.

slow af (backed-up mobile orders) this starbucks is beautiful and in a very good location. however, i would not recommend going to this location if you're in a hurry or on your way to work. i'm a huge fan of mobile orders, but my order wasn't ready until 30 minutes after i had placed it this morning (when you're on your way to work, this is a lifetime). i was one of ~15 other people waiting for their orders when i got there. other people waiting were voicing that they were going to be late to meetings/appointments, mobile order tickets/printouts were going missing and people weren't in the queue, etc. not a good experience. wish i would have gone to 96th & meridian, they are much quicker.
easily the most expensive starbucks in town. yay! the employees are nice. so that gets an extra star. the iced tea i pay $3.50 for everywhere else in town is $4.55 here.
the drinks are awesome and well done! only complaint is that alot of college kids go there so theres never anywhere to sit!

i live right down the street from this location so it's my go to right before work. for the most part the drive thru rolls by pretty quick for how long the line can be. the drinks i get are spot on. the customer service lately is horrible. the last couple times i went, the employees at the drive thru work with no integrity. for example, i paid with a $10 bill total was about $7 and some cents. she only gave me back my cents. in my head i'm like this doesn't add up. so i asked for my receipt and she says absolutely. i'm like you knew you gave me this amount of change right, you owe me two dollars. and she doesn't hesitate at all to give me my two dollars. so she sighs and acting all mad because i caught her. seriously? i was in disbelief. i know it's only two dollars but that's not right. next one is a guy, paid with a card this time because i wasn't trying to get jipped! i asked politely, can you do me a favor and heat this up (muffin). he rolled his eyes at me.. so here i am waiting while i hear this guy mock me with his other co workers.... ugh. how upsetting. i was furious! never stepping foot in here again.

i always go to this starbucks before work and love the drive through option and the sleek look. however; getting in and out of there is insane!! gulf to bay is one of the busiest highways, & without having a light, it makes it far more difficult. i hope the move works out for them, but i will not be going to this one before work anymore.
the two guys who helped me were super nice and quick, stirred my coffee !! which no other one has ever done!! so i definitely will be returning for sure. clean, quiet environment. enjoyed it.

dear starbucks, thank you for living on the corner of 9th and south street. you are dependable and comfortable and you boost morale around this part of town. most importantly you make up for all of the god-awful small coffee houses in the area that i always get suckered into trying. i salute you for being consistently decent. never change. but seriously, stop changing your menu and make pumpkin flavor a year round thing. alright, peppermint can join the year round bandwagon too. love always, -a devote non-coffee drink drinker.

i've been patronizing this starbucks every now and then ever since they opened. working on the "northwest" end of the cbd near the main post office, i am only a block from the hyatt, and often find myself cutting through the buildings on my way to poydras street. every time i do, i often stop at this full service starbucks located inside the hyatt. as far as food and coffee goes, this place isn't any different from any stand-alone locations. however, it has a fairly large seating area and free wi-fi. couple that with an endless stream of tourists in the hyatt, this place is the perfect spot to sit & people watch. (if you're into that sort of thing). since i usually cruise through here on my limited lunch break, i never take advantage of the seating or wifi. however, if i had the time or if i was a guest at the hyatt, i know i'd be down here with my i-pad, just surfing, chatting and taking in the sights. and drinking a refreshing beverage of course.

good new starbucks, nice set up, but terrible staff. i was there when shannon was running the crew. i was going to stay for a few hrs studying but left early. she kept yelling about the pretzels smelling like dog. shouting instructions at other employees. complaining about the floor. talking really loud in general. singing loudly. really annoying. made the shop and the rest of the staff look like a mcdonalds. and it wasn't that busy and she couldn't get orders right.

surprisingly the store is actually pretty big. i love that they keep the restrooms locked but also very nice tidy neat and clean. their staff are very friendly and patient. i love that the customers are also patient in return considering we all still have to mind the covid 19 health measures. my order was taken very easily and payment method was my starbucks app on my phone. i had no problems. my order was made quickly and correctly. the only thing that was a little confusing for the staff was that i had ordered a drink that typically came with black tea but instead i requested their passion tea or passion tango tea. no matter, they were able to figure it out and make my drink very quickly. delicious.

this is a very nice starbucks! it's right in the mall and has a system set up for the line to avoid any confusion. i found the staff to be very helpful and welcoming. i visited here after the christmas rush was over and they didn't seem frazzled or burnt out like many retail employees do at that time of year.

i love this starbucks location and staff! they are always happy to take my order and have genuine smiles for customers. no matter how i want my drink they listen and have great suggestions when i want to try something new. the store is clean with a nice atmosphere inside to sit and enjoy.

this is our favorite sbux. a wide diversity of staff. they always have a smile for me and my wife. manager's beard grows and grows... night crew are cleaning machines. place is spotless in the morning. always gets my drink correct but my wife changes her order often and they always give her exactly what she orders.
usually starbucks is always spot on, but today my boyfriend and i bit into a cold breakfast sandwich. you could imagine how happy we were.

friendly staff. convenient location... often there is a bit of a waitmy only real compliant is they run out of cold brew 60% of the time... why not plan and make more cold brew? i feel so petty complaining when there are way bigger problems in the world today... this just seems like an easy fix. update- unfortunately i spend a lot of time waiting for orders or drinks here. i usually get plain coffee, cold brew, or green tea but somehow there is usually at least a 10 minute wait... the staff is super nice and it's close to home so i keep coming back but after todays 12 minute wait with 1 person ahead of me ... i think it is probably faster to drive to a nearby starbucks or coffee shop. since it's been a consistent issue i'll go somewhere else and try again in a few months. maybe something will change :-)

no caption needed this was in a iced caramel macchiato look like someone shaved in it. had to wait almost a month to talk to a manager on the phone because no one was in at the time and only wanted to give me one free drink.

this starbucks is located inside of publix, which is in the same plaza as another starbucks. competition between the two starbucks has been created since the opening of the one inside of publix. they offer everything as any other starbucks, but with a shorter line and a shorter wait.

slowest starbucks. went to drive thru today and it was bad service again. one time i waited several minutes for someone to just come on speaker at the drive-thru but ended up driving away. told myself never to go back after that and i absolutely will never go back now.

this is a very convenient location and close to the selmon and i-75 on/off ramps. the staff has always been very friendly for all of my drive thru and inside orders. yes, the wait time can be rather extensive, and understandably so. for the most part everyone is ordering a craft beverage of some sort, not to mention perhaps a suit item so the way is to be expected. if you're looking for a quick experience this is not the place and i would suggest getting your coffee elsewhere so you're not stuck in the drive thru lane filled with regret. this location does have one detrimental flaw and that is the drive-through lane. it wraps around the building and parking spaces making it difficult for people to reverse out and leave. it is not always crazy busy and we have had some good luck with short wait times on weekend mornings. pro tip: treat the employees with respect and kindness, do you never know what they are going through that day!
this location is always extremely slow. nice staff but need a better system to get customers in and out. each time i visit i wait for almost 15 minutes.
the cashier was in no hurry and chided customers "don't come to starbucks if you don't have patience". i would have left if i hadn't already waited over 10 minutes just for a regular coffee.

relative to other starbucks, this one is generally okay- nothing horrible, nothing great. the building doesn't look like a typical starbucks, and the first time i went here, i actually drove by it. it's easy to find parking, and the place is on the small side- about maybe 7-8 tables plus a few chairs inside, and then an outside patio for when it's nicer out. i've always been able to find a table inside, though. the service is decent, the line fluctuates between quite long and empty, and the noise level fluctuates between quiet and very loud. service is okay, speed of drinks is okay, food is okay. the bathrooms were clean, and they have free wifi. generally a decent, middle of the road starbucks, but it's main advantage is its location right on 202, near the kop mall.

so sad to be giving a starbucks 2 stars but after visiting this place 4 days in a row it's a pretty accurate rating. i came to this starbucks daily during my vacation in tennessee because it was literally across the street from my hotel and it was nice to walk to get my morning coffee. however, every single time i went i waited at least 25 minutes to get my drink. that's ridiculous. it doesn't matter if you come in the morning because the line and waiting time will be ridiculous. it doesn't matter if you order it through the app, because you have to wait till they help everyone in line first. it doesn't matter if you come during the day because somehow they still take forever. if it wasn't because i have a starbucks problem or because it was so close to my hotel i wouldn't be coming here after 2 visits. ever. and if you're a local, stay away. find another one or go to dunkin'!

its starbucks! when you catch a 550am flight out of new orleans to tampa and get a coffee ( double shot espresso) right outside the gate you just fell out of, yep! better than sex.....!!!!!! and in an airport, yep. you gotta wait in line since i'm not the only one who lives there... and yes, it's not cheap!!! since when did you ever have something cheap at the airport? a pack of gum is $3, and bottled water is $4....suck it up. your traveling, expect it!!!!! got another one in atlanta 2 hrs later!!!!

one in a million chance you will get a good coffee. never consistent with their quality. the line is usually long, but even if it is short it still takes a very long time to get through it. nice workers there, but like i said coffee is not their strong suit. even though that's what starbucks is known for.
you should check to make sure the lids on your coffee you serve are on properly. spilled all over the suede and leather in my 70k lexus.. thanks for that.
i come here almost every weekend. they know my order by heart and treat me like family! my drinks always look picture perfect. totally recommend.

this is the location in canal place. line moved quickly and i ordered the peppermint hot cocoa. the cashier rang me up then a barista said, "we only have skinny mocha for hot chocolate." i'm not a regular visitor but how do you not have enough syrup to make the hot cocoa especially on a cold day?

i would have given them a four but the gal that made our coffee wasn't the friendliest which was a bummer because we came a couple days later and the guy working the drive thru was very happy and polite. i guess some people just have there off days, i get it.

i ended up here, largely against my will, and regret it. it's worse than it was when i last reviewed it. dark, dingy, dirty ... i guess the best you can say about it is that it's a nice hangout for the city's homeless population. but gads, even the homeless should have something better than this! the iced coffee i had was awful. cloyingly sweet, syrupy, sloppy, and artificial tasting. and as we left, the garbage can overflowing with its refuse was a nice touch.

who ever is responsible for ordering their supplies at this location needs to start ordering the ingredients for the cascara cold brew. they've been out for over a month and other nearby locations have the beverage. pretty frustrating.

i have been to starbuck's stores all over this lovely country of ours, and usually receive excellent service, and delicious coffee. unfortunately the same cannot be said for this particular location. from the management down through the ranks, rudeness and condescending attitudes abound at this store. it's as if they all could care less if you patronize the place or not. nearby locations that meet the usually excellent starbuck's experience are in st. clair square mall, and one located in the target store; both located in fairview heights, il. just a short drive down the road from this terribly awful hell hole of a store. please understand that this review was written after many and repeated 2nd chances. oh, it is filthy too!

prices in reno nevada are higher than they are in the bay area for a cup of starbucks. the line for this starbucks was about a 20 minute wait. if your working in this city of at the hotels i don't think you would be able to get starbucks on your 10 minute break without coming back late. after ordering it took about 10 minutes to receive our breakfast sandwiches and 10 more minutes to receive our drinks. there were about 20 other customers in the store with only 2 employees. we came early january so it could still be high tourist season but the farrah hotel should have a large enough staff capable or making coffee in a more efficient manner. i ordered a caramel macchiato and it was cold, tasted like pre coffee and did not taste like what i am used to buying in san jose ca. it could be a slow starbucks and not during winter or high travel season do they ever have to make drinks, but it's starbucks and starbucks is always jam packed no matter where you go. conclusion is try to find a bette estate bucks in reno if you can.

most starbucks you can expect the same customer service same quality of drinks etc. this location always has new employees, long wait times and can never seem to have any of the food items available. literally you can drive to another location and save time even with a longer commute. their drive thru is the slowest ordering method possible and non of their employees have any hustle. it is too bad the starbucks is so horrible because they have one of the best locations in all of south reno.

this starbucks brings me back to the old school days. me and hubby used to come to this location all the time. our all time favorite is the white chocolate mocha. we used to sip on that and chat upstairs at their lounge section very often. this is probably my favorite starbucks since it brung me so much memories. since is my favorite, why 3 stars???? i've been checking in quite a bit lately. their drinks are either hit or miss. for example, i've been getting the matcha green tea latte. sometimes the drink is just right but other times, the barista probably didn't shake it enough...... resulting in "chunks" of powder at the bottom of the cup. sometimes the whipped cream looks so nice while others "cheap/stingy" looking.

ordered a sandwich and they forgot to make it. not a huge deal at first. mistakes happen. but i stood there and the barista (alexis maybe, pink hair) wouldn't make eye contact with me at all. i could have been standing there bleeding out and she wouldn't have looked at me. i waited it out trying to get her attention, as i had plenty of time while waiting for my flight. but finally another employee noticed me, apologized that they forgot, and took care of my order. maybe a bad day for alexis, but with the way she was interacting with her fellow employees, i believe it was just a bad attitude and bad customer service.

teavana shaken iced green tea every time i come to this location, they fill your entire drink with ice. i take a few sips and the whole drink is gone. it's a total rip off.. i'm certain i could ask for a tall with no ice and it would be more than what i was served today.

when brewed correctly, i love starbucks coffee, however, many times i find myself being served coffee that taste burnt or doesn't take like coffee at all, just milk. for the outrageous prices, this should never be an issue. 01/07- went into this location and ordered a coconut milk mocha macchiato. after spending $5.67, i realized the coffee was so burnt it tasted nasty and couldn't bring myself to drink it . i went back and complained, which clearly bothered them . after telling them that it tasted burnt , the employee seemed confused as if this isn't a common starbucks issue! anyways, they made me another drink and it had so many pumps of caramel and mocha that i literally couldn't drink it. every sip consisted of globs of caramel and mocha . i had to throw the entire drink away , $5 to the drain. it was clearly done intentionally. their was even caramel on the side of the cup( view pics). save your money and a headache and drive to another location if needed.

recently opened (end of july/early august). limited seating with most seating in the foyer of college plaza. seems as though they were trying to make the best of limited building space, but this seating only works for short conversation with a coworker-not studying, as the foyer has large amounts of noise and foot traffic. quality and customer service is at starbucks baseline or slightly below. they seem to have trouble nailing a simple "blonde roast with no room" order and the baristas lack the starbucks enthusiasm you might see at other locations. i imagine this will change with time, but i'd recommend studying or meeting with your friend at the second cup on the next block until that happens.
3 stars because the counter people are nice -1 because they have no drive thru -1 because they have no food

i have used their mobile order multiple times and come to the store 15 minutes later to find they aren't even open despite their hours telling me they should be open. so now they have taken my money and i have no recourse. bad management if you aren't open when your hours say you are.

i have been to many starbucks locations, and none are as good as this location! none of them! i really do not understand the negative reviews here. every time that i go (which is fairly often since i work across the street) the baristas are always friendly with, not only the regulars such as myself, but everyone in line. the drinks made here are always better made than any other location i have been to. for such a small location, they sure do get that line moving faster than larger locations with far more workers. if you are reading reviews, listen to the girl who visits this location on a weekly basis.

great service. connected in building with chase bank which i do go to. the barista was very helpful giving a great suggestion i would have never thought of - iced egg nog latte with chestnut praline. oh my heaven.

we are on a cross-country road trip (again), and my family loves to make that occasional stop at starbucks once in a while when it gets late and the driver needs a boost. excited to have found a starbucks that was still open at these late hours as we were entering the unsuspecting town of reno, we rapidly pulled our van into the lot and rushed to the front door like maniacs. not to worry though, we were rapidly tended to by sir chaz. he seemed a bit busy listening to the drive-through customers on his earpiece, but that did not stop him from welcoming us with a bright smile. i ordered my usual custom drink. julia my niece came in behind me wanting something to eat and after much debate, decided upon a simple bagel and cream cheese, and a chai latte. next came dante. they asked for two cups of water which chaz was happy to give us. this is where the other barista jessie comes in. she skilfully made our drinks possibly the most perfect they have ever been on this trip so far. we needed to use the restroom and were so delighted to see that it was sparkling clean from top to bottom. the entire interior of the whole store was spotless. chaz and jessie were so busy energetically running back and forth maintaining the entire store to the highest standards, that i started to wonder how it was possible that these two could do it all themselves. i tipped them a dollar for their good service but i should have given them more. my drink did its job and kept me up the rest of the night until i reached my destination. this was a fantastic starbucks experience to say the least.

i have no problem for tipping for good service. however the big guy with the beard and knit hat always shakes or moves the tip cup once i am done paying. now, i never tip at this starbucks simply out of spite. i mean come on bud, you are pulling a leaver on the coffee urn filling up a cup and charging me 4 bucks, get the hell out of here with your tip cup.

i give this place 2 stars for its service only, which is friendly. however it gets only 2 stars because they don't know anything about coffee. the customer behind me asked what the difference is between a regular drip coffee and an americano. the barista looked at her for a long time with a confused look and then told her that it was a "darker coffee with more caffeine because it has espresso in it." if you know anything about coffee, you know he's wrong on all counts... didn't expect much, but misinformation is worse than just saying "i don't know."

i worked at this location for over 2 years as well as many others in the area for a total of 6 1/2 years. i can tell you for even being one of the older ones, this is by far the cleanest store in the greater stl area. the staff is still the most friendly group of people every time i go in-even the newbies i don't know! the environment is also a perk since there's no drive through. while living in fenton, i would drive out of my way to go to this starbucks instead.
this starbucks is cramped. every time i have been in there, the coffee doesn't taste as fresh as other starbucks. the staff is friendly, but it's certainly not a first choice for me.
not the nicest bunch of employees! part of the reason we will not go back! have been to many branches & each get a drink daily so we are very disappointed with their customer service!

this has to be the worst coffee experience i've ever had. the employees are rude, and the coffee is downright disgusting. i have no idea how one can make a coffee so gross. i ordered a simple dark roast. it had a thick sludgy consistency. it was extremely bitter and sour, nothing like coffee. i rarely dump drinks, but this cup tasted so rancid that i had no other choice. i definitely would not recommend this business.

so traveling across the usa ordering the exact same thing from starbucks along the way have been for two years now. came to philadelphia walked up to order and a i could be wrong but you shouldn't come to work stoned. you should actually listen when your customers order. see if you're going to get the order wrong it shouldn't cost more than it has cost through the entire journey. so needless to say i'm definitely a starbucks fan, my girlfriend and i have it every day. the coffee was good the muffins were good the service definitely not up to par.

my wife goes here way more than i do, to do her homework and such, but so much i think she works there as a regular customer! ha. i have got a couple drinks from this place and have never been disappointed yet. i enjoy the strawberry's and creme frappe as well as white chocolate mocha and other frappes. the atmosphere is just like any starbucks, nothing special just a little coffee shop with some tables and free wi-fi. i love that they have the free wi-fi here. the people are friendly workers and customers, the places is clean, and they have a little outside area to sit as well.

i'm a lifelong starbucks drinker, love the place and the happy culture and the service. i went in this morning and the two baristas were major grumpsters. i know it was 6am but man i do not go to starbucks for that sort of attitude. total turn-off and not a great way to start my day, having to put up with extreme attitude to get my coffee.

this is my favorite location on the westbank, a zillion times better than the one on terry parkway. the employees are super nice and friendly, and they remember my drinks and ask about my day. the atmosphere is good, perfect for work, study, reading, or light conversation (inside voices and no screaming children... usually). they were recently remodeled, and i'm going to be an old fart and say that i liked it better before. they added a "community table" (that's what i call it), which is long and sits a few people at the one table. as someone who cherishes personal space, i'm never going to sit there. now that i've finished whining, i do enjoy this location, and i appreciate the pride that the employees have in their store and work. it's mega-obvious, especially when compared to the craphole that is the terry parkway location.

i have been a loyal customer of this starbucks, mainly because of it's proximity to where i live, and while i am near a few starbucks in the area, it seems that the baristas here are friendly and helpful, which is definitely a nice feature of a smaller starbucks. one of them has had very good suggestions on things, for example, today i tried a shot of blonde espresso in my blonde roast. i never would have thought to have tried that but i actually really enjoyed that! i was worried that espresso would have been way too strong and made me feel jittery but this was really good, except that it was a little strong on my stomach, but it definitely gave me the perk that i needed versus the plain blonde roast. she also had good suggestions when picking out a gift card for someone and i finally found the perfect one, which was really cute and said "hey thanks" as a cool thank you gift! i felt that she went out of her way on a couple of occasions to help. now that this coffee shop is under new management, i have seen a lot of improvements in that i feel that the manager and people here really care and want you to be a happy customer, which i didn't feel before. they've let me sample multiple coffees which were delicious. my only reason for giving 4 stars instead of 5 is that i think sometimes there is confusion about the transactions of reloading my gold card and then taking my balance out of the reload and sometimes the line moves a bit slowly. all in all, i am happy here and i will continue to return, very nice neighborhood coffee shop!

my partner and i are really sick, sore throat and all. we stopped at this starbucks and were delighted to meet this amazing barista named natasha. five stars for her amazing personality, beautiful smile, and exceptional customer service skills!!!

i have over the last six months donated now $27 worth of my drinks to starbucks because i didn't have time for their unadvertised slow food movement which pertains to waiting 30 minutes or longer for a drink. they could blame it on the time of year. they can blame it on a busy drive-through they could blame it on lack of employees. because they've done each of these things every time i've asked them about it the last time i left a review on their facebook page january 1 they had nobody in the drive-through and one person in the store 37 minutes later i got my drink two minutes later i realized i waited that long for them to make my drink wrong. here's the thing i understand and i'm patient and flexible with crappy fast food. but if i'm spending six dollars or more and sometimes triple dad to buy my husband a drink and bring home something for my daughter then it would be nice not to have to dedicate an hour of my time to get it easy drink from starbucks which is 1.2 miles from my house. you might ask well why do i still go? and i'm asking myself the same thing but i just keep thinking that one day it's not going to be such a miserable waiting experience and they'll eventually figure out how to be quicker and more efficient on the bar .
i have been to this starbucks multiple times. they are very busy and they move fast. i haven't had a complaint on my drink. they are just another mall starbucks.

i frequent this starbucks a few times a month and i'm always happy. the staff are friendly and the place is clean. so far my orders have been perfect, never messed up. one time they had a young girl singing and playing guitar, which was nice. the place is usually never dead, although never too crowded. i can always manage to find a seat.

perfect "last chance" yummy coffee stop before dragging through security and boarding your flight. or the perfect pick me up after arrival or while waiting to pick someone up. the baristas are speedy and very nice.

our 10 year old son got in line behind the only customer waiting. others lined up and were served before him - despite the fact that they arrived later. we asked what was going on. apparently he should have queued in a different direction. the "barista" never said a word. terrible service

horrible service here! i was literally the only person standing at the register, no cars in the drive thru and 3 employees walked around, past and f'ing through me without an acknowledgement. i had to ask someone to help me twice...and then they didn't apologize! this place need to replace their entire work force on monday afternoons. this was really bad!

have you heard? the manegercall the police on 2 young men who "wouldn't buy anything " and sat at a table to wait on a friend. i'm so guilty of going to starbucks and using wifi, and not even ask for water. horrible service... there's many starbucks to choose from people. don't choose this one
the staff makes an effort to remember the drinks of regulars. sometimes its the little things like that which make the biggest difference.

when i'm up north, this is my favorite starbucks. it's a little neighborhood joint, so you don't have anything too loud, too obnoxious or too much in general. great meeting place, great working place. it has somehow even missed that after school hang out from all the kids. the employees here always seem to remember me, even though i keep changing my drink order on them. it's just a nice, friendly, neighborhood coffee shop.

i drove up few days ago, along with other people waiting in line and then proceeded to window they had tiny sign stating they were closed which is completely fine and understandable, however it was so small and i wasnt the only one who missed the sign there were a bunch of other people in line who thought they were open as well. my problem was the people working in there the lady opened the window and rudely says did you not see the sign were closed. bad customer service! sorry i did not see your tiny little scribble sign. bad attitude!

they close at 7 on sunday, it is currently 6:10 and they have taken all their pasteurized out of the case, turned their ovens off, and were incapable of cooking any the food the have on display or on their menu. i find that if your open that that means you should be capable of offering all items on the menu until close. i am going to reach out to the manager of this starbucks and hope that i can update my review based off of how the manager attempts to rectify the problem. i now have an extremely disappointed three year old and it is difficult to properly explain the reason he can't have something i told him he could. needless to say i will be going back to my regular starbucks and be avoiding this one at all costs. too eager to close down for the day and get out of work then to serve their patrons.

if this were your first experience of the starbucks franchise, you would walk away neither overly impressed nor depressed. for those of us who've easily been in dozens of them, the feeling is about the same. in our particular case this week, it was a necessary evil -- we were trying to locate yet another tourist attraction (this sounds like complaining, but trust me, we were having a fabulous time) and the iphone and its handy gps were nearly dead, so we ducked into starbucks for a drink and a good long battery charge. i'd never had a starbucks smoothie before and i'll never have one again. it was just not like a 'real' smoothie -- no fruit chunks, no bold fruit flavors, nothin' interesting, and a strong preservative taste to seal the deal. but chances are i'd have said the same thing if i'd gone to any other starbucks nationwide, so don't take that the wrong way. the girls behind the counter were loud and goofy, but at least they were having fun at work. plenty of parking out front.
still a very slow starbucks. i have found that this location takes forever whether you go inside or through the drive through.
great starbucks location. very friendly service the few times i've been here. love the drive thru bonus.
very large plenty of study space friendly attentive staff very very very cold staff said they have no control over the heat. everyone had their winter coats on. i had to leave it was so cold.

i was in the store earlier today & things were busier than usual. a couple customers were being extremely rude to the employees, but they still kept smiles on their faces. ashley & andy were especially helpful, despite the stressful situation. my coffee was good, as usual.
it's a starbucks. they make coffee. it was adequate and caffeinated. it's all good!

extremely friendly staff and they're efficient. when i arrived, the drive through line was considerably long but it moved quickly. the sugar free hot chocolate was better than expected being that it was sugar free.

i usually love starbucks but this location was sooo slow!! i am not exaggerating when i say it took more than 20 minutes for me to get my drink. and there wasn't a lot of people when i got there. the employees were taking their sweet time, laughing and having conversations while the clients waited. very upsetting because i know starbucks stores and i never had this experience before.

kudos to this starbucks for having a back bone. some unpleasant woman named "precious" came in with some stupid looking man, demanding that their drinks be remade. however, the drinks were not from that day. starbucks apparently has a "same day same store" policy which the baristas rightly enforced. when the baristas informed them of the policy they got very rude and demanding. the guy that was with her dropped the f-bomb and demanded to speak with a manager. they almost became threatening. these two were clearly scammers. precious j. and whoever that guy was, i hope you both get trespassed and never come back. bye.

the first person to review this starbucks could not be more off. most of the staff came from the location that closed across the street and they're always so welcoming. the drive thru is always quick and efficient. yes, sometimes the line is pretty long but i've never waited more than a few minutes. if i don't want to risk a wait in the drive thru i order ahead on the app which is super easy. the inside is a nice size compared to the old whitestown location and the outdoor space is awesome in warm weather!

i'm just not a starbucks fan, its really not their fault! i am yet to find a starbucks drink that isn't 100% tooooo sweet. not to mention overpriced. the starbucks in iv is very small as well so its not worth trying to study there. overall i would just go to a different cafe or library any day.

i honestly don't know why there are only bad reviews for this starbucks when i've had nothing but positive experiences at this location. i mostly order my drinks through the app and it's always been ready by the time i get there and if i made a mistake ordering, they quickly fix my drink without hesitation. the customer service at this location is on point. i always make an effort to come to this location even if it's a tad bit further because the customer service is so awesome. hopefully everyone else who had bad experiences will have better ones in the future.

drove for about 4 hours and would stop because i wanted starbucks. finally pulled over here, no one else in line and i asked if they had any cheese sandwiches. i had looked, the barista just said if it ain't in the case we don't have them. then i asked for what i always get and that is frappuccino with some ground espresso beans in it they refused to make it. they said they could not make that drink at starbucks. so we got in the car, headed north on i65 stopped at the next starbucks, the lady found us a cheese sandwich when we asked and she made my drink no problem. thanks lebanon starbucks for providing good service!

very friendly staff - and apologetic. seems that in the mornings they are always out of the sandwich i want. the biggest issue i have though is the lack of speed. i can place a mobile order and 15 minutes later walk in and it's still not ready. only once in the last month has it actually been waiting for me. i'm not sure why it's so slow all the time, there always seems to be plenty of staff working. regardless, the staff is super friendly, i just wish things could be faster.

showed up at 8:30 am. having barely been open a few hours the store looked like it had been through a holiday rush - disgusting bathrooms, overflowing trash cans, a sticky dirty floor. truly embarrassing if i was the owner. it's as though the previous night's crew just closed up shop and the morning crew came in and also did absolutely nothing. small things go a long way - clean your store.
larger starbucks w good outdoor seating that is shared w other businesses. good indoor seating as well - perfect for studying.

i would give this place a 0 if i could. the customer service here is horrible. the cashier, krystal, rolled her eyes at me the entire time she took my order and was extremely rude. they were out of almost everything at 9am on a sunday. instead of the cashier communicating nicely other options to what i wanted she just snarked and rolled her eyes. i was very caught off guard by this because i rarely right bad reviews, but had to after this experience. horrible service, long wait time, and they are unprepared.

am i dreaming? starbucks opened less than 5 minutes from my house, and its a drive through!!! i'd been dreaming of this moment for all of my suburban mom life (which has been less than 10 months at this point, but still...). dragging a baby out of the car to get coffee in the cold or rain is less than ideal, and dunkin was the only close option. now there's a starbucks drive through right on rt 130! i still miss la colombe (and being able to walk to get coffee) but this is the best option within 5 minutes of my house.

i had to sit down and write this review because after a few times of having my order be wrong in the drive thru, i couldn't not say something. i get the easiest order you can get. trenta iced green tea, unsweet, no water. yes, i could go to the one downtown, but it's hard to park, and i have my little one in the car with me and it's just convenient to drive thru, i don't even mind the consistently long waits at the drive thru (20-25 min) because i don't have to get out of my car with a kid in pj's. i do however get frustrated with my order being wrong and then still having to go inside to change it. today they got the teas right, but gave me venti, a few days ago, i got one mango iced tea, and one green tea...a few days before that, i had to go inside because they gave me someone's tea from the mobile order, and she got my drive thru order. i've been to so many super busy starbucks, and they are hustling to get everyone through in a quick manner, and with the right order, this one seems to need more espresso or something to speed them up and also some drilling on getting the orders right in spite of being busy. nothings worse than going through the drive thru and then having to go inside to fix your order. i am very polite, and don't usually make a big deal about it, but this needs to be handled.

excellent service! whenever i come in- i'm greeted my name! delicious beverages- always made perfectly. and, the beverages are always made really fast- everyone there sees my work t-shirt and understands that i've got to get back, and i've never even had to say a word! so considerate of them! i am never given a hard time for not knowing what the cup sizes are called- or the fact that i can't pronounce all the beverage names! your refreshers are delicious, and the bagel i once had was so yummy! perfect texture, with real butter that was in its prime! go starbucks on central! i hope you keep prospering amidst this pandemic.

mobile ordered to here a few times. usually very good and drinks are out there. one time i went in and the drink in the cup was completely wrong and told the barista who blamed it on the label being "wrong" when it wasn't because i read it out to her and it was exactly what i had wanted. would've prefered if she just owned up to it. anyways usually good and looks like good seating for those who enjoy sitting at starbucks and working.

"cookie monster," or the incredible drive through employee formally known as ryan, made my starbucks visit sunday absolutely spectacular. he was funny, kind, quick and turned an otherwise average visit to starbucks to above and beyond. i believe it's employees like him that make a difference in someone's day by spreading joy. thank you ryan - coooooookies!

this starbucks does not rock my world in any way but i have to stop here when i am in tampa because i am always driving by it on my way back to orlando and convenience matters more than quality. service is generally fine -- good but unremarkable. the speed of service, however, costs the location its stars. i find that the wait time here is often far too long, even when there is not a huge line. still, where there are no other real options and time is of the essence, you will find that you get what you need and sometimes that is enough.
parking and driver thru here sucks especially on a rainy day. if you're looking for a place to plug in and web surf probably not the place.

the coffee was burnt and i got charged extra for carmel but then when they gave me the drink it was what they would've normally put on it if i didn't get charged extra $.50. i shouldn't have to ask for more caramel when i'm getting charged for it i ahould be getting it. there's no need for me to hand back my drink to request what i ordered. i'm so glad you can't tip when you use a debit card because half these people don't deserve one at all!!! the male was extremely beyond rude!!!! never ever going back to this place. he was absolutely uncalled for talking crap about customers behind their backs. he had dark hair and very metro he was working july 8th wednesday 2020 @ 1:00pm!!! just so management knows who he is since i didn't get a name. he should not be working with people if he is going to act like that. so so so rude and horrible customer service. he also for got to give me a straw and went i was coming back he put up a 5 min break sign after he saw me coming back! so i knocked until he opened the door and i got my straw. smh
they are always out of food items. don't follow your orders. rude when you ask a question. super excited about having this new starbucks but hate the service:/
