great service this morning from the guy taking your order (chico) & the barista lady! i was asked how i was doing before my order was taken and the barista lady kindly informed me of the changes coming in 5 days to all canadian starbucks - the introduction of la boulange pastries!! apparently starbucks bought out this famous bakery from san fran and we are the last market to incorporate their pastries into the food selection! the barista lady raved about their croissants... apparently to die for! who doesn't love a delicious buttery melt-in-your mouth croissant?!? i guess i'll just have to try it out myself in 5 days time!

when one door closes another opens. so goes the old saw. and as the starbucks in eagle plaza location closed this new one, called the riverside location, opened. the plaza location was convenient to my house. but it was narrow and awkward inside, the parking lot could be miserable and it had no drive through when that was the thing devoutly to be wished. you know, those no makeup and bad hair days. so this new location is welcome for more space inside. although no better parking, more of it but in a really awkward lot. inside you'll find a pletohora of high backless stools at high communal table that almost no one wants to use unless last resort and paucity of standard height tables with regular chairs that are almost always the first choice of everyone who enters. what is with that in starbuck's designs of late? end of rhetorical question musing. it also has a drive through. it also has more outdoor seating than the old location but the tables are packed like sardines in a can. some of the same, friendly plaza staff found their way here. there are some new people who are clearly in learning mode. and the entire store is in "newbie facility" shake out. so patience is in order while they get their footing. this n that: location note: near the new eagle tennis club and just down the road from perks of life. if you'd rather frequent a locally owned coffee place, perks is a good option to do that.
i stopped by while i was waiting for a flight arrival at tpa .my coffee was perfect. the attendant fulfilled my request as asked . i would visit again
this is the third consecutive frapp i've had here that is watery. it's literally cold chai tea with whipped cream on top.
love the drive through here. courteous and quick. friendly staff who allow for just the right amount of conversation while passing out drinks.

my orders are consistently wrong, and the drive thru time is annoying.the staff is always friendly and kind, but everything is done in a shaky disorganized way that makes me wonder what's going on behind the scenes. maybe they're often shorthanded? i don't know. last week i woke up early enough before work to visit this starbucks. i ordered a grande iced coffee with cream. they gave me a venti, which i suppose was nice, but it had no cream. i decided not to mess with correcting them, then got a few blocks away and realized they hadn't given me a straw. this is a typical visit for me.
very friendly, assistant manager, amanda, was especially nice. iced tea is wonderful, but even better is how relaxed i feel enjoying it in the nice environment. they always make me feel so at home.

this is a busy starbucks so sometimes the lines are long. but the baristas move fast and your drinks are prepared quickly. there are not many places to sit but if you do get a spot it is comfortable and fun to watch the suburban square shoppers scurrying about.
great service and friendly staff! always welcoming, and my order is always perfect! cant wait till they reopen for in store seating.
shout out to the drive through crew. always engaging and friendly. as a regular who needs my coffee to survive the day to day i appreciate the effort they put in. quality customer service.

i've never had any issues with this location, but the staff is now advised by management to go against starbuck policy and when someone orders an iced coffee, light ice, they are not allowed to fill it to the top. i order a large size and all i want is more coffee than cream, that extra 1/2 a cup of coffee makes the difference, but what i received was a milky white with a hint of tan drink. when i told then that was way too much cream they poured a little out, and then splashed in a little more coffee, which made really no difference to the white color whatsoever. it sounds like management is trying to save a couple pennies and instead of acting like a real starbucks they're rationing out the amount of coffee one can have. luckily this isn't the only starbucks near me, or even the only starbucks on campus!! i advise finding another one.

ordered 7 items from the drive through just now. noticed that they gave me a bagel mislabeled in a bag that said it was my breakfast sandwich. i went in to switch it out which was easy. didn't realize until we were much farther away that they also forgot to put in our two cake pops. tried to call and at least change the amount we were charged on cc but no one would answer the phone. are you guys just throwing random things in bags?
still...this location is one of the most unfriendly around. i also didn't receive what i ordered. i will not be coming back until they get their $h*t together.
frequently get lukewarm coffee. they always remake the coffee when i have time to go in and ask, but this is starbucks...they should have hot coffee at all times.
just wanted to thank who ever made our drinks!! they were the best! haven't gotten well made irish cream cold brew in a while. this time it was perfection!

pros: i always hit this place at low traffic. never waited more than 2-3 minutes for my tried and true iced coffee with cream, no sugar. of course that -is- probably the quickest prep item there. the wifi is quick, regularly see downloads of 100-200k/sec from major sites. i do spend time here looking at my serato whitelabel releases and have no complaints. cons: store is anchored on east side of strip mall, which means the slim layout of the place + most of seating on the east can make for toasty morning visits during the summer even with the blinds down. the parking lot can feel be tricky at times, people drive way too fast through the narrow lanes so triple check before you back out of your spot when it is busy. baristas are all nice to me, so over time i've gotten to like this location even more. it's walking into a cheers episode.

i will never go to this starbucks again. the last 2 times i've been there the same lady is so rude. and now this time she had to remake my drinks and didn't even do them right. i feel if you have an off day i get it but 2 times in a row.........

the streets of santa barbara are almost empty, but this starbucks is open for take away service. so glad that they can work during these scary times. super friendly and nice!!! just make me feel good. thank you for that amazing service, great staff

absolutely the worst starbucks with incompetent staff who asks you to repeat your order 5x and still gets it wrong. didn't even order a fancy drinks literally asked for a ice black coffee and they put milk in it... service is slow which i can't seem to comprehend since all of their customers are drive thru

we frequent this location. (3) times this week we have sat in a very long line only to find out they ran out of tea refreshers. could you possibly put up a stationary sign indicating this? so frustrating & waste of time. even before the holidays, this location frequently runs out of bagged coffee & stoppers. this location could be managed better.

if i could give one star. i totally would. i ordered a bagel. the woman grabbed the bagel with her bare hands. i didn't see her wash or hands or put any gloves on. i'm kind of disgusted. with all the food issues going on lately. i would've expected better service and cleanliness.

starbucks is my go-to place to work and kill some time for really one reason: free refills on tea and coffee (for members). this location is the only starbucks in the area, but it is definitely not my favorite in tampa. they are always crowded, never feel like they have enough seats or tables. the outdoor area feels like it is sitting in the parking lot, and about half the time in the afternoons they seem to be out of something...sometimes more than one. they are super nice here, and the problems aren't enough that i don't come when i'm in the area--but if i'm heading elsewhere, i would far rather be at the kennedy starbucks. this one meets my caffeine needs, but it isn't the most relaxing of locations!

i wait in the line 32 minutes to get my order and when i order they told me everything i order they told me they didn't have it and also all the time different price they charge me and also they are fucking so mean and when they give me my order all of it ice that is fucked up really fucked up and also the fucking drink it's just like a water taste
this is such a nice little target starbucks. they had more syrup flavors than the starbucks by my job, and the line moved quickly.

this starbucks sucks! you ask for light ice and your cup is 1/3 filled with ice. when you tell them that you asked for light ice, they just look at you. the female employee with really dark hair that's bleached blond doesn't know how to make drinks either. she's the one that put all of the ice in my drink. it tasted funny too, different than any other starbucks made it. they need to work on teaching employees how to follow instructions and properly make drinks.

there are a couple of nice workers but the manager is a bitchy slug of a human being who shouldn't work in any restaurant or any other customer service based industry. all i wanted was a water and she told me to get it myself and then proceeded to roll her eyes and grunt when i asked for a cup. you work at a starbucks bitch, that's not my fault you're miserable i just wanted a drink ‍

this location is amazing the employees are the best of the best top notch they strive to do their best at all times when they are unable it is someone had an emergency or they are low on the things they need to prepare. our orders, please don't take it out on them. they truly try to do their best. they work back to back shifts sometimes as well please don't make cry like a person i know did love and live our best lives now

i really do dislike this starbucks. there's no urgency & more than half the times they miss something on my very easy order. the drive-through is usually more accurate & loads faster. i would almost rather drive further for better service.

first mistake was obviously going to this starbucks when there are plenty of awesome coffee shops within walking distance, but anyway....the service was the worst. no eye contact and made me act like i was a problem and a burden. don't easter your time take a nice downtown walk over to park ave coffee or drive to kaldis coffee

i've been going to this starbucks for nearly three years and 99% of my experiences are great. being attached to a hotel means there will always be lines and the staff handle them with speed and accuracy. i'm not sure why, but starbucks coffee tends to always taste burnt, so i still with the various tea and non coffee drinks they have. matcha latte, passion tea lemonade, you name it and they can whip it up. freaky limited edition drink? make it a venti. are you craving 12 cake pops and an order of egg whites? they will hook you up. the only downside about this site is they don't accept starbucks's rewards or special promotional deals, like happy hour, so plan accordingly.
my daughters walked here and it was drive thru only, very frustrating. they were not able to get coffee

this starbucks is always slow in the drive through. today, i waited 10 minutes (normal for the drive through). i ordered a latte, which is obviously a hot drink. for some reason the woman added ice to it. i saw that on the sticker and so i asked her if there was ice and she said no. i left the drive through and took a sip and the drink was cold. so i had to drive all the way back around the block to get back in line. the drink also had whipped cream when i asked for no whipped cream. i went inside and politely asked her to make me a new one without ice. she did, but with a very unfriendly attitude. no apology or anything. she must be new.

please make the "no smoking signs" bigger, and more visible during the night. right now the signs are too small and not visible at all. at the streets, in the parking, please make those arrows more visible to indicate right traffic way.
very crowded starbucks with long lines and long wait times for orders. best thing about this location? lots of seating both indoors and outdoors. this one's not bad if you're not in a hurry.
drive through starbucks newly located on hampton. staff was friendly. first time i've ever been asked what my name was while ordering. friendliest service i have received at any starbucks!

i'm conflicted on this review. it is obvious by my 90+ check-in's here that i am a fan of starbucks. however, the customer service at this location seems to be lacking. there was a time when they were a lot better but in the last few months it has been declining. let me start by the presentation of my drink. my paper coffee cup shouldn't look like it was dipped in the coffee. there shouldn't be coffee around the rim of the lid either. second, they have a hard time getting my order right lately. i order the same damn thing every time; a tall with 4 splenda and light ice and a grande with 5 splenda. my issue is they always forget the light ice. light ice doesn't mean one ice cube in my drink. i order light ice because i want to immediately drink my coffee and not have to worry about 3rd degree burns. if my coffee has light ice it shouldn't burn my mouth and make me use profanity. other than that, it's a starbucks.

this is the worst starbucks i've been to. the bathrooms were filthy, the floor in the dining area was a compete mess, they didn't get my order right, gave a very poor attempt at fixing it, i asked to have it redone, then waited for about every other customer that came in after me to get their order before they could remake mine. this location is pathetic, 5 employees can't handle a few customers.

the baristas here are always friendly, they know my drink order and usually have it ready. occasionally, the pastries aren't the freshest, but they do a good job. be careful in the morning; it can get super crowded. super. crowded. but they do a good job of getting through the busy times.

i come here some mornings to treat myself to a nice cup of caffeine and calories. the drive through line can get a little crazy (5+ cars) and inside can get a little busy (also 5+ people) but if you watch the baristas, they are working their butts off! these guys are pretty fast which makes sitting at the bar waiting for my drink that much more entertaining! not really a review of the coffee since it's the same everywhere, but i really like the baristas there. super nice, humorous, and not hopped up on espresso. :)
the coffee here make me wonder if starbucks really cares about the taste of their coffee.

a starbucks off the 3401 walnut building. lots of staff members, and a small upstairs with more seating where you can study in a slightly more comfortable space. the baristas here aren't the friendliest, but i've never gotten the wrong drink yet. place is not particularly handicapped-friendly unless you use the main entry and sit at the bottom floor. no way to get upstairs. bathroom is a bit dirty, though.

this starbucks just moved "down the street" from the paoli train station (where there was no parking) to here - where the old burger king used to be. i have not yet gone into the store, but i used the convenient drive-thru today and got in & out quickly. glad there is parking!

this starbucks saved my family and i from a bad time. after having dealt with horrible service at a local restaurant for my mother in laws birthday, we came here to grab coffee and cut a small cake for her. the staff went above and beyond, especially one of the gentlemen working. he gave us plates and forks and made sure we were ok. they were out of decaf and we came closer to closing and he did not hesitate to brew some for us (it was night time so caffeine didn't seem like a good idea.) our drinks were all great and we were able to share a special moment for a special day in an unexpected place. my respect for the staff at this starbucks for exceptional customer service!
they are friendly and my drink is always how i want it. i'm here most days early in the morning and the wait is never too long.

oh you know, your run of the mill starbucks. the bathrooms were clean. this starbucks seems much larger than other locations. there is plenty of seating as well as outdoor seating. the staff was friendly but the drinks do take quite a while. not sure if i will be back to this starbucks. it's in the most convenient location to get in and out.

i've been hitting up the drive through starbucks off of turnpike. they are really striving hard to get people through the lines quickly. i order my usual 3 drinks for the fam. the only complaint was the black coffee had what appeared to be grounds at the bottom of the cup,& tasted burnt. that was according to the family coffee connoisseur!
barista quinton is the light in my life -1 star because they take too long :'( waaaaaa

my anxiety hits the roof whenever i walk in this place. i have been to a lot of starbucks and this one is the worst. management here should really consider doing the mystery shopper thing to get feedback. very long waits when it is not busy and mistakes. it's a shame this is the only one conveniently located near me. i have switched to dunkin'

this is a great location for a starbucks, in the center of everything. this location has become such a staple for the community by participating in local events and supplying power to most of the town when the power goes out everywhere else. the only downside is the kids that hang out on the porch. i don't mind the loitering, but their behavior and language is what's off-putting.

this little mix i requested was delicious ok first sip, then after there was no more strawberry lol idk how that happened there appears to be more than it actually is. other than that i think i was on to something

dear starbucks on pater and mccarran..... we ordered two cold drinks. paid for them.... and then waited 45 minutes to receive them. wtf?!?! no apology, no refund, no up sizing the drinks and no "thank you." never coming back again.

this is by far the worst starbucks ever. i normally bypass this location (despite it being the closest) due to their terrible service. today was no different. i ordered a grande mocha latte. it was ready when i got there. fabulous. i (stupidly) got in my car and drove off. about a mile up the road i pick up my cup and feel like it's a little light. open the lid...its 3/4 full. look at the photo. i didn't take a single sip. like this is what i'm talking about with this location. either one of three things is going to happen. 1. you'll wait forever. 2. your drink will be made wrong (in taste or just a completely different order than what you asked for) 3. you'll pay for a grande and get a tall.

i'm not a coffee person by any stretch of the imagination. in fact, i always order a green tea at starbucks, but that's neither here nor there. the thing that i love about this particular starbucks location is that it's a shoe's-throw away from the tla, so you can kill some time here (upstairs, of course) just hanging out before or after hitting up a show. so, essentially, this review has nothing to do with the quality or the service of the starbucks itself.. but just how it's convenient for my superficial killing-time needs. ;)

i have been doing mobile orders for the last 3 months, weekly orders - sometimes every few days. the customer service is horrendous here. the baristas and cashiers blatantly ignore every customer in the cafe. my mobile is almost never done when i arrive, even though i make sure to order at least 10 minutes before arriving, these days 15 min. yet once again i stood in the cafe and waited for my incorrect coffee order. i inquired twice about my sandwich and i don't know why i was ignored and pushed to the side but i finally couldn't wait anymore because i was already going to be late to work because of this that i asked for a refund. once i asked for a refund - suddenly my sandwich came out of nowhere, it was ice cold sitting behind the counter where i couldn't obtain it, and i now didn't have time to ask her to remake it. the service here is absolutely terrible. put your order in anywhere else.

one of the better starbucks i found. newer above average size and spacious layout. a couple of extra cushioned seats. bathrooms clean. food case is well placed. plenty of parking. easy access in and out. the usual friendly and efficient baristas. lines can get long but never long waits. really not much to complain about.

smaller starbucks in the olde city section of philly. can get cramped when busy. seating is at a premium. typical food and drinks here. service is the usual efficient folks/kids that work at these places. never any issues. parking of course is tough in this area. does the job for starbucks fans that live and work in the area and want a drink or bite to go, but if you are looking for place to bring your laptop and sip your latte slowly there are better starbucks to be found.

fortunately, coffee drinkers are addicts so they put up with a lot to get the coffee. very rude workers. petty. no customer service. they don't even try to be decent. convenient location is best attribute. usually there's parking, but wow, putting up with the disrespect and rudeness of the "help" is very hard. 90% of customers are using apps.

brand new starbucks and they have a drive thru too! so convenient, especially when traveling with cranky kids who couldn't care less about me wanting an americano. so far, they're doing a great job here. the staff is nice, the place is immaculate inside, and the noise level is perfect. truly a pleasure to stop by. good stuff all around, keep it up guys!
nice starbucks, but i got bit by mosquitoes when i was there, like 6 bites. i don't know if it was the weather or something, but the coffee was nice, they have comfy chairs too. nice.
always spot on. we will skip other starbucks just to go here instead. i appreciate the coffee and you guys never disappoint.
speedy and friendly service. excellent customer service and great food! highly recommend.

i am super impressed with this starbucks! the staff is friendly and prompt. i came early for a meeting with a colleuge and they were offering a buy a holiday drink and get one free from 2-5. well when 2:00 hit it was crazy packed! the staff happily served every customer with bright smiles and cheery voices! they made sure to keep the place clean and tidy! they were even kind enough to allow me to order one of my drinks then while i was meeting with my college and then order my free one when i was leaving to take home to my husband!

do you ever just feel like some people go up and beyond with their energy! well that's how my day started at 5am- some people just leave a lasting impression on you, that kinda makes you smile and say- today's a good day! thank you helena for your awesome energy, and outgoing personality! you rock!
slow service. ordered one iced coffee with cream and one americano plain. took over twenty minutes to make. and no apology for the wait., i don't care for starbucks coffee, but was desperate.
just watched the batista make my order. 1/2 shot of espresso, no sf vanilla and some caramel drizzle. pretty much milk with flavoring. not worth $5!!!! this was my last straw for this location.

this is the most inefficient operation on the planet. last sunday around 9, i drove up to this location, and as usual, it was completely backed up. i parked my car next door at the dry cleaners to avoid having to wait in that line, which experience told me would take forever. i entered the facility and there was no line. i placed my order for 2 croissants, 2 blueberry muffins, and 6 vanilla scones, and because i was in a hurry, i told them to skip heating anything up. all of the items were in the bakery display. i was then directed to wait to receive my order because there were 15 orders ahead of me in the drive thru. nothing in my order had to be prepared; just tossed in a bag. there were at least 5 people behind the counter working, but i was told "she is working as fast as she can". so not only is this food overpriced (and frankly, most of it available at your local market), but the service is terrible. when i told them i was not going to wait for them to service the entire drive thru customers first, and to either give me the food or a refund, the manager refunded my money and thanked me! so, i drove to mcdonalds on olive where i placed my order, paid my money, and was out in less time that i spent at this starbucks with nothing to show for it.

i'm surprised that during a busy time there is only one cashier. the person before me had an item return and it took such a long time. no one said anything to me while i waited and waited. i come here a lot and just expected to be welcomed and not ignored. today austin made my day! he gave me lots of ideas on how to enhance my drink. nice! i feel like an important customer and will be back soon. my cascara latte with an extra shot is perfect! thanks austin!

if i had to rate all the sb starbucks stores, this would be the worst. this location has all the trainees here and i'm sure they think they can get away with it because they know the foot traffic store is on state st. if i'm wrong about them being trainees then they've got real problems because it seems like everytime i come back to give this place a chance, they do something that annoys me. for instance, if i order an iced coffee with an add shot and you call out "iced coffee" is that's the same thing? so why in god's green earth would you have me ask "does it have an add shot?" only for you to confirm there is one. also, the person taking my order asks for my name and asks for my order and then asks for my name again. they obviously don't care about their standards here because they have no shortage of traffic from all the tourists
the service was great the coffee doesn't taste the same like in cali, sorry but my opinion only, my friend loved it... not me. and plus it was inside a gas station plus casino.

i have had a 50/50 experience at this location, tough to find your first time. the staff are friendly but sometimes it seems they are a person down. drinks are always as expected but slow on occasion. the location is convenient and the centrally located.

i've been to a lot of starbucks locations. but this one specifically always have problems with mobile orders. either extremely late or if you get there "someone" must've taken the wrong mobile order. happens way to often. i minds well just go order in person.

worst starbucks i have ever been to. used to live right around the corner so would stop by at least 3-4 times per week, but eventually stopped because they could never get my order correct. a venti americano with 15 sugar packets and cream - it's not that hard! at least half the time i went there and ordered that, it would be given to me without sugar or cream or both!
u cannot even enter the mall until 10 am! i guess they open for local in mall store staff to use between 9-10 am!

if you've read through the other reviews, you know i'm not exaggerating when i tell you this is the slowest starbucks you'll ever go to. seriously don't even try if you're slightly rushing. is any management going to see these reviews and do something about this? i can see this place getting less and less popular for this reason, people are tired of it and not coming back.

i'm confused as to why this starbucks doesn't have a complete 5 star rating! for starters, there's actually a parking lot...in philly! mind you, it is shared with a few other stores and a tdbank, but dont worry, they wont tow your car for parking in their lot for 5 min. to run into the bucks for a morning latte. secondly, this location received a complete reno, not so long ago. the difference is great, and the flow of traffic to the register is much better. the real reason i love this starbucks however is the people. the associates are so nice, know you by name, and can spell out what you want before you can tell them, simply because they remember. because of these reasons, i will go out of my way to stop there, even though there is a starbucks 3 minutes from where i work. :)

as a loyal starbucks customer for my whole life, i've got to say that this is one of the best i have been to. i am never disappointed. they frequently have a long line but get through the line in about 5 or so minutes. great customer service from all the baristas. i get a drink that can get messed up easily but coming here i get it perfect each time.

i ordered a tall drink and i think they must serve smaller size cups than regular starbucks, or did they all downsize the tall drink? um, it seemed like a micro mini cup size than the tall you would get at starbucks locations. also my drink was barely warm at all! i don't know what happened here today. disappointing.
good service friendly staff. props to kevo aaron and anna marie very nice people. do wish they had a little more seating and single seats though.

i was always a frequent patron of starbucks, especially this location because it's so conveniently located to my house. i just can't fathom paying starbucks prices anymore when there's so many other delicious local coffee spots in this area. the inconsistency of their beverages and breakfast sandwiches is unbelievable. no matter what kind of hot coffee you get it almost always taste burn, all iced drinks come with entirely too much ice even if you ask for light ice, and i've bit into way too many cold centered breakfast sandwiches. maybe they have gotten so mainstream and overly popular that they just don't put any care into what they are giving their customers anymore. i'd much rather go a few minutes out of my way to coffee places like bandit or black crow for a quality cup of coffee.
sitting for the outside is offered, but we can't smoke after making a purchase? very wrong and i won't be back.
unfortunately this location has really gone downhill. used to be one of my faves, but now it's understaffed & slow and they're always out of baked goods/espresso/milk/stoppers, etc...

my past 4 visits here have been awful. i usually get iced coffee or cold brew. they are usually out of cold brew but the one time they had it they didn't cut it. so i got a venti cup of cold brew concentrate (maybe that's why they are always out). luckily i was on my way home so i was able to cut it in half at home. the iced coffee always tastes burned. the good thing is the baristas are friendly. the food has tasted good. i'm going to be visiting panera for my coffee fix from now on!
very quick service. i walked inside. i am having the chicken and artichoke sandwich warmed up. i am drinking passionfruit lemonade tea. very clean.

this by far the best starbucks on 4th street! i drive out of my way to come to this coffee shop. it's your standard starbucks inside and menu, but what sets it apart is the staff. they have always gone above and beyond to help and be friendly. for example, out of a bakery item you ordered and they already rung you up? they'll take it off your order and comp you a similar snack. they're always friendly, upbeat, and quick with a smile and conversation. you can really tell that the employees here enjoy their job, their coworkers, and interacting with customers. definitely makes it special and different from your average starbucks.

the people who work at this starbucks are literally the nicest people i've encountered. i walked in a probably five employees said hello to me and two of them struck up a conversation. also they made my drink quickly and without any errors. i really don't normally review starbucks because they are all the same but i was struck by how friendly everyone here was!!

this is a place. a dark place. that somewhere. somehow. voldemort will use what fragments of his soul are still hiding out there...and you all know they are...will use this evil institution to fill his minions with false hope and caffeine once again to try and take down that kid with the scar on his forehead again...........anyway. only thing i remember about this place as it looks like thousands of others just like it, except this one might have a barrista (can't we just call them employees, i mean seriously, like making coffee for a living should be a desired after career for anyone that has graduated any level of education) who has 7 different colors of dreadlocks or some other dirty hippie stench. i always like to order the large coffee and they always ask me if i'm talking about the vente and i look at them with that look like...."i don't know what you just said little kid, but you special"....and i leave or if the coffee has kicked in too soon i torture other customers by using the restroom and turn off the air vent.

is it too much to ask that the cashier have some sort of personality and acknowledge the customer in front of her? no acknowledgement, no hello, no attempted add ons and no thank you! everyone else working was very nice but first impression is everything and this one sucked.
amazing this store is so funny. the workers laugh over everything i say! come here! pleaseeeeeeeeeee come here rn the people will love to assist you :)
these baristas are super knowledgeable and extremely friendly!! i love come to this starbucks. they know how to make a awesome coffee and my chai tea latte. you can't g wrong coming here!

let me start by saying i am a starbucks fan, i do enjoy the friendly customer service i receive at this location and this is why i continue to return time and time again. however id like to understand why it is you have signature drinks such as white chocolate mocha for example and i am certain each staff member is trained on how to make such drinks with the proper measurements of ingredients being added, thus making it a signature drink that if you order it, it should at least have that familiar taste to it each and every time. for the life of me, i cant seem to figure out why that isn't the case at least not in my experience to this location. i generally get the same drinks each time, however depending on the person working it can taste as unfamiliar to me as the person serving the drink. all id like to know is why? if everyone is making the same drinks nothing extra is being added or removed, how come i cant seem to get the same drink each time with the same expectant taste i came here for?
they said my order would take 10 minutes and it literally took 20 min. to make 2 coffees. insanity
i love starbucks...period! nothing more to say. need suggestions, let me know. i got you

so i have been going every weekend. i used to go starbucks with my 2 dogs in the drive thru for over a year. i have a 2 mixed breeds. my one is a chesaspeak bay retriever named cinnamon and a mixed breed named gigi. gigi looks like an american bully but she is mixed with a little bit of everything. so i was told because my dogs scare people from any employee, not the store manager, because of how my dogs look and sound i have to order inside and come in to get their puppinchinos. i understand people are scared of my dogs. which i am a understanding person. i was not asked roll up my windows which i lock them when i go through but i was never given that option. i did contact cooperate but they said they will contact the store manager. i have more of an issue that the employee told me and not the store manager.

i went into this location on saturday, january 15th, at around 4pm to cash in on a free drink i had on my starbucks card. the gal working the cash register was very nice, but couldn't figure out how to find my free drink, so called over, what i assume, was a shift manager. a gal with short dirty dishwater blonde hair. she told me a couple of times that i needed a barcode on my email i had received, to cash in on my free drink. i tried several times to explain to her that the email i received didn't have a barcode., to which she continued to interrupt over and over. she refused to let me speak. i couldn't believe someone in a customer service position was perfectly fine with being so rude to a customer. i will never go back to that location. i've probably been to a hundred different locations over the past 20 years, and have never been treated as rudely as i have at this location.

right on main street in new hope. nice outside seating, although your view is of the intersection. quick service with a smile and the typical starbucks quality. no parking at the store since it on main street so don't think you are going to just pull into the parking lot and run in.

this is the starbucks closest to my house, and i look forward to seeing the staff here. they are always so pleasant, and fast. i order from my app and by the time i get there it's all ready. technology is so wonderful!

man, reading some of these reviews, i feel badly for the baristas. they must have to put up with a lot since the store's right next to rittenhouse square. anyway, this is a perfectly fine starbucks with perfectly fine service for a starbucks. if you're feeling on edge, maybe go somewhere else because, well, this is a starbucks and you're not going to be waited on hand-and-foot, but i found the baristas here to be perfectly pleasant despite a rush, so it's a 5-star review from me.

the employe this afternoon was very rude and does not deserve to work at starbucks. i will never come to this location again. that's is so sad as this was my new stopping spot but i will go out of my way to have nicer employees help me. i also don't understand how you could be in the middle of the holiday season and not serve pumpkin spice lattes anymore.

slowest starbucks ever!!! i was late to an event on saturday while stuck in the drive through for 20mins!! sunday i thought i'd try again considering it's the only local starbucks with a drive through. and the line was around the plaza. and not moving. line inside was just about out the door after weaving in and out inside the store. and then the mobile ordering "wasn't available". it shouldn't be this hard to get a cup of coffee! i drove 5 mins down the road to the other starbucks on g2bay and 19 and i was in and out even with a few people in line. but again, late because of this location. the staff clearly needs more efficient training or better management. i've very rarely had quick, easy or polite service. such a shame bc in a great location.

i go to starbucks all the time and never had a bad experience until i came to this one and noticed my drink was really light. i opened it up and my drink was more than a quarter of the way empty when i received it. i feel i should get a full cup of coffee if i'm paying for that so i went to ariel and she was extremely rude to me and made me feel stupid for asking her to fill my cup all the way. she proceeded to roll her eyes and pour someone else's steamed milk into my cup, handed it back to me and never once apologized. i'll drive the extra couple miles next time to get better customer service

this starbucks is located in a publix. i believe it used to be a safeway. the location is new and updated. the storefront inside the publix is small but they offer everything from drinks to pastries to sandwiches. the prices seem pretty comparable to an actual starbucks. plenty of parking in the shopping center as well. the staff was friendly and nice. we just got bacon gouda breakfast sandwiches but they were great and nice and warm. i would come here again.
they always get my order right but i wish they had more tables because this place is always poppin' and there's not enough room to study. the baristas are so kind and always welcoming!

i wanted to take a moment to give a shout out to this location. my daughter and i made a special trip because the staff is so great. the seating area outside is nice and spread out. they welcomed our dog and visited with us. we will be back!

i really like the layout of this starbucks. it has ample seating for the area and lots of different options. i have found myself here more frequently recently since i've had numerous business meetings in the area. it is always busy but not crazy. i also appreciate how tidy the bathrooms are!

the guy who was at the front was having a bad day. i just got two waters around 7pm, so it wasn't busy at all. he just acted like he was having a crappy day and didn't want to deal with customers. okay, i get it. so i left a tip anyways. but when i got my water, one had a long light brownish gold hair, i thought it was from the girl who got the waters. i told him and he rolled his eyes at me, told me "uh. that's weird because she doesn't touch the waters at all." i know, it comes from a fountain. but dude, there was a hair in my water, inside the cup when i just got it, and i have jet black hair. no way it was mine. still sitting here, sipping on my new water ‍ he needs an attitude check.
i love this location. rachel and pioquinto are my favorite baristas. they are terrific!

me and my husband love starbucks!!! we stopped at this starbucks on our way to brandon we ordered what we normally get but the price was significantly higher than what we normally pay! so of course we asked the shift supervisor mason who said we can charge whatever we want at this starbucks ... then we asked the cashier genny and she said it's an up-charge to add extra sugar. so we asked her to take off one drink she took it off the shift leader had to come and take it off so he was rolling his eyes being absolutely rude! then genny added the amount on a gift card when i asked her to put it on my card she said well you have to wait for the shift supervisor. in total we were waiting over 15 min for a small drink. the drink was sitting on the counter and we had already paid so i asked her to get me the drink and she said "well if i didn't have to deal with your receipt you would have your drink!!!" i will never be back to that starbucks that was the worst service i have ever received!!!!

we were in yardley and went to this starbucks numerous times. i had their very large decaf latte (with only two shots) and it was made perfectly as you would always expect from starbucks. and they had current new york times and wall street journals for purchase (not often available unless you are in a kind of "class" area/place. great views and ok seats,
starbucks is starbucks. nice atmosphere and typical $bucks

they served me cold coffee. as soon as it was handed over i could feel the chill. i ordered coffee with cream. come on starbucks, keep fresh and hot coffee ready! you are a coffee company and if you can't serve a good hot cup of coffee you should be ashamed. i handed back to the barista and she replaced it and said the person must have gotten it from a pot that was turned off. why would that coffee be available to be served. i'm a starbucks fan and this was downright disappointing. do better!

on the good - this starbucks is huge, having started its life as a fast food seafood place (the one named after a pirate). on the bad - the parking is a mere suggestion and it can be hard to get in and out of this location. i've used the drive thru many times with no issues, but recently met up with a group of friends to play board games. we took over the long table near the registers and had a fantastic time. a few other customers stopped to ask about the games we were playing and we invited a few to join (they declined - maybe they saw the "serious gamer" gleam in our eyes). it was a lovely afternoon. my only complaint was that the air conditioning was set to slightly arctic. however, i never travel without a hoodie so i survived pretty well. of course, the freezing temps gave me a chance to add that their restrooms were sparkly clean - so very devious, starbucks, but thanks for keeping the loos in great shape!

i frequently visit this starbucks when i am down town, which in the past 8 months has been a lot! this starbucks is very central as well as it is very close to my boyfriends (and soon to be my) place! i love starbucks with all of my heart, you could say i'm a bit of a coffee snob! i like that there is parking near by, although i usually just walk over. i've always been pleased with the service as well as the beverages here!

i always have pleasant experiences when i stop by this location embedded within the tampa premium outlets. small corner store centrally located by outdoor seating area, fountains, guest services and foodcourt. this starbucks provides friendly and attentive service, and shorter lines than most. so far my drinks have all been made well. i can't think of any mishaps or misspelled names on my cups. for a small shop there are plenty of places to sit and eat or lounge. not to mention the number of places to sit comfortably right outside. i tend to stop in for a chai latte or light snack while shopping since i'm not a fan of the foodcourt. since i'm a huge fan of the neighboring coach store, this starbucks can't be missed. since tampa locals seem to need starbucks to live, they nailed it by opening one in every mall here. the outlets are no exception.

filthy is an understatement. there are gnats and all kinds of bugs everywhere. last time i went the display food had bugs all over and they informed me the manager didn't know what the doors were and threw them away.... tables always dirty, floors never swept with crumbs everywhere, and too many staff members are rude and cussing while carrying on their private conversations loudly.

one of the better starbucks in boise, has big parking lot, a drive through, and outdoor patio seating with umbrellas. the service is spectacular and of course the food and drinks a perfect as usual. i definitely recommend this one if you are in the area.

this starbucks took 25 minutes and 4 tries to give me and my wife a grande iced caramel macchiato and venti skim chai. the drive thru line is a closed line so we were held hostage and could not drive away. they kept handing us drinks that were the wrong size, which is annoying but forgiveable, or the complete wrong drink (like, why did you hand me a tall latte and try to tell me it was a grande caramel macchiato?!). stay away!

i had started this review differently and was planning to go on a rant about slow service and how i've been to other starbucks and blah blah blah. this starbucks is big, it's clean and the employees are nice. they have a drive through which seems silly to me but i know people that like to use it. all i know is that when i stop in there, it seems like the employees move at glacial pace. if you're stopping here, plan to slow down a little (or a lot, depending on how fast you normally go) other than that, it's pretty nice and well, it's close to my office.
this starbucks has the slowest service. with a small line it took almost 30 minutes. maybe they need more than 2 people working on a busy saturday.

came to this starbucks and i will definitely be coming back. i frequent starbucks in different cities vet often. i love that they have a larger patio to sit outside, a drive thru, and a very convenient location to easily get in and out. ample parking as well. the service could of been quicker on a not too busy saturday mid-afternoon. but i didn't mind waiting a little bit longer. but my dog thoroughly enjoyed the puppychino from the barista and meeting new customers that walked by. i will be coming back soon! great experience here at his location
i come here quite often and the staff is always friendly and asks about my day. they always get my order right and have great customer service. the drive thru line moves pretty fast too

full service starbucks coffee & breakfast sandwiches inside i95 southbound rest-stop (to your far right.) clara barton is last i95 southbound rest area before delaware memorial al bridge. pls note: separate entrance into starbucks from outside of reston w/ regular & disables parking. staff were excellent! 4 stars (not 5) due to bathrooms being part of rest-stop & not serviced i.e. not clean, hand-towels empty, clothing hooks inside stalls broken. starbucks opened at 6:15am today - 9pm (not sure of posted hrs.

the worst starbucks i've ever visited. not that any are good, but this one is especially bad. i always ask for less syrup because their drinks are sickeningly sweet. they can never get this right. not once have they ever made a drink the way i ask. i will never be back.

one of the worst customer service i have ever seen!!! this girl was so rude to me! she did not greet me at all just stood there staring at me and waiting for me to say something. then took my order and didnt say a word just started dojng her thing and talking to her co workers!! just the worst!! and the drink i ordered was so disgusting!
long line, slow service & bad it system that kept freezing and the cherry on top - an unhelpful staff

it's starbucks, ya' know? and there's nothing wrong with that. i like this location; in the morning, the west-facing patio is out of the sun's death rays. the staff are usually caffeinated enough to be friendly. and there always seems to be nice people in this store. it's no exo, but it's a nice starbucks at a nice location.

i love starbucks. i get coffee about every other day. never have i had to wait 25 minutes for my coffee. 15-20 people behind me got theirs before me. this is after reminding them twice. this store needs to get it together.

i love the location and its outdoor seating area of this starbucks. turn your outdoor chair around so that it faces the sidewalk and pretend you're at a parisian cafe watching the world go by. on a cold or rainy day, they have a very nice indoor area to sit in. they have breakfast sandwiches, salads, snacks of many types. my husband always buys his whole bean coffee here. we love to come down with our dog to get a coffee, frappicino (sp) and a snack to people watch. they also have tea for the tea drinkers. bottled water, orange juice and other beverages are available for purchase. would i come back here. you know i will. i'll see you in the funny pages.

i stopped in this starbucks on my way from delaware county to new york city (all the way up from florida) and the barista, her name was armani and a target employee named brenda were awesome. they were fast, friendly, helpful and professional. this was the kind of customer service i ensure with my own business so it was good to see it in the hustle and bustle of the city. thanks ladies!

picture it: it's the middle of winter. the current temperature is dipping below zero. all you want is a steaming hot, caffeinated beverage. but, you're short on time and really? who wants to get out of the car it took so long to warm up? the seat is finally toasty warm and your feet are finally beginning to thaw and besides all that, every surface of pavement everywhere is covered in ice (which, we all know, isn't the least bit fun to attempt to traverse on foot--unless, of course, those feet have ice skates on them). enter starbucks drive-thru. yahtzee! not only does this location offer drive-thru service. but, the service is fast and friendly. the barista didn't seem the least inconvenienced to be leaning out into sub-arctic temperatures to hand me a steaming cuppa fabulous and even smiled while doing so. now, that's service. get to it!

this is a very packed starbucks right off the freeway in my hometown. on weekdays you can count on everything being good--the baristas take a lot of care with the drinks. my wife is very picky, always asking for extra hot, and this location gets it right. on weekends the backup teen crew is less strong, slows down the drive-thru at times and makes the occasional mistake. overall the coffee is done well here. not enough seating. people like to hang out at this location, and it's hard to get a seat except when it's nice outside. parking--just not enough spots and very poorly designed.

i really like the layout of this starbucks. there is a variety of seating choices (long wooden benched tables, small armchairs, and standard tables, with outdoor seating as well), with plenty of electric sockets for laptop use. and being that this starbucks isn't the biggest, i think they use the space nicely. my drinks have always been made just fine here, and the baristas are friendly. parking is pretty easy, so what more could you ask for? a great location to stop at after a morning workout at spectrum!

today is my birthday...i have the starbucks app and have entered my bday in before so i know they have it....i went in and asked how i get my free birthday drink and they said i have to get an email...i checked and i was like ok but i didn't get an email can i just show you my license i promise today is my birthday... no... no free drink for me. even though many ppl have told me they have showed starbucks their license and gotten a free drink on their bday....also worse than that i ordered a drink i order all the time it's the dragon fruit refresher... paid $6 on my birthday and today it tasted like pink sink water.. had zero flavor rad... thanks guys
i don't know why i came back. told myself never again and here i am 20 mins into my order and no drinks... terrible.

not impressed, but i'll stick with 2 stars instead of 1 since i'm not a regular. staff was snooty (obviously friendly with each other, but i felt like i was intruding on the "cool" table at high school). my blackberry tea lemonade (not even an espresso drink) took forever to make, despite being the only one in line with 6 staff members. (again, i'll give benefit of doubt...the drive thru was hopping.) lots of outdoor seating, but who wants to be outside in southern arizona? i missed the driveway 3 times trying to get to the dang place, too. all in all not a great place from what i experienced.

i've given this location several opportunities to change my mind. but every time i come they disappoint me. i'm a pretty basic coffee drinker, just a basic java chip trap is what i get each time and every time they have made it has been terrible. from a compete mess covering my hands in chocolate to completely watery, or flat no chocolate. the only reason i even placed two stars is because of a funny gentleman running the drive threw admitting his faults and making it laughable. the rest of the staff i've dealt with im lucky if they even make eye contact with me and i'm a pretty friendly person so i'm surprised.

coffee and service were as expected. the location itself is challenging to get in and out of, being that it shares a building with a famous fast food restaurant. the bathrooms were a bit confusing, you have to get a key code from a barista, and honestly were rather messy. though for the bottom line, the coffee was crafted exactly as i'd expect, and service was friendly. so i can not be too harsh. i would just reccomend some kind of sign with directions for the bathroom doors. and to keep up on them a little better.

if i can give this starbucks negative stars i would. i was staying at gsr for a night and sunday morning my family and i decided to get some breakfast sandwiches and some teas. my daughter and i both love their black tea lemonades so we ordered 2 of those and my brother ordered the peach green tea. cashier was very nice and helpful. the girl making my drink i believe her name was maria or mariah but she had like blueish hair was extremely rude. i always try my drink before leaving and while i was waiting for my other drinks i tried mines and it i already knew it was wrong by looking at the color. it tasted disgusting. not only that the way she made it was wrong. what i ordered is a shaken black tea lemonade and i had asked her to remake my drink because it was not sweetened at all but she made a remark and said well you need to specify it when ordering well fyi the cashier never even bothered to ask and usually at starbucks they do. so she then takes back my drink and asks me to hold my lid. i myself work in a same field as hers and i tell her that i didn't want to cross contaminate and asked her to toss it out and give me new one. she takes my drink back and just pumps sweetener in one cup and poured the exact same one over! and not only that she didn't even use the shaker like she was supposed to. i had to ask the other employee there and she said yes she's doing it wrong. out of my whole order i was missing my daughters tea as well and had to inform her. my brothers peach tea was made the same way. she didn't shake it. she just poured it all in a cup and put ice in so his drink was overly sweet. how was i supposed to shake my tea and make sure the sweetener tea and lemonade gets all mixed up? it's not possible! i don't know how the employees are trained here or if the girl was having a bad morning because it really looked like she was out of it. i don't blame her since it's reno but for paying almost 40$ for starbucks i expected it to be good. worst starbucks visit ever. my daughter is only 6 years old and she even noticed that her tea was not what it's supposed to taste like. if a 6 year old says something then you guys really have a problem and need to fix it asap since you guys are a well known name. i don't think i will be visiting this starbucks location when i come back to gsr.
as of friday november 18, 2016 they will be closed temporarily for renovations (pics enclosed), you will have to visit other nearby locations.

this is your average starbucks. i went at around noon on a sunday so of course it was busy as could be! the wait time wasn't too long, maybe about 5 minutes to get my drink. i ordered a caramel frappuccino. it wasn't the best one i had ever had from there, unfortunately. it was like it had no ice in it or had melted or something. it wasn't warm or anything, it just didn't have the consistency you'd expect from your typical frappe. my husband ordered his usual cappuccino and he said it was pretty good! i'll definitely be back to give this location another try.

first time at this location, well it is brand new. stopped in to take advantage of double star day and some pumpkin spice goodies! got a grande psl and a pumpkin cream cheese muffin. i got the muffin warmed and it was perfect for dunking in my latte. tasted sooooo good! my latte was hot and delicious with cold whipped cream on top. very clean and spacious interior. i sat in a counter height chair down at the end of the prep area, very comfortable!

whether it's 5:30am or 5:30pm, customer service should be top notch. i called in advance for travelers and was told "it might be ready when you need it if we don't get a lot of customers this morning." i did pay for the travelers when i arrived so i would expect to be treated as a customer as well.
great location, good staff we ran the gauntlet in no to get to this starbucks which was under a mile from our hotel near the airport. large location, friendly staff and they got our drinks right!
my favorite starbucks. the people are always welcoming and the know i like my iced americano with extra ice. good place to study and relax and chat with friends.

i go here all the time as it is right near my work. christina, seth, dante. . . the entire staff is awesome and more than happily indulge my indecisiveness when i want to try something different. yay!
employees here were extremely polite and when my drink took longer than expected they gave me a card for my next drink to be on them, if i lived closer i would defiantly be back!

i love how cute this starbucks is. i also love how much parking they have. i must admit this review is biased because i'm enamored with the new tea lemonades. the guava white tea and the peach green tea are my absolute favorites (with and without alcohol). anyway, i had a meeting here and although there is a lot of seating it doesn't have as many private or separate spaces. it gives you a college union feel (i.e. long tables, lots of windows, etc). but i liked the vibe and the customer service was amazing! the manager was super friendly and the barista let me taste smaller before committing.

starbucks are usually starbucks, except for the staff. they usually make the difference any place you might go. well, the staff here was very nice. they watched my laptop while i left for a moment. they made excellent suggestions while ordering. the only complaint this day was that someone left something in or on something for too long. there was a burning smell for a while. it was nice and cold inside while it was hot outside, and this makes all the difference when drinking coffee in the summer.

what can i say? it's starbucks. they do have some of the more friendly starbucks employees i've encountered at this location. i think because of its proximity to 465 and carmel, there is always a line at the drive-thru. you will get much faster service and be much less irritated just parking and going inside for your coffee. trust me, you don't want to be stuck behind the carmel soccer mom ordering her venti 1 pump caramel, 1 pump white mocha, 2 scoops vanilla bean powder, extra ice frappuchino with 2 shots poured over the top (apagotto style) with caramel drizzle under and on top of the whipped cream, double cupped.

everyone that works here is very friendly. i love coming in in the morning to get my morning coffee and sometimes a nice pastry item. i spent a lot of time here at night to working on my homework and everyone, no matter what shift, is always so friendly and eager to be helpful. this starbucks is always clean. it is a little small but there are plenty of places to plug in your laptop if you need to.

if it weren't for the fact that this was one of the few late night places on campus that accepts dining dollars, i would have avoided this starbucks like the plague. staff here are incredibly rude and condescending, & come up with bogus charges (like adding extra syrup when stores are not supposed to charge extra for iced drinks made without ice) when you place your order, it's almost like you owe the manager, 'miss pam' a favour, despite the fact that you are a paying customer. the fact that this store is affiliated with penn dining is the only reason why people still come here, as the attitude of all the baristas is absolutely appalling.
used the walk-up window today - love this option for quick service with a smile! staff is friendly, seating is ample with patio.

stopped into this starbucks for octane blast to get me through the morning. i've visited many of these coffee shops and this is the only one that seems to lack a certain level of organization for ordering. i walked in and was totally confused people standing everywhere...literally a line out the door for placing orders and receiving. i ordered a solo expresso macchiato and a blueberry scone. they must have earned it in the microwave, because when i received it it was a blueberry brick. however, the overall experience was a pleasant one. the staff was extremely accommodating...scone was replaced and the coffee got me thru the day.

i went today and was greeted by raven who i have seen there before. she suggested a new choice of beverage and started my with a smile on my face and in my heart. kudos to raven, starbucks is lucky to have you represent the organization.

service use to be "5" stars...been 4 times recently, they're always out of the protein boxes and chicken sausage breakfast wraps. i have yet to find they keep these items stocked. the summit starbucks is "5 stars"!

this review amends a previous review about this location. since they have added google internet, this starbucks has great internet. though the service here is not my favorite ... you can't blame the baristas because this is one of the flagship stores with so much foot traffic that it is probably hard to follow people. nevertheless i have started to get to know a few of the baristas and they are courteous, friendly, and helpful. the place is relatively clean, with my only gripe being the seating because of the location near ucsb campus. during finals and midterms ... students crowd this area but i am okay with it....
i love this starbucks! staff is always friendly and i like the second floor seating. i think this is the only place in sb to get coffee at 6am

this starbucks is probably one of the worst mannered. employees seem irritated quickly & are not friendly. i always get my frappuccino double blended as i hate crunching on ice. the barista taking my order asked me in a very rude way why i get my drink this way & when i answered he scoffed at me. i will not be returning or giving my business to this location. anymore.
friendly workers, of course expensive. i can't understand why they don't put up a fence by the drive through. the view is all of their garbage, very unsightly!

i'm not sure how i've managed to never review this location! but, here goes... i've been to this location so many times over the years. mostly drive thru. but when i lived in the area, i did occasionally come and do some work, away from my kids. the inside is a bit small and can sometimes be hard to find a spot. but it's a pretty busy location. i've never had a bad experience here. does one ever, really, at coffee places? we bean heads love our caffeine dealers. my peppermint mocha today had a bit to much flavoring in it, but, it was still an uplifting cup of joe. altho, i do think it's total crap that the price of my favorite flavor goes up, simply because starbucks deems it a "holiday flavor" right now.

visited this starbucks on veteran's day. my best friend is a veteran and we thought we would go check their special for a free cup of coffee for veterans. my friend went to order and asked about the veteran's day coffee. a barista rudely said back to my friend "um excuse me what are you even talking about". there was a sign right in front of her about the special. the barista then looks at the sign and says "oh is today even veteran's day?". then asked for my friend's military id three individual times. not a very nice way for my friend to spend her day. disgusted by the way this barista treated my friend. her name was tess. refusing to come back to this location due to her behavior. disgusted, and ruined my friend's day.

starbucks are all the same, sure. but this is the worst one. takes forever, make drinks wrong, staff unhelpful and not cheerful. more worth it to go to the target next door, go in, and use theirs. you would probably save time doing that too.
prepare to wait for at least 20mins gift your order. it happened twice, not doing it again.

small location hidden in the first floor of the canal shops. there was a small line when we arrived but it took a bit to get or drink. they don't have seating in the store, but they're a couple of tables right outside it in the mall. they were all packed so we waited for one to open up. since it's a smaller location they have a smaller selection of options in the cold case and in the shelves for merchandise. they didn't have the you are here mugs at this location when we went. side note- we picked up an uber from here and they couldn't map this location so we had to pin point the shops. fyi. overall: b- okay stop but not the best in the quarter for the chain.

this starbucks does a great job of getting you out the door quickly with the resources they have. it's quite a bit faster than most starbucks that i frequent, and i've come here when there was a line of 3 or 4 people waiting for their drinks. don't plan to sit and work for 8 hours, the location is very tiny and there are 2 tables in the whole place. also, the bathroom is locked, so don't plan on stopping in to use the bathroom, paying customers only! probably the most efficient starbucks i've visited!

when i visit this starbucks, if i take the drive thru, i sit in line for 30 minutes, then another 5 because they dont make your drink til you get to the window to pay i walked inside today and myself and about 10 other people have been standing around waiting for our drinks for 20 minutes this starbucks needs restaffing, badly. every time i'm here it's like it's the first day for everyone working

the remodel is awful. sterile and not friendly or inviting for hanging out. most likely this is by design by someone to drive more traffic through and discourage long stays. coffee is still good but the food is pre packaged and meh at best. so for over all id rate sb my fav for coffee but they ruined a good thing with the remodel.

i made the mistake of walking in to place my order, big mistake! at the time i was the the only person is line and watched as multiple cars placed and received their orders before i received my one cup of tea. the customer who came in after me received his order, had time to chat with the employees and even left as i was still waiting for mine. i order the same item over and over and it's never the same. i don't understand why it's so inconsistent. i'd rather drive out of my way to another location than stop here again.

i think this is a great starbucks location in tampa. it's located on the corner of a busy intersection, at kennedy and westshore, making it equally accessible for you to leave and go take care of multiple things that you need to do in the area or, for you to come from various locations in the area to take a quick coffee break. unlike starbucks locations that are closer to downtown, this location is located in a plaza with ample parking, making it a more stress free option when you are on your coffee run. the inside of the store is spacious, with lots of table seating. i also really like the addition of high top table seating in the middle of the store. it makes for a more communal feel and it also allows slightly larger groups of people to congregate and meet up, always a plus for a coffee shop. the staff here are courteous and take care of your drink or food orders fast. and the bathrooms are quite clean, another plus considering the high volume of people that pass through the store. will definitely drop by if i'm in the area again :)
probably the dirtiest starbucks i've ever been too. this includes rest stops. clean it up. windex the doors. clean the bathrooms. gross

want to be late to class or an appointment? come to this starbucks. you know you're desperate for good coffee when you're willing to wait in a 20 minute line, when there are other places that sell coffee around campus. the coffee, was good, the staff moved as quickly as they could, but they had one person working the register and a line of 50 people at a time. starbucks should hire more staff for this location to please customers and ease the burden on their employees.
it's in a mall but we never could find it! called and called and called yet they nevet answered the phone! really poor!

i'm a basic bitch that loves starbucks so i will leave nothing positive reviews! service is pretty good for the center city location! it sucks that it's pretty crowded but that's not their fault! bigger location would help but that wouldn't keep me from coming back and getting my ice cold latte!

enjoying a flat white with friends on this cold january evening. love this location. a nice mixture of high school and college kids plus plenty of us more mature crowd. the baristas are always friendly and always happy to recommend a new item. parking is very convenient with street parking and lot parking.

great location. staff very friendly. i wish i could remember the name of the young man who took my order. he had red hair and was outgoing and had a nice friendly smile. lauren prepared my drink and it was yummy! i hate seeing the reviews complaining about the clientele, it was a beautiful day and i sat outside with a homeless man. instead of complaining sit down and engage.

this starbucks is a perfect model for customers on the go, even for impatient customers who are always in a rush like myself. we all know when we don't have our coffee + we are rushing, grumpiness can ensue. the staff here though is on top of it, friendly and fast.. even when dealing with not-so-patient customers like myself! will always come back here when it's convenient.

not my favourite starbucks... it's much too busy for me. it isn't well lit and the size of it is kind of oddly shaped, very long, which makes everything squishy. staff are nice and i would say that i go here primarily because of the convenience factor... why not have a latte to sip as you stroll around southgate? at least they make your drinks properly here.

always friendly here. they got busy while we were here and they handled it well. lots of customers with multiple drinks in their orders. they made great recommendations depending on my taste. they will always steer you in the right direction. if you haven't tried a clover brew, you've missed out. you can get any of their beans ground fresh and made to order, hot or cold.
made it the way i like. not the cleanest place i've ever scene. also the lady next to me spilled her hot drink all over my one leg boo .
good luck getting what you order. the management tends to yell at the staff while you wait for your drink.

i have reviewed this starbucks before, and for this exact same reason. why is this location just completely unable to remain open for a fixed schedule? for the past 2 weeks they have either been closed or running on a reduced schedule. there is no excuse for this. someone on your team got covid? not an excuse, you are a fucking business. your team is doing training? not an excuse, do it outside of business hours. there is nothing more important to a business than making money and this reduced schedule/not being open is bullshit. this location just needs to be closed down. there is a starbucks the next exit down with better service anyways. please starbucks, get a hold on this location. get it under control. poor leadership and business decisions being made here.
life saver! absolutely love the staff here, and the coffee is always amazing. if you want a good laugh with great coffee, i strongly suggest coming here!

this is the worst starbucks i've ever been to. the people who work there should go to star bucks reeducation camp. they ignored me for their friends who were behind me. it took more than 15 minutes for me to get the three drinks i ordered. were also rude to other customers in the store. they should close this store and start over.

thankyou for the recommendations to add 2 pumps of raspberry to my oprah chai tea. yum. also the green tea iced latte venti is my favorite and tomato mozzarella panini n cake pops and iced black tea but make it sweeter, more sugar!

happy to add a star from my previous review. ordering ahead on the starbucks mobile app for pick-up in store is super convenient. i've enjoyed their almond milk honey flat white over the past couple months and always enjoy indulging in a holiday drink. this location's drive thru is usually pretty long but it goes pretty fast. seating inside is currently closed due to covid but you can still go in and pick up your drink if you ordered ahead.

give mary a raise!!!! what a sweetheart. i was apprehensive about ordering a drink i'd never had before. mary reassured me that if i didn't like it she would make me something else. in fact she said, "i don't want you to spend $5 on a drink you don't like." one of the best starbucks experiences of my life! also, i love the drink.

asked for a large coffee and they looked at me like what is that. i said oh sorry a venti coffee. worse coffee i have had in a long time. no wonder i going there over 10 years ago. sorry i had to try it again.

i think this is the best starbucks in the santa barbara/goleta area. it certainly has the best atmosphere. feels more like the seattle area ones with some care put into the interior. warm and inviting. interesting wall art and a good vibe. a lot of seating options. large area outside. consistently pleasant service. very comfortable, handsome starbucks. open late too, which is much more urban feeling. great! a lot of students study here so it can really fill up.

i'm on my way to work a overnight and stopped here to get a caramel frappe. i get my drink looks really pretty until i take a drink and let me tell you it taste like a water frappe with whip cream and caramel. horrible
i love my coffee but they were really sloooooooow! i miss the old staff they had.......

i've given this starbucks multiple opportunities to redeem itself, and it has yet to do so. while the location is impeccable, products are inconsistent. as someone who has been a barista, i can see drinks being made incorrectly. the staff as a whole is rude (a couple exceptions, but not enough to persuade me to go back). the nail in the coffin was asking for a skinny drink, receiving a full fat/full sugar drink, and the barista wanted to argue about what i had asked for. i get that this is a busy location, and i can overlook the occasional issue, but this location is consistently inconsistent.

we stopped in here to quickly warm up with a cup of coffee. i always am nice to people serving me, especially in big cities, because their jobs are probably stressful. plus, unless you were raised in a cave, you should just say please and thank you regardless. even with that though, i'm bummed the staff was terse and unfriendly. when i asked if they had decaf coffee they almost seemed angry i asked. soooo awkward. i've experienced better because the layout didn't seem conducive to this location either. for the size of the space, the tables were oddly placed and everyone was packed in like sardines. the people next to us were doing some odd things and we were exceptionally close to them. while the coffee was fine, just consider getting in and out. its the best route!
nothing warms you on a cold dreary day like a vanilla latte. love this starbucks! always busy but always great!

this morning i decided to treat myself and my daughter to some starbeezes. the drive through line was long but i was not worried about that as we had left early to allow plenty of time. i do undertstand that it is morning and that wasn't the problem. i am a avid coffee drink so when my order isn't right it doesn't start my day off on the right foot. i had ordered a venti but received a grande. i did notice that both our coffee's were on the light side so i removed the lid's and they very alittle over 3/4 the way full so that was a disappointment as well. i wanted to go inside and get my larger coffee but was unable to as my daughter chimed in i don't want to be late for school.

spacious with decent amount of seating. large parking lot, with additional parking lot available. not sure what it is about starbucks, but there are always massive amounts of flies no matter where you sit. barista was friendly and patient with us while we were ordering.
