never seen a covered drive-thru quite like this at a starbucks before- way cool! and that boise sky tonight - wow!!! at first we went through the drive-thru. later i visited inside to use their wifi. shhh- i'm a paying customer, no judging! ha! everything looks typical of most starbucks stores. clean and comfortable with easy listening music overhead and friendly baristas behind the counter. there was almost no line either inside or in the drive thru. but the little there was moved quickly. they made my drink correctly and fast - yay! was a little disappointed to find this starbucks apparently does not have idaho designed products. no "i'm here" mugs or localized artsy gift cards. darn. kudos to the barista at the window for his extra patience when my cell phone decided to randomly reboot just as we were pulling up the starbucks app to pay. gotta collect those magic drink stars! wheee!

nice upgrade on the remodel but i think the staff needs more training. i was charged double for an iced coffee. as i'm watching my drink be made he pours cold brew instead of coffee. leaves the cold brew pouring since it's on tap and starts talking to other staff. it continued to over pour for about a minute before i started to say something to him. he makes the drink again with cold brew and calls my name. i informed him i wanted iced coffee not cold brew and he informed me they are out. would have been nice to know when i ordered. and was still charged double for iced coffee and not cold brew. will be saving my time and money and never returning.
love this place. very cozy, though i never sit down. i always just grab coffee and go. the staff here is almost always great

most joyful morning staff i've seen in awhile! also, while it may be difficult to pull into according to some of the other reviews, it's also located within walking distance to car repair places such as church brothers and greens auto repair, which is why i came here in the first place. not a bad spot and i would totally come back to say hello to the fantastic baristas.

i have tried to love this location, and earlier this week there were new people working in the morning and things actually felt like they were improving, but today the same crew was back and the problems persisted! mobile orders not ready (15+ minutes after being placed) or not made at all because they are out of something. drinks consistently made incorrectly. today i am not sure there is any espresso in my drink it is so pale. i truly believe the problems stem from the morning manager. she is rude to employees, barking orders, being very rude, but not actually doing much herself. when she is there the employees look miserable and their work obviously suffers. she also gets very rude when concerns are brought up by customers. i was so excited that there was finally a starbucks in algiers, but the service leaves something to be desired.

the baristas here are outstanding! they're friendly and make grabbing coffee here very pleasant. and yet the parking is so atrociously awful i often drive to this location, see that there is no parking, and then head somewhere else for coffee. there is no place to wait for a spot, and i've even been double parked into a spot before. it's just a disaster

i came to this location at about 7:45 am and they were slammed! even though they were hustling, every single employee greeted me with a smile as i walked in the door.. i made my order and left in less than 5 minutes! needless to say my friday is off to a great start! thank you starbucks employees !!

starbucks are the same for the most part - but this starbucks is pretty rough. the parking is minimal, and you're likely to get trapped by the drive thru line if you do manage to get a parking spot. the line is usually 4-8 cars deep, so getting through the drive thru usually takes at least 20 minutes. the employees are friendly and professional, and the coffee is fine, but the parking and drive thru time is abysmal, likely because it's the only one nearby.

my favorite starbucks. i'm biased because it's down the street from me, but it's never done me wrong! i used to come every single day in high school, and they've only gotten my order wrong, maybe three times. even for as busy as they get, the baristas are always in top of things. although high school was quite a while ago, i still enjoy coming to this location. now, when are they going to renovate like the other ones have in this area ??

long drive-thru time but i am not going to blame the workers. i am going to blame the company for selling food that requires heating. the person who took my order was super pleasant and my coffee was hot and exactly as ordered. i've never had a bad beverage from this location....just don't go if you're in a hurry.

welcome to the schoolyard, where first impressions are all that matter. you trip on your shoelaces running down the hill and forever you'll be known as the kid who rolled headfirst down the playground slope. to that end, i agree with kristine d.'s recent review of her interaction at this starbucks. if this starbucks isn't careful, they'll forever be known as the location with that one employee who finds the most basic tasks too complex to complete. his name, according to the several grumbling regulars behind me in the growing line, is alberto. he will perpetually ask you to repeat your order, to scan your phone again. it's not a bashful, flustered "sorry, i didn't hear you." it's an expectant, dismissive, "again? speak up." there is no apology in his tone, only entitlement, as if by ordering a tall coffee we are standing in the way of his efforts to do as little work as possible. it's always easy to tell when this particular employee is working register - which is all he ever seems to work - because the line will stretch twice as far as the average morning. even his coworkers seem put-out by his enervated and apathetic pressing of buttons and mumbling of orders. "so that's a grande mocha, which comes to a total of..." "no," a customer says, "i ordered a tall latte." the manager in the background rolls her eyes and offers an apologetic smile.

went in here with some friends around 10 a.m. on a thursday while on a girls' trip to s.b. and ordered the same drink i always do - a venti iced coffee with half and half. the drink was $0.60 more than usual. i asked why the price was different and the unfriendly lady ringing my drink said it was because i asked for half and half. what??????? i told her that if it was a charge for that, i would've put it in myself (which i used to do before a starbucks at another location once told me that they would be happy to do it for me) and she annoyingly asked if i wanted a refund. obviously, i wasn't going to hold up the line, but now i feel like i should have. unbelievable!

this starbucks is a hit or miss! i have gone there every day for the past 8 years! sometimes the coffee is good. sometimes awful! the lines are ridiculous! weekends i go to hendersonville as you may as well park at this one! the people there are very friendly but they get my coffee wrong at least 4 days out of 7! if this continues i will start making y on coffee at home!

this store is dirty and gross. employees are rude and i even saw water leaking from their ceiling onto their coffee machines for hours. dirty!! onto their drinks. avoid. go local!!! so many other cheaper and better local coffee places within the same block.

this is not the best starbucks but they get four stars for having quick service and the standard starbucks fare. if you get there early in the morning, there is typically no line at the drive-thru. the parking lot for this starbucks is horribly designed and the drive-thru is even worse. it is especially at the apex of a u-turn. if you have a larger car, it is impossible to get close to the callbox. i have seen lines here back up to ten cars. the wait can be long, so if you are in a hurry, this is not your spot. on this particular visit, i went inside to avoid the drive-thru line and ended up leaving before placing an order. starbucks could probably put another location in ambler. this one just gets too much traffic..
worst service ever! so freaking slow i would fire all 3 girls that worked today if i were their boss
the happiest group of people work here, especially considering how constantly busy it is.

we visited this starbucks in the evening. it was pretty spacious but that could be because i'm used to nyc-sized starbucks. the bathroom could've been cleaner and parking was a little hard to find. i don't necessarily blame them for this, but there were groups of rowdy kids just hanging around since there was a pokestop nearby. it was annoying, so we took our order to go. the employees were cool. everything else was pretty standard for starbucks.

nice, cool spot always. never know what type of entertainment you might get in there with it being downtown on meridian. only thing i ordered what i usually order at other locations and it tasted slightly different(white chocolate mocha latte) not sure what was up with that

the staff at this location are always sweet and quick. i've spent hours studying, chairing with friends, and grabbing a coffee to go and every time i'm pleasantly surprised by the customer service. on days they mess something up, they quickly and apologetically apologize for it. the milk and creamer at the front is a little odd, but works ok with the space. i recommend relaxing the patio and people watching in the afternoon.

what an unpleasant visit to this starbucks! out of bagels. the outside tables were dirty and clearly had not been cleaned for a long time. there was a used band aid near one of them also. the restroom was absolutely filthy. no paper towels to wipe your hands and toilet seat covers strewn all over the floor. the store had 5 employees working but clearly keeping the place clean is not a priority here. starbucks stores vary in quality i know but this is about the worst i have seen in tucson.

got a strawberry refresher and a sausage sandwich with egg no cheese. and you can guess...there's cheese on my sandwich argh. no biggie but when you're in a rush and don't have time to exchange it can really kill your mood! the drink was really good though! they mixed it really well!

as cozy good as caffeine gets on this casino floor! this starbucks is tucked away in a corner and could get pretty busy. the young people who are working there are very personable and do a commendable job of keeping up with the long lines when they form. if you are staying at harrah's or visiting and need caffeine, this is where you want to go for good coffee as it arguably is the best option you might have.
i smellllll.... cough-feeeee.... say it with an new york accent, it's fun! =]
our favorite starbucks we ever regularly went to, they are always fast, super polite, and never mess up drinks. shoutout to alex, the best barista who always knew our drinks when we came
i suppose that this is okay if you need a quick cup of joe before you fly out. it serves a purpose and the you get served quickly.
great service! i had a birthday treat that didn't show up on my card and the friendly barista gave it to me, no questions asked! i appreciate such a friendly face and a nice welcoming attitude.

my favorite starbucks i've been to so far in philly, everyone is always friendly and most importantly it's always fast no matter how many people are crowded in here! they know how to be absolutely efficient and i appreciate it so much!

used to come here to work while having a coffee and then some tea for a few hours. love the place because the staff is nice (some more than others) and they know how to make the promotional drink (cocoa cappuccino) that i used to get all the time (maybe 2-3 years ago now). they still make it for me when i go in which is great :). had to cut back on coffee intake and only go in there from time to time and they still remember my drink when i go. thank you all! 5 stars only because of the staff that remembers my drink :p other drinks i get is a tall 2 pump mocha (good balance of coffee + sweetness and hint of chocolate taste) and peppermint mocha. also love their spinach croissant (though sometimes i feel it is a bit too oily, just depends on mood)

man this is the only starbucks with a drive-thru for many miles and the girl that has been at the drive-thru window (lauren) is terrible. i say this because the last three times i've been through the drive-thru she has been at the window. every, single time, she cuts me off and i have to let her know i have more to order... every single time she gives me attitude when this happens and every single time she gets one of my drink orders wrong! i've never had an issue with any other employee or anytime i've gone inside. lauren doesn't belong at starbucks, wrong attitude and wrong drink orders... disappointing.
great starbucks in the area. they have a team of wonderful and friendly staff. the place is a bit on the smaller side, but it is rather cozy.

this starbucks has an awful high turnover rate and training new people tends to slow down the delivery of orders. there was once upon a time, when this location had a steady crew with the two "kims" working here. they were very friendly, consistent in their barista skills and super accommodating when they failed us, (which was very rare). now, it's a sure hit or miss and i have to pray to the sweet baby jesus above that these baristas get it right. so far, it's been pretty good but unlike today, they had no product for my pink refresher and the new store in algiers had no splenda. i opted for the violet refresher in lieu of pink (lol) and it doesn't taste bad at all. needless to say, this is my neighborhood starbucks and we've forged a good friendship with sandy who's been there a while and i look forward to her making our drinks.

this location is never crowded when i go in the morning (before 7am). they have parking spots right outside the entrance on meridian. they have messed up my coffee a few times. i would say i hate to have foam in my coffee. for some reason i ask for no foam and i still get it. a lot of it. i will not stop at this location again. i've given them enough chances. not worth my time when other locations can get it right.

usually starbucks are just known as starbucks. however, we're unique in st. louis and this location is often called "the starbucks that used to be a bank." it's pretty much your standard starbucks with a bit more seating than normal. they have a few patio tables and a a decent amount of indoor seating. you'll see an abnormal amount of students studying here. i don't think there are many colleges nearby so i suppose they're high schoolers? anyways, i don't really go to starbucks very often but this one ain't so bad. except if you're into drive throughs....cause there ain't none!

filthy dirty. we were there at 9 am and it was disgustingly dirty! then i had to go to the bathroom, big mistake!! as i walked in something green dropped from the ceiling and stained my shirt. at first i thought it was bird poop but then i realized it was the air freshener. then i couldn't turn on the light. i told the lady at the counter that the lights didn't work and she rudely said, "i know"! you should surely take the time to do to the other starbucks on state st.

this is by far the best starbucks in town, i come here to study and it's pretty quiet. there's plenty of seating and is pretty large for a starbucks which is really nice. they have the happiest, nicest employees i've ever met at a starbucks. it's always very clean as well. i always love coming here and it's becoming a regular spot for me.

worst customer service i have ever received at a star bucks. very simply argot 90% of our order and patiently waited 15 min & realized they must have forgot to make the last drink we ordered. no problem, it happens. but when we brought the issue to the attention of the barrista she rudely and sarcastically told me i just take them in the order i get them and were busy so you're ill get to your drink in a minute. took about 20 min to get a drink made. the entire time she was talking to a man (friend, boyfriend or the like) on the other side of the counter not paying any attention to what she was doing. i then asked the girl taking the orders for the managers name and phone number., she then rudely stated i'm taking care of another customer right now, you're going to have to wait. i told her actually we were also customers, who were there first and had not been taken care of appropriately. all i was looking for was a polite "i'm sorry but we'll get right on it). it's not the fact the drinks were made out of order or that i had to wait, but how they handled it when i addressed the issue. with family in town i had just spent about $40 there in the last two days. for the premium you pay at starbucks over indulgent positive customer service is expected. won't be visiting this starbucks at all in the future, nor will my friends and family.
big open windows, great customer service and clean atmosphere! if i was to have a business meeting, this would be a nice location in which there are multiple tables for 8 occupants.

the staff at this location always seem to be in a hurry and angry. i have my order ready. i approach the counter and as i am reciting my order, the cashier rolls his eyes at me! this has happened with more than one cashier. honestly, is it terribly difficult to write down venti sugar free caramel macchiato, skim milk, upside down?! another visit here i had just received a gift card for my birthday via facebook and was unsure how to redeem it. i asked the cashier who again, looked irritated that i was doing anything but handing over cash. she mumbled something to a colleague about it and the other barista said it could be scanned directly from my iphone. the only reason i come to this location is its proximity to my apartment. otherwise, i'd prefer to travel the extra mile to the nicer, friendlier location. get your act together street road starbucks.

the area for this starbucks is great- it's at the center of germantown in chestnut hill. there are plenty of chairs and lounge chairs to sit and do your work. it's quite so you can be really productive. just like any starbucks, the staff are friendly and attentive when you walk in the counter to order your drinks- other than that nothing special with the service.

i've been bummed out ever since hubbard & cravens left riley hospital. while i prefer visiting a local chain, the newer starbucks location in the eskenazi fifth third building is the next closest place i can get my espresso fix alleviated. they offer discounted days and coupons that you typically don't see at other starbucks locations. it also opens at 6 am, which is a treat for us early-morning types. maybe the 4 stars comes out of desperation due to lack of nearby espresso; whatever the reason, this location offers one a nice, new location to take a quiet break from work on the northwest side of campus.

first off the mice gross me out at this place. it's not the cleanest starbucks around. but really make sure you don't ask for a receipt at this place. i asked steph for a receipt on my order today. she said okay in a few minutes. then she asked me a second time what i want. i said a receipt. then i wait for her in the back room for several minutes and she comes out and asks me a third time what i want. i said a receipt and i'm not sure how else to explain what i want. she was totally bothered by the requests and said it would take a while to get one. fed up, i left. if they can't accommodate a simple request i'm guessing i know why they have a pest problem there. they really don't care. she and the staff can stay there and hang out with all the mice as i don't want to go back...
worst starbucks in nashville. slow service, overly busy, and the lattes and mochas are very bland tasting. very clean store though.

update: omg. i'm just done with this starbucks until they figure out that whatever they are doing is not working. i get it that it's in a busy location on a main street but all the more reason to make sure that things are running efficiently. the lines are forever long and sometimes the people on the registers are sooooooo freaking slow! beyond slow. the other day i stood in line for more than 10 minutes...i was third in line..! it's not like the line was out the door...i was third. then it took forever to get my drink...i drink black iced coffee...that's it. it just seems ridiculous to me that this store can't seem to get with it. it's a great store...the location is picturesque and perfect, it's comfortable and spacious, but if your in a hurry then it's not a good place to be. i live around the corner but i get in my car and drive 5 miles to the location in flourtown because they are quick and friendly and their employees always seem happy to be there. the chestnut hill location just needs an overhaul badly. also....think that mobile ordering would help this situation..!!!? nope! you can mobile order, then come in and find a place to wait and stand in the way while you wait and wait for your mobile order to be done. or you can wait a bit until it might be ready but then come in and find that because they didn't mark things not available in their system they don't have a single thing that u ordered. so now you have to re pick all your stuff based on what they do have. i've had to do that the last two times i mobile ordered which is supposed to save time. it doesn't at this location. i think i'll keep going to flourtown for a while and maybe during that time they will figure things out in chestnut hill.

came before 8am. great service at the register but terribly slow at making the drink. i ordered an iced green tea. i waited over ten minutes. will go out of my way to another location versus coming back.

i come to this starbucks cuz it's on my way to work. if i don't get my coffee fix i can be a real jerk! the baristas are always friendly whether it's drive-thru or inside. my coffee is always perfect so i can continue on my ride. the parking can be iffy since panera shares the space. but if i'm in need of strong coffee, starbucks is my place!

disgusted!!! racism!! can't sit in starbucks while black and wait for the rest if your party. gold rewards status will not patronize another starbucks until some firing take place and i don't mean relocating and a public apology from corporate. until then starbucks will not get another dime from me.

i have gone to this location three days in a row and have yet to receive a single cup of coffee. i waited in line in the drive-through for over 10 minutes each visit before leaving. all i wanted was a simple cup of coffee with cream. i guess i will going to the 7-eleven across the street now on.

while starbucks isn't the greatest coffee in the world, i have coke to be really attached to this location. the mid-day crew has come to know my order when i come in. i spend hours in here reading my bible and doing sermon prep. i the venti iced coffee, heavy cream and 3 stevia.

caroline is the sweetest starbucks employee i have ever encountered. as i was waiting for my coffee she asked me about my plans for the day and i could tell she genuinely cared. she really changed the mood of my whole day to being filled with positivity and i will now only be coming to this starbucks location. thank you caroline!!

this location is brand new, has a drive-thru, & the baristas are all super sweet! i've been here a few times already and my chai latte is always made perfectly by friendly and upbeat employees. not to mention, very fast! i'm typically in and out of the drive-thru in less than 5 minutes. there's also ample parking in case you want to go inside.

yelp keeps suggesting that i review starbucks, so here goes... it's starbucks...there's nothing that i can say that hasn't been said already. except for maybe this: i despise coffee. as my mom used to say, it's an insult to the palate and an abomination to the stomach. so, much to my surprise, they actually have a pretty decent tea selection. not that i'm an expert...i recently had to take up tea drinking after voluntarily giving up mountain dew after a 20 year bender. every once in a while, i have a friend who wants to meet at starbucks and i grit my teeth and acquiesce. i usually order the plain black tea. and, to my astonishment, it's pretty freakin' good! better than the lipton that i get out of the keurig, which isn't half bad either. the other day, i decided to live on the edge and order the chai tea, black. holy s***...what have i been missing? luckily the starbucks chai tea k-cups are available on amazon for a reasonable price. back to starbucks at the park place mall...the employees are super friendly. i'm literally the last person on the bandwagon, i know, but they really seem to go above and beyond. so yeah, go there...like you don't already know.

you know how abercrombie only hires hot people? well, i think this starbucks is recruiting cougars. nothing gets my pike's place up to 145' like a hot older chick in a christmas sweater & tight chinos. rrrrr!
i like this location alot. everyone is really friendly and good customer service. i go there almost every day. this is one of my favorite starbucks coffee location.
the workers are very nice but sooooo slow. it is consistently the slowest starbucks i've ever been to.

let's face it, we all kind what starbucks is about. you have so many choices and sizes at a premium price. i am fine with that. their customer service is heralded as top notch. i have found the cool refreshers to be a great option to coffee or tea. the issue lately with the starbucks locations i have visited is that they do not fill the cup. it seems trivial, but when you pay top dollar, you want your beverage filled, not an inch or so from the top. next time, i will complain but it was just too busy. bitching in front of others and backing up the line was not what i wanted. let's hope they browse yelp reviews and get it right!

if you're looking for a place to chill and sip on a frappe, this is not the place! this starbucks is cramped with very little seating. like, i counted 4 tiny tables. i came in one week and there was this long single line going out the door; it looked like everyone was getting off a school bus, that's how tight it is inside. youre better off going to the starbucks inside safeway. maybe. the service here is alright. it wasn't messy when i came but i wouldn't call this starbucks ghetto.
super fast and friendly. come here all the time and the line moves fast and serviced is exceptional.

coffee was ok. the wait to even order was over 20 minutes at 7 am this morning. i can't seem to understand why only 2 people were working for a busy time: 1 making beverages and one on the register. there was a third employee on the phone but she did not seem too concerned with the line almost out the door....

i go here because it's the closest starbucks to my home, so +1 star for convenience. another star for a large parking lot. although i have never been to this location when it is "busy," the staff is not very warm or welcoming. i've gotten this vibe at other starbucks locations when they were so crowded with 10+ people in line. on one occasion, one of the employees handed me my drink with the lid not tightly on; therefore some of it spilled onto the counter and also a decent amount on my hand. i didn't have any burns but it was not exactly pleasant. did he apologize? no, he told me that i don't need to clean up the spill and he will get to it later. not cool, man. go here to get your coffee. i've never gotten my drink done incorrectly (granted i only drink iced coffee without anything added or a simple blonde roast). don't come here expecting warm, smiley service.

i really enjoy this starbucks. they always get my drink right. they always get friendly service. and it's a very convenient location for me personally too. it's right next to all my shopping locations if i need a quick delicious drink. i'll come through this location because i can get my drink before i start shopping and have something to walk around with and enjoy. because honestly, who wouldn't want something delicious to drink while shopping. yes l will have to admit it's much longer to go through the drive through. so if you are in a time crunch probably not suggested. i'd always order through the app at that point. i like how they have both options. you can go inside and order or through dt. if you want order on the app walk inside and get the drink quick. i've personally tried a bunch of coffee shops and i always just come back to starbucks. there's nothing that beats it. i am and have been a fan of starbucks due to all the convenient locations and options they have to order.

waking my yelp account up specifically for this review. having spent perhaps too much time at too many starbucks, i can say that this starbucks has the nicest staff and is the cleanest starbucks in all of delaware county, probably philadelphia as well. i've never had an issue with my drink being wrong. never hard to find parking, either in front, on the side. i've never had to park by smashburger but that would be another option. gets pretty crowded closer to lunch. for anyone who has criticized this location, they can go to glen mills, media, springfield, villanova, bryn mawr...or really any other starbucks with an angry disinterested staff that can't be bothered. the brookhaven location feels like it's a totally different company.

i can always count on starbucks when the little shops let me down! i changed my mind several times, even gave back a drink, and the staff worked with a smile the entire time! not only were they nice, but they were fast! the last thing i want is a slow coffee shop.

there is no reason why every single time i come here to pick up my mobile order it takes 45+ min. i'd drive away like the 4 other cars i counted, but i already paid. in conclusion, go to another starbucks in the area! oh, and they gave me the wrong order/double charged me so i had to wait another 10 minutes to get a refund

i'll soon be moving to the castleton area and i've been making a mental list of the things that i'll miss the most about the 86th/ditch location. the starbucks. i have been to a numerous amount of starbucks and spent way too much money on their coffee. no regrets, though. this starbucks in particular has honestly won my heart. and it just so happened to be conveniently located right down the street from me for the past two years. why is it so grand? the staff. customer service is key and the group of people that work here are incredibly friendly. i have often gone in the mornings when i'm barely functional and they are more than happy to deal with my zombie self. i was even in there one day when a new guy was being trained on his barista position. everyone was patient with him - workers and customers. i think starbucks is a great establishment and they wouldn't have the success they do today if it weren't for workers like those at the 86th/ditch spot. so, thank you! i'll miss being right down the street, but i'll for sure make my way over every now and then after i move.
great location! they are always open on time (6am) and the staff is super friendly. never had a mistake on my order. top notch!

this is the 1st starbucks that i have ever walked out off... it was basically empty, around 4:00 pm, 1 car in the drive trough and one client asking for 2 bags of coffee to grind... 3 people on the drive through/ drinks 1 guy at cashier who has to leave to learn how to grind and 1 person around. 10 min later nobody gets my order i left
as always, this store goes above and beyond. they brewed a batch of decaf for our group. good for starbucks, good for us.

a bunch of confused employees, over staffed yet slow and no one knows what's going on. manager is rude and the food on display is moldy. response to telling them said they're display doesn't reflect the provided food. lmao come onnnn

nice to have good quality coffee like starbucks at a rest stop but they seem to be pretty slow considering the amount of people working at this store. i guess beggars cannot be choosers here. definitely a contrast from the nyc starbucks stores. my drip coffee was good.

katerina! i don't think people like her should work in public. especially when u have no skills of communication and willing to make your customers happy! so what happened to me: i stopped at this location to get coffee and from the beginning katerina gave me an awful attitude! after she messed up my order and on purpose didn't fix it. she made me wait till she serves everyone else! katerina was screaming at me so i just decided to leave. never again i go back to this place and don't recommend anyone if u don't want to ruin your mood!
the atomosphere is nice and the music is good for studying. it can get loud sometimes when a large group of people come in to study. however, the internet is terribly terribly slow.

i visit here very often since it's the nearest starbucks to my place. parking is usually easy after 10am and it's free. the best part of this starbucks is they have great customer service. some staff members are super friendly, like joseph and curtis, it's always a pleasure and heart-warming to have their service. however, every once for a while, they will have few new faces who serve people with cold faces and rarely respond or reaffirm to customer's saying. that really make big differences comparing with my regular experience there. i wish those staff can pick up the basic etiquette to work with customers more since i wish to feel like coming home in my neighborhood. the ratio of chance for me having cold interaction is 1 out of 5, so i give them 4 stars.
nice people work here, always a smile . they run a busy shop fast and efficient . coffee tastes same as in all other star bucks so i guess it's the employees that make this one special .

well location isnt an issue. there everywhere. i dont drink coffee so my review is on everything but, why is the venti still kinda small? i order tead and a few sips and shes empty??? the prices seem kind of steep but hell everywheres steep. staff is always happy to serve you and there baked goods are rather delicious as well. i hate line ups and well theres always on here. always. seriously i dont think i've ever walked into a starbucks and never had to wait in line. meh its a-ok

the asian food restaurant that used to be here had some really over-salted miso soup. it wasn't very good. if you think about it, coffee is a sort of bean broth. anyway, the drive thru is convenient and pretty fast when the line isn't too long.

when i arrived at walt whitman plaza, i was thirsty for starbucks. i was surprised that they didn't have any blonde roast coffee today. i got the trenta strawberry acai with lemonade. my drink came out correctly and in a timely manner.

simply put, it's the kind of speed and service you'd expect from a suburban starbucks. the product is exactly the same as at any other of their gazillion locations. but what sets them apart is friendly faces and a better execution than your average big bean experience. i popped in twice over the course of a couple days while visiting some relatives in the area. the place was clean, the wait during the day was short and the staff seemed to have a handle on what they were doing. even with cars lined up outside, it felt like they were giving the walk-in customers priority. it's always annoying when you walk into an empty starbucks only to wait forever for a drink because there are a couple cars idling in the drive-thru. i especially liked the latte they made me. way to get the most out of that foam!

absolutely consistently the slloowweesstt starbucks i patronize. i don't understand why service is so slow here. i also don't understand why i continue to patronize this location other than convenience. after being stuck in drive thru for an unacceptable amount of time this morning, i will rethink this!

just one visit here, but the staff is not friendly at all. most sbux have reasonably friendly baristas who make eye contact and smile. not this location. the attitude is just, 'give me your order, get it, get out!". we found another location the following day with people that enjoy life!
customer service is always on point and they make good suggestions if you ask. if you want sweets/food you just gotta get there in the am. love this target as well it's pretty spacious.

this starbucks has one of the longest lines with regularity that you will ever see. plus it's a small starbucks with a limited menu. i would only give a full-sized starbucks 3 stars because their coffee tastes burnt and their prices are ludicrous. i know some people are obsessed with starbucks but i've never understood paying more for worse. i far prefer dunkin donuts or tim horton's for quick and basic coffee, and if you want good coffee,you need to order online and have a good burr grinder and a chemex or french press.

not a fan of this particular starbucks. this isn't my usual sbux but whenever i come here, i feel like the wait times are abnormally long and it has nothing to do with the amount of people there. the baristas move at a very slow pace and seem side tracked by side conversations. and it's always dirty. coffee that hasn't been wiped off of the counter it straw wrappers and crumbs/debris on the floor. today i ordered a frappuccino and a granita and i swear it look almost 10 mins from start to finish. and the one with the goofy eyes (sorry) made the frap wrong when all he needed was a little extra attention to read what it said on the cup. i'd rather wait through the line at the starbucks that is actually in the mall before coming over to this one again.

consistently great starbucks location. even though there's a good amount of turnover, the baristas are always top notch. service is routinely fast and friendly. the thing i like most about this branch is that they take the time to make my drink properly. i often order long shots, but many locations don't run the shots that way, despite my instructions. i'd say i only get long shots about 10% of the time. anyway, here they always let the shots rip for the full amount of time and you can absolutely taste the difference. good job for never cutting corners on my drink. five solid stars from me. i really hope we can move past the pandemic and get back to sitting and drinking our coffee in the shop. it's really a great place to do that.

i've visited here several times with horrible experiences and after my visit today i've decided i'll be driving 20 minutes out of my way from now on to get coffee. i mobile ordered 10 minutes before i got there and stood waiting for my drink for 20 more minutes while they had 6 people working an unorganized mess. not to mention the barista's friend coming in and ordering their drink while she took her time to chat with him and then put his order ahead of the other 10 people that had been waiting. unacceptable.

so, it's about time i come back and write an overall review of this starb. i still stand by my review below that the experience with barista c was appalling, but as a regular here, most of the rest of the staff is awesome. they know my drink, my name, etc. they are a good group!

service is fine...but the quality of their drinks are mediocre at best. i would not recommend getting their iced coffee. i've tasted better iced coffee when i made it myself at a hotel. their iced lattes are ok, but not worth the price. only satisfying drink i've had there has been the pink drink. but..way too much ice, even when i asked for light ice. i never bought food from there, because i always saw flies buzzing around the food in the display case. unsanitary much? and my final thought applies to starbucks overall: their drinks should be cheaper. or, at least make the size difference between a tall and a grande greater.

went in and they had no iced decaf and no apology. okay. and they never have salt or pepper for their food items like the others do. claim they order it but it just doesn't come in.well, call corporate and solve the problem instead of just accepting it!

the staff here is great! they are in a busy location so it's always crazy busy every time i come, i'm talking a line outside the door. there is no drive thru at this location and parking is a nightmare. that's the only downfall. it's downtown so you have to find street parking and walk here.

not enough seating for normal folks wanting to sit and enjoy a coffee with a date. is taken over by students using starbucks for a study hall. funny cause some of them don't have a purchase that i have noticed? sorry but i pay good money for starbucks coffee .

this is one of my favorite locations. the baristas are super friendly and helpful. the inside is comfortable and useful with a huge table with plugs. there is outdoor seating available for when the weather allows. the drive thru is fast. the only issue like others have said is getting to this location from curlew. my fix for that is to cut through the shopping center and turn right onto tampa rd.
have only been to drive-thru. great service and there is never a wait here! prices are high but that's to be expected given the company. very friendly and professional staff.. i will come back.

pumpkin spice season! i love starbucks and when i relocated to south jersey i knew it was probable that one would not be as close as i had out in san diego (a drive thru one 3 blocks away). well the closest one is about 3 miles and so when i'm in the area and feel like treating myself i stop in. this location is usually busy but there is ample parking and the baristas are usually quick getting the orders out. i think that the service kind of depends with who you get and if theyre having a good day or not. i have had the friendliest service to at times indifference and even a little snobby. so, customer service is inconsistent, which is disheartening because like i said i am a fan of starbucks. this location does have a decent offering of baked goods and food items as well and the usual starbucks swag available for purchase.

best customer service. quick. super friendly. more than happy to help you. best coffee. knows their menu inside and out. hand down best starbucks. keep it up ladies!!! and thank you so much for my coffee (my soul) every morning.

this starbucks always have fresh coffee. the staff is very pleasant and i have made this location my go to place. i love the convenience of the drive thru. and the cleanliness of the outside and inside. it's located in a popular location which makes it easier to get to where your going.

one of my absolute favourite starbucks locations in edmonton. staff is well trained and always carry themselves professionally. drive thru is fast, efficient and is great for me as i am usually grabbing and running. starbucks has some amazing products and this locations provides fast, friendly and genuine service.

i have never had a problem here! hands down my favorite starbucks. the staff is the absolute best! great customer service, clean shop and always get your drinks right. saturday mornings are always super busy but the staff always seems to zoom through the line and maintain their great job!

typical starbucks, but the workers are so much nicer than i expected! they were so easy to talk to and even cheered me on as i studied for my actuarial exam. by now, we all know what to expect at a starbucks, but that was one thing that stuck out to me while i was there -- and i was there for quite a while!

if i could give this place zero stars i would. it's the slowest and worst starbucks i have ever been to. they're terribly inefficient, unorganized and walk around like lost children back there. the girl taking orders kept losing her badge for the register and she's making food at the same time. there was only one person making drinks. they were talking to each other and completely oblivious of how long their line is. we stood in line for almost an hour. they ran out of food. for being an airport starbucks, it's horrible. don't waste your time here
cashier lady with lazy eye had an attitude and kept getting my order wrong and getting mad at me at it me for it everytime

this store has totally gone downhill. mornings are always crowded with lines going out the door, only because of its location being in the heart of university city. due to busy mornings, the staff is extremely rude because they can't keep up with the volume! i guess they also don't care because they get the business either way. so, what happened to all the polite staff and great baristas that use to work there? all new faces (at least during the day), which would be fine if they knew what they were doing and if they treated their customers with respect! i do find that evening/night service is still a bit better though than the day. nevertheless, customer service should be the number 1 priority of any business, even if they don't agree with the customer. the cashiers here are really rude and the baristas either mess up the orders and/or don't fill the cups all the way. what is point of getting venti if you are going to get a grande beverage in a venti cup! and believe me, if you say something, they snap at you. fine, then remove the sign above you that says "love your beverage or let us know. we'll always make it right", and, teach your staff to actually do what the starbucks 'mission statement' promises! also, each time i've used my own personal cup, they are supposed to discount my drink by $.10, but they always seem to conveniently forget to do that unless you specifically remind them ahead of time. you also don't get a receipt unless you ask. i was disappointed twice this week when i ordered egg sandwiches to go. i forgot to ask in advance to not have it toasted, but did ask shortly after placing the order. she gave me a dirty look, and rudely remarked that next time i should 'request' it before the order. wow, it's just a sandwich, relax! the second time i remembered and asked in advance. however, after i walked out, about halfway down the block, i realized that the bag felt light, and noticed that the sandwich was gone! well, it fell out from the open bottom of the bag somewhere and i did not even notice it! so i went back and of-course the line was long and i did not care to wait another 15 minutes, so i stood and the front of the line and waited till the customer in front of me was done with their order, and then i politely asked the cashier for another sandwich explaining to her what had just happened. meanwhile, another (not so happy) customer, who had also bought a sandwich had got the wrong item, so he rudely interrupted us to complain about his own sandwich. this confused the cashier and got her ticked off. eventually when i proceeded to reiterate and finish up my issue, the cashier angrily looked at me and said "you didn't aks me!". i told her that i just did *ask* her, and she said, no you didn't, i did not hear you! seriously? then she said that too much was happening and she could not take it! so another not-so-friendly cashier took over, and rudely gave me another sandwich in a new bag (i made it sure was sealed this time). i had wasted 10 more minutes just so that i could get another sandwich. it was not my fault that i had this problem, yet i was treated rudely, and got no apologies. this is really bad. a store that doesn't care about or respect their customers, nor does it care about the quality of their service and the attitude of many of their staff members, doesn't care about the quality of their products, does not deserve my business. and starbucks is not cheap, we already pay enough for what we get, so i'd rather go somewhere else where i will at least be treated with respect along with a good cup of coffee. i hope that corporate starbucks reads this because they need to know about the bad service and about all the wasted food that gets thrown out and the beverages that get poured down the drain due to mistakes of the not-so-well-trained employees!!

this review has 4 stars because i like this starbucks. it's generally very efficient, despite how busy they are, and the baristas are always polite and professional. but this review is about a customer. a customer who made it very clear he would prefer the group of females working this morning to call him "friendly freddie" going forward. i was there this morning, monday march 26 around 8 am. there were 4 females working, and the only other customer in line was this mike pence looking mofo that i now know is named freddie. he was standing by the pastry case at first, just standing there eyeing a girl in the drive thru. he gets to the register, where mia is attempting to take his order, and he's still eyeing the drive thru, and seems to be asking mia a question about madelines, but when she tried to answer he said "oh i was asking shelby, because she always knows." literally called this girl shelby over from the drive thru where she was helping another customer, to ask her where regular madelines were. guess where? yeah, right in front of his freakin face at the register. and you bet your britches he asked her to reach over and grab it from the credenza for him. by this point, i could not control the actions of my face and eyes, and i can tell the barista at the bar noticed these looks. after this guy was done paying at the register, he still lingered, of course so he could get a good view of shelby in the drive thru. so i'm trying to take my order, and this guy is still in front of the register, which is now my personal space since mia is looking at me directly, asking what i would like, and dude would still not move. then he continues on talking about himself and how nice he thinks he is and how everyone working there should refer to him as "friendly freddie" going forward. the way he was acting was incredibly demeaning and uncomfortable to everyone around him. and he is clearly a regular. the manager of this store needs to address this and let "freddie" know that it's never okay to treat females as if they owe him something. it's never okay to talk to someone as if they don't exist, and it's not okay to go to the local starbucks and sit at the bar so you can stare at someone for hours at a time.
this is one of my favorite starbucks in the tampa area. the barista's are top notch. perfect location on gandy blvd.

i went to starbucks today to get my wife a quick coffee. i saw there was a long line at the drive-thur maybe 10 cars or more at 12:05pm . so i went inside there was two guest in front of me and i had to wait 20 minutes for one venti iced coffee with three pumps of syrup there is no reasonable excuse for that. this has not been the first time this is happened but it will be the last for me. if you want my money you will have to earn it.

this starbucks is fine. i would agree with other reviewers that there isn't a real sense of urgency with the baristas. however, i've only been a few times in the late afternoon when i'm not in a rush and there isn't a real line. not a good place to work- lots of teens hang out around gay street and they want their starbucks fix too. might be different during working hours? i think the non paying customer issue some reviewers were talking about a few years ago doesn't happen as often anymore. kind of a shame that a starbucks is in a nice location on gay street vs. a local business. fenn's coffee is a short walk away and has a homier, local vibe (without kids) and i've seen people working there.

gosh darn lousy experience...dirty interior, xtremely slow drive-thru, no smile employees...nine of them behind the counter. what a disaster of an operation and certainly not well managed. tried the drive-thru first and line wasn't moving, so we went inside. chaotic looking scene, with full garbage bin overflowing the counter top of the cream/sugar area, dirty floor, littered table tops. two employees at drive-thru window busy talking to each other, not doing anything?! employee at inside register must have been new. hesitant on questions, taking our order, etc. asked for no whip on cold drink, got about half normal amount on top anyway? all this at 11am on a sunday. we were not impressed. too bad they've got a monopoly here...a little competition would do us all good.

this starbucks is super convenient to me but parking is not ideal. this location is usually very busy so tables are prime real estate. this location is also highly frequented by the homeless which is not usually a problem unless they are soliciting money. the staff is usually friendly and courteous but this location is never very clean. the floor rarely looks swept or mopped. the bathrooms are not maintained very well throughout the day either.
what a shit show. i waited 30 minutes for a drink that takes 1 minute to make, and it wasn't made very well. all i can say is get some help bc y'all need it
nice job! really appreciated the service and special pour over. my chai tea was perfect as well. nice to have good service when on a long trip. thanks so much!
your coffee is way to strong omg i rather get the latte but u guess it is kinda ok i lake wawa coffee better though

these guys love me and i love them too! :-) i come here every weekend. this place is always packed (because it's right across the street from a college). feel free to chat with the staff when it's chill. they're all really cool! i also recommend the lemon loaf... holy cow... so good!

you know how women always make jokes about how men are extremely sexually unsatisfying in the bedroom? well, let's just say starbucks is a man and i'm his woman. it started out one morning as a beautiful and fantastical idea - maybe even kinky. go to starbucks, enjoy a nice hot cup of coffee and slob on some spectacular oatmeal. sadly, this fantasy was never realized. as i pulled into the drive thru, i glanced begrudgingly at the seemingly endless queue of cars lining the asphalt covered pavement. although the thought of waiting in this line seemed almost as pressing as waiting for the universe to end, i decided that maybe the coffee would be worth it. spoiler alert: twasn't. my tires squeaked as they fought with the pavement leading to the drive thru speaker: "i would like an oat milk latte please, no foam - and make that unsweetened." like songs from a siren, these simple words floated effortlessly from my mouth. however, i had to repeat myself multiple times. i felt as though i was screaming at a brick wall. eventually, my order was completed and i was able to get through to the worker: now to wait the rest of the way. i graciously drove my vehicle to the drive thru window: "apple pay?" the worker asked, to which i replied: "yes." i paid for my (or so i thought) delicious order. however, as soon as my iphone 12 plus grazed the very last atoms of the card reader, i was handed a drink - but not mine. confused, shocked, concerned, and unsettled, i begged for the coffee i so struggled to order. after another order confirmation (one of trillions), i finally received my latte. delight! cream! delish! wrong! wrong! my hastiness got the better of me. as i pulled away from this establishment, i noticed a few things about my drink. 1) there seemed to be foam. 2) i was unsure if it was even oat milk. 3) it didn't bring me pure ecstasy and joy. this was truly a devastating blow to my ego. upset and belittled, i chose to move on and order my coffee elsewhere. at the end of the day, i learned a lesson. to never come back to this starbucks. have a wonderful evening.

i visit and spend a lot starbucks stores and hold them to a higher standard than a other coffee places as they charge a premium price for coffee and food, it's hit and miss like others have said and this is just a slower store which is why it is always claustrophobic in there. many of the baristas are very nice but this location is not very intuitive and the older manager lady here is sour. sorry i'm not the only one who has commented on this. it would be helpful to put the mobile orders where customers can get to them. i

this starbucks is always are able to get your order out quickly. i was just here for the free usable cup with the purchase of a holiday drink and it was we jammed packed. to be honest i thought i would be in there forever but i wasn't. the staff is always super friendly and accommodating. it is a very convenient location, kind of small on the inside but there is a seating area right outside the store with plenty of room.

this starbucks can't seem to decide on their hours of business. if you make your way to this store it's 50/50 whether or not they're open. doesn't matter what time of day. the web site says they're open, google says they're open...pull into the lot and there's nobody there. the one further westinghouse front of lowe's is much better.

the corner it sits on makes it difficult to approach in a car, unless you are traveling east bound on grant. so i usually park across the street and try not to get hit while legally crossing the street. yeah, the intersection is that busy. but once you make it, you can reward yourself with a six shot venti americano. you'll need the jolt to make it back across the street.

this is one of my fav local starbucks. fast & efficient- & most importantly consistent service! this location has indoor & outdoor seating, layout friendly to both solo as well as group work sessions- & tends to be the quietest starbucks location in treasure valley. (busy, but generally quiet noise level.). if u need a respite from eagle road- pull in here!

the best starbucks in the city! i go about 3 or 4 times a week and i love everyone here. their service is exceptional every single time i visit ! i live on the north east side but i only go to this one that is by my work because they are so friendly and awesome ! and the drinks always taste better, but it could be my biased taste buds ;)

stopped for a quick lemonade on our way but like someone mentioned in their review, if you are in a hurry, don't trust their driveway. it's very slow and even if our drink was ready a while ago, no one was there at the window. this was the same for the two cars before us.
ordered tall coffee got just half a cup instead. ordered muffin which felt like a couple of days old probably...

this location could be a gold mine. if the kids working behind the counter worried more about making the orders and serving the customers than the do bickering and telling each other how to "do their job". lucky for them, caffeine is worth the aggravation of these bratty teenagers.
love this location! always great service, friendly,fast, always get my order right, and drive through is the biggest plus.

worst starbucks drink i've ever had. ordered the nitro cold brew with cold foam. first off the coffee was warm. normally there's ice in my cold brew or it's extremely cold. this was unacceptable! it was warm and the "cold foam" was warm as well and very thin. just terrible. my fiancé ordered it and when i realized the disaster we didn't feel like going back in traffic to get back to the location. disappointed. spent almost $6 on one drink and can't even finish it because it's not very good at all.

i have never gone to this starbucks and had it be less than a 20-30 minute ordeal. the customer service is terrible, they act annoyed by you. i understand it's a busy location but there is no sense of urgency and when there's not a single person in front of you and you ask about your coffee after waiting 15 minutes and they roll your eyes at you? i've given this location several second chances, but i will not be returning. too many other great local coffee shops in the area.

starbucks is a starbucks, and anywhere in the us u go is the same, but what matter, is who is working...i notice a lots of starbucks ran by unprofessional employees....maybe the manager is coold and okay for the company, but being super friendly is not good, because after a while all the boundaries between the customer and the employee will be gone and they won't care more.... i also notice some dome employees, and they careless if u like the drink or the service. this location can't manage 5 or 6 customers goes on line at once, and it will be a mess. i give 2 start for the starbucks not for the location, because ill never go back there

i really like this starbucks. i like when the big front windows are open to people watch. the exposed brick wall of the building also gives it a little less of "cookie cutter" starbucks feel and a little more of an "unique coffee shop" feel. (whatever, it helps my sense of guilt when i choose the man over local...) there are always people meeting up or studying. i'm usually there in the evenings and i'm taking away one star because my black coffee usually tastes a little old, but i get it - it's the evening, not a whole lot of coffee orders.

i love this starbucks location. the staff is super nice - not to mention quick and efficient. i've never had a problem with my order not being right and i've never had to wait more than a few minutes to complete my order from beginning to end. i highly recommend this starbucks - one of the best in tucson!
poor service, took twenty minutes to get two black, iced coffees out, and when they came out they were pumped full of simple syrup, took forever and they didn't even get the order right.

one of the worst starbucks i've ever been to. i was able to order right away and then the wait began. i waited 15-20 minutes for one iced coffee. there were at least 5 people working but only 1 making coffees. it wasn't even slammed. on top of that the inside of the store was quite dirty. the floors were awful and nothing was restocked. it was evident that customer service was not a priority here.
going going gone! sorry to see this starbucks close. it did not have the largest layout but it was a great place to answer emails over a cup o' joe.

as a general rule, i try not to write reviews for chains, but i make exceptions on occasion. this store seems to be investing in the community and is trying to find a balance of being a drive-thru and a destination. it's definitely an anchor for irvington. overall it's a pretty comfortable place to chill and enjoy free wi-fi. the demands of being a drive-thru store makes it so the baristas are not as friendly as i've seen in other stores. i wish lazy daze (or another indy coffee store) would get it's $#!+ together to be a real competitor to sbux in the area, but from what i've seen, that's not the case at this time. coffee: standard starbucks food: yes vibe: standard starbucks wifi: yes. free. plugs: yes.

busy and central location makes this a coffee stop and social event. this location is open longer than the close-by peets' or the other starbucks, in the la cumbre mall across the street. the staff always keeps a good pace and sets a high standard for the slower starbucks.
what's up??? i waited in line 10 minutes to order, then waited another10 for my drink. if you are understaffed, put more people on!

the environment was amazing! never felt so welcomed in a chain coffee shop before, my kids and my self were greeted as soon as we came in. i was hesitant because there wasn't a drive thru and it was nap time lol. the cashier made me forget all about it. while we chatted and i redeemed my birthday reward they welcomed me to the neighborhood! also giving me suggestions for things to do with my children in the area. this conversation was short but sweet! the barista was just as sweet! i will be going back! my smoked butterscotch frap was delicious.
great service, awesome drive-thru and a friendly staff.

clean but mobile app ordered, although confirmed did not go through. at least on their end. so caution when using mobile order. they were good about it and it was nice there otherwise your typical starbucks!! !
amazing! i believe the girls name is britney. i work next door at ulta in the salon and she always hooks us up with free drinks so nice of her!

this starbucks- inside frys- is the best starbucks on the planet! the ladies are beyond awesome! even when dealing with the jerks they maintain their professional demeanor and do their job! jada and bre are the best!

this is easily one of my favorite starbucks. whenever i order via phone, my drink is always ready with no mistakes. the staff is pleasant and they work quickly. also, i adore the location since it is near where i work, and it is always on the cleaner side considering that some many students hang out there. although the students are well behaved and quite pleasant, i don't think i'll be there working.

if slowness was a virtue these guys would be kings..... show up on monday but be thinking about what you'll want on tuesday because that's how long it will take to get your order. and when you do get your tasteless incorrect beverage you will wish you had gone elsewhere. if you do find yourself trapped at this hellhole try the drive through.... seems they process 6 car orders per one counter order.... and no that doesn't mean they as quick at the drive through, they just aren't terminal like the counter is..... avoid avoid avoid!

this starbucks is very often super busy. i have rarely come when the line is not a mile long. so i am so glad starbucks came out with an order ahead option on their app. the seating can get a little tight, but usually you can get a seat. on the weekends, all manner of newtownian folk come out, and it is hilarious to eavesdrop on their conversations. the nice thing is there is a large parking lot in the back, so access is easy.

this place is horrible! i will never visit again. i went through the drive through and ordered a medium, caramel latte and a blueberry scone. when i got to the window to get my order the scone was cold. i shed the lady at the window, who seemed annoyed already, could she reheat the scone as it's now cold. she's stated, rudely, she had already warned it. i said to i knew this but i was in line for a while it isn't warm anymore. so she warned it and have it back. below is the pictures of the scone she gave me back. broken in pieces. like i am a dog!!! i took it back at complained to the manager but she seemed scared of her employees as the woman and i hear words in front of her! if i wasn't hungry i would have returned the food and went elsewhere. this place is horrible

i love this location. it's bigger than their old location down the street. the staff is very nice and respectful. they are fairly fast at making your drink, depending upon how crowed it is. i'll definitely be coming back.

the customer service here is amazing! they actually messed up our order yesterday, but there was a bit of a language barrier with the cashier. she was super super sweet and the mess up wasn't a big one we ordered 2 vente caramel fraps with 4 extra pumps and two extra shots each... we got it except in a grande size. whoa was it strong!!! but we still took it and went on with our day. today we went in and the cashier (i'm thinking she may be a manager) remembered us from yesterday. and told us that today we would get the right order at no charge! i mean, who does that now days? we will keep coming back (we're addicted)

i am a huge fan of starbucks and i usual come everyday. i've been stopping at this particular starbucks since mid-october because my morning commute changed. unfortunately, i've been disappointed with this starbucks on many occasions, mainly due to inconsistency in the quality and taste of my drinks. i always order a latte or a cappuccino. lately, i've been drinking more cappuccinos because the lattes i've been getting here taste so weak. being that a cappuccino has less milk, i've been going with that. i don't think i should have to order an extra shot just to get a latte that doesn't taste like pure milk. the younger girls who work here make better drinks and deliver good customer service. there is an older lady who works here who lacks in both categories. i feel bad pointing this out, but i shouldn't have to feel like a burden when i come here. maybe service would be better if we could tip? i'm not a difficult customer. in fact, i have great working relationships with all of the baristas in my hometown store in wyomissing. i've never had a single complaint there. honestly, i don't even care as much about the service as long as i'm getting excellent drinks. maybe they're neglecting to tend to the espresso machine properly?
best starbucks ever! i've been coming here nearly every day this summer. they craft the beverages perfectly every time. i've never had to wait long. friendly, kind, and inviting baristas!

bought 2 protein boxes with the hard boiled eggs. eggs were rotten which was really gross. had to drive back and wait on line to be offered a refund. i feel like if you bite into rotten food and then have to drive back to the location and wait on line...you should probably be offered a little more than a refund. oh well, that's starbucks for ya.

much like it's counterparts in the silver legacy and el dorado, this starbucks sells the exact same drinks you would get at any stand-alone location but inflates the price due to its, "convenience", of being in your hotel. i get it, but at the same time, starbucks is already overly inflated so the addition of this kinda adds to the sting. unlike its counterparts, however, i've always found the customer service at this one to be particularly good and in line with most of the stand-alone locations. the other two casino locations i mentioned above are hit and miss. this one always gets the service and quality just right.

i'm a frequent visitor to many of the locations around my area. i usually order the same thing; skinny vanilla latte and a breakfast sandwich. i went through the the drive up and was greeted by a very friendly barista. while i waited she chatted me up and was peppy for the 6am hour. i was pretty tired and her contagious attitude helped pep me up. when i got my drink i gave it a sip and tasted awful like burnt coffee. i went in and out when i explained it didn't taste right the manager took it right away apologizing and had the other barista make a new one. his demeanor was very different and was disgruntled having to make a new one. when i got the new one it tasted the exact same. i was sorely disappointed that drink went to waste. they could have had 4-5 stars but when you can't make a standard staple drink on your menu that's a problem.
is great place for read book .. and cofee the space is magic and g re at i love the university and love this special place
by far my favorite starbucks. great staff, consistent drinks. mobile ordering is pretty awesome too. it gets pretty packed, but what starbucks doesnt?
super good matcha tea! gabe was extremely kind and hard working to get my drink out fast! thank you starbucks

the worst caramel macchiato i've ever had. i am a frequent starbucks drinker and caramel macchiato is my go to drink before work almost every morning. i was on a road trip from tennessee to chicago stopped by this starbucks location to pick up my regular order and i don't know how they messed up one of their most popular menu items but it taste disgusting! i'm not sure if the milk is bad or the coffee was watered down but it does not meet starbucks standards. i have never felt the need to leave a bad review anywhere until now. so disappointed!
very long line... service was friendly though. had to partake in a mocha frappuccino. they're really good stuff!

starbucks is crappy and mediocre, but the location on mass ave takes it to a whole new level. grungy tables and floor, confusing store layout, and a terrible frappuccino all made this a lousy experience. i only came here out of desperation for a place to hang out for an hour or so, but i think i'd be better off on a random street corner.

the absolute worst experience i have ever had at a starbucks!!! i've never experienced such rude workers in my life. i'm still in disbelief at what was said to me at the drive-up window. will never go back.

this place has always been wonderful. great service and a great location with plenty of parking. i use to go once to twice a day but have limited myself lately. the baristas are really nice. this place has a drivethru with very quick service. inside has a lot of seating but everyone is very close together. only 2 spots by the fireplace and it is always full. in the summer months their outside seating is great with views of the indy skyline or methodist lifeline helicopter runs.

for anyone who drinks starbucks more than a few times per week, we all know how precise and proficient they are. unfortunately, this specific starbucks is not. they have longer than usual wait times (6-10 mins online ordering), the drinks are never right or never taste the way they should. yes, the staff is friendly, but not efficient. if you can avoid it, please save your money and go to one closer!

worst starbucks ever! do not visit. i wouldn't of even given them a star if yelp didn't make me. i'm here on a sunday morning when they should be at their best and their baristas are nothing but rude. i ordered 2 drinks both of which have had to be remade at least twice. the barista at the bar thinks she can speak rudely to the customers as well. make sure not to speak loudly, otherwise you will get yelled at. if you can skip and move onto the next stabucks i would.

this is the starbucks near me, i visit it often!!! really crazy i don't drink coffee. i'm a fan of the strawberry refresher with lemonade no water, i enjoy the protein boxes, i love cookie crumble frapp, and list of other things!!!! chris the drive thru person with the wonderful accent is always so nice!!!

30 minute wait for a cup of brewed coffee? there were only 6 people in the line inside (drive-thru was wrapped around the building). seemed like there were a lot of workers but the woman at the register was so slow. the coffee was fine if you have the time.
nice employees, cute coffee bar area. this location was really crowded when i went. my gingerbread latte was fine. not much else to say.

i usually never write bad reviews but today i had to. one employee was so rude. i was getting a coffee and an employee named michelle was so mean to multiple customers and gave a nasty demeanor when calling names for drinks. which is sad because multiple employees there are very nice.

ehh, this starbucks is ok. i came here mid afternoon on a saturday and the place was packed. waited in line for at least 5 minutes to order and then another 5 for my drink. luckily, my drink was prepared well and correctly. the service was average, nothing to complain about but nothing spectacular. the barista making the drinks was disheveled in appearance and had liquid all over the front of his apron. granted, i get that spills happen but his apron was gross, he should have changed to another one if he was going to continue making drinks. overall, average experience. this place seems to have pretty good reviews so perhaps i caught it at a bad time.

disrespectful employees. yelled and threaten to call the cops on my fiancee because all she was asking if the drink that the barista has made was hers. she asked 3 times and everyone of the employees ignored her. the barista gave her an attitude once he gave her his attention. my fiancee tried to speak with a manager but none of the employees wanted to cooperate to direct her to one. one of the employees claimed that he was the "manager" and refused to give her the district manager information. a scene has already started in front of the many customers that have been or was being served. he ended up giving her the "manager" number which my fiancee called and when she asked who she was speaking to, they hung up on her. the managers number ended up being the store phone number. nothing was resolved. never go here unless you're looking to fight with the disrespectful employees.

i'm seeing others who have the same horrible service with the most rude employees ever in any place i've ever been to in my life. deliberately overcharging too? no. not anymore. i guess its time to give up the urge to ever go to this place again. i won't do it. enough of their horrible treatment toward a customer. they really don't care. a simple order like iced coffee gets so complicated because their "baristas" are impossible to deal with. this is not a real starbucks and their employees show that its not.

these people are the rudest starbux staff ive ever encountered. it was a sunday morning at 10am and there was a line out the door. there was only one register open. when i called them on it, this nasty looking broad with a mustache started talking back. so i told the manager who basically gave me the same attitude. i called the district manager who said they would handle it after i told them how long they made everyone wait. apparently this one girl ashley (fat and obnoxious) is upset about and told me not to leave my water around. never have ive ever been told this at any starbux.

bumbling and questioning the customer was my experience with this location. i'm a regular customer but i usually avoid this store. today, my order and payment, process were messed up, and the fix of both were cumbersome. i had already left the store when i realized my food order was wrong. i came back and was questioned if i had really ordered what i wanted. i think the person taking my was very distracted, and i believe that was the reason for the issues. but she didn't really accept full responsibility.
clean shop. orders taken quickly and correctly. coffee arrived hot and fast and perfectly made.

i ordered a tall soy flat white , but it looked more like a dry cappuccino . so much foam! not the first time either. unfortunately, it's a hit or miss location. some days their coffee is textbook & delicious, other days, not so much.

so i know there are a lot of "neighbors refuse corporate brews" campaigns around and, in a coffee city, why choose starbucks? first of all, i like the consistency in flavor and price. i can't do cc's coffee-flavored water and pj's is over a dollar more for the type of drink i order. but, all of that aside, i will go out of my way to go to this starbucks location because it is a wonderful oasis where everyone is friendly and efficient and things are clean and orderly and there is lot of safe parking. the baristas (baristos? they've always been all men in my experience) *kind of* give off that douchey "i'm super knowledgable about coffee" vibe, but they seem to care a lot about customer service and that is an a+ in my book.

by far the worse starbucks i have visited, this says a lot as i travel for work, i assumed being just off the interstate that i would be in and out rather than waiting just over 20 mins for a cold brew with a minimum crowd. needs organization or some management to keep things streamed lined. hard to expect much from plan city though.

the spinach feta wrap is always delicious. i can't say i'm super excited about the flat white however. it was, well, flat. tasted like hot milk with a wee bit of coffee. maybe that is what is is supposed to be, but i need more coffee impact!! i say meh.
