dear barista matt, why do you have to be so incredibly rude? my little brother didn't understand the new tops that you could sip your drink with, without a straw. matt mocked my brother and continued to ridicule him for accidentally wasting a straw. he did this out loud for everyone to hear. additionally, when a friend asked for a straw for his frappuccino, matt rudely directed him across the store instead of giving him one of the straws sitting right behind the barista counter. matt you were rude you ruined our experience we won't be returning to this location because of you i would start to change your attitude and act a little bit nicer towards your customers. i don't recommend this store because of matt
had to have my daily starbucks! it's not for luxury it's a necessity!! love this store since it's so close to my house. clean store, yummy drinks & a nice crews! until next time,...probably tomorrow!

this starbuck's is open 24/7 (& on xmas) lovely spot for late nighters craving their starbuck's favorite drink new decor inside & out but the night shift staff nicole & john are fabulous helpful & friendly a great spot to head when indy rolls up the sidewalks!

sunday morning scenario: line snaked through sbux into the marriott lobby. one barista on register. two baristas on coffee and food. one barista doing nothing. one barista at the espresso, etc. area. i patiently waited my turn and ordered the same thing i have ordered every day since 8/28; a venti non-fat, no whip pumpkin spice latte and picked up a mango juice from the fridge. i did not have enough on my gold card so i also handed the cashier $10 and asked her to put it on my card first. she condescendingly "explained" she could do it all at the same time. she put it on my card and rang my order and gave me some coin change?? (still haven't figured that one out). i patiently waited forever and watched as several people who came after me got their drinks. i politely asked the barista if he had a venti pumpkin spice latte coming up. he did not. meanwhile the register girl yells down that i had ordered a grande. i pulled out my receipt to verify that i had indeed ordered a venti. he finally made my drink (with whip and most likely vitamin d milk) and gave me a card for a free drink next time. i get that people make mistakes, but register girl seriously needs to not be in the customer service position of ringing customers. (there were other snide remarks heard while waiting there that i won't even go into.) i love sbux and like i said, i visit my local spot at least once per day. not even remotely impressed with the management of this local.

parking is limited here and they are always short staffed. i have been here numerous times were i have been waiting in line forever and ended up leaving with out ordering anything. today i went to this location to give it another try and i have to say it was quick getting in and out. i ordered a trenta passion tea lemonade and it was perfect. apparently they are building a bigger starbucks next to the bank of america in the parking lot so we shall see if the service changes but overall today was a good day to make a purchase at starbucks.

... can't believe the experience i had on saturday 12/14/19 with one of the baristas. her name tag said "cat". i order a venti pike coffee with almond milk. she proceeds to charge me .60 cents for milk alternative, i said are you sure... it's suppose to be lattes you charge extra. she explains that almond milk is not the standard milk and it's an upcharge. after they proceed to make everyone else's beverage who came after me, she hands me the coffee with a drop of almond milk. i said i would like some more, and said i have ordered almond milk multiples times before to know that they normally don't charge for it for coffees. she grabs another venti coffee and pours half of the coffee in of the original coffee, and dumps some almond milk in it . so at this point, it's half a cup of coffee with almond milk and no lid and slides it back to me. i honestly can't understand why she was so mean and rude and lacked professionalism. i walk away and say forget it, and leave without the coffee, pretty much in tears. she made it into a scene and i felt humiliated in front of others in the store. i almost walked out and said you know i want my money back, she went to the register and fiddled around with it. eventually i just left, no coffee, in which i paid for and pretty astonished on they way i was treated.

ugh...this place just keeps getting worse. whether it's the summertime fly infested dining area, the grime everywhere or the fact they never have half the food on the menu, this place makes a strong argument for going to mcdonalds for your coffee. today's passive aggressive 'screw you' to patrons was setting the thermostat to 60 degrees. people were wearing full out jackets inside. seriously, everytime i think 'maybe there a new manager' and give it a try, i wish i hit a drive thru anywhere else. close. just close.

we go to this location because it's close to school and it's really convenient. they have messed up orders in the past but have always gone above and beyond to ensure we leave happy. today this was not the case -- we left not wanting to come back. the drive thru was busy so we went inside. my friend ordered her food. when i ordered, it took 10 minutes for the girl to put in my order: a breakfast sandwich and a refresher. by this time my friend had already received her drink. it took about another 5 minutes to get my drink which wasn't bad but frustrating because i had already waited 10 minutes to have my order put through. we stood at the counter waiting on our sandwiches for over 15 minutes. not one person asked what we were waiting on or how they could help. the girl working the counter right in front of us kept looking at us but wouldn't say anything. the lady 2 orders behind us got her two sandwiches before we got ours. at this point they still didn't ask why we were still waiting. i asked how long our sandwiches we're going to take and their response was "who did you order with?" i don't understand how this answers my question. so i said "the lady 2 orders behind us just got her sandwiches. how long before we get ours?" she said "oh well there's been some issues with the oven". i said "if there's an issue with the oven, why did the lady 2 orders behind us get hers first?" she just looked at me. there were no issues with the oven. we watched them stand around and talk for the whole 15+ minutes. she handed us our sandwiches and we walked out. there was no attempt to ensure we were happy. there was no customer service at all! after today i am very disappointed and frustrated. we will not be back and we will be going to another location that has way better customer service. if you want to go here, i suggest not wasting your time and find another location. if i could give 0 stars, i would.

i absolutely adore justin and abby at this location. they are so sweet and kind to everyone every single day, and constantly portray an uplifting attitude. i look forward to seeing them each and every time i come to this store.
standard starbucks, like mcdonalds, you can expect consistent food and drink at reasonable prices. often jammed with commuters but that is to be expected with the chain.
clean dining and bathroom areas. friendly staff. they warmed our food up quickly. our drinks took a bit longer, strange since it was only iced coffee, but they came out perfect.

the staff has always been nice. the drinks are made correctly at least 75% of the time. the drive through has always been crazy busy which is no reflection on the starbucks itself. it's to be expected often but this location especially is normally quite busy. we order mobile order ahead and have started going in to pick up our order. it's always ready within about ten minutes from when it's ordered through the app. quick, easy.

i care in because of the new article i read. now what i don't understand is how there are suppose to be writing isis on cups. but it's two different muslim people working there. that i don't get. i think is a set up. the baristas was nice and even while crowded they were still nice. high prices and med speed service

i'm really not sure what all of the complaints here are about. it might be because i purposefully try to avoid suburban square during high-traffic times (like the middle of the afternoon on saturday) but i've never had a bad experience. true, the line can be very long sometimes but that's to be expected for a coffee shop in a popular shopping hot spot. in my opinion, the service is very quick and efficient. it'd be nice to see more of the employees smile, but they've never messed up any order of mine. i've come here to work on assignments for graduate school plenty of times. i have always been able to find a seat. plus, there is free wifi. what's not to like?

this place provides the standard starbucks high quality product served efficiently by pleasant barista staff. i love the comfy outdoor couches (weatherproof plastic of course). that said, i find the inside small and cramped with only high top seating except for a single lower table. not welcoming atmosphere since the remodel necessitated by the closing of the bank branch with whom they shared a large lobby with confer station areas and work/conference tables. i go there on nice days and for drive through. hate the interior.
worst starbucks i have ever been too every time i've gone service is always good but the drinks are terrible tastes like burnt coffee every time

just opened thursday, feb 11, 2016. it's already constantly packed solid. being next to villanova university, i'm sure they knew business would be good for them. my biggest gripe is there is no drive thru but aside from that, i'm very happy how centrally located it is. it's close to my house and near lots of things but also there is ample parking. i've never seen so many parking spaces around a starbucks before. getting in to starbucks is easy enough. but this is a very fast-paced starbucks and not relaxing. it's crammed tight with college students, local middle school and high school kids and people on their laptops. the seating isn't comfortable. mostly tables and no comfy chairs. also, there is no noise control. it's very loud inside. this isn't the best starbucks i've been to but i think it fits the area and it's good for what it is. i will be back for sure.
morning crew at this starbucks is absolutely awesome!!! happy, attentive and great service. plus the baristas make my coffee to near perfection of how i like it.

okay, i am not one to flame a restaurant, first of all. but.. this is ridiculous. this is an establishment that serves food and drinks, and i have never seen so many flies and other bugs in a building since i once went horseback riding in 6th grade. am i exaggerating? no. i am sitting here, right now, in fact, twitching every minutes when a fly lands on me. and it's not the same fly. after swatting four on my own, i gave up. four. there is also a giant wasp-like insect about 5 feet from me. also, there was no toilet paper in the bathroom and i had to ask twice before they brought some. again, if this was a stable, a meadow, or even a bookstore or a seedy bar, the bugs and lack of tp wouldn't bother me. but people are eating food and drinking drinks that have been exposed to some serious insects. and if these are just the bugs i see, i don't want to think about the bugs everywhere else. seriously this is foul. two flies attached to each other (mating? fighting?) just landed on my table. whoa. also, i have had to pretty much assault my friend brittany while trying to get some of the bugs off of her. now i look like an asshole. you might be asking, "but anna, why don't you just leave?" because traffic is about to get really bad, that's why. get it together starbucks
supervisor was rude to me and other employees. poor customer service! find a different location.

even after i convince myself to go here and i go here, i can't convince myself to stay. it is almost always a disgusting line (15+ people) due to this being a university coffee shop. people are either there right after class, or they think that no one will be there between class- along with everyone else. there are a lot of tables along the side of the building, but they're awkward for being social and often overwhelmed by people. impossible to be a relaxing, coffee atmosphere. there are two other cafes on campus, and this one is of course the most expensive. and while the baristas are all nice, because they're students and it sucks just as much for them as you, the business itself is dick. privately owned, they never participate in promotions, not for any cause. they don't even comply with the frequent starbucks card system. i lie- they stock the aids coffee for sale. it's not worth the wait for things this expensive, this bad for you. go to main building, thirtyone41, and get affordable coffee/tea/muffins. i'm not spending $3.25 for the exact same tazo tea (actually, they only give you one bag for a large at starbucks, but two in the main) that costs $1.84 a block away, with a nicer atmosphere and no line. no, thank you.

you take time to write a review if is really good or the opposite:(( i like this location, nothing really that can be criticized other then long lines every time but i like the crowd:)) the outside patio is great!!!! but... i only give 3 stars because every time i go they misunderstand my order. now i learned to read what they write on my cup... misspelling my name is fun! but me repeating a few time "coconut milk please" is embarrassing! if i see they put my drink in dairy blender i know they got wrong and i ask again.... some are funny and chat with you, make a nice comment but yesterday both girls(the one that took order and one made my drink) forced me write the review this morning:(( she took a long time to make this mocha frap with coconut!!!! milk... chat with her coworker that was carrying buckets of ice, making jokes between the two, got distracted with so many other moves around, meantime started like few other drinks... literally i saw my drink waiting at every step to get to the next... it was in blender for like over 2!minutes because she served a few other drinks.. she even took time to rinse the blender cup and top before she got a top for my drink to serve it... my drink got to me very watery because it was melting already and not much flavor... i don't think i had all ingredients in it... but i had to run... otherwise i would have told them to remake it... did not risk it for another long wait. what can i say... i will go there again because the nice location and very convenient to enjoy a sunny morning...

ummm can't really expect much from airport starbucks but some will surprise you. i think hawaii and cancun annnnd seattle have done a number on me so i'm so picky now lol. got ice green tea latte, no need for syrup or extras but i do ask for light ice and some that notice that will add extra matcha but.... not here. it seemed like i was being a bother when i mentioned the little things i didn't want and want, coconut milk, light ice and no syrup. ( the look was priceless with light ice) i have a venti cup of iced green tea coconut milk flavored latte. i will definitely drink it and just note not to come to this location unless i want a basic all the bells and whistles drink young kids get. something told me to get a grande just in case but i have a 3 hr flight.
brought a cold back from my trip to mississippi (thanks airplanes) so stopped in for a hot chocolate while my daughter was at kung fu. it hit the spot - rich, smooth and soothing. a nice treat!!
been here twice and i am impressed that even with a long ass line the whole order a drink and pick it up process is fast and smooth. works for me. plus almond milk cappuccino? holler!

come here often, but today we ordered a fizzed tea. person at the counter took our order. when we got to our car, i notice the tea wasn't fizzed. went back in and they told me the machine was broke. why would you not tell us when the order was placed. when ask to exchange it for a refesher. there was none on display and the claim there were none in back. terrible service. they always act like they're too busy. won't be going back to this location.

its a good location.i don't like the coffee at the place next door, but combine a starbucks coffee with a tart from the other place and its a win-win. well maybe not for my waist line or pocket book. i like they have large common tables with plenty of outlets under them for computers. i really appreciate the multiple outlet on the walls. too many people bogart every one of the power plugs, but thats not going to happen here.

lack of basics including heating (it's cold through out even with several layers on and a down coat; even worse is the doors were swinging open or are left open so any one standing in line or sitting nearby really freezes). also several times i received the wrong order so staffing training needs are an issue. the customer counter (napkins, spices, etc) was often not refilled. staff was very friendly though. we never plan to come here again. more locally owned coffee shops nearby are definitely a better use of time, value and money.

very good service and friendly staff. i was happy to find a starbucks with good service here in tucson.had several unpleasant experiences with the drive thru on speedway so this was a welcome find. at times the drive through seems to get priority leaving only one server for customers in the store.but all in all a good place to visit.

cashier with the dark pink and blonde hair has never been friendly. as the first person you interact with in the morning....this should be unacceptable coming from someone working at starbucks. disappointed.
they never make the drink correct. that's all i ask. just make the drink the way i ask. everytime we go there it tastes different. same drink, different flavor.

if you've been to one starbucks, you've been to them all, when it comes to the food + drinks, prices, and the overall appearance. we stopped by at around 8pm to do some studying + work, and just bought a cookie. it was perfectly toasted and delicious. what i love about this starbucks is there is plenty of seating. there's large shared tables, bars, small tables, etc. there are also outlets everywhere. like some of the starbucks in my area, the staff try to make the atmosphere more welcoming. this starbucks also has a riddle of the day (where you can ask the barista for the answer) and a bookshelf in the back to leave & take a book as well as some games. the parking lot is not proportional to the number of seats in the starbucks, but luckily it was not busy so we were able to get a spot. they also have reserved parking spaces for those just stopping in for mobile orders or anyone who was only parking for 15 minutes. for any night owls that prefer to grind + do work later in the day, i would recommend this location!

i don't normally review large company chain type places such as starbucks. but, i've been stopped by here several times in the morning during the busiest hours and the drive thru has always taken five minutes or less. they're always speedy and all of the employees are always polite and nice. i've also never had my order screwed up which is amazing. also i do gotta give the company a shout out for addressing the volume of their plastic straw use and encouraging more use of reusable cups. i appreciate that they listen to what customers have to say on that matter. now if they could just switch to paper cups without a plastic coating that can be recycled - then we'd really be talking! also considering the location of the store, there's decent parking. also if you are really in a pinch and are looking for parking so you can go into the store and study or something like that, you can always park at costco and walk over - just be careful crossing the road between the starbucks and costco!

terrible parking and terrible customer service from one of the workers here. she's super rude and didn't even greet anyone coming up to her. just assumed what size i wanted my drink (which was wrong).
great job keeping up during corona virus limited service. had drive through yesterday. fast and friendly. order ahead was correct and on time. pick up was easy. good job guys!

how long does it take to make a bagel? i ordered in the line? and now my frap is melting because they had me pull over to the front. i didn't even get curbside service even though i clearly wanted drive thru service since that's where i placed my order. i got out of my car twice in this heat while waiting. ridiculous guys. wtf.

love this starbucks, love these baristas. jacob is the best - friendly, fun, remembers drinks and names - he brightens the day. carrington remembered us from a previous starbucks and is really good to us. it's our favorite starbucks!

this starbucks is in a cute little part of webster groves next to gazebo park, where events and farmers markets are held in summer. it's just like any other starbucks but this is right next to my work so i go there all the time, usually around 2:30-3pm when i hit the afternoon slump. the staff are friendly and always prompt with my order, and they rarely mess it up. my go-to drinks are the white chocolate mocha with non-fat milk and no whipped cream, as well as the java chip frappacino, skinny and without whipped cream. the clientele at this location are a mixture of business people, hippies, young teens (nerinx hall, an all-girls high school is just around the corner), university students (webster university is within walking distance), artists, entrepreneurs, and stay-at-home moms. you really get a mixed bag here. i typically don't sit and stay to drink my lattes, but when i have in the past, the furniture is comfy, with a variety of seating choices for individuals or small groups, and the place is kept clean and orderly. it doesn't wow my pants off, but it maintains the high standard of starbucks that i expect. a solid standby.

since when does the drive thru not take your trash for you? since starbucks removed the drive thru trash can, i've been able to give my starbucks trash cups to the drive thru window to make space for the new cups. today the woman wouldn't take it. she looked at me like i was crazy. that's new, because at every other store i've been to, they always can take it from me. maybe this new location is too good for customer service.
worst starbucks ever. beans are always burned, line is ridiculous, and the team is slow. nordstroms e-bar and the new espresso joint are much better, faster, and nicer

worst service here! sadly making me look for my coffee elsewhere. no reason to have such poor, unprofessional and generally unhappy/unhelpful service. every-time. this is not a one time offense otherwise i would let it slide.

warning...as a previous reviewer mentioned, the employees here are horrible. much more concerned with typical teen angst issues than their jobs. if you have food allergies, please note the following-- i ordered a coconut milk beverage, and specified no whipped cream. i have a very intense lactose intolerance issue. i *should* have watched her pour the drink and i should have asked her to use a clean pitcher for steaming. that's my bad...normally i'm all over that. i did not assume any untrustworthiness on her part, and i also didn't want to deal with her eye rolling. i am 100% sure she gave me cow's milk. i almost took myself to the emergency room, it was so bad. i never drink cow's milk because it sets off lactose intolerance issues worse than anything. the worst part is i had so many appointments for the rest of the day and so many things to do, and i had to cancel half of them, and struggle through the rest while thinking i was literally on the verge of a nasty lactose-induced death. i've never had such careless, thoughtless treatment at a starbucks...! i will not return to this location, but i'd say it's a safe guess that the manager doesn't give a sh@#, given who works there. bad managers hire bad employees. if you have any food allergies, please be very safe and do not purchase anything from this starbucks location. the same for anyone allergic to self-centered, emotionally stunted teens who hate their stupid jobs and can't wait to get out of here because oh my god ainsley's having her big party tonight you guyssss! hang on one sec, i have to help this stupid customer, *sighhhhh*

a starbucks is a starbucks, right? not really i have visited the good and the bad. what i appreciate the most about this starbucks is the exceptionally friendly service. i have only had fantastic interactions at this location and the staff goes above & beyond to ensure you have a great visit. plus it has a drive thru, what can be better than not having o get out of your car for your daily dose of caffeine. my only complaint, they don't carry cinnamon chip scones. :(

this is literally the most poorly ran starbucks i've ever encountered. the staff is extremely friendly, but they never seem to do anything with a purpose, even if they know there's 50 people in line. i've often seen them pause for a few moments to chat with no sense of urgency to get customers through. while this would be normally infuriating at any location, more so at an airport. if it's a staffing issue, then please staff better, especially during peak hours. but it's never okay for it to take 30+ minutes to get to the cash register. wait is to be expected to receive your drink. but to order? that's simply not caring.

the service here is one of the worst i've seen at starbucks. they try to be so quick (even when it's not that busy - i guess they are rushing to take a break) that they miss the customer service part. i'm serious about customer service - some may say, it's just a cup a coffee - i would say, so is folger's instant crystals, and when they have store and baristas, i will expect the same from them.

i was asked to leave for not having a mask. i was happy to wait for my coffee outside so i asked if they could notify me while i wait outside. they said no i can not let you know when your coffee is ready. they were extremely rude. on top of it they were wearing clothing affiliated with a political movement that was found to be offensive. i would not recommend this starbucks.

one star because the parking lot here is terrible. it's that bad. traffic from the drive-thru line backs uo out onto brentwood blvd. when this happens, which is frequently, it's extremely difficult for cars exiting the drive-thru to leave. nearly impossible to get one of the very few parking spaces that they have, and overall just a mess. this place was planned extremely poorly.
i just a iced carmel coffee. delicious refreshing , made perfect. starbucks is the best.

i am very impressed with the manager at this starbucks. she is professional and nice. i had a problem with a drink and she was very courteous and helpful in replacing it. it is a very busy business and they still manage to be happy nice employees.

first time coming to this location and nikki was amazing, she was so sweet and polite when taking my order. also liked the fact that their drive thru was pretty quick. most starbucks take a little longer. nikki just made this overall experience really great. loved her, oh right, the drink was delicious!

they never have anything! every morning, noon, or night i go here i can ever get what i want because "unfortunately we are all out of that"! its ridiculous, why have only a certain amount of products knowing ya'll always running out of stuff. annoying

i ordered a caramel frap with add shot and when i took my first sip, had no flavor at all! taste like blended iced water! so i told the lady there was no flavor at all so she remade it, did not apologize and then when i received my remake she did not add the shot so when i brought that up to her she gave a face and got so irritated and the other lady ended up just remaking. i dealt with that third remake but was still not satisfied! they did no take their time to make the drinks or they need more training cause this one had a little bit of flavor. worst starbucks i've ever been too! you would think it would be better quality considering it's located in a hotel / casino. staff was not happy or friendly, horrible customer service.

this was our first visit to this location and they were picture perfect! it was swamped but they didn't make us feel rushed, bless the barista that asked me 'and what else?' vs the always obnoxious 'is that all?'. our order was relatively simple this time but all the drinks around us were pretty customized and i didn't hear one mistake. also, by the time the rest of our order was done i needed an ice water refill and the bar barista was perfectly gracious and didn't come across inconvenienced in the slightest to handle it for me. i'm only afraid to go back because this visit was so smooth
i stopped in for the 1st time in probably a year. its slightly redesigned and it was not freezing cold. my drink was perfect, which was unfortunate because i drank it too fast.

i love starbucks, but it shouldn't take 20 minutes to get a drink. also, if there's a line out the door you probably should have more than one person making drinks. someone fast, preferably. head a block over to 19th for faster service.

now that ikes is gone there are not many places to hang out over coffee nearby, but i had to meet someone here once and i'm glad i did. there's a huge table that can be used for groups. i was ready for a discussion on race but i was the only minority in here for the hour i stayed.

the only reason i suffered this stupid starbucks is that apparently sparks doesn't do indie coffee shops. cosmic just closed (my favorite) so maybe i'm just pissed off about that. maybe we should follow russia's lead and annex sparks and force them to drink indie coffee. understandably the idiot barista put diary milk in my drink despite clearly telling her i wanted soy (although i would have prefered almond which starsucks doesn't carry). how f cking hard is it writing on a cup? she didn't even make the drink, she just had to write on that stupid cup "soy." do they teach that at sparks hs? writing? listening? starsucks may not be the worst corporation, but the only money that goes back into our local economy is the wages they pay, and the workers are probably just eating corporate junk food and buying clothes at some corporate chain. with an indie store, the owners are spending their profits here and they're buying local coffee and supplies. do you not realize we have the worst economy in the country and need to shop local? thanks for the sh tty drink you green apron-wearing, mermaid logo franchise, cr p food-selling, over-rated bullsh t coffee chain

this location as others mentioned is very hot or miss. it's the closest of the five in the area to me, but also the one i frequent the least. it's a mall location so those of you who may hang out for business or for meetings may find space and noise louder than usual. the staff is usually friendly but not as 'warm' as some of the others. it feels less cozy and more about customer turnaround as a result. the plus side, never had a problem with drink orders done well!

i've driven past this starbucks many times and thought it looked like an interesting place. i've never stopped, but today i found myself downtown and thought i should stop in and have a look. overall i'd say it's a pretty nice starbucks, but it suffers from one fatal flaw as a work location (unless you live nearby) and that is the pay parking. i didn't spend a lot of time driving around looking for free parking, because i don't think it exists in this area. $3.00 an hour. it's great if you live or work nearby and have the parking taken care of or are using transit to get here. otherwise, if you're driving , forget it, unless have a meeting set up here. work for a few hours and it's going to cost you, not to mention the inconvenience of having to go plug the meter. it's at the bottom of an apartment building, so i'm sure the residents know this location well. busyness - there are a lot of apartments around here so i expect this place is pretty busy in the morning. i wouldn't mind a starbucks near my house. noisiness - for being pretty steady, it hasn't been too bad for a friday afternoon. it's winter now, so i don't know what kind of crazy person would order an iced drink to get those blenders going. but, it is canada. ambience - this isn't your usual boxy starbucks. it clips off the bottom corner of the building so it has an unusual layout. the windows seem to line about half of the walls, so it's quite bright inside, with high ceilings. power - i didn't really see many outlets at the tables, so you may have a problem unless your laptop is miserly with the power. free wifi - available and works well. staff - friendly and efficient, as usual. seating - there are 21 seats here, with one large table to work at. if you're into relaxing in an easy chair, there are four of them tucked away in the corner and there's an electrical outlet there. perfect place for a power meeting or power nap. the rest of the tables are the square type with two chairs each. i like them much better than the useless round cafe style tables. security - it's a nice neighbourhood, but if you've read my other reviews you'll know i'm just paranoid about that kind of stuff.

as some of the previous comments have mentioned, this starbucks is great. i'm not from around this part of town, but i needed a place to get some work done. hence, starbucks. i've been here for roughly an hour and i am amazed at how many regulars these baristas know (on a first-name at that), which is why i'm taking a break from writing in order to leave this review. i never thought i'd leave a yelp review for starbucks, but these baristas deserve some serious recognition. i've never been to a starbucks where the baristas and customers, as a whole, have a relationship as good as this. i sincerely hope the manager reads this comment and acknowledges how awesome they are (wednesday, january 9th at 3:00 p.m.).

i love this starbucks. my favorite place to study. the baristas are so friendly/happy and frequently give discounts and freebies to regulars (and free samples of food to everyone). also, i've never not been able to find a place to sit. outlets are usually available, only a handful of times have i had to wait about half an hour for one to open up. also, when the weather's nice, there's a great covered porch outside with lights and ceiling fans (and outlets). it's true that their internet is restricted to t-mobile/at&t/sbc customers, but i think that actually keeps the place from getting overcrowded with students during peak study times (i suppose they go to pjs or rue instead). however, if you know someone with a password for one of those providers, you can use it unlimitedly (i've shared my password with a friend there and we were both able to log-on using it at the same time). i would love to support "local" coffee shops, and i've tried, but all the places close to me have fatal flaws: --too crowded and no tables during peak hours/times (rue on carrollton) --too few outlets and trash all over the place (pjs on maple) --apathetic (bordering on rude sometimes) baristas, terribly low lighting, too few outlets (rue on magazine) --zots (way too dark)
nice to have a real starbucks inside the hotel! friendly staff and well made drinks, as expected. there is an outdoor patio or small beach area owned by the hotel so you can relax outside.

as far as starbucks go, this one is pretty great and pretty typical. they always get my drink quickly for me and the staff is really nice. even when there is a long drive through line, it moves really quickly. something that is important to know about this starbucks is that it is small. there is not a lot of seating space, so i would not suggest going to this location for a meeting or to be with friends. it is likely you will not be able to sit anywhere in a group. they do have window seating. this is usually where i sit when i come here to study and do homework. i like this spot because it is the perfect height to stand up and work! i would also suggest wearing a sweater or some layers because i am always chilly here. as always, i suggest getting a "for here mug" to make your starbucks experience more cozy!

coffee is a lot harder to find than daqueries in this part of town, so starbucks would have been fine for a fix, this is on the far side of the marriot lobby, went in at 1:30 in the afternoon and they were already closed, wtf?

i visit this starbucks every morning before work. i order the same thing every day. i wish i received what i ordered every day. lack of consistency. today my coffee looks like milk with toffee syrup - forgot the coffee? one day my toffee nut syrup was replaced with gingerbread?!! what if i was allergic ?!! i stopped going to dunkin in part because of a lack of consistency.. this is just getting absurd.

as everyone knows, you can't swing a dead.....um, never mind feline fans.... take two: starbucks are everywhere, right, so why review one? well, because in contrast to schultz's ideal vision of the company and its products, not all sbux are consistent in providing legendary service and the world's best coffee or whatever the mission statement is....yes, i am a former barista! however, this is my neighborhood shop and i like it, so there. i don't really go there for the coffee - breves, my rare indulgence are still better at the monon, lulu's and mojoe's. but, i love the passion tea (with melon syrup, since valencia was pulled...what was that all about?) and i like the staff here. they are friendly and efficient and more interested in waiting on you, usually, than regaling each other with dating nightmare stories, showing off new tattoos or re-capping the latest project runway episode - wait, that was me discussing pr with the barista.... anyway, starbucks also tends to be my "go to" place for grabbing something to eat in the car as a lunch substitute. and, with their new healthy goodies like the oatmeal and the whole-grain roll with peanut butter, i am leaning toward being a "born again buxer" in the mornings! i just hope the corporate offices don't pull these items like they've done with so many other things from days gone by....seriously, was i the only one who loved that hot muesli-steamed milk-fruit concoction they had a while back?????

it's starbucks so i can't say much. i enjoy a latte now and again, but of course, it's pricey. this particular location can get really congested. staff is okay; they don't quite have the friendliness of most starbucks baristas i've encountered. i wouldn't recommend coming during peak times, or if you're in a hurry.

this is my family's usual (and favorite) starbucks in our neighborhood. they are quick, courteous, and usually remember you and/or your usual (even if you regularly just go once a week). i appreciate the fact that they all work together so well to get you your items quickly and accurately whether you are in the drive through or go inside. another nearby starbucks focuses on the drive through much more and it can take you quite awhile to get your items inside (even if you are taking it to go and the only ones waiting). it's like night and day with the service and friendliness of these two and this one most definitely wins! if you are parking to go in, i highly recommend that you park on the part of their parking lot that faces out and away from starbucks and over on the half that is in front of fed ex. it is much safer pulling out of that area, because many times people come racing around that corner over by where the drive through comes out. i've heard of others who avoid this starbucks because of this, but you only need to park in the correct spot. this is the only negative, so when you choose to take my advice on where to park... there is no reason not to go here. i also highly recommend their rewards type program. like i said, we usually just go once a week (and also often on road trips), but we still have earned free frappuccinos several times.

one of the better starbucks in my opinion. they actually keep their outdoor tables clean. (the starbucks on hazel dell has disgusting tables). and the baristas are cool too. for some reason they have begun locking one of their only two doors before dark. this is a main door and not on an alley so there is no security reason. from the patio to the open door is almost on the other side of the building. i don't get it. it's 2018 folks, make it convenient for your customers!

i usually go to this starbucks every morning. for the most part it's like any other starbucks, they have messed up my drinks a couple of times because i usually change it up everyday. and sometimes the barista will see me and make me what i ordered the day before. i guess that's not really a bad thing that they recognize me as a customer? sometimes the staff is slow in the morning. and if you get there in the late morning, don't expect to get any to yogurt, sandwiches or bistro boxes. they will all be long gone.

it's a starbucks, like any other one, and it's just a few blocks over from the school i teach at. there's a drive thru and a confusing parking lot, but i understand why it's set up like that. it's a busy intersection; people are going to be coming in from belle chase and from terry. it's fine, just park. there's never a wait if you go inside, although the drive thru line can get a little long. i only order maybe 3 things ever and i'm in and out. the staff is nice and polite, recognize their regular customers, and the bathrooms are clean. no complaints here.
great pick me up pit stop on a road trip!!! the drinks are on point and the staff is cool. gets the job done.
this location has a consistently friendly staff. makes my monday's a lot easier when i get to interact with people that are nice, quick, and knowledgeable of the product offerings!

this is, without a doubt, my favorite starbucks. it's brand new and is so nice. the interior is beautiful. they have high top tables with outlets attached. you can sit or stand while working! they also have tables against the wall that you can sit at. they also have bigger comfy chairs in the corners by the windows! this starbucks is pretty big and has so many places to sit. the staff here is also really nice. they area always so friendly and make our drinks really quickly. i think that this starbucks is great for all kinds of people. a great place for people who want to do work or study and great for people who are meeting up with friends! this has quickly become my favorite place to study and do homework. i highly suggest checking it out!

this location is always very friendly and accommodating. however the last couple of times i've been and gotten a pink drink, it's looked like this. sort of hard to see in the photo, but it's not mixed well such that the coconut milk is sort of floating in pieces inside the lemonade stuff. the first time i thought they were just in a hurry or something, but today it was not mixed well again. highly recommend the drink! but it tastes much better when it's mixed smooth.

this was the first time i've ever come to this starbucks, but i will be sure not to come back. the poor barista working was left all by herself while her partner was in the back doing who knows what. while they weren't incredibly busy, the woman before me had placed a rather large order. the barista then called her partner out from the back for some help. he asked what drinks he could get started on for me and my friend. i placed my order for an iced green tea latte and he gave me the wrong size. there is quite a size difference between a grande and a venti but the price difference is small so i'd much rather have the venti and get the bang for my buck (considering more than half of it is full of ice anyway). then he hands me the cup which is basically milk that he threw the matcha powder on top. he did not mix it at all and handed it to me. the girl barista quickly pulled the drink back and said "umm this needs to be mixed" but by the time she said that be had already run to the back room. he definitely was not a trainee, i would have had patience for that. he was just in a rush, depite the fact that there were no customers in line behind me. i honestly would have rather waited a few more minutes to have the woman barista serve me than to have this guy. he made me feel as if i was wasting his time and an inconvenience to him. overall i'm a bit disappointed because the store itself is nice and the woman barista was nice, but the other barista really set a bad first impression for me.

at first i was skeptical about the location of this new starbucks. but it turned out to be perfect! looks small from the outside but very spacious (and clean) on the inside with plenty of seating. also has a very nice outdoor patio. nicely designed, large drive-thru. very friendly baristas. great location & great coffee!

this starbucks is a real stinker! overcharged! refused to refund! i was overcharged for a vente sized decaf americana. the cashier indicated that a vente decaf brew was unavailable; however, he could offer an americana instead at a higher cost. i urged him to brew a decaf or offer me the americana at the same price. the male cashier refused. after paying the cashier, i requested the female server to provide management's contact information. she refused. moreover, she confirmed that i was indeed overcharged, but she declined to offer a refund for the difference. we are guests at the grand sierra and will also pass down this over charge at checkout. moreover, starbucks senior staff will also be notified. below optimum staffing! management is responsible. caveat emptor!

love that this starbucks is close to my house. they are always friendly. my only issue is they have 4 wall outlets in the entire store. 1 of those is hidden behind the condiments bar, leaving virtually no outlets for the entire seating area other than the 2 comfy chairs. not cool. it's as if they don't want people hanging out or working from there. i probably won't come back here to chill or work. sad, cause it's an easy fix to run a strip of outlets down the wall. please fix.

great service and gray drinks. they always have my order written down before i even get to the register and i always get smiling very nice service. the food is great too! now they are very pricey, but well worth it if you are a coffee lover.
it's right by my work so i come here all the time. staff here is great, good service. lunch time extremely busy though with a school nearby!
i loveeeeee starbucks it is sooo delicious escpecially the cake pops and frappecinos!!!!
great customer service on every visit. the employees ate always so chipper. they make my visit thru the drive-thru great.

this is my go-to starbucks when i have a lot of work to get done and need a good environment to work in. compared to a couple other fishers starbucks locations this one is a little smaller in square feet but seating is more ample in my opinion, i've yet to have problems finding a spot to set up shop. the staff is always super friendly and take the time to notice regulars. i have never had a negative service experience at this location personally nor have seen anything negative with other patrons, even considering i've spent multiple days working in front of the counter for 12+ hours. i'm impressed. coffee is coffee, starbucks quality coffee across the board. pro-tip: bring an extra layer if you get cold easily. during the day and especially as the night goes on, it can get pretty chilly inside (which is nice on hot days).
slowest.starbucks.ever. not to mention inconsistent & sometimes even rude. i've already complained to corporate but nothing has changed.

i was so happy to go to this starbucks! they filled up my drink all the way to the point where it was overfilling. plus the barista said they ran out of strawberry acai but they actually had enough for just one more and i was so happy this literally made my day

worst starbucks i have ever been to. one worker on a saturday morning, stuff all over the place, out of caramel and other items, nothing in the cases. looks like the remnants of a hurricane hit this store. will never come here again as i don't have time for this nonsense. going on 15 minutes waiting for my drinks now.
tried the drive thru. it's wrapped around the building so i go inside only to find nobody in there but 4 people just standing behind the counter talking. good job guys. way to run a business.

have had issues at this location before accusing me of stealing so i'm always weary about coming back. thank you vaughn for helping me today. you were awesome! i hope you're working next time i'm in so i can have another great experience.

been here twice now. first time, they messed up my grande black iced coffee. second time today, also destroyed my grande black iced coffee. black coffee should not taste like it has 1000 packets of sugar in it. my bagel and cream cheese consisted of a black brick and two tiny packets of butter. i even asked the lady who handed me the bag "this is with cream cheese, right?". sucks for me i didn't check before i got 10 minutes down the road. so now i'm out $10, with coffee i can't drink and a burnt frisbee. i'll be walking a few extra minutes to go to dunkin donuts from now on.

customer service is still great. nice employees and my order is always good and correct. i'm updating this review because the drive through wait is horribly long and has gotten worse over the years. i typically can't stop here in the morning because the wait is guaranteed to take ten to fifteen minutes. if they work on speed this review will go up.

starbucks is the mcdonald's of coffee shops, right? so, i should probably temper my expectations. that said, the whole experience is corporate, lame and i can't wait for it to be over one i'm there. from the other customers who would push you out of the way to get their joe 30 seconds sooner, to the barista that mumbles the drink orders as they come up, the need to go there is a useful reminder to never let yourself go to sleep without coffee in the house for the next morning.

i ordered an ice green tea latte but the clerk made a regular latte. she had an attitude when i corrected her. she was apathy about her mistake. this is no the first time we experienced poor customer service at this store. my friend was cut by the entrance door and bleed heavily from the cut. couple employees came out to clean the blood off the floor. none of them care to ask my friend how she was doing. this store completely lack professionalism, care, and respect toward their patrons. collectively we all spent over $1000 per month at this store, we all decided to spend our money elsewhere. we all very disappointed; this store deserves 0 star.

when you have a broken machine customers who aren't having a drink made from that machine shouldn't be told it will be a wait. an iced drink should take a matter of seconds to make. maybe having a plan to organize your workers for a situation like this would be more efficient.

i love starbucks and will probably never stop, but this location is making it really hard. mornings are busy, but the staff barely acknowledges customers, and about a quarter of the time they get the app order wrong - usually because they put too little ice or too much milk when we specify extra ice and only a splash of milk, for example. they also seem annoyed during the rush when i bother to ask the baristas to please hand me my app order, which i can't grab myself. they don't know who i am, even though i'm in there at least 4 days a week. one good thing though is that the app order is always ready in a timely fashion, even if it is not always exactly the way we ordered it.

love the personal treatment received by each barista. wasn't a huge fan of the male manager/register. not sure of his title. all window staff at drive thru are awesome! i look forward to seeing nicholas. he's a crack up. makes me laugh. couple of the girls are super bubbly and fun too. love this location as my favorite drive thru

playing the starbucks stars game today. need to keep my gold card status for the little one in our life. the service today was excellent. when i went up to the counter to get a free refill i was asked how i liked the pineapple black tea and i said i didn't. they happily switched my refill to strawberry green tea. much better.

i live a bit more than 5 minutes away, and this has been my favorite spot in the neighborhood for like a year and a half. food: cream cheese brownie is pretty good. i didn't really try any other food though. drink: one of a few starbucks stores that serve green tea frappuchino. i don't really drink anything, but frappuchino and smoothies. personally, i think they're all good. atmosphere: nice leather chairs, tables and counter. if you want to read some book, there are comfy leather chair far away from the blender noises. personally, i like to hope on one of the three seats near the bar-counter. i love to see them very professionally preparing drinks of all kinds, and look at the line of patron. staffs: professional and friendly, especially the blond, young guy. not sure what's his name. brandon, i guess? anyway, he's extremely professional, friendly, cheerful, and polite.

average starbucks. i have been through the drive thru and inside many times. parking lot is always full and sometimes i have to park in the neighboring hotels lot. the employees are friendly, however there are always new people here. accuracy of the orders are sacrificed because they are always training new people. i am pretty leinient on my scoring because of that. i am not reviewing starbucks products on this review because i have done so in others. their products taste the same as every other one around.

best mango-hibiscus drink! i'm not a regular at starbucks but i've been in several & this one was by far the best one i've seen! very open with tables or comfortable chairs and tables by window. bathroom was clean and had baby changing table. there's also a small semi private room/area for work or studying or possibly nursing. all staff friendly! also had many more choices for a non-coffee drinker like me where others i've been in typically offer much less. i recommend this location! did forget to get my free birthday item though-oh well maybe later:)
go to get coffee at 3:30 in the afternoon and the store is closed! how's that happen? website says it's open until 9:30pm. hey starbucks what's going on?

i've never actually been inside this starbucks. as often as i've gone it's always been the drive through. so i cannot comment on the interior but i can say the drive-thru baristas are always friendly and they get my order right 100% of the time. can't ask for anything more than that. honestly the only reason they don't get 5 stars is because this location is so darned inconvenient, which isn't really there fault but it's still a thing so..

first off i would like to say that i (we) visit starbucks quite often but today was terrible. today is st. patricks day, and we are a business, so we decided to place an order, as we do on all holidays. needless to say our employee is still there... 1 1/2 hours later. they lost our order. he kept telling them he needs to get back to work. we have a full restaurant and full lobby. i called them and they said " we will get to this order as soon as we can" . i told him he needed to get back to work...just get our money back but they said they could not do it. i have heard from several other people that this location has went downhill but i usually don't listen to people since i have never had a problem, until now. i just wanted to let you all know that this has upset alot of people. i usually don't write reviews but today i was very upset.

place is always really busy but everyone there is really nice. one guy, i think his name is grant(?), is super super fun to talk to. store manager is super sweet! overall everyone is really nice, just slow!

for goodness sake! when you have eight orders stacked up, and another eight customers waiting in line to order, please consider cutting out the chit chatting with your buddies, and focus on actually doing your job. employees who are off-duty and visiting... please stop the socializing when your friends are trying to complete orders while customers are standing there waiting, and watching you all banter around. socializing and friendly chatter are great, but, please know when to cut it out.

first, the guy taking the orders said he wasn't taking cash right now because the girl that takes cash wasn't there. next, there were about 6 people behind the counter and it took forever to get my drink. i have never seen a starbucks team work so slow. last, my drink didn't even taste good. didn't want to go back and wait another 15 minutes. also, there was a girl, that was several people ahead of me, that was still waiting on her food. they had forgotten all about her. they need new staff!

not the easiest starbucks to get to because of 202, but other than that, it's pretty much your typical starbucks inside. during our visit, i think the cashier was in training as he seemed to be struggling with the orders, but other than that, no major issues. my order came out quickly and correctly. there is no drive-thru at this location.
waited 20 min for an iced coffee. people came and went and i still couldn't get my drink. terrible service.

beautiful setting, wonderful coffee, and most importantly beautiful people. this starbucks has been my go to place for almost a year now, and i absolutely love it. the staff is wonderful and loves to start small conversations with you and the coffee is well prepared. the atmosphere could be better and they could use a few more chairs but overall it's a wonderful place.
great place. they have very clean bathrooms and trust me i've seen quite a lot of bathrooms in my day!
line takes forever in morning. the drive thru people are very rude sometimes when you ask about the products they offer.

super-fast drive-thru (even with the kind of wonky layout). staff was personable and friendly from the order taking to the pick-up window. they made me a delicious strawberry frappuccino to go with my banana nut bread - which they offered to heat and did.

the worst ever starbuck you may ever experience. the staff are rude to the customers and one of the staff brought outside food and sat next to us and it was very uncomfortable because of the smell and he was very loud, it doesn't feel like i am in a cafe very disturbing and unprofessional! i know many people will agree on this, which is not to allow certain people to come in this starbucks. many complaints were made about this but something should be done about this. woman are being harassed here and no one is doing anything about it.

very disappointed with this location. i get a large ice water with mactcha scoops. it's always been 87 cents. i've paid for green tea latte and frappe. price is not the issue. today it came up to almost 3 bucks. she said its tea and "labor" involved. ice, water, powder seriously? i can see it it was hot brewed then poured over ice. it's still got chunks of powder in it. for that price it better be clump free. difference was put back on my card but left a bad taste nonetheless.

amazing staff and store! i love visiting this starbucks location! my drink is always made perfectly and several times the staff has gone above and beyond to make my starbucks trip great!:) the pumpkin cream cold brew is my go to seasonal fall drink! the perfect blend of creaminess and pumpkin spice!

i love the drive through at this starbucks. i realize that sentence makes me sound like the laziest person alive, but i'm typically on my way to a friend's house whenever i swing by this location and the drive through is just too convenient to pass up. everyone who works there is super friendly from my experiences and the line moves quickly. they even plug your drinks with those little green plastic things so there's no spillage. i just wish there was a more convenient way to get out of the parking lot, instead of having to drive back through the strip mall lot.

this location has gotten a bad rap in the past but the day i was here, it was wonderful. megan greeted me when i came in, was very friendly and eager to help. i forgot to load my card... i know, pet peeve... but nobody was behind me waiting. megan was patient and sweet about it. when a group came in behind me and didn't know what they wanted, she said she would stand ready to help when they were ready and not to worry about how long it took to decide. the baristas were very fast and stayed busy making drinks quickly. everyone who walked in received a warm greeting. i'm glad i gave this place a chance.

i've never wriiten a negative review on a business based on their political stance. but in the case of starbucks, i feel most compelled to do so. my family and i have enjoyed your drinks and your assosiates are curteous and respectful. i can not agree with the foolish statements made by ceo howard shultz regarding illegal immigration. our nation has laws that are to be followed and respected. when an indivdual such as ceo shultz advocates giving employment to individuals who disrespect our laws, i can no longer respect him yes, legal immigrants are always, always welcome.it's who we are as a nation.illegal immigrants are not.instead of advocating to hire 10,000 illegal immigrants, maybe starbucks should consider hiring 10,000 homeless veterans. aren't our men and women in uniform worth at least that much? a former happy customer, gone for life. #resistthe media

i have been here twice now. the first time was great. i got my drink and there were plenty of places to sit and the barristas were awesome. the second time was awful. i asked for a skinny mocha frappachino light. it came with whipped creme, which i expressly asked not to be included. it's not really a big deal; when i brought it up to the barrista, he made me feel self conscious in front of the other customers and only offered to scrape the creme off! i really didnt have a choice to ask for a new one without looking like a jerk. it was busy at the time i was there but next time i'll just stick w a black coffee.

almost every time i come to this starbucks they get my drink order wrong. i usually order an iced quad espresso with 2 % and a few pumps of a flavored syrup (from other starbucks) and i've never had a problem. when i order it here it looks extremely light or super watery. the third time they remade it i just didn't say anything and went to the starbucks across from wiregrass because it still didn't look right and i didn't trust it(drink was right at wiregrass location wish i took a picture to compare)this starbucks is closer to my house so i try to give it a chance but for now i'm going to just stick to my usual one by wiregrass. staff is friendly but baristas need more training making iced espresso drinks. (noting i use to work for starbucks so i know how the drinks are made)

i'm used to quality service, came back from europe where baristas are awesome. i drive about 15 minutes to get to this starbucks and their service is getting worse every time i come here. two days in a row i asked for a brownie and chocolate chip cookie at 845 pm and they are out of it. those are two main things a coffee shop should have... coffee was good tho.. so i will be taking my business else where, can't go to red lion and blvd or the one inside target which is the worst of them all.. probably try abington area next time.

it was kind of a surprise to eye a starbucks in a shopping complex just off one of the seedier strips in the city, but a very welcome one, as my need for a warm wake-up drink was high after hours of sifting through dusty thrift-shop merchandise. i ordered a cappuccino, which was the usual tasty treat, and a fruit and nut bar, something starbucks has been carrying recently as a healthier option. they're basically a granola bar, lightly sweet, and what really makes these stand out is the addition of dried apricots and cherries. yum! maybe it was just the time of day, or the fact that coffee shops in this area are few and far between, but this starbucks appears to get quite a bit of business.

waited in the drive through line for literally 35 minutes for one hot cocoa. with only 3 people ahead of me in line. waited at the window for 5 minutes without being helped because the worker just left the window unattended; had to flag another staff member down. watched them in there with absolutely 0 urgency as i was at the window. i'm usually understanding of workers in the service industry because i know things can get hectic, but this was straight up infuriating. avoid this place if you can. ruined my whole mood.
no stars. this place is the worst. service sucks , people suck, all around just sucks.
managed to mess up a vanilla sweet cream cold brew. only place that's managed to do so.

i go here a lot because it is so close to my house and right next to trader joe's which i frequent but every time it is so crowded - with a line around the building for the drive thru and a line inside as well. the parking situation is terrible. you basically can't get to their parking because of the long drive thru lines so i park in the main parking lot but you have to walk across the drive thru line to get into the store (they've now put fencing up so you have to walk around). at least half the time i am in there they either completely forget to make my drink or make me a frappuchino instead of a latte (seriously why has this happened on more than one occasion?? latte sounds nothing like frappuchino). *sigh* but i'll still keep coming here because #imlazy and a #coffeeaddict. positive notes? the coffee is good when i get it, sometimes i get a free drink out of them messing up my order because otherwise they just throw it away and they offer my pup a free puppuccino usually!

i always come here for starbucks before a shift before work. every time i order mobile, it takes about 15 minutes to get a drink with the barista making everyone in-store drinks before mobile orders. they usually only have 1 barista and 1 cashier during the weekend when this is a very busy starbucks. i can't depend on coming 10 minutes before my shift because it typically takes more than 10 minutes from waiting in line, to ordering, and then getting my drink made. they should definitely have some more people on staff.

i come here all the time. most of the time, the staff is on point! a few times ive had one particular young lady at the drive thru who just doesn't understand coffee, money or customer service. tonight in particular, i ordered a dirty chai with an extra shot and she told me i was ordering a made up drink... ummmm...no. that's a very common drink served at most coffee shops. it's literally on their menu. when she went to hand me the drink, she just shoved her arm in my car and didn't say a word. just hoped i saw her. it's very weird and she needs training... everyone else is great. just not her....

the place is clean, the service was awesome- some of the nicest baristas i've ever had, they were fast, the drinks were perfect and i was pleased. i feel like i'm betraying my normal baristas at the frys location who are awesome-but these ladies need some recognition! great job!
not sure what has happened at this location. the service is extremely slow. my order is always wrong; whether it's the wrong size or the wrong milk..... won't be coming back.
i was very excited for this location to open. they have already exceeded my expectations. they are the friendliest starbucks staff by far and all of my drinks have been spot on. thank you!!

starbucks in the e concourse on mon. mid-afternoon 4/16/18- the best people working, cheerful, made a great shot- in- the- dark, and looked us in the eyes - nice to make a little human connection any day but especially while traveling.

poor customer service. went in and purchase 2 holiday collection cups and a drink total was about $50. dionne short blonde hair girl was extremely rude the moment i walked in. i was amazed the holiday collection that was released a day early. she started saying comments underneath her breath as she thought i didn't hear her and right away said you can't use your point which was no problem. treated all other races in a nice respected matter but because i was hispanic she was extremely rude. she also didn't take my order down correctly. when the person that did my drink told me try it. i said it doesn't taste right he said no problem ill redo it she comes back saying oh i must of not heard you correctly and rolled her eyes. once again poor customer service. dionne treat everyone equally with respect if you are unhappy at your job try looking somewhere else or leave the attitude at home.

the best thing about this starbucks is seeing the line of cars wrapped around the building as you walk inside to no line. the worst part? waiting because everyone working is handling all the requests from the drive-thru. other than that, it's an average starbucks with the usual offerings.

very nice starbucks in heart of doylestown. this starbucks sits in a historic building which adds to the cozy inside atmosphere complete with a fireplace. there is also outside seating overlooking downtown. the service is very good and i have been pleased. parking can be tricky with a small public metered lot behind the building. there is plenty of street parking if you can find a spot.
well, it's on the base, so those that can access it can enjoy the tasty that starbucks offers. the lines are decent and the service is excellent! use your app and get those rewards!
every time i've been to this starbucks inside target there's a line and it's understaffed. every. single. time. not quite sure why. the target starbucks near my house is great. this one is not.

i just picked up my mobile order from this starbucks and i had an excellent experience so i wanted to make sure to leave this rating. i usually drink blonde espresso but, i ordered regular espresso in my drink by accident. the lady at the drive thru told me they were happy to remake it and told her don't worry, because it was my mistake. she was adamant that i get the drink i wanted so with a long line of cars behind me, she had my drink remade quickly. in my eyes they did not have to do that since i ordered incorrectly. but this truly was exceptional customer sevice in my book (and i used to work for nordstrom if that tells you what level of customer service i'm used to getting/giving). again, it was a small thing that really made difference. i will probably make this my new "regular" starbucks just because of this one act. thank you starbucks
service is better during the day. night shift doesn't care about anything but closing early. stood by the counter for 10 minutes while i waited for my drink that was sitting on the counter.

whoever is in charge of scheduling at this starbucks needs to do a much better job. it's a saturday morning of black friday weekend. they should expect a large volume of customers, correct? somehow they only have two people working right now, one of which is clearly new (as in the one girl is actively training the other on how to label cups, what goes in orders, etc.). literally just waited 20 minutes for an americano. how about scheduling three people, especially if one of them is new and clueless?? i am from out of town, and from where i come from if you get this horrible of service at a starbucks they at least apologize and give you one of those free drink vouchers. i get that the girls behind the counter were slammed today, but it doesn't change the fact that i still waited 20 minutes for my coffee, and didn't even get an apology let alone a free coffee voucher. disappointing starbucks, to say the least. looking forward to going back home where starbucks is done right.

when it comes to coffee i try my best to stay local but i often find myself craving starbucks' unbeatable scones. when this happens i drive to this location and indulge. the coffee is good and the pastries are even better. it is always clean and totally friendly! for those that don't feel like parking and walking 10 - 15 steps to get to the door of starbucks, this one has a drive-thru. if you see a line don't drive off, the line moves quickly.

awful customer service. the brown haired woman that is working the closing pm shift (may 26, 2019) has terrible customer service. she did not greet us once when we walked in, merely starred down at some piece of paper she was writing on. due to her lack of attention to customers, i inquired if the cafe was even open. it was 6:20pm and discovered they were open till 7:00pm. when she finally rudely asked what i "wanted," and i responded with my order, she crudely stated, "we aren't making those. i already washed the machine." no kindness, smile, apology or anything. firstly, when you are open for 40 more minutes, it's seems irrational to have already cleaned your equipment. secondly, even if you did already clean it, you could at least deliver the news in a kinder, more customer service centric way. i've traveled all over the world and have never been spoken to like this by a starbucks employee. avoid this store and entirely and go the the other starbucks less than a block away. it is not worth dealing with a store that supports a culture of bad attitudes and impoliteness.

would be 4.5 stars... but i will happily round that up! we went on the day we visited longwood gardens. since we left late morning, we had to find a place to eat for lunch before visiting the gardens. considering i'm a starbucks, we decided to eat here. this location is just a few blocks away from the entrance to the parking lot of the gardens. easily located right off of us-1, this starbucks is part of a strip mall, known as the the shoppes at longwood village. hey, if the shoppers need their caffeine fix for buying clothes or groceries, they certainly have this place for that! if you see the sign for this strip mall, then you'll know that starbucks is in there. this, however, is part of a smaller building, which is separate from the main building where other stores (including "anchors") are attached. because this parking lot is huge, finding a spot was never a problem. we walked into the store. it was small, but not the smallest that we've ever been to. they had some areas for you to sit at, but it's never enough to accommodate a huge crowd who wants to sit down and unwind with coffee and/or food while accessing the free wifi (which was how i checked in here via yelp! lol). at least the wooden chairs were okay and the table was clean. the people behind the registers were nice, and our paninis were toasted up without issue. we also got some iced white mochas too, and they were good, especially on a warm day. as we were eating, a lady from behind the register approached us and offered some samples of two of their seasonal items. one of them was a michigan cherry oat bar, and that tasted really good! another item was a small berry drink. i don't remember the exact name of it, nor didn't i care for it as i thought it was just okay. it's not often i get free samples from starbucks since i was being a patron, and because of this generous act, the full rating is warranted. overall, the store was nice and customer service was flawless. i would say that this is as good as it gets. woo! :d

the employees at this starbucks know their customers really well. the drinks are good and the service was friendly. this is starbucks really small and narrow so don't plan on going there if you want work done because noise echoes a lot.

okay, lets start with the cleanliness of the store. there is trash overflowing the trash receptacles, there is trash on the floor, the tables are dirty, the area where the employees are preparing the drinks and food looks like a bomb went off. now on to service where, i could allow the cleanliness of the store to slide a little if the service had been outstanding. sadly it was not. for some unknown reason this starbucks has decided that the person working the register should also be the person preparing the order. note to management,"this does not work!" just like all the other starbucks i have been to around the world, your register people take the orders and keep your line moving. having people stand in line waiting endlessly for someone to take their order pisses people off more than people having to wait for their orders. let's move on to the special giveaway today - when asked about the reusable cup giveaway dustin (who is a stand out at customer service) asked the harpy who appeared to be the manager about the promotion. her snippy response to him was we're out. now i could see if i cam into the store at 1-2 in the afternoon and asked about getting a promotional item but when you get to the store at 10am the expectation is that they should have had sufficient stock on hand to accommodate at least the folks who are in the store early. shame on you! get it together people, this is no way to run a starbucks. wanna see how it's done? go to justison landing store.
staff super friendly here! good ol' starbucks. no complaints. no seating inside as of today. all staff wearing masks. spacious and clean.

i went in today for a pick me up in the afternoon. i got an i sweet tea no water added so very dark. after the ice melted and i was towards the bottom...i noticed the huge black dead fly. my cup had a lid and i was inside my home. i'll never visit starbucks again.

this is a busy startbucks. but i still love it! the coffee is great, and the staff is sweet. i came in during a not so busy weekday with my daughter ,ordered coffee and sat down to eat our panda express. the barista came over a couple minutes later an offered an extra plate for my daughter (she was eating off a napkin because we were sharing food) and it just made me so happy that she was like looking out for us like that. it was a small random act of kindness that went a very long way. something like that, says a lot about the business.i mean really, i didn't even know they had plates for customers to use! anyways. its not just about the amaazziinngg coffee ;the staff is just as amazing!

best starbucks customer service ! this is the first time i come to this location and the stuff members are awesome ! we went through the drive-through and the girl on the speaker understood my fiancé accent , he usually always struggle , also the lady at the window was very nice and caring! i really liked coming here !

overall i've had very good experiences with this starbucks. my only complaint is that they only have small tables and a window bar with stools. i wish they had some comfy chairs, and bigger tables that barely fit 2 laptops. but i realize that it's a small starbucks and i assume they're doing the best with what they have!
