this starbucks is very busy as it is located in the heart of midtown and on the corner of walnut but it was a great place to grab a cup of coffee and spend the afternoon studying away! i came here with some friends to try to find a few tables to cozy up and study for exams coming up. we found some empty spots by chance and there were outlets for us to plug our devices in! i recommend a visit here as it is down the street from all the major shops and also a place to get some work done. the best of both worlds! the quality of the coffee is just as you would expect at a starbucks and the customer service was speedy and friendly!
it seems every time i go to this starbucks they short me on my drink :( just like this morning, i ordered a venti but when i opened the lid it was the amount of a grande...

ordered a latte with whip cream 2 times from here ... half the cup is whip cream the other liquid. when i addressed it the a-hole barista was very condescending. the managers aren't any better. this is one starbucks location to never visit!!!

this place isnt closed it was just remodeled recently. it use to be my favorite starbucks to go to because the employees were so nice and always got my order right. but since they've remodeled it they've gotten new employees who are rude and rush orders. everytime i come they give me the wrong drink. idk it's just not worth it to go here anymore. i usually just get a simple latte and they always make that drink weird somehow do today i switched it up and got the strawberries and cream frap and they just left the artificial flavor in the bottom without finishing it. it's just gross and cringy. definitely the last time im coming.

this starbucks location on hillsborough avenue in tampa is a well managed starbucks location that is efficient at keeping everything moving and getting your order ready. the staff here is very friendly and accommodating to make sure that everything is prepared properly. good experience every time i have been here.
the best place to read or study . also the people who work there are the best of the best.
terrible wait time to pick up a coffee. need to speed up service. multiple cashiers? or another drive thru. no straws and asked for half syrup but not half.

treated myself to a gingerbread latte with coconut milk. i didn't know starbucks had so many dairy free options: soy, almond, and coconut!! barista chris suggested doing coconut milk in my latte and he wasn't wrong. it was just as delicious as i remembered gingerbread lattes being. he was so sweet and really brightened my day. this was without a doubt the best starbucks i've been to.

i purchased 2 hot peppermint hot chocolates without whip cream on 11/8. when i got to work took the lids off one was barely a half full. the other was missing 1/4 a cup from top. i was so angry and disappointed specially after paying over $7. for both. i've included the photo.
yep it's a starbucks geez. been here million times, who hasn't!?!? lol i mean location is the big thing. everything else is about what you would expect!!!!
exceptional service and great food and coffee as always! a very positive environment created by friendly, and service oriented staff.
one of my favorite starbucks in the city. the staff are always friendly and coffe always fresh n hot.

in the past i've been dissatisfied by the service and quality of product but on this trip i was shocked to see that, regardless of the cavalier attitude of many patrons, the barrisa was not wearing her face mask. i let her know that since she's preparing food she should be the most cautious of anybody and i walked out without placing an order because i didn't feel it was worth the risk.

so i've been coming here for a while. i've had average experiences. i usually come on the weekends and for some reason they can't get their stuff together at the drive thru. today i ordered a tall nonfat mocha frap at the drive thru. i pull up and the guy who took my order notifies me that he forgot to put the cup on the station so they didn't make my drink and that he's sorry and i need to wait. 2 minutes later he is about to hand me a paper cup that clearly isn't a frappuccino. i tell him what i ordered, he pulls the sleeve on the cup down and shows me the sticker that says tall nonfat mocha frapp, and says "this is who i'm dealing with". at this point the manager comes over a minute later and says she very sorry and hands me a card that says $4 off my next drink. if i remember correctly those drink cards used to cover any drink now it's limited to $4. anyway finally after sometime he brings me out a venti and says "they made a mistake twice and they made a venti, so we will just give it to you" thanks to the smug guy and the slow service they get two stars. they would have gotten one but the manager seemed apologetic.

the workers were very rude. they were ignoring me even though i was the only one waiting. they look at me and completely ignored. afterwards i heard one of the workers saying thay she didnt feel like talking to people that day. i was in shock. i will never go back tk thsi starbuck ever again.
love this starbucks, know me by name and know my drink. definitely feel like i am at a "barista". thanks everyone for making me feel so welcome

been here once. got an expensive thing they called a drink that tasted more like a dessert and now don't drink at any starbucks still. everytime i've gone by here the trash is piled high. not even that good of a spot to sit down at either.

this starbucks is awesome ! personally, it's becoming a favorite! i believe this is a newer location with a newish design, they even have cool plants, curtains, shades and a modern interior which is cozy and inviting. the bathroom facilities are newish too. what i personally like of this starbucks vs other starbucks in the area is they play music that's at a good volume, not too high and it's a smaller cafe so it doesn't get overly noisy like some busier cafes which can become a bit overwhelming. i find that the baristas are friendly and welcoming, positive and in a good mood! this is a nice cafe to relax and grab a quick bite. warm, friendly atmosphere. i enjoy it! wish it was closer to me!

good experience. maybe it's me i just don't get the hype of starbucks at all. i had a gift card for starbucks i thought it had 6$ and change- turns out it was eight two cents. my husband ordered the white mocha drink and enjoyed it. i got the sweet cream cold brew. very good. my daughter has educated me on many things starbucks lol. so relieved to see gender neutral bathrooms (insert sarcasm here) apparently this is a big deal in the yelp check in world. the starbucks is comfy and relaxing and we sat in the cozy leather chairs. i don't know . . . i always feel so out of place in these kinds of coffee places. i'm usually super basic- black coffee no cream no sugar- can i be any more boring?! i think back to an episode of curb your enthusiasm when larry david went to a place like this and has no idea about the menu. his order was "just give me a vanilla bullshit drink". that's what i've always got in my mind when i go to these kind of snobby coffee bars. lol.

woke up early thanksgiving morning and headed out to a local coffee shop near our vacation spot only to find it closed. i ended up at this starbucks location and let me tell you, the crew working in there that morning was simply incredible. they went above and beyond. everyone seemed truly happy to be at work and interacted attentively with each customer. my visit made me wish this was my local starbucks!

everyone knows starbucks, and either you like it or you don't, so there's not much sense in reviewing the menu. well, except! i am currently obsessed with their chicken sausage breakfast wrap. this wrap (which the location closer to my job is often out of by the time i get there) the drive through (which aforementioned location is also lacking), and the friendly workers make this starbucks my first choice for breakfast on my way to work or a caffeinated pick-me-up when i'm tooling around town on the weekends.

one of the coolest looking starbucks yet. as always drinks are on point and delicious. we kept it simple apple juice for the little guy and the new ice coconut milk mocha macchiato for my self venti (yes please). the decor is awesone and seating is comfortable. you know me a starbucks fanatic so as always very pleased. kudos starbucks.

i came to the wayne store on a sunday afternoon. i ordered a chili mocha frappuccino, it was wonderful! i came back the following friday and ordered the same thing in a larger size. the first mistake was that they made it hot. so i had her remake my frozen cappuccino, hence the term frappuccino!! she proceeded to dump the contents over ice and throw some whipped cream on top. i paid almost $6 for that fucking drink. i am extremely disappointed. if i wanted cold coffee over ice cubes i could have done that at home myself for a lot less. i know that is not how it is done in that store. big thumbs down

today is supposed to be my day for returning phone calls and emails, and lots of paperwork. but horror of horrors, i've been on the road so much that i completely forgot to check the status of the pantry for green tea before i started boiling the water this morning. fearing a bad case of "the shakes" from early morning tea withdrawal, i grabbed my car keys and headed down the road to starbucks. this particular starbucks sits on the corner of dale mabry and linebaugh avenue within walking distance of my condo. when i first moved to carrollwood the space was occupied by a skanky long john silvers which thankfully closed, was gutted by the developer, and re-opened as a really nice starbucks. the interior looks less like an industrial-strength coffee house, and more like a very comfortable study / reading room with lots of places to sit and relax. i'm not a big fan of coffee, and especially not the jumbo grande mocha frappacino double-shot espresso salted caramel latte crap that most people seem to like. instead i just wanted a basic cup of freshly-brewed green tea -- oh, and one of those yummy-looking blueberry scones as well, please. the barrista seemed to appreciate my needs -- perhaps it was the dark circles under my eyes, and the way i was chewing on my lower lip -- and she quickly rushed to pour me a large green tea and fork over the scone. *sighs happily* the tea was flavorful and calming, and the scone was fresh and delicious. i found a corner table and sat down to read the front section of the tampa bay times while watching the other harried morning commuters lining up for their fresh shot o'joe before starting their work day. this particular starbucks opens at 5 in the morning, and stays open till 11 or so at night. they have a drive-through window which seems popular with the vapid "latte crowd" who are too busy blabbing away on their cellphones to come into the store -- fine by me, like......ya know? well done starbucks, and thanks for saving my day!

i disagree with kara. this is not the worst starbucks in the area and actually has one of the nicest coffee lounge spaces in town. tall lofted ceilings look out to mt rose and the surrounding summit sierra mall. it's actually a very scenic location. the coffee is like every other starbucks, good but not great.

okay this is by far my favorite location. the management is wonderful, the staff amazing, and they even make great drinks!!! i love everything about them and they are always there with a smile. drive-thru takes a little long but i don't mind since i'm just too lazy to walk my butt in there sometimes i have messed up ordering on the app i don't know how many times and they just wave at me and say no worries we got you. if i had any new complaint it would be the price and they can't help that love this place.
very good location with great atmosphere. the staff are really friendly and helpful. i go there quite often. free wifi. good collection of breakfast and coffee. they have mobile payment facility.
poorly organized. i waited too long then they has screwed up my order. they have two water faucets running full blast all the time wasting tons of water.
we love starbucks. this one needs learn their menu. we ordered three drinks, all three were made wrong. this seems to be a repeat problem when we go into this location.

on a business trip and decided to stop by and get a cup of coffee before my meeting. what can you say about a starbucks? same coffee and food. the service is another story. four people ahead of me and it took 15 mins to take two orders. i know they want to up sell the scones and the other baked goods but when a customer say no move on. i looked at my watch and i left the store. the service is terrible.

typical starbucks, but this one have outdoor seating under a roof. also very convenient parking right in front of the store. you must get a coffee from the clover. a ver unique way to enjoy your coffee. another great location is it is next to belle meade premium cigar. get a coffee and a smoke and enjoy life, its short.

this is the starbucks located near gate e16. i have traveled to many a starbucks from coast to coast and internationally. the ladies that are at this location are not only the fastest i've come across, but also the friendliest. all starbucks should be trained by this team. howard schultz take notice!

i like this starbucks location. it's very relaxing and they have a good amount of sitting space both inside and outside. my drinks are usually mixed very well here also. sometimes they're under-staffed and get long lines. today i had to ask for a refund because i was waiting over 15 minutes and the manager wasn't very friendly about it. however, overall i enjoy my experiences here and everyone is very friendly. they also give great recommendations for new coffee drinkers.

customer service was horrible today! left my phone there today. realized what i did and came back for it 20 minutes later. baristas were totally uninterested in helping me when i asked if anyone saw my phone. it wasn't even busy. i called back later to ask again and whoever answered said it could be in the safe if it was there but he didn't have access to it. i asked who did and he said he didn't know and that they were extremely busy. didn't ask me to call back or try to help at all. my phone was stolen at their location and no one really cared to help a customer out. let me clarify one thing again...not all of the baristas have horrible customer service. i'm talking about the ones that were working there on 12/2/2012 from 1:00-3:30.

love the location on mass ave, but the service here is always spotty. they had four people behind the counter so i couldn't figure out why it took over five minutes to give me a brewed coffee. it was already made and just needed pumped from the pot to the cup. the barista had the personality of a wart on a frog. she's wearing a mask but wouldn't speak up so had to keep asking her to repeat. she appeared to be asleep with her eyes open. working with all of that caffeine i think would present an opportunity to stay awake.
this place rocks! great friendly staff who are efficient and fluent in coffee drinks. a+
always clean, good coffee, great service, a d easy to pay on the app. starbucks really gets it. bathrooms are usually clean and nice. good parking and friendly service.

i decided i wanted a vanilla cream cold brew and a little 'chinno for my pup after taking her to the dog park. took them over twenty minutes just to get me to the window (not that many cars ahead of me.) then they told me they were "too low on whipped cream" even though it was noon a tuesday. so now i have a crushed dog staring into the store waiting for something that isn't coming, took them over 30 minutes to get me one damn coffee and they gave me regular cream in it. already had bad experiences with this location now i am never going to return. the girl even had an attitude when she said no to me like it was my fault for having a dog that wanted some whipped cream.
they always tend to "forget" to do online orders! make sure to order hours early but don't be surprised if your order isn't ready when you get there!

this has the be the worst starbucks. i live right here and always mobile order, but lately the app always says literally everything is out but when you go they don't know why because it's not out. i had to start mobile ordering the second i woke in the morning for it to be ready in time because even mobile ordering it's never ready at the time it's supposed to be ready. i used to mobile order about 5 minutes before i'd walk out and it's 10 minutes from my house, say i order at 6:45 it'll say ready for pick up at 6:55 i get there for 7 and it's still not ready and i end up waiting another 15+ minutes sometimes my order gets lost, i've had to cancel my order so many times because of almost being late for work even though i ordered so early and made sure i had enough time. the first few times it's ok but it started happening every time and i wake up so early for work that it's not usually busy when i go. my drinks never consistent- i order the same thing ever time and a few times it was either not full to the top like it usually is or alllllllll half and half no coffee what so ever. the lady even noticed it before i did once & offered to make me a new one bc she knows my order, i never see her anymore i now go to the one on vets and it's been such a change- the same drink that i've ordered for a year consistently tastes and looks sooooooo much different at the vets store, it's perfect every time. it's always there when i get there i walk in and walk straight out even if i ordered as i'm leaving my house. i've never had any issues with the app saying items were out when they weren't. even when they were extremely busy once my drink was still ready when i got there- i don't know who manages the chateau starbucks but it needs new management bad.

definitely not my favorite starbucks, but the location in conshy, right next to the giant, is fairly convenient. don't expect to lounge around here - it's tight, and there's not much room for studying or doing work. in the afternoon, tables may be empty but aren't always clean. still, service is generally friendly and fast.

this location usually very busy. seating is decent. the problem is for some reason the customers here all think they live in beverly hills and shouldn't have to wait or suffer the fools behind the counters. the employees here have always treated me well.

first time at this starbucks in north valleys. the staff was very nice and service was prompt. their drive thru seems to be very busy. i was there mid morning. the dining area is quite small compared other starbucks i have been too. chairs were comfortable, but tables were pretty small. there are two larger tables for those with laptops. you can function on the smaller tables, but are confined a bit against the wall. the dining area was well kept and there are outside seating options as well. the music was a little loud for my liking. any conversation you would have would have to carry over the music, thus increasing the overall chatter level in the dining area a little louder then normal. the men's bathroom was well kept and clean and all personal hygiene supplies were stocked for use. overall, i enjoyed my experience at this location. if they toned down the music a little bit, then it would definitely be a 4.5 to 5 star rating. cheers!

i normally hate reviewing chains. but people should be warned about this location. i'll start off by saying that there are some nice and competent employees that work here. unfortunately there are some ever changing employees that seem to drop the ball. this happens quite frequently at peak times in the morning around 8-10am and in the afternoon around 2-4pm. i've gotten used to taking a paper and standing around while i wait for my drink, only to have it not be made. to be fair, they have always apologized. in some cases i've even gotten free drink tickets. but this has happened more than one or two times. the most ironic was when i used my free drink ticket from a screwed up/not ordered drink experience the day before and then proceeded to get the wrong drink the next day. i get this place gets ridiculously busy, and that some people that go here are really impatient. but it seems like there are communications and morale problems here. perhaps a change in managers and some team building might be in order. i'll stick to going to the jazz and java at the nursing school. they never screw up my order and it gets processed faster.

love the drive through. its great right before going to centennial park. i usually get what i ordered after i drive away. some other ones are unreliable but this one seems to consistently get our order right.

good product, poor customer service. the wife and i came to this particular location on st. patrick's day around 1:15pm to order our usual; while we're sitting in line we hear the window attendant taking the lady in front of us' order in what seemed to be a cheery mood. as we pull up to the speaker their tone is completely different, snappy, and pompous as they (oh and their name is stephen) tell me i can't order a frap because "they're brand name and call them frappuccinos" which normally would t bother me because it's a common occurrence when i try to order via shorthand with the starbucks franchise but stephen in particular was just seemed to be in a bad mood all of a sudden; seeming like being petty toward his customers made him feel better. then as we pull up to the window to pay he rips my debit card out of my hand then proceeds to hand me our order and say "here's your stuff" and closes the window. i've never had an issue with this location before but i hope proper actions are taken to make sure stephen doesn't feel the need to treat his paying customers like a nuisance anymore.

y'all need to step y'all's coffee game up cuz y'all coffee wack asf i literally went up 3 times because it wasn't what i wanted and somehow y'all still managed to fuck up smh i won't be coming back to this one that's for sure

best outdoor seating at a starbucks ever--outdoors, but with a roof and outlets (and a fireplace! that wasn't on, but was still cool). i was on vacation and would pay to have a starbucks like this closer to home.
great place, very friendly staff our drinks always come out fast. drinks always taste so good , ordering on mobile makes the drinks come out faster.

i came here randomly and actually tried the online purchasing option. it worked very well and walked right in and was handed my drink. the place has a decent amount of seating options. the employees here very nice and overall place is clean. i'm not too sure about the bathrooms though. the drink was very good and the parking here is great unlike over crowded philadelphia. i felt comfortable hanging out here and enjoyed relaxing and consuming my beverage. i will for sure stop by again when i'm in the area.

great service as the staff are friendly and will go out of their way to satisfy the customer. i enjoy coming here for my favourite cup of coffee while i catch up with friends. the snacks are always fresh and in stock. i recommend that you try the flat white. it's a like a latte only much creamy. it costs a little more then the other drinks but i think it's worth it. the only downside to this location is that they don't have a drive through. however they are in a convenient location right on baseline rd. i have not tried the patio but then again i haven't been in sherwood park that long. maybe when the weather warms up :-)

if i'm going to go to a starbucks (primarily for the lemon pound cake), this is one of my favorite locations in tucson. convenient parking, generous seating areas inside and out, and friendly staff. the lines can get kind of long, but generally my drink (and lemon pound cake) are delivered quickly enough.
super friendly service with a smile. i cannot remember the gentlemen's name who took my order, but he was super nice and made my day.
a very crowded starbucks! the staff was extremely nice, but unfortunately did not have my go to chai tea :(

look, i typically steer clear of starbucks when there are local options available, but i spent about a month going to this one when my coffee grinder broke...and i like it-- a lot! the employees here are much more down to earth compared to the starbucks by my house. they remembered me shortly after i started frequenting this location and their conversation is pretty genuine. i also felt like they burn their coffee less at this location...or is that my imagination? but the *number one reason* i feel compelled to write a 5 star review for this location at speedway and country club is the fact that they recycle! as explained to me by one of the baristas, recycling is not a corporate mandate at starbucks, rather it is a choice made by each individual franchise. with all the plastic milk jugs and packaging that they receive supplies in, starbucks generates an insane amount of recyclable plastic waste that quite often goes into landfills. not here! if you are going to buy a triple-double soy mocha chocha blended whatsit (or just a good ol' 'cuppa), you should support this particular starbucks, one of the few in town that recycles.

i fully admit i came here after the may 29 company-wide shut down to talk about new policies that said anyone can come in and hang out without having to purchase anything in the store. to be honest, i was waiting for a friend who was running late for dinner down the street, and i had time to kill. so i tested the new policy. i sat down, used their free wifi, spent about 30 minutes, and left. no hassle by anyone. let's be honest, i spend enough money at starbucks at a monthly basis that i don't feel much guilt about using their free wifi for a few minutes.

now that i'm working 2 blocks from this location, i'm frequenting it more. the location is pretty cool right on the corner of st. charles and canal. it's probably the largest sbux i've seen in la so far. i love the vintage style of it and the lights hanging in the main room are cool. the trumpet artwork display hanging from the ceiling in the side room is cool too. they sell the usual sbux fare and seem to keep everything pretty well stocked for being a busy location. i've been here about 4 times so far and the staff have all been very friendly. they all have asked if i want to add an extra shot to my latte. i guess they really push the up-selling here. no prob. they work the line fast and i haven't had to wait long at all for my drinks. as others have noted, it's a great place to people watch. i generally don't stay here too long as i have to get back to work. there are plenty of tables and large windows to sit near and enjoy. there is also the separate side room that is kind of like a library study area with the long tables. the back wall has plenty of comfy seating, too. i'm sure i'll be back soon.
loving the feel of this starbucks!!!! always clean and a great "group" table if you want to come with friends or study groups....

i went on a sunday afternoon. poor customer service for a neighbourhood coffee place that can attract regular customers. they didn't call out my order. it was left sitting on the not visible area of the pick-up counter for at least 10 minutes. the other coffee i ordered....they forgot to make it. the baristas didn't even apologize. the vibe of a establishment is dependent on the vibe of the employees. at this starbucks: no customer greeting, no music playing, and unkept creamer counter. it's a sad starbucks.

i'm a huge fan of this starbucks. it's the closest one to where i live, and the workers there are always friendly. i always feel like i'm talking to an old friend, even though i typically only go a few times a year (i can't stay away from the pumpkin spice frap...yum!) i've been through the drive thru a few times, and they have doggy treats in the window for your furry friends. how sweet! :)
super standard starbucks but good staff and clean area. clayton is super nice to walk around too!

i regret wasting my money on this. i never get ventis but decided to treat myself today mistake! this probably has no coffee in it! it is basically white and so sweet like it's only milk and syrup!!! where's my coffee at? oh they probably forgot it! waste of my money and now i don't even want to drink it because of how bad it tastes
this place gets the order wrong every time. tea tastes watered down. i'll travel the extra distance and go to neshaminy or oxrord oaks.

the worst starbucks experience we ever had. three people working; no customers before or after us. had to ask them to wait on us. when the person handing the drink to me at the bar announced my drink, it was not announced as what i ordered. when i restated what i ordered and asked if this was what was in my drink, he said, "that's what you ordered isn't it?" i said, "that is not what you said it was." he just smirked. never to return to this location. i will stay with the baywalk location. they know how to treat people

this is by far one of the worst starbucks in reno. the baristas are slow af. placed a mobile order hoping i wouldn't have to wait as long when i arrived. boy was i wrong. got there and still waited 20 minutes. after 10 minutes i checked on my order with one of the baristas and it had "just come out" proceeded to wait another 10 minuets and my drink was made wrong. paid almost $6 for terrible customer service and a messed up drink. to think i've been a gold member since 2012. maybe it's time to throw in the towel and stop giving starbucks my business all together. get it together please!!

we were staying at a nearby hotel, mobile ordered, and picked up our food at the hand off plane! our order was not quite ready for us but almost! there was not much of a connection at the handoff but our food and beverages were great! we will return on our trip!
avoid this location rude employees. terrible customer service. got my order wrong. super slow.

morning crew on saturday needs to up their game. baristas didn't get tops solidly on the cups. this didn't start the morning off well. they had difficulty comprehending simple directions. in spite of having to clean up spilled coffee, i gave the place three stars because during the week this place usually gives good service.
love starbucks, love their coffee. but crystal was extremely rude at the cash register today. i hope i never come here again if she's working. definitely needs to be trained on customer service.

this location has the absolute worst customer service in which i have encountered at any starbucks! one early morning last week on my way to work, i decided to stop in going inside rather than drive through. there was 1 person in front of me in line to be helped and no one else inside. with 6 baristas assisting no one, i found it odd that after drive through cleared out and that they had helped the person in front of me that i was still waiting for my 2 item order, 1 of which being a croissant, almost 10 minutes later. upon asking if my order would be ready soon, the young girl replied, "i don't know, probably." i shook my head in amazement and waited another 5 min. as i received my order and proceeded to walk out, i turned to look behind me and found the rude young blond girl along with the cashier giving me dirty looks from hell! grow up little girls your job is only available because of your customers!

i stopped at the drive through for a cappuccino. service was very polite and very friendly but a bit slow. the cappuccino was the hottest i have gotten in a long, long time. definitely worth the slight detour i took to get there.

since i work in arnold, i visit this starbucks nearly 4 times a week. unfortunately, this is by far the most crowded and backed up starbucks in the st. louis area. the employees usually do the best they can, but the shear volume of drive thru, online, and in store orders is very overwhelming due to there not being another starbucks in the area. regardless of the day and time, it takes no less than 15 minutes to wait for my order and sometimes approaches a half hour. starbucks needs to either increase the number of employees on the clock here, or better yet, build another drive thru within a 5 mile radius!

the staff at this starbucks location was very disorganized and inefficient today with all customer orders while i was there. they seemed adequately staffed (5) yet did not seem to know what they were doing. they didn't even have the added distraction of drive-thru services. whoever the manager is needs to address this.

my star rating is low because this is the smallest and busiest starbucks i've ever been to. at any time you would expect to find a working person get coffee you can expect the line is out the parking lot, across the street, and backed up to the light. i can't imagine how much business they are losing simply because of that. if they opened up another location in the area that would greatly help. i'm also not sure if they changed their to go cups but mine are always leaking! i have coffee stains all over my car :( fix the location problem, fix your cups and i'll be a happy camper

i really like the interior, but i don't like how online it says open till 11 but then i get there to work on a paper and they tell me at 8:56pm they close in 4 minutes. they didn't have their hours posted anywhere in the store. i also don't like how they start putting their food away at 7:30pm.

mornings are always insane at this location. i work in the area so this store is my go-to. this particular morning was chaos. i placed a mobile order thru the app and it said the 1 drink i ordered would be ready in 5-8mins. incorrect. it should have been updated to reflect the circus show going on in the store and said your drink will be ready in approx 50mins-never!!!!! that's how long i had to wait until i realized i was never going to get my drink. everyone was looking at each other and stating how crazy this was! others had been waiting just as long and the frustration in everyone's faces was brewing. the staff was calm and really tried to maintain some sense of order. there was a girl going thru the older mobile orders stacked up on what appeared to be a book shelf. she tried her best to pull orders for ppl waiting but at some point i wised up and asked for a credit because i couldn't wait any longer. they issued a refund on my mobile app and a credit towards my next order. the 2 stars goes to the recovery effort.
this starbucks is the slowest i've ever been to, six people behind the "bar" and almost twenty minutes for tea go toa different starbucks you'll save yourself headache

this location is no different menu-wise than any other starbucks. i visit here frequently on my way to work, this location is rather large in comparison to others and offers nice outdoor seating as well as the indoor cafe. they do not have a drive - thru and mornings can get busy and parking a bit tight, thankfully there is ample parking in the adjacent wal mart parking area as well as the strip mall. you may encounter a long line any given morning, however the staff here are incredibly friendly and move fast. i always enjoy coming in and being greeted warmly.

this starbucks forces you to buy something in order to use their bathroom. that would be okay if they were a small business, but since they are a large corporation i find this policy aggravating. even in new york city the staff will give you a code irregardless. i won't be buying starbucks ever again--hopefully. i think the bathroom code was 5832 of you need it.

happy to report a five star rating! i have been highly critical of this location for a very long time. so must be different management huge improvement. the drinks where perfect the chai tea latte with foam was extra and fluffy perfect taste. my macchiato was absolutely perfect and delicious. my bagel perfectly toasted and not only that my order was ready in five minutes. no confusion no waiting boom it was done. hopefully they keep up the good work. and they eliminated the fly problem!
this place is a clustefuck. i would blame poor management making the baristas look like morons, but i honestly don't know what the root cause is.
i come here nearly every morning before i go to work. staff is always friendly, my drinks always delicious and they are made in a timely manner. i love you starbucks

some of the previous reviews on here or just plain silly no one at starbucks ever asks for a tip. yes it was a little on the slow side, but it's a small stand in a large mall food court . its pretty much like every other starbucks very busy and very efficient.

so , i order a peppermint mocha iced coffee, not a frappuccino . i have ordered this drink many times over the holiday seasons . in brandon , tampa , orlando and jacksonville locations . never had a problem. when they gave me the drink it was light in color heavy cream vanilla with clear liquid on the bottom. clearly not a peppermint mocha but i take a sip to see what it was, and it was just pure mint . i definitely didn't taste chocolate and i definitely didn't taste any coffee. so i went up to the cashier and i said "hey this is not my drink i ordered a peppermint mocha." she then told me that was my drink and i pointed to the picture to point out that it was not a peppermint mocha . she said it was . unfortunately i had to go to work so i couldn't sit there and argue with her. i threw my drink out on the way out, it was a un-drinkable. one of the most disgusting drinks i've ever had. so i basically just gave starbucks free money. please do not go to the starbucks unless you just want to give away your money.

great staff!!! sean, lindsey, zeb, stacy, sara, aaron, molly, beth & alicia, daniel, greg, & of course the ever refreshing, entertaining, fabulous, friendly, bubbly, cheerful personality rodney great place to hang out or stop by, while shopping on mass. ave bring back ashtrays!!! while sitting outside tonight with our fishmonger, mary & her daughter b , daniel did an amazing job @ cleaning outside & feng shui ing the tables, while lindsey & greg worked equally as hard inside

this place was packed in the early morning, everyone getting their coffee and danish fix. i actually came by twice, once in the morning, once in the afternoon before our drive back home. definitley a slower starbucks compared to others, but they make my skinny vanilla latte perfectly! only con is you can't reload your starbucks card app or use a free bday drink at this location.

being a chain coffee shop, i know starbucks is pretty much the same everywhere you go; however this location in particular is always so speedy fast when it comes to my last minute morning coffee runs! staff are always friendly - with smiles on their faces - pretty much all throughout the day...helpful when in need of a little pick-me-up.... morning, afternoon, or in the evenings! parking can feel a bit like you're searching through a hunger games lot at times, but usually worth it! #nocoffeenoworkie
beware of the half filled cups!!! beware of the male barista with scraggly long hair!!

classic starbucks experience, ordered my typical iced vanilla latte, and drink was on point. took them less than four minutes to have my order ready. the store is a bit small but there were plenty of two person tables and the staff made sure the keep the place clean while i was there. on a random note, i noticed that almost everyone around me was simply drinking water. not sure if this is a trend but i can see how staff may be a bit displeased when people come in, order water, and stick around for their free wifi.
the slowest drive thru ever 15 minutes in line not acceptable maybe more staff. need to speed it up

its like there are two different stores here. the morning crew is friendly and efficient, although swamped with business. but the order is always right and they fly around like you expect. go in the afternoon, the little "manager" walks around doing nothing. the staff moves like snails, and its 50/50 whether your coffee comes as ordered (25 minutes later). my last visit there were just 3 cars in line at the drive-thru. great! a chance for a quick stop mid-afternoon. 14 minutes later i ducked out of the drive-thru and walked in. ummm. no line here at all. 2 people waiting for their orders. must have have been the driver trying to buy a franchise at the drive-thru. a breeze now right? i left 25 minutes later with a chalky, poorly made specialty coffee that the obviously new guy asked for help with on every step. the manager ignored his pleas, leaving the other "kids" to give him partial instructions and frustrate him nearly to tears. all while the the three customers inside watched the chaos behind the counter. poor management to say the least. kudos to the morning crew. shame on whatever you call that circus that goes on in the afternoon.
this location has consistently performed below expectations. they not only are the slowest, but often do not produce the drink ordered.
good service, friendly staff and i got my drink order quickly. i recommend trying out the cinnamon raisin bagel. it's vegan!

great location for coffee among all the restaurant bars in st pete. nitro cold brew is the best! seating inside and outside. a little roomier inside than some. tables could be cleaner. wasn't very busy.

i don't see why this starbucks has only a couple stars. service was excellent, my order was perfect and the place is clean. i don't live here but it's a normal small store. bonus is it's in an albertsons large shopping center.
always unbelievably slow after remodel. drive-thru wait is routinely 30 minutes. i've started going out of my way to go to other locations. hope they can pick it up again for their customers.

awful customer service. i always get a large strawberry açaí refresher, no ice, no water no strawberries. just the base. and a large cup of ice. i get this at every other starbucks and never have a problem. but these ladies were mad at my order, very rude about it and gave me a hard time. they made me pay extra and then complained that i didn't have to pay even more than what it adds on the system. there's so many starbucks that are better than this one. disappointed i decided to stop at this one though on my way to work.

this is one of my favorite starbucks locations to get coffee from - the employees are always patient as i'm sometimes indecisive in what to get and ordering ahead with the starbucks app makes things super easy; they always have the order ready within 5-10 minutes tops! usually i only come to starbucks to complete the star dashes and earn points...and check out the seasonal merchandise! they've done a great job spacing out the tables at this starbucks location and encouraging the use of masks. pumpkin spice lattes, here i come! :) thank you, 17th street starbucks!

1st time here and will not return. simple order "fell thru the cracks". waited 20 minutes for two coffees. expensive time wasting experience. also got no receipt for my order or i would have asked for a refund.
long wait usually but the staff is super friendly and helpful everytime. worth the wait!

overrated as usual, starbucks. service the same as all over the nation. nothing special. new covid19 mobile ordering on door for pick-up is not on monument side, but other door. dumb walk up pick-up system. ordered ahead on mobile app and still had to wait. wtf?!

starbucks drive thru in reno! my review is regarding drive through service only. the line moves quickly, always offering to warm your danish/muffin & perfect drinks on each visit. although, this 5 star review is for the drive thru window employee. she always greets you with a smile & good morning, how's your day? making everyone's day a bit brighter- thank you!
coffee and staff are nice. but the lines here are abominable. plan a minimum 10 mins during the week in the morning.

this is my least favorite starbucks. the customer service is awful. the drinks are hit or miss too. i wish it wasn't such a convenient location. i come in hoping for improvement, but am always disappointed. must remember to go to one of the many others, or a different coffee business altogether.

starbucks is always good. i love the oatmeal and passion tango ice tea. my boyfriend had a coffee frap he liked very much! the only reason i'm giving 3 stars to this starbucks is because i looked all over for a recycling bin to recycle my cups etc and it was no where to be found. there was a whole story on the wall about how starbucks is trying to be green etc. but there was no recycling bin?! they definitely need a recycling bin and a compost bin! then they will get five stars!!

i used to visit this starbucks nearly everyday for years! i conducted a majority of my studies while attending an online school there as the environment was easy to focus in. i established connections with some baristas which supported me as i grew and i am appreciative for having interacted with them. this was my favorite starbucks even before the renovation, however after i had more the reason to go. the added space was a fantastic addition for such a bustling store! furthermore... of course the coffee was great.

today, i witnessed horrible behavior from the manager. starbucks corporate needs to investigate and fire the manager at this location for the absolute horrendous behavior and attitude she has for her employees and customers. your employees are not slaves. treat them with dignity.
awful and unfriendly service. long line. disgusting condiment are. better give yourself an extra 20+ mins for a cup of coffee. nearest to gate 71. yikes

it's great location and popularity is apparent from the constant line of customers, extending outside the doors. although this causes a small wait, i am impressed by how quickly the baristas keep the line moving. i ordered my usual coffee frap light. one star goes to the barista for asking me if my name was with or without an "h". i truly appreciate people asking about an "h" at the end of my name instead of spelling it incorrectly as sara. just a thing for me :) my order came up quickly and i set off for home. i took my first sip of the cool, refreshing drink and.......i tasted caramel. instead of coffee frap light, i was given caramel frap light. this has happened to me several times before, which leads me to conclude that they need to adjust their system to stop confusing the two. despite this setback, the drink was still prepared nicely. the perfect consistency and combination of ice and the flavored syrup. too often have i received a frap that contained chunks of ice and with barely any syrup. one star for their customer service. i generally do not like to complain, but since this mix up seems to happen often at starbucks, i thought it may be helpful to give them a call. they were more than accommodating. they asked for my name, and informed me that they would replace my drink, free of charge, whenever i came in next. thanks for such great service starbucks.
slowest drive through i have ever been to. avoid this place. nobody took my order on the speaker, the wait was over 20 minutes for 2 coffees. and... why is the cafe closed? drive through only?

this is by far the worst starbucks i have ever visited. the staff has no sense of urgency. i have placed mobile orders and arrived to the store 10 minutes after ordering only to have to wait another 10 to 15 minutes because they didn't get my order started until i got there. most recently i placed a mobile order for a food item and found out upon arrival at the store that they were sold out. this happened twice this week! they don't update their mobile site with sold out items. this is the closest starbucks to my home but i will never go back to that store. i will go out of my to another starbucks.

by far the best starbucks in the area. great staff, clean store, consistently accurate with orders. truly a team willing to go the extra mile for their customers. my mom means the world to me and they treat her like royalty, so when someone asks which starbucks to go to (this or the other on germantown pike across from shoprite) my answer is always this one.

this starbucks is by far my favorite. i can only write a review about their drive thru.... always speedy, they never make mistakes, and the location is ideal if you're on your way to the mall or work. it's my hidden coffee drive thru!!!!! also some of the most talented baristas around. they only starbucks location i can say "make me something amazing" and they do!!! thank you for helping me make it through so many 12 hr shifts!!!

this starbucks is great, the people here are very friendly and the location is a nice place to just sit back and watch the traffic. my friends and i love coming here for our bike meet ups and catch up while watching nice cars drive by.
this is the best starbucks in indy. the staff is great and know how to make drinks. talk about getting right in customer service....always top notch and fun.
rude baristas used to be a fun place to go to a couple years ago now i don't even look forward to going there anymore

the people at this location were really nice and friendly, and one of them went the extra mile when i requested my coffee be prepared a little differently than usual. i tried one of their bistro boxes, the protein one, and it was very good. i just wished that coffee didn't cost more than a gallon of gas! i figure if starbucks continues to grow, they could start discounting their foods! rd

this place has been around for quite sometime now... i've been a regular customer for about 5 years now. never had a bad cup a joe here!!! and yes, it's a place where the baristas know your name : ) sure as hell a good time!
this particular location becomes a cluster very quickly. not only is the drive thru /parking situation weird. but they consistently are running out of cold brew.

worst starbucks ever! went twice in a week on vacation to nola. they were out of green tea on one occasion, another occasion out of vanilla creme fraps. i asked how do you run out of vanilla fraps, to which the cashier replied it happens all the time l, believe you and me! so clearly poor management here. then the cashier rang up a grande drink even though i corrected her three times it was a venti that i had wanted. when the drink was made up as a grande and i corrected the barista, the barista forced the cashier to chug the drink. to which she did making other customers wait before placing their orders. lastly, to make starbucks at this location worse, the bathrooms were out of order the four days we were there. starbucks should close this location till they find management that wants to provide a pleasant experience.

my name is tara and i am a starbucks addict... its my one corporate crutch... also i am a nyer (brooklynite actually) so i am used to a get in - order - get the h out kind of pace at starbucks. this was a pleasant change of pace. the girls were so sweet and made my drink perfect (unlike some ny store that do not comprehend "very little ice"). they even held onto my umbrella for me when i left it behind.

frequent here. there is usually a line in the drive thru and subsequent wait. today was a it long though i was the only car in line. customer service was poor today as well. when i did get my non specialized cold beverage the cashier did not look at me and leaned it our the window half tilted. this rubbed me the wrong way. service is normally better. perhaps he was having a bad day. inside the store it is clean and cozy. the bathrooms are also very clean.

excellent service! i love when i don't have to wait more than 10 min. . it's an enjoyable experience when the staff is working hard and engaging with the customer. . this starbucks is consistent and i go out of me way to get to this location.
it is hard to screw up sbux, as i call it. it's close by, and the drive thru is open late which i am happy about. so far so good. let's hope it stays that way!

i witnessed one of the baristas wiping down a toilet(not using gloves) with a wash rag, she lifted the seat and really got it clean. then, check this out, proceeded to wipe down all the mugs and cups for sale with said pee rag. that's foul.
the service was great met my friend there for coffee. it has be come my favorite meeting place. my coffee was very good. smooth and full of flavor.

nothing wrong at all with this place when i visited it! my daughters and i stopped in for a little treat after spending the day at museums. service was very quick. staff was friendly. i was worried that we would have to go into the constitution center to get to the starbucks (like... i thought it was just part of their gift store or something) but it's completely separate from the rest of the building.
sarah at this starbucks was the nicest barista i've ever come across! keep it up, sarah!
the line here is way too long and the way the line is lined up is not efficient at all. just asking for grumpy customers..

if you are looking for the perfect combination of coffee drinks and people watching then this is the place for you. with the perfect location in new hope there is never a dull moment with the hustle and bustle of this wonderfully populated town. the location is perfect for people watching but the parking can be tricky. on busy days such as the weekends all meters are taken and it may take a few trips around to find a spot. the starbuck's is a hidden gem among all the other glammed and obvious shops throughout new hope. the simplicity of the brick is important. with the pleasant porch that has chairs and tables, sets the mood even before a customer sets foot in the shop. the interior of this starbucks is very neat and tidy with a relaxing feel. if staying a long time do not fear, the bathrooms are very clean but only have one bathroom for each sex which can become inconvenient if a line forms. as it is such a prime location, many customers are only to be expected. at certain times such as weekends there are lines that are not the most convenient when waiting for your delicious beverage. the beverages and snacks to go with are very good. do not be afraid if you have a special request, the workers are very friendly and appreciate good clientele as much as you appreciate good workers. walking in the large doors you will be engulfed in the delicious smell of different coffees. after getting your drink and scone you can choose to go to the inside seating where there are both seats with tables as well as comfy arm chairs to sit back and relax while sipping your drink. on a nice day i recommend sitting outside and enjoying the town from one of the tables on the outside porch. this starbucks is best for the atmosphere the town itself brings. i assume everyone knows the basics of starbucks from the different options of drinks to tall, grande and venti size. since starbucks is a chain coffee store i would say the one in new hope is best for its surroundings and the feel of the place.

it's a starbux so its the usual - good quality coffee, consistent. i'm here in the evening & it's not crowded, but steadily busy-no waiting. had a cranberry bliss bar that didn't seem to be quite as good i remembered, not sure if it's my memory or it just wasn't as good. but hey i like starbux, & this one is as good as others- but no special "hi, how are ya?" 's

i ordered the green tea latte but with a cappuccino ratio of milk. unfortunately, the cashier wrote, "latte, half foam" so i got a very heavy drink. i explained the miscommunication to the barista, she knew exactly what i wanted and remade it perfectly. 5 stars for the young lady!

other than traffic on overland making getting out of the parking lot being a bit dicey at times, i adore this starbucks location. it is my reward spot after surviving shopping at the hell that is walmart, which is across the street. they are quick to take your order and on par with wait times at other starbucks locations in boise. i've had some great short chats with the folks working the drive up window.

this starbucks is my 2nd least favorite (the least is on hillsborough at i-275) because it's smaller than most and it's located in a small plaza at a very busy intersection. most times i'm driving west on east bay so i'd have to do a u-turn at the lights, then squeeze into one of the tight parking spaces, or drive through the small and narrow parking area to get to the back for the drive thru. inside is smaller than most, too. there are a few tables and seats, but i don't recall a community table. i believe there's just 1 restroom for male and female and the door is right across from the registers. i generally don't like to sit near the restrooms and would like to see it out of sight. there's a small patio area in front that i rarely sit in because 1) it's next to the drive thru so there's always cars driving by, 2) the busy intersection of east bay and starkey isn't much to look at and 3) most times it's too hot and humid in florida! most times the baristas were just okay, not super friendly or talkative. one time i asked for no ice with my iced drink but i was given ice, but most times my drinks were made perfectly. i really only stop by this starbucks because it's convenient; definitely not one where i'd want to hang out, simply because the parking is just too small. sorry.

this starbucks is a perfect example of why i dont frequent starbucks, but i had a gift card to use. only one person working - making both the drinks and taking orders. obviously this is a set up for a shit show!! he was unable to get anyone's drink right and did not listen to the orders. i asked for regular milk, but instead get skim. it was bogo from 2-5, and he had no idea. person next to me ordered an iced latte, and he makes an iced coffee. very annoying and frustrating. will definitely avoid at all costs.

this is a great starbucks. actually serves some great food with great java. the atmosphere is very professional at this location and it is super clean. gotta say this location is my favorite. they make seattle proud.

i went in to meet a friend, the hot chocolate was good but it took at least 10 minutes to make! my friend who lives in belleville (i am from and live in chicago) said that this location is always slow. if you want coffee but have some place to be afterwards this s not the place for you!
i love the service i get from this starbucks i like the management ,& workers are excellent especially the morning staff could not be happier

service sucks at this location. not only are they systemically rude (which is a leadership issue) but they are not proactive. seeming high turn over..today once.again asked for something easy and simple and again it was not done, then the young man handed me the cup.to do it myself. shame on me for going back.

awesome. i locked myself out of the house and car this morning (with the car running), and promptly locked myself out. there was no spare key in sight, and of course i forgot my wallet inside. i went down to my local coffee shop (gracious goods cafe) who would not spot me a small coffee (although they allowed me to sit and warm up), despite having $1.56 of the $2.35 cost. after tracking down my roomie, i took the train downtown where she gave me the key and a couple bucks to get a coffee, which i was now desparate for. after asking how my day was and telling the barista about my crazy day so far, they gave me a latte on the house. made what was already a frustrating day a little sunnier. thanks dolls!

mostly management's fault vs. the probably not-over-paid sunday employees. first, this is one of the smallest, most unpleasant **spaces** of any starbucks i know of (a gold card, frequent-user). layout is awful. and noise level. etc. i've been to this sb maybe 20 times over 2 years. it's almost always crowded / noisy / dumpy feeling, never feels cleaned up. the too-small space feels like it perhaps never can be. on the last day i went (sunday)--and literally the last day i'll go.......disclaimer: we all have "bad days"--and it's not fault of this person but that of management: ...lady at cash register seemed to have a cold; to her credit she sneezed away from the food but management should have a system to quickly ameliorate this. young man bagel toaster...or whoever ? was supposed to be doing this ?... took far too long for simple coffee + bagel ... he seemed...distracted. but everyone usually gives that impression at this sb. 'took so much time that--believe it or not--something 'can't remember ever doing--i cancelled the bagel order...then...just gave up, walked away, leaving my $1.50 when the process to get my gold card out again ... for $1.50--was just ridiculously not worth it, mannnnnnn. 'dropped my cell phone, yeah, during this but ..... just the whole vibe .... as usual ......... not good. management needs to kick this place into gear. there were 5 or 6 ??!! people, working behind counter, noisily but not to customers' advantage.....with full line of waiting customers. no one took the incentive to ... tend to customers--as usual here. 1. they did not warn that they'd have to "americana-" the coffee (i.e., didn't have what was ordered already brewed / takes long time / cash register lady was too disinterested / distracted to hear me cancel and change to what was ready. 2. they didn't, and should always: ask customers name, write on bag to get food order separated from the many other orders...due to (the usual) long line waiting for the distracted, slow service. note: don't blame just employees. management: 1., clean this place up, clean the windows, rearrange those few chairs / some plants maybe? this place feels like there's no one in charge--in sharp contrast to, say, the sb over near costco...or 95% of others. 3. always tell customers if an ordered coffee is not ready right away--like 95% of other sbs. assume: all customers want coffee, at least, as fast as ... at the 95% of other starbucks, even if food is ordered also. 4. and, last: good bye. there's a panera bread nearby in golita--'will bail out of this sb from now on. ps (...yeah...i do type fast...it is ridiculous writing all this, otherwise...for that matter, yes, i did come in in a bad mood--because?--i expected a poor experience--and got even a worse one than expected. however, as a business traveler with decades' experience, possibly this critique might lead to improvements...to a win-win for this sb & its employees. working at a well-managed place is a win-win for these, presumably local, college students for whom we customers have sympathy. repeat: it's systemic / managerial at this location--do not blame just the employees working that sunday.

this little gem is often overlooked as it sits inside of sheraton city center. over the years i've seen two makeovers, and i believe this last one may provide optimal space. the hotel itself has added a seating area outside of the coffee shop, just in the hall area. the shop had removed several couches and put tables where they use to be... this is helpful to the business travelers that mostly frequent the hotel. the starbucks isn't as artsy or involved as a free standing starbucks. the staff isn't as hipster or as talkative as some of the other shops. they are mostly younger, high school or college students, who are friendly and upbeat. the service could be a little better, some of the lines get too long and at times the baristas are unsure of certain drinks. especially ones that aren't on the menu... i don't mind the wait, considering it's not one of the busiest starbucks. go here if you have time to spare, and are in the mood to lounge around in the lobby.

just had the worst experience in a starbucks ever! we've been gold card holders with starbucks since 2010. additionally we shop starbucks daily. multiple times daily. this location for a new location is one you should skip. there is a dd down the road. i entered the cafe this evening intending to order our usuals in addition to some after dinner treats. i approached the counter and was met by a very sweet young lady with red hair (i think). we began conversing on everything from the weather to pumpkin spice goodness. i asked her when that becomes available etc. immediately from behind her a woman ( who later i find out is the manager on duty tasha) speaks up and rudely addressed me. spoke over the employee as well as me. she then walked away. i was dumbfounded. who treated her so badly that it was necessary to behave like this to a customer, disregard an employee and show so much disrespect. i was so floored i just walked out. i couldn't imagine spending a $1 in there. i will make it my duty to make sure no one i know even considers entering that store. this is either someone who should be fired or perhaps training as a whole should be addressed for that location. if as a manager she can behave this way who is holding the other staff accountable? are the drinks even made correctly? are the dates of throw away followed? are people charged properly for their drinks even? i mean with this type of employee at the helm on an evening shift i'd be scared to walk out of my business.

i visit this starbucks a couple times a week--i only use this one because of convenience on my way to work. it is consistently the slowest starbucks i have ever visited. it often takes 10 minutes or more in the drive through. they are in absolutely no rush. in addition, half the time i order they have run out of whatever i'm ordering. if you are crunched for time, you may want to drive out of your way to visit another--you'll probably save time in the long run.

there are two starbucks within spitting distance of each other in this area. one is located next to a pizza place and has a drive thru. the other is located inside giant eagle. i believe this one is the one with the drive thru. quite a line at 3pm but it moved pretty fast. very friendly at the drive thru and my order was correct. also free with my star points. pretty easy in and out if you use springmill rd.

ok so while in reno i needed coffee of course. this sb was right up the street so i got to visit pretty much every day i was there. this was a well run sb. clean with a very professional staff. they always got my orders right and each drink was made well. they had free samples to try also. of course they had all the usual sb stuff like wifi and chairs and tables. overall this starbucks was a notch above your typical sb and if i lived here i would visit every day.

you know what to you get at starbucks, let me tell you what makes this one special. i really like this starbucks. of all of the stores in the area, this one has the best seating, parking, and staff. the store has 4 leather chairs with side tables. a bunch of two and four top tables with both standard chairs and barstools. there is a booth that runs 100' along the wall opposite the counter. it makes working or meeting there an absolute pleasure. it's the biggest store i have seen in the indy metro area. the parking lot is big, although it's harder to get into with the us31 reconfiguration.
really nice cashier, service was fast and location was in a nice spot. i would come back to this spot. i would recommend this spot

let me start off by saying i love starbucks and i frequent them often. this location is near my job and i've been in there about three times so far only because my order never seems correctly made. i always order a grande hot caramel macchiato with extra drizzle. every time i get them from this location it is as if i'm drinking a cup of milk. i ordered it today and it as if i'm drinking a cup of milk with caramel. i never taste the espresso. this is the only location that i have this problem with. i don't know if the measurements are not being made correctly or if the company is skimming off the top and giving out less espresso then what is supposed to be in that drink. please go over with your barista's how to make this drink properly. starbucks is not cheap so to have a drink go to waste because is not prepared correctly is unsettling and makes me not want to visit this location again. when you spin five and six dollars for a drink it should be made correctly. i don't think it the baristas fault, i think it's what they have been trained to do because again i have gotten this drink by several different people and each time i'm walking away feeling like i'm drinking a cup of milk. i want my expresso!!!

they always make my drinks perfectly here! the barista kay especially was so so nice! i was so frazzled getting my drink size wrong, but kay was so sweet and understanding! thank you so much kay!! you're awesome and made my day!!

i ordered hot cocoa and the reindeer cake pop. unfortunately, they forgot to add the whip cream. the cake pop was delicious and i even shared it to my sister. i recommend to order the reindeer cake pop so you can try it your self.

um. what happened to the caliber of employees at starbucks? especially with the prices going up! pathetic service, zero attention to detail! they made my iced drink hot and dropped my bagel on the ground... the last bagel. i love to write good reviews but i could not avoid commenting on this. they did apologize several times... there is just a lack of enthusiasm and care that cannot go unnoticed. btw i'm a former barista that happened to love serving customers and providing and above and beyond experience for my guests... maybe my expectations are too high, i'll stick to my new local, mom & pop coffee shop... thank you very much.

the staff at this location are incredibly slow and easily distracted- they take much longer than necessary to prepare drinks, even when there are almost no customers. they also prioritize drinks based on the regulars, which will almost always be served before other visitors- i would definitely recommend the starbucks on gulf to bay over this location!

back to being a starbucks gal. i was going to dunkin' off missouri & the evening service is terrible. the moral & energy here is consistent. my only thing is the amount of espresso they give in a single shot is extremely minute. not even a full shot. but i'd rather come here where the service is great.

i'm going to qualify this review by saying we are not huge starbucks people. we are definitely casual customers, who might stop in once in a while for a drink. generally, i find the coffee to be a bit on the overpriced side. but when you're out and about and need a caffeine fix, starbucks will usually be a good bet. this one is located near home for us so sometimes my husband and i slip over here for a drink in the evenings before they close. we've also used the drive thru for quick pick ups here and there. and can i just say? a starbucks that has a drive thru is a wonderful thing indeed. i wish all of them (or at least more of them) had them. as a mother with several small children, drive thru's are always appreciated! this location is easy to find, easy to score parking, the staff is pleasant and friendly, the selection is good, the store is clean and the seating options are plentiful and comfortable. this is a fun place to hang out in, chat with someone and just enjoy a few moments. i can't speak to bringing in laptops and working the day away as i'm not the sort to do that but i'm going to guess it's a good place to get that done too. if you're in the north valleys and need a starbucks fix, this is the place to go.

this is an average starbucks near the entrance to terminal a and the baggage claim area. i have been a couple times while waiting on delayed luggage. my order has always been correct so no complaints there. there are a few nice chairs to lounge in while burning time.

the most charming (i.e., least corporate) of the area starbucks thanks to a repurposed interesting building and a trendy neighborhood location. it needs more seating and easier parking, but it's still the most pleasant starbucks in new orleans.

this starbucks is right where i work so i stop by frequently. service is consistently friendly and nice. obviously it depends on the hour how busy it is thus how wiped off tables could be, how prepared ya gotta be upon being the next customer helped, etc. nothing to complain about. have always been served well!
stand alone starbucks located outside the travel plaza, much more convenient than going inside the plaza if coffee is the only reason for your stop.

this is my preferred starbucks to go to as the staff is always super friendly and polite. they have never made me feel like i needed to feel honoured to have them acknowledge me, which is a common trend i find at most of the chain locations. i do not drink coffee, i am not a starbucks addict and i just am not a frequent customer by any means (with my lack of coffee drinking) but i have recently discovered a drink i love! a vanilla frappucino with raspberry syrup and whipped. i order it when my starbucks junkie boyfriend drags me along to satisfy his withdrawals. i know very little of the lingo and i order small, medium or large, and the staff do not try to correct me or lecture me on the language of the coffee addict world... and i like that. most other locations have refused to really help me when they find out i am not one of them. so a big thank you to the wonderful staff that has never shamed me as an outcast!

this is the starbucks that i always go to. i have never had any issues with it. they are very quick and friendly. drinks: all of the drinks have tried have been so good. my favorites are skinny carmel macchiato and magon dragonfruit refresher. pricing: they has decent pricing. many people say it is overpriced but for the quality i think it is just right. reward: if you are coffee lover and stop at starbucks a lot, you should get their rewards program. it is very easy to earn points and there is always opportunities to earn more every week. this starbucks is good with service and quality. they always have the seasonal drinks. if you need a coffee fix then stop here.
coffee is awesome of course, but what i don't get is why they close so early!?!?! i don't know if it's an arizona thing, but usually in ny you can be in the burbs and the coffee joints are 24hrs.

#218 been here lots of times and they have never got my drink wrong...except for today. it wasn't exactly what i ordered but pretty close and actually was very drinkable. i used to drink starbucks almost everyday but the cost is now prohibitive. this is a good location no major issues.

i came to starbucks more or less everyday for 3-4 years. just came today after a long time. look like nothing have changed. terrible service! it's an busy location. right on usciences campus. there is always a long line. i don't mind the line however, staff could be a bit more friendly.
anastasia needs to focus on her customer service and less on her speed. if you don't order in less than 5 seconds don't worry she'll pick for you!

out of venti cups and refused to sell us a trenta because they only had 20 of those cups left for the day. i'm sure the girl working was just tired of explaining that, but her tone and sarcasm was unneeded. her exact words were "i can only sell you a grande, because as you can read on the sign we placed above the screen we are out of venti cups" first world problems for sure- but just unnecessary to be rude.
always good service! need plant-based food options (all locations). i have noticed much better snacks.

this is a very nice, friendly starbucks. it's a little hard to get in and out, due to the layout of the drive through and parking lot. every time i've gone there they make my drink perfectly and i'm a bit picky. brentwood is a very busy road but i've found the service to be prompt even when they're busy, unlike some starbucks.

i came in to this starbucks and their mobile ordering had gone down, so there were a handful of angry patrons. the staff here handled the rude crew with grace and patience, and kicked into high gear to get everyone taken care of. not every patron was grateful for their hard work, but i hope the staff knows that it didn't go unnoticed. it's almost like people get crabby without their coffee! :)

great location and friendly baristas! perfect reason to stop in for coffee and read. i'm always happy with my cup of joe here! the pastries are usually limited, not sure if it's because people have a major sweet tooth in the area or they are under stocked. seating is a bit sparse but they have a few tables outside for a sunny day.

by far the worst starbucks visit. first of all the location was a disaster. second after taking my order she flipped my cups rim down on her filth filled counter. third i ordered a simple chi latte and it was not even something i could drink

this is probably the worst starbucks employee i've ever encountered. twiddling his thumbs while bring the empty creamer to the counter. they don't get replaced i have to state the obvious. please give me some half and half. wanted me to bring coffee to him from the island at first. i had to take the h&h from him. horrible service with an attitude.
manager and staff are awesome. same good stuff as every starbucks. best staff i've seen

first off, let me say that the staff here is awesome. i have never had a complaint about a drink. it's like they all went to a carnegie course because they remember my name and they are pretty fast considering the volume. i'm not a fan of the atmosphere or clientele. people stand in the way of the logical pathway to the order station. the area to wait for your drink is overcrowded. i saw a mercedes with a trump sticker which should tell you that people inside have more money than brains. great staff and coffee with a poor shop design.

this starbucks has bad customer service. they didn't wipe off my drink and i told them i had rewards twice i wanted to use and they said sorry i already put it through when i asked a third time about making sure they used the reward.

well, i sure hope i'm not letting the secret out, but this starbuck's is a hidden gem. the looks of it are deceiving as it appears to be a run-of-the-mill strip mall location without a drive-thru, but it does indeed have one--you just have to drive around the side of the building. because it hides out, the crowds are virtually non-existent. also, they get the orders right every single time.

great starbucks with great service. i've been here a few times after staying at gsr and i never had any problems. the drinks taste great and gsr sometimes has coupons where you can get 15 dollars in free food and it works at this starbucks. also right before closing, sometimes they offer their left over pastries for 50% off which is nice to snack on if you're hungry.

every time i order from them they always mess part of the order up. the first time they gave me the wrong sandwich. the second time someone stole my drink so i asked them to remake it and they made me the wrong drink lol. i'm just trying to have breakfast before class and they mess me up every time.

the partner that just got off work ordered five drinks thus causing a already slow team to slow down even further. they first made my hot latte that i ordered cold. i placed my mobile order at 4:28 and i have been waiting til now for my mobile order (the time is 5:08.) they need to get a grip.
world's slowest starbucks. always the same. never get fast service here. plan on a fifteen minutes wait everytime.
