janae (sorry if that's misspelled) in the drive through for this location is by far one of the most incredible people i have come in contact with. i've always had great service at this location but there is something special about how she brings a smile to my face every time i go there (which is a lot) i love seeing your face friend! you make my day start off just right! happy holidays!
the staff is their best asset. their service is fast and when i had an unsatisfactory drink one time, they quickly resolved it, no problem and with a smile.
slowest starbucks ever. workers don't care about the customers. 5 people working and only kept 1 on register, 1 making the drinks. other ones just standing around talking and not working at all

just like everyone else i enjoy starbucks but the one thing i didn't enjoy was today i went to try their cloud macchiato unless you like to pay for a cup thats 3/4ths foam and just strong coffee with a hint of sweetness i wouldn't recommend

we have been coming to this starbucks since they've been open. we've seen managers come and go, staff changes and remodels. consistency has never failed. the baristas learn our names, drinks and faces quickly. they are always friendly. though at times it could be chaotic and an ocasional drink gets messed up overall we love our backyard starbucks. great job to our new manager "brittany".

so i came in this morning through the drive thru and when i got to the window i was greeted by a huge smile and a great attitude by an employee named matt. he was very energetic and pleasant. i only had a $100 bill and before he took it he said he had to check if he could break it. he came back to the window and in the nicest way possible said they couldn't break the $100 and asked if i had another way to pay. i said unfortunately i didn't but i could go to a store super fast and break it and come back and before i even had a chance to finish this amazing guy paid for my coffee. i just got out of the hospital this morning and this coffee was all i was looking forward to, and this guy took it upon himself to make sure i had it. i couldn't thank him enough. people like this make the world a better place and i will defiantly be back. with smaller bills of course ;) lol.

oh man, did these guys get a new staff? things seem to be served so much slower than in the past. i live across the street so it's super convenient and i wouldn't stop coming back. but man, i have to give myself like 10-15 extra minutes additional now compared with the old staff.
what's up with all the private convos and your rolling eyes? customer service means nothing here.

worst starbucks i ever been to. service is dry and slow. and a few times my cup was half full. like nothing but foam. not the cleanness starbucks. employees always look mad. the only reason i still go, is for free wifi. hire friendlier employees and better trainers. no one ever smiles.

well, we thought we would try this location again. and, once again, tables looked dirty and were sticky, garbage was full and something spilled on the floor (which i pointed out). took at least 15 minutes to take care of it! however, it was nice to use a "real" knife to cut my sandwich. don't think we'll be back here at this location.
great place for coffee..at silver legacy casino..lots of choices..real neat staff..people real nice..m

i really liked coming to this location however lately going inside customer service has gone downhill. load music unfriendly staff no connection with people. drive thru is better and one of the fastest. hit miss on the drinks sometimes you get more foam than drink. the last one i got was not even full so i paid for a grande and got a tall in large cup. no good at all. hopefully things will change

i was originally going to keep this short & sweet. you know, an espresso with 2 lumps of sugar and 2 stars. then i got to thinking about starbucks. they were the originators of the chain specialty coffee shops. they have grown by leaps and bounds and managed to make money. they are a fortune 100 company that has stayed independent, with out being franchised. new opportunities are available for having a starbucks, inside a business, hotel or hospital. i think starbucks is grossly overpriced. why do i say that? if i order an espresso in a fine dining restaurant, i pay that and more. i am unusual in the fact that i love coffee, good strong colombian coffee, period. i make that at home, so i do not see the sense in buying it out. i also have an espresso machine. i am not into lattes or cappuccino's. they have a new line of hot chocolates, that are sure to be a hit. i have had the opportunity to try many of their sandwiches, which come into the starbucks, already made. the ones i have had are very good. i also love their breads. the multi grain berry bread with egg salad is exceptional. their muffins are wonderful. but, they're still overpriced! .

starbucks is my favorite coffee place and this location is always popping! i usually attempt to mobile order just because the location is crammed into a small shopping plaza and there isn't a whole lot of room for drive thru traffic but it works! when i've mobile ordered it's always been ready quickly, when i go through the drive thru it's usually packed but moves fairly quickly and the employees take multi-tasking to a whole new level! i don't know how starbucks finds such amazing employees for each location, and i mean that i have literally never went to a starbucks with bad employees, but they knock it out of the park! my drinks are always made great and if i order good it's always made to perfection! don't let the traffic scare you off! i promise you won't wait long!

i have to give this starbucks a little love. it is my "go to" since it is around the corner from where i work. i have been to other starbucks in the area and i feel like this one is my favorite! i like the carothers pkwy starbucks because i can almost always find a parking spot and grab my drink in time to get to work. i would agree that probably 50-75% of the time that i stop in in the morning there is a long line, but i don't really have a comment on that other than...... get the starbucks app..... it's the easiest way to order your stuff, especially if it's complicated and you get stars, free drinks, etc. i have sat in a long line in the drive-thru here, but unless you have kids or some other reason why you can't quickly get out of the car.... 1. park 2. order your stuff on the starbucks app..... 3. get out of the car and pick it up off the counter 3 minutes later 4. get back in car while you laugh because you still see the person in the drive-thru sitting in the same place they were 10 minutes ago.

not patient with older clients. man in the hat at the register was not a best foot forward.condescending with a smile. not only to me but o the man with limited english ahead of me. food ok and the new butterscotch coffee way too sweet. atmosphere nice. lady making the coffees was professional and personable.

the lines may be long but it's worth it. when i ordered she was so nice. greeted me with a smile and said "good morning and how can i help you? " i placed my order but forgot i was hugging a water bottle to purchase. i received my coffee and cinnamon blueberry bagel, toasted of course and this lady too greeted me with a smile. then i realized i still had the water and had not paid. so i quickly put it back and told the lady and we laughed. she offered me a cup of water and assured me it was filtered and triple filtered. haha they're busy but not too busy for to give a smile.
ridiculously slow and then forgot half my order. 40 min it took to stand in line, place order and get my order. will not be going back, this is the 3rd time with the exact same results.

i love starbucks. i always hit this location when i am in the area. they have a drive thru and have an outside sitting area. the location is clean, busy and easy to find. the parking lot can get crowded but overall it is not hard to get in and out of. they always get my order correct and are friendly whether i go in the store or utilize the drive thru. the coffee is always fresh and the food choices are standard fare for starbucks locations.
always busy and always understaffed. only stop by here if you really need coffee. the wait is usually not worth it.

this starbucks consistently under-performs in customer service. the wait times both in the drive thru and inside can be very long, no matter the time of day. they need to hire more baristas to handle the long rush hour lines. i waited at the drive thru window for 2 minutes on a black iced coffee, after waiting in the line a total of 10 mins. i have seen extremely rude service in the store, to both myself and other customers, which makes me hesitate to go back in. sadly, this store is underwhelming as it is so convenient for many.

clean and modern decor, very friendly and welcoming staff. drink made right and served with a smile. the only negative is they do not take starbucks reward stars although they do issue them- this is more disappointing because on the pastry case they had a sign saying redeem rewards here.

friendly staff and a tremendous caramel flavored frozen drink! located right in the middle of downtown franklin, and they do a great job of managing orders, keeping up with the population, and blending in with franklin's atmosphere.

this is the worst starbucks i have ever been to. despite my daily sbux habit, the fact that the fifth street location is nearest to both my home and my daily commute, and the fact that the tax rate in saint charles is less than any of the other locations i go near (thus decreasing my total beverage cost by at least a dime), i still refuse to go back. this location goes through so much turnover and it shows. i drink the same beverage every day and ordered it over 60 times from this specific location in 2015 and have not received it right the first time around once (this is not an exaggeration; i just counted the number of purchases i made there this year and quit counting at 61). frequently, i have not even been given the correct beverage the second time around. the worst part of their service, of which there are many bad parts, is not the long waits, the scary drive through/ parking situation, or the apathy of their employees. the worst thing about this starbucks is that i have never been apologized to when they make a mistake. the last and final time i will ever go there was when they gave me the wrong beverage three times in a row, didn't apologize, didn't acknowledge that they had done anything wrong, tried to make me feel bad about ordering a drink that had alterations to the standard (i wanted soy in my tea latte), and when i finally just asked for my money back, the manager had to think about it. my advice: find any other coffee/tea shop.

the best location for a starbucks for me. i zoom down spruce st swing a right on 39th and zoom up walnut and i'm back at work in no time. friendly staff and great service. beautiful seating arrangement and easy flow to the store. always clean. always fresh.
we had a guy named bobby help us out. horrible attitude. very rude and actually snatched the money from my hand. very unprofessional as much as i love starbucks i won't be returning to this one.

my friend ordered a flat white and it was ok, not as well prepared as she's had at other starbucks. i had a breakfast sandwich and friend tried it as well and lets say it mostly went in the trash. to sum things up, the experience at this starbucks could have been better if we were greeted with a smile and some enthusiasm, however young lady at counter couldn't do either as much as we were trying to be infectious with ours. great customer service can a lot of times compensate for mistakes. hope this helps improve things at this location.

worst starbucks ever. if you are going to take up valuable airline terminal space, you better be good. not even worth one star. worst service. lines for 30 minutes. horrible staff. avoid at all costs.

my husband and i live in montecito and come every morning to this starbucks. although most of the employees are wonderful, there is one barista that is consistently rude to my family.. her name is nina. based on her behaviors, she always ignores us, delivers an angry stare and refuses to even acknowledge us..she's like the devil working at starbucks. we are fascinated starbucks is grossly negligent by supporting a hostile workplace by allowing and supporting this behavior. she/nina truly believes she is entitled to be rude and hostile to her customers.... what's up with that starbucks? my family is starting to boycott this starbucks for supporting hostile behavior in the workplace...as we are starting to try out other coffee venues in the montecito area. get with it starbucks, don't allow one hostile barrista to destroy your business. truly,

i have been going to this starbucks for 7 years. seven! i have no complaints regarding my drinks. i order a variety of concoctions, so it's never the same thing for me. they do a good job and even when i have to tell them how to make it, they typically do a great job. so drink-wise, they get 5 stars...but, the slightly more mature gentleman that they have working the window has absolutely horrible customer service skills! whenever i great him, he never says hello back, he just gives me a total for my drink. he has never said you're welcome even after being told thank you (which i do every time). today, i asked for my receipt and he said the receipt machine isn't working very curtly while never looking at me. i can absolutely understand having an off day. we all have them. it's not great to have them when you work in the customer service industry, especially when you're dealing with hundreds of customers a day, but it can happen. unfortunately, he has made me go out of my way to a different location because of his rudeness. i don't know if that's his natural personality and demeanor and he's like that with everyone or if he chooses to be like that only with people that are not of his skin color. but, whatever the reasoning is, his customer service skills need to be addressed. i have never had this issue in avon or at the one on 56th st. and lafayette rd. it's only him at the 10th st. location. i'm even considering calling and making a verbal complaint, because no one should have to be subjected to that.

i love this starbucks the people here are friendly the managers are always training their stuff correctly. sure it's a little difficult to get in and out of parking lot and there's no drive-through, but that makes zero difference compared to the quality customer service that you get from the starbucks amazing people here!!!
i had the best customer service experience thanks to genna! she was very kind and just super helpful. this girl definitely deserves a promotion.

i'm not sure why starbucks gets so much bad publicity and poor reviews! guessing it's the sheer size and magnitude of a corporation and haters gonna hate. well, if you weren't surprised i love starbucks and i'm a huge fan of founder and ceo howard schultz and the work and ethics that go into his company. imo the coffee is very good, the service is usually very friendly and astute and the app is a game changer. being able to order ahead at a million locations and have your tasty beverage awaiting your arrival and skip the long lines is amazing! also, you earn rewards towards future tasty beverages yay. also, starbucks offers heavy cream as an option for those of us on low carb diets and also all natural, low carb sweetener. stop the madness and rate fairly please. thanks for stopping by :)
love this location! the baristas are always up beat and friendly, knowledgeable and faat. they updated the seating and added. ore tables but took away the comfy arm chairs to relax in.
always freezing here!!! come on guys make your customers comfortable and turn up the heat!! everyone sitting here is wearing their winter coats!!
went through drive yesterday! service was perfect! thank you. very upbeat and order was perfect.
i came to this starbucks while visiting my mom. this starbucks is very clean, has plenty of seating, and family friendly. they've got a changing station and high chairs. staff is very friendly.
i love all starbucks stores. my visit was fast and friendly. my ice coffee was delightful. thank you for excellent customer experience ' i will return! gloria~

i like local. but, it's the only option out here other than pj's. pj's is local-ish, but their coffee isn't to my liking, so here i am. it's easy to sit here and grab a few moments of zen before my evening class.

this review is all of the starbucks from santa barbara to canyon country california! i called them all and not one of them has/had brown sugar shaken espresso!!! it's really ridiculous how this drink is advertised yet i've been looking for it for over a week. i don't know who the buyers are for starbucks but someone really screwed up here. very very disappointing.
seriously the most friendly service at any starbucks i've been too. everyone seems like that want to be there.. which is always so refreshing!

this location is quite hit and miss. i don't recommend the drive thru here plus it's an inconvenient parking lot if you're trying to grab a quick cup of joe. that being said, if you're meeting a friend for a leisurely coffee date, this location might be the right one for you! slower paced and not very busy.
this is probably the slowest starbucks. i order using the app and it's not ready or wrong. this location is so close but service is not good.

i really enjoy starbucks. i enjoy stopping off at them from time to time to relax and clear my mind. although, trying to find a parking space at this location is almost impossible sometimes. with that is added stress which i don't need. what's sad is the location is very convenient for me. i'll be craving a cup of jo as i pass, look at the lot and decide to keep going.

this is my local star ucks and i'm happy to report that it is one of the better ones in central indiana. it's located in a strip mall so there's no drive-thru which i think makes it better. it's more intimate and you get your order much faster than at starbucks that have drive-thru (i always order at the counter). the seating area is very comfortable and not too bright. i would say the vibe is more like the coffee shops of old where you sit and enjoy your cup o' joe while reading a newspaper (rather than today's starbucks where people are working on their laptops or having business meetings).

drink was good but it took 22 minutes for me to get my drink upon entering the door. no apology for the delay and no manners by staff whatsoever. after the cashier yelled, "next!", i walked up and said, "good morning". no response. i said thank you for my drink. no response. rude!

most of the partners are insanely rude. there's absolutely no sence of urgency, which is easy to forgive since this is a tourist hot. however when there's no one in line, 3 people waiting on drinks and 5+ partners on the floor it shouldn't take over 10 minuets to get drinks out. but before you go in and pay for a latte and get a cup of foam with dead shots, remember that you're in new orleans and there are amazing local coffee shops on every corner and will actually give you the drink you asked for.

tracy is the sweetest lady there. i go there most mornings before work, and we always chat and talk. my breakfast is always delicious and made quickly. even if it wasnt good, i would still go here just for the people who work here. the customer service is beyond compare.

the baristas are sweet but wow, save time by going into the lobby if you can. this is the slowest starbucks i've ever been to yet. been sitting in the drive thru for going on 15 min now and still haven't reached the ordering station. i've moved 2 car lengths during this time. as a former barista partner, thus is extremely awful for drive thru times. management really needs to work on their speed and times, especially after 4:30pm. this is ridiculous. been here 4 times so far and regretted every time i chose the drive thru option.‍

cashier didn't seem to know what she was doing. i asked for a peppermint mocha frap and she kept asking if i wanted it with coffee or vanilla. i told her i want it with coffee and oatmilk. when i received the drink i noticed it was significantly lighter in color but thought it was because i asked for oat milk. after leaving i realized she gave me a vanilla frap . i obviously asked for a peppermint mocha. and said peppermint mocha repeatedly.
thanks to the kind gentleman at the drive-thru today! he made my day and the best way to start my birthday count down off! 3 days away! the service was fast, friendly and great!!!

this starbucks is one of my all time favorite locations in the bay area. the staff is always extremely friendly, they repeat my order back to me for correct construction, and most importantly, they remember my name. (i'm not a daily customer, so i value this immensely) the parking is very limited at this location, but i'm usually a drive thru customer so it doesn't bother me too much. i've been inside for meetings and i've found the lobby to be smaller than some starbucks, but it's always been clean and well maintained. i cannot imagine much to improve on the already stellar setting, other than an expansion to the property. all around, this is a top notch starbucks and i'll continue to call them my friendly neighborhood baristas.

always busy, always fresh. first time i've noticed a board with pics of the variations on drinks. great idea. it sold me on the danica. a blend of coffee, caramel. recommended for bold flavor. try it out!

please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please clean your dirty bathrooms!!!!

i'm not sure how much of a difference a yelp review can make with a chain but i'm at this location almost everyday. this last friday the computer never printed out my order that was made in store and i waited a long time for my things when i was asked what i ordered. they got right on making everything the machine had failed to print out and ever since they have since covered my coffee three times. they are the nicest staff who really cares about their customers. they care to keep their regulars happy and if something goes wrong they do what they can to make up for it. nothing like this would deter me in general because its just coffee.
ewe!! went for a night coffee. paid $15 and did not get what i ordered. it was horrible. won't be stopping at this one again!

this location is excellent. i will forewarn you it can be busy at times (the lines for the drive through can get long) but the workers are quick and somehow even friendly while working so hard. i live off page in overland and all the fast food type places up there have terrible service so it's nice coming to a place like this that is the total opposite. there was even one time a while ago when i realized i forgot my wallet while in the drive through and they gave me my drink for free. because of that action i have gone out of my way to come back to this location many times just to even it back out. 5/5.

this is one of the few starbucks locations in the tampa area with a starbucks reserve coffee selection and a clover brewing machine. what does this mean for you? it means that you have a wider variety of rarer coffee blends brewed fresh individually by hand at the time you order and not a second sooner.
slow and dirty best explain this location my iced coffee was horrible and i got snapped at by a bitch employee who wears a jeff cap. i will never visit this location again and won't miss it at all

while this is the closet starbucks to me i can't actually visit them regularly because of the lack of consistency of the drinks and the staff. there are def some employees that are friendly and smile and the others just barely acknowledge your existence. the frappes are also sometimes good and sometimes completely watered down depending on the barista. i shouldn't have to dread seeing certain baristas and hope they make things properly.

skip it unless you enjoy mediocre service from snarky wannabe hipsters. they arrange the 6 tables such that 5 people plus their coats and computers occupy 12 chairs. when i complained i was told that it's above the employees pay grade. was also told there is no manager and they don't know who their regional manager is or how to contact them. hard to imagine starbucks hitting a new low yet here it is. i'm sticking to local coffee going forward even if it's 10 minutes out of my way. after all, it's below my expected service grade to expect actual service when i spend $12 on breakfast at a starbucks.
i drive out of my way to go to this starbucks. annie is great and there is a fella inside with blonde hair who makes my drink perfectly every time!
worst starbucks in the world. cold sandwiches, slow, dirty, rude and always mess ups.

sad to say this place is "my starbucks", the closest to my work. this is a collective review of the 3 or more times a week i usually visit. the staff here either hates their life or their job or both! even though you're paying at least 6 bucks for your drink, don't expect it to taste gourmet at all. it's really disappointing that starbucks has gone so down hill. there is only one girl who is nice and friendly there, the rest act like you're bothering them. i ran into one other friendly chick who even had to remake my drink for whatever reason and she restored a little hope in me..however, i'm sure she's a borrowed partner bc she wouldn't last long in that store before they drained the positivity out of her! guess i'll be traveling to the ina store to get my fix. sorry that those rude customers have ruined it for the rest of us who just want the delicious drink that we paid for.

friendly people and a nice place to work or spend sometime with friends. they have drive thru and also outdoor seating (which is good in summer). this place is usually crowded and takes time to get your order in the mornings. since lot of people drive thru and evening during a week day you can find spots to sit and spend time. but on weekends it's little crowded i would say to spend time there and get a spot to sit as it is kong is busy with neighborhood people. this places it really good.

fast and friendly. i'm there almost every morning the pretty much know my order/name. they can get very busy which the parking lot and drive through isn't designed for. yet the staff do a great job with keeping the line moving, the energy up and the orders correct.

this starbucks has plenty of space and the staff is very friendly. the décor is awesome and it's a great place to meet with other people. the only challenging aspect of this coffee house is their parking lot. it is limited and getting in and out it's complicated. if you make it in, you will be in a great starbucks!

probably the best study spot in manayunk given that there're only two free wifi spots but volo has slow wifi so.. standard starbucks menu, always stocked up on pastries and grab-and-go lunch boxes. the go-to recently has been cold brews or the pink drink. staff are always nice. the line gets long in the mornings but moves quickly. the back area has a plenty of seating and lots of other people doing work. pretty quiet most of the time. not a lot of outlets though.

well store seems to be very clean, i did order online and was just picking up. they have two lines one for pick up and one to order in store. signs are up"mask required", tables taped up, and plenty of spaced out marks on the floor for social distance. coffee was great but my one reason for a 4 star review is that it took almost 15 minutes, which is more than it usually takes when i place an online order, which meant i was in the store waiting for longer time than my usual. other than that clean, friendly staff, and always great coffee.

understaffed and extremely slow. don't come here if you're in a rush.. this is the worst starbucks i have ever been to. the lines are always long and slow to get through and the baristas show no sympathy when you do eventually get to the front. i only go here when i'm in no rush and extremely desperate for a coffee

have been coming here a few times a week for the past two years. it's starbucks, it's the only one close to the hospitals and such here on s grand so it's a popular spot. they do get busy but it's always service with a smile, they have been hiring some new great baristas recently as well! always enjoy time spent here! *update* still coming here, still great staff - kori, erika, ella, miranda, the whole team is just fantastic! also for anyone complaining about wait times, this place gets slammed - it's on s grand one of the busiest streets! perhaps try taking a seat and people watching while you wait, or do a mobile order as you leave the house (make your drink extra hot.)

worst experience i have had anywhere. a few of my friends and i decided to hangout here and get some coffee. there is a moes a few buildings down so i decided to get some chips and queso for us to share. we sat outside and once we were done with our coffee and queso we threw our drinks and bag of chips away. we stood outside starbucks for a few extra minutes just chatting and a starbucks employee came outside to change the trash bags. when he went to empty it he took the bag of chips and threw it into the street! we were all really confused as to why he did that but i decided to just pick them up and put them back in the trash. the employee saw me and said "i'll do it again!" we were all genuinely so confused. he came back outside as we were leaving, took the chip bag out of the trash once again and put it on the table where we had been sitting outside. i really don't know what his problem was but it was honestly the rudest experience i've ever had with an employee. i will never be coming back to this location and i hope you won't either.

from out of town and stopped in for a coffee. the barista sydney, i believe, at the cashier did not greet us even when my five year old nephew said hello. he just annoyingly smiled at my nephew. then for some odd reason the jerk off kept staring at me like i killed his first born. wtf? he was very close to me snapping on him. not to mention i'm pretty sure he put regular milk in my coffee instead of soy even though i made sure to ask when i didn't see the soy container on the counter. my stomach is all types of tore up. what an a-hole.
can't really think of anything bad to say. the store is brand new so it looked super clean. ambiance was very nice. very relaxed atmosphere. workers kept bringing me samples of drinks.
i was the only person in line and they intentionally made my drink wrong (tall half calf vanilla latte)

i'm a pharma rep and am always ordering a lot of drinks when i go in. today this location was especially busy, but the baristas were still so kind and helpful despite the fact that i had to place a 17 drink order. they worked quickly to get me all my drinks and make sure everything was right. i appreciated their pleasant demeanor and helpfulness!
love this location. i come everyday. the baristas are awesome. lawson the manager is one of the best around. very customer service friendly. this is why i keep coming back on a daily basis.

putting covid aside this starbucks is managed poorly. the hours of operation for this location are horrible. it makes zero sense. there is a second location only 4 miles away that keeps consistent hours. i just went today to this location at 9am and the drive thru is closed and no cars in the parking lot yet a huge sign saying " we are open", but your not. this location just recently extended the hours of operation to be open till just 5pm instead of 2 pm. very disappointed.

i really like the cotton candy frappachino. it is amazing! must have. its a non-menu item, but just ask for it and they should know what you mean. the food is okay, but the drinks are awesome. just play it safe and just get the drinks. what i love about starbucks is that the baristas are so friendly and they sometimes make small talk while you wait for your drink. the reason while the last star is not there because it is sometimes a little bit dusty and when it is really crowded the drinks take long. i recommend this coffee shop to everyone who likes coffee (i dont drink coffee).

a good chunk of the staff here is inattentive. out of the 6 or 7 times i've been in here, ordered and sat inside, i've gotten the wrong drink 3 times, waited 15 minutes for a cup of water, and had an order completely forgotten about -- all while the staff is either goofing around or not paying attention to anyone at all. i've watched so many people walk up to the counter and say "i don't think this is what i ordered" or "did you guys forget about my drink?" i'd give them a break if they were busy when i come, but they're not. i really don't get it.

your typical starbucks. a good stop on a nice day while shopping in beautiful suburban square; there is plenty of outdoor seating available to enjoy the sun while you self-medicate your caffeine addiction.

fun fact: the oprah chai (in it's tall version) has only 150 calories. i can't account for the sugar content at moment and quite frankly, i'd rather not know but i will say this particular chai offers a healthy dose of caffeine kick and spice. i giggled at first when i heard oprah had her own bebida at starbucks...but now, now, dear reader, i laugh no more. in true oprah fashion, she means business. as for this particular location: the barista was exceptionally friendly and the space was clean. the natural light would make for a great work/meeting space.

this place always seems packed probably because it's the only one near university street on campus. i ordered my drink ahead on the app and it was not ready at the predicted time. all the seats were full so i had to take my drink to go.

oh starbucks, how i love thee. traders point starbucks, you're my #1. i hope we're bff's forever. when there are so many starbucks around, the people who work there are really the ones who can make it or break it. this starbucks has some pretty cool people working there, and they're great at keeping the drive thru moving at a decent pace, keeping the inside clean and organized, and getting to know their regulars. hands down my favorite starbucks, all because of the people who work there.

my chai tea just taste like steamed milk and it took 15 mins just to get it. every time i come here it seems like it taste forever and now the drinks aren't even worth it. next time i will try to go to the one in gate 17

standard starbucks, but i like frequenting this one because there seems to be outside seating when i need it. drinks are good and i have no problems here. drive thru can get out of control at times and make the parking lot more difficult to navigate.
friendly staff & convenient drive thru. my order was correct as well with no problems.

standard starbucks. good amount of parking. they have a decent amount of outdoor seating and smoking is no longer allowed outside. seating inside is also good. the staff is very friendly and there is an open mic night every thursday if you're into that.

this starbucks is such a great location (and build) that it hurts me to give this a two star rating, but here it is. this starbucks was my favorite a year ago and something has changed dramatically. keep in mind that my rating is based over several visits and not just one negative instance. that's not how i roll... :) the staff: individually, there are some really great people working here (and working incredibly hard.) however, they don't appear to be having any sort of fun. which, i get it, not every day is a joy. however, this doesn't look like a team. i watched for 15 minutes as teammates passed by each other without saying a word to one another. there was no joking, or laughing, or even a smirk. if they're not having fun with each other, they're not having fun with customers. this team needs a team-build coach!!! the service: i had a one-off situation in which i ordered a venti iced coffee and paid for it. moments later the barista advised me that they were out of venti cups and then handed me a grande iced coffee. he then told me that the cashier would adjust the cost. i was immediately confused as a customer. the cashier apologized and explained "hey, if you're hanging out i'll give you a refill for free" and then adjusted the cost. kudos for effort, but this is a service fail. some better approaches: 1) connect with me, educate me, and then present me with options. (ex. "i'm so sorry, we're out of the venti cups. would you like to go with a grande and i'll refund the difference, or would you like to go with a trenta for a few cents more?" 2) get creative! "hey, i'm so sorry we're out of venti, would you be ok with me giving you a venti serving in a trenta cup?" 3) if it's rung up, it's your error, and needs fixing... well, fix it. (ex. "hey, you're getting an upgrade today at no additional cost, please enjoy this one on us!" i get that stock levels of other cups are a concern and giving stuff away in a sales environment is scary, but gaining customers for life is a far better investment in your time and energy. my goal is not to tear this location down, but to give a customer perspective. this place was friendly and a great place to visit and i'd like to see this location return to it's former glory. best wishes!

i can see why this starbucks has a higher rating than some of the others. it's quieter and therefore you get a lot more customer service from the baristas. how are drinks were made perfectly. that's not always the case. there's a nice little patio area and the inside is a little more cozy then some of the other locations. also, it's a lot cleaner since they're not so hectic. if you're a drive-by girl guy, they have that too. it's pretty easy to access it off of oracle, and be right back on the road. i like to hang out if i'm going to enjoy some coffee. give it a try, you might just like it! i wanted to stick with my great review i had given of the 4 points, but when i saw the two dead cockroaches in the bathroom i felt sad. however... the people who work here are so nice and friendly and i actually saw them cleaning! to me that makes this location even better. i just wish they would go in the bathroom to see if it needs cleaning and get rid of the two bugs. maybe they haven't seen them quite yet lol. i still like this location because it's quiet and more cozy and some others.

the low review is primarily due to the service that has gone significantly downhill. i usually stop at this starbucks pretty regularly before work. this starbucks has the slowest drive thru of any of them in town. yesterday after the line not moving for 15 minutes, i just left. this morning, there weren't many cars, but enough that my drink should have been ready when i pulled up. instead it was another 5 minutes after my card was processed before i was handed my drink. "just go inside" you say? it isn't any faster, and i've had them put my order on the drive thru counter. it took me 5-10 minutes to get someone's attention to grab it for me. and the point of a drive thru is for it to be quicker than getting out of your vehicle, walking inside, waiting in line, ordering, waiting for your drink, and then walking back to your car, getting in and driving away. they need to take the service in the drive thru (and inside) up a notch.

not my favorite location, lines are always too long in the mornings and they take forever to take your order then to fill it. barista's constant talking to each other about non-starbucks related orders are annoying and could be partly responsible for their lack of attention to details. the drive thru starbucks up the street is even worse so i guess this is the best city line has to offer.
renovated to slow down service. i understand long lines but 3 cars and still over 15 mins? will skip this one from now on!

the parking spots are right next to the drive-through lane so getting in and out of the parking lot is a nightmare if you're not going through the drive-through! other than that, the staff is friendly and the coffee is good as always.

i'm really glad this starbucks opened up on harbour island, long overdue! we can now have coffees and teas and custom beverages at standard starbucks prices and right near american social and jackson's! it's a pretty standard starbucks with points available for collection (not redemption), same high quality beverages and usually carries the seasonal specials. one of the great things about this starbucks over some of the others in south tampa is this one isn't that busy and it has ample electrical outlets available. almost every seat has charging. it is well kept, staff are friendly and seating is comfortable. there is however negatives too- you can park in the paid parking, and get your parking validated here (for free), but that makes parking a little inconvenient. it also closes a little early for a regular starbucks (read 8pm). the biggest negative is they don't allow you to use starbucks reward points. you can collect them but can not redeem them here. so it kind of eliminates the use of points if you only come here for your starbucks.
great location and friendly staff. lots of open tables and seating at all times of the day. whenever i'm downtown this is my go to spot!

so so so happy this location has reopened for drive thru during the pandemic. the staff here are fabulous and david is a social media whiz keeping everyone up to date with their hours and what was going on when they were closed. 2 thumbs way up! having to work during the pandemic is stressful but being able to enjoy a treat like a matcha green tea latte makes the day a little easier. mar props to david for keeping the community in the loop and for constantly doing updates like how we can do ride up on our bikes and do mobile orders for those of us trying to find ways to treat our kidlets who miss normal. for now, starbucks bicycle frappuccino dates are a great way to celebrate special days like birthdays!!! thank you starbucks

if there were a minus star i would give that!! the person behind the counter couldn't even look up to greet me. i could hardly hear a word she said and never once said thank you!!! than the girl who put my iced tea and sandwich on the counter mumbled to let me know my order was done and again grunted a thank you. wow this starbucks sucks. i've been to many and never had such an experience....blah
everyone at this location is really friendly. my coffees have always been spot on. this particular visit, tried the pumpkin spice cream cold brew. i loved it.

i'm a regular at this location and the staff is always friendly and welcoming. my drinks are always made the way i like them and i'm never waiting long - even if there's a few cars ahead of me in the drive-thru. when i come in with my daughter (who's 4) they acknowledge her and treat her with the same respect they treat me, and as a parent that means the world to me.
whoever hired the girl working this drive through should be fired! she can't take an order to save her life and got a line up out to the road bc of this moron! seriously wth it's 1pm!!!

not my favorite usf area starbucks, but convenient on my way to work. parking is often an issue and many times when i visit the baristas are cleaning or vacuuming or conducting other loud tasks - not an ideal location to sit and chat. though the seating is plentiful and diverse - a couple big comfy chairs, a high top, a long bar that regulars sit at to chat with the baristas. my last 2 visits have been okay - one was for a frappuccino that took far too long to make between the 3 baristas working and no one in front of me - the guys after me got their drinks before me and they were quite chummy with them meanwhile i'm standing there awkwardly waiting. the girl doing the register was new and took awhile to ring me up but it was fine. last night i wanted a passion tea lemonade and was told they were out of lemonade. i was recommended to try a passion tea apple juice and reluctantly agreed. then the computer froze up for a minute and i was given it on the house! bonus! i'll take some free starbucks, though i admit i did want the stars on my card lol. this one is much less crowded than fowler and not much further from usf. i also wish this was drive through or there was some drive through on my way to usf but this one is pretty good. fowler is my favorite for the friendliness and speed, but this one is good too.

they do not let you use your starbucks card to pay, however you can get cheesecake factory. =) they do still make good drinks and it's inside the barnes and noble. i wouldn't go out of my way to come here, but i'm a book nerd so i will always be a customer at least until someone messes up my coffee (even if i don't get my stars for being a gold card customer).

if i could, i would give this location a a big fat zero for customer service. i figured the last time i went was fluke maybe the representative was in a bad mood. then last week two of my coworkers and i visited this location because it was close to home-goods. upon entering this location i had my phone and order ready i walked to the counter where two representatives were helping the person in front of me. the representatives walked away one went to play on her cell phone and the other make the drink. so i waited, another representative came from the back and took the person next to me order, whom had just walked in. not once did the representative ask me if i had been helped . ok tori, stay calm, the one that went to check her phone finally walked up and in her most annoyed tone asked me "how can i help you" i gave her my order and she advised me that what i wanted wasn't on the menu. ok, but can you make it . she paused and said yes. she seemed annoyed that people were walking in. starbucks are everywhere, i will not be coming back to this one.

the ladies working on 11/6 when it opened today were amazing, patient, and so friendly! i know they were hit with a lot of people when they opened but they still made our drinks delicious and hot, and handled customer questions and swaps so professionally. it's hard enough to work during this pandemic, and having red cup day only adds to the pressure. i wish i caught their names because they were great! happy holidays, ladies!
its a starbucks, they are all about the same but this one seem to have the world smallest parking lot and drive tho, people are always trying to get in and out and its just a mess out their.

this starbucks, like most, is uber busy between the hours of 7am and 8am. but the staff is cheery, the facility is clean and the chairs are comfy. safe yourself sometime and go in, rather than waiting for the drive up; the line insides seems to be speedier than the line outside.

every time i come here i'm disappointed. i've gotten a cool lime refresher that was only filled to 1" below the rim and even then it tasted watered down. i've gotten frappuccinos with too little frapp base so they didn't blend right. i've ordered extra extra extra bone dry cappuccinos and gotten a full on latte instead because they don't know how to make foam. not even 1/2" of foam - pure milk all the way to the top. i've never had them remake anything because i never have the time afterwards because they take so long. i used to be a starbucks barista so i know what the drinks should be like, and i know that it's not hard to make them, especially with the large number of baristas they have at all times. but of the 5 times or so i've gone to this location they've screwed up my order every time. stay away.

ok here we go again isabella is one of the greatest baristas i know very rare in this business. i appreciate your hospitality readiness to warn you up with a smile and a fire. you need more like her... shawn ain't bad either lol!! thank you both you make it fun to come in.

i mean it's starbucks! need i say more lol. came here for a guava green tea lemonade. the line was so long and took what seemed like forever to get thru. when we finally got up to order, they didn't have lemonade! ugh. oh well i just ordered the drink without the lemonade and it was still good. staff was friendly and got our order right.

worst starbucks i've very been too. i worked as a barista for starbucks for 3 years and i've never seen someone with such poor costumer service. the manager named ally, gave me my latte with no foam and then when i asked for extra foam she threw out my entire drink and insisted on giving me a cappuccino. while i was arguing that i did not want a cappuccino but just a regular latte with foam, she proceeded to finish making me the cappuccino. only to throw that out and then make the latte (again). waited so long to get the drink, had to make a scene, just because i wanted a latte with foam. if i could leave 0 stars then i would.

it was 9pm. my 6 yrs old wants cookies. the told us we are close and not taking any costumer. we where the last costumer and i explained we onky want cookie for my daughter they still refuse to help. bad costumer service
going to starbucks today was the best! the barista made the greatest recommendation for a special frapuccino and it was just perfect.

if you like there pike place coffee then this is the place to come because , it seems, that's all they ever have on tap. they have plenty of pour over coffee. that's like you make coffee in the woods , traintrack, style of coffee. pour hot water over coffee grounds and that's your cup of coffee. it takes about 15 minutes for them to pour the hot water over the grounds because they are waiting on people in between pours. for the money they charge i should have a choice of several flavors . they also have plenty of rock hard, low chairs and tables to sit at. you get a free backache just for sitting there drinking there pike place. have to see a chiropractor after every cup there. not my place to injoy a cup of coffee

male employee working the window. the drink was made incorrectly and they wanted to add the missing whipped creme in a separate cup and told us to add it to the drink. we asked that the drink be remade and he slammed down a cup, walked away rudly and went and threw something in the trash angrily. we asked for a refund and a female emplyee comes to the window and my husband told them that the man was being rude and she told him you are being rude. she then says you have your refund. i said can i have a receipt to confirm it..she showed major attitude after handing us the receipt. they didn't have breakfast buns and croissants at 11:00am on a saturday morning. this starbucks will not get my money ever again! managament is to blame..if my students don't learn at my school as the leader i am to blame. if you have rude emplyees and unspoken products at 11am on a saturday poor leadership and management is to blame.

extremely convenient drive thru is always a plus but while the quality of product lacks id rather walk into another starbucks to get my drinks made right. the very berry hibiscus lacks its color integrity flavor and if your lucky they might drop 3 dry bereys in your drink.
friendly staff and accurate orders. can't ask for much more from starbucks :)

worst burnt tasting coffee i ever had in my life. expensive is an understatement. i'm told you have to get one of the flavored coffees or some fancy drink and you'll like it. if you can't just get the base coffee right they shouldn't be selling it. anyone can dump a bucket of sugar in something to make it taste like desert.

made a mobile order this morning to try their new pistachio latte. my order was ready when i arrived. i was a bit confused so i waited around -- thinking my name would be called, but there is a sign clear as day that says something along the lines of "mention your name for uber eats and mobile orders." whoops during my short wait, i ordered baloo a puppucino (free cup of human-dairy whip cream for dogs) , because i couldn't come home empty handed. the line was wrapped around the plaza, so i was so grateful to have a mobile order. this location has been remodeled, so it's super open in the inside now which is perfect for covid precautions. there are no tables inside, you can order inside if you desire. there is one set of chairs outside. the decor is super modern and can't wait to see what the rest of furniture will look like when they decide it is safe to add it.

ordered online and got there and only my drink was ready. i asked if my other item was coming and the lady had the worst attitude about it when i just asked very nicely if it was coming. will be returning but hopefully i have a better experience next time.

although the coffee is terrific, the two stars is solely for parking options. i feel starbucks needs to place this store in a different location where parking easier accessible for their consumers. there is no reason in 2019 i need to wait over ten minutes to park my vehicle to purchase a regular coffee. i will do my best not to visit here again.

a starbucks, is a starbucks, is a starbucks.... and this one is a starbucks. that doesn't say much, but simultaneously says it all. i'm a purist with my coffee and always just order coffee.... no frills. there should be a separate line for people like me who order, they turn and fill a cup and we are gone.... i don't even need to go to the separate counter to pick-up. but i digress... this was coffee and it was good.
slow and slightly rude. i usually go out of my way to go to the other nearby locations. this is one of the slowest starbucks i've been to.
parking can be difficult during morning hours...drive-thru is your best bet!

i stopped by this starbucks to order my grandson breakfast. his usual breakfast consists of egg bites, strawberry refresher and a cake pop. i tossed in a cold brew coffee for me. our order was filled in lightning speed and the staff was very friendly. the eggs were hot and our drinks were perfect.

i know...high price.but it's worth it hehe..coffee at a top eatery is far more costly and rarely more savory...and really....what is money...when you get free friendly atmosphere, manager, workers, etc. yes it's nice espresso/whatever/or mocha and sometimes a nice healthy snack..or a serious brownie hehe lucious for me, and i go to the outside seating and do art work, and share with surrounding peeps and workers for free...its, cool and refreshing for me...
simply put the barista doesn't really care much about customer service. drinks are not made to starbucks standards.
i love all starbucks. the location is easy to fine to get my fix. the staff are very nice and accommodating. the parking lot has plenty of parking. i am fan. gloria~

i ordered a iced starbucks double shot today at the starbucks on davis montana afb store. i asked if i could get a straw with my drink and was informed that it is starbucks new policy that straws will not be given with any of the new lids. even after requesting a straw the sales person said no straws with that lid. i am sorry but if i request a straw no matter what lid is on my drink i should be able to get a simple straw. i'm sorry but this store is on a u.s. military base.
i had two egg and cheddar english muffins with a cold-brew iced coffee. it was absolutely delicious. the staff was very funny, kind, and helpful!

i have been in this location a few times and never had any bad things to say until now. my mom sister and i were shopping in the area and decided to get drinks. we ordered the iced caramel macchiato w/ almond milk, caramel frap, and a strawberries and cream frap. when i tried the macchiato it did not taste like almond milk. i do not even know what they put in my drink since it did not taste like the usual one i always order- not sure if maybe the milk was expired or they made it differently but it tasted awful. i couldn't even drink it and i'm not the person to complain and ask for a refund but i could not even swallow it. when i went up to the counter the barista making the drinks seemed passive and annoyed (not a big deal because she was busy). then i went to the cashier and told them about my incident- they did not seem very apologetic but rather passive. they did ask me if i wanted another drink made but i just did not like the treatment and was very disappointed by this location so i refused.

i've frequented this spot in the last few months because i've been working at a near by office. the thing i like about this store is that it has a drive through that typically moves at a decent pace. the bummer is that i order the same thing every day and it is never made the same. in addition i get charged differently for the exact same order (if i don't mobile order it). pretty much a decent spot that does have some outdoor seating.

they are super slow in this location. they have 10 people behind the counter, but you walk in and the line is out the door. very inefficient. 2 registers, 1 person taking the order and the other person takes the money. how about utilizing both registers so the line moves quicker. i waited 10 minutes for a croissant. that is absolutely ridiculous. why are there so many people working inefficiently?!

the clay terrace starbucks is a good representation of the brand, although it is a little small. the service is good and the drinks well prepared. a great stop if you are already here shopping, but not worth a special trip.

this location has a lot of space for seating which i really enjoy. starbucks ran this promotion in january with this silver tumbler that was refillable with free coffee for the entire month. i enjoyed stopping in each morning and getting my free coffee, always greeted with welcoming smiles and quick service! there are many starbucks in town... but i prefer to be at this one!!

6/26/2018: when traveling somewhere around the planet, there will be several starbucks franchises practically everywhere in every city from reno, carson city, minden, california, new york-all fifty us states, canada, alaska, singapore, philippines, south korea, macau, hong kong, japan, thailand, malaysia, europe, etc.. i like the starbucks' ice cream scooper i bought in beverly hills ca a few years ago; however, while traveling to lost wages, i put my prized starbucks traveling, stainless steel, coffee press on a table, and i turned the other way. . . ppppppfffffttt~gone! the nerve of people taking my coffee press inches from my elbow! well~it's time buying a new cold coffee beverage container . . . i just wish the one i bought today was safe keeping hot coffee inside but, oh, well . . . not. and~the number one reason i keep returning to starbucks: drumroll, please . . . peppermint coffee with a double shot of espresso surely makes me into a nervous chihuahua~lol! arf-arf-arf!!!!

the weekday morning crew at this store is absolutely the best. they are always friendly and quick, using a style that makes you feel invited and not rushed. they have offered excellent suggestions every time i have asked for ways to tweak any of my favorite drinks. i pass by 2 other starbucks on my way into the office because the service at this location is truly the best.

used to be a favorite spot, but recently the tea was watered down yhe the soy latte had an odd after taste. the workers i am accustomed to. it's a busy spot with limited seating, adaptability is key. don't rely on seating and chatting during busy hours. cleanliness is ok and the noise level is average. i guess you can say it's a hit or miss. the location is conveniently located and there is outdoor seating. a good place to wait while you car is getting work done!
as others have said this starbucks isn't great. slow service, very unfriendly staff but my drink was good so for a airport stop i guess it'll do.

the drive thru here can be sssssoooooo slow. but 24/7 is awesome and as andrew s. said if you wait to long you might get a free java outta the deal.. but that has only happened once to my dozens of times of waiting 5+ minutes for a cup of joe.

it's a chain store coffee shop, you know what you are getting. this one closes waaaay too early for me to frequent it regularly. the staff is nice even though one girl sounds exactly like edie mcclurg from planes trains and automobiles...every time she asks me through the intercom how she can help me, i want to launch into steve martin's bit from the movie. gobble gobble!!!

i wish this location would get their service together. they're sooo slow. the drive thru is slow and going inside seems even slower. the baristas act like it's a chore to speak with you and if they can avoid making eye contact they do. it's not a friendly environment. the pastry i purchased today was stale. even though there was only 1 person in front of me in line and only 1 person at the counter waiting for their drink it took almost 15 minutes to receive my drink after i paid for it today. the layout inside is awkward. i keep returning in hopes for some improvement but it's consistently poor.

i've been here on a few occasions and i do not have any complaints. inside is clean, the baristas are nice and get my drink right. be forewarned if you order multiple drinks in the drive thru it will get muddy, but the order takers have always been patient and will repeat, repeat until they get it down right.

customer service is usually pretty good, however starbucks says they care about their employees but then start them off at $9.25/hour when the company has the ability to pay a living wage. pay your works more. especially during a pandemic. you are not essential.

starbucks is not really something a challenge in terms of food. you know what you're going to get. customer service, however, is very important. i've been coming to the egg harbor & greentree starbucks for years. as a regular, i can say that the staff here is extremely friendly and helpful. one issue you may have depends on the time of the day. at some points, high school lets out and this place can be as loud as a school cafeteria. other times, several parents decided that this starbucks is a good place to bring their children. being in washington township, they have the "my kid does no wrong" attitude and often the parents will allow their infants to cry, or their younger children to run around as if this were a daycare. it does affect the experience of a nice place to relax with a tasty beverage. the service gets a 4-star rating. the crowd sometimes kills it though.

as i sit watching while my drink is being made, i witnessed an employee restock supplies, warm up food, smash her bare hands down the garbage and proceed to making drink orders. major food violation for all to see. starbucks needs to train employees to wash hands more regularly? this consumer will not be retuning to this one.

everyone at this starbucks is nice and that is a plus. however, they are also incredibly slow. it takes them way to long to get my very simple order. i used to work for a starbucks so i can appreciate how busy they can be. i suspect they put all their attention to their drive thru and neglect the cafe. i prefer to go to the dunkin donuts next door. they are way faster and it is a much better experience.

super disappointed that i just had to sit here and wait 15 minutes for my one drink. there was a lot of people in the drive-thru and apparently they got priority. there was only one person ahead of me inside the store. and they didn't even apologize or offer me anything for having to wait for so long for one drink
this is "my" sbux. it's busy all of the time so the drive thru can take for.ev.er. i have never had a poor customer service experience besides the wait and i'm hear nearly every day.

went here today went thru the drive thru and had my grandson who is 4 months old with me. ordered 2 blacktea lemonades sweetened. drinks were not sweetened at all. had to go in to fix it and instead of making new drinks the guy opened the kids and added more sweetener. poor service

it works for moms to bring their kids to, but it isn't there for any man to do anything but be of service at. no self service coffee stations, and door codes at the bathrooms make this a rather exclusive to the mom-with-child kind of setup.

love the coffee here, love the decor even more. i've never seen a starbucks decked out in such a coffee shop-esque manner. sounds ironic, but it's true. this place gives me the feel of heart warming coffee shops sprawling up around taiwan. this location has a second story (balcony like set up) if you want more seating (or just more private seating) in case the tables on the first floor are full.

stupid me.. why did i go to this location... it's always given me problems... even the wife said, "i thought you were going to the other one..." our breakfast plans fell through, we needed coffee and we were headed home. we decided to use up a starbucks gift card and we ordered coffee and sandwiches. impressively, the sandwiches were done in seconds and we had them before we even paid. then we waited for over seven minutes and the sandwiches were cold by the time we ate them... we got our drinks and went home to eat. while drinking our drinks in the car, we noticed both of our drinks were wrong. the cashier wrote jcf, for java chip frappe on our drinks, but the person making them thought the j was a v i think... both of our drinks lacked java, but were completely vanilla beaned up... so why didn't we go back to fix the error? well, this location and parking lot is the absolute worst. once we were driving down germantown, we would need to make an illegal u-turn, then go to the mall to turn around, just to get back here. with the traffic already building, it would add twenty minutes to our trip. forget it... as a side note, i did complain online to starbucks and they gave me $15 back. we spent slightly over $20, but i'm satisfied with that resolution. we won't spend that $15 at this location. karma once again for not listening to myself or you other yelpers...
there is a reason this is one of the two locations in philly with a clover press. nice atmosphere even when it is busy. feels like a neighborhood hangout.

this location is by the high way and supposedly getting the fast service, but it is not , nobody service in the front and i know they are open ! sugar and cream station is dirty, seem no body clean the station for a while !

this starbucks has a fantastic view of the catalina and run on mountains with outdoor seating. the perfect spot to grab a coffee after a hike or walk in saguaro national park! the staff is also very friendly which is a plus. fyi - they don't carry the starbucks arizona mugs. staff recommended 22nd and harrison or speedway and wilmot.

well i'm certainly surprised today. usually there is this one barista that is super irritated and has the "i don't give a crap" attitude and for some reason always doesn't make my beverage right and is usually rude. today though, today.... mmph my latté was def good and he seemed less rude almost like he was actually trying to be nice. however he still had the unhappy face he usually wears. i always hated coming here bc the the guy barista could be really rude at times and it's like i always leave feeling super pissed off. but because it's convenient i would come here when i'm in the area. i hope things continue to get better from here...

i recently started visiting this starbucks more frequently. the employees are so friendly here! my drinks are always made correctly and always available when i arrive. (if you're not taking advantage of mobile ordering, you're doing it wrong.) just today, i ordered a drink that doesn't normally come with whipped cream. today, it came with it. i'm not allergic, just didn't want the extra 100 calories, so i asked the barista if she wouldn't mind just scraping it off for me so i wouldn't be tempted to eat it anyway. she said of course, and then before doing it said, 'you know, i'm just gonna make you a new one!' totally unnecessary, but i appreciated the sentiment. so thank you, nice starbucks employees!

i still refer to this location as the new starbucks. more room. two long rectangular tables that seat at least eight each and plenty of smaller tables. very good service and consistent high quality products. drive thru as well.

if there was a "zero star" option, we would choose it. this is probably the dirtiest starbucks we've ever patronized. from the obnoxiously loud,coughing and sneezing barista(if we get sick, we blame him!), to the filthy tables we sat at, to the other patron( a "regular", we surmise)who acquired a container from the store and proceeded to open a can of dog food and feed his dog at the table next to us~ this entire experience was disgusting.

believe it or not, this has nothing to do with the overpriced, cheap coffee. everyone knows about that. it depends. sometimes there's no line. sometimes there's a huge line. sometimes the baristas are great, and sometimes they give me a ton of attitude for requesting they put half and half in my coffee because i'm late and don't have time to do it myself. like, sorry you have to actually put effort into your job???

this has got to be the saddest location i've ever been to. we sat in line (drive thru) for over 20 minutes total. they were extremely slow! i ordered the chestnut praline frappe nonfat with whip when we finally got to the menu. waited another 5 minutes or so before getting to the window, and they handed me a hot drink. when i explained i had ordered a frozen drink, she said (very rudely might i add) "did you not order the nonfat chestnut praline?" i said "yes, it was supposed to be a frappe." she rolled her eyes and slammed the window shut, and started throwing cups around while trying to make it into a frappe. she then asked us to move forward and she'd bring it out. after waiting another 5 minutes or so, she finally brings it out, doesn't apologize. i start drinking it, and it didn't even taste like it had espresso in it. it tasted like it was just syrup, ice, and milk. i'll never come back to this location as long as i live.

i tried the new guava passion fruit drink. i love guava and it was really good except that there was very little "drink". the cup was filled to the top with ice so that it was gone in just a few sips. i now know to order with light or no ice. it was a rip off the way i got it.
starbucks is great, we know and expect that. this one is super friendly. downside - new seating arrangements are crappy cafe tables. not cozy at all anymore.

3.5 stars - mostly only for shock value. let me preface this by saying that i am not a fan of starbucks. i think their coffee is over-caffeinated and often burnt, their espresso drinks lame at best, and everything over-priced. in fact, when i am trying to decide on a cafe to go to, starbucks doesn't even make it to the list of options. however, i must admit that starbucks is extremely convenient: they are everywhere and they are open late. so, when i needed to find a place to study late tonight, i decided to go to the green hills starbucks for the first time in...maybe 2-1/2 years. so, i walked in tonight with my friend and i couldn't stop my jaw from dropping over and over again and the exclamation, "wow," from coming out of my mouth. why? because the place has gotten an extreme makeover. it's probably to do with the whole new starbucks branding/marketing goals. but, anyway, all of the furniture has been replaced with wooden counterparts and comfy couches. it has an entirely different feel: less commercial and more local and authentic. the gigantic study table with 20 electrical outlets has been replaced by benches on the wall, bar stools, and any other kind of seating you can imagine. and the outlets are now impossible to find because they have been painted the same exact brown as the wall, which led me to create an extremely awkward situation as i walked around the entire border of the room looking for a plug for my dying laptop. despite an inspiring renovation of the entire atmosphere of the place, obviously, the quality of the drinks has not improved. astonished by the new look, i inquired the barista about when they redid the place. to which she replied last year in february. embarrassed, i chuckled, took my drink, and slowly walked away. clearly, i am behind the times. anyway, great new feel and atmosphere (this one is much more social) for starbucks even though the drinks haven't changed. so, if you must resort to going to starbucks, this is the one i would recommend.

this starbucks is on my way to work, and i frequent it a few times a month. i just get coffee, "venti bold", and don't do the fancy drinks. unlike most locations, this one is an odd nexus of incompetence: 1. about half of the time i visit they are out of bold coffee. you would think they would learn to always have enough on hand, but the incompetence prevents it. they prefer to take my money, then tell me i'll have to wait for them to make some more. lesson: when patronizing this store don't pay until your coffee is in-hand. wawa is three blocks away, and keeps their urns full. 2. front counter service is the least of their worries. you know how most starbucks locations are energetic and the counter person is eager to trade caffeine for money? not this one they actually move slowly, respond slowly, and speak slowly. it can take a while before someone notices a person waiting at the counter. 3. i've never seen the general manager on-site. i think that explains a lot about this location.

i'm a starbucks fan, i just am. this one of all of them near the franklin area, is the worst. they always have not enough people working and the service is so slow. unfortunately this one is most convenient and the drive thru, who doesn't love the drive thru. only a couple times that i've been here have the staff been friendly or helpful, usually i have to check for a pulse. of all the locations nearby this one the iced coffee & espresso shots taste the most burnt, which is annoying. it's also loud. i came once to get some work done, and the speakers were so loud i had to leave. maybe that's the goal?? plus the internet is real choppy here. not my fave...

love this location! super clean and the staff is always wonderful. you're always greeted with a smile and the service is very prompt! conveniently located next to target so i can shop and stop in for breakfast, lunch or a quick drink & treat! they keep everything well stocked. though the target has a kiosk for starbucks inside, i always prefer to come here! it's also next to an urgent care and when you have an unexpected visit with one of the kids late in the day it's a nice place to stop and get a pick me up to get through the wait times.

ok obvi normally i'd dgaf about writing a review for starbucks.. but not this time my fellow yelpers. i thought this was the swanky flagship rittenhouse starbucks so my mayur c. and i went sunday to try the new fraps. we got a venti and asked if he could split it in two. literally nbd. first the cashier was like uh.... and then the barista came over and was like uhh.... i thought they were about to be like "lol jk of course we can" but nope. so awkward. then i wanted to buy a mug they were featuring at the cashier but didn't have with all the other merch so i asked her if they had some in the back. *que fiasco* she didn't know how much it is, neither did the barista, the manager was mia. meanwhile a line was forming behind me and she kicked me out of line at which point i was like yoo bye felicia, i'll get this at the other non swanky starbucks. finally after we got our two cups (barista was not having it..and fyi he refused to do the splitting of the cups for us so strange) she called me back over to buy the darn cup. wow what an experience....and not in a good way.

every single time i've been to this starbucks, i've had an amazing experience. the staff aren't just nice, they seek to make my day. and they do it every single time. they have to have amazing leadership to pull this off every time. it's the way customer service used to be.

it was decent. that's about all. they seemed to not know how to make an oatmeal or open a new carton of coconut milk. my dying son found nothing on the menu appetizing and soon after leaving had to throw up. was it the service that made him nauseous? the food? the terminal disease that's eating away at his organs? no one can be sure. one thing i can be sure of though is that i'll be visiting this starbucks again because the other option is mcdonald's coffee and that's doodoo. pure. doo. doo.

i was feeling light headed while waiting more than 5 mins for my drink. there was a chair next to a table. i sat down, the women working there told me not to sit. i told her i already ordered i'm just waiting for my drink. she rolled her eyes and said no seating! never ever coming back again!

zero star if i could. short hair girl barista with attitude made my drink wrong. simple request: no ice! she left her eyes at home without reading drink notes or doesn't like her job. look how much dragons fruits bits she put for my $5 drink. what a joke!
staff was super friendly great service! order a ice vanilla coffee and the coffee was made just right! couldn't be happier!

i am at this starbucks all the time. i work just north of downtown (between downtown and germantown. this starbucks is the nearest coffee shop i can walk to (or a coffee shop inside the farmer's market, but i prefer not to do that). it's in a hidden little area within the doubletree hotel. walk down the diagonal sidewalk thing and you'll find it. the lines can get very long (a lot of state employees frequent this location in large groups), but they're pretty fast most of the time. they also do a happy hour (between 2 and 4, i think), when it's buy one get one free on drinks. it's just a starbucks, but it's gotten me through many an exhausting day!
great and fast service. their chocolate cake pop is to die for. i can eat at least 3 a day, i'm addicted. nice and helpful staff

i love starbucks in general, but i particularly like this one. mel is knowledgeable about products and externally helpful. she is a pleasure! my only complaint is the small parking lot and narrow drive thru, but it doesn't stop us from going there.

this location is close to my house so i tend to visit a few times a week. staff is always nice and my drink is always amazing. but...they need a second register!! it seems when i visit that it is always busy and a line forms immediately. they need to keep the place a little cleaner as well. dusty and sticky on the chairs and tables (i take mine to go but i see things while waiting in line).
