small inside, one long walk has windows which takes up predominantly the whole store and makes the interior a bit warm. staff is very personable and polite the way customer service should be. service was quick and there were no lines for 10am. seems like a locals spot lots of old folks hanging outside with their dogs. i like this location
always messes mobile orders. employees don't even look like they know what they're doing. worst starbucks ever

when it's early morning (11am) and you are about to get into a full day of festing, you are gonna need a jump start. enter starbucks. we grabbed some coffee here on the way to french quarter fest, and even though they were busy, the staff got our coffee to us quickly- and with a smile! the iced coffee is as good as any starbucks. this spot is pretty small, and we were in and out so fast i didn't notice if there are places to sit, but if you are downtown for festing or shopping, this is a good place to get some brew.

i wanted to start by saying the hours here on yelp are not correct. i'm not certain what the hours are but i came through/passed by multiple times between 5-630 am on my way into work and it wasn't open. i was able to get breakfast here one morning but that was after 7 am. the line moved very quickly but their menu is severely limited. i had to settle on my 3rd breakfast option because they were out of the other 2. i got the feta wrap but it was still frozen cold in the middle which sucked. the good thing is my iced chai latte was very good but took awhile to get made. would be open to return next time i'm in town.

i love the location and they have plenty of work space, but they are so slow! i stood at the counter for two minutes before being greeted, then waited another ten for my coffee. i came in at noon on a monday, and there were only four customers inside. the drive through had a steady steam, and there were three online orders completed as well, but i still don't see how that means an inside customer should wait ten minutes for a coffee.

nice starbucks! neat location - i should say it's an unusual round building, and that makes it unique. seeing a lot of people having lunch meetings here [laptops galore] was reassuring that i had landed at a good starbucks. very quick - in and out in less than 5 minutes!
incredibly slow service, even for a starbucks. the staff here do not care. if you got through the drive thru, it's a minimum of 10-15 minute wait

this starbucks is shit. first off the line took way to long for 2 drinks. second the girl taking my order repeated it to me 7 times and still didnt get it right. then not only did my drink jist taste like water and no flavoring. the water tasted like swamp water. literal suer water. disgusting

very slow service. i write this review as i've already been sitting in the drive-thru for 15 minutes. also, they seem to always be out of trenta cups. what's up with that? this seems to be the slowest, most disorganized starbucks i've ever visited. i happen by here about once a week, and it is consistently poor.
today i ordered lemonade. there were strange stains on the lid. and on my last two visits the staff was very rude

avoid this location at all costs!! i was so excited to have a newer, updated starbucks in the area, but that excitement quickly disappeared after the first few visits. i always use the drive-tru, which is supposed to be quick and convenient... not at this location! the few times i purchased coffee at this location i spent an excessive amount of time (always 10+ minutes, once almost 20 minutes) in the drive-thru. yesterday, i was driving by and saw a very short line, and decided to give it another go. my mistake. there was no line, only one car that pulled up right before me. they quickly ordered their one drink, pulled up, and i ordered my one drink. from the time i placed my order it took 15 minutes to get to the window. the line of cars behind me was now at a whopping 9 count. the time it took to place two coffee ordered the line quadrupled in size. let me repeat that... i was the second car in line, and it took 15 minutes to receive my iced coffee. thank goodness i wasn't that 9th car in line, they're probably still waiting for their coffee. every time i come to this location... i wait... for a very long time. it's an ongoing problem that seems to not be given the attention by staff that it needs. it's a shame because it's a beautiful and convenient location.

before i asked for non fat frappuccino and no whip but they put whip cream on it and then they restarted it but ended up giving me light frappuccino which had the light syrup and tasted really bad i just threw it away. today i asked for non fat latte with no whip the same barista added whip and when i told him i don't want whip he did not do the latte again just scooped some of the whip off the top. when i asked him are you sure this has no whip cream in it he said it is a drink that has a lot of chocolate in it and a lot of fat in it but i assure you it does not have whip cream in it. i know what i'm doing and i calculate the calories of chocolate and everything else. wow lots of attitude for so much money put into a cup of coffee.
hey, guy with the braids dancing inside... when you look outside and someone is waiting, then that should be your queue to engage and provide service. not ignore me and keep dancing.

a bit chilly, they definitely keep starbucks well air-conditioned in this little spot close to 15th & locust. service was prompt and efficient with the barista and the cashier relatively friendly. bathrooms were clean. there were a couple nicer seats, but the seating in here leaves something to be desired with the little wooden tables & chairs; free at&t wifi.

this location is not far from my house so i use it as a meeting place for clients. i like it because it is brand new and there are enough places to sit. the help there is pretty good. of course the coffee is what they are known for and their little pastries.

guys, we know your busy in the morning but slow it down and get it right. ordered a white chocolate mocha and it was very bitter and 2/3 full. as a business owner i would never deliver food that is sub-par. you have one shot to get it right. you are the face of the corporation. people don't pay for that. it is part of the hospitality business. went the next morning to give them another chance... wife ordered a caramel macchiato and i ordered another white chocolate mocha (drive through). the orders came out tasting exactly the same and neither of the drinks tasted like what we ordered. took the drinks in to let the cashier know... she was quick to point out that there was a label on the drinks that said what they were :-/. i tried to be nice about it but i immediately found myself telling her i knew how to read. they did remake the drinks and they were not too pleased about it. neither were up any standard that starbucks should have and they did make sure that the drinks were not the same... but still, a customer spends $11 dollars for 2 drinks, you would think they would be of quality. if you want coffee i would recommend the gas station across the street... reduced to one star. won't be coming back.
i've never seen such fast working starbucks employees! they get the job done and move on to the next in lightening speed. wish they were this fast at my local starbucks.
just fyi for people who like to work and study in coffee shops-- this one only has one table with an outlet nearby. which sucks, it's a really nice clean environment otherwise.

multiple tries. i have given this starbucks multiple tries now, and each time i think to myself stuff happens and running such a popular business is difficult. i have given this place the benefit of the doubt multiple times now and today was the last straw. i showed up a couple minutes after 7 and ordered a sandwich and a coffee. over twenty minutes later, i walked out with a half-finished coffee and no sandwich. i'm a patient person. i really am. when i am given bad customer service at a restaurant, i give them the benefit of the doubt. but this place has disappointed me so many times. pouring an iced coffee and heating up a premade sandwich should not take 20+ minutes. i wouldn't be writing this if this were a one time incident, but the truth is that this is standard for this particular location. i've tried both drive-thru and walk-in and it doesn't seem to make a difference either way. avoid the wait, go to the starbucks up the road in the shopping center next to raley's.

best starbucks in reno. the drinks are always made right (and the baristas there make great recommendations for new drinks to try also) and they are really really fast at making the drinks for you. they're pretty nice there too.

i love starbucks coffee and not all the stores are the same. this location is nice because it does have a drive thru window if you just want to get it on the run. inside is pretty clean and kept up. not very big but there are outdoor tables if you want to sit outside. i always ask for my drink extra hot and for some reason this location never has been able to do that for me. the parking lot is not very big and is usually crowded since they do share it with other businesses. i usually park in the raleys lot and walk over

worst starbucks i have ever been to in my life. incompetent staff that just try to run you out the door. the only reason that i have continued to go this one is because it is about a block away from my house.

so far so good at this bucks. i was so excited to see another one with a drive thru...right next door to my dentist...it'll be hard to say yes to fluoride knowing that i'll want a latte or a peach green tea lemonade right after my cleaning. now i have a starbucks in every direction that i leave my home. this can't be good for me! so, you know how they say sometimes you can go to a starbucks and someone in front of you anonymously pays for your order? it happened to me for the first time at this one! it really made my day, so i ended up paying for the dude behind me to keep it going. what was cool is i was trying the everything croissant for the first time (and it was just as good as it sounds) so my order along with my go to drink (toffee nut mocha) was a little more than my normal spend of no more than $5. i've only been in the drive thru, but already my service has been fantastic. i can't wait to go spend a little time inside when i'm not running around as it looks like a big one.

i got to starbucks at 6:20 and they were not open yet. i asked the worker who was smoking outside at what time they opened and he said around 6:30 or 7 implying that they did not have a set time for opening and closing. when i have gotten there and it is opened. the overweight white man with a scruff has been ridiculously rude. rolling his eyes at customers including myself. so sad to feel uncomfortable at my local starbucks!!! i think that he's the manager. if you don't enjoy people giving you attitude, avoid this place!
great place to have a sbux coffee but only if you are being served by susan,dana or christie...
place is cute, but there's a rude older woman that works here. everyone else seemed nice though!

i really like this starbucks. everyone is generally very friendly and the drinks are always good. my only real qualm is drive through speed can be a little slow. all in all, it's still my favorite sbux in the area.

as a former starbucks partner (not this locations, another) i was extremely disappointed. there was a line forming and four baristas all making drinks?? seemed unnecessary. then i saw a male in a gray thermal and maroon beanie come out of the back, start taking orders and making drinks no hat no apron, i can only hope he washed his hands. the eggnog lattes my boyfriend and i ordered seemed a little off and the pastries we had warmed didn't feel warm at all. i'm a true starbucks fan and rarely have a bad experience... very sad.
a typical starbucks, however less stars due to the lack of comfortable, cozy seating.

as a location, this starbucks is difficult to say the least. the line for the drive thru is frequently wrapped all the way around the store and maybe two or three cars waiting in line on 4th street as well. this is particularly the case in the morning when their can easily be 15 cars waiting. the drive through line also makes it very difficult to park in the lot so you can't app order and pick up easily as well. there is overflow parking behind the gas station next to it. inside, this is an amazingly comfortable coffee shop. it is clean, wide open, and very accessible. despite the volume of customers, they never seem to run out of what i'm looking for. customer service has always been great here for me as well! starbucks coffee will certainly scratch the itch but it's nothing to write home about. it's good but it doesn't blow you away.

this location has just tanked over the past few years. to the point of where many of the batistas just don't even bother to read what you want on your drink order. i get the same thing every time, vento cold brew, light ice. if i mobile order, i am guaranteed to get almost all ice. if i have time i order in line and ask the barista at the register if they can put just half a small scoop of ice in to try and get my drink order right. a few of the good ones know now (shout out to omar) and will do it without me having to ask. the real problem is watching the baristas operate they are models of inefficiency. this surprises me as you think starbucks have this nailed down. watch them make drinks, there is so much wasted movement. some of it has to do wit the layout but others just don't make sense. the perfect example of this is the ice especially in cold brew. the set the cup down on the counter, then someone takes it over to the tap, pours in the cold brew, then takes it over to the ice, then takes the coffee and ice scoop over to the sink and pours in the ice scoop into the drink. most times the coffee spills, or they actually pour some coffee out so they can fit that heaping big scoop of ice in (oh i forgot to read your cup said light ice). then they take it to the bar and it waits for someone to put a lid on it before calling it out. making some simple changes to this could get the drinks out so much faster with less waste. i will end my rant now with hopes that starbucks can make some changes here to help make this location great again.

my brother and i went here and our coffee was just okay. i had better a different locations. it was almost empty inside but the guy making our coffees was taking a very long time. then a lady he was friends with came in and he took even longer to make them. he forgot to put the vanilla syrup in one coffee but corrected this. he also almost spilt the second one. we probably won't be returning to this location.
great starbucks. i got a chile mocha in the rain. the barista was friendly and said i had good taste for ordering the chile mocha. the drive thru was busy but moved quickly.
first time here yesterday. wasn't busy at all, friendly staff, & quick service. one of the best starbucks i've ever been to. service with a smile, that's the way it should be and the way it is here!

where's the flatware? the only "cutlery" this starbucks has is plastic. and they don't have plates. when i asked for one they had to pull a demi from the display case. i broke my plastic fork on the muffin and then spotted my dress shirt when i had to eat the stale breakfast sandwich by hand. is this where the industry leader is taking us? not to be grumpy (i seem to be writing a lot of sub-3 ratings lately) but this starbucks is located in a business hotel. we bleary eyed patrons in $1,000 worth of dress wear are dodging grease dribbles before meeting clients. (thankfully i remembered to blot not rub!)

this starbucks has always been the worst. i don't know why i keep coming back. i think they are going to change, but they don't. why don't i like it, it's because it will easily take 20 mins to go through the drive thru. i get they may be understaffed on occasion, but are they always understaffed? if so, can these people get some help?! please?! some other locations kill it with their speed and maybe i've gotten spoiled. but spending 20 mins + in line is ridiculous. if they apologized at the window and said sorry for your wait, i'd still be mad, but less for sure. i just want to drive off, but i feel like that's a crappy thing to do. one time i ordered a breakfast wrap from here and i waited so long, my sandwich was cold by the time i got to the window. i asked the guy if they heated it up and he laugh/smirked and was like "yah, want me to do it again". yes, actually i do, but i just waited in this shitshow of a line... i'll pass.
service isn't as efficient at the marsh road location, but it works.

starbucks is my go to caffeine fix in the morning! while i never really have complaints about any starbucks, this is my favorite one to go to. the staff is super friendly and everything is always very clean! it can get a bit crowded during the rush hours, but nothing unbearable... and besides, that's what ordering from the app is for, right??
super friendly service, some of the staff even knows my name by now. :) also, they're great at offering suggestions for when i want to change up my usual order and try something new.

they sold me not one, but two moldy protein boxes and that's supposed to be a mango dragonfruit drink. "we're out of the dragon fruit but it's just the same stuff." server said. but if i'm paying for a $5 drink, i want the fruit. and i want my overpriced apples to be edible.
the workers at this starbucks are so rude . never seen such a bunch of unhappy people. they mess up the order and then give you an attitude.
they are super nice and fast! just stopped in and the baristas are sweet, clean store and fast service! drinks made perfect. ali was a sweetheart.

the workers at this starbucks are always very kind and they make my drinks very quickly. that's perfect for me since i'm usually running late (sorry i run on asian time :x). overall, great service, great location, and great drinks. they've never messed up any of my drinks. my kinda place!

great staff and service. this one is new as of 12/16, so they are still getting into the routine. plenty of room to sit down and work or chat. the drive-thru is surprisingly convenient, but just remember to use the inside lane, since the outer is for the bank. yes, the door is shared with the bank, not sure why others are shorting 2 stars for laziness. oh, and there are a couple soft chairs and the nice high tables.

stopping in right before i headed to my daughters game to get an easy fav carmel frap! the staff is always so welcoming and friendly. i'd definitely recommend this starbucks!! thank you guys sooo much
worst cup of coffee ever. tastes like it sat in the pot for 6 hours. if you're going to have coffee on brew, please make sure it's fresh. this is gross.

this starbucks is closest to me. i gave it one star already because of the cold food and even cold coffee. i'm usually in a rush and i always go via the drive-thru. my husband wanted a breakfast sandwich. i regrettably gave it another try this morning. i didn't bother ordering a coffee, but two breakfast sandwiches. as usual. sandwiches are warm to hot on the outside... cold.. not cool, but cold on the inside. my son has worked at starbucks for - 2-3 years. never at this location. he told me they must be warming the sandwiches straight from the freezer. he said that at the stores he has worked at they take out the sandwiches out of the freezer one day ahead and put them in the refrigerator. that way they are not frozen when heated. i have on a few occasions spoken to the folks at this store about the cold sandwiches. i was advised to order them "open faced" to make sure they were heated thoroughly. but it just doesn't matter. either they really are hard frozen to start with or they are ignoring my request to warm them open-faced. i have no idea. luckily this morning i was going back to my office and i heated the sandwiches in our microwave. but i hate it when i'm on the road and i have to throw it out! well, i gave it another try... for sure, no more.. too bad. the staff is very friendly.

this is my most frequented starbucks due to the fact that it is close to my office, but i also really like the staff! everyone who has helped me has been extremely kind! i'm that girl who is known to have full on conversations with the staff members when the restaurant is not too busy! my only "complaint" is that they use stickers to mark your order on your cup rather than in hand writing with a sharpie! the sharpie makes me feel special for some reason. haha!

not bad service, just weird. it's a pleasant place to relax and get some work done. the staff is just very self-involved. i don't want to hear about your weekend construction and trips to the library. just a coffee.
loved this starbucks!!!!! not only did i have one of the best caramel macchiato there but the trumpet chandelier was beautiful. definitely selfie worthy.

the absolute slowest starbucks i've ever been to. saw 4 people on shift but only 1 doing orders and 1 making drinks with 10 people in line. they would make 1-2 drinks and then disappear into the back room. horrible. would not recommend.

there's always a long line wrapping around the building, because the baristas like to screw around instead of doing their job. i know this for a fact, because we go inside instead of waiting in the drivethru. it needs to be fixed.
it's a almost starbucks in a target store. it's loud dirty it definitely does not have the standards of a real starbucks

i was a barista at one of the tucson coffee shops with a cult following, so usually starbucks is just average for me. but this starbucks truly breaks the mold. they're incredible! one of the baristas is such a ham and really made my day. i don't know his name... tall with curly hair. even now as i write this review he is checking in with his regulars about their kids and weekend golf games. the other baristas are also very kind. and my drink is delicious and at a perfect 140 degrees (not my request, i'm not one of those customers). i just moved to a condo near this starbucks and i'll will definitely be adding this one to my weekly routine.

thoroughly impressed how the three workers busted through a continuous line of 30+ customers this morning. there line of people is intimidating but actually moved very quickly. only waited a couple minutes for my coffee to be made.
smallish location partnered with a bank on the interior. everyone was friendly. nothing spectacular here but the promo of extra stars for buying a baked good. 3 stars for a small coffee and brownie!

it's starbucks so the coffee, etc is fine. but it has the worst service i've ever experienced at a starbucks. you need new management. average wait time is 15 mins, no matter what time of day. best example i wrote this waiting for a coffee at the drive through on one hand.

this a very friendly and clean starbucks. my favorite one close to me and have always had great service and always greeted every morning by name. parking is difficult in the small lot right in front of the entrance, but i park in the main parking lot on the side and have had no problems. no drive through available otherwise one of the better starbucks in town.

love my large sugar free cinnamon dolce, soy latte .... these are great. every few though you will find one that is more bitter not sure why, but sometimes it's not perfect. i like the drive thru ability... so i come back.

horrible. this is my review based on the last 2 weeks. 1st order: my order was spot on. 2nd order: they forgot the raspberries syrup i ask for. 3rd order: they gave me someone else's order. 4th: i thought i was ordering something simple, but they didn't get it. i

at this starbucks the baristas are not only efficient but friendly! also, props for starting neat initiatives--such as their "grounds for your garden." they allow you to take coffee grounds for your garden-for free! way to go starbucks!

this store is understaffed and needs to stop the online orders. while those of us present in the store waited an extremely long time on our drinks the one barista made 10 or 12 different orders for those ordering ahead. there was one person working the cash and one barista. being located in a busy mall spot that also contains a huge hotel required more staff or stopping the online ordering. after asking if there was any bottled water the response was, "there is none out there?" no, there is none out here. cash person asked the barista if there was any bottled water to which the barista said, "there's no bottled water?" oh, we are out. well, glad we have that settled! but why on earth were they out of water? this location need lots of improvement.

order a venti non fat latte and i got one with some type of sweetener in it which i tried to drink because i was on a tight schedule and had already left the area. my stomach couldn't handle the sweetener so i took it back and explained what happened they opened it said well you drank enough of it never apologized made me a tall and i took of sip of that it tastes strong and not like a latte for this price i expect the consistency i usually get and would it kill them to apologize for the inconvenience

after 25 minutes of waiting in line, the 3 staff members forgot to make our drinks. the head barista, arumi was very rude while talking to her cousins that had just walked in. instead of making our drinks, after i reminded her of our order, arumi proceeded to introduce her family to the rest of the barista staff. there were 12 people in line to order and that did not affect any pace of the starbucks 5 points staff. several people waiting in line began to comment on the poor service. i will not come back to this location ever again. horrible customer service.

giving this place 5 stars. i can't believe people are really bitching about the "quality control of breakfast sandwiches." "my sandwich didn't have enough turkey bacon." get over yourself. it's starbucks. this place isn't competing for a michelin star. the people are nice, the service is prompt, the goods are as expected, and the place is clean. don't give it one star because your pumpkin spice latte had too much foam on the top. bitch please. ya'll giving starbucks bad reviews on yelp like ya'll won't be back tomorrow ordering the same basic af items. smh.
horrible service! make sure that you get in their face because the like to pretend you are not there. too rude to even acknowledge your existence.

the bathroom is worst that i have ever seen. the last time it was cleaned was 2013. starbucks should ashamed. it was just not one person. you would not want to use it. let alone have your children use it.
why the hell has it taken 20+ minutes each time i've ordered today when it's not that busy? this is ridiculous and i will never come here again.
maybe it's because i love sb, but this starbucks rates higher for me than most others. it's in a great location, friendly service and clean.

idk. any joy i get from visiting this starbucks diminishes at the annoying light that takes forever wish i could chose a different way out, but not so. the store is nice and quick, an friendly, everything i enjoy about the brand, its just i don't want to visit because of the hassle to get out of there. what this intersection needs is a pretty round about. that way everyone keeps moving and doesn't have to wait forever at that damn light. maybe i'll go again and try to get out another way. ughhh. im getting old !!!
great starbucks! the baristas are very nice and the place is very modern and clean. mickayla was so sweet and even brought us our drinks to where we were sitting.

early flight means starbucks coffee. myself and my co-worker got drinks and breakfast sandwiches. everything was really good and the staff was friendly and efficient. no complaints. i'll be back next time i fly.

this starbucks is a hit or miss. sometimes i get the most amazing beverage and sometimes it's way off mark. today i asked for an iced caramel macchiato stirred and it tasted like milk with caramel flavor and came unstirred. *smh* :-( i would have asked for them to make me up a new one but i was worried that it would be extremely heavy on the espresso. so i just stuck with it plus i was to lazy to ask. so my bad i guess but if you pay $5 for designer coffee shouldn't it be perfect??
never disappointed in starbucks, and i especially like the blended tea's that they have. y-u-m!

this starbucks in horsham is staffed by friendly baristas who aim to please and you do not get too much of the upsell (dont you want something to eat today... ) schtick. the drive through window is convenient but it is a pain to get in and outof the parking lot due to the traffic. nothing special but nothing wrong with this location.

this location usually has an extremely long line but thanks to being early wait time was relatively short. all the folks behind the counter were friendly and energetic but they don't have any breakfast sandwiches.

as soon as i walked in every staff member that made eye contact with me gave me a warm hello. i was actually having a little trouble with my starbucks app today and they were very patient and helpful with that. my bagel came out absolutely perfectly toasted and my frappaccino came out great as well. i would definitely recommend this starbucks.

i'm not normally up in this area so it was probably my first and last time coming here. no complaints about this establishment. it was clean, had quick service, and my coffee and scone were delightful. just about what you'd expect out of any starbucks.
still not sure why a 3 star rating overall. they are still a 5 from me. the drinks are always awesome and we get great service. got the mocha cookie crumble frap this time. so good

date night, apparently there are 2 locations in oldsmar and i was at one while the beau was at another. while i waited for him to join me at this location, i ordered my hot chai tea. jonah was absolutely wonderful. he was personable and friendly and exceptionally warm. we chatted about how our names are different (my alternate id) and how we don't often hear them, though mine is growing popular due to anime. once my order was placed, it took only a few minutes to have it in my hands so i could wait for bruce to join me. i'd come back.

this location needs a couple improvements. the layout is poorly designed. it is very crowded. they could also use an extra barista. i only saw two working. was everyone in the back room on break? the place was packed and tyler was busting his tail making the drinks. he was very pleasant doing his job. the restroom was pretty dirty, i thought i was in a hollywood starbucks. the floor could use some attention. management needs to have enough workers to keep the place clean. the two that were working were busting butt and doing a good job.

i'm sorry; i know it's still covid and they're a new franchise who may be training more folks, but this starbucks is the worst i have ever experienced in town. the people are so nice, but there is a massive disconnect between ordering and receiving your coffee/drinks. even the busiest shops in franklin/cool springs turn around customers quicker with double the crowd. we've tried to order in-store, online pickup, drive thru - every option comes with a minimum of a 15-20 minute wait as they all run around and forget who's order is actually finished and who's needs making. really want to like this spot, but with other coffee options nearby, can't see us keeping up with a place that holds no value on decent service. hope it improves!

well sorry to say that the staff isn't as friendly or patient as other locations. the refrigerated cases are dirty saw flies walking around it could be from the door being opened often but my suggestion would be get a closed case. coffee was good so hope to see it improve.

i'm not sure what happened, but this starbucks used to suck majorly. i used to wait sometimes 15 minutes in the drive thru with 3 cars in front of me! and, if i went inside, i used to feel like i was intruding on the staff - the attitude was sort of like "oh crap, another damn customer". now, however, something has changed. i went there today after lunch at aroy thai and the staff was friendly, fast, and great! i actually felt welcomed to enter the store, rather than loathed, as before! and, the service was quick. i felt compelled to hang out with the fun staff, rather than grab my drink and run the hell away, as before! i am a renewed fan and can't wait to order my next quad tall cappuccino! yousa!

this is not my favorite spot, but it serves its purpose. the crew are fast, friendly, and efficient. i do not hesitate stopping here. again, it can be hairy getting in and out at busier times of the day.

awful customer service. i walked in to grab a cup of faucet water just to take my medication with and the barista (cameron s. ) was very rude and told me to get in line. that day the line was long and since i did not need to pay for the water i did not see the need to wait in a huge line while they can just grab a cup and run it under the faucet. instead i had to wait in line for no reason. won't be back to this location again.
not a real sb, no rewards so i don't understand how they have a storefront in a downtown metro area? no wifi and they screwed up my drink, nice work! won't be back.

i just wanted to say how much i absolutely love this starbucks. we drive 15 minutes to come here all the time and study. every barista is amazing! i wanted to give this review because of the service i got today. they accidentally made me a hot latte instead of iced but one of the baristas made me a second one and then gave me both!! he was super nice about it. love this starbucks
i usually don't like starbucks, but this one has fat, friendly service and a big outdoor patio with comfortable furniture.

calm down people it's just starbucks. can't believes people are writing 2000 word reviews on starbucks it's your typically starbucks, normally i don't review starbucks but i was here and the experience was like any other starbucks. starbucks.

i purchased a acai strawberry refresher and it was amazing. besides that i wasn't that impressed with the barista, he looked annoyed when i placed my order. i had to changed my order, and it was only one drink, he sighed and seemed annoyed. it's completely understandable to want to change an order, and an annoyed response shouldn't have happened. otherwise, great drink!

i was starving and needed coffee desperately when i stopped by this location. it was a cold day outside so needless to say all the indoor seating was taken. the barista was very friendly and was quick about getting my coffee started. my spinach wrap was a different story. it seemed to take awhile and when they said it was ready, it was a breakfast sandwich. apparently another customer had taken my wrap. the barista apologized and started another wrap for me. they served it as soon as it was ready, which meant it was super hot. the taste was good though. not my favorite starbucks location, but not bad.

i usually use the mobile app for this location because of the parking lot (not their fault) and the traffic. i walked in and it was busy with very few employees. 3 for a morning does not seem like enough. anyways, i am standing at the pick-up counter and the girl yells out my name, sounding confused which is acceptable you don't know many customers. she tells me they do not have coconut milk and if soy is ok. i am not allowed to really drink soy, but i already paid over $5 for my drink that was supposed to be coconut milk. so i said yes, and the guy who received my cup looked at the order, very confused. i am thinking that he must be new. i watch him make my drink because on my order it is a grande green tea latte with coconut milk light ice. no where on the order did i put sweetener. what does he do at the very end, he adds sweetener. i was mad and irritated since my drink was not ready, it was completely wrong, and making a scene early in the morning will just ruin everyone's day. so i took the drink and left. i despise this location because i have to tell them my order when i walk, two times so they do not mess it up. i hate that it is the closest one to my work.

this is by far one of the worst starbucks in tucson. the drive thru line is always ridiculously long so i go inside to order, which is not much better. for the past month i've been ordering using the app in an attempt to not spend 30 minutes in line. however, 4/5 mobile orders have been disastrous. i live 5 minutes or less away and order 15 minutes before i leave my house on the way to work and my orders still aren't ready when i get there. they've not received my order 2 times even though they were paid for using the app. one time they didn't have the ingredients for the order and had to make me something else and still charged me for the more expensive item i ordered on the app. i've finally decided it's not worth it. i'll just leave earlier and go to the starbucks on park before work.

this airport starbucks although pretty to see have the most limited option for product selections. they have only a few drink container. for someone who been going around and don't have time to stop at a starbucks to get a souvenir mug, this is awfully disappointing because i been to crappier airport starbucks with wide range of selection. i want to write this review in hopes that they will read it and that they will increase in their selections. other than that, there's not much difference in airport starbucks. just disappointing souvenir/product selections.

first of all i love starbucks. my husband and i were looking for coffee the day we left reno. we figured any starbucks is the same. i was disappointed with this location and had to wait over 25 minutes for two coffees! the way it was set up it was awkward to say the least. it seemed we had to push chairs out of the way to walk out....the one and only door we had to enter. the workers were friendly enough but it just seem to take forever and was awkward.
got my order all wrong. not the first time at this location. spend the extra minutes and save the extra money and go to dutch bros

this review regards this location, not the amazing food and drinks. this starbucks is a local hangout for high schools, like baldwin and shipley. i think that everyone agrees that their parking lot is atrocious. it is impossible to find parking, especially in the morning. it is always hard to sit down and some on the seating in the left side of the starbucks is really weird (there are two big tables that are simply awkward). some of the baristas there is super friendly, but it isn't rare that i get a non-attentive barista.

one of the best things that this starbucks has going for it is its drive through and friendly service. it's fast and efficient. depending on how crowded it gets, the line can get ridiculous because of how the traffic can flow into the strip mall that this starbucks is located in, which can be a pain to get into if you happen to be on the opposite side of the street and trying to cross traffic. if you chose to go inside, parking can be tight, especially during the the morning. if there is no parking in front, you can park behind the store itself and walk a little. the inside has a few tables to sit, but space is at a premium, especially when crowded. the line for orders can sometime merge together. when the weather is nice, there is a small patio with a few tables and chairs that allow you to watch pendleton pike traffic while enjoying your caffeine.
they never have bananas...clearly the manager here has no clue how to stick their store.

everytime i come to this starbucks, the drive thru wait time is insanely long. even when there are only a couple of cars in line. i know this is a new location, but get it together. let's get this drive thru moving faster!

i have to say wow! andres (rose gold hair) is absolutely amazing. i visit here often. probably twice or three times a week. i usually am lucky enough to have andres at drive thru window and he just has the brightest personality and can turn a terrible day into the best day! makes my drinks the best and has never messed up any order of mine. he's so friendly every time i stop by. he's truly one of the reasons i continue to come to this location.

this starbucks has great employees especially trevor, jonathan, and patti but i'm not sure if she still works there. these people are great, they are easy to talk to and they are super friendly and they get your order right! i defiantly recommend going to this starbucks for there great service and hard working employees. they deserve a raise!

okay location compared to newtown and other locations. baristas are rude, and that is my only complaint about this starbucks. drinks are good. if you don't care about service, you shouldn't have a problem going here.

baristas work hard as this location is always slammed with customers. i used to be able to put a mobile order in and count on it being ready when i got there. i don't know what happened in the last few weeks but every time i have put a mobile order in i've had to wait up to 20 minutes in the lobby for my order. so i'm taking a break from starbucks for awhile. it's really disappointing.

i live in irvington so i may be a bit biased, but i am in-love with this starbucks. they are always fast and friendly. they somehow make the drinks better. they always add extra syrup or whatever they can add. i love the location and the atmosphere. if i ever leave irvington, i might just travel here to get some starbucks because no other place compares. haha.

i like this starbucks; as others have said it's true some of the barista's can be slightly slow moving. i've been here about 3 x's only, though so i may not be completely right about that. i think i've ordered frappacinnos, only, which i always thought were well made. there's a parking lot. that's a nice thing as street parking is metered. ^off topic: there's a ups & zesto pizza in this retail fixture. i've been to the pizzeria only once but really liked it! got 3 slices. nice portion sizes! so much took third one home.

this is the new starbucks that just opened on vista on the end of the vista village shopping center. i almost drove by it but saw that it was open today. i stopped in and got the white chocolate mocha for me and a caramel ribbon crunch frappacinno for the hubby. great service and same great coffee that i expect from starbucks! great location! my mornings just got easier!

thank god for this starbucks vs the sheldon linebaugh. yes its still starbucks, yes it can be $$$, but the people here work hard and are always friendly. this team has it together vs sheldon and linebaugh

coming through the drive thru i began to have a very nice conversation with the barista that was working the drive thru we were making jokes and just enjoying the conversation, i jokingly said don't burn my coffee and she immediately became defensive and an awkward silence began. and when my coffee finally arrived....it was burnt

good morning yelpers. nothing too long today. only that this location is super busy from about 7:45am to 8:30 so if you have somewhere to be at a certain time get there early or use the mobile application. otherwise the customer service is. great. despite he busyness they still make your coffee with love and care. thank you starbucks staff

today i had the worst experience of my life inside starbucks in the main street location in carmel thanks to one of your employees (i don't know her name, i only know what the girl with short hair is) i only recorded a four second boomerang video and the employee was not kind enough to approach me and ask me not to do that, she yelled at me from her work station that what i was doing was illegal and that she would call the police (i felt humiliated since i am mexican and only i am a tourist here) i do not think i did anything wrong or at least i did not know that it was something wrong, she rushed to carry out my order and escorted me to the exit as if i were a criminal, never in my life had i i felt so humiliated since all the people looked at me in amazement as if i was the worst of people. i didn't even have time to defend myself and it was very unfair...

if this place didn't sell starbucks products and have the starbucks logo all over the place, i'd confuse it for a local, neighborhood coffee shop. the building itself is pretty small and there isn't much seating space, but it's cozy. it's my place for a coffee treat so i'm not sure what's like during the work rush, but i've never had to wait too long to place my order or receive my drink. the sugar area is always clean. not sure how this place will hold up once all the new granary residents are moved in and there's a whole new set of foot traffic, but for now it's a pleasant experience.

at 10:30 the drive thru was packed and slow. the employee working the drive thru was slow and i had to ask for stir sticks/stoppers for the 2 drinks. that was odd, but not too bad. what was bad you ask? having to ask for my change. i handed her my cash and was expecting almost an entire dollar in change. she took my money and said "thank you. have a nice day." my response, after starting at her for a few seconds... "may i have my change?" confused look from her.... "uhm... yeah. uhm... how much was it again?" i don't know if you expected the change as a tip or just had a brain fart, but seriously... give me my change. you didn't deserve a tip after making me wait as you screwed up my order, took your time on my order and then neglected to give me change. i'll skip a return to this location... at any time of the day.

i live and work around several starbucks and although it may be slightly out of my way at the time, i will always come to the starbucks on 5520 calgary trail. bren, the barista, is an absolute treat. i feel working at a coffee shop you need to be in a positive mood dealing with grumpy bears before their morning coffee and bren really sets the bar. he takes the time to get to know you and always calls me 'friend' which i find makes it more personal! haha... my coffee is always done proper and in a timely manner. not an overly busy coffee shop either so to sit down and get work done isn't much of an issue.

i love this starbucks in the heart of westchase. the staff is always very friendly and the ambiance really gives you a feel of the neighborhood. the outdoor seating area is pet-friendly so i often see owners with the cute dogs outside mingling on the weekends. there is parking within the complex and it's also friendly for those jogging or exercising in the area.

great staff and this place saved me with the wifi. i stayed at the sheraton down the street which has awful internet service. great staff + fantastic coffee + reliable wifi = phenomenal i'm not a big "chain" person but i continue to adore my starbucks!

daniel took my order and he was absolutely amazing! the customers in front of us were unsure of what kind of drink they previously had that they enjoyed. it was a lady and her daughter, the lady spoke spanish only. however, daniel did his best with the help of the customers' daughter translating for him to craft a drink he thought she would enjoy. both customers left happy and smiling. daniel then took my order and managed to balance making five drinks over three transactions, while helping as a cash out register for non-starbucks items, and he even had some delicious looking strawberry samples ready for people to try. oh, and he did this entirely solo! he did all this without even breaking a sweat, and had the brightest smile i've seen on anybody. many words can come to mind to describe daniel (can you say, "employee of the month"?) but rockstar is what comes to my mind. keep on spreading those smiles, daniel, i appreciate you.

i'm not a coffee drinker, so i can't speak to the quality of the coffee here, but stop here often with my husband who loves coffee any hour of the day or night! starbucks has an offer going on right now where if you take in an empty bag of their ground coffee (which we use at home), they give you a free cup of coffee. downside to this location is it can be difficult at times to pull in and out of the parking spaces as it is on the corner spot of a strip mall, and traffic can get very busy at times.
they took away all of their indoor seating. that really too bad. options were important and their window seating was a good thing.

very frustrating waiting for my first cup of coffee in the morning and all 4 ladies behind the starbucks counter in terminal a are more interested in talking to each other than doing their job. once my coffee was finally made the barista didn't even make an attempt to give it to me. she set it on the counter as close to the edge closest to her as possible and then turned away to continue her conversation. i hope they don't actually get too busy this morning because folks could be waiting a while for their coffee and if you're one of those people that need your first cup pronto then you may want to pick it up somewhere else. thankfully i am not! the upside? i am sitting here now in the airport enjoying my caramel machiatto and cinnamon bagel. yum!

pricey airport starbucks sitrep they have the basic level 2 starbucks products & service available at this location. the setup i am not a fan of a starbucks - whether it be in an airport or a stadium, etc. - that uses the location as an excuse to charge up to 35%-50% more for their product. paid nearly $3 more for the venti blonde & sausage breakfast sandwich i could have gotten for less at my home starbucks. the lowdown they got most of the goods you want at a starbucks, but they will make you pay for the "privilege" of being in an airport.
i purchased a starbucks from this poorly ran store asked for a water cup she refused because this is not a corporate location poorly run don't use this starbucks.

while i don't go to starbucks often, this is still a convenient one for usf/new tampa folks. (the one near the va on bruce b. downs has an inclined plane entrance and the parking is limited and congested.) when entering the plaza that houses this sb, be careful. if coming from amc highwoods 20, there is a y shaped road. unfortunately, people love to speed down this road and there is high probability for an accident. i usually go on the right side and twist around the back. i find its best to park near the title company and walk past orange theory to get to the main entrance. this sb has a lot of outdoor seating if that's your thing. the interior seating is adequate, but if you want an outlet you're going to have to come early. the interior is clean and the employees are friendly. sometimes the line can be long but it moves pretty fast. the baristas are pretty consistent with the drink quality and consistency here. they have the standard free wifi as well as bottled drinks,pastries, and small sandwiches. the negatives are price (a venti salted caramel mocha is $5.82 with tax. ouch). also, this is one of the coldest sb i have been to. in the summer months , its nice. but any other time, the a/c is a bit much especially if you're staying and doing work or meeting someone -basically anything longer than 30 minutes. i am a big guy who loves cold but if i'm saying its too cold, its cold. i would recommend bringing a sweater for ladies or hypothyroid sufferers.

the new iced teas that they have here are deeeeeelish! here i am sipping on a venti mango black tea lemonade as i type this. the price is rather reasonable, and the tea is not too sweet. i am also a fan of the pretzel. must get it warmed up for the added bonus. i have never been a fan of the pumpkin spice craze, but thumbs up to all that support it! within walking distance, it's nice to have variety.

this is my very favorite starbucks. they consistently make the best chai lattes. it's amazing how much the taste can change from store to store. they are super friendly and even when it's hopping they are pretty speedy. highly recommend!!!

if i could give this location 0 stars i would. i keep giving them a chance but this is definitely my last they are ridiculous with drive thru time we are waiting sometimes at least 5 min! i used to work in fastfood and we would always try to get our food out in under 2min but not this location like today i waited 5 min for a tall green tea frapp i usually wouldnt even bother to review but today was ridiculous and i'm livid. anyhow there are better starbucks and dds to go to other than this one.

this is a really good starbucks. i love that it has a drive though and carries nitro cold brew (which i am addicted too). i see danny almost every time i visit and he always remembers me which really makes my day! the only downside is that this place is usually packed so the wait can be a little longer.
i like starbucks. despite my hatred of chains. they seem to always get it right, they are friendly, and well, that's really all i expect out of my coffee place.

i am out of carrots. i am out of sticks. this location has time and again proven that they are utterly incapable of producing the most basic of coffee drinks. i order an "iced coffee with cream, sugar, and a shot of espresso" every time. they repeat the order back correctly to me. yet somehow they manage to omit the sweetener on an infuriatingly regular basis. i do not like the taste of their coffee, which is the whole reason i get an iced coffee with sugar so that it shocks my taste buds and i am blessed to drink a tasteless, sugary caffeinated drink. the lack of sugar makes the drink absolutely horrid and i cannot understand why i keep thinking they'll get it right this time. i keep giving them the benefit of the doubt but not anymore. not again. i am dumbfounded. bewildered. utterly aghast at the surgeon like precision in which they dupe me into thinking they did it right this time. it is unfathomable and inexcusable and i will never make the mistake again.

although there are several starbucks locations around the country that are open 24 hours now, this location will always be the original "24 hour starbucks" to me. located conveniently right off of the i-4 at the thonotosassa exit, this place is a must stop if you are making that typical trek from orlando to tampa or vice versa. why, you ask? well, because there is delicious coffee and tea, clean restrooms, and delectable pastries. really, what more could you ask for? there is even a drive-thru for added convenience! the size of the starbucks is ideal--room for casual seating and lounging, but not so huge that it's overcrowded and crazy. i think the drive-thru option helps prevent that. also, the location right off of the i-4 (note the giant starbucks sign over the highway), makes it easy to pull in and out of. i've never experienced any problems here, and also i associate it with roadtripping across florida, so...5 stars!
anybody starbucks starts at a very mediocre position, however this one consistently delivers better quality and customer service as opposed to some of the other tampa bay locations.

a starbucks is a starbucks is a starbucks. there's a reason these places are so popular, and it's not because they each exhibit a sense of individuality. walk into a starbucks on the south side, like this one, and your experience will most likely be the same as walking into one on the west end or north side. i rather like the feeling; when i'm going for coffee, i want to be able to relax in a comfortable, familiar setting. i'm also currently addicted to these places, since they brought back the holiday special beverages. yum! unfortunately, this one isn't all that great for people watching, as it's right in the middle of a shopping mall parking lot. ah, well - can't win them all.
i've never had any issues at this starbucks. they are always friendly and i never wait long for my drinks. at the end of the day, it's starbucks

so me and my son were at this morning and as we were picking up his order this young lady was slightly rude and threw his straw on the counter and we left so i will be contacting the store manager to let them know what happened .
i'm sorry but the staff here is just rude. i'll just go to the one down the street next time because they are way nicer. guess i should have known going to a starbucks inside of a target

i have been to many starbucks coffee places in many states and i loved the familiar smell when you first walk in. my drink was perfect. there was some nice out door seating. i was disappointed at the very few selection of coffee mugs and also that the table was pushed directly up to the shelve where you had to interrupt people sitting to browse. there is a refrigerated section that takes up the rest of the wall and gives this location a super market feel. tucson is over 100 degrees out and most of the seating is outside. it's just not a relaxing atmosphere. i worked at a much nicer location several years ago and the retail selection was just as important as the drinks. i'm happy that they have the classics still!
waited over 20 min to get an iced coffee and over 40 min to get a breakfast sandwich. they definitely prioritize the drive-through, would not come back to this location.
i've been standing for several minutes and no one has got to us. there's multiple employees standing there doing nothing. will not go here again. don't support this location.

they've managed to make three mistakes on a single drink order. however! they are extremely kind, to me, and from my observation, to some difficult customers they must handle. a great spot to enjoy a beverage and focus on computer work.
avoid this location. they will mess up your order each and and every time. there's a starbucks 3 minutes away in the target complex, go there instead.

ordered coffee on the mobile app as i was walking from the tattoo place on church street. the place was packed with people when i got there but no one was in line and right as i walked in the barista places my order on the counter. 5 stars for the speedy drink making. i can't rate the customer service since i didn't actually interact with anyone but i'm satisfied with not having to wait for my coffee.
i go to this starbucks almost twice a week and they are so awesome. the customer service is wonderful and they are always always super friendly.

drive thru is not slow which is a game changer! lol the girl was so nice that when she saw my dog she offer us a puppuccino without even asking! great service and nice location. loving their peppermint mocha latte!

since when does a frappuccino come with zero shots of espresso, forcing you to pay almost $3 to add coffee to your coffee drink?! two drinks took 10 minutes to make and one of them was just a drip coffee that i had to ask for after paying & getting my bar drink. not only that, but the "service" is straight up surly. while i was there (all 20+ minutes with only 2 people in front of me) i saw 2 women trying to collect their pre-order have to go through the whole rigamarole of re-ordering because their orders were nowhere to be found. *edited for clarification; while i know that any starbucks, at any time of the day, is fairly busy, this was at around noon to 1 in the afternoon, not a peak time. very few cars in the drive thru & fewer customers inside the store. and i managed a rival coffee shop (also a drive thru store) for 5 years. if our service was ever this shoddy, or our service times were this long, everyone got reprimanded. my visit was an extremely poor, but accurate (if my past visits are taken into account) representation of how starbucks treats their guests.
i was having a bad night and the very friendly young man made my experience not only a great one but he was very sweet telling me he hopes my night gets better.. way to go starbucks i will be back!

i pop into this starbucks location 4-5 times a week to grab my beloved iced coffee after my workouts and these people are the best! i've seen them in a bad mood! they're always friendly and goofing around and having a great time! i go to a lot of starbucks both local and when i travel and i have to say the employees at this location are the most consistent with their great attitude!

went here saturday ordered a low fat latte, add whip, it was 7.79 and a lemon loaf bread. the bread was warmed and the latte hot. went this morning, ordered the exact same thing, it was 8.09, bread was cold. why the .30 difference in 1 day? good news was drive thru was empty. consistency?? not much!

i stood in line and the cashier told me he'd be right with me. stood there for a good 5-7 minutes while he was making drinks and the other cashier just looked at me like i was none of her business. just walked out of line and ended up getting nothing. great service!

i love starbucks, but this location has the most inconsistent output in terms of taste. i don't know if it's just because they are always so busy or what, but i have 3 standard drinks i rotate and they never taste the same, nor do they taste quite right. they do have very friendly staff, but be warned the lines are often long and service slow.

located on whyte avenue along what some have dubbed "coffee corner" this place is packed, overflowing, loud, and too busy. i generally head to the independent block 1912 across the street rather than a chain place if i can help it.

not sure if i have the right location. writing for lambert airport, east terminal. starbucks across from gate e-17. worst starbucks ever. ordered my drink, it came overflowing, running over the lid. server wiped the bottom of the glass and nothing else. i asked if she had any napkins. "no, but i can give you an extra glass". what good is that going to do me? i asked if there were napkins on the kiosk. yes..... just what i want before i board a plane. a sticky drink spilling. good grief!

y'all that was some good service. just rolled in from houston and we used to go to one of the busiest starbucks in the city where there was always a line and a wait. this starbucks had people rolling through with almost no wait. i was so pleased at how efficient they were and our order was perfect from the food to the drinks. lol

love this location! the staff are always so friendly even during the busy rush hour times! this is my local starbucks and i know i am going to get excellent service every time i go here. also a great place to get some work done!
i like this starbucks because it has a drive-through which is usually run prety efficiently and quickly!
very quick & attentive dylan was very nice & helped me pick out what drink to order! 10/10 recommend

i will grab a quick drink multiple times daily from my favorite neighbor starbucks. the people who work here are great and even when it's busy i don't wait longer than a few minutes. it's also a great spot to meet for biz or catching up with a girlfriend.
much better experiences here lately! i think they finally got some new staff so they aren't so overwhelmed at this busy location. lately everyone has been friendly and drinks come out faster

i was in a hurry, i got in line, placed my order. then waited. the girls served lattes and everything else to the people behind me before even making my drink! she said it was because they're drinks did not require espresso. if i'm not mistaken lattes most certainly do require espresso!! other starbucks won't even serve a premade tea before the next drink!! so basically i stood in line for my turn and got cut by five people!!!!
great ambiance, great service, sbucks coffee. 'nough said.
this by far my favorite starbucks. the staff always greets everyone who walks in. my drink is always perfect.

the other starbucks in hermitage. i was there saturday morning while my car was being worked on. this one doesn't seem to be as busy as the other starbucks in hermitage. i do wish they had a larger patio

i know they are new and still trying to figure out their sh** but i've gone there a handful of times and i've tried the drive through and mobile order and no matter what it takes 20 minutes to get a drink. there are a couple girls in there that look like they are overwhelmed and don't know what to do. they have also been out of to-go items a lot. happy to have a new starbucks and a drive through though, but it is less than convenient
just waited in drive thru line for 35 minutes for two nitro cold brews. how?!?! this is easily the slowest starbucks i've ever been to. absolutely insane.

this is the best starbucks in america. you will be hard pressed to find one that is more elaborate than this one. its so good its hard to put into words, its an experience. if you come here to visit or you live here you must come see this place. in a city that known for neglecting chains, starbucks has set the mold on what you need to do to compete in the new orleans market if you are a change. this place is unbelievable.

this place is always there for me when i'm at the hospital for my son. i used to work for starbucks and in the morning that i would have to leave the hospital to work, i would stop here. they would always give me my coffee for free. friendly staff, nice place to sit and bring a bite of food.

stopped in for a quick coffee. parking was easily located, almost right in front of the building. this location is well staffed. i counted at least four baristas &/or attendants. our service was swift & greetings/interaction with the staff was pleasant. customers were streaming in and out during our visit as others just enjoyed the environment while people watching, conversing with one another, and attending to the daily emails and/or reviews i will be back.

i just had the most terriblest time at this starbucks. this store is in fact open and the young girl morgan was awfully rude. she was mean from the start & talked about me while in front of me. i never had an experience like this at a starbucks before. if i could rate this zero star i would.

this sbux is sickkkkkkk... why i love this sbux: 1. the crowd. its filled with whites and asians :) thats right i said it b*tches!! 2. its clean (most likely due to crowd) 3. outside patio is big 4. workers arent afraid to say hi or be friendly 5. shitter was clean hmmm. i cant think of more. parking can be challenging and i saw a cpl bums when i was there. bums dont bother me too much until they are near and start talking. as for the products, its starrbucks. korean words: sul tang: sugar coppee: coffee cha: tea yuja: girl

this is a starbucks that is located at 42nd and woodland and is part of the university of the sciences. it serves students, faculty, and anybody in the area and takes all forms of payment including the student bucks. this starbucks is run by the sodexo company but has the drinks and foods associated with starbucks. the drinks are made very well usually, but be aware that on certain days the store may be out of many things such as bagel,s, danishes, crescents. they can also be out of syrups due to high demand. due to covid there are no seating options for guests in the starbucks area. social distancing is encouraged and masks are mandatory. they are quick so you wont be waiting too long.
worker ignored me for about 10 minutes and then was very condescending when i finally got her attention. no clue why she was ignoring me or why she was so condescending.

went in this morning to get my coffee that was almost $10 (which i'm aware of) handed the cashier a $50 as she handed it back and immediately said "i can't break that". without even trying to see if a manager could check the office or help in any way. so i leave to try to go to the bank next door but that was closed because it is a holiday. so i go back to starbucks to attempt to see if someone else can help me and end up getting the manager. who was able to help me. i've worked in hospitality for the last 15 years now and there really seems to be no such thing as customer service anymore. if i'm paying almost $10 for a damn coffee why couldn't you take two extra seconds out of your hourly paid time to help me.
this is currently my favorite starbucks the staff is always friendly the coffee is always starbucks but the atmosphere is always comfortable. big thanks to jake
me: hi can i have a rice crispy treat? barista: do you mean a marshmallow dream bar? me: bitch don't play games with me... typical, average, iiiight, just like every other starbucks in the world...

okay i'm shocked!!!! it is the busiest time of the year & starbucks have 2 employees working!!! tomorrow is christmas eve day!! i really feel bad for the young man making all the drinks!!! the young lady is at the register and trying to help and keep customers happy. i figure starbucks is either cheap or no one else would work. just makes no sense!!!

i've always had good experiences in this location. they are very friendly and i even scored a free drink the other day. i enjoy stopping in this particular starbucks. i docked one point because the parking lot is annoying. it is hard to find a spot and easily get in and out of it. once you get parked though, its worth it.

the best starbucks i know. the batistas here are the best. i've asked them to just make something up for me several times and each time they've given me something that i thoroughly enjoyed. so i trust them enough to just pay and let them surprise me. not to mention the energetic and friendly vibes they're always radiating. they'll also always make sure that they get your order right. if you don't like it, they're more than happy to redo it to your liking. and this isn't some corporately enforced attitude. they are genuinely willing to redo your order and make it right. the store itself is setup well. it's nice and cozy in the corner and also has good spots to just study or to do work. people are always here chillin. the store is always kept clean. almost every time i come in, someone is cleaning some part of the store. out of all the starbucks that i've been too, this is definitely the most well run. it'll continue to be the one i'll always go to.

i enjoy this starbucks as i have yet to go to a really bad one. i say the building is nice and clean, the customer service is pretty good. the smell of the coffee in the air is always a good fragrance to me. the dining area is clean and free wi-fi. my coffee drink or espresso has always tasted so good. the sandwiches and pastries are great to and very healthy and delicious. i can find no fault in this starbucks as it lives up to the reputation as a great coffee house in my opinion!

when i work full time, i like to come here during my lunch break; the friendly people always make my day better! when you come around lunch time, there may be a bit of a wait, but i think it's worth it for your afternoon pick-me-up and smiles!

no drive-thru, small location, mediocre staff! they seem less friendly than other stores not because they are snobby, they're just clueless! i guess starbucks is becoming the job of this decade, as compared to mcdonalds of the 80s lol. it's not exactly a happy store, not the starbucks culture!

the design of the drive thru is asinine. your vehicle will get trapped with all the cars waiting in line because the design is so bad. also the staff at that location seems to be very nonchalant about service.

very poor customer service every time we go there. went once earlier this week & both of our drinks were wrong. gave it another shot during a less busy time. both drinks were still made wrong & the young girl at the window was quite rude.

wow. i've never had baristas make so much conversation with me i almost didn't know how to respond. it's sad we live in a world where we like being ignored or maybe it's just cause i'm from la. the girls working the morning i went in were seriously the nicest people ever. should've went to ucsb instead of ucla i guess

drive-through was slammed and early-morning took about 15 minutes to get coffee. the coffee was delicious i tried the new punkin and a salted caramel. got to the drive-through to pay and the employee was very friendly made the experience worthwhile will come back again soon!
always love to visit this location. they are always so helpful and accommodating. they never seems to disappoint. this is probably my favorite starbuck locations in tucson!!!

when i'm driving on the 101, this is our stop to make to stretch the legs, use el baño, and get a drink. it's more about the strategic location than the actual location. it's usually dirty on the floor, the service is at a minimum, and it is average on speed. too bad it's not known for the better! i hope they raise the bar soon. this does not mean that the employees don't care (who knows? only they do) but it does mean that it is sorely lacking when it comes to other more cheerful and clean starbucks.
avoid at all costs. your drink will take 20 minutes to receive. go to e-bar or a coffee spot outside the mall.

this store must have really poor management the young women in the store were working in ridiculous heat with no ac - it was cooler outside!!!! and they were still quick and professional. it was terrible

excellent starbucks location. i visit a couple times a week and everyone is always nice to me. some even know my name and know what i order each time. it makes me feel like a celebrity. from time to time, they have let me try new menu item samples, which is always a plus to me (i mean, come on...free food and drink!). the facility is normally pretty clean and the staff, although busy, seem to stay on top of it. my only very tiny con is that it seems they don't offer the free coffee grounds anymore. years ago, they would have packages of it by the door to take home for composting. i guess it was too big of a pain in the butt to maintain.

i love starbucks and i've been to a ton of different stores across the country. but this one is beyond frustrating as every time i go i need to wait 5 minutes for the workers to finish conversations amongst themselves to take an order...or stop halfway while making a drink. i normally think starbucks has the most efficient and friendly workers, but this store is the opposite of that.

this is my favorite location on the westbank. it's super convenient and my iced coffee is always made just right. a few of the workers even know my order as soon as i walk in. the staff is super friendly and willing to help you with anything. clean, cool atmosphere that's worth the overpriced merch. the only thing that kind of upsets me is that the decor changed from like a retro new orleans style to a weird corporate like starbucks with bean fields and whatnot.
this starbucks location is located within the harrahs casino. it's a normal starbucks, so it's great!
just had an employee rather rudely get in my face about "being there without having purchased anything" when one of her coworkers had made my order about 30 minutes prior.

as usual we got our fast and very friendly service. loved the contactless service they are providing along with their reliable regular customer service. they have no touch card scans and no touch on your cup. masks and gloves were used also. way to protect customers starbucks!
this is consistently the worst starbucks. the wait times are always unnecessarily long... like 30 minutes long. it's faster to drive to another starbucks than wait here.
this starbucks is pretty dirty compared to most, it's kinda hot sometimes, and the music is a little loud if you're working, but with the new management i heard about i'm hoping things will improve.
great spot for some coffee and a meeting fast service no disturbance what so ever and hard workin employees

best baristas in town. they can make whatever you want however you want it. they also make wonderful suggestions if you're looking to try something new. never had an issue with an order. when going through the drive thru the line always moves fast. the team is so pleasant and welcoming. it's definitely my favorite starbucks by far, and i've visited many up and down the east coast!

today was my first visit to this starbucks. i went to meet a friend, and when she arrived, there was a sign on the door saying they were closed due to safety concerns. i have no idea what that was about, but they were serving via the drive through. by the time i arrived, they'd removed the sign and let people in, so we could still sit and hang out. but i have no idea what that was about. kind of a weird start to a visit. the staff was really friendly, though they seemed to be slow with making the drinks. my hot tea took a solid 8-10 minutes to be made, which is unusual. the food and beverage case had a nice variety of food (sandwiches, salads, etc) and bottled drinks that i typically don't find at the starbucks locations i tend to frequent. definitely a plus for this one! the interior has a lot of bar type seating, which made good use of the space. the outdoor has a nice sized patio, too, if it's not super rainy like it was today!

an open letter, redux; all of your great employees are gone or on a very limited amount of shifts. you are now, officially, a complete and utter epic fail across the board. most of my co-workers have moved on to other locations and i'm trying to get some help from my gps to do the same w/o compromising my am commute time. my hats off to your legendary ability to suck in such a well rounded way. fin.

this starbucks is clean and new, although the tables could maybe be wiped down a little more frequently. the baristas are super nice and friendly! unfortunately i think they forgot about my order and i ended up having to wait over 20 minutes for just a regular coffee. i was a little annoyed, but they sincerely apologized and then gave me a size upgrade which was definitely nice of them! the inside is small but most people tend to order to go. it's right next to the convention center, so most patrons tend to be people going there or from the hotel this starbucks operates out of.

extremely friendly! me and my wife and her dog are moving from seattle to nashville and spent the night near the starbucks last night. before hitting the road we stopped here for coffee and breakfast. the two ladies that took our order and gave us our food we're both exceptionally friendly. the food and drinks we're both fresh and the appropriate temperatures. they also offered us a "pup cup" me and my wife both had the double smoked bacon cheddar sandwich. i got some large guava juice thing and my wife got a frappuccino.
love this little starbucks. great drive through. super friendly staff.

absolutely the worst starbucks in town ! i have never seen such rude baristas in the 18 plus years of going starbucks ... which we visit on a daily basis! while waiting for our drinks, two customers complained about their drinks being wrong and the three idiots working there acted like it was the customers' fault and were extremely rude to them ! we waited for over 15 minutes for two lattes to be made while the three idiots talked. do yourself a favor and drive 1/2 mile to the location on damonte !!

it's rare to see a corporate environment like starbucks to have a small business, friendly attitude. i went in with a friend and was met with the wonderful service of jim. if you can go when jim is there please do yourself a favor and go in, you will not be disappointed.
