i have been coming here for over a decade. small with the usual expectations. a couple of outside tables are available. wifi is free but you have to ask what network. four bucks for a grande cappuccino..
can't complain with starbucks! just watch leave extra early if u want to go to this location in the mornings during the week because it gets crowded.
worst starbucks i have ever went to very unsatisfied with the service. they were out of everything i tried to order. the girls had terrible attitudes and were beyond rude.
quick service and the barrista has the right vanilla to blonde roast quotient correct for my taste buds. thanks starbucks!!
good customer service i went in yesterday sunday the 26 and tatiana made us feel very welcome !

definitely not my favorite starbucks to visit. although my order was made quickly, the customer service was nit great. and to be honest, i would rather wait longer for my order and have a friendly barista, than what happened today. very upsetting, considering this starbucks is the closest one to where i live.

i have been a regular here for about two years now and have come to count on quick and friendly service. yes, as others have mentioned the parking lot is small and spaces are rather narrow. if you drive a truck or large suv, i imagine maneuvering during rush hour could be kind of a hassle. typically, i enter the lot from horatio so i can scout out the best parking. many of the baristas have been here for 2+ years but lately there have been a few new faces. for such a great/busy location it's unfortunate that there is no drive through but that's a limitation of having a starbucks in a strip mall. as for me, i'll keep coming back for a reliable and quick fix of caffeine as i head off to work.
starbucks by e29.... customer service is terrible!! rude and slow. if you don't want to work...quit!

great staff, very efficient!!! everyone has a great attitude and welding smile, no matter how early it is. i love this starbucks. i go to this location and only this one. the manager and assistant managers are very active with there staff and get involved in the customers.
please rope off the back of your parking lot so that your patrons don't get their cars towed by grand view apartments.

this starbucks at west edmonton mall above the ice palace is always very busy, every time i go, there's always a crowd of people waiting for drinks. however, i don't know how they do it but they are just as fast as any other starbucks with half the amount of customers ordering. my guess is that most starbucks have drive thru's so they still have the same amount of customers as this one at any given time but you just can't see half of them because they're in the drive thru. you can sit at their tables above the ice palace and it actually cools you down, if you're like me and hate malls haha. so if you're at west edmonton nall and want starbucks but see the line up and walk away, don't fret, it always moves fast. go get your java fix.
quick and painless coffee fix ...came from hotel couldn't stomach the coffee there had to find a real star bucks now i can start my day

i absolutely love the baristas at this location (as of 12/26/2018) they have the greatest attitude i've ever experienced --- and on top of that they have your order ready within seconds. i feel like they all deserve a raise (i love their dragonfruit lemonade refresher) i will fight anyone who gives them grief, a bad attitude, etc! these baristas must be protected from evil

the starbucks was small and the variety of items was even smaller. it was also very busy there. the staff did not seem to be fluent in english and wasn't the nicest. on top of that, they also got our simple order wrong.

really like this location. great customer service and they have a very organized system, especially for how busy they are. they are very covid compliant with masks and social distancing. we ordered inside and had all 4 of our drinks within 5 minutes of arrival which is pretty awesome. love their refreshers and this time i got the pink drink which was delicious

i've never been to such a slow starbucks. corporate needs to come back in to retrain staff on how to churn through the customers without making each order a successful minute ordeal. one star; because it's in the hotel there's not even the benefit of relatively clean bathrooms.

if you can stop at a different starbucks, skip this location. ignore the "prep time" when ordering through the mobile app and add 10-15 min to it. this is not the spot to grab a coffee on your way to work, unless you're willing to be late. also double check your order especially with food- last week i ordered the bacon/gruyere egg bites and they gave me the roasted red pepper bites this location is completely disorganized and has ridiculous wait times with slow service...i've been to literally a dozen other locations across several states that are always on point with fast, friendly service. i won't be wasting my time and money at this branch any more, which is unfortunate since it is so close to me!

i have never gotten starbucks before so this was my first visit for a casual business meeting. ample parking and plenty of seating. i went to the register and gave ryan a very vague "i want something hot and super sweet". he narrowed down my drink by asking a few more questions and within 5 minutes i had my new favorite drink. he even offered to make me something else for free if i wasn't happy. i just wish it had a drive thru!
wifi at this location is slower than a dial up connection for those who know what that is.

in the past two-plus weeks: order on mobile, get to the store, the cafe is closed. i didn't have time to wait in the long drivethrough line, so i abandoned the order. (went online; starbucks refunded me, thank you.) stopped by the next day, cafe closed again. another day, cafe open, went to the counter, waited over a minute while being completely ignored by the staff, so i left. today i went back, mobile order for a nitro cold brew. 7+ minutes after the "drink will be ready time" i asked. the order had been placed to a side because they didn't have any cold brew. and i'd have to wait at the counter if i wanted a refund. (went online to get the refund later.) a poorly managed store. i won't be going back.

how is it possible that a coffee place is bad? i actually like starbucks but this one does not stand up to others. my favorite starbucks happens to be in a grocery store which is hilarious since they are actually employed by the store. this location i have hit up at least 10 times and each time the service has been less than adequate. too bad because i would gladly keep spending money there.

i have been to a handful of airport starbucks, and i understand why they overstaff some of them for when the rush hits, but this was a slower day and while a hive can work well with tons of bees buzzin' around these folks need to work on their system. it wasn't busy but the 7 or so folks working behind this tiny hub of a starbucks were all younger and super chatty with each other where it made it difficult for folks to know if their orders were being placed or not, or correctly. i don't speak starbucks and asked for a large, but got a medium and felt like the staff while efficient was being short with the clientele in favor of side conversations and jokes with other employees. i totally understand liking the people you work with but when it's at the expense of customer service, you should probably keep it to the break room.

the absolute worst sb in the usa!! slow slow slow....... and half the time the drinks taste terrible. i've brought this to corporates attention multiple times. they obviously haven't been able to rectify this. just make sure you have time to wait. and try your drink before pulling away from the window.

lovely drive-thru location with friendly service. situated in the same plaza as club fitness which makes it super convenient for grabbing my preworkout morning joe. there's plenty of seating both indoor & outside patio. merchandise for purchase includes the typical overpriced starbucks mugs, tumblers, coffee beans, brew systems and more. cold case packed with grab & go lunch-a-bles, pastries, yogurt, fruit & so on. ~christy
i love the drive thru. the old starbucks up the street was not drive thru and difficult to park. the staff is sweet. their faces pop up at the ordering center. and they remember you!
really slow service two people working the whole store you expect more from starbucks . i will say that the staff was nice even under pressure

favorite sit down sbux on northside. store is always clean, baristas have never served a bad drink and are always friendly. remember my name which is a bonus! convenient in and out or place to chill with a book. nice location off the monon on my bike.

awful starbucks location. my wife waited in line for close to 30 minutes.. only to find they had nothing vanilla and basically no food. they could only make caramel, peppermint, or cinnamon drinks. ever heard of inventory management? it would have been nice to know they were literally out of pretty much everything prior to standing in line for half an hour. when my wife got to the counter and politely voiced her frustration, the cashier just had a blank stare and acted rude. terrible service, and whatever drink she ended up with was inedible.

great place to take a break. not too fond of the outdoor seating since it's right on the corner of the parking lot where cars come and go. i stop here on my weekly shopping trips because it's convenient for me. still can't get use to the coed bathrooms. the servers are sweet and friendly here

granted this place is in a tough spot, in iv and packed with students but as a student myself it's frustrating how bad the service here can get. i usually stop by in a rush, like most others here i'm sure. the coffee bar (hand crafted) drinks are consistent. the problem is the poor service and dirty environment. for example, once witnessed an employee clean the bathroom and come out with the dustpan with floor bathroom dirt and throw it away in the sink behind the counter, next to the coffee machines.
once again this location had no via decaf in stock. now i realize they may do many things right but if they don't keep all their products in stock, for me, obviously, they are worthless
all i can say is, i love my coffee!!! i use my coupons from my iphone also i have the starbucks app that gives me great deals and points
love this location!! they always get my coffee just the way i like it. on occasions they have messed up my order but are always accommodating both in service & courtesy.

by far the worst starbucks on the west bank. my drink is made wrong every time and the baristas are extremely rude. not one person smiling or even attempting to pretend they want to be there. the manager is even worse, she not only is nasty with her employees but customers as well. this is not the customer service starbucks claims it has and they should be ashamed of themselves for letting this woman treat people so badly

for a starbucks, it's as good as it gets. the location is obviously the best being on high street, but what you wouldn't know is that the staff are always great! the manager/owner is always really friendly and funny. generally, there's always a place to sit, which can be a problem at some locations. and the drinks are always made as ordered, again... can be a problem at other locations. great location, great staff, and consistency.

i'm a regular at this location because of the ina construction but now that you can cross the freeway i suggest going to the location in front of target. by far the slowest service ever. every time i go it's 20-30 minute wait no matter if they're busy or not. the drive thru is the absolute worst. went inside to see if the wait was better and ended up standing there for 25 minutes. what a joke it is to have 5 people working the line but having 1-2 drinks leaving the counter every 7-10 minutes because employees are too busy talking amongst each other. seriously, drive to a different location. it's worth the extra drive.

this location has shorted me twice now when i ordered multiple espresso shots. on 2 separate occasions within the same month i ordered 4 espresso shots as a solo order. when i got home and measured it out, i was only given 2 shots.
my second favorite starbucks location in shpk. good ample seating plenty of space to sprawl and good customer service. and there is enough parking here. plus a good patio space.

this is my favorite starbucks out of all the reno locations. besides being the closest, it's also open the latest. it used to be open all night then got changed to 11pm, not bad considering most starbucks locations around are only open until 7,8, or 9pm. the baristas are friendly too! as usual, like all starbucks-they'll make it right if your food or drink doesn't taste right. if not, a quick call to their customer service will solve your issue(s)

i've never waited 40 minutes for starbucks before. additionally, i saw a lady put literal green tea in my iced green tea latte. it's supposed to be matcha powder! the person at bar didn't even use the appropriate lines on the shaker container and my entire cup was filled with ice! extremely poor training and highly unprofessional in the delivery. i hate giving baristas crap especially if it's a busy location but come one, i've been a barista at a high volume location and you can't mess up this much! avoid.

each time i visit this starbucks, i am disappointed. most starbucks locations are super awesome, but this location consistently keeps a staff who are snarky, aloof, and disrespectful. step it up kids!
yay! finally! the old del taco building put to good use!! great space. lots of seating and tables for reading, studying... and there is a drive thru if you're in a hurry!

stopped in on a sunday afternoon out of sheer boredom. service is friendly and accurate but the tables need to continually be cleaned. someone was sweeping and cleaning when we first arrived but within 15 minutes nearly all tables needed a good wipe down. as a tip, the new chili mocha is really tasty. very happy i tried it.

the products are always on point. but since this is the only starbucks within miles, the lines are always long. never any available tables. you will definitely want to pre-order your stuff if you need it fast. we need more locations,corporate! giddy up!

coffee is coffee to me so maybe not fair to judge but this location of starbucks just had too many people that seemed to be using this as their early morning place for hygiene. a small line at the bathroom and the place was a little unkempt compared to the sb i went to about a mile away the previous morning. this is a good location. very convenient and did not have time for the extra distance.

my absolute biggest issue with this store is their mobile ordering. what's the point of putting in my order if i still have to wait 10 min for you to acknowledge that i'm standing at the pickup area waiting for my coffee that's cold by the time i get it. put the orders in the same place as the rest and not behind the counter!

do i have to give stars at all? this has got to be the worst and slowest starbucks. i used mobile order at 7:15 pm. 15mins later my order was ready. had i known drive thru was gonna be faster i would have done that. it wasn't even that busy. everyone in here was doing everything but making drinks. i'll stick to the starbucks on vets and kingman.

i stopped by this starbucks a couple days ago to get my free drink from my rewards. upon entering i was greeted with a friendly hello and i was glad to see no line. the food selection was the same as any other starbucks and i got my drink after a slightly longer wait that usual. i ordered a venti caramel frap with two shots of expresso and extra caramel drizzle. the taste was fine but as i was walking to my car the drink ended up turning into a latte. there wasn't a lot of ice and i'm sure it didn't melt in the cold reno weather. overall, this place is a solid choice if you want a starbucks drink.

this has got to be one of the worse starbucks ever. we had to report the order several times and she still got it wrong. the speed from ordering to getting the drink wasn't too slow but the order processing was painfully slow. double check your order before you leave

this is my go-to starbucks when i have a starbucks craving due to it's location on my drive to work and because it has a drive-thru, yay! my review is primarily around their drive-thru. almost every time, i have a wonderful, friendly quick experience. it probaby helps that i don't come at a super busy time due to an early work schedule but i appreciate their cheerfulness that early in the morning. mitstakes or cranky employees is rare here for me. i highly recommend their bacon gouda breakfast sandwich! a breakfast here isn't cheap, but a nice occasional treat! oh word of warning - during the school year, during nice weather, this is a popular place for nearby high school students to go to after work - it gets crowded and loud!

ordered a frap with coconut milk no whip! they put whip. when they re did it they took it around the corner and took the whip off. i trashed it. unacceptable if you have a dairy allergy. i will go to another location for now on.

i had the chance to meet a lady by the name of leilani and the team this afternoon at the glenwood store. the day before i bought a starbucks cup at another location, but it really wasn't what i was looking for, as i visited glenwood starbucks the following day...i realized that they had the cup i have been looking for a while now! leilani was able to exchange the cup i wanted, when other store refused to help. thanks, ladies for the amazing customer service and my amazing guava drink!
great wifi and shared lobby with hilton is a great idea. lots of sittings and even for a small meeting.

great location. service is always prompt and friendly. kyle is the best employee i ever met in any starbucks. he really knows every customer and knows how to connect with every single one. all starbucks needs a kyle.

this is a starbucks, so we know what to expect when it comes to coffee and tea drinks and that's a good thing if you like starbucks. the people are friendly and helpful, almost offering a smile all the time...the seating area is big, clean and bright.... and they have a drive thru for people in a hurry. they also have outdoor seating.

whatever guy is working right now.. it's 6pm on wednesday night, 12/6, is the most rude starbucks employee i've ever encountered. i can't believe i'm even writing a review but i've never had this happen at starbucks. i know these jobs are hard to come by as starbucks is a phenomenal employer, so whoever the dude is working the starbucks drive thru on robb needs to at least treat people with some form of kindness. i'm in disbelief..

enjoying a sbux iced "reserve" blend - dragon, i believe. super dark, plenty robust.....apparently this new "line" is only available at certain locations (i work in wayne, pa, and they informed me there was none in that area that carried the reserve line. tastes like real coffee!!!!

smaller than most starbucks but was efficient in getting out drinks. hubs got a green tea and it was normal :) i got a icedcarmel macchiato and it is probably the best i have ever had. coconut milk i think enhanced the espresso and did not leave me with any after tastes or film. i don't think i have ever tasted the espresso in previous macchiatos i have had. good job ;)
always a fan of the very helpful staff at starbucks :) they are always smiling and peppy, no matter how early / late it is.

typically i avoid drive thru lines when they are really long but this place was so fast & on point with my order it was crazy. there was at least 10 cars ahead of me & i kid you not i waited only two minutes for my order. my carmel macchiato was so good i finished it within minutes. i'll definitely be back!

1 star for this location only because i love starbucks! i have been to this store several times...after yesterday i will park my car and buy from the starbucks in nearby frys, or drive to another location! i'm puzzled as to how a starbucks store can get their service so wrong!! a few times, i have had excellent service via the drive through usually around 6am. up until yesterday, i thought the worst store was in the las vegas airport. that being said, in the past 3 weeks i have experienced terrible service here on 4 separate visits. isn't anyone training these people??? yesterday i ordered my coffee from the mobile app (at 2:36pm), when i drove up to the store (a good 15-20minutes later) the drive through was packed but the parking lot was nearly empty, so i parked and walked in. two or three people were sitting at tables, and i was followed in by an older couple. i walked up to the counter with the sign, 'pick up mobile orders here'. i saw the guy was completing a drink, and i saw my little red cup ready on the counter behind him. i asked him if he could grab my mobile order, not looking up he replied, 'yes'. even though he walked past my drink several times in the next several minutes to take drinks to the drive-up window, my coffee sat there. over and over he walked past it to take an order to the guy at the window, then back past it again (this time with two empty hands) to start filling another order. i continued to look in the direction of the other guy behind the counter (by the till) who seemed to look my way a few times. the couple, who came in after me, had already ordered and paid, but were now standing at the counter chatting with the guy. finally another woman came in, i thought this was a good sign, assuming he would acknowledge her and the other couple would see someone else was waiting behind them. nope, he just continued to chat with the first couple while the other woman was now waiting. at this point, the guy filling the drinks was done with the order for the older couple. he motioned that he had their drinks, grabbed a cup and began to fill another order!! let me say again, i did not stand there 'quietly', i asked a second time for my mobile order, i knew the guy had heard me and he still would not make eye contact. i paced, stood on my tippy toes, looked at my watch several times (it was now 3:25pm). i considered walking out, the whole thing was bizarre, but i had paid for the stupid drink and i wasn't going to leave without it. finally, after the 2nd woman paid, i walked over to the other guy. he didn't look at me or acknowledge me in any way. i asked him loudly if he could grab my mobile order for me??? he said, 'oh you have a mobile order, you need to go to that counter (pointing to where i had been standing, pacing, etc!! really buddy...you can't tell me you did not see me there!! ). i explained i had and refrained from commenting on his snarky remark. so he finally handed me my coffee. one of the other bad experiences here was about 2 wks earlier. on that day there were literally 5 employees behind the counter. i walked up to the girl filling drinks and asked for my mobile order. she said, 'yes, just a minute', she was not only filling orders but also attending to the cars. she glanced repeatedly at the 4 other employees huddled behind the counter, as if hoping they would help (she was the only one working). i felt sorry for her so i waited assuming she would grab my coffee on one of her trips, nope. she continued to fill other mobile orders, (no other customers were at the counter) while glancing several times at the other employees. they were deep in some kind of conversation never looking at her or me. finally, one broke free from the pack, so i walked over to him, he did not acknowledge me, i asked him if he could grab my mobile order. he was rather rude and then i realized he was the same snarky guy who gave me my order two weeks prior!!! i just feel stupid for waiting so long especially after several other bad experiences at this store.

convenient location, but starbucks need to install some sound absorbing tiles in here. can hear the counter staff laughing and talking clearly, not what i want for a rest stop. sorry for the bad rating. i am usually an easy 5 star. the rest room had tp scraps all over the floor, needs policing.

hellooooo new, conveniently placed starbucks. fellow sludents and i have been waiting for you for quite some time. first perk: they kept the del taco building, i just love it, it looks like a spaceship. second perk: there is a drive-thru and they made it accessible to the shoulder going on to 64 so you don't have to wait at the stoplights just to get back out. third perk: the interior. so cool. the big "saint louis" light up logo, the all recycled wood surfaces and industrial metals with the starbucks reserve section is sharp. the seating is great: some high top tables, and tables facing the all open window view. in a wrap up: it looks spiffy. and lastly the fourth perk: the service was stellar today. i know it is only the second day but every worker was smiling, working hard, and not complaining. even when it was jam packed (and it definitely was) this morning at 9 am they were bustling about but not snapping at each other while they navigated the starbucks station. i like the atmosphere, the staff, the accessibility and, oh yea, the pumpkin spiced latte. i'm a happy camper.

this drive thru is literally my nightmare. can't help the horrific parking situation due to location, however it is by far the slowest drive thru i've ever been to. i live around the corner so i go this starbucks on my way to work, and i have to plan to leave extra early just in case the drive thru takes an extra 15-20 minutes, which happens almost every time. i just don't understand it. three stars because my order is always correct and the staff is pleasant.

this starbucks has all the good starbucks taste you would expect, but the workers are often chatting about who they are going to get fired next, making them take longer on your order. if you find it funny this might be a good thing, otherwise there are better starbucks out there.

i usually drive through starbucks but today i had to use the facilities so i went inside. i ordered my usual iced blueberry black tea no liquid cane sugar two pumps sugar-free vanilla. it was not very busy. i used the restroom, came back out and waited and waited and waited for my ice tea. while waiting, another lady asked where her egg bites were. she already had drank 1/3 of her venti tea and still no food. it appeared that the 2 young men "working" were too busy talking to a young girl to fill orders. one girl was working and she was doing everything. i'm not sure they left out the cane syrup, as i watched her make my drink. by that point i just wanted my drink and to leave. not a happy customer today. i'm hoping sbux sends me a request for a survey, as they often do.

service is always speedy and accurate! i'm a creature of habit so i always get a soy white chocolate mocha latte. lately i've been a fan of the pepperoni turnover. grabs seat outdoors kraut in the cafe if you have some time.
i really wish this starbucks was bigger. it's such a nice place to study but it gets so crowded. however, it does have good (working) wifi!
yeah i was clearly say they're open till 10 pm on friday nights but i pulled up after 9 and they said they were closed. weird.
rude/indifferent staff, sloppy service (e.g., hot coffee lid half off), parking lot is always tight. the broomall starbucks just a little farther east on route 3 is much better on all counts.

this is a problem i do like starbucks and if i want to get something quick i can always go there. but this is like the fourth or fifth time already going to the starbucks in franklin and they never have what i want they always tell me when i'm ready to pay after waiting five minutes or more then that. i'm sorry we are out of this or that. i mean come on when you were taking my order you should say hey sorry we don't have this do you want to get something else. i mean it's easy to say yeah is this much and i know starbuck it's not cheap. that's why i'm paying for what i want before you finish get my order make sure that what i'm ordering is on there don't make me wait and then tell me you don't have any of it.

it's hit or miss on everything. at times your drink is perfect, sometimes it is half full or they didn't mix it right or something (hit or miss). sometimes they run out of straws or run out of cups or run out of sugar. (hit or miss)
st. louis, mo airport good coffee as usual. workers need to drink coffee to wake the heck up!!! customer service sucks. my "warmed bread" is cold. bad attitude from the servers...

the 4 stars is for the nice gentleman that tried to help me log onto starbuck's wifi. sadly, my chrome book did not want to comply, but a server there today spent the time helping me connect and i appreciate that. this place is packed with nonstop people lining up all throughout the afternoon! who would have though this hidden gem tucked away near applebee's would get so much foot traffic! clean, organized and consistent with the starbuck's brand.

this is one of my go to starbucks places but it's just an ok location. they're always constantly busy. the drive thru wraps around the building almost every time i'm there. 3 stars because they can actually make the drinks. so if you can wait for good drinks, deff. come here. if not, just go to the mall or down the street to target.

i have been there several times, walk-in, ignored, drive thru, wrong order! i am a basic latte drinker, no funny order requests. burned coffee i will not abide! being ignored, no more! get a dedicated cashier with no headset these folks can't multi-task!

i travel through out the country and visit a starbucks daily. i truly believe in the product and experience. this location needs assistance. customer service is lacking and productivity is almost non existent.
love the remodel. decent service. i love the holiday flavors, especially peppermint mocha which can be made vegan with soy, no whip.

oh, mill woods town centre starbucks - i have tried, really tried to like you. i tried to forgive you the first time, when the barista made my orange refresher with extra ice instead of light ice and tried to tell me that there wasn't supposed to be any orange pieces included in the drink. i forgave you when you forgot my cookie and made me wait in line to let the new staff member on till that i had paid for that delicious fruit and oat cookie that was calling to me from the pastry case. but three strikes and i am afraid that you are out. my americano misto was so watery that i had to toss it in the trash. i am quite sure the proper number of espresso shots were not put into that concoction of watery, milky, semi coffee flavoured beverage. i don't mean to nitpick, but at $5 a drink and with a starbucks nearby no matter where you are in the city, my drink has to be right or else i'm driving to the next 'bucks...even if it is not quite as convenient. sorry our relationship was so short, it's not me - it's you.

i'm mean when starbucks cravings call you answer!! and i can say that they never disappoint!! my go to drink is the iced green tea unsweetened. the only disappointment is that they never seem to have guava anymore. but you add guava syrup to your green tea and i've gotten people addicted!! anyways plain iced green is also very enjoyable.

super great service. happy employees with great a/c. really appreciate the upbeat attitude. accidentally ordered a tall but needed venti size of the refresher. they mixed it into a venti before i could blink. awesome customer service!

the billboards say open 24/7 and they mean that, as i've stopped here on many an odd hour and holiday. great drive through service and employees who are very friendly despite those odd hours and holidays, too. only reason i'm holding back the fifth star is that the logistics when leaving the drive-thru are a bit annoying but if you are on that dead stretch of i-4 between tampa and orlando and need that caffeine fix, seeing their sign on the horizon over the interstate is a beautiful thing.
as far as starbucks' go, you could not ask for a better team. this store is always running very smoothly - even when the line is to the door and there are 20 cars in the drive-thru!
a great place, good atmosphere, great area, excellent wifi, electrical receptacles and everything you would expect from starbucks. prices may be a bit higher for we're in an affluent neighborhood.

wow.. nice new starbucks.. hella slow service. friendly but took almost 15mins for my praline chai latte. forgot my drip coffee waited again because half n half 5mins 1 star for slowness +1 star cause they noted i was pretty ticked.. explained had a lot of new ppl , but thats a management issue not a customer .. gave a $4 gc but not sure that equates to a long wait and slow service update: chai praline tea latte was hella water down if you dont mind the wait its a nice new starbucks!

this starbucks is deceptive. first of all, parking is horrendous - there's just not enough of it. secondly there is hardly any seating - a few bar stools and a few tiny tables for 2. thirdly, the retail area - used also to barricade off the drink line area - is too bulky and wide. this location is deceptive in size - it looks like a big store from the outside but is cramped and crowded inside. meanwhile the usual fourbucks menu items prevail. service was fine. it's just too small.
one of the better starbucks in the area . there always friendly and talk with us when i come in with my daughter. then even make suggestions because she likes to order off the secret menu .

this starbucks is spacious and well laid out. the drive thru is an added convenience. i visit several times per week and the staff is always courteous. i prefer espresso and related drinks and am always pleased.

i have had wayyyy better service at other starbucks. they were "out" of most of the frozen coffee drinks, so when i asked about a frappuccino, i was told rudely that they were out of that too. plus, the drive thru line was very slow. not bad service, but i have had must must better service at other franchises.

this starbucks has personality. i went in yesterday and one of the employees was singing and everyone else working was super happy and very engaging with each customer. last week, i went through the drive thru and after they said they were out of the oprah chai, i ordered a regular one. when i got to the window, they told me it was their treat that day. i like free coffee obviously, but more than that i like employees who are happy and take the time to notice ways they can take care of their customers. i've always had great, speedy service and my order has always been correct at this location. only downside is they keep it freezing inside. bring a jacket. thanks!

i first tried this starbucks because of how cool it looked. bad idea. every day, i order my starbucks oatmeal without it being re-packaged in the wasteful paper bag. on my first trip to the spaceship, i ordered as usual. when my order -- bagged -- was delivered to me by an irritated-looking woman, i pulled the oatmeal and fruit out and said "i don't need this bag. will you reuse it?" she snatched the bag from my hand, scowled at me, held the bag up in front of my face and crumpled it into a ball. i said "wait! i'll recycle that!", but she smirked and threw it into the trash as if to say "screw you, asshat." the overwrought drama and hatred in her reaction freaked me out -- what kind of employee acts like that to a customer?? that's when i saw the title "manager" on her name badge. i've been back to the spaceship when the lines at the awesome, friendly starbucks (in the cwe or at 55 and loughborough) are too long. but the spaceship team is always sour and surly, disorganized (too many people behind the counter, but no one doing anything), and the orders nearly always fubar in some way. this is certainly the coolest looking starbucks in st. louis. but it seems to be managed (during morning hours, at least) very, very poorly.

i love the girl at the window she is always so sweet she had long dark hair she is the only reason they got an extra star. jose the manager he needs a refresher course on customer service. i on average spend$100 a week here and when i ordered my iced coffee with extra cream and when i got to the window and there wasn't enough cream i asked for a small side and jose told the lady at the window to tell me they will have to charge me additional almost $3 (mind you my coffee cost $3.18 with tax) and then handed me like a little cup with like no creamer. if i don't like my coffee i can give it back and they will make me a new one but if i ask for a side of cream to fix it my self i will be charged?? if it wasn't so close to my house and always slow i would have probably started to go to a different one a long time ago..

the service at starbucks is amazing! and the drinks are great to. i recommend you get double chocolate chip frap it is amazing. but there are some downsides with starbucks sometimes the floor can be very stick and the tables. that is my review i highly recommended going there. but get it to go.

clean starbucks with lots of comfy seating inside and a nice outdoor seating area too. baristas were friendly and since it was slow they even rinsed our hydro flasks and kids before refilling our reusable travel tumblers with iced lattes. the double smoked bacon sandwich toasted open face was delicious. conveniently located right off the freeway. lots of parking!
so much better inside than the old location. staff is always friendly, and drive thru is the fastest i've ever been to
this starbucks has the friendliest drive thru workers. always so happy and upbeat. it makes waiting in line for my coffee tolerable.
slow and slower....don't understand why it takes them 15 mins to make a green ice tea. need better management.

i really like this starbucks, it might just be my favorite starbucks! i say that because all of the employees are very friendly and sweet. i came in dripping wet from the rain and one of the baristas offered me a trash bag so i wouldn't get more wet, how sweet! this starbucks is conveniently located to my gym and work so i'll plan to come here again. they definitely give service with a smile, which goes a long ways these days! i think i'll choose to come here more often!

very unhappy with my last experience at this location. came in to get a refill on my drink and was told it needed to be within 30 mins of getting original drink. i asked where it said that and she had said no where. instead of being accommodating and making an exception for this one time she said she can get me a new cup or give me 10 cents off. i thought the purpose to bring in cups for refill was to be more mindful about the environment, clearly starbucks does not care about their customers or the planet.

i frequently buy coffee at starbucks in memphis, jackson and nashville, tn. this was my first and last trip to the 21st street location. the counters and floors were not particularly clean, and there was a bug crawling on the brownie in the display case. i called there clerk over to tell him about it, and he told me that the food in the case was only for display because they couldn't get rid of the bugs in the case. after that display, we didn't order any brownies, and i am not sure if i will be able to choke down the coffee i had already bought.

3 years later, and they're still understaffed. i've also found that their drinks are less consistent than other locations. today i ordered a vanilla sweet cream cold brew (for which i had to wait at least 10 minutes), and it was both overly sweet and watery tasting. i've never had this problem at other locations. overall, kitchen gia right next door or abp is quicker and better.

the employees here rock! they are always sweet, kind, and offer a big greeting when you enter. but the store itself is just getting filthy. surfaces, windows, floors, furniture...this place needs serious attention.
invested with flies!! beware if coming inside. almost until workable conditions if you're looking to get some work done
great coffee in wonderful location,grab a cup and stroll the streets of this wonderful town.
standard starbucks. clean. free wifi. friendly staff.

i'll start by mentioning i am a regular at starbucks, i am there everyday. as i ordered my drink today an employee decided work was the appropriate place to talk about a protest she participated in. she also mentioned how she spoke poorly to the police that were present at the protest. you are free to have your beliefs, but work is not the time to publicize those beliefs, especially when all you patrons aren't anti- police. i won't be visiting this location again and i'll be sure my pro- police friends and family don't either.

very small and cramped, but does the job when you need some good coffee quick while catching a flight. staff is pretty nice and tries to get your order fast. line would go out the door when it's busy and crowded, but that's pretty much expected when it's the only starbucks for the airport terminal (there's a larger location within the terminal, but that at gate a after your go through the security checks). i would not get coffee here or attempt to wait in line if its long and you have a flight to catch, because you would need to go through security after you getting this coffee, and they might ask you to toss it out when it comes to screening.

three years ago this place operated like clock work. not sure what happened, but it seems that they are always out of drink ingredients and staff take forever to assist you, even if there is no one in line ahead of you.

this is your typical starbucks at the service areas on i76. if the line is short you will be in and out, but long line do not bother waiting as they are extremely slow in general. i ordered a trenta green tea lemonade with 5 pumps of cane sugar and no ice. the staff where very pleasant and friendly. there was no line so i was in and out in no time at all. the drink was made perfectly and tasted great.

writing a review on starbucks in general seems a bit useless- we all know it and have our own opinions of it. this location specifically stands out because it has transformed a landmark building from a useless eyesore into a gorgeous, functioning gem. a gem which provides tasty caffeine!!! i am sad to say that my first experience here was not a good one. i got a ham, egg and cheese breakfast sandwich. the sandwich was still frozen on the inside. i realize they don't have a kitchen there, but it was an unwelcome reminder that its basically the same thing as a microwave breakfast sandwich from the grocery store. ew. i'll still go there for my caffeine fix. a lot.

love this starbucks, only visited twice, but i was only there a short weekend. great location, just a short walk from the epicurean hotel. epic place to meet new people and pets. very friendly patrons as well as employees at the location. the barista was very nice and understanding as i geeked out at finally finding the special metallica starbucks card. i told her that it figures i had to come to tampa to find these cards. side note i had searched for them in phoenix and had no success. she was also nice as i explained i had to load the card to my app before i ordered. very patient barista! ok once i gained composure back and was able to order my drink, which i don't think they get many orders for, skinny hot chocolate no foam, she passed the order on to another friendly and happy barista. my drink was made to my liking. we sat a while at this location while we planned the day. it was also a nice quiet spot to hail an uber and wait.
a starbucks in the brewery district - what a surprise! pretty good location - a spacious, bright area with lots of laptop-friendly tables.

i came here before my doctor appointment next door and it was a zoo. i have been to busy franchise's downtown and in other cities, but this location is chaotic and does not handle crowds well. the person ringing my order up did not listen to me, and didnt understand what a doppio macchiato was!! i ordered a decaffeinated one with extra foam. he wanted to know "what size?" i explained espresso drink, comes in one size....took forever to get my drink and drank the regular doppio macchiato since time was running out for appointment. i also got a cheese and fruit box. perfect snack item for me. i drink starbucks coffee at home and like their food items, but only come when i have a doctor's appointment. i wil jot go out if my way to come back to this location.

first visit while in this area. slow filling mobile orders - over 10 minutes. otherwise, same expected high quality with pleasant barista-ship. i'd say this location is difficult to access from pendleton pike with crowded parking lot. i'd bet drive their is its primary business - and that may work better.

this is the worse service i ever received it took twenty-five minutes for two drinks. the drive thru was busy so i decided to get off and order going inside thinking it would be faster, i should have just stayed in my car. there was only couple people inside. it seems that they were not organized. they had five people making drinks but it still took forever. they got my drink wrong and it was going take another ten minutes to make me a new one. i left very unhappy and won't be coming back to this location.

i love this starbucks. the staff is friendly and always has a smile on their face. the usual for me is the white chocolate mocha hot with an extra pump of syrup so that i don't have to add sugar and a slice of banana bread but today i tried the mango dragon fruit tea and omg was it amazing and refreshing i will definitely be trying it again

the crew here are always very efficient! the music is too loud so its difficult to make or take calls. it's frequently too cold. i've brought people here and we have had to leave because the music is so loud. this review is after more than 2 dozen visits so it is not random.

stopped by this starbucks before a trip to the dmv. my order was made perfectly and the girls that worked there were nice. the only problem was the cleanliness. i went later in the day so i understand it being a bit messy but it wouldn't hurt to wipe down your tables and check your bathroom every hour or so. i did like that this location had ample outside seating, as well as a counter top seating section. overall the look was very modern. definitely better than other starbucks i frequent.

i love starbucks and i got to drink my first pumpkin spice latte out of the red cups. location is very convenient. the staff members were courteous to my friends and i, while we took a long time to decide what to order. we didn't have to wait long to get our coffees. also something to consider is how there are empty seats on a sunday, considering how everyone drinks coffee on sunday afternoons.

as a former employee (partner), i can say without a doubt that the drive thru window staff is a nightmare. in the morning, add 10-15 min to your drive thru order time. @management, please train new employees during day shift so we're not all late to work for bar drink orders. the baristas deliver, it's the order being taken slowly/incorrectly and slow handoff that delay the process. go to another location.

first time i ever remember asking for a refund, but after waiting almost 30 minutes for my order of one coffee i'd reached the end of my limit. it should never take 30 minutes to get an order from a well managed starbucks, and that to me is the issue at this one - very poorly managed. i ordered on the mobile app from my home at 9:10 am. the app told me my order would be ready at 9:25. at around 9:37, with my coffee order still back in the line, i asked for a refund. being busy is not an excuse - if you know you are going to be busy you plan for it with proper staffing. the music in the lobby was very loud. from where i was standing if my name had been called, there was not way i would be able to hear it from employees calling names at normal volume level. again, just a total lack of awareness of the manager on duty today. it will be a long time before i return to this location. i learned today they don't really appreciate me as a customer, so i'll take my business elsewhere.

legit north county spot. it's in a crappy lot by a strip mall but has a lot of parking. really close to the hwy too! the inside is very, very small. the drinks are of normal starbucks quality. the location over by dunn and 270 is larger and nicer.

good furniture. i like this location because they feature the most comfy chairs and couches of any starbucks in south tampa. that makes hanging out here a pleasure. the menu is the same as anywhere else and the staff turns over, so what else can you say? if you want to linger, then this one is your starbucks location to choose. it's also next to a panera bread if you want a pastry or sandwich and don't like any of the starbucks offerings.

since i came twice this week i'll write about both experiences. earlier, i ordered via drive thru. the lines are usually long at this location. today was lite in comparison. even with a lite line their drive thru service can be a bit slow but that could be perspective since we expect things almost instantly in a drive thru line. i ordered my iced doppio as usual and it was made perfectly with all of my little additions. if you order banana nut loaf, ask them to warm it up. it will change your world. today i ordered an iced doppio and it didn't turn out as expected. so they actually have on the menu an iced doppio that comes grande and i think there are things added in it that i'm not too fond of. so if you want a double espresso just ask for a double espresso... i'll have to remember that next time other than that my bacon gouda egg sandwich was great. the only negative experience this trip was coming inside the store this time and with only 2 people in front of me, it still took while before i was able to order. guess i'll have to use the app more to order from this store specifically.

i go to this starbucks almost every morning before work and would just like to write this to say how absolutely amazing skyler is! i don't think i have ever dealt with a more pleasant and polite person at any business, coffee or otherwise. there have been a couple great baristas at this location since they opened but none as awesome and consistent as him. his attitude and personality just makes you smile and starts your day off better.

on a saturday morning this was the only option for me to get a hot drink when i was in cab as both tim hortons were closed. there was just one guy working so he would take the orders and make the drinks. good thing it wasn't busy and just one customer in front of me and then another came after i ordered. there was just one sign board but i saw all the tea bags so i ordered my mint one. the guy working there asked if i wanted the one with caffeine or not. i haven't even been asked and i didn't even know they had mint in both options. he told me the jade citrus mint was decaf but after i checked and it's mint majesty that is decaf. i didn't mind either way and i always order the majesty so i'll continue ordering that one. i appreciated the offer and letting me know not all teas are decaf. after taking my order, he finished preparing the previous customer's drink and then got my tea before taking the next order. along the outer perimeter there are upright benches to sit on to wait for your order but didn't look comfortable to sit for long periods. also nice that there are 2 places to get lids and napkins.
the atmosphere is nice but the staff was not helpful and did not take my name. they appeared new or understaffed as they did not know what i asked for. not what i expect out of starbucks.

today i had to sit in an incredibly long drive through line to order my usual drink. no big deal, it's starbucks, and it's busy... i get it. when the drink was handed to me i could tell it was wrong but the employee was already speaking on the headset to the next customer so i decided to pull around and go in with my toddler in tow. i had to stand in another line of 7 people. finally i get to the counter and let them know this is not what i ordered. he said ok. they dumped the original drink and made the new drink in the same cup. no apology was given, no offer of a free bakery item, they didn't even let me keep the screwed up drink. i am baffled by this. the worst part is that the second drink i got was still not right. this is supposed to be a cloud macchiato. i get this drink all the time. i wasted 25 minutes of my time at starbucks today that should've been 5 minutes, for subpar coffee. won't be going here any more.

it was 5:15am, pouring down rain and freezing cold when i was trying to catch a cab on south street. there were none and i was standing under the starbucks awning for a few minutes when an employee opened the door and told me i could wait inside for my cab to arrive, even though they weren't open. it was so nice and thoughtful. i'll continue going there on my way to work :)

today we got two kids size chai teas, one vanilla latte, one cold brew. one of the kids chais was milk and the other had chai, i told the cashier and asked for a cup to mix the two together so they could each have some of the chai mix, she gave me the cup without offering to fix their drinks. the cold brew ended up being a nitro which we did not want. never had this kind of experience with starbucks.

i've always had a great experience at this location unfortunately today was not one of those days. i was sitting in the drive through watching my drink be made as the girl drops the cup and spills 50% of my drink. instead of remaking it she dumps what's left of my drink into the blender and adds tons of milk. totally lazy and very unsanitary.

mostly average starbucks. they are still a little slow, which could be due to being a new location. other than that, it is your average expensive high calorie coffee joint. so far, it's kept pretty clean, and the service is friendly. basically, it's an ice cream shop, loaded with caffeine, that no one looks down on me for having in the mornings. thankfully, it's located right next to a dentist. so after i have my java chip and a bunch of pastries, i can go next door and have all of my cavities filled.

best starbucks i have been to in the country. my wife and i frequented this starbucks quite often while we were waiting to move into our new home and have internet activated. we sat in the lobby for hours at a time. the lobby is very comfortable, the staff is very friendly, our orders are always correct and done quickly. can't say enough about this starbucks. i have frequented several and this is the best one hands down.

starbucks on the washu danforth campus (bauer hall-the business school). this means a few things: 1) changing hours based on the school calendar. when school is not in session, it may be closed or open for a short amount of time. 2) unpredictable hours. if the school has a special event, it may be closed. 3) lack of seating space during peak hours like lunch time. though there are a lot of tables, it shares the space with the b-school cafe, which has a lot of undergraduate and graduate students there all the time 4) you can use the app to pay, but can't redeem rewards or order ahead of time for pickup 5) wonderful staff!

definitely a fan of this starbucks. this is probably the largest starbucks i've been to in new orleans or new york or anywhere else for that matter. this starbucks has a decent sized outdoor seating area perfect for enjoying your coffee while watching life go by on magazine. the inside has a multitude of seating options. i like it in the back of the store where it is a bit quieter for getting work done. service is pretty quick and efficient although not very friendly, and i regularly frequent here. decent selection of sandwiches and pastries, but then again why eat this over priced crap when you have a plethora of high quality eateries up and down magazine street. stick to the coffee and the great location this starbucks has to offer...
always the best service during the week in the mornings. the staff is fantastic and friendly. i go mostly to see them and they make great drinks.
avoid the line. avoid the disappointment. avoid this starbucks. simply put, it just "isn't."

closest coffee drive thru from my house.. must be a starbucks thing to not give good service. orders never right. rude. just give me my coffee please correctly. not much to ask especially when i pay 5 6 bucks per cup.

this is a typical starbucks. there aren't any cushy chairs which is kind of sad and it's very crowded because it's right near a lot of classroom buildings for penn and drexel. however, the baristas are always very friendly and they try their best to move people through the lines. i would recommend going here during off-peak hours such as early in the morning or in the evening where you can usually get a seat.

this starbucks was okay. it was crowded but i was still able to get my work done. i ordered a hot chocolate with whipped cream and it was delicious. can't really go wrong with that. i was there until closing and towards the end of the night the staff was a little loud behind the counter. i do understand that it's the end of the night but i feel like starbucks is supposed to be a semi-tranquil place to get stuff done. i put my headphones in and it was fine but i don't think i should've had to do that. everything else about this starbucks is just like the rest. can't really go too wrong with a starbucks. parking is crazy, though. definitely not enough for the amount of people that go here.

i'm sure most would consider me somewhat of a coffee snob. i blame it on being from the seattle area, which is obviously also starbucks' home :) but i am in no way too good for starbucks. especially because when it comes for convenience and ease of acquiring said coffee on my way to work, nothing is easier than this place. usually hoppin' with all the people going to work in intech commons, etc (at least in the morning)... but the staff are nice and pleasant, and they do the best they can in a rush. thank you, for helping me out when my bod desperately needs to be caffeinated sometimes in the morning. also, my workplace thanks you for saving my productivity on those days. ps. if you ever get the bug up your backside to make it a drive-thru, do it! people will love it, and it may ease the congestion inside the store in the am. :)

dan is the man at this drive thru. actually, most of the staff that service the window are friendly, personable, attentive, accurate and prompt, but dan adds entertainment to the list. good coffee and other drinks. not liking the new items offered through la boulange or however, you spell it. most of the pastries need to be heated to taste good, as they are refrigerated or stale tasting otherwise. on the plus side, they seem to have more offerings than in the past as far as pastry variety. quality of food is no where near panera, but tops on beverages. you will encounter long lines at windows on rush hour. parking lot is tough and not driver friendly. use caution.

small location located in a small webster groves shopping plaza. surprisingly parking is a breeze and the café is easily accessible. there's minimum seating inside and outside on the sidewalk patio. plenty to explore within walking distance in case you'd like to park & stroll. staff is very pleasant. ~christy

i've been to several starbucks all over the states as i'm sure many people have, but this specific location is the best. the crew they have working there is the most friendly group ever. i guess i can only speak for the early morning staff because that's when i go, but i always challenge them with made up drinks and complicated orders and they do a great job. also, while they are trying to figure out my drinks they are good at small talk and don't just leave you sitting there in the drive through like a number. i can't speak anymore highly of this group. keep it up!

the only thing good about this location was that the two ladies working it were very friendly. long lines and slow service. the prices were so much higher than any other starbucks. they crossed out the prices with black sharpie in the display case and increased them when putting in the register. an egg wrap and a latte was $20.

props to ryann for being super nice and making me a new green tea soy latte when i just asked for more soy milk (since the green tea powder was chunky and undissolved at the bottom) awesome customer service!!!

for me there are more cons than pros to this starbucks. i have visited this location about five times. two of those times i have had borderline rude customer service - which i think is rare for starbucks. i have usually had to wait in a long line. there are a lot of tables in the place, which is good if you need a table, but if you're waiting for your order you have to cram in around the tables - it's just awkward. don't even get me started on the parking - it's in a strip mall so you have to compete for parking with krogers customers. that all said - the drinks i've ordered have all been tasty. i'm a bellevue resident and just glad we have a coffee shop in the area.

yay for drive thru. yay for patio. yay for change tables in both men's and women's washrooms! yay for lots of natural light and seating. boo for waiting a looooooong time for my drink. yay for friendly barista. boo for pretty small merch options in a location this big. yay for coffee!

very quick service. very reluctant to get dark roast at any starbucks especially at end of day. usually taste burnt. not at this starbucks. nice clean and fresh i got the gold coast dark roast. will for that reason and heat service make this a frequent stop when in the area. would of given 5 stars but parking is too limited

it's starbucks, so if you've been before, you know what you're getting, right? service has always been great here, friendly and professional. they know how to make starbucks drinks, so, again, you know exactly what you're getting. nothing here pushes it into 5 star range for me, but i've always received fast, friendly service, and my order has always been correct.

i usually don't leave comment to starbucks but this location is so lovely i actually went there twice in an afternoon....the building itself looks interesting enough but the inside's even better! it's the coziest starbucks i've even been to. it got a fireplace and soft leather sofa. it's not overly crowded and the staffs were very friendly. i didn't want to leave!
worst starbucks in a 30 mile radius. dirty, poor customer service, repulsive barristas and incorrect drinks. i'd like my $5 lattes to come with a nice experience.

today, i went to starbucks. starbucks is literally my favorite place to go, i ordered my usual today then got up to the window and saw that my drink; had to cups on it, i wondered why. so, i took the other cup off and it was leaking!!! they could have transferred it into another cup on top that it had way too much ice in it, i couldn't even get my straw in. the lady offered to help and poured some of the ice out, she was very nice. but, i've had problems with this starbucks before. both of us (my mom & i) received drinks that did not taste like usual. step up your game starbucks!

small location inside a rest area but with a full menu. the milk and sweeteners are done by the baristas, not at a station, so make sure to ask for what you need. service was slower than at dedicated locations but still fairly friendly. not bad for a quick pick me up on the road.

me and my sister ordered separately, i got my drink right away, we ended up having to wait 20 minutes for my sisters, me having to go up to the counter and remind them more than once that she ordered right after me, and people that ordered a long time after her were getting their drinks and leaving. they said they didn't remember getting her order and then didn't jump into making gets right away. they didn't apologize and were snotty when we tried to explain she was right after me. myself having worked at a starbucks kiosk, with only 1 or 2 on shift at a time, we handled lines well and never gave another a drink if they ordered after someone, this starbucks, with very inconvenient parking, and one of the largest staff i've seen, took so long for the drive thru and were running around inside forgetting multiple orders after ours and were not very friendly if it was brought up that they had forgotten. the only thing i liked was the design of the building.

this starbucks location has always been problematic. i go to numerous starbucks..and this location comes up short in terms of customer service. there are some excellent baristas..especially. jc , amy, john and ben. however, this is counterbalanced by the vast majority of the baristas who show gross deficiencies in customer service skills. there is a homeless population who patronizes this store. i am ok with that..and , in fact, i am on friendly terms with most of them. the problem comes.. once again..with the baristas who tend to see any person of color.. who is a regular ..as freeloaders and/or homeless. everyone should be treated with respect regardless of their circumstances. ..and the staff needs to stop painting patrons with a broad brush. what differentiates each starbucks is the level of customer service. this store consistently ranks at the bottom in this regard. i prefer other starbucks where the customer service and training appears to be far superior.

congratulations to this mindless group of assholes that work at this location for losing a lifelong starbucks customer for good! after ordering my drinks with my mask on, i went and sat down in a lobby full of maskless customers and removed my mask while i waited for my order. i was promptly told to put my mask back on or go wait outside in the cold. when i asked what the difference was between me waiting for my drink in the lobby with no mask on and the other paying customers sitting in the lobby with no mask on, she said "they already got their drinks". when i said "fine can i have some water?" she told me no, and told me to get out. this is not about keeping anyone safe anymore. not that it really ever was. this is 100% about keeping people in line and making them follow absurd rules for no rational reason. there is absolutely no difference between not wearing a mask while you're waiting for a drink and not wearing a mask while you have a conversation with your friend and a drink on your table. i demanded a refund and told her that her customer service is garbage and that because if her the company is losing business. she then says "the company is doing just fine" and handed me my money. all while her cloth mask was hanging half off of her face. screw starbucks.

the line can be really long in the drive through.. going inside seems to be punishment for not waiting in the drive through! i tried that once and they filled 5 drive-through orders for every body inside it seemed! drinks are always yummy though!

best starbucks in tampa! the manager here, dena, is absolutely amazing! very cheery, always willing to assist staff as well. they take great pride in their starbucks and it shows! very relaxing atmosphere by the way... there is sand and lounge chairs right outside the front door. order your favorite starbucks drink and go sit out by the bay. tip: the starbucks is located in the westin hotel. if you are driving westbound on highway 60 toward clearwater, you can easily turn in and out of the hotel. but if you are driving eastbound toward tampa, it's a little more tricky. when driving eastbound, turn left at the light before the westin (rocky point drive) and then take a right at the three way stop. you can then turn right into the westin -drive past the valet - and turn left into the parking lot. when leaving, make sure to go back the way you came and turn left at the rocky point light to continue on your eastbound track. (the hotel exit onto 60 only allows right hand turns) :) you're welcome!

my previous review had taken into consideration some growing pains at this location. sadly nothing has changed. a simple order of "coffee-black", paying, and then hearing "i only have enough for a tall, can i too it off with something else?". who would have thought a cup of their medium roast would end up impossible 80% of the time? --- 12.8.16 - update "sorry we don't have any coffee", offers me an americano instead and charges the full price difference. talk about customer service! still single star'ing it here.

i used the drive thru today and ordered an iced lemon pound cake to go with my coffee. received a blueberry pastry instead and didn't realize it until i got home. from now on, i should check to make sure what they said is in the bag is actually correct.

this is actually the second time i've been to this starbucks; i was here last night. i thought maybe last night was a fluke so i decided to try them again today. it was no fluke. last night i tried to order and iced trenta peach infused green tea. they were out of this too. so i tried to order another green tea. i was asked if i wanted black tea, green tea, etc. i restated green tea. this morning i went inside to order a grande iced chai tea latte and see if they perhaps had lids to their cold cup commuter cups. i placed my order for an iced grande chai tea latte. the barista asked me if i wanted my drink iced and what size. i hesitated but continued and asked her if perhaps they had any extra lids for their cold cup commuter cups as i had purchased 2 cups but that location was out of lids. she made half an attempt to look for lids and advise me they didn't have any.i'm beginning to think i must be a glutton for punishment for all the times i return to starbucks and continuously receive poor service and not the beverage of my choice.

not every starbucks is the same. you can easily separate the cream from the crop when you look at their level of service and consistency in making their drinks. this starbucks unfortunately curdles and drops to the bottom of the pack. the service is just plain bad compared to other starbucks, it takes forever to get served and the drinks do not taste as good as other locations. it is obvious that the management here has a long way to go in living up their corporate standards.

walked from hotel. sign on door lobby temp closed, whatever temporarily means...no explanation, not a covid thing as was open for carry out the other day. drive-thru thriving otherwise i would have walked up to window. forget me i guess.
very loud and obnoxious employees. not a place to relax in peace, drink a cup of coffee and study, read, etc. do not recommend.

so i placed an online order and when i went to pick it up, that's always great to be able to customize and place your order on the app. so when i went to pick it up, it was already, but i did ask for a cup with ice, there three gals working behind counter. as the gal is serving the ice i notice that one gal, has her hair loose, a kind of ponytail but lots of hair coming out from it, and she keeps playing/touching it with her hand. it's just so not right!!!! specially or someone handling food/drinks it's just unsanitary!!! honestly during covid and all she needs to keep her hands super clean, why are sanitation rules being enforced? my drink was a cold drink which was a different gal doing, or at least i hope it wasn't the girl with her hair all over the place. not going back to this location for now.

poor customer service. the screen where you can review your order is often not working and if you try to correct the order at the window they seem annoyed. i ordered a cold brew with cream and at the window it had no cream so i politely asked that they add cream. i watched as the barista proceeded to pour out half the coffee and add cream to the point where my cold brew was completely undrinkable. this has happened on multiple occasions. they will also sometimes offer a cup of cream on the side and if i refuse (because i cannot mix my drink while i'm driving) they dump out half the coffee and add an unreasonable amount of cream. i have worked in customer service for years and as a bartender and it is not difficult to correct a simple mistake. on this occasion they were not even busy.

nothing i can say about starbucks that has not been said already. the coffee is excellent and the service is wonderful. on this particular day, i purchased an ice tea because it was hot and was walking around downtown indy. the ice tea was refreshing. i can always appreciate starbucks whenever i have a craving for coffee. it is terrific and makes me feel good. a great pick-me-up in the morning!

starbucks is known for reliability of their products and customer service. this location, however, has the rudest staff i have ever encountered at any starbucks. pushy, rushed, would not allow me to add to my order once it was completed (essentially denying a sale for the business). very rude and unlike starbucks.

great starbucks with awesome staff. the coffee is always fresh, and it is served with a smile (seriously). the only thing that keeps this one from being a five star in my book is how small the store is inside. it's a good spot to grab a quick cup of coffee, but not so great to chill for a while.
love this location! every chance i get i run here for a coffee. friendly and pretty quick!

of the many starbucks i have been to across the us and canada, this one most consistently gets the orders wrong and has the most uninterested and bla se employees i have encountered. there is so little to meet the expectation of an experience here that it is just another place to get coffee. i prefer to go to the starbucks kiosk in target though it is much less convenient.

any place that makes my coffee and other drinks amazing. will always get 5 stars from me. not to mention it is walking distance from where i live. so just a hop, skip and i'm there. guilty of being a starbucks lover. although some of them aren't the best this place here rocks. it can get a little busy since it is located within the outlet stores. so you get creative and download the app to order ahead so when you walk in your order is right there awaiting you. no complaints about this starbucks, kudos and keep the coffee's coming.

i like starbucks in general, but this particular location is fantastic. it is definitely not your typical starbucks. it was remodeled a few months back and now features a very cool open terrace mezzanine seating level above the bar. they also have some outdoor seating on state street. this location also has one of those clover brewers and several varieties of high-end coffee available that they custom-brew for you. this location is across the street from my office and i go there several times each week. most of the staff knows my name and they also know my drink depending upon what time of day it is that i come in. this seems to be the case for alot of other customers besides too. that's impressive when you consider the volume of business they do here. the interior is kept clean at all times and due to the unique floor plan, ther eis almost always somewhere to sit as soon as my drink is ready. there are a few starbucks location on state street in downtown santa barbara, but i think this location is the best.

this starbucks is kind of special because it's located in this old converted house on university avenue. during the winter-time when it gets cold, they light the fireplace inside and it is quite nice to sit there with a hot cup of coffee.

my sister and i are frequent starbucks customers. this locations customer service was beyond mediocre. we walked in and none of the store associates greeted us or asked us if we needed assistance. we waited about 5 minutes then asked if someone could help. starbucks should be a company that trains all associates on high caliber customer service.

i don't what it is about this starbucks - location, drive-thru or what, but it is always jumping! i went on a wednesday at 7 pm, and a few days later on saturday at 11:30 am, and both times the drive-thru line was long, the line in-store was long, and almost all of the seats were filled. on both trips the line moved quickly, and i was greeted with the wonderful trademark customer service that is expected from starbucks. there's not much else to say, the service, drinks and food are consistent with other starbucks across the city, but out of all the ones that i've visited, i'd have to say this is the best for people watching!

do usual starbucks menu or usual starbucks just interested but polite customer service surrounded by potholes that could destroy your car recommend going to a different starbucks if that's what you want if you just want to get a cuppa coffee stop by speedway or circle k

disorganized, the slowest service i've ever seen a starbucks have, and they run out of supplies every time i have ever been there (even ice). i tried so many times but i won't be going back to this one.
been here often for coffee to go and in house. service has always been great and order has always been correct. yes it gets extremely busy but i've always seen the staff do their best

all brand spanking new of course, this new location shares a space with a mattress direct and features two separate vehicle entrances and a drive-thru. outside seating totals 4 tables while inside has a large table of 10, a 4-top, 4 couch seats and a couple of 2-seater stools. free wifi of course and standard menu items, staff is friendly! the place is right across from the sunset hills shops like home depot, old navy, bed bath etc. so it's convenient during shopping trips, it looks like they are also catering to the business park community in the vicinity. i'm not even a starbucks fan but i like trying new places. the vanilla sweet cream cold brew isn't bad although i thought it would be sweeter. everything here looks to be in order and clean, and i hope it would be seeing as they just opened their doors.
this is a study hall starbucks. always full and busy. like all starbucks, good coffee. but like all starbucks in santa barbara, the atmosphere is bare bones.

as much as i try to avoid starbucks because of how pricey it is, i always find myself coming back. one of my favorite locations is this one which is right across the street from where i work. they just recently moved into this shopping center from their old location that was next door to peg's glorified ham and eggs. i will say, having the drive through really is nice. i used to go to the old location but there was no drive through so this is definitely more convienent. i love the baristas, i always get a warm greeting every time i make a visit, especially from tiffany and a few of the others that i cannot remember their names. the inside of the store is beautiful, it is much larger than the otger location and very open with lots of seating. i haven't had a bad coffee from here yet. i usually get a flat white with coconut milk and it always tastes great, i'll be back in the next week or two i'm sure.
