if there were 0 star option i would take that option. this place doesn't deserve my money, this place has the worst coffee and worst customer service that i have ever been.

i purchased a grande vanilla bean frap and while driving halfway home back to my hotel pulled a hair out of my mouth i then looked inside to cup to notice two more hairs in the drink!!! called the manage and said said for me to come back that day or the next and would buy me a new drink and also make it up to me. i drove back the next day and the girl working offered me another drink, which i did not want. so they just have me a refund, nothing more! really surprised they did nothing beyond that! #thumbsdownstarbucks #stillgrossedout
excellent location, clean and app orders are ready promptly. only suggestion would be more consistency in their brewing.

this starbucks location is conveniently located off route 130 right off a turn. i always seen it and thought it was busy. but this morning i had extra time before i started work so i gave it a try. there were probably about 5 cars ahead of me but it moved pretty fast. seemed like the staff knew what they were doing and moving orders right along. i asked for a white mocha and the barista suggested a grande toasted white mocha. so it was my first time trying it. pretty good i must say. but if you drink a white mocha you know it's hard to mess it up. just a whole bunch of sugar :-) i love it tho. i paired it up with one of the breakfast sandwiches on the healthier side: the egg whites, cheese and turkey bacon one. which was a great compliment to my drink. now i know where to stop by for my starbucks fix.

starbucks needs to check the freshness dates on their meal-sized plastic box treats. seems at times freshness dates are overlooked. snack and coffee freaks be aware. coffee is hot, or coffee drinks are ice cold. as we all know starbucks has a coffee version for everyone. the hot breakfast sandwich choices do not disappoint. this applies to any starbucks anywhere it seems. at the corner of state and victoria in sb the starbucks crew is always friendly and nice. they do fix my orders just right every time. no worries at this location on my coffee requirements. a most convenient location at times! use the app, it makes it so easy to get in and out of this busy starbucks. if you need a coffee upgrade or a new flavor version, handlebar is not too far away.

the service here is good and they have a cute outdoor seating section. but as others have noted, the parking lot is awful and it is often 100% full in the morning and at lunch time (the only times i've ever stopped by).

how hard is it to pour liquid into a cup to the proper height of the cup. i took the lid off and the coffee instantly starting pouring out. not little drops, but pouring out obviously overfilled and spilling in my truck, on my clothes. you don't want to leave room for cream and sugar, that's fine, dont fill it to the rim thinking nothing can happen. last i check there's still gravity and pressure on earth so movement and grasping the cup will change the dynamics of the damn contents inside the cup people!

one of the better starbucks in the area. the staff is always friendly and quick to get your drink the way you want it. the staff is always friendly and extra helpful when completing your order. whether its five in the morning or during the rush they greet you with a smile and make you starbucks experience a great one...

obviously, this has all the same offerings as a regular starbucks. but, because it's in a penn building, there are some pros/cons: pros: - lots of seats and study space - nice outdoor seating options - dining dollars from penn meal plans can be used - convenient campus location cons: - not allowed to use mobile app for payment - they don't accept any starbucks deals or coupons (however, they do participate in some campaigns such as frapp happy hour.) - few outlets for laptops despite large study space - gets busy/full during school year - staff can be rude/sassy, atypical of starbucks (you can't even get a glass of water)

this is my all-time favorite starbucks location! i have been a loyal starbucks gold card member for some years. there have been a lot of good, even some great locations... but this one by far is my favorite. it is very convenient for me because it is on my way to work, but there are times i have had to go to other locations for different reasons. every time i do, i am disappointed because of the high bar this location has set. the manager is exceptionally helpful, friendly and professional. the staff is amazing. friendly, upbeat, proficient. i have never had problems with my order. they're smiles make my day! kudos! i'm a fan! ~annie kidd

i guess it was ok, i am just use to the staff at my usual sb's remembering me. spoiled yes. the staff was nice, and the coffee exceptional. i paid with my iphone app. that's always fun. i will go back to my usual starbucks from now on.

i wish this location would get it together! half the time items are unavailable, the workers have attitudes, or my order is incorrect. i only keep coming back here since it's the only one close to the neighborhood.

anything starbucks makes my heart skip a beat. love the vibe, love the atmosphere love the drinks. always good and always relaxing. don't have time guess what there is a drive through. also a free parking lot. getting on the lot is a struggle but it's worth it.
best starbucks in the area! only a drive through would make this a 5 star. friendly staff makes it a great place for coffee.
my experience was amazing! my cashier gabe was very thoughtful and answered all my question. he made a fantastic drink and got it out fast. i would definitely come again!
what do you say about starbucks..this location friendly service and good americano..like the most

i am very upset with the service at this starbucks. when i ordered a pink drink through the drive through, they got my order wrong, so naturally i go back inside and ask that they remake my drink. the employees smirk at my request and proceed to make me a drink, and the drink they made me was basically just coconut milk. these employees were rude, incompetent and careless. if i could give this starbucks a lower rating i would, but it isn't possible. this is the drink the starbucks employees gave me versus what the drink is supposed to look like.

virginia, who was working the drive through today, is probably the best employee that they could ever ask for on the customer service end. unbelievably sweet. on top of that, she knew the name of the person in front of me and knew what they would be ordering before they ordered it. she just had it ready. never let go of her.

just like any other starbucks. it's a light or two away from the interstate, not yet into downtown greenwood. plenty of parking but not enough seating. place was packed when we went. bathroom was kept clean. drink was made perfect.

headed out on my morning walk with my mother as always we were in search for a starbucks. opening our search on our starbucks app we saw the magazine st. location! excitement! we decided a good 3 miles was just what we needed to receive the deserving coffee taste that only starbucks could provide. when we arrived and prepared our starbucks card app we noticed how huge it was! beautiful outdoor seating and comfy chairs and tables for the masses. everyone is so friendly here!! and my mother not a fan of public restrooms came back with a great report, not only were they clean but they were big! two thumbs up for that! we grabbed our breakfast muffins and warm tall coffees and headed outside to enjoy the crisp morning air. where everyone greeted us with a morning salutation! we will definitely be incorporating this starbucks in our daily walks when in louisiana! thank you!

definitely not the best location. i was thinking with it being so new, maybe that's what kept everyone continuously slow and rude. i decided to just start going inside due to that being much quicker than the drive thru. there are two blonde guys who work there that are amazing and have a great personality, but every woman that i've come across there is just mean. hopefully it picks up and their attitudes improve..but for now, i'm sticking to my coffee and tea at home.

as i've been forced to travel more, starbucks has become my in-city lay over after i check out of my hotel and before i head to the airport. this place is great. it's located in a(n) historic locale (on a cobblestone street(!!). i was all like is this rosewood/ravenswood?) the seating is ample and the bathrooms are clean. not as cool as the concept starbucks in amsterdam, but up there with some of the more cozy and quaint starbucks i have visited.

why no sign about being closed at the entrance to the drive thru or even the board where you give your order??? i waited behind another car for ever and then pulled up and tried to order not knowing that you were closed. there was a sign on the drive up window but what good does that do? i drove around after finding out that you were closed and told the 6 cars waiting in line about being closed. you are welcome! this was on 8/8/2020 in the afternoon.

this location is not super busy, and seating is comfortable. however, no sofa style so perhaps designs for students or business folks that need desk space. also, there is no drive thru if you care for that. the staff is very friendly and drinks are on par to starbucks standard.

well i was just here around 11 on a week day watched 6 people go threw line before me some with multiple drink cashier took in well over 60$ right in front of me i came to the register and ordered my drink was 6.77 i had 6 or 100$ cashier says well i can give you change for that and but you can get a smaller drink ? well no not what i want or ordered how can such a large business not have change mid day for 100 when i just watched them uptake almost the sum of my change ??

this location was packed on this beautiful sunday so we had to wait a little longer than normal. because if this the wonderful staff gave us our drinks for free. everyone should go to this starbucks if you want incredible service from an amazing and hard working staff. thank you for making our day so special!

line was almost out the door and they only had 2 people working behind the counter. took over 45 minutes to order and get my coffee. hire more people if your store is this busy because that wait was ridiculous.

omg people weren't lying when they said this is the "slowest" starbucks. we waited easily 20+ minutes in the drive thru when there were maybe about 5 cars in front of us. it reminded me of the sloths in zootopia! and on top of that, i ordered a skinny peppermint mocha frap which i'm not sure why, but they topped with salted caramel syrup. the guy working the drive thru window was nice though. i just don't recommend going here if you are in a rush. you'll definitely be late.

i think it's hard to review chain restaurants like starbucks because they have so many company-specific standards to meet that you really should have a similar experience there no matter what location you go to. my visit to this location was exactly like any other starbucks i've been to, except for the wifi. i was greeted when i walked in, the baristas were friendly, i got my drink quickly, and i was very happy with my chai tea late. however, i planned on spending a couple hours at starbucks--i had a lot of school work to do and i drove several miles from my house to come to this location. but it totally wasn't worth the trip because the wifi was incredibly slow. when i was watching my lectures, they kept stopping and needing to buffer every 5 minutes. i've never had this problem anywhere else. the slow wifi was so annoying that i left early and went down the street to corner bakery cafe--where i was able to watch my lectures without a single problem. definitely won't be coming back to this starbucks to study again.

this starbucks is very rude, always seem to have smart remarks. aren't very updated on new drinks and almost try to make you feel stupid for saying something wrong. this starbucks location is a bad review in itself.
pretty simple to serve starbucks but the guy with the ponytail got both drinks wrong. had to redo one, due to allergies but i kept mine (a preference omission). sad

they just remodeled this starbucks and its much nicer now. very open and lots of big tables to share with fellow coffee shop junkies. i'm not a huge fan of starbucks coffee, but since its basically across the street from where i live, it makes it very convenient when i need my caffeine fix. the staff is very friendly and accommodating.

ok so when i go to this starbucks, on occasion, bonus points because i use the stairs and not the escalator - i feel welcome right away. the little cart/stand underground just has a certain positive energy for me. anyway - my drinks always seem to be right, and delicious, and fairly quick for a busy location. twice i was there and deasia heped me out - her warm smile and happy demeanor was enough to make my entire day!! fyi- its kinda small because its just a little coffee stop but it is in a great location and always well staffed. the staff is always pleasant and accommodating. i will always come back here when i am around the area.
seriously the slowest starbucks ever! there's always a ton of people working yet it takes 15 min to get an americano. don't even get me started on the drive thru.

worst service i've ever received from a starbucks. they had four employees "working" while most of them were standing around while there's was eight people waiting for there drinks. the manager on duty or supervisor didn't seem to have any sense of urgency on getting the drinks made and neither did the employees. terrible experience and even worse service
very good coffee. great for any morning fix. moderately crowded, but has good cappuchinos. i love the smoothies. tip: bring food from starbucks to he movie theater for a movie snack.
free wifi, free power for devices, over priced drinks. exactly what you would expect.

this is by far the worst starbucks in the city of philadelphia. long lines, one person making drinks, five other people that i think are "working" are just hanging out behind the counter talking about scheduling and paper towels. this place is a poor excuse for a starbucks. do yourself a favor and don't go here unless you have 45 minutes to waste.

early-morning coffee . this starbucks is great. every time i come here it is very clean. the people that work here are always very quick and efficient. always amazed at how they can make such complicated drinks so quickly. love my grande nonfat no foam pumpkin spice latte with a dab of whip cream. plenty of parking always friendly and helpful staff.

friendly staff. i never know what to order and feel pressured usually bc there's a line behind me of caffeine deprived, impatient people. but today was diff and the cashier helped me pick something to have. the toffee nut iced coffee and it was really good. glad i listened.
great location! tpa land side terminal is newly remodeled and the starbucks is located in a great place. if you are dropping someone off it's a great place to grab a quick coffee!
maybe don't have a drive thru if it's slower than going inside. everyone was very nice but i think they need to work on getting people through the line faster.

not up to the high standards of service that most starbucks are know for. i ordered a triple grande caramel mach and when i got it, it felt like only two thirds full. i was in a hurry so i didn't complain as i should have. as i was leaving i stopped to go to the restroom and a pregnant young woman coming out warned me there was no toilet paper. i went into the other restroom and it was also out of paper and dirty. very disconcerting.
this starbucks is so slow if you want to grab and go this is not the one to go to!. waiting in line over 15 minutes!!

i love this starbucks so much. the staff is always so friendly and wonderful. they know our orders in+out and can recognize us in the drive through. i feel at home there and the workers seem to enjoy being with each other which i absolutely love and is so important to me to see them having fun while working. i really do love them all and consider them as friends. i always say bye when i leave to everyone and hear a chorus of "bye sarah" while i'm walking out. one of my favorite places to be. i love you guys!!

by far the worst starbucks i've ever been to. there was a worker by the name of brooklynn who snapped on her employees and was not very jovial at all. in the service industry service with a smile is important. i enjoy having coffee to uplift my mood, but my espresso energy was drained from my body from the sheer toxicity of this place. the employees have to work with this person? how they manage to make it out alive during a shift is beyond me.cake pops still rule though.
unfriendly staff, bad customer service, and the beverages don't taste good. unfortunately, most of the starbucks in clearwater are this way.

does not accept starbucks gift cards. had my gift card in hand on the counter the entire time while ordering. only came to starbucks to use a gift card and ended up having to use cash. manager was actually the one to take my order.

i really just love this starbucks for it's convenient location and it's spacious patio and the indoor is just as cozy, with plenty of seating for both. of course i love to swing in and get their green tea or vanilla lattes. i just find this location best in the area. it's located next to boundary and the cheshire for conviency. you can hop on 40 easily or just head down clayton. but this place has about 15 outdoor tables!!
so confusing, awful drivethru/walk up window set up. if it's busy good luck figuring it out.

i placed a mobile order for a venti iced coconut milk macchiato and a berry trio yogurt. the employees ignored my order for 45 minutes. when i went to ask, they rudely said that i probably ordered it at a different location and demanded to see my receipt. after showing them, they made my drink and forgot about my yogurt. worst starbucks experience ever and i'm a gold member. i go here all the time, but this was the worst service i've ever had.

the baristas here are great and it's definitely a great spot for people-watching on south street if you are able to grab a window table/seat. it's always busy but the service is very friendly and i have yet to receive an incorrect order. plus, they also have the evolution sweet greens juice, which i haven't been able to find elsewhere.
this location is always super busy but service is quick and friendly! even in the morning you're never waiting that long.

the coffee is good here and the car line typically moves efficiently. if you're in the mood for a good, convenient coffee, this starbucks works well. i haven't been inside so i can't vouch for the interior service or setting.
don't go here if you have any kind of time constraints. slowest starbucks ever!! took 10 minutes to order and another 20 to get my order.

the ladies at this starbucks in target are so disrespectful and spoke to me like i wasn't even a costumer. i asked if i could please have a java chip frappuccino and the cashier said "we actually aren't making fraps right now" and i said "why not?" and she said "because we just aren't". in a very rude tone, and i was taken back by how she spoke to me. then, they gave me a huge attitude about it and ended up making the frap for me.

i have never been to a slower starbucks and a few of the employees were off-putting. while you wait for your drink, you get to watch how the staff works. one man making sandwiches throws them on the counter and a very tall worker with a plaid blue shirt tries to be funny by using ridiculous voiced and being condescending to people. they got all my drinks right, but took them forever and the employees did not make for an enjoyable experience. update - still the slowest starbucks i have ever been to.

if there was a score of 0, i would give it! 25+ minutes for a simple hot chocolate. store was over staffed with 6 people behind the counter - not focused on helping customers, but hugging one another, playing on their phones, running around the store and going to the back of the store while ignoring customers. a lady who claimed she was the manager was oblivious when she finally came over to start making drinks to help , now making 7 people behind the register. she proceeded to scold me , that she was unaware of what her employees had been doing or not doing because she just got on the floor. in the meantime she had been less than 5 ft away putting up boxes! when i told her i had been waiting, she got annoyed with me, sarcastic, and never apologized or accepted responsibility for her and teams' attitude. if this is the best this store can do, then just close down, because your careless employees will ruin you.

the baristas are bunch beauties. drinks are always made correctly and make me feel as good as some cocaine on a monday morning. sounds depressing? actually it's the opposite. me and a couple boys last week did a full send and nailed some broads. woke up monday morning did the white and it made me feel like i was born in a new light.

went through the drive thru yesterday (4/5) and the person at the window was not wearing gloves when they took my money and handed me my drink. they were also not wearing a face mask when the mayor advised everyone to wear a mask. during this pandemic i expected the starbucks corporation to have better guidelines for serving their customers and protecting us all from the spread of the covid-19 virus. i feel it is dangerous for my family and the reno/sparks community to patron this location and all starbucks if they are going to continue these practices.

consistency and friendly service is the hallmark of any starbucks. this location has lived up to this standard. my order is always ready at the time specified on the app and given to me with a smile (you can tell even when masked). thanks starbucks!

i love starbucks! i can drink something from starbucks every day. this is the first starbucks i've ever seen with two bathrooms. usually, the men and women share. inside is clean, nice and neat. the service is great, they really go above and beyond for the customer at this location.

visiting the area and came here to order the drink i always get with my friend and they made them completely watered down and wrong. also talked shit right in front of my face and had horrible hospitality. if you want to spend your money and throw out what you bought right after then come here!

on fat tuesday you have a staff of 5. are you serious? you are on one of the most popular corner poydras and st. charles day of mardi gras and you don't properly staff your location. come on starbucks. they have one person on the register and on guy who looks like he is trying to look busy so he doesn't have to do work. this is ridiculous. the staff is moving slow as turtles no smiles no hi how are you. heads down they have one door open and the line is out if the door. the only good thing was i got bathroom tickets. do better

i am not a starbucks person but i do come in for tea sometimes. i have often noticed when in the broad ripple location that they have the absolutely nicest/best team. they are so happy, polite, professional and make you feel so warm and welcome. i like this location so so much. i come in when my daughter is at dj class. good stuff.

love this starbucks because there is an outlet wherever you sit. if you sit at the community table or at the bar, the outlets are right under the table/bar, facing down. outlets are on the wall for the rest of the seats. of course, it wouldn't be starbucks if the temperature isn't at freezing, so bring a jacket. there are also lots of seating outside. the baristas here are nice and very accommodating.

the workers where very rude. i bit my tongue and still said thank you after being ignored for 5 minutes so the employees could talk about there personal lifes. the coffee is pretty much the same at all the starbucks so the worker's make the experienced and they suck at this one.
saturday morning is crazy. everything was good, but 30 minutes to get your drink?! sheesh. should have had 1 instead of 2 on cash register and 3 instead of 2 making the drinks.

i can say the sunday afternoon staff was courteous and helpful! the area indoors and outside was clean. keep in mind thoughtless customers will not think of others and leave their trash on the tables! not cool! i am never feeling guilty when i have my skinny vanilla latte! vente and satisfying! i will also do a different bistro box which is always calorie frugal! i can do healthy at starbucks!

it's starbucks. my default rating for fast food chains is 3 stars, and they didn't do anything special to rise above that. they didn't do anything bad enough to lose a star either, though i was seriously tempted to drop one for the lack of drive-thru. there is at least a parking lot though.

this store by far is best, not all starbucks are the same, you can tell people like there job, some store don't take time to make a good drink and sometimes don't put extra shot but this store is a good starbucks.

great customer service and they try really hard for the volume of customers they get.. would be 5 stars if i didnt have to listen to all the complaining entitled students that expect a drink in 5 seconds when they see a line out the door.. try the french pressed coffee, i didnt even know that was an option until some one at the other store on campus by the library told me about it when they didnt have to, worth the wait.
had to wait 16 mins in line for 3 shots of espresso... didnt get a "hello" "sorry for the wait" or acknowledgement for that matter at the window. rude staff. unnecessarily long wait.

simple - my go to for iced coffee and the occasional chai latte. the germantown pike location is what keeps me going to starbucks simply due to their very nice young ladies that work there. starbucks is starbucks, but the baristas at this location really try and give the costumer what they want. that is why i am giving this chain a 4 star rating.
i mean come on it's starbucks and starbucks is amazing !!! this is got to be the best place to grab a bite to eat or the main purpose coffeee !! forever a customer

this starbucks is located in a big "gateway" shopping center and is my go to after i hit up saladworks or the deli or the taco joint at lunch time. in the morning it's swamped but at lunch i can usually get my four-bucks (try almost $5 for a grande iced macchiato) caffeine fix. le sigh...

i've always been a lover of starbucks, however this visit has changed my mind. my first few second of talking to the drive thru worker named dan were awful. i kindly asked him if we could pay separately for the two drinks and he refused. after i asked why, he said i should've asked earlier before coming to the window with a rude/sassy tone. he then proceeded to charge me wrong for the drink even though i said i wanted to use my free reward. (i still haven't got my starbucks money back by the way even though he used my free reward). while he was grabbing my drinks, he was acting annoyed while talking to his fellow co workers. on top of that, my order was made wrong and the coffee was cold by the time we received it. i will never be returning to this starbucks location again thanks to the horrible customer service i received.

wow order drink today. it was wrong and i thought they are supposed to make it right per there policy they did but wow why the attitude??? not happy as i visit sb daily may need to ban this location from my visits. i bet this location may not be open too long !!!
friendly end unique staff which you don't get at a lot of other starbucks. really has the broadripple charm. my drinks have always been made just right and never had anything i didn't like

excellent service!!! thank you for being so good!! had a small issue with the way my coffee was made but they fixed it to my liking right away!! the cashier was so so so sweet. i'm sorry i did not get her name!!

i just love having a bit of heaven in a cup on my way to work in the morning. such a treat! this location is brightly lit, clean and roomy. everyone we've met there has been friendly and helpful. the store is gearing up for christmas and have added some pretty nice shiny bits. free wi-fi , nice location and great company while enjoying my hot beverage. what more can i ask?
yes, it's just a starbucks, but i love this location. it's cozy and relaxed and a great place to work remotely in the middle of the day. the people are friendly as can be, too.

this location is always hit or miss. sometimes my order is great and sometimes my order is wrong. i counted once and figured out they had messed up my order more than 12 times out of maybe 20. and when i say messed up my order i mean either forgetting something, completing giving me the wrong item, messing up a drink order, or something else. it became an ongoing joke anytime i went there. the staff was usually helpful when i reported the mistakes but it just shouldn't happen that often to begin with.

this is definitely the best starbucks in the goleta area. the baristas are fast and make the best drinks. also, their iced coffee is much better and much stronger than the iced coffee at the fairview location. this one is also huge and has great wifi. they have really stepped up their game in the past few years.

i came today 2/19/21 at 8:50 the door was already closed! and they should be closed by 9pm and also they ladies was inside and they saw me and no one opened however they went out from the store after 9 so i don't find any reason as they closing early and also every one of them was having more than one drink! and was laughing and no one cared as the three ladies closed early than 9pm as the door should be closed by 9! last time i will come to this store and i don't recommend it to anyoneyou can save time and go to another store.

this is my favorite sbux in town. they are always friendly, and the drinks are well-made. the parking lot can be a little treacherous when they're busy...today i parked in front of snarf's (they weren't open yet) and walked across the drive through line. saved myself 20 minutes by getting out of the car and walking my happy a$$ into the building instead - just sit in your cars, people, it makes it that much faster for me. also, i love that the building reminds me of a spaceship and has easy access to the highway.

from what i've been told, this is one of the highest grossing starbucks outside of philly. for as busy as they are they can have a lack of urgency and an attitude of boredom but typically have their act together and getting a drink is usually a smooth process. attractive and comfortable inside. that being said, i have been going here twice a day since i moved here six months ago and not once has an employee ever used my name, which is a major faux pas in starbucks culture. parking can be a challenge. by the time you get all of the employees parking their cars it leaves a few spaces for customers thus i end up going through the drive through more than i would like which itself can be "challenged" at times.

i have stopped going to this particular location because of one thing: employee x. he is usually grumpy, always trying to sell people extra products, and has loud, awkward and inappropriate conversations with some customers. i've never seen him smile. even though this location is close and convenient for me, i won't return if mr x is working. if you go and he's working, you will know within a minute and you won't be pleased.

this location is super small and you will have to wait a bit for your drink. unfortunately i have gone several times already and each person that does my drink does it wrong or it doesn't taste right. also their lemonade is super sour. other locations use a premade carton so i don't know what crappy homemade lemonade they use but it taste pretty bad. i saw they pour it from a pitcher..that's how i know they are making it inhouse. also most of the employees don't understand the concept of light ice. by the way watch out that they don't over charge you on refills. it happened to me. i won't be going back. i keep wasting money everytime i go and i always leave unhappy.

an amazing crew that is serving out great coffee and genuine connections! terrance, emily, hannah, torrance, mo and betina quickly made this store one of my favorites and a great way to start the day. love nerding out on coffee with the baristas!!! 5.5 outa 5.

this is a pretty typical starbucks. i don't go here very often, but when i do, the drinks are always made exactly how i expect them to be made. the employees are always nice and chat a little bit at the window while waiting for the drink to be done or payment to go through. the patio is decent. no complaints on these things at all. where there is a complaint, however, is that this location is very small, and the parking lot is a terrible joke. it's on a very busy section of broadway, where many different commuters pass all day long. it gets super busy. it's not set up for super busy. the drive thru line is usually jammed packed and flowing out onto broadway, causing a major nightmare for busy traffic. it actually is dangerous at times. people will flat out stay in a traffic lane just to stay in that damned drive thru line. there's very little parking, so even if people weren't lazy as can be, there's still a hassle there. i wish they would redesign this location somehow to make the busy line flow better, or at least to get it off of broadway for safety reasons.

my current 3 star review of this starbucks location is based on a recent visit at the location and could change based on future visits. i really enjoy getting their pike place coffee and the spicy chorizo sandwich or the bacon and gouda sandwich for breakfast. but one morning i wanted to switch it up and ordered the steak breakfast wrap and put my order in through the mobile app. when my wife and i arrived to pickup our order she had gone inside to get our food and drinks. when she got inside there was a note on a bag that said see the cashier. my wife was confused and asked the worker at the counter what the note meant and they told my wife to go wait in the cashier line for help. after waiting in line the cashier informed her that they were out of the wrap and that they could make something else instead. my wife proceed to request a bacon and gouda sandwich and waited five minutes without anyone working on the sandwich. she went up to the worker who was making the food and asked when the sandwich would be done. the worker then threw down the tongs and said they would get to it right away in a somewhat attitude like voice. after waiting a total of 15 minutes for a sandwich when we had put the mobile order in 15 minutes earlier this was a pretty big let down. if we decide to come back to this location i will update my review as it reflects on the most recent service which i hope will be better than the last time.

not all starbucks are the same and there are plenty that are just plain corporate. i really like the eagle and franklin location for the following reasons 1. it's very personable. it's like cheers but for coffee. i know scarlet and several of the other baristas by name 2. there's outside patio seating as well as seating inside. the seating inside is portioned out nicely. sure, there's the community table but there are nooks and two tops designed for solo coffee drinkers 3. the location. it's easy to get in and out. some locations feel like you're driving in downtown tijuana. i just want coffee not a wacky race thanks eagle and franklin starbucks, you're a fan favorite, i just wish more locations were like you.

i frequent this location because it is so close to my work. sometimes going more than once a day! my drinks are always hot/refreshing and made just the way i like them. sometimes i'm even upgraded to a larger size at no charge. since i've been going here i see the same faces on both sides of the counter! that's saying a lot for a coffee shop. i often wonder if some patrons ever leave! after their recent facelift it has helped with flow of the queue that forms in the morning and lunchtime rush. less in shop seating now from the looks of it, but i've never actually sat and enjoyed my drink. the only thing negative i have to say is that they usually run out of sandwiches before lunch even starts! and there is one staff member who could benefit from smiling once in a while... but over all my experiences have been above average!

this morning while visiting reno i was surprised to find very few starbucks offer a drive through. i finally found this locationthe line was long, but it moved quickly. the employees were professional and friendly. thank you!
i will drive passed the other starbucks location in brownsburg to this one because they always make my drink right.

this is one of the starbucks that makes you sooooooo glad you can now order from the app! the people are normal customer service workers.. over worked, short staffed and under paid in sure as it shows in their work. this is not one of the fun hang out happy people starbucks. the building is nice, the lay out seems backward and causes a lot of congestion. and there for some unholy reason is always a line out the door! i have never walked out of a star bucks before getting coffee, the source of my life.. but i have here. it's just not worth the wait... they take forever! coffee is coffee.. but if you go here order from the app it's going to still take a while.. but it's less than waiting in that line!

it got two stars because its the closest starbucks to my house. every time i've come here my drink has been crappy. the last time i went through the drive thru they made my drink wrong and i had to go inside and have them fix it. the girl was nice and i was nice about it but jeez

oh how i love this starbucks, mainly because of where it's located. today was my first time to this location. it's been long overdue for starbucks in the neighborhood. it's very clean, nice decor, plenty of seats (inside and out), overall a nice space to enjoy the riverfront! the employees were welcoming and friendly. they seemed very knowledgeable of the drinks. however, they were very busy, lines were long and the service was slow. i ordered the usual, grande carmel macchiato, it did not taste as if it was made properly. i told the barista it tasted funny and they gladly made it again. this time it tasted a bit better lol. although i did not have the best service today i will go back to give them a second chance. if you live in the area definitely give them a try. im sure they'll get better with time.

this starbucks sucks. the customer service is horrible, they all act as if they don't want to be at work, and seem unhappy there. the first time i went, i ordered a small carmel macchiato with an extra shot. when i received my drink it was a bit watery. i let that one slide and didn't think much of it. however, second time around i ordered the same exact thing but a medium instead. it tasted horrible. again, it was so watery and had no flavor. i was so disappointed because i love starbucks. i will not be returning here again. save your money and go to a different starbucks.
the slowest starbucks on the planet. i watched grass grow while in the drive thru line.
this is the newest starbucks on the main line! it's the cleanest, has the friendliest, most informative staff, and the layout is perfect for working, studying or doing art!!!

tried a flat white for the first time on the recommendation of a friend (thanks amy p!) the first location i went to said no, i couldn't because they were out of whole milk. this location though, they were more than happy to oblige. the service was quick and efficient. the barista was both bubbly and sweet as she made my drink order. the drink itself was tasty, not sweet but rich on the tongue. next time i get this, i'll see if they can add vanilla to it.

i have to say that i am generally a big fan of starbucks for a) their customer service and b) their 'go green' mentality. however... the workers there seem more rude than i usually am when i wake up without caffinee. also the lady who glared at me for my 'complicated order' decided to charge me extra. when i said she charged me for the wrong thing... she just said 'it's how much it normally costs'. on top of that... she made my order wrong. i'd rather go to caje... that place tops all the other java and tea places here in iv.

this is a great starbucks --always clean-always organized and a great staff--mainly kristina who is speedy and makes my drinks perfectly always! i like that they always have community newspapers available for everyone to read but i don't like the cop who comes in regularly and always take the whole freaking pile of community newspaper and takes them home !!! hello! the papers are meant to be read there and recycled for others-- not taken with you! this place is better than the coffee shop up the hill where the staff talks way too loud!
regular starbucks coffee at the airport. entrance of terminal b. they actually have quite a few choices and get really busy.
i love this starbucks! it's got everything - nice barristas, beautiful atmosphere. i come here almost every day and it's always a pleasant experience.

we've been here three times as of this moment in the last month and haven't been able to get the pbj boxes. i'm not sure why they are on the menu if they aren't something actually carried here. makes no sense at all.

i had a meeting today at this location and it was a nice summer sunday @12:30. the place was crowded when i arrived with no available parking. i waited for about 5 minutes, parked and went inside. all of the tables were taken with electrical outlets which was fine because my pc was not totally dead. typical sunday morning crowd. in less than 15 minutes someone left and i was able to charge up. i ordered my drink and took the last 2 napkins. bummer. i wiped the table and in 5 minutes the napkins holders were full. i had to smile because this is great service. the place was clean and orderly. the only pet peeve was the free wifi was spotty and it took several attempts to login. meeting was great and it was another successful starbucks boardroom meeting. :)

so good. the people that work there are always so friendly!! the drive thru can be a little slow, but that is only because they take the time to craft your drink/food with love! i will miss coming to this starbucks when i move. o and the parking lot doesn't suck near as much as the rest!!

really people...dawn is how you spell the name of a woman named dawn. don is how you spell the name of a man named don. the coffee was fine, but seriously, did my beard fool you?? this spelling like this has happened too many times in this state, i'm just going to start calling everyone out that does it. stay tuned. also, why do you ask for our names in the drive thru??

it's a starbucks in a really neat building! i love how starbucks has re-claimed this space and made it usable again. the major downsides are that it's very busy and the drive through is incredibly slow. major upside is that this building is a two-fer and you can get chipotle next door.

slow. understaffed. impossible drive through. slow inside. horrible layout. even on a saturday morning this might be the slowest. update: i go all the time to this location. every time it's very slow. when not slow they mess up the order. maybe they are used to being behind so when they are caught up things get lazy.

honestly, for better coffee and better customer service, go to caje or even the arbor. i've been here three times and every time i had to wait for the employees to finish their conversation before i got hit with a rude "are you ready to order or what." i would've given an extra star for making my order correctly but they can't even do that! ordered a chai latte and got watery mud. don't even bother coming here.

good luck moving around in this clutter claustrophobia nightmare! if one wants to chillax at their local starbucks and do what one does while chillaxing at a starbucks, this location is not the location for you! if you wanna be crammed in a little space around a ton of rude people and hapless employees doing their best to serve said rude people, then this is the location for you! i choose to do neither, i'm out!

pros: the staff here are super friendly and maintain their composure during very busy times. cons: it often takes 10-20 minutes to get a regular or iced coffee if you order at the counter. also, some of the staff seem to have a knowledge gap when it comes to drinks and such. one guy was unsure how many espresso shots were in a drink and another person didn't know which sugar free syrups you had and just said "i think it's only vanilla" he didn't bother to ask or check. however, the most frustrating thing is the qa with some of the products. the last few times i have been in there the iced coffee was practically see-through it was so light. it seems like they added too much water to it. i've been drinking black iced coffee at starbucks for about 20 years, at locations all over the country, so i'm not just being a karen. i've actually had to ask them to replace it with cold brew, which usually looks better. i want to give them 5 stars because most of the people are nice overall, but some of the service aspects are lacking. hope they fix these things.

i'm a starbucks fanatic and i like to share the love around to all the different locations, meaning i need one around everywhere i go or i'm lost and sad, lol. well, i happened to be heading to help out at a stray rescue adoption event in fenton and swung by this starbucks to grab a venti pumpkin cream cold brew (amazing thing!). first thing i noticed was that these weren't happy people. the staff was unsmiling and there were so many behind the counter that they were tripping over each other. of course the lady in front of me in line was being a "not nice person" so i'm sure that didn't help. she wasn't nice when she came back in because her order was wrong, either. karma (or was it? lol). the manager, i'm pretty certain she was the manager, made a comment about it loud enough for everyone to hear, which was very unprofessional, in my opinion. my drink, which took forever, was delicious. my experience, not so much.

on the road and truly need some caffeine, went out of the way to get to a starbucks that was still open. arrived at 9:45 and barista refused to serve us, even though the coffee shop is open until 10pm. fine if i knew that, should change store hours to posting 9:30 then

this is more of a review for all starbucks in general, and specifically, for its chai latte. i've been on the hunt for great chai latte (since i've given up on coffee), and so far, starbucks ranks number 1! taking away one star because consistency in quality is not always there - from starbucks to starbucks and even within the same starbucks.
ever wait in cue at drive-thru for twelve minutes...and you're the only car? why would they need iced tea in 87° weather? at rush hour??!?! you just never know!

this is a reliable starbucks. it has moderate seating, not as much as some locations, but at least 3 tables and several relaxing chairs. it has a drive through- which in st. louis we know that not all do. it is always clean and i have never had any issues with orders.

service is so slow. i've been there multiple times and end up being in line for longer than 15 minutes each time just for one drink. i use to go to the old one and have incredibly fast service with 6 cars ahead of me. i prefer the original starbucks in brownsburg because they are faster when you are in a hurry.

seriously worst starbucks experience so far. ordered a pour over, the male cashier didn't have proper change, 3 other employees just standing there... i asked if someone else could start the pour over while he saw to the change. i was given just a regular coffee being told it was a blonde... this is ten minutes in. then i have to wait for 1/2 and 1/2. 15 minutes in no change yet old coffee that turned grey when adding the cream. my son comes in to look for me because it has been so long. i tell them the coffee is old, i get told it's not, bologna, smelled old, looked old... none of them looked like they were going to do anything but let me leave with old coffee, i asked for the originals nail order of a pour over... but from how slow it had already been and how long it had already taken i asked for my money back. girls leaning against the register... waiting for change for ten minutes! manager aloof and childishly laughing about it with employees. not good starbucks! no wonder at all why this is rated a two!!!

today i was reminded yet again, why i stopped going to this starbucks. call me optimistic, but i thought i'd give it a second chance. the parking lot is extremely small and crowded even though they "added" some extra spots. the only parking that was available today was in the 15 minute parking spots. some other patrons and i were asked to move our cars from these spots so that others could park there. i don't mind moving my car but there were no other places to go. and the starbucks employee was somewhat rude about it. please do yourself a favor. if you're in jenkintown and you really want a cup of starbucks coffee, just visit the barnes & noble across the street. save yourself the time and stress.

tends to get crowder but i need my daily fix. i like this location because of the. close proximity to the turnpike and route 1. also has outdoor seating and better parking than the starbucks that's closer to my office.
just moved to roxborough, and i walked there after going to the bank. very friendly staff, they complimented my accent and gave me great service. i shall return. :)

stuff is awesome the service is fast and amazing. this starbucks is always busy but you never wait to long. starbucks coffee is a little over priced but once in a while its good. love the holiday flavors. pumpkin spice one of the best of all times.

earns a star for its size and cleanliness. earns a star for staff being friendly. remove a star for closing at 2 pm weekdays. remove a star for not taking starbucks rewards. remove a star for being closed on weekends despite being center of the city. ever since the riots/looting of 2020 and pandemic closures, bpg, who own/operate this location, have seemed to make it so that everyone who visits or lives in downtown will go to shitty dunkin donuts. but joke is on them because the former loma cafe reopened to now be milk & honey coffee and food and art and oh yeah is open in weekends. but still, milk & honey is on 4th which means those in the teens have a long walk. especially since the other starbucks downtown is also closed on weekend as is i.m. coffee. wilmington, the dying city. (also, never visit pine street unless you want to be shot)

why review another starbucks? well, for 2 simple reasons: 1. this store is unique for being a plush one and for being located inside an er hospital building, which itself looks like a deluxe hotel! :-) 2. yelp urges me to do a review, possibly because i have posted a few pics and a tip. - go enjoy! it's a beautiful, full service store with one exception that it does not redeem your earned rewards, although you can still earn stars for your purchases. details you can skip: while visiting a friend in reno, he suggested that we should come to this starbucks. i was totally surprised that he brought me to an er hospital building, though, which by any standard looks like a luxury hotel with large atriums, waiting lounges with artistic sculptures, flowers; patios overlooking beautiful gardens! i thought my friend was probably going there to visit a friend in the er. but no, he promptly brought me to this very stylish starbucks, which had a very plush interior decor and a comfy outside seating area in a lobby overlooking the charming garden! now, i understood that my friend brought me here to show off their er hospital and this starbucks! so, we got in the long line for our brew and pastry. the baristas were friendly and helpful. they moved the line rather quickly with 2 cash registers open. we happily enjoyed the goodies in its charming "lounge" area. :-) i liked this place so much that i went back there the next 2 days for more. i liked its seating area in the lobby, too, at a table by the large picture window overlooking the small garden below. however, despite its appearance of a genuine, big starbucks store, it is only a licensed one by the starbucks franchise. that means it cannot redeem your earned starbucks rewards, nor give gold card members free refills, although you can still earn stars for your purchases. :-( oh yes, they have free parking in a parking structure across the tahoe tower wing. :-) july 2015
we mobile ordered and our food and drinks were ready asap. this location is so nicely kept. the staff is always great.

we frequent this starbucks because the staff is always very polite, cheerful and seem to all be having a great time! and generally very quick with the service, too. thanks you for your cheer and smiles!!! and the coffee, too!! :)

nice group of folks work at this location. friendly and work hard to take care of customers! always enjoy stopping by this location! drive thru is always busy but due to covid that is expected. inside is always clean. the location is tricky due to a busy corner. parking is tight at times also. hope this helps you make an informed decision!

i am a current starbucks employee at another store and felt the need to say how amazing this store is. the staff are incredibly friendly and it's my favorite place to hang out and get some work done if i'm in the area. i don't understand how anyone could have complaints about this store. every time i come i pick up tips on how to be better at my store and give better service. 11/10
service was great as always . my fav ginger (josh) wasn't there though. nothing like a true north first thing in the morning

staff isn't very friendly inside they look very annoyed every time i've been inside, not very inviting to sit and relax and drink or eat, usually the drive through seems better to use at this location
the workers there seem to treat every customer with disdain. every order was incorrect and they were soooooo slow. terrible service.

this is a review for the charbucks next to the wyndham garden inn on route 100 in exton, this starbucks is walk-able from there and literally saved me throughout the weekend of a conference where i was forced to stay at the wymdham. i came here for breakfast - got some decent coffee and a breakfast sandwich which beat the heck out of the pre-packaged "grab & go" garbage that the hotel was offering. i alsostopped in around 3 pm during a break in the conference and got a much-needed macchiatto (sp??). thank you, fourbucks.

unlike most, i think starbucks is quite overrated and too expensive. when my office was nearby i went there fairly often. now almost never and i wonder why i ever went there. the coffee always tastes burnt and always requires "additives" to make it palatable. the pastries they sell are grossly over-priced and mediocre at best. bread compny or eiensteins are always better choices.
great location but limited parking. lots of drive-thru traffic. one block west of hanley on delmar.

i've been here three times in the last 24 hours. two of the three times they got my drink wrong. one drink they had to make three times. each visit i had to wait a crazy amount of time. i'm going to look for another location.

parking is meh, but besides that it has a drive through that is normally very quick, workers are always chipper and fun to deal with and the coffee lives up to starbucks standards. will be here often!

i don't make a habit of reviewing chains but this starbucks is officially my savior. mistakenly i have decided to become a graduate student. although the webster university library is gorgeous, its too far. i needed a place that was close to my home and a place that i can go straight from work without giving myself time to think about how terrible it is to go straight from work to studying. the place has plenty of outlets and the service is great. the gals behind the counter will even watch your stuff so you an use the bathroom!

quick friendly service! our drinks were perfect. it's not a very big starbucks, it's attached to another building, which i assume is some kind of offices of some sort. but for their location and size they weren't too terribly busy, and i was in and out pretty fast both times. staff was cool too. both times it was different people, and all of them were friendly and joked around/held conversations. will be back to visit this one if given the chance.
worst starbucks ever. there is always the longest line and 1 ringer and 1 barista. i've never waited less than 15 mins for my drink. if you know you're a busy store staff accordingly. awful.
stopped in and waited 10 minutes for the drink to be made, a single person making drinks, really?!

every starbucks is the same bland, boring, sterile yuppie infested, "i'm in a rush, get my latte quick" type place. awful coffee, lousy rude service, terrible sandwiches, overpriced everything. i avoid it always unless i get a free gift card.

isn't the point of mobile order to get your order in line for preparation? twice this week i placed my order 10 min prior to arrival. the app said would be ready in 6-8 minutes both days. as i entered i noted who was in the regular line. both days people who placed their orders after i arrived (about 10 min after i placed my mobile order) were getting their beverages and mine hadn't even been made. yesterday my order was ready 20 min after originally placed and today it was 17 minutes. very frustrated. i get it is a busy location....but both days it looked like they were picking and choosing what drinks to make...not necessarily in order placed. and to top it off today they were out of vanilla syrup at 7:30am...poor planning.

i've recently become a pretty frequent starbucks customer, and as far as locations near and convenient for me this one is my favorite!!! the staff is always happy, friendly, and incredibly nice!! i've not had a single bad customer service experience yet. my coffee is always ready within a minute or two and i'm never waiting long so even if i'm running a little behind i still have time to get my coffee, it's awesome! and everything is always delicious, especially compared to all other starbucks locations in the area.
this place gives excellent service. employees are super nice and helpful. i will drive a great distance to visit the starbucks versus others.

best starbucks in the city!!! they are prompt, courteous and friendly. because ive been going there for years, they know my order. all the employees are great. fran is the best!!! she is a gem!! kelly, adrian aya'naziah and seth are wonderful!! always upbeat and glad to see you! this starbucks is like the cheers of philly..............where everybody knows your name and drink!! cheers!!
check your drinks before you pull away from the drive through. was just here and they used the wrong milk for the drink i ordered. waste of $5 :(

this starbucks has the worst drive thru, doesn't matter what time of day the line is always wrapped around the parking lot. seems no one inside cares. if you have the option go to another one until they change management or staff.

ahh, yes. the super-local coffee shop "starbucks". as far as your standard starbucks is concerned, there are always friendly and great staff working here. always a great place to stop while you are walking the monon trail or exploring the broad ripple shops. if you are a creature of comfort and want something familiar--this place is a safe bet. if you are looking for local coffee shops, check out hubbard and cravens or monon coffee right around the corner!

who doesn't love starbucks.. at this location it's a hit or a miss. they are always busy and you never know if the person at the window is gonna be nice or rude. they do have the most up beat nice man working the window sometimes but there are these two ladies who are just rude. like here is your coffee now leave and basically doesn't care if your order is correct or not. but i still go anyway bc it's close to home.
loved that they offered me a "pup cup" for my dog when we went through the drive-up. it made his day!

this is the starbucks i go to most often. it's usually very busy, and the space is l-shaped and narrow. t's often hard to get a place to sit down inside, and there's plenty outside, if that works for you. it usually doesn't for me. why am i giving this starbucks four stars? good service and good location. i often go to trader joe's to shop afterwards. it's often on the way to or from home, and i came here for trenta-sized passion iced teas to counteract dehydration during cancer treatment.
extremely sweet baristas! this starbucks is always so friendly and clean! drinks are great too.

this rootin tootin new starbucks is a great place to stop, particularly for causeway commuters. more on that later........ uness you were born on a different planet or raised by wolves, you know what starbucks is all about. they're the originators of the overpriced coffee drink and have hooked millions of americans on the fru fru mocha late hold up the drive thru line frapa drinko. but despite all that stuff, they are also incredible convenient and make getting an influx of caffeine pretty darn simple. this shop is brand new, and located on the outskirts of lakeside mall. getting in to the drive thru is simple. there is a large computer screen at the ordering area so verifying your order is correct is simple. be aware that they have a camera and can see you when you order. don't be picking your nose or they'll know. each time i visited this location i've used their drive thru. it moved pretty quickly and the employees were very friendly and helpful. now for the pro tip for commuters: if you drive the causeway daily you'll know that the right lane of causeway blvd. moves more quickly than the center lane. stay in the right lane, turn right at 17th street and get in the starbucks line. when you leave starbucks exit on the side closest to 17th street and you'll avoid all of the traffic and have a tasty beverage. you can thank me later.

can never go wrong with starbucks lattes! the location is in a pretty busy plaza and sad to find out there was no drive thru for the quick caffeine trips. they can get pretty busy in that location since they are in the center of busy shopping plazas. on a sunday evening we actually got our drinks pretty quickly.

this convenient starbucks has a plethora of outside seating. while the area is right on linebaugh, it can still be nice to sit outside and enjoy a nice coffee beverage. for those dog inclined, they are dog friendly. i haven't seen a dog outside lapping up a double espresso yet but i encourage someone to see what happens! please report back. yelpers want to know.

i love this location, everyone is always nice and has a smile on their face even when they're crazy busy. i go here all the time, i feel so comfortable and welcomed. yesterday was my birthday and there was a problem with the app not coming up with my birthday treat, however two baristas looked it up for me and then gave me the drink for free and were very polite and professional. every time i come in they always greet me and some of them even know me by name

so slow. i have been here a few times over the years and every time i remember why i try to avoid the exit 19 starbucks. they could care less about customer service. if you get in the drive through and there's only a couple of cars, don't be fooled. it will take 20 minutes. today, i finally got to the window, i paid, and then she disappeared- almost forever. i could see my drink sitting on the counter. i just stared at it for an eternity. pure torture.

okay, four and five star reviewers... have you ever been to a real starbucks? or are you just buying bottled water here? this is a franchised 'bucks, not a corporate one and it shows. if service was a vacuum cleaner, this place would be a dyson. seriously. rude people that can't get a venti tap water right. heaven forbid you try to correct them. can you say stink-eye? the store is beautiful and comfortable, but i drive all the way up to marsh rd where they know how to make drinks properly, they remember you after one or two visits and they're some real friendly folks to boot.

what a great way to start my day! elijah was welcoming and reached out to shake my hand and introduce himself. we engaged in some pleasant conversation while my beverage was being prepared. he's a keeper!!

vastly improved. the staff has turned over and this is a much better starbucks than it used to be. i've been going regularly again and my order is always correct, the service is lightning fast and the employees are pleasant.

came into the store and got 3 drinks, 2 apple juices, 4 pastries and reload my app. the cashier was very friendly and patient with us. great experience for a friday evening at 7pm. drinks: tall flat white, tall decaf mocha, and venti caramel mocha pastries: regular croissant, everything croissant (so good), chocolate croissant (all time fav) and chocolate chunk cookie.

this is the first time i've been in the starbucks and i've been around for a long time. i just never make it to this little stretch of west chester pike. but since i discovered the bagel place down the road i figured i'd stop in for coffee to go with my my bagel. the service here is not great. they're not rude but they're not really friendly and they are kind of slow. however i have to say that i got one of the best starbucks cappuccino that i've had in a long time. for that reason i gave these folks at four-star rather than a three star rating. parking here is definitely a lot easier than it is at the other havertown starbucks. i don't think this gets as much traffic plus they share a parking lot with the pretzel company so i think that has a lot to do with it.

i could never give any more stars to a starbucks. their coffee is good and all but i hate the rude hipsters and snobs who flock and take over. this particular one has a back entrance away from the beards finger banging their crapple laptops so you can order and then bolt. *edit* karen is hands down the best employee there and deserves a raise, everyone else gives sub par service and drinks.
3.5 stars my neighborhood starbucks.. fast and always friendly service. the only thing that sucks is the lack of parking.
great service today! my skinny coffee frappuccino w/sugar free vanilla is so delicious
this starbucks is probably one of the speedier ones on state street, you can really get in and out of there really quick.

hands down the slowest starbucks drive through i have ever been to. i have tried on multiple occasions to get coffee before work and even if there is only one car in the drive through it takes at least 10 minutes if not more and this is before 7 am. i made sure to look inside as well. there is either no one inside or maybe one person ordering. there's no sense of urgency to get people in the drive through on their way. as much as i like starbucks, i can no longer stop here for coffee. today's trip = 3 cars in the drive through and i'm going on 11 minutes and have been waiting at the window for my cup of coffee for 3 minutes. stupid!

the last time i was there, i went through the drive thru. i waited almost 20 minutes for an iced green tea. they aren't very friendly. one time i ordered, they told me one price at the speaker box and a completely different price at the window. i don't think they're very competent.
consistently good. try the orange spiced iced coffee!

i only went here once while i was visiting...but their bistro lunches are the best ive ever seen....maybe because its in the middle of a college & people are constantly on the go...ive been to a lot of starbucks & i really like this one...if i lived in philly this would be my starbucks of choice :)

so... i ordered a coffee and two pastries. no chocolate shakers by the mixing area. i tried to get the attention of a barista who was talking about her dental work to get a chocolate shaker, but she seemed too busy explaining her dental work to notice my tries to get her attention. another barista saw me trying to get her attention and told me they've been out of chocolate for awhile. "really? then what do you use for the coffee drinks?", i thought to myself. it was around 7am on a saturday, so not busy at all. the area where customers mix their coffee was pretty dirty with sugar and stuff. i don't know the coffee industry but shouldn't someone be on cleaning duty when it is slow? oh, and the pastries were still wrapped in the plastic they came in. smh...
i'm still upset about the stupid trash can issue of my previous post, but otherwise it's a decent starbucks locations.

so straight off i'm going to say i'm not a big fan of starbucks. i really only end up coming here with my wife when she is in need of a coffee fix. this is a pretty standard starbucks. the one thing to note is that the location is a bit tricky to get in and out of. there isn't much parking, and brentwood boulevard can get quite congested. i would recommend only coming here in off hours. if you come during peak times you make get a bit frustrated by the traffic. since it is a small shop, it is also better to take the drinks and go. i would not recommend planning to do any work or reading here. it works best as a to-go spot.
the slowest starbucks. ever. they're probably still making my coffee. and it was literally an iced coffee. you just add ice.

if the crew wasn't so nice they'd only get one star. i decided to give them another chance. and what happens, my grilled cheese had a sheet of ice because the cheese didn't get melted properly, and my impossible brkfst sandwich wasn't hot either! and not the target starbucks, the one near chili's
i like this starbucks, although there's always a huge line and only 2 employees, one running the register and one making the drinks. i feel like they should really have some more help.

josh made my experience great such a friendly upbeat person in your business goes a long way i got the most amazing caramel macchiato with extra caramel and extra vanilla today and it was made with love will definitely be back thanks for a wonderful experience ps thanks to whoever made the coffee the coffee was sooo great

i love starbucks but not enough to go to this particular shop again. first time i went the barista got annoyed that i had three cards: an empty discount card, a gift card with some credit and my debit card to reload my gift card), then she asked me to step away and wait for my coffee. i went to the bathroom to wash my hands and thought i left my wedding band. when i inquired about my ring she said that they wouldn't keep a ring if it was left behind in the toilet! next week i came back for a latte and to reload my card. there was a woman behind me and when she placed the order the two baristas just kept chatting away for several minutes while i waited by the cash register. i turned around and headed towards the exit door then one of them says "did you need anything, sir.". i said "nope" and i heard her scoff at me. like she was annoyed by my perceived rudeness. i now only go to the one by castleton, the staff is always friendly and it has a drive-through.

ok, as far as starbucks go. would love a little more friendly vibe but haven't seen that from here.....ever. not saying it's unfriendly...just blah. varied crowd if you want to people watch... i mean it is downtown clearwater after all.

just moved from cali and super stoked this starbucks is literally in my backyard. the staff / team here have the best friendliest customer service so far. my order is ready and fresh for pick up. thank you for your excellent service at times like these (covid19) keep up the great work, stay safe and always keep smiling you make a difference.

so, i stopped in here for an espresso hoping to use my $5 gift card for starbucks. no dice. i was informed that this is not a starbucks. of course there are starbucks signs everywhere and the barista was wearing a starbucks hat and apron. my espresso was served in a starbucks cup and cup holder. my espresso was in fact starbucks espresso. had a short chat with the manager who gave me the corporate line of bull - they are not "really" a starbucks, blah blah blah. very disappointing at least the coffee was pretty good. . wonder if i will ever be able to use my gift card? mark.

wanted to redeem my birthday drink reward, so the cashier started my order on a venti cup, to later find out i couldn't redeem b/c it was a casino-starbucks. i'm (sort of) fine with that, but for some reason it was very hard for her to transfer my drink to a tall cup: (first time ordering the new...) cinnamon almond milk macchiato, extra hot, with 2 pumps of syrup. i confirmed that she "accidentally (?)" wrote 2 pumps of "cd" but she said cinnamon dolce is the name for the syrup. when i found her holding my cup by the lip i should have said something, that can't be proper "food" handling. i got my drink in a decent amount of time, but it was a cinnamon dolce latte. i had the (friendly) barista fix my drink, but it didn't come back extra hot, the way i order all my drinks at star'. after waiting about 8 min. in line, going through the bday set back, having her remake the drink, and paying $4, needless to say, i can't give any more than one star. i pay for a custom cup of coffee and it's got to be right for the price.

very small and cramped. workers were slow and unfriendly. no scanner to pay with your phone...she attempted to type in the correct info like 5 times so i could pay... wasn't too impressed. my drink tastes good though hence the 3 stars. i did see a really cool starbucks just up the street though and it is in an awesome and authentic looking building. so skip this one and go there!

the worst starbucks that i've ever been to. super slow and drinks came out terrible. i ordered a grande iced caramel cloud macchiato, and it came out 80% cloud foam, the remaining amount was probably 3 gulps of coffee at best. i didn't have time to return for them to correct the drink, so maybe management would have to learn about it via yelp.

i am not sure if it is due to the fact that there are so few coffee shops in the area (next closest one to my knowledge is the small tim horton's in the esso station) but this starbucks location always seems to be busy regardless of what time of day that i go. the food and drink quality is pretty consistent with other locations that i have visited. the overall quality solely at this location also seems quite consistent regardless of the time of day i go at. they are always well staffed behind the counter and the only real issue i sometimes notice is that the cashiers will ring in quite a few drinks but rarely come over and help the person working that end of the bar once they have dispensed with the lineup.

starbucks comes with all the standard setbacks: chain restaurant, average coffee, and questionable service. this location adds difficult parking and supersaturated drive-thru as additional headaches. with that said, it is close to home and the staff is comprised of interesting folks that are friendly and conversational. you may not find this to be true on your first visit, but the place grows on you...

this place is freezing. yes, in tucson even, this place is uncomfortably cold. the building benefits from shade so it shouldn't need too much ac, but they blast it as if they're on the sun. much warmer and more comfortable outside somehow.
the worst. can i do zero stars? orders take forever to fulfill. they are almost always out of certain pastries and cake pops.
it is great to have a starbucks in the silver legacy. it is the same as other starbucks, the real advantage here is the location.

extremely friendly and helpful staff. this is a great location being attached to the skywalk system too! you can easily get to the hyatt/pnc building indoors if it's raining/snowing. walk down the escalator and you're there!

i was surprised to find poor reviews on a starbucks location, but after a few experiences here, i completely agree. management needs to get involved to stream line and smooth out their work flow. twice, i placed mobile orders where after arriving and standing there for another 5-10mins and asking for help, did i then find out they were out of the particular sandwiches i ordered. if they can't update the app to say they're out so i can't order in the first place, then they should at least place a note there with my name so i can speak with a barista rather than continue to wait. i understand any food industry can get hectic and busy, but you'd think after a while, they would've worked on some process improvements. customer service is not always consistently up to starbucks standards either. too bad.

i stopped into this starbucks near closing time, and was told that they were "out of coffee beverages". i ordered a frappuccino so i can only assume that they meant that and not that they were actually out of coffee. it was close to closing time, and its possible that the staff just didn't want to bother, but i had to drive to mt juliet and a frappuccino would have really helped.

i was staying with a starbucks guy so each morning we had to stop so i took part and really thought this location was a good one. very clean, good service. i also had the steak and cheese wrap which ended up being pretty darn good and fulfilling. more so than most starbucks options in my opinion. so good coffee and a good start to my day.

this has been one of my favorite cafés since it was a 24 hour store. it's not open all night anymore but still a good cafe. clean restrooms and strong wifi signal. recently renovated in a retro design, and the baristas wear vintage style hats and ties. i always stop here when i'm in the area.

i love this location. the coffee is great, my orders are correct, and the baristas are quick and friendly. the interior was just refurnished and looks great. they have a long conference style table now with electric outlets. it can be really busy between 7am and 8am; if you have trouble parking, there is an overflow lot around the back.

i'm surprised the ratings for this starbucks are so low. the baristas have been nothing but friendly. i'd give this place five stars if the first floor wasn't so cramped up, if going to the second floor didn't mean the temperature went up 10 degrees and i lose cell reception, and if the first floor had window shades so i don't go blind sitting there as the sun rises in the morning.
it was good. really like the vibes i was getting! was busy, but understandable because all the drinks were heavily modified, i like my drinks simple. please support our baristas by ordering simple.

standard s'bucks product and service, with a friendly staff. lot (and different kinds!) of seating that will really look great once covid stuff is over with. definitely one of my favorite s'bucks interiors to date.
