let's just get this out of the way- it's open 24 hours. this means that, after 1am, you're going to run into drunks or people in recovery. the locals usually clear out by midnight. the staff is pretty great. as you might imagine, cleaning up vomit in bathrooms on a regular basis tends to take the edge off of a person, regardless of how caffeinated s/he may be. the outdoor seating is ample. and rarely unusable, as the weather tends to be agreeable. sure. there might be a lot of weirdos, but you can usually just pull up a chair and engage in whatever inappropriate debate is taking place. just don't try to talk with the guy intently watching the game outside on a portable television. if ohio's losing, he's pretty cranky.

3 stars because the service here is dismal at best. i've been to this location more than 10 times because 8th very close to my apartment but if i have the time i head west 6 blocks to the next closest location. no matter what you order or how many people are in front of you you will wait at least 5 minutes for a very simple drink (ie black coffee) and if it's busy i've waited up to 15 minutes. normally i'd walk out but when i'm craving iced coffee magic the more hip local establishments fail miserably every time. the only saving grace to this location is the upstairs area, which is bright, with a good view of south st for people wqtching, and has a lot of seating and tables to read relax or do work.

i ordered a traveler for a meeting. a traveler is a cardboard box full of delicious- whatever you want. i get the blonde roast cause that's what those brainy people like. i've worked here at the tgh for 17 years and love that there is a place like this here. it's a safe haven for employees and patient family alike- stepping into another world for a break from life. i called at night to order my traveler. it wasn't ordered in the morning so i was late to my meeting. made me look bad, but i still love this place.

i'm a starbucks lover but this location is one of my least favorites. i was very excited when they opened this location so close to the hospitals nearby, expecting some convenience. it has been more of a headache purchasing at this location than anything else. two things to note for this specific location: 1. the parking situation is atrocious. very limited parking. after the 7 or 8 spots are taken, you have to basically find grass or any concrete to squeeze your car into or you can venture to walmart, park there and walk back to starbucks. honestly, you are better off going to another starbucks nearby. 2. the service can be unusually slow with some inaccuracies in the drink recipes. i have ordered mobile before from the i275 highway and my drink was still not ready. i recently ordered the pink drink and there was no coconut milk in the drink when i received it. i didn't bother to tell them. not my favorite location considering there are other coffee options and other starbucks locations in the area.

i've been to this starbucks a few times and the service is extremely slow. efficient, speedy service is such a huge part of starbucks' reputation and this one is failing in both of those areas. on my last visit, i pulled into the line and immediately heard the driver of the car in front of me yelling into the speaker. i didn't think too much of it until it was my turn to place my order and i was asked to repeat my self several times. when i started customizing my order the person taking it seemed utterly confused by my requests (coconut milk and extra sweetener) and his tone quickly became curt and rude. i stopped customizing and finished ordering, when i got to the window i was met by another rude employee. shockingly my order was correct but the service was terrible.

**this review is for the stand-alone starbucks in the delaware welcome center parking lot, not the one inside the food court.** i have visited here twice and both times the staff was friendly and helpful. the place is clean and there is seating. there are two independent bathrooms. the one i used was in pretty good condition especially when you take into consideration the rest stop locality. both times getting an iced green tea frap was easy and the results were delicious. both times i asked for no whip cream and both times it was taken care of easily. there is a drive through so if you get food in the main building you can visit this starbucks drive through before hitting the road again. the staff here made a real difference for me. i don't imagine they have many regulars and i completely appreciate that being friendly and helpful to a two time visitor holds very limited value. lets be honest, i'm not hopping on the road and driving up to delaware for my next frap. what i can do is tell folks about this location and encourage them to visit this location as they embark on the i-95 east coast driving experience. this stand alone starbucks and its friendly staff is its own caffeinated oasis alone the i-95 corridor.
they have updated and now accept the app. staff takes their time to get to know you. drinks are always spot on. see you soon...

this starbucks is at a busy intersection and the parking lot always seems to be full or almost full when i come here. despite the limited parking, i really like this one. it's a starbucks so you're basically going to get a standard as far as food and beverages go, but i have to say i had a really great customer service experience there yesterday. the girl taking my order was incredibly friendly and very cheerful. the girl making my drink even started a brief conversation with me while she was making my drink. they always ask for your name when they're busy so they can keep track of drinks, but they weren't busy and asked for mine anyway and then called me by it through the experience. i hit this place on my way to baltimore sometimes, so although it's a mile and a half off of 95, i don't mind the extra time it takes to get here if i get friendly service

absolute worst service ever for a starbucks. ordered drinks and cake pops and waited in the drive thru for 15 minutes before even paying. lady working drive thru window was very short and rude with us.

i want to give 0 stars! this was by far the worst starbucks i've ever been to! i wanted a drink before my class so i go in and there are 2 baristas working. no one in line and one person waiting for their drink. one of the baristas turns around to see me, looks at her other person and says she's going to break. and leaves. the other girl continues doing whatever she's doing. 5 minutes later she finally turns around and takes my order. that was okay but the other barista came back and they started arguing over something. the barista left again and then the one who stayed just started doing dishes! while me and this other girl where waiting and a line began forming. super annoying. i ended up leaving because i was going to be late for class. so the entire thing was a waist of time and money. super upset:(

i've quite honestly experienced better service at other starbucks locations. that said, i really do appreciate the fact that i can use my starbucks gold card (or app) here just like at any other starbucks location. this location isn't one where you would be likely to sit and enjoy your beverage: instead you are expected to buy one and go elsewhere to enjoy it. on my most recent visit, the staff member serving me didn't understand what i wanted until i rephrased it twice and even after i answered her questions she provided me a slightly different product than what i requested (which really wasn't a problem but it was rather odd). i do appreciate having access to starbucks in this location but i wish the service were better, especially when it is summer and not very busy, and that there were more of a seating area available.

i'm not sure if i've seen a more well designed and aesthetically pleasing starbucks than this one. they have a beautiful patio outside with a privacy screen and the inside has ample space between the seating. one big table, a couch area, private areas, and a wall of small and medium tables. if i lived over here, this would definitely be a choice for doing work. the staff were all in a great mood and very helpful with my sleepy self. i opted for the pumpkin crepe cold brew and it was ready within a couple minutes. the area to order is a little tiny if a line does begin, but i think they move pretty quickly to mediate that problem. also my grande cold brew was $4.50 - what a steal for starbucks since i usually pay between $5-7 at other locations.

fast and friendly service. normal starbucks coffee. their location is good and they have a huge parking lot right behind them. so they are normally pretty busy. they also have enough table for different groups of people.

hey. it's a starbucks so wysiwyg. however the staff here is always friendly and efficient. there is lots of room here and it never seems crowded although it is busy. good place for a post eagle creek run.
bathroom was worse than a truckstop glory hole. not open late enough and has terrible internet.

came to this location for the first time today. manger was awesome and super helpful getting my app and gift card balance to transfer. the staff were also very friendly and the coffee was perfectly made. the inside was awesome as well with a cozy fireplace to warm up next to. i will definitely make this a weekly spot.
we went through the drive through and it was a great start to our morning, everyone was wonderful and speedy! not only did our drink look so lovely but it tastes... *chefs kiss* thank you :)!!!
despite a long line, it moved fast. the staff was nicer than average, and they clearly knew their regulars. no drive thru

nothing like standing there waiting to order for 5 minutes while the guys that work there talk crap about the customers in the drive thru and the things they ordered (dropping f bombs in front of me and my 3 year old). make eye contact he still ignores me. then they managed to make my daughter's chocolate milk wrong and my iced white mocha wrong. won't be going back to this location.

this is a happy little coffee place in irvington that just happens to be named starbucks. the baristas really know their stuff. i like just the plain ole pike place but sometimes i will venture out to a caramel macchiato. today i tried the spicy chorizo monterey jack breakfast sandwich and beware of the spicy. this really made my tummy feel nice and warm. the music is so soothing. i could definitely stay for an hour or two.

i come here about once a week to work and get out of the house. everyone here is always very pleasant and friendly. it can get crowded at times, but there's usually ample seating close to electric outlets if you've got a laptop.

my favorite place to be. and everyone makes things to order correctly, and even offers you other options for flavors. i love it here! my usual...hot tall white mocha decaf...stirred lol. sometimes two pumps of white mocha, sometimes 3.

first day, and it's already doing a booming business. i went to my normal location across the street, and the sign was gone from the building--first clue! they were hanging the "now open" banners as i walked in. be forewarned. the drive through is not functioning yet & they have it blocked off.

love this starbucks. they have their mobile ordering system down. every time i order on the app, i walk in and my order is ready. i can be in and out in 30 seconds. this is very helpful when you're running to a meeting and need to grab something quick. definitely recommend. also the baristas are usually full of smiles and welcomes.
the staff were some what friendly and there service was okay. the place is clean except the men's bathroom seemed like someone took a bath near the sink. the floor was all wet around the sink.

i won't be going back, there's an older women w blonde hair and bangs who was not so very nice. i noticed her greet everyone nicely at the register, when it was my turn she wasn't friendly. instead i got an up and down look as if i was wearing something she didn't like (im in a tshirt and jeans) but i am tattooed. then she had to correct me on the proper name of a drink even though she clearly understood me and repeated exactly what i called it anyway. my drink was over filled w ice, and my cream cheese was not cold, almost like it had been sitting out.

i've been coming here for years. they have never messed up an order , been rude , or done anything worth less then 5 stars. it's a starbucks. it's good. it's overpriced but you know that pulling in. they do a great job!

communication between all employees should be first and foremost. today it clearly wasn't a priority for them. as i ordered a coffee and oatmeal and paid for it using my debit card, it took them over 15 minutes to tell me, we're sorry, but we are out of oatmeal. seriously? even though they refunded me the difference via same pay form, it's something they should have known they didn't have. now more so since they prioritize the drive through and call in orders.

i moved to stl from socal two years ago. started going to the starbucks at chippewa and landsdowne because it is within walking distance of my home. for 1 1/2 years, i put up with the poor customer experience because i figured that's just the way it is here in stl. then, i happened to drive by this crestwood store and tried it out. i couldn't believe how clean, friendly and professionally trained the staff is and the store is very comfortable. so, now i get in my car, drive by the chippewa store and head to the crestwood store each morning. believe me, it's worth the drive to start my morning with a friendly store with exceptional service.

i have ordered a couple of times here and sometimes their coffee is good, but on occasion you get a cup of milk with watered down coffee. on one occasion i asked for a caramel macchiato and it tasted like milk with caramel. no coffee whatsoever, and i was disappointed because i love starbucks! but overall it's a good way to grab a quick coffee if your on the go or if you're late. and the next time, i might ask for an extra shot in my macchiato :). "happy eating!"

it seems like there is a starbucks on every corner, but not all starbucks nor their employees are created equal. this location is always polite, respectful, and timely. it's the little things. today they made my day! in rare form, my eight month old puppy pulled on her leash and my brand new beverage went flying before i even made it home to put the straw in it. when i returned to this location, after cleaning up the mess and walking back, they replaced my beverage with no questions asked. it's the little things like this that make this location my favorite in philly.

standard starbucks. the people are pretty nice. once, someone in front of me in the drivethru line bought $5 of my purchase with the idea that you pass it along, which i did. they said it can go for a dozen or so customers in a row sometimes.

this place needs some work.. ordered a caramel frap and a cookie dough cake pop.. i was told the cake pop was out of stock. i asked which flavors they did have.. and she said cookie dough or chocolate. so i said okay i'll take the cookie dough. it was silent and she goes "so you want a cake pop?" i confirm, yes the cookie dough one like i asked three times now. get to the window- i ask if i can pay cash but still use my rewards, she says yes. takes my phone and then charges my card- wtf?? come on starbucks. get your crap together.

this starbucks sets the standard for the many starbucks in tucson. the friendly baristas, the quality drinks, and service never disappoint. the location is prime and convenient. the best coffee on the east side of tucson!

drum-roll please! review #100 coming right up! i chose to review starbucks as my 100th review because it is a guilty pleasure. something that i only allow myself once a week. but something that i crave everyday! this particular starbucks is great. the baristas are always friendly and welcoming. the drinks are made correctly and quickly. i usually go with a salted caramel hot chocolate or mocha. i also occasionally order the white mocha. if you can't tell, i enjoy coffee that doesn't taste like coffee! in the summer their iced teas are the perfect thirst quencher! their breakfast pastry items are always delicious. try the cranberry-orange scone and you will not be disappointed! their breakfast sandwiches are unique and pretty tasty as well. i always choose to go in and place my order over sitting in the drive thru. the line for their drive thru is always ridiculously long. it's usually quicker to go in!

used to be my favorite starbucks now i try to avoid it. while they used to be friendly and fast and never messed up my order... now they're rude! always trash talking loudly near the counter, slow! and over half the time make my drink wrong but since it took at least 5min to get it i don't have time to correct it. wish they were as good as they were a year ago it's gone downhill fast. and a side note the new decor sucks!!

staff worked slowly and managed their workflow poorly. they took several orders from different customers and started making them all, but they waited to give the orders until they completed everyone's. the cashier took our order and my wife wanted to change the size of her drink. the cashier became irritated with us for changing. this would have been understandable if we had paid already but all she had to do was change the order, but she was clearly bothered by this extra work we demanded of her. lots of untrained and inefficient staff with poor customer interactions.

at the drive thru i ordered a nitro cold brew with sweet cream and apparently this location doesn't know how to make this drink or they don't carry this drink there because i was handed a vanilla ice coffee at the window. i was running late to my conference so i just left and figured i would just drink this ice coffee but it was very bitter. i would prefer they tell me up front they don't serve the drink i ordered instead of giving me a substitute.

this starbucks seems more organized then most. but more importantly, the coffee and other drinks are consistently starbucks... so you know what hat your getting before you order... in the outlet mall so no drive-thru lots of outdoor seating a little cramped inside

got here today. and they didn't have napkins. i understand sometimes things run out before next shipment arrive but management you can still buy general napkins next door at supermarket just so your customer don't have to leave with oily fingers

i'm a die hard starbucks fan. the employees and the managers here are super friendly and pretty fast for how busy they are almost all the time. they do have a great location for before and after work for a lot of people.
the staff is always very friendly, the store is always clean and a great place to meet friends for coffee!
amazing service and great atmosphere. the manager joe greeted me nicely and i love how they seem to know all the local customers. cheers y'all.

i love the convenience of the drive thru window. the employees are very friendly, always smiling and give me my coffee quickly. today i tried the grande caramel flan latte with an extra shot of espresso and skim milk. i like my coffee strong! no need for any extra sweetener because the coffee already caramel flan goodness in it. it was delicious! this is my second day in a row getting this treat! hopefully it will fuel me to ace my online nutrition test here in a min. all in all, you can't go wrong this starbucks location. even during the busy hours the staff is on their a game. try a latte today!

generally terrible customer service. unfortunately i think the location is what keeps them in business - close proximity to 295 for travelers. i'm guilty of stopping there during travels to pa because of the location and every time i kick myself - mediocre beverages and food that's either sold out or never heated all the way -- certainly not a good representation of the starbucks brand and consistency.
my favorite sbux in stl by far. i go out of my way to go to this location before class and work. they're always extremely kind and the order is always right. jack and kahkee are absolutely great.

the friendliest starbucks in the city. always gets my order perfect and always making an effort to ask how customers day is! super clean and definitely handling all covid19 precautions as far as i can tell.

i came for the wi-fi as pjs coffee has lacking wifi power just a few shops down the road. there was ample seating and my regular coffee was promptly handed to me. creamer bar was well stocked; clean floor and loud music like all the other homogonize starbucks you have ever been too.

i like this starbucks!! for one, it's a decently sized starbucks with plenty of places to sit down, and is usually not too busy (c'mon - this is audubon, after all.) more importantly though, the people who work here are so nice! they take a personal interest in their customers. my friend asked for them to make an oreo frapp, which is kind of like a secret "off-menu" item, and they followed his instructions and made it right! these baristas are the best!

i usually love coming in to this starbucks it is one of three near my home, but today a gentleman clearly cut the line and i was next to be served and the barista stilled served him. this was not cool especially today because i was running late. i told the barista and she didn't even apologize.

came here this morning by myself. i mobile ordered online so my coffee was ready for me when i got there -- but the staff was friendly and greeted me by name. since i need coffee everyday, i'll be back tomorrow for sure... i'm here on vacation all week ;)

what can i say, it's starbucks! it's consistent with other starbucks. the prices are higher, it that what you get at the airport. there is no place to sit down here, the nearest is the food court across the way. for the most part, the staff is friendly. they can be busy from time to time so expect a little wait. overall they are decent!

great new fast starbucks in four season's shopping center. this location makes drive thru a breeze. enter the starbucks lot from n woods mill @ olive blvd. traffic from this store exits onto olive blvd. this starbucks is in a brand new building on a corner lot that has been empty for years (in front of dierbergs). standard starbucks products available. i ordered a flat white and it was a delicious as always. extra friendly service. check out this new store! i will be back soon.

if you don't mind waiting 20-30 minutes for your drink this starbucks is for you! consistently slow service and always a line out the door. take my advice and walk the extra few minutes to the circle tower location.

the starbucks coffees and lattes are the same as coffees and lattes and coffees as same the are lattes and coffees starbucks the. this wonderful palindrome is not the reason i'm giving this starbucks a 5-star rating. this jawn is getting its 5 stars due to its friendly staff. i don't really like talking to people, but the morning staff is so good at remembering orders (you want an americano with a churclate durnut? uhhh, yeah wow thanks) that i can't help saying hi how are you? or thanks! all the workers seem genuinely enthused even when dealing with crowded rush hours and lousy soft misters. this quality service makes me feel better about spending the ridiculous starbucks price. i feel good about the purchase until i get to work and log into my bank account. but that's a whole different issue.

horrible latte - i ordered a tall latte and it was scorching hot; i actually burnt my tongue! and the overheated milk rendered it so bitter :( i asked them to remake it 'warm' and it was still super hot...i couldn't drink it. the girls seemed to take it personally and weren't very open to my feedback but i think the machine needs to be recalibrated or something.

most of the time the coffee here is ok some times is better then others but overall it's pretty ok. the problem is the workers they r very rude at least saturday night shift workers since that's when i came and they were very rude to me and my husband. when i used their restroom the toilet wouldn't flush so instead of leaving it broken for the next costumer i went to let one of the workers know but when i tried to tell a male worker i was ignored till about the third time i called him then when i told him that one of their toilets wasn't flushing he looked at me like he was mad i bothered him. another girl worker came up at that point i'm guessing hearing what i told him and gave me such a look of anger and irritation like i had broke the toilet myself and kept staring at me with such anger till i walked out the store with my husband and our drinks. so heads up to anyone going their if you encounter anything broken in the restrooms maybe you should just leave the employees to find it out for themselves since you probably won't get a good response for letting them know.

nice little location by the eller house. staff seemed a bit new and explained my drink selection (french vanilla latte) is usually three shots of flavoring, and asked me how i wanted to split the hazelnut and vanilla shots. which i thought was weird, but what do i know. the latte was lacking, which is why i knocked the one star off. i found a table and used wifi for work and making work calls and it was easy.
we always forget about this starbucks when we are working in town, but happened to be able to stop here to use the wifi for work and enjoy a cup of brew. wasn't busy at all which we liked.

stopped by on my way to work about 8:40am- the drive thru line wrapped around the building, which blocks a couple of parking spots in front. if you are parking and plan to go inside, just remember that you may have to wait for a drive thru car to move out of your way before backing up. service inside was speedy. cashier knew what he was doing and was very friendly. my drink was finished by the another barista and handed to me before i even left the register. my oatmeal was warmed and bagged about 2 minutes later.

i love the university's starbucks. the employees are always on top of orders, service is super quick, and everyone is super friendly. shane is my favourite employee! every time i have had him take my order he is always very clear. he looks customers in the eye and he is very happy towards everyone. the starbucks at unr is efficient yet it still provides a nice pace for those who want to just hang out and study.

time to upgrade my opinion of this location. it's keeps getting better. i've noticed a complete change in attitude and service levels for walk in customers. friendly, attentive, and making a point greet customers. not sure if it's been a change in local management that's made the difference, or if the newer linebaugh location and lessened the pressure on this store. either way, now deserving of 4 stars. nice comeback!!

be prepared to be underwhelmed and even slightly dissatisfied. i am without a doubt a very loyal starbucks customer and have been for years. to the point that i definitely have my local to me locations but i stop by on a daily basis. this does not make me an expert, but it does make me a consistent consumer who is knowledgeable about starbucks products and services. this location is located inside of the gsr hotel in reno, and is frequented by predominantly hotel guests and staff. i can't imagine someone stopping by the hotel just to go to this specific starbucks. the seating area is ridiculous. while it is open seating, it's located near the check in/out counters, as well as, gambling sections. it's just not a great spot/location overall. the service is close to horrendous, and the staff is just the same. i ordered two different drinks, both fairly easy to make, and i was the second person in line, with no one else behind me. my drinks took close to 25 minutes to be made. yes, you read that correctly, 25 minutes for two drinks and no one else was waiting. once the drinks were ready, despite the fact that i was standing fairly close to the counter waiting, the barista put them on the counter and walked away. did not call out my name, let me know that the drinks were mine versus maybe a mobile order, etc. then to top it off, both drinks were made incorrectly. unfortunately, i did not discover this until i had already left the counter and was on my way outside of the hotel. no one wants to trek back into a hotel that is currently under construction, and frankly has become a hot mess to get in and out of. and let's face it, who wants to deal with unpleasant people when they are handling our food and or beverages?! would i stop by this location again? more than likely only under desperation. my two stars are because if nothing else, the seating area and counters are clean.

they have never messed up an order, but they need to update their hours!!! they are not open until 11 pm on sundays... i pulled up to the drive-thru at 10:00 pm and they told me that they were closed for the night! i was kind of sad after i drove all the way out to edwardsville just for starbucks.

i love this place. i spend a lot of time here (yes, i drink a lot of coffee and tea). to add a "needs improvement" comment, i think this location would serve its' customers better if they spent more time on cleanliness. - they really need an employee to address the trash cans, which aren't emptied until they're overflowing. - they really need an employee to walk the floor with a dust mop, picking up paper scraps, dropped napkins, and the like. - they really need an employee to wipe down each table. - they really need an employee to address the restrooms (refilling tissue, seat covers, soap) and sweeping the floor. the manager is very nonchalant and appears to not notice these problems. in a place with such a revolving door of customers all day every day, cleanliness should be priority one. add the recent virus outbreaks in the news, and i'd say sanitizers should be everywhere. if i were managing, the above items would be addressed 2-3 times a day, between rushes. i still love them though.

once i ordered a drink that came out hot when i specifically told her i wanted it cold and when i notified the barista, the lady that took my order said i didn't say anything. that was rude, almost as rude as the dude who thought i wouldn't notice the difference from a plain milked down tea latte and a gingerbread tea latte.

worst starbucks experience, ever. even worse then stopping at a rest stop starbucks - gasp. i met a friend here on a saturday morning, and it was chaos. we were getting on the road and wanted a quick pick-me-up before our long drive, but it would have been faster if i drove another shop and got it. first, the person who took my order did not hand me my baked good (which normally happens when it's not heated). i asked about it after i paid, and he confirmed i had ordered it but didn't get the coffee cake for me. i thought maybe they would hand it to me with the drink, but that did not happen. in the meantime, a woman who ordered after me was handed her pastry at checkout, so apparently that does happen, just not for me. i ended up having to ask one of the other store personnel to get the coffee cake for me, after waiting for some time for my drink. then i went to put half-an-half in my coffee, and the debacle continued. there was no half-and-half out on the service station, so i inquired about it. i told a barista there a no carafe at all to begin with, yet she brought out a carton expecting to refill the carafe; but she couldn't, obviously. instead of letting us use the half-and-half, she disappeared with it to look for a carafe, which took more than 5 minutes before she found one and then filled it before we could finally use it. there was no order in the store and it was just mayhem. i don't think the staff is well trained and could definitely use more supervision or additional training. i don't think i'll stop by here again.
the staff was amazing!! they took extra time with my nephew and made him feel so special! parker and i will definitely be back, cause seth was wonderful with us!

its a starbucks - but this one was actually fought for by local residents. the historic review board was not having it, but the populace rose up and told them to get stuffed - and, now its here. the place is rarely anything close to empty. lots of hipsters and oldsters on any given day or time - laptops plugged in, books being read, parking lot full. you might have a problem getting in. thats a great thing considering i have friends who live close that always walk over... imagine if everyone drove in. yeah. anyway - i like this joint, and usually run into someone i know when i make it up from s tampa to pop in here. its the location and people that make this starbucks stand out.

i had never really gave into the hype of starbucks (sb). it was just one of those places that everyone seemed to enjoy. coffee, smoffee...what's the fuss, right?! ...wrong? i think i may have become a new convert. one early morning, i needed to feed the post-workout beast within. ya girl was hungry. without trying, i had time to spare before work and wanted to try something different. sb seeped into my thoughts, and i hopped on over to loughborough commons. per one of my favorite youtuber's vlogs, she constantly raved about the bacon gouda sandwich. so i went with that, along with the strawberry acai refresher with coconut milk. this particular store is small and sits at the corner to the entrance. the drive-thru was giving the ladies morning rush hell, but they still managed to greet me (somewhat dully) when i walked in. the store was clean and airy on the inside. after a few secs of menu gazing to make sure my choices were the one, i gave the cashier my order. quick, simple. the wait wasn't too long; the workers were going as fast (and accurate) as possible. the cashier didn't ask for my name and i'm not apart of their rewards program, so they had to yell out my order to me. ain't no thing, as long as it was accurate, lol. the strawberry refresher was amazing. mixed very well and so fresh. i'll definitely be ordering that again. the sandwich? just ok. the applewood bacon was very crispy. the egg was cooked to my liking, but i just wasn't wow'd. beautiful looking sandwich, tho. honestly, the sausage/egg/cheese sandwich from bread co. is way better, imo. in addition, my total was pricey. sb is pricey in general, but nothing over the top. being a new convert, i'm willing to try all of what's offered at starbucks. hopefully another trip and experience brings about more stars.

i love this store! every employee is extremely kind and they never fail to say 'good morning' when i walk in. it's a huge plus that i come so often, they start my order before i reach the counter. two thumbs way up

just went through the drive through... it was pretty busy but the young gentleman working the drive through window was super nice. wish i would have gotten his name. it's nice to see that even though they are busy, they are still giving great customer service!
very nice starbucks. plenty of room. quick service. plenty of bakery items and packaged type items to choose from. this is where i go for breakfast.

starbucks is what it is. but this is the worst location. i would like some friendly service with my over priced coffee. still 2 (not 1) stars because the overpriced coffee is just as good as any other starbucks.

this starbucks is on my way to work but i'd rather go anywhere else. takes absolutely forever and the staff will literally ignore you in line or at the drive thru, no acknowledgement that they even know you're there.

possibly the friendliest starbucks i've ever been in. i sat inside for a few hours quietly working on a project. the baristas made the initiative to make every "guest" feel welcome. very upbeat and positive!

psych. turns out this starbucks isn't so great. if you go inside the service is so slow because apparently their priority is the drive-thru line. i've waited forever multiple times for a drink when it actually would have been faster to go through the drive-thru that was ten cars long. i've also gotten the wrong drink 3 times in the past two weeks (including today), prompting this update. those girls need to get their act together.

the irvington starbucks is my favorite starbucks by far!!! i come in just about everyday and the place is clean and they have amazing staff!!! i come in so much they know my name and they always engage me in conversation. it's a good work environment and the music is always good here!

i mobile ordered. never received a notification that my order was ready so after about 10 minutes i went inside to wait. they had placed my order where the regular drinks are instead of the mobile order pick-up location. won't go back.

i love starbucks tea & get it often @ this location. i don't understand why this location is extremely slow. it doesn't matter the time or the day there is always a line. they r not very friendly @ this location either. therefore, i had stopped buying starbucks tea. i recently started back hoping things got better its been about a year. no luck. i went this morning around 10:25am there was a line wrapped around the building & flowed into the side road where traffic is pretty busy. i parked to go inside thinking it would b quicker. it was not. a large group was waiting inside. after waiting to place my order i asked the very young cashier who the the manager was & he said he was. i said to him, i just want to let u know there is a line around the building & is even flowing in the side road of traffic. he said, yes we are busy and asked what i would like to order. very disappointing. ive never had this bad service at another location.

ordered my drink extra hot, as usual. order repeated to me properly. drink v v cold. obviously the barista too lazy to warm the milk up even though they weren't busy. come on guys, $4.50 a drink, you need to give me what i order

i tried their oreo cookie frappuccino, and my husband tried their lemon pound cake.... delicious! the employee at the drive thru taking orders was extremely polite and bubbly, and let me know i could substitute dairy for coconut milk, etc. after i let it be known i'm taking a chance with dairy today.

by far my favorite starbucks location in reno. consistently fast and friendly service, with very easy access to parking. many starbucks parking lots are a nightmare, but not this one because they are in a large shopping center area. i almost exclusively use my mobile app to order and pay ahead of time. during covid, the pick up experience between different starbucks location has been frustratingly inconsistent. some locations even make you stand in line to with people walking in to order because they keep prepared drinks behind the counter. this location has by are the best system! right when you walk in the door they have an organization system. your order ticket is placed in a bin with your name on it and when ready, they place the order there was well. very impressive. wish more locations would use this system. there are several starbucks locations closer to my house but i will drive out of my way to visit this one because of the convenience and service.

i think this is the nicest starbucks i've ever been too! it looks like it was recently renovated ans shiny sparkling new. i was pretty surprised to find it done up in such a rustic style, comfy couches, different shape of chairs. it's very restoration hardware. can't comment much on service and food quality. i was here at 7pm and it was dead. my order was taken quickly and served just as fast. as for the coffee, well, i try to avoid starbucks at all costs and don't think it would be fair for me to review based on that. my review is strictly based on the "looks".
yes the coffee is good but an additional bonus is that the employees here are for the most part very attractive. a+

absolutely love this new location! it has just recently opened and is easily my favorite one to study at! it is actually a little further of a drive from my house than other starbucks locations, but i choose to make the drive every time! first of all, i have to complement the staff! the employees are so kind and are eager to please their customers. during one of my visits, i ordered a drink that they were out of and was completely refunded and given my "second choice" at a discounted rate. i thought this was so kind. next, the atmosphere is great! there are many tables of all shapes and sizes, so i am convinced there is a place for everyone to get "comfy" here! they also offer nice furniture in the front of the store by the windows, which is not something all locations have. the restrooms are impressively clean and are very well-maintained; really, the whole place is! if you are looking for a place to be productive or just meet with some friends over a cup of coffee/tea, i highly suggest this starbucks location!

experienced the most delightful customer service here! i was torn between the new drinks and cristal was more than eager to assist me! definitely will be coming back because of how pleasant cristal was!

this is my spot! i find myself at this starbucks location many times a day! coffee is one of my biggest passions and i love the environment this location offers. this coffee shop is located in the heart of downtown franklin. right of the corner were you can sit and people watch all day long... its very relaxing i must say. i am all about hanging out with my iced coffee, and friends on the corner watching life happen all around us. wonderful starbucks! wonderful people! its all good!

starbucks is great and their coffee is always pretty good fine coffee. however, costumed service at this location was horrible!! the guy was extremely rude and i didn't feel welcome there at all. at first, he talked to his co-workers about how he got "his new tattoo in philly" while i was waiting for 5 mins for him to come up to me and get my order. he was extremely rude and disrespectful. he messed up my order 3 times and even on the 4th one, it was still wrong but i took it anyways because i didn't want to deal with him. i don't know if he was having a bad day or what was happening but it was 7am and seeing an incredibly rude person at 7am is way too early for bad vibes, in my opinion.

this location is actually the location right across the street from west edmonton mall, right beside boston pizza.; it is not located inside the mall. i've gone to this location a few times and to be honest, it lacks in customer service on the occasion. there are a few barista's that not only provide a quick service, but also provide a friendly attitude while on other days the service is extremely slow coupled with a rude attitude. depends on the day. there is a drive thru at this location as well, which is convenient.

this is one of the absolute worst starbucks! the employees are rude and condescending! they behave as if they are doing you a favor by taking your order! they pay absolutely no attention to your order and so therefore if i ever got an order correct on the first attempt, it may be a miracle! if they paid half as much attention to the customer as they do to each other's personal lives, then maybe they could get an order correct! i have unfortunately visited this store on more than a few occasions and am still waiting for them to fix the problems. save yourself the aggravation and go to another location!

nice location, right by the whole foods and the new atlantic books outlet. the bathroom is clean and private, the service varies from time to time but in general it's good. there are a ton of windows and four big comfy chairs, as well as a sitting bar. there is also outside seating. the best thing about this location is that someone is always dumping their newspapers in the communal bin, so no need to pay for one! :)

the nashville airport has two starbucks locations. the other one can be a disaster (line of 50 people). i'm almost always flying southwest (the other terminal), but sometimes i'll come to this starbucks because i think it often has shorter lines. that's all. it's a starbucks and maybe the lines are shorter than that other one.

i give up on starbucks. a few weeks ago, i ordered a mocha. i waited for 15 minutes while the barista stared at me every chance she got and completed three other orders of people who walked in behind me. the only thing i different that day was i was wearing a uso cap with a marine corps pin on it. only when i went to the register for a refund did she hold up a cup a and said she was making it. i sent an email to starbuck customer service and have not received a reply. now when my neighbors give me gift cards for watching their pets, snow blowing driveways and everything else neighbors do for each other, if they insist on giving me something i will ask them it not be a gift card from starbucks. and i won't be getting starbucks gift cards for the service men and women when the uso has events.
typical starbucks, coffee is always good and i enjoy the vanilla scones. a nice treat for a morning sweet fix.
this is one of my favorite starbucks in tucson. building is relatively new, so it is clean and stylish. service is always fast and friendly. my strange requests welcomed with a smile :)

what can i say? it's your average neighborhood starbucks that fails to live up to the hype. i'm not sure what the obsession with this place is. i do come here occasionally because i am usually offered (by a friend for example) a free meal because this is what they enjoy, so i take it. if i had to pay the full price for this stuff, i'd never come here. i'm sure most of what i'm going to say is going to apply to every starbucks out there. this is given the fact that they hardly do any real work to make the food besides heat it up and for the drinks, follow a set recipe. the only thing that'll possibly differ from other starbucks locations is that this one tends to burn any food that's heated up - consistently. you can see the burnt bottom of my ham and cheese croissant in the photo. must just be a feature of the oven here. now, onto the stuff that applies to every starbucks i've ever been to. if you want a popular item, you can't always expect it. there will be times that items have run out. i bought this food today at 10:00 am and this was the first time they've had a ham and cheese croissant in days. in fact, when i asked if they had ham and cheese croissants, the employee responded with "yes we have one ham and cheese croissant". i'm not sure if he was trying to ration the ever-popular ham and cheese croissants by only selling me one, or that he really only had one croissant left at 10:00 am after not having them for days. with other items, you will find that this applies to any starbucks. the second major thing to note is that you're paying for half filled cups of whatever drink you get. in my pictures below you can see how much of my green tea frappuccino was just ice that i threw down the sink - over half of it. this was despite the fact that i asked them to double blend the drink because i suspected that this would happen. it sucks that their frappuccino drinks are never actually blended well enough. i've had green tea milkshakes better than this frap from a mall booth, and i was actually able to finish drinking them because they weren't half ice. it's just another way that starbucks tries to scam you out of your money, though. a few other small things... the bantam bagels are okay but are far more bread than cream cheese. definitely dry and in need of more cream cheese flavor. however, this is the closest thing you'll come to a bagel and cream cheese at starbucks in your morning rush, because starbucks doesn't put the cream cheese on your bagels for you. also, for all the money you're paying for their overpriced stuff, you still have to ask for napkins. they do not come standard no matter what you order. weird... i thought my $20 drink, bagel, and croissant warranted a few napkins free of charge. also, at this location, i once ordered on the mobile app and the inventory wasn't updated, apparently. when i got there, they didn't actually have 3 of the items i ordered in stock. it was okay, though, because they made up for it with great customer service. they refunded me for those items that weren't in stock and still let me choose another item for free. i do have to admit that their customer service is usually on point.

came on saturday at around 830am waited in drive-thru about 20 minutes, i was late for work my iced coffee was all ice i'm very disappointed!!!!!! get faster waiting that long and still having drink made wrong is crap!!!

just came in this afternoon with my thirsty kids in the 90 degree weather to buy a bottled ice tea for myself and get three cups of ice for the kids and i. i went through the relatively short line at the cashier, paid for my bottled ice tea and had to wait more than 10 minutes just to get some ice. the manager/employee, who is the only person making the drinks at the store at the time, was super rude to me and deliberately made me to stand there and wait for my three cups of ice for a while. he won't simply give me the ice. i felt i was treated badly after having already been in line and paid for my drink. i did not expect to wait in line just for some ice. this operation is poorly run and i will not return to pay to get harassed again.

i have been consistently coming to this starbucks for about a year. during this year that i have been coming, there have been continuous moments of frustration where i have had an order that was wrong, incomplete, or in some way or form messed up. i love the food, the bacon egg bites hit the spot, and the drinks are lovely, however that's only when i get them, and get them whole. when i order i at times would get a drink and be given a completely different one, buy egg bites and get biscuits, or just not receive part of my order at all. today, as the psychopath i am, ordered a hot chocolate. the hot chocolate i received looked almost as if it had been drunk out of or in part dumped out. nah. no.
nice, new open floor plan! would have been 5 stars but they don't have a single cushioned seat or couch available for relaxed seating.

i have seen some customers get pretty picky at this starbucks and never once did the workers give a hard time to anyone. they are fast, super nice, and make all the coffee correctly. sweet ladies and men, wish they had more eating and no lines whenever you go in, but it's wonderful to see the starbucks on campus. this starbucks is always filled with students. we actually lost out phone here and one of the workers picked it up, called us back and told us he would hold it at the register until we came back. what better service is that? amazing. my coffee was made perfectly and even though there was a line there really wasn't a wait. can't compliment this starbucks enough. great job!

charged my card twice /different amounts when i went thru drive thru they had my card for longer when normal and i had to drive back latter to get refund no apology or anything like it was my fault they messed up. definitely the worst starbucks experience i've had. also they didn't give my receipt or ask if i wanted one shoulda known as usually they ask
never answer the phone, also they are very rude and ugly every time i go there. i don't recommend going to this location.

this is a great starbucks. the staff is wonderful. plus its less than a mile from our house! this is just to give another shout out to the wonderful staff at this starbucks. you are so nice and helpful and appreciated!

if you want to head to the worst starbucks in the area, go here. they are literally soooooo slow and incompetent. i feel like no one here had any real training. i honestly feel like maybe macy's just threw some of their employees in there like yeah, figure it out. i tried this location multiple times thinking each time they were having an off day, and nope, they're just rude and roll their eyes when you order your drink. i ordered a triple blonde espresso over ice with vanilla sweet cream cold foam. the barista didn't understand what i wanted, then another barista chimed in and said it was a tik tok drink, and that it's ordered to avoid paying for the "extras".. like i don't understand what i was trying to get free? and i don't even know what a damn tik tok drink is. like girl if you don't want to work there and help the customers then gtfo and go work somewhere else. short girl, heard her say she was from chester county.. that's no excuse to be rude. and they gave me regular espresso instead of blonde!

waited 40 minutes and paid 6 bucks to get a cup of iced milk instead of the shaken espresso i ordered. i typically wouldn't leave a review, but this has now happened to me multiple times. i will not be coming back.

i often go to this starbucks because it is closest to my home. the service is hit or miss probably because they seem to have new trainees here every time i am here. the long time (e.g., more than a year) employees are usually very friendly and accurately make my order. the problem is the trainees are often clueless especially when i redeem a reward and have to ask for advice from a more experienced employee. this tends to slow down the ordering process and results in overcharges. i often have to repeat my order several times because many of the barista trainees have short term memory issues which is sad because they are so young. given the high customer traffic at this location i think starbucks should not staff this store with so many trainees. i gave this location two stars mainly because of the good service provided by the few, and ever diminishing, experienced employees; if i were to base this on the trainees, i would give no stars.

awful coffee! ordered a hot coffee with a cold foam topping. i have ordered this multiple times at various locations and never had an issue. i ordered through the app and picked up at the drive thru. was never told they made it an iced coffee up til i was at the pick up window. when the young girl handed me my drink and i noticed it was an ice coffee i asked her why my coffee was made are used instead of hot like i ordered. she proceeded to tell me that they cannot add the cold foam to a hot coffee. i told her i have had this ordered at various locations and had no issue. she never offered to remake the coffee nor apologize.

minus 1 star for not giving me a paper protector for my cup. seriously? you hand me a cup, in the drive thru, over my head, with no "thingy"? not sure if it's an idaho thing to save the environment, but really... drive thru wasn't bad, staff was friendly, drink was as expected and it is around the corner from the gym i go to in the morning. i'll be here more often!
starbucks products are great! this store doesn't have the friendly service like most. i will come here again if i need coffee and am in the area, but it's nothing special!

how much is a cup of grande coffee exactly? i stopped here for a refill and after the long line of waiting, the waitress charged me 2.6 for a grande (refilled) coffee ? so even it is not a refill, why i am getting it 2.24 else where... god , if i'm not too tired to argue and the line is still too long.... don't know what's the feeling. thought it should be a comfortable rest stop. now i almost felt asleep waiting in line.. and btw, tried to order drink on app, the stupid map on the app only find ud starbucks the closest.. ahhh ...

bluf: i only go to starbucks in a pinch because of morals. support your local coffee shops. i am not a college age white girl, i dont wear yoga pants, and i hate furry boots; but i was drawn to see what the spiced pumpkin latte was all about. i made my gf order it for me though to save me the embarrassment. it was okay but nothing special. not sure what all the hoopla is about. my gf got the salted caramel latte which i didnt think was good at all. i like just plain black coffee and any time i stray from that i remember why. this line is always long but they are efficient and it goes quick. they spelled my name right (john) so that was a little disappointing.

poor service. waited minutes just to be asked if i placed a mobile order. then when i received my beverage it tasted odd, but i didn't think anything of it. when i got home i looked at the bottom of the cup, and there was a white substance that i didn't order in my beverage. they need to get better.

sunday at noon, and they were not organized or efficient. the drive- thru was packed so i thought going inside would be faster. nope. i think they take care of drive-thru customers before in store customers. after waiting a good bit for my grand mocha, i had to ask for and wait longer for a warmed morning bun. huh? food and coffee are consistently good, but their service needs help with efficiency.

all starbucks in santa barbara are shabby! they do not reflect the excellent starbucks i know from the seattle area and worldwide. this one is the worst in santa barbara. it is just so tired. i've had some of the nicest service here and some of the most shockingly rude. there is hardly anywhere to sit. small tables fill up fast. but i thought it was really sweet that they had a christmas tree in the window. and they have a clover. good to get your coffee and get out!
i don't think i have to explain this one... my faith in my favorite coffee store is really going downhill recently.

i love starbucks and go pretty often. however, at this specific one i have had a few issues with customer service. having had a similar job to this before, i realize these things are unacceptable. my first issue was a while back, but still memorable. i had ordered a medium drink. when my drink was made it wasn't only a small but it was only half full. not only did i not get what i ordered but it was half empty. there is no way while serving that they didn't realize it was a problem. my second issue happened friday. i had ordered a new drink for me and was excited to try it. however the whole time the woman was making my drink she went on and on about how gross she thought the drink was. i was a little disappointed by hearing that. i loved the drink but didn't love that experience. i love starbucks and that won't change even after this happening. however, i do know that is not acceptable service. i have had great experience here because it is my closest starbucks, so this doesn't represent the staff as a whole.

it's a starbucks, pretty standard. nice people work here, and they do have a snazzy fireplace that gets turned on during colder months. this particular location gets wicked slammed on weekday mornings, so you're much better off going inside that sitting in the never-ending drive through line. mind said line when you're backing out though!
used to be a customer here 4 days a week, but i walk in dripping sweat when it's boiling out without a wallet and ask for a cup of tap water and am told that i can't have any. thanks a lot.

terrible. the last few times my husband has stopped by over a few weeks he's complained to me either about slowness or the order being a little funky. last week he asked if my quad venti tasted like a quad. i said as a matter of fact, no. neither did his - he said the girl at the headset couldn't seen to get the order right no matter how many times he repeated (mind you, he ordered *two* drinks). today, he texted to say both lattes came with soy. only mine was supposed to. he brought that to their attention and they replaced it. sadly, he only took a sip when he got home. "taste this." i gagged when i did. he thought it was just him. nope - i don't know if that was out of irritation with him or what, but it tasted just awful. i shudder to think why. this place is a joke.

i like to refer to this location as a "manhattan style starbucks" because it's size and general atmosphere remind me of multiple locations found on the upper west side. today, there wasn't much seating available as this location is on a university campus and there were a number of students and faculty lingering with their drinks while focusing diligently on various papers, books, and laptop screens. even so, i was able to walk right up to the register and order a hot chocolate. surprisingly (in a good way), my drink was not blisteringly hot. rather, it was at a temperature that allowed me to enjoy a hot drink on a cold day as soon as i had it in my hand.

wow...definitely surprised this spot doesn't have a better rating. i affectionately refer to this starbucks as "my starbucks" (a la braveheart with the crazy irishman..."what do you mean, 'your island?" - "yes...ireland...it's mine." the one at the bellevue is closer but the line is forever and my order is always wrong. the one at 16th and walnut has the rudest staff ever. but this one...friendliest crew no matter what...my drink takes a little long? i don't even say anything and the manager hands me a coupon for a free drink at my next visit. can't give it five stars purely for that reason...sometimes you're standing there in an empty store and your iced coffee takes a strangely long amount of time. and i miss cafe hausbrandt :(.

i was enjoying a venti ice coffee and talking when we notice mice running across the flloor & my girlfriend screamed and immediately spoke to the manager and she brushed it off. never coming here i dont eat with vermin

i happen to spend a lot of time and money at this starbucks, every time i go i am amazed at how slow the drive thru line moves. the lady baristas are ok but jay and guy put them to shame with their impressive customer service skills. drinks are inconsistent but beggars can't be choosers.

this starbucks has decent space inside, but the drive thru is probably the slowest ever. i always hope it will be different but i just spent 25 minutes in a drive thru to get an iced coffee, and there is no exit once you're in the line (so i couldn't leave or change my mind to go inside or elsewhere). i understand busy times, but almost half an hour is excessive.

i come to starbucks waaaayyyyy more than i should. i know this location very well. the drive thru is always courteous and prompt but sometimes i like to go in. the restroom is always disgusting. i know these coffees are costly and i know starbucks can afford a cleaning service to clean their facilities. their baristas clearly do not think they get paid enough to keep a clean work environment. not only was the restroom disgusting but so was the lobby. the floors had massive amounts of trash along with what looked like weeks of yuck built up on the floor. i'd love to offer my company as some relief. please contact stellar cleaning services llc if you need a professional to clean this place up. now let me start on the baristas... there were some awesome workers about 6 months ago but now they are all young and worried about hanging out and how much fun it would be if they could get another of their friends to work here with them. omg please split these people up! they do not get the job done well when they are busy talking about the weekend coming instead of listening to the order being placed through drive thru!! they definitely need a class on how to do your job and do less socializing. bottom line, starbucks costs too much money to not be taken care of in a top notch manner!

i love coming to the starbucks. the staff is always so friendly, drinks are made quickly and never really a long line for mobile orders. with the pandemic and every thing going on in the world, the little things make a difference. "we love you" written on a cup just brightened my day!

this starbucks is by far the best in customer service! the staff are super friendly and remember our drinks since we've been frequenting there often! although starbucks coffee can be a bit pricey what keeps us coming back is the service! they rock! thank you starbucks people for being so nice and giving something a little extra to your customers!

i love the pumpkin spice latte and it always comes out a week before my birthday! it is the epidemy of fall! as soon as pumpkin spice latte comes out you know fall is coming and the flavor is rich sweet and pumpkin! the thing about starbucks' pumpkin spice latte that makes it so special is that they use real pumpkin in their latte. i have to say it's as good as it gets and at this location they are quick and accurate in getting you your order the way you want with a smile and friendly face to greet you! what more could you want!?

coffees are great and perfectly made but low stars are for service. if i place an order on line and it says ready 3-7 mins and 18 mins later it's not and walk in people are getting drinks before me than there is a breakdown in who should be serviced first this has happened everyday this week between hours of 6:50am -7:10 am.

a pretty solid starbucks. the mobile orders are always quick for me, usually about a 6-8 minute wait. the baristas are nice and the space is open, although i haven't spent time inside in awhile. my favorites are their nitro cold brews, and that seems to be a pretty popular drink, because sometimes they run out of it. the only reason i gave it 4 stars is due to the location... the parking lot here is really cramped, and i always get anxious getting in and out of it. otherwise, i love the batham bagel bites, mini scones, and of course the coffee - especially those nitro cold brews as well as the flat whites.
the starbucks at the southwest terminal was speedy & friendly. they would be outstanding if the gals would wear hair ties & the kiosk was a wee bit cleaner.

this starbucks never gets drinks made to order, the drinks are always super watered down and they never taste good. feels like a "half-assed" starbucks, including employee effort, overall food, and customer service. you definitely don't get what you pay for. go to a different starbucks.

the staff in this location are great always friendly welcoming. it is a very small location and sometimes very cramped so unless it's not a grande pike i am ordering i pre order through the app!! i love this!!! in the summer they have a patio that is nice to sit in no way would i chill inside and have a coffee way to cramped!

i am not out in the conshohocken area too often so i generally don't visit this starbucks. however, this starbucks is so subpar, i'm happy i don't have to visit. i came in and the line was very short. i then ordered a couple of items including a chocolate chip cookie, protein pack and a smoothie. my friend decided to get a coffee with hazelnut. after that, we got a seat and waited for the food to come out. however, it was a pretty uncomfortable seating arrangement as everything felt very cramped. i suppose that is because the starbucks is so small. after a long wait of around 10 minutes the drinks finally came out. unfortunately, my smoothie was pretty average especially compared to smoothies that i have had at other starbucks locations. my friend's coffee with hazelnut was pretty bad according to them. the staff here was very nice, but it was not enough to make up for the other disappointing areas. altogether, this starbucks isn't great as the food and drinks are pretty ordinary and there is little seating. however, the staff is nice.

this is the worse starbucks! i have been twice in the past week and ordered through the app, which stated it was a 1-4 minute ticket time. i live in new orleans east and i order a latte while i was 20mins away and once i reached the store my coffee wasn't ready. they are extremely slow and should hire more staff for the morning rush! i had to cancel my order twice within the last week. i prefer to order from starbucks on st claude!

i love new locations and i am extremely satisfied with this location. love the decor, service and all the amenities (drive thru, outdoor seating, can walk to whole foods which is a minute away). one of the baristas welcomed me by name on my 3rd regular visit; it shows the baristas emotional intelligence.

again my favorite place for my carmel frappe. however i do not know why starbucks builds one such small areas. this is on the corner of a busy intersections and again not nearly enough room for vehicles.

i go here almost everyday and today i found a hair in my coffee as i was drinking it i tried calling to bring it to someone's attention but no one answered the phone. this is extremely unsanitary there should be more precautions taken when employees work around food.

i like this starbucks so much it pains me to have to share it with any other customers. dogs love the pup cups and get excited every time we visit. working on a way to take this whole starbucks to my house. i think there is room in my back yard.

oof - this location is severely understaffed. you would think a 2,500+ attendee conference at the jw marriott would call for more staff, but quite the opposite. 9:30 on a monday morning with one person working the register and the other one doing drinks/sandwiches by herself. the line grew and grew and was easily 50+ people deep. it took me a solid 30 minutes to get a breakfast sandwich and water. when i walked by mid-afternoon, the line only had five people, so maybe it was just a timing issue. still... if you're at the jw for a early morning meeting/conference, i suggest finding your coffee source elsewhere unless you're willing to wait in line a long time. *note that they charge for h20, even if your order in a sbux cup.

what the heck?! this starbucks location has now entirely blocked off, with their starbucks green cones, campbell entrance and exits! they did have the campbell exit open until now which made it convenient to leave from the drive thru window. makes for one entrance/exit off glenn which becomes quite the cluster you know what especially during busy times. im contacting city of tucson to see if that's kosher.
awesome service christina is amazing remembered my drink from last time i was in today, in fact remembered it so much that she reminded me i left something out! loooovvvveeee great service like that!

oh, how i love me some starbucks! there isn't one where i live so i always make sure i get my fix when i'm near one. their service was super quick and staff was incredibly friendly and helpful. i had trouble understanding how to use my gold card and the barista walked me through everything which i really appreciated. she also introduced me to the green tea latte - yummy! you gotta try it out! awesome service and great product!

each day star rating would change. the core staff is always friendly and quite excellent. they take care of you. they understand the type of drink you want. place usually clean. lately a lot of staff change. there typically is not much sitting room. now with holiday season approaching there is less. there is always a wall with boxes. this has become a place to get coffee not a meeting place because there is absolutely no room. it may be one of the smallest starbucks i have seen.

while they focus on getting the cars through the drive-through in a timely manner, they lose focus on what a five dollar cup of coffee should be. i gave them two tries and i got quite possibly the two worst cups of coffee i've ever had. a call to the store with my frustration was met by a very young and sharpie voice telling me to come back and let them remake my cup of coffee. doesn't that defeat the purpose? the bottom line isif you're looking for a good cup of starbucks coffee and have a few extra moments make the drive to winthrop.
one of my local starbucks... this can be a very busy location but they are on top of things and keep the line moving. i only wish they had a drive-thru at this location.

this starbucks is more a homeless shelter (5 stars) than coffee shop (0 stars). all of it's 20 or so seats were filled this with homeless people, and the staff either do not care or they are too beat down with pc to do anything. don't get me wrong: i have compassion. as a society, we should tackle the homeless problem. but this is a for profit business. most for profit businesses try to make a profit. they often do that by accommodating paying customers, and trying to get them to spend lots of money and come back. many of the starbucks in santa barbara seem more interested in creating safe spaces for homeless people to hang around, use their many electronic devices, and talk to each other or themselves. i will not go back to starbucks until they focus on creating a clean and safe environment for the customers. so, 5 stars if you are looking for a homeless shelter, 0 if you want to take your family for coffee.
outstanding staff. drink was made to order and i was allowed to work and relax for awhile before visiting friends at the nearby venue.

this is the only starbucks i go too! i'm in love with all the staff! there's one in particular his name is gizz and he's the sweetest person i've ever talked too! he's a tall boy with dirty blonde hair and he always perfects my drinks and when i don't know what i want he makes it perfectly!

this starbucks sets the bar for teamwork, customer service and quality of product. even when they are backed up they are still faster than most. they talk to each other, they finish each other's drinks. jen is an amazing store ambassador, she is happy to see people and they are happy to see her. fiona knows when you change your drink and checks to make sure that it wasn't an error. there's a woman with shorter and ofter colored hair that also seems to be an anchor on the team. im forgetting some and there are so many on that team. i wish i knew her name because it's definitely worth mentioning. i don't know the store manager's name but to her credit she has an amazing staff. you should check this store out..... no wait....don't...i don't want the lines too long. :)

this is the armpit of starbucks. the worst location in the entire edmonton area. terrible service. they get your coffee wrong at least half the time. the wait in the drive through is a minimum of 5 minutes per car. if there are 4 or 5 cars in front of you, it's a minimum half hour wait. and then the staff will probably get your coffee wrong and have to remake it. i have been here several times thinking it was new store problems. but it's not. the store is so inconsistent you just can't predict how it will go. my sister in law was a frequent customer here until yesterday when the barista screamed at her (after she pointed out they got her coffee wrong again) and then threw a large coffee against her car. this is unacceptable behavior in an adult, let alone in someone working with customers. neither of us will be back. ever. she and i have both given them more than enough time to work out the new store kinks and hire some decent staff. apparently it's never going to happen.

barista got my food order wrong- ordered a cinnamon coffee cake (said it clearly, twice). ended up with a breakfast bun. very loud inside the facility and nearly impossible to exit the location to go north on oracle rd. saw several potential customers struggling to figure out how to get in the store. perhaps it's a signal that you should try elsewhere. my last time going here.

i love starbucks and this is definitely one of the nicer ones i have been to in my travels. but the one thing i can say is that no matter how busy they are it always takes longer for me to get my drink here than at any other starbucks. i'm just not sure why. maybe it's just the mood i'm in today. haha. staff is super nice though.

starbucks has such high quality control that reviewing one is pretty much a silly exercise. however this starbucks should be avoided unless you have lots of time to spend. case in point today is a sunday. hit the drive through at 1pm. spent 16 minutes waiting at the drive through window with no cars in front waiting for one normal drink. in two trips here over the past month, both were the slowest starbucks trips ever.

eh, this starbucks is hit or miss. unlike some of the other reviewers here, i've typically had pleasant interactions with the staff as it pertains to personality, but the wait times here are tremendous. they're so slow that the traffic gets backed up well into the street, which, during a morning commute in brentwood, is very frustrating. in short, this is a fine place to go on a slow saturday morning when you can go inside and have time to wait, but stay as far away as possible if you'r eon any sort of schedule.

this starbucks was clean, and service was friendly and quick! the very big downside to this location is the parking lot. there is a double-sided, one-way lane that hooks to the left into the oncoming lane of a two-way road. the space is very tight, and if the two cars parked at the end by the store aren't parked far enough into their spots, you may have to do a bit of neck-craning to make sure you're going to make it out without a scratch. otherwise, i like this sb and would go again.

i have a big secret. this is my favorite starbucks location and i go here 2 to 3 times a week. okay, that's not a big secret... my secret is that i have been going here for a few years now and frequently of late and i have never been inside. i'm usually in a hurry when i go to this location; i am either headed to work in the morning or getting a quick caffeine jolt in the afternoon or evening and i always use the drive thru. the baristas here are incredibly friendly. come to think of it, i can't remember a time when i encountered an employee that was rude. we all have bad days and i would totally forgive one or two bad experiences, but i can't think of any. my regular order of a triple venti white mocha (and shhh sometimes 3 petite vanilla bean scones) always comes out quickly and consistently. i guess i need to rethink this starbucks. i need to remember to think about it not just when i am in a hurry, but also when i am looking for a coffee shop to sit, relax, and read a book.

after waiting in the drive thru for a really long time, we finally got our coffees. mine felt really light so i opened it and it wasn't even 3/4 full (and that's including the whipped cream). i had to park in their not so great lot and go in to get a replacement. the rep remade it and gave me a $4 courtesy card, but that drink never should have gone out the door. also, the chalkboard sign outside said they would be closing early for thanksgiving - it is early march.

this is the fivebucks in the lobby of the big marriott in center city and it. gets. busy. and crowded. and messy at the sugar/creamer station. and attracts a few vagrants who are quickly ushered away from tourist eyes, meanwhile, i needed a latte and i latte i obtained. what a rush. try not to come here from 7:30 - 9 am on a weekday. mob scene.

i really don't like this starbucks. they take forever, when the one just up the street on 82nd doesn't have a problem getting my coffee out in less than a minute. i just don't think it should take 15 minutes to get my coffee.
this is one of the slowest starbucks in the airport system.. been in line over 5 minutes and 3 people have been helped so far... not enough employees working.. there are 4 and one is stocking items..
