let's face it, it's starbucks. a little bit on the overrated side, compared to the hip coffee shops that are now dotting the reno area. i don't hate their coffee, but it's not my favorite. however, it always seems to be a good choice for meeting up. easy to get to, lots of parking, and plenty of tables and chairs to pass the time idly by. no, i am not a regular, so they don't know me here. and the cashier/barrista made that very clear by depriving me of any semblance of a smile that the others have written about here. but i have to say, when i asked if i could have my cappuccino in one of the real cups, and not in one of their famous to go cups, he indulged me. for that i was grateful. my friend had one of their italian sodas, and when i asked her how it was...her reply: eh. but we had a good chat, so all in all, it was not a complete fail.

they charged me and then skipped my order and eventually somebody asked me why i was standing there and somebody else said oh sorry. and then it was too late and i gave up. but it all worked out because i went to denny's and i had my food in front of me in less time then i waited at the starbucks for my espresso.

this location is by far the worst! i live right around the corner so this is my primary location... not once have they gotten my order right! i have complained so much to their headquarters, but do not understand how they are still functioning! they never have any of the syrups to make half the drinks and always use so much ice!!! i ain't paying $6 for ice y'all! cmon!

this starbucks does not know how to make the drinks. i order a double chocolate chip frap and it was watered down and was barely chocolaty at all. horrible plus bad service when no one was inside or in the drive thru.

happy vibe at this starbucks! the baristas are so personable. quite often i am greeted by name and my drink is already being prepared. i was particularly impressed with elmer, who gave five star service every time- equivalent to somewhere like the rosewood or four seasons. great place to chill and do work on the computer as well.

this starbucks is pretty reliable in terms of service and drink quality. the wait in line can take a while but they make the drinks very quickly. i got a caramel macchiato and they made it perfectly - not too sweet, with the right amount of caramel, foam & coffee.

not a fan of this starbucks. i would go as far as to say it is the worst in in indy. the staff is slow and unfriendly and the location always seems drab and dirty. there are plenty of other locations just wish they would redo the staff and interior of this one because it is closest for me.

waited for 30 mins for our drinks inside, saw others bypass us when they were behind us in line. note, if you want a drink in a reasonable amount of time, apparently you shouldn't come on a saturday or go inside.

always been pretty happy with this starbucks - parking choices can be less than optimal during peak hours and drive thru often wraps around the building but that is pretty typical for most starbucks in the tampa bay area. lots of seating options (when dining area isn't closed due to covid) - this location probably has one of the largest dining areas i've ever seen at a starbucks! only drawback is when they keep mobile order beverages out of reach/unattainable, as that largely defeats the purpose of ordering ahead/efficiently. also, recommend ordering via mobile app and running inside to grab it versus waiting in the drive thru. :)

b4 work, this starbucks does brisk drive-through business on the outside, with some customers stepping into the attractive-though-smallish dining area on the inside. as my friend bill c. notes, customer service is exceptional for a coffee place. the staff person working the counter was visiting with a regular as i arrived (but quickly took care of me), and was introducing herself by name to a newcomer who followed. bill c. didn't seem too thrilled with the baked goods, but my apple-raisin muffin was just fine. the standard pike place coffee was very good, too. perhaps it's not as hip as e'ville main street, but out here at the crossing there's better parking.

this is your typical small starbucks. true, it's not "the thing to do" in the french quarter. but, if you work at the canal place mall, at westin, or in the office tower and frequent this starbucks, like i do, the staff will remember your name and your regular order. that's all i ask for in a coffee shop.
slowest starbucks ever.... three years i've been coming here and they never disappoint... guaranteed time to get a latte ... 8 minutes. i will say the people who work there are nice but come on

i get it, you get busy. that's normal. it's how you handle it that matters. 25 minutes for a coffee is unacceptable. what's worse, when people complained to the manager, they were dismissed. the young woman taking order would say she had no idea how long it would be! you better have an idea! you know when the orders placed, you know when the name is called. step it up a cville!
when i am in town, this is my place for espresso. curtis even knows what i want without asking, triple shot on ice. my go to coffee shop when i am here in nola

we arrived at this starbucks right as they were closing during a severe thunderstorm. we had our screaming baby, but the employees at this location welcomed us in, even as they were preparing to close, gave us free coffee, and waited out the worst part of the storm with us. when we were ready to leave one of the employees even walked us out to the car with our diaper bag and helped us get safely in to the car! they went above and beyond and it made a big impact. we will definitely stop at this location the next time we are close by!
the baristas always know my order, this is my favorite starbucks it can be a lil messy on busy mornings but i have rarely come on in the afternoon to find it that way

every time i go here they made my order with sugar and claim that they don't add any. i'm very very sensitive to sugar and ask every time. i've gotten it from other starbucks correctly. here i always have trouble! the blonde woman is always rude to me also

i stopped here and got a refreshing cold teavanna iced tea (peach citrus white tea) based on the recommendation of the staff member who was working here when we visited (part of the reason for the visit was that my companion needed to use the restroom). the beverage was really quite good -- better than i had imagined. it was a good choice for a hot day. i'm thankful for the beverage recommendation.

convenient location and friendly service. i stop often and my pick up / drive thru orders are always correct. today i purchased a gift card in the drive thru and was helped by juan. he brought several gift card options to the window and completed the order efficiently. thank you juan for another great experience.

ah starbucks. well once again brand recognition is in full force, looking at the outside i could have been anywhere (madrid? penang? sydney? - nope i was in st. albert) and the inside has the same cozy, casual feel to it. the store front faces west which is great for our area because that means they get the afternoon/evening sun. the service is good - not great as sometimes you have to repeat your order or the staff feel the drive-thru takes priority. the food and beverages are exactly what is expected from a starbucks (that's what you get from a standardized chain) but i find this fact comforting because i know what i order will taste the same no matter where i am in the world. i like that there are multiple plug-in locations - on the walls and underneath the bench seat. this is great if you do need to work for a long period of time because then you don't have to fight other people for the limited power. there are 5 starbucks locations in st. albert and this is by far my favorite - for some reason, even for how "out of the box" the store is i really like the feel of this one - it is less busy then others, the staff are always welcoming, and the parking lot is very user friendly. i love to meet up after dinner for coffee with friends throughout the year here.

i like the location of this starbucks, and it holds a lot of memories. bf and i used to play the game of life on my iphone after work here before we were dating. the staff are friendly and while it's usually pretty busy i find there are lots of places to sit. the neighbourhood is safe so there are no complaints from me on this location, there is even a decent amount of street parking and parking around back!

nope. just nope. cost way too much for what you get. they're coffee is way too acidic, and far too pretentious. when i want a cup of coffee or an espresso i'd like to be able to state i'd like a small, medium or large. venti pfft! besides that, it gives me an upset tummy. so over s***bucks.

it's nearly always too busy, which i suppose is just a thing to deal with. when not too busy, it's a lovely place to sit and enjoy a tasty beverage with a friend or with a book. they have a deck so in the summer, they double their seating capacity so long as the mosquitoes aren't biting. i've been there several times and have received adequate service. yesterday, i wanted their egg-whites, feta, and spinach sandwich. it was unavailable and so the person apologized and then removed the sample from their display -- this tells me they care about their customers. the fellow making the drinks needed help, though -- there were 5 people waiting for drinks by the time i joined the line.

had the worst coffee this morning! i order the same drink all the time and i knew it didn't look right... but i was running later than usual and didn't say anything... but my drink was terrible!!! not the way i wanted to start my day! i ordered a grande, it was so light, i'm thinking i was shorted a shot. i used to love this location but i feel like it's been hit or miss lately... and it wasn't even really crowded.

not one of the better starbucks avoid if you can. lousy food selection minimal pickings and i agree with the other person the guy who made me my drink was short and snippy. take your money elsewhere if you can if you're going to need a high calorie totally unhealthy frappe you're better off getting much better shake at sonic for a lot better price with a smile too

this is the most inefficient and just plain awful starbucks i have ever been to. they've never gotten my order correct and all it is only an iced vanilla coffee with heavy cream. i went there once and i had to go back in the store three different times and i still left with a drink that didn't taste how it normally does from all other locations. i only go here when it is convenient as far as location. don't even consider the drive thru...unless you're prepared to wait thirty minutes for your incorrect order.
ok drink from the drive through. they were a bit short at the window but the drink was ok. will go again because it is soooo convenient of a location

the first time i came here was during the morning rush and the line was really long, but i needed my cup of coffee. lol. even though the line was out the door it went by pretty fast. the barista that made my drink was so nice! she called my name as soon as my drinks were ready (i ordered my favorite holiday drink: grande nonfat caramel brûlée latte & hubby's venti caramel frap with extra caramel) and she even told me if i didn't like it or needed more caramel to just let her know. but it was perfect! for years i've been going to starbucks and no one has ever told me that especially when they are super, super busy. i could tell right away the baristas working here love there job. they have the best customer service and value there customers.
the service is ok, coffee and teas are standard. if service was better it would get a higher rating, they couldn't keep up with 6 people working and 8 people in line.
a great afternoon stop, nice patio outside and beautiful inside seating. the coffee is addictive ! ((( great service too )))

worst experience ever. have never experienced so much attitude from a drive thru just for asking for an extra splash of half and half and then when it wasn't made correctly and i wanted it remade it was like i asked for the entire world. thank you so much carolyn for ruining my afternoon! starbucks should be a place of smiling and happiness.

i have stopped in here several times, it's a good break point driving between phoenix and el paso. i have always found the service and the food and drinks (english breakfast tea - mmmm) to be great. it's usually busy so i can see why some might get frustrated but weather i'm here with all 4 of my kids getting lunch or just taking a break i enjoy my visit. keep up the good work.
nice and cozy environment perfect for studying and just hanging out with friends and family. food is amazing and the drinks are great. and the staff are very friendly too

i live around the corner so i go to this location frequently. i don't judge them on their packed drive-through or parking lot - it's the way most things are in seminole heights. my problem with this location is that every time that i visit it after around 10am they are out of everything. milk, food, cold brew, flavor syrups, literally everything. it's really frustrating to wait in that long drive through line only to find out that they are out of milk.
eggnog chai...only available during the holidays, but it is literally heaven on earth.

this is one of the best starbucks i've been to in a long time. the food comes out hot and fresh and the drinks are made by expert baristas. the speed is good for the area they're located in and it's amazing!!!!

4 stars for the coffee and really low in the service. i've come here to buy just coffee and they fail to give me the discount for bringing in my own cup and they don't give me my receipt for the discounted price on a cold beverage later in the day

love starbucks but this location is always busy. 10:30am and i'm 10th in the carline to order. sometimes they bring out a barista to take orders and speed up the process but that is inconsistent. in my experience the service is always friendly but the cashiers inside and at the drive through do not seem to have the urgency you'd expect for somewhere that gets this much traffic.

the newly renovated starbucks is super nice! there are lots of chairs and huge tables. i love it! before there were never seats in this store, but now i'm sure we'd be able to find a seat at any time. it's really just very large! the parking lot is still very tiny and you have to go around the roundabout, which can be tricky. you must watch other drivers who have no clue or are in too big a hurry and will almost sideswipe you to hurry and park in a handicap spot. well in the space between handicap spots. so rude. we live super close and pass by often so it's easy to come here, but usually safer to go to other locations. the new strawberry frappachino is excellent on a hot day!

this location has a couple of very sweet employees that always try to be friendly and upbeat. it's always a pleasure to see them! however, i come here atleast 5 times a week and i never get the drink in under 20 minutes. i've had to leave because after 20 minutes i am officially late for the next appointment and i just don't get the drink i paid for. the best way to get a drink is to order when you are 20 minutes away so you don't have to wait more than 5 minutes in the store. the owner of this store should be embarrassed about these numbers. but i've been coming for a year and it's getting slower!

it's a drive through! it's early in the morning, you're on your way to work and you want to beat traffic. here is the perfect place to get that cup of coffee. staff will make you feel like they care about you. service is quick, then you're on your way. a must for teachers traveling from odessa into tampa. you know you need that 5:30 a.m. coffee fix to sweeten up your day! conveniently located right before the entrance to the veteran's expressway.

drink quality is uniform to any other starbucks i've been to. the people working, however, had zero communication, be it with me, or the other workers. i think the only words exchanged were my order and my total. they also were slow and disorganized - there was some asian kid behind the counter with a coat and backpack on making drinks. he was talking to some friends on the other side of the counter, but where everyone else was wearing the black shirt, apron, and hat, it looked like he works there (he did know what he was doing) and he stopped by on an off day with his friends to make drinks for them and a few customers. really weird. parking lot looks cramped, but to be perfectly honest, it isn't nearly as ridiculous as the starbucks in southington, ct, so i can't even hold that against them in comparison! when i'm at our friends' in the area i'll stop by if i need my starbucks fix, but if i was a local i'd probably try to find another.

terrible service by barista too busy chatting. manager was rude! the place was empty and i've been served quicker in extremely busy starbucks. i overheard the barista say 'it's my last day' so hopefully things might improve but i'm hugely surprised by the managers attitude because they are usually so good in starbucks! my drink was wrong but it wasn't even worth mentioning!!

we will start with the good, this particular starbucks is located just off of i-4 and is open 24 hours a day. after that though, it kind of heads downhill. when we stopped in, it was late afternoon, and nobody else was in there. for whatever reason, it still took way too long to make a few simple drinks. another reason for the low rating, there is no place to really hang out here, as there is with just about every starbucks. no chairs or couches, the place is purely built for you to go in and out, and that is all. overall, just not the starbucks experience i am used to getting.
just loved this starbucks! this is a reserve store which completely made my day! lots of great options to choose from! this is one of the most unique starbucks i have been too- i loved the décor!

i love this location. it's just where you need it. right across the street from a doctor's office. my fav drink is the green tea frap. the rice crispy treat is really good too. free wifi and excellent customer service at this location.

i love this starbucks. it's my go-to attagirl prize for getting my grocery shopping done at the nearby walmart. i've always received prompt, friendly service and it's nice that they have a drive thru.

new starbucks. love the anterior. spacious and pretty! they also have a wide array of coffee beans you can buy (more than regular stores). they also have a big desk space for u to sit/read (unlike some other sbux around the city). i'm glad this place opened up near rittenhouse.

standard starbucks but in a great building - the flying saucer building! 3 stars is a stretch considering after 2+ months of nearly daily visits at the same time, they still can not remember my name or drink. guess i am asking too much.

this is by far the best starbucks ever. i used to think all starbucks' were the same, but this one is special! what makes this place great is the incredibly friendly staff. i frequented this location a ton during my 3 years of law school, and the baristas always brightened my day. i have never encountered a nicer staff at a coffee shop. the inside of the starbucks also makes it great. there's a fireplace and a ton of seating. the hours at this location are also a plus.

coffee with a smile, fast friendly service. it's really nice having a starbucks on base that i can stack up rewards points. speaking of rewards points you can't claim your rewards at this starbucks, while that is a bummer i didn't see it as a reason to big enough for me to deduct a star since there are plenty of other starbucks around town.

beautiful interior, very clean, very friendly staff, they give a very warm environment fast service amazing drinks, pretty good food hard working staff very rarely did i see an employee not working they have an employee faith that's very sweet and helpful was polite and professional and knew what she was talking about. young man not sure what his name was didn't get a chance to talk to him was very hardworking and very set on doing his job keeping the store clean and helping those around him i recommend this place to all who look for a very comfortable and great place to relax and have a great drink.
2 stars for coffee.. sometimes it's very weak and flaw. 5 stars for customer service. polite & friendly baristas.

in my book, this is one of the best starbucks in center city. the coffee is always good and the staff is friendly. it is always clean at this location as well. can't say that for several other starbucks locations in the city.

the worst starbucks in town!!! their drive-thru wait time is horrendous. the line didn't move an inch in 5 minutes. i was in the drive for a total of 13 minutes! on avg., the wait time is 10 minutes. the orders are never correct and inconsistent. i ask for lite-ice, i get an iceberg!!! i go at least 4x/week on my way to work, hoping things would change. i even tried going inside, it's even worse! people were waiting for their order, not realizing it was ready because their names were never called or written on the cups. the orders were only labeled with a printout. this location will not be getting another cent from me! this place should be embarassed!!!

my favorite location ever. the staff is fast and friendly even on busy days. the drinks come quick, and correct everytime. if you're unsure on what you want they'll make recommendations based in your likes, not on something they may be pushing. overall great location.
my favorite starbucks in town. near my casa and is well staffed. even with a line they are quick and friendly.

the coffee is good but the service at this location is always horrible. the staff looks dazed and confused especially if you ask for something like more half and half since the container is empty. no reason to wait 5 minutes for a cup of decaf, gave up on getting cream. i could understand if this place were busy.

i come here every morning to order my breakfast. depending on who is staffed the drinks can be hit or miss. i think the caffeinated drinks are way too strong and needs to be mixed with decade coffee. i had to order skinny drinks because the the originals had too much sugar, caffeine. and some of the food has too much sodium.
all staffs who were friendly and helpful with fast order to take out. nice services and very good.

this starbucks is in a hopping spot right in the middle of the philadelphia premium outlets. so be prepared to wait in line for a while. they do a pretty good job of keeping things moving but it is crowded. they do have some outside seating which is nice. all in all its your average starbucks.

this store is nice store. i was be there many times. these girls were very nice and friendly. only one thing that was letting me felt "do they really know the 'meaning of grande'". i was ordering the same orders by couple days which was fine. but, today (02-09-2020), they gave the funny orders which were green tea part. am i wrong? or we both were misunderstood each other by the meaning of "grande". so, that's the side by my understanding.

whatever starbucks is doing during their employee training, it's the best business model to date. i've never had a bad customer service experience here in my life and i'm here weekly, for years. yes i can admit it, don't judge. i really want to know starbucks corporate secrets. they're killing the customer service game. it's one all businesses should implement. i always leave happy after my starbucks employee encounters. they are all so jovial all the time to the point where i've thought to myself, should i leave my career to become an employee there. i even one time asked a barista what their secret is and she told me it's all the americana shots they're doing together in the back. well it's working. see you guys tomorrow!

we were in need of coffee numerous times while in indianapolis and starbucks is a fairly reliable place to get strong coffee. the employees were friendly and helped guide us with ordering something that we would love since neither of us get to starbucks very often. i'd say in about a year, we've only been to various locations about 3 times. i usually go for a chai tea latte but sometimes something with more caffeine. our first full day, i didnt sleep the night before because of anxiety, so starbucks saved me from being a zombie. overall, this starbucks is pretty standard but i liked the employees and the store had a very zen atmosphere. this specific location will definitely help you get caffeinated.
literally the worst starbucks in america. they messed up my black coffee by putting sugar in it and gave me lattes with no espresso. terrible all around. nothing good to say.
delicious drink.... mobile ordered was ready soon as i got there baristas were friendly and greeted me when i came

i feel like this starbucks should not have a drive thru. it's pretty awkward the way it is set up and people sometimes cannot back out of parking spaces. the line gets very long and out into central ave many mornings. everyone who works here is very friendly, however service is at times inconsistent. this is my neighborhood starbucks. i visit every other day. i think it would be much better if it were set up similar to the 4th street and 9th ave location.

made my drink wrong 3 times... it was a passion tea with stevia.... not hard. wasn't even that busy. and the baristas were annoyed every time i asked them to remake. it was wrong the 3rd time too but i had to go to work. thanks for the bad tea.

loyal starbucks customer and this is the friendliest starbucks i have ever been to. hands down! i'm a yelp reader but not a yelp reviewer. so i'm giving a review for this starbucks because that's how strongly i feel about this location.

about a month ago we came in and ordered a iced coffee with milk and syrup around 10 pm. the iced coffee was more water than coffee with only about a teaspoon of milk added. i go to starbucks very often and this starbucks location just sucked! the girl working late that night wasn't any help when i told her i've never seen iced coffee like that before, she just looked at me and said rudely, " do you want me to make u another one?" just rude. never again here! save your drive and go to another starbucks

this isnt my usual starbucks, but stopped here because of convenience. i ordered an iced caramel macchiato with soy milk, since i am lactose intolerant. when i received my drink i automatically new something was off due to the color of my drink. it looked very light, like almost just pure milk. and it tasted pretty much only milk, so i think they didn't add the right amount of expresso shots. however i needed to be on mu way so i didn't ask about it. also later i started feeling off... kind of how i feel when i drink cows milk. sure enough when i looked on the cup, it was not made with soy milk like i asked. so now im at work, sick, and tired. i know mistakes happen, but pretty much everything about the drink was incorrect.

came in right around lunch time, and it wasn't too crowded. available seats for a good conversation with a friend. barista/cashier was very kind and friendly. my vanilla chai latte was yummy, though it took a little long for them to make it.
friendly and great service. love that i can get my mobile order at the outdoor window if i have my dog.

i love going to this starbucks to study! it has such a comfy vibe here that makes me feel like i am at home. on top of that, my barista mickey was awesome! he answered all the questions i had and seems to always be in a positive attitude! this guy makes the location amazing! aside from the atmosphere the coffees great and it's nice always seeing local law enforcement in there to get some coffee. maybe the only downside is the limited parking, but that makes it more exclusive!!
favorite starbucks and a new favorite drink.....green tea frappuccino with soy!!! excellent!!

i've been here for 20min waiting for 4 drinks. i have watched 3 phone in orders come in and go out ahead of me. i have also watched 4 customers who ordered behind me leave and there have been at least 10 cars go through the drive through as well. i guess i should have stayed in my car.. not sure what the hold up for my ice tea is, is but i've never been inside a starbucks this long. boo... (the drinks were made well...so there's that)

nice starbucks inside harrah's casino. only problem, you've got to walk through a cigarette-smoke-filled casino to get to it. harrah's is an old-school, dirty, smelly, old casino. to my surprise, the starbucks was clean and relatively fresh.

hooray for free bathrooms and free ac. boo for no toilet paper someone got left in a shitty situation here for sure. unlike other sbux, their restrooms are not gender neutral. on the plus side, there is a changing table in the men's room if you have a little one. didn't order drinks so i cannot speak to that. overall on a saturday night this place was much calmer and had no bathroom line, while the sbux further down chestnut in midtown village was a zoo and had a toilet line.

let's face it we mostly do not visit starbucks for the coffee but for the experience, the baristas are always with a smiling friendly face and super knowledgeable, they look me in the eyes and the customer service is outstanding. speedy service, nice but not spacious on the inside - great outside seating area. for the size of this location the cleanliness is always great. keep it up baristas! :)

brutal. wife called for two coffees and a sandwich. stood there for 30 mins waiting. finally asked and they said they'll make it. i said i'm leaving take it off the tab. worst service ever and still haven't gotten my refund.
3 solid stars. it was pretty clean to be so populated and they served regular starbucks and did not try to rip me off! unlike the starbucks on south broad street!
don't hang out here long if you need to use the bathroom. only 1 at a time for the men. awful.
loving all the stars i earn since i work across the street! just wish they were open later now! but it's starbucks so i'll make it one way or another!

this starbucks is inside target, i had gotten a wrong order from another store and was in target, i mentioned it and andrew working was incredibly polite and corrected it for me. would absolutely return to this location. not to mention it was delicious when i did get what i wanted!
the staff looked like no one wanted to be there and the seating is not relaxing or very inviting. they can make a decent drink no problem but the atmosphere is not what it could be

(3.5) ~ good overall coffee & service. notes: the main entrance is the same for both the bank(right) & starbucks(left). my flat white was good, but not great;) and my one wish would be that they add a clover brewing system;)

i love this starbucks location! mary at the drive thru is always super friendly and asks how my day is going. i always appreciate that! it gets fairly busy during the weekday with lots of people getting work done, so outlets are sometimes at a premium and finding a table mid-day is sometimes difficult. obviously starbucks is a chain but this location is fantastic-- accurate on the order and a friendly staff that makes it seem like a neighborhood coffee shop. keep up the good work!

i made a google account just to review this place, more of a yahoo kind of guy lol. this starbucks should get zero stars if it was an option. the drive through never moves fast enough, incredibly slow. and when you get to the window they take ur card and give ur drink and slam the door. so if ur taking all this time, and not treating your customers well what the hell are you doing? also found out from sitting in the cafe early mornings, how much behind the back talking there is. i have to sit outside now, because it bothers me so much. looks like adults but feels like it's run by a bunch of teenage high school girls. i've worked at millions of customer service jobs growing up, and understand what it's like to close and open. but while i went up to use the restroom & ask for the code, the reason they don't move fast enough is apparently because of closers. just grow up & do your job. incredibly unprofessional, lower your voices and prioritize things your customers can hear. will be calling corporate about the interesting choices of words i hear around 9-10am.

i never thought to do a review for a chain like this, but i've come to really appreciate the quality this store offers. the employees are always pleasant, and orders are made swiftly and correctly. i had gotten used to the quality at the location on valrico & 60 which is... subpar. i now drive the extra 15 minutes to go to this location instead because the experience is unparalleled!
it's the closest starbuck's to my office, so i keep going back. they don't have the quickest service at this location. the staff is either very friendly or just kind of bla.

the hot americano's (extra shot) here do not taste the same as a corporate starbucks. as an example, compared to the circle center monument corporate starbucks store. i'm unsure if it's the machine type that does not properly brew the espresso beans, or the bean they're using? i can tell you this though, the machines they're using is not the same as a corporate store! suggest a little extra 10 min. walk to this store. on 55 monument cir., indianapolis, in 46204.

while the drive through has always been really fast, the store employees have always seem cold and rude...by far the most unfriendly starbucks staff i've had to joy of meeting. the blank stares or annoyed looks i've gotten anytime i've greeted them good morning or said thank you does not make me want to return, period. i get that it is a busy location and i truly wouldn't care about their lack of hospitality if the quality of my orders were okay..but i've gotten stale pastries, watery iced teas, and incorrect coffee orders there the handful of times i've visited. if i'm paying $5-6 for a drink i would love to not feel like i'm annoying the staff with my existence while still getting a bad order.

awful service. ordered ahead on my phone and still had to wait 10 minutes for my drink - that turned out wrong. when i told the barista it wasn't supposed to be iced he said "oh, next time it will be in the house." right...

so this is the second time that i have come to this starbucks and both times they were like snails. i waited in the drive threw for 30 minutes and then when i came inside and waited 30 minutes for a cup of coffee while there was a total of 4 people working. they were talking more then do anything anything. will not be coming back!
went in to get a cup of brewed coffee. berista said, "it is 45 minutes old and did i still want a cup?" no! is this starbucks? won't ever return to that store. so disappointing!

stopped here while shopping at canal place while in town. it's a very small location tucked away in the corner on the ground floor. the line builds up rather quickly and ,on the day i went, service was slower than molasses. there was one person taking orders and one barista on duty. i understand that locations can get slammed with orders but it was almost as if it was the barista's first day. he just moved very slowly. i couldn't order a java chip frappachino because they were out of java chips. so they may want to evaluate their ordering of supplies. i'm interested how service is when business is slow . hopefully not the same and, if so, i would give him a break.
this starbucks always has good ring and good customer service my latte was very yummy and had great taste as well as it was heated perfectly thanks starbucks team !

start off the morning. they didn't have two of the breakfast things i order this morning! this was the case yesterday morning. i will not be back. a big store like this can't keep up with orders i would be finding out what the problem is.

this starbucks is probably my favorite. even though they don't have a drive thru like the west end one, there is always parking available and the line is never too long. plus, they have some cool seating with bar tables, short tables, armchairs, and booths. its a nice place to sit and talk or work.

one of my favorite starbucks to go to when i get a chance. they always remember me and my drink. they make it perfect with a hint of love. it's perfect location makes it easy to grab on your way into target and out to go shopping. there is no better way to spend your then drinking a starbucks while shopping and looking around. the only issue i ran into is a new lady working there gave me a hard time about a birthday coupon and she got really sarcastic and rude and didn't even say happy birthday or try to make the situation better. the worst experience i had. other then that everyone else is lovely. also sometimes i feel they are short staffed, most the time i only see one person up front.

i used to luv having a coffee in here as i live in the neighborhood but lately it's occupied by homeless people, they literally sit there all day with all their shit around, looks like their base. the management doesn't do anything about it which is a pity because they are losing their regular customers..

i frequent this starbucks to study. it is a larger starbucks than most in tampa area (i've been to 6+ recently to study); it has over 6 'reading' chairs (no table), 6+ two-seater tables with one side of booth seating and your standard long table with 8-9 seats. there is outdoor seating as well. the staff is friendly and competent. i've had a few experiences at other starbucks locations where, in a slow period, they're joking / messing around to the extent that it's disruptive to people trying to read or study. i've been to this location over a dozen times total and i've never had that experience here. five star location for sure, i'd highly recommend coming in to either grab a quick drink or stay a while.

this probably one of the worst starbucks i visit. they are so slow with getting the orders including the mobile orders which they claim never prints. i'm sorry but what good is the mobile order service if it never works. update: i haven't been here in a while and i thought things got better. nope, it's worse. customer service is very poor.
this starbucks is always out of something, every time we come here. the breakfast sandwiches are always frozen in the middle and coffee/tea orders are always wrong! this is a terrible starbucks.

really nice, big and relaxing location with a nice range of seating options. no drive thru but easy parking for in & out. staff are friendly and efficient. i particularly like the wall decor - a great eclectic selection of photo and paint prints. i especially like the large lush garden scene and the labyrinth. oh yeah - the coffee is dark and rich, just as i like it. there are the usual other options as well, of course.

hanging out in a coffee house/cafe to do some work while sipping a drink and possibly a snack? having to feed a meter the entire time is not the best plan. this place you have to find a metered spot and then feed it. meters are expensive here. the store is not as warm and inviting as many other of these and it's not as nice looking inside either.

city line ave, phila border located near target ;) & a few tempting eateries in this short strip. okay starbucks with a outdoorsy sitting area. parking? don't know, i was at crown plaza for a conference when i found this place. giant busy target parking lot right behind. be creative. server never even told me my decaff was ready. but the pro is the coffee was not too sweet. i like when i can drink it as is. i hear it is college student central, and i like that -being surrounded by a bunch of st. josephs and pcom smarty pants. ;-p
this starbucks is my " i feel crafty" stop. it's right near home depot and pet store. they have great staff. jade rocks the house!. she goes out of her way for her customers. well stocked.
waited 15 minutes for drinks while staff went to the back leaving one person upfront. got drink and it was nothing but foam worse starbucks.

this is a great starbucks! i have spent many hours studying here. the staff is friendly and not only ask your name, but really try to learn it! i always find it to be clean and organized, and the coffee is delicious!

this starbuck location is very friendly and accommodating to college students. there is a full menu and the hours usually last till midnight. per usual, the design is very open and the number of outlets is quite plentiful. just make sure you don't run your cord across the floor how i did as a safety hazards. oops (tt). the service at this location was very warm and open. one person greeted me and acknowledge my presence in the queue and try to prepare my order so i was not waiting too long. my experience was good and i would go back for a caffienated drink and/or to study.

wow. i go to starbucks at least once a day and i've never had a bad experience until tonight. the customer service was horrible and no big deal that he made the wrong sized drink, but when he went to remake it, he used the old drink to put into the new cup, added no new whip cream, it was runny and he left it all sticky and didn't wipe it off or offer napkins and when i asked for my receipt, he said he couldn't find it. i asked to speak to a manager and he said there wasn't one on duty. unfortunate that every other starbucks i've been to has cheerful, polite workers who seem to love their job but these employees seemed to hate life and customers. coffee should make people happy. 2/20/17 @ 825 pm

this is really a top notch starbucks! it's larger than most, with lots of little areas for you to hang out with friends, or just sit alone and get some work done. the baristas are knowledgeable, quick, and don't seem to get flustered if there's a bit of a line. there often is, but they get through it quickly and you never have to wait long on your coffee. they truly care about the community as well. i work for a children's theater, and they've welcome us with open arms to bring our kids in to read scripts. there's a super cozy fireplace as well, and great outdoor space for the summer months. this starbucks does a great job of serving you like a neighborhood coffee shop and doesn't feel like a big chain conglomerate store like some of the rest

so, i wanted to change my review from last time. i came back here to study but i realized that some of the tables were very wobbly and not in great conditions. furthermore, the bathrooms (both of them) were very disgusting. as well, when i got my pumpkin spice latte, there was already a lot of splatter on my cup, and the rim looked like someone drank out of it (though it is more reasonable to believe that the latte had overflowed and left its mark). however, i highly recommend the pumpkin spice latte - very sweet and warm. service was okay.
they're fast and friendly. they got all 7 orders correct! they don't water down anything. generally, everything tastes the same.

this has got to be the slowest, most incompetent starbucks i've ever been to. that says a lot, since i've visited hundreds. i waited in the drive thru line for 35 minutes for one item, and no it wasn't during rush hour or anything. actually still in line! this is the reason i typically visit the goodlettsville location. should've known better.
love this starbucks. everyone is usually always nice and polite... thumbs up from a true coffee drinker :)

very friendly staff!! comfortable atmosphere for a starbucks. i don't care for starbucks, but i like that starbucks. i had a caramel frappuccino and the foam was so sweet. terra the cashier took her time with me. i had a gift card that needed to be scanned. she took her time and made sure everything went through correctly, my final bill was .23. stop by for a refreshing coffee drink after riding the street car.

this is the worst starbucks i've ever been to. the cashier literally said to her coworker "i dont work two feet over there, i work in front of the cashier only, dont ask me to do anything" she was wearing a pink face mask on 06/15/2021 and is heavy set. my father went through radiation treatment and has no more salivation glands in his mouth. we ask for a large water cup and they said "we dont want to give you a cup because we were shorted cups" we asked for a different style cup and offered to pay for it. the girl shuts down and goes "i dont need to deal with this" and turns around to her manager and just points at us. i grab the receipt and she snaps at me saying i didn't need to snatch it when i didn't.... she was the most unprofessional person i've had the displeasure of meeting. her other coworker proceeds to talk about me negatively like a full grown teenager when i can hear her, she threatens me by saying "you're lucky i'm behind this counter" which is extremely unprofessional, like what did i do??? the heavy set pink mask gets the supervisor and i explain what happened, then he is mortified by the actions of his employees and says "everyone is the same rank back there i don't know why she thinks that over there isn't her job. i am extremely sorry for the unprofessional behavior and threats you received from this staff."

here all this time i thought i was cool . nudge,wink,...... my son told me to order the grande,carmel frappuccino one day, i was hooked. so, on the occasion i get there at 5:25 in the morning, and they are taking orders, i will order my manly drink, and sip with delight and slurp the whip cream with my straw, when i get to the bottom. mmmmm, :)

it's a starbucks, you know what their coffee tastes like (meh) and what their prices are like (high). so i'll tell you why this became my starbucks. they recently renovated the seating and it's a great study spot. reliable wifi, big tables in the middle, a comfy couch in the corner, and seating all alongside the window (read: convenient outlets and people watching). huge improvement to the awkward tiny circular tables they had before. it can get randomly crowded sometimes (why any family would decide to bring all their children here for an afternoon or why a mom would bring 8 teens to drink frappucinos is beyond me). the barristas are nice. one of them made fun of me for having been there every day for a week when writing the paper that wouldn't die, i appreciated that he noticed. i still prefer independent coffee shops like citadelle or toast, but when i'm too lazy to go that far, this will do. plus i'm a sucker for that passion iced tea in the summer (get it half sweetened, it's better) and for $1.62 (?) it's pretty good.

since it's opening a few months ago, i have been to the drive-thru at this location twice. both times, i was helped by rebecca, who was simply a delight! she was so kind, upbeat, and had a great attitude! the first day i visited, i was in a crappy mood and her energy turned my whole day around. wonderful customer service like hers doesn't happen every day, which is why i was so thankful to see her a second time. nothing beats getting your coffee with a friendly smile!

this is the closest starbucks to my house. not bad most of the time, standard. they drive me crazy cause i request light ice on cold drinks as they fill the cup and you end up with about a third of a cup of fluid. if i don't get light ice the 4 dollar drink is gone by the time i get home. ordered light ice today and they filled the cup. i think i'll just skip this starbucks and stop at one where i'm not paying for ice instead of a beverage.
you did it this time starbucks employee!!! i made a few simple requests; light ice and a heated crescent nothing crazy. well..... too bad they're stingy. disappointed with the service.

you'll find this location attached to the hilton bayfront hotel. they have an entrance off the street and one inside the hotel also. they have shorter hours due to the location as well so be sure they are open before coming this way. they do not have a drive thru. they have the standard food, mugs, snacks and beverages of all the locations. staff was friendly and the store was clean. they do not have a scanner for the phone app so the cashier had to manual input the number, and i felt bad for him having to do that! they has my drink finished before i was done at the register. they also had the florida ornament mug, which i picked up here as well! no bathroom inside, odd location, off site parking and no drive thru being this place down a bit. overall: a great service. short hours. plenty of seating!
new location is open - avoid it. inside is dirty. the service is pissy. and the drinks made taste awful.

recently renovated. the pros: more modern looking, there's more fridge space to offer more non-coffee items, the baristas are still the same ones pre-renovation. there's outdoor seating now. the cons: power outlets eliminated all except for 1-2, the new furniture seems to be intentionally less comfortable to keep people from hanging out for a long amount of time. they still make the beverages in a quality way, but sadly, it is no longer the coffee shop that's designed to be a place for casual conversation.

i've grown increasingly frustrated with this starbucks. i now go out of my way (driving ten minutes to another location instead of only 5 mins from my home to this location. they very rarely get my drink order right ( it will be very clear on the order sticker as to what it is) and i am more often than not requesting a remake. i am a daily starbucks consumer and every time i have decided to give this location another chance, they have messed it up.

let me just start by saying that i love starbucks! i continue to frequent this location due to it's convenience on my drive to work. with that said, i continue to be disappointed starbucks - you rarely provide me napkins when i order food. and yes, i am guaranteed to spill something in my car. you have also stopped giving me my receipts - or even asking if i want it.

if i'm paying $6 for a specialty drink, i expect them to fill it to the top. i go to this starbucks all the time and sometimes they are great and other times they struggle. i normally complain but i didn't have time today. it's ridiculous. give the customer what they are paying for.

they are consistant in one thing- making my drink wrong. every time i go it seems that the baristas are untrained, and i end up getting something wrong with my drink. today my drink literally tasted like vomit!! staff has been extremely rude about returning drinks- which i already feel horrible about as i'm not a "karen", asking me things like if i even knew what the drink was supposed to taste like. this store needs some serious work from management, an attitude check, and more training! the line takes forever as well overall not worth your time.
gave me my coffee for free because the wait was long....fyi... not long at all! i was in and out! woot! woot!

busy location for driving through during weekday mornings. not sure how it is during the weekends, but assume that it's just as busy if not more so. the interior is very big with lots of seating. they have yet to mess my coffee order up and the people at drive thru are always pleasant, smiling and friendly with me.

this starbucks is the slowest in clearing airport lines. i've been flying through bna and using this location since it opened years ago. now, unless you have 20+ minutes to wait, do not stop here. time to move on to other options.

it's always busy which doesn't bother me, however service over the years as gone down. yesterday i went in to pickup a mobile order and i saw the man put non fat milk in my drink that was supposed to have almond milk. i informed the barista who looked annoyed and said i was lying. her co worker who put the milk in told her i was right and he did put the wrong milk in. the manager i assume was sitting on the bar side and went back over to help them and re make my drink. i am just frustrated by the lack of an i'm sorry we will remake it no problem. if i am paying $10 for a coffee it should be made correctly. this location is close to my night class and work or i'd honestly go elsewhere.
small sb. took a long time in drive through. didn't seem all that busy but maybe orders were large. very small.

this is my coffee shop to work at daily inside or on the patio. the employees are always so nice (in addition to the amazing drinks). recently, i left my purse on the patio when i was moving inside with my computer bag. they were so kind and quick to take it and hold it in the back for when i realized by mistake i left it. great employees at this place.
love their selections of teas and coffee. very friendly. wish it had more seating, great location.

so definitely lots of pros. they stayed open during the pandemic and was able to get the drive thru line moving quickly. mobile ordering in the morning was a breeze. always offered to give my pup a puppucchino when i go through the drive thru. the major con about this place is the drinks are inconsistent. its always a bit disappointing when you realize they added way too much cream into your cold brew and full pumps of vanilla (even when you ordered it with just 1) after you drive away. but alas, i will be back...especially since pumpkins back on the menu

i have to agree with reviewer kirstie n about the love hate relationship. i tend to love them a bit more than not though. i have had awesome and the next can have the wth is this drink effect. that aside, we were homeless here last week, no motel, and no job to go to with the customer messing it all up, so we sat for 3 hours in front of this starbucks without getting kicked out :-) we kept going in and buying drinks as our outside table 'rent'. staff here was great, and yes, the drinks here were awesome this day

people come to get coffee, and many come to work (largely online) while drinking coffee. your internet cannot be that slow. you're a gigantic corporation, so you have the money to boost it and remove the router from the area where the bank vault used to be. it is impossible to work here.

the long was so long this morning, and nothing ruins my day more than slow lines and a dirty coffee shop. but! this starbucks is exceptional! first time at this location and the line is moving so fast, drinks are being called out so quickly and efficiently! place is super clean even with a lot of people! my husband and i were so impressed by how well this store is run! awesome!

boom boom boom, service is excellent, the same people frequent this place including myself and they remember your name, this is by far the fastest starbucks in the city of tucson, keep up the good work.

sometimes you need a little bit of generic basic bitch things in your life. this place is one of those places for me. my favorite starbucks in the city. everyone is friendly ( usuallyi only go in the mornings, the late staff looks miserable at times but i understand).

this starbucks is extremely busy. literally every time i've been here, different hours of the day. expect a long wait whether you go in or go through the drive-thru. but in good news, they have never messed up an order that i've placed, and i'm very picky with my coffee. if you like starbucks and are willing to wait, this place isn't that bad. i recommend the drive-thru because the inside is pretty tiny and everyone packs in there, so seating is very limited and it's pretty loud.

am so excited about the new starbucks at 3400 civic center blvd. it is truly like a coffee shoppe that is independently owned, not part of a major corporation. the staff are incredibly service oriented. it must be the test they pass to get hired!!! one of the women asked me to call her ahead of time so she could save a pumpkin scone for me!!!! and i plan to do that! friday after i see the doctor, my treat is to go to starbucks, have the pumpkin she saved for me along with a café ole!! could not be happier!!!!!

nice & friendly staff but dangerously small and unorthodox parking lot. (totally not their fault!) great location 1.5 miles away from my daily rte 95 commute to and from philadelphia. they uphold the starbucks standards and i recommend this location if you're in the area.

i rarely go to starbucks- but once i a while i go to this one for an iced mocha. the wonderful thing is they're consistent. this particular location is small and gets packed. so i suggest skipping the drive thru and walking in. it's much faster.

usually starbucks are pretty much the same in most ways. this one though is my favorite because they had "giving rocks" that were painted in different ways for mental health awareness ! i love love love the idea and it made me smile. as someone who struggles with it myself it's nice to see little things of positivity , advocacy, and happiness. they also had well placed social distancing spots and cleanliness.

perhaps it was just a bad day. my drink order was wrong. i had to send it back. it happens. never mind that i was seeing complicated frappucinos being served to people who came after me in line before i even got my wrong drink order. but that's one thing. i guess you would think i ordered something off the wall. my order... a large half decaf regular coffee. you see, they make you wait until they make the complicated drinks for everyone else, and then they took care of my simple coffee. and when i asked if it was half decaf since they didn't state it, the barista admitted it wasn't and offered to make another, but not before they had to complete one more complicated latte that came after me. maybe it was just a bad day. but they reminded me why it's better to go to dunkin donuts or wawa if you just want the brewed coffee.

what a great starbucks! usually i go through the drive thru, and they got that down to a science, always super quick! the other day, my wifi was down at home, so i decided to come in here and get some work done until it was repaired. i ordered a pumpkin nitro cold brew with sugar free vanilla and it was fantastic. also had some bacon and gruyere egg bites, they are drool worthy! the employees are just as friendly to everyone inside as they are to you in the drive thru. i sat there for hours and heard them greet almost everyone coming in, "good morning how are you! welcome!" the wifi was super fast there too. its such a great location to focus on your work that they've convinced me to return and work from there some more. definitely recommend!

hours say open at 5:30am on yelp. it's 6:36 and the door is locked with one person inside getting ready. traveling on the interstate so just wasted about 20 minutes of travel time. review is for hours only. didn't get to sample wares.

the customer service needs a little work and the place is not very clean, because when i sat on the table it was dirty and o should not have to say something in front of all the customers after having a cashier with little manners.

vandy rn went to starbucks at 9:30pm 2/10/18 and ordered a frappuccino and a chicken bacon panini. it took not only 15 minutes in the drive thru and i was the only customer but when i got to work and opened the bag this is what i received for a $7.00 sandwich! this was my only food for my night shift and couldn't go back due to timing which caused me to not eat dinner. truly sad! thanks starbucks!

one of my favorite starbucks places! the workers here are wonderful and the service is even more amazing! came in for coffee and for some reason my starbucks app would not pull up so i can redeem my free drink. the girl who was at the window told me not to worry and have a wonderful day. just amazing customer service!

they ran out of sugar in the raw...they're located across from an acme. why not go buy some? would you tell people who use other types of sweetener alternatives (stevia, equal, etc) "sorry, we're out"???

honestly one of the worst starbucks i've ever been to. staff isn't friendly the place isn't welcoming and chances are every time you go in here you'll be waiting for 10+ minutes in line and for your drink. good luck.

i want to point out that i am a coffee lover first and a loyal starbucks customer.this starbucks is one of the best around. they have employees that have been working there for years and treat you like family. sandy, whom you can almost always find at the cash register always greets you with a smile, she is one of the most pleasant persons you could ever meet. don't forget about the manager isa, he always goes the extra mile for his customers. if howard schultz, the starbucks founder stopped by this starbucks location, even he would be impressed with the quality and service.

decent coffee chain, though it lacks the charm and quality of a small town cafe. the frappuccinos are icy drinks perfect for hot southern summers, however almost everything here is very sweet. often, i get items 1/2 sweet with only 2 pumps of syrup, including their iced teas.the oatmeal and breakfast sandwiches are their best food options. the service is usually quick, and it's a clean place to use wifi and have a cup of coffee.

first impression was that it is has a unique layout, and it a pretty good size for a downtown location. there was plenty of seating, and we were able to get a table right away. impacts on the experience were the spotty wifi; poor acoustics - the ambient noise was more overbearing than in most locations, conversation really travels here; and lastly, though it is a matter of opinion, it was pretty cold as well.

received a postmates to order/ pick up from this location. went 10 min out of my way to order from here, only to arrive & the door is locked with no explanation to why. i understand they are probably cleaning but i've seen over a dozen people walk up and try to open the door to just walk away confused. there is no note explaining why, and they won't answer the phone when i can literally see them in there. result, i had to cancel my delivery & miss out on money. next time inform customers as to why & how long you're closed. very unprofessional. -6 stars if i could!

this starbucks is fast and friendly. i order the same thing at starbucks all across tennessee, but this location seems to always make the yummiest version of my order. the staff is friendly, very patient, and helpful!
girls were cold and the coffee was hot. sure would like it the other way but hey you can't be too picky these days when it comes to six dollar coffee.

hello. it's starbucks. what else do you need to know? this one does have more space than the average and a nice outdoor seating patio. otherwise, order your normal caramel nowhip crapuccino and enjoy!

starbucks is starbucks. i prefer the locally owned brews, however in a rush you can't beat the perfect same consistent coffee from starbucks. their 2am--2pm treat receipt is a great deal right now. (make a purchase before 2pm then show your receipt after 2pm same day to get any cold grande drink for $2)... and besides the coffee, i am really liking that berry hibiscus tea. i can't get enough of that this summer!

i used to love going to this location...when there was a very nice barista (never caught her name) but i haven't seen her in awhile. since her departure, kim is always there and she is horrible. she's always chatting away with the other baristas, not helping customers in line or making drinks for the waiting customers. she is rude and her drinks are inconsistent. this last trip, i overheard her telling the other barista that she won't be getting the vaccine and will get a fake vaccination card. i stopped going to that location even when i'm shopping at albertsons, i will go out of my way to the other locations.

taylor is a grouch. telling a target employee how tired she is of making drinks as she is making drinks for customers. sighed and rolled her eyes as another customer ordered a frappuccino. if you hate your job, at least be discreet about it.

these folks move through molasses with seemingly little sense that they work in an airport and have planes to catch. i've been in line already for 10 minutes with little progress. staff need more help and need to step up their game.

the store was clean and service was very quick. i love that starbucks has switched to paper straws now! it's great for our environment. as far as the drinks go, this was the absolute worst caramel frappe that i have ever had. it was watery, flavorless, the caramel was thin and the whipped cream was melted. very disappointed.

i never review a chain restaurant or coffee shop, but i couldn't resist with this one. this starbucks is amazing and i think it's because of the staff. i usually do the drive thru and they are the nicest employees ever! they always ask what my plans are for the day and how i'm doing. they are always so sweet and genuine! i don't go to starbucks often, but when i do, it's this one!

not impressed. was there on a slow friday morning and couldnt get good service. the heavy set woman with her head covered was exyremely rude. she took my wifes order and gave her a total with me standing there. when i told her i had an order she seemed annoyed. when she was writing our orders on the cups she had her hand on the inside of the cup half way down it. we pointed it out to her and she got upset at us and put her hand up and said whatever and then walked away. this was the worst service i have ever had. i will not go to that starbucks if she is there anymore.

it's a smaller starbucks, that does not have a drive thru but they can really get things out quickly. i order ahead on the app when i am on my way to work (usually about 10mins ahead of time) and 9x's out of 10 they have it ready when i get there. the staff their goes out of there way to learn you name and make you feel like the care. i have noticed they have had the same staff for a while which is nice because they take the time to know you and your order. i once ordered at the wrong starbucks and they made it for me no questions asked, no fuss. this is probably one of my favorite stores. however be care if you go right before the high schools go to school, there is a large group of catholic school girls who go and if you get your in behind them you are going to have to wait a long time

sometimes even while really busy everything comes out perfectly but today was bad the flat white was made different the oatmeal which i would think is pretty simple i was given one agave while two is just perfect for me. i'm going to take a break for a bit it's too expensive to be disappointed when you get it.

honestly the whole starbucks team in this target needs to be fired or learn how to be decent people. i've never come across a team so rude. the most unfriendly people i've ever met and they don't really make drinks right. if y'all hate your jobs so much then quit instead of giving your customers a unfriendly and unpleasant experience. i frequent this starbucks a lot and every single time except maybe once or twice, i've had a pleasant experience. justin is the only one who is nice to customers.

i ordered my coffee and breakfast eggs this morning. coffee came and then finally 15 minutes after i was able to get someone's attention about my food being skipped. barista was unhelpful asked me to order again, i showed him my order then proceeded to fill other peoples orders after mine. after 30 minutes left after demanding refund and nothing happening. i go here 6 days a week very disappointed
the drive thru trash can is back after 3 years!! the drive thru moves a little slower these days but the consistency of the coffee has improved. i know this was a concerted effort on starbucks part.

this starbucks regularly runs out of coffee. i kid you not. just visited awhile ago and ordered a grand pike's place, and they said, ok, we'll have to brew some. and then it took them 5 minutes to even start brewing it. this happens regularly. you would think a starbucks would always have their flagship coffee freshly brewing. instead, they practically discourage you from ordering coffee. yes, at a coffee shop.

i left my coffee at home, so i ran through starbucks and i lead with, "i left my coffee at home and i just want regular coffee that isn't too sweet but still sweet. i just put creamer in my coffee, but ik ur creamer isnt sweetened like store bought. i just need coffee, pls." a small pause, barista was like, "i think i have just the thing. do u like a lighter or darker toffee color?" "lighter." "ok, pull around. i'll give cream on the side, too! (i pull around). she says something but i don't know what she said. then she repeats it. "no charge, i'm sorry u left your coffee at home!" best barista ever!

sorry chad, but i'm gonna have to disagree with you on this one. i've been to both starbucks (maple & magazine) and the maple street store is far superior. yes, seating can be a pain sometimes, but other than that, this store is better. staff is friendlier. the best part is the patio, at least when it's not too hot outside.
this was my first time at this starbucks location. there is tons of parking, generous seating arrangements, and fast/friendly service. one of the best locations i've visited.

i go here to get coffee for my often and every time i'm in here i wait 10 plus minutes for a house coffee with cream in it. i live in this community and still feel as though they (the employees, which most don't even live here) treat everybody as though we are bothering them.

avoid at all costs. this location has experienced management and staff shifts recently. it used to be a really great coffee shop now it's become a mcdonalds with constant pestering to upsell. not to mention its filthy.

this location has the best and most cheerful employee (i believe his name is richard) i have ever encountered, and i go to starbucks regularly wherever i go. if i could nominate a starbucks employee of the year, it would be him. the location is spacious, and a quiet place to do work (thanks, free wifi). i don't feel rushed by employees to leave, even after monopolizing a table for almost 4 hours. clean, comfortable and welcoming! what else could you want from your local starbucks? i've only been to this location once (but will be coming here from now on), but i can already tell there are regulars that come to this starbucks. many of the customers greet one another; one guy even came to pick up his packed lunch that his wife had left for him behind the counter. 5 stars.

wow. the drive thru was crazy long and there's very little parking. i parked and walked in can can't believe those people wait in the drive thru that long. i think it was a bank a long time ago. shared with jimmy john's.

a little nasty and testy when you ask for refill. the workers automatically take for granted that you walked off the premises when you've been sitting here for 45 minutes working on your computer. this is not good business practices by the employees of starbucks.
gave attitude and was short and snappy! poor customer service. better off going to other coffee shops in the airport!

i live very close to this new location and was so excited when they opened last year. unfortunately, every time i come here something goes wrong. i'm not a picky coffee person. i order standard things usually unmodified. i waited for 30 minutes due to the nitro coffee machine being down once and i had to ask if everything was okay, i've had numerous wrong orders both drive thru and inside and today i woke up a little earlier to swing by at 5:30 to get an iced coffee and they weren't even open and employees were waiting outside (i'm assuming to be let in my a manager running late). it's a friday and i wasn't the only car in line waiting in confusion. they are supposed to open at 5. i never go that early but still, i hope that doesn't happen often. i'll go again due to convenience but it's not really because i think it's a great location with outstanding customer service.

as a former "partner" (starbucks employee), i just can't kick that sbux habit. i know the standards, and i hate the stores that don't follow standards (looking at you, canal place location) because they are truly there for a reason. i will call you out on it gladly. that said, these guys can make my iced venti 3 pump classic no water passion tea (summer) or my triple grande nonfat mocha (winter) correctly every time, and with a smile. i truly appreciate that. plus, if you can grab a table, it's a great place to study or get work done.

i walked in and purchased a drink after a pretty rough morning... i wanted some time to cool down and gather my thoughts. there were about 8 people behind me who also ordered their drinks. i stood there... all 8 people received their drink, except for me. i looked at the back counter and there was my half made drink just sitting there. i proceeded to let the barista know. she grabbed the drink without a word and put it in 'line' to be finished alongside other remaining drinks that were being made. i waited another few minutes for ice to be added to the cup, along with a lid. no apology... nice.

this is one of my top starbucks in boise. sure, the parking situation is a nightmare, but the location of this place is convenient and the inside is spacious. it seems almost like it is an old converted burger king with the indoor patio seating that the old locations used to have. it has more than enough tables and chairs and there is separation so that it doesn't get too loud. the outside area is non-smoking... but then again, all of boise pretty much is. it is shaded though and away from the main street traffic, which is bueno. i love the location, the service, and the layout. can't beat that :)

only me second time in this location. we had to wait a little bit before somebody asked for our order, but it was a little busy, so it was understandable. the two grande mochas were made to perfection as was the strawberry refresher.

worst customer service ever. i made a mobile order for a drink , a bag of ground espresso and a sandwich . something happened when i placed my order and the sandwich was removed . instead of just asking me if i wanted to purchase it after standing in the store for 3 minutes they had to print the receipt to prove i was wrong. it is a $4 sandwich and a honest mistake i am not trying to get free food. alright you win and get a horrible review and no more orders from me
landon is a fantastic barista not to mention an awesome dancer!! great performance after a long, long flight!!!

i am not even going to try this location anymore. not sure about other times of the day, but midday they are the slowest staff i have ever seen. i am not sure if it's a lack of help or if they just have zero sense of urgency. i have walked out several times. i'll take my business to the starbucks in the neshaminy mall--parking + entering/exiting the mall and i will still crush my wait time here.
my iced caramel macchiato has no caramel? how is that possible? cafe seems to be doing well- lots of customers and friendly team. we just need caramel in the caramel drinks!

i love the convenience of this particular location but someone needs to explain to me why a full two hours before they close they had all of the pastries out of the case. i understand the need to start cleaning up before closing but two hours? have they never heard of impulse purchases? their manager needs to get the team on schedule because we had six people who all wanted pastries. it's ridiculous.

starbucks are not as commonplace here as they are in dc. for me, this is ok because i happen to like pj's (local chain) and any place that serves community coffee. however, i still have some starbucks giftcards leftover and when they sent me an offer for a free birthday drink, i had to hunt down the nearest location. this starbucks is not large by any stretch of the imagination. but it's got a ton of natural lighting and has a cozy, welcoming feel. this would be a great place to study or meet up with a friend for a chat. the drive-thru is non-existant and the "parking lot" is just a step above that. however, off-street parking seems to be pretty easy in the neighborhood so it's not a deterrent. kudos to the staff here for being friendly and efficient.
it's fine if you want a run of the mill starbucks item. if you want an amazing coffee look down the street for volo.

i have recently moved and this is my new starbucks location. i have gone repeatedly and it has been the slowest and worst service i've ever gotten at starbucks. first, no one ever acknowledges you when you come in and go to the counter for mobile order. you have to say "excuse me, i'm here to pick up my mobile order." today, i mobile ordered at 8:13am... but 8:30am it still was not ready. at other locations, my order can be ready in as quick as 4-5 minutes. there was one girl doing all of the drinks for in store orders and mobile orders. her tickets for mobile orders were excessively long and when a guy asked to see where his drink was in line, i asked as well, she told him and did not acknowledge me. i left because i would be late for work. there was at one point, 12 people there waiting for mobile orders. my total was $8.24 and i did not receive my order this morning. quality of drinks and food are usually fine but it just takes way too long and they aren't very friendly or accommodating and there's definitely not enough workers for the demand of this location.

this place is ridiculous. seriously why pay 5 bucks for coffee when you can get it for a dollar at mcdonald's. not to mention snobby people. some customers and some employees. i don't care if you are called a barista or not to me you are a coffee pourer. don't make me feel like a low ball because you sell expensive coffee!! (end rant)
very slow starbucks. was not very organized and did not really know how to deal with the large crowd.

the absolute slowest drive-thru service one can find at any starbucks. i've spent over ten minutes waiting at the window at least three times now. i would be understanding if they said something like our computer is operating slowly, but instead i'm left sitting and waiting without any heads up as to what would be slowing down the process. the store is clean, well kept and a great place to work from if you can work remote. so two stars for that, but three down because of the drive-thru service is so bad that i don't believe starbucks as a company would be proud of it. (note that i'm not saying time in line waiting, but time spent literally just sitting at the window)

the baristas and staff here are wonderful. always cheerful and efficient and such a nice way to start your morning. i just had a "pay if forward" moment when i forgot my wallet and a barista covered the cost of my coffee. sometimes it's just the little things!
