love this place! it's clean, and the employees are friendly enough, and it's the best place to go in summer, winter, fall, or spring. it's quiet, so it's great to listen to music , read a book , or do your homework . in the summer i usually get a chocolate frappuccino. in the spring i usually get the pink drink. in fall i get the dragon fruit one. and in the winter a hot chocolate. and i like trying the unicorn one whenever it's around. this place is great and they always put fruit in the fruity ones, and whip cream and chocolate sauce on the hot chocolate, and a frappuccino. and i am always willing to try something new at starbucks. plus they have almond milk! i prefer almond milk in my drinks or coconut . but, i would recommend putting more whip cream on because it's upsetting when they don't put it on all of it and the whip cream melts because they didn't put enough on! but otherwise it's one of my favorite coffee shops! yum!

a slightly below average starbucks hence the below average star rating. they were out of things and it was early in the day, the coffee tasted like mud and the staff was particularly "out of it". usually at a starbucks there's some millennial zombies working who don't know anything and then one employee who knows everything. this location seemed to not have the "knows everything" employee no one could answer my simple questions about the varieties of coffee and a question about the food that should've been easy dance. i asked if they could make me a pot of the blonde coffee and they said "no" because it was later in the day, i often ask them to do that at other starbucks and they have no problem with that at all i realize they stop making the blonde earlier in the day. i was super friendly when i asked and they do it at a lot of other locations so it must be indicative of something else. i hate to be negative, i really do, but the place was also not really kept up. as far as cleanliness goes it was definitely a bit messy and no one was attending to it. there are definitely better choices for your money.

if you're looking to get work done, do not come to this starbucks. for a solid half hour they were playing extremely loud rock music (complete with a lot of yelling) that was blaring throughout the store. the music was quiet at first, and at a bearable level, and then one of the employees it turned it to the highest possible volume. i looked around at the other patrons there and everyone seemed equally as annoyed. this is probably the 3rd time the high volume of the music has made me left this store. i will not be coming back.

my go to starbucks!!! staff are friendly and know my cinnamon dolce latte obsession. lol. i was annoyed when this place was closed for construction because there aren't any really near me other than this one besides the bhp one... and there's just too much traffic. drive thru here can be slow but it's not usually a big deal since i stop after work. can't wait for pumpkin spice to come back because i'm basic. not sorry.
these baristas/managers deserve a raise! they are on their sh*t!! service and drinks are consistent every time!

this review is expressly for adam, the night shift supervisor. without his incredible attentiveness and incomparable customer service, my friend and i would have been stranded in the cold while waiting for a tow truck. not only was he very accommodating while we waited, he made a mean caramel apple spice drink! 10/10 goldblums!

oh my gosh!!! i just left this location and got in the car with my husband and said, "if i wasn't married i would marry that starbucks employee!" i have literally never had such a great experience at starbucks in my entire life and i've even worked at starbucks. whoever the guy running bar on a tuesday 10/29 was deserves a huge pay bump! wow. ok so i'm a super snob about filling up a grande amount into a venti cup. literally every single time i order it that way they always ask what i want with all the extra room. honestly there's not a whole lotta extra room. 10 ounces to be exact - so he filled up the grande cup and poured it into the venti cup and it goes well over the highest top black line. like a lot. and it's the perfect amount for the pumpkin cream on top and this is the best day of my life. thank you thank you thank you!!!!

cute little location near terrytown & gretna. looks like a log cabin somewhat. i stopped here a few times during a recent business trip to pick up my fix. note the small parking lot, tight drive-thru, & you can only turn right leaving.

so you now have to wait in line to get the code to use the restroom and then wait in line to actually use the restroom. oh and the barista who has the code miraculously does not have a pen to write it down so you can remember it. this is after the remodel where they though it was brilliant to serve you out of a trailer with no food and half the menu. maybe go to largo or something?

used to be one of my favorites, but a fair number of the staff aren't as friendly as the previous employees. i like their product, but half of the starbucks attraction is atmosphere and if employees are grumpy it ruins the vibe.

this starbucks always gets my order right. whenever i go in to any starbucks, i order 'sugar free iced caramel macchiato with almond milk, no drizzle'. every other starbucks serves it to me hot first and has to remake it iced, or they forget almond milk, or they add drizzle. clearly i've made it into a skinny drink. today the barista was working alone on cash and making drinks and he got it right and he was friendly, a teenager. always clean. always polite. thanks jasper ave starbucks

this is my least favorite starbucks! i've been here over 5 times (since i live close and it's easy to stop by), but i'm always disappointed! a few of the baristas i've experience via the drive thru are not professional, are rushing me as i order (ex. "ok, is that all?... as i'm continuing to list my order). they do not handle special coffee or tea orders well! (changing the milk or syrup type) they always mess it up and give me the wrong coffee. other locations nearby handle special orders perfectly fine. also, all of my friends in the area have mentioned how much they dislike this starbucks location.

its a perfect typical starbucks! lots of reviews on here about how the wait in the drive thru is so long and i will say it was a 5-10 minute wait, but i feel that's typical for any coffee place at peak hours. tip: get out of your car! the wait inside is much shorter or order your drink ahead of time on the app and they will have it ready for you on the counter to pick up. the drive thru is a little tricky because you have to drive around the whole building because it is a one way parking lot. when we ordered our drinks they where made perfectly to our liking. the girl at the window was very sweet and apologized for the wait. its just a typical starbucks with everything any other american starbucks has to offer.
slow service and sloppy staff. no sense of urgency and numerous people standing around doing nothing while many people wait.
thank you thank you thank you! to manager of the starbucks for donating to my classroom for preschoolers with autism! they donated so many straws for a sensory activity and we are so appreciative!

if i were rating this solely based on the amazing individuals that work at this location then believe me, you'd see the biggest 5 stars possible. these employees get hounded daily by huge lines of obnoxious students. i've seen them be berated, i've seen them when they're flustered, and i've seen the way a lot of jerks treat them. i absolutely love every employee here and hope that everyone else appreciates them. since this location is at the ua, and on campus right by the library, it gets slammed throughout the day. there are times where there are lines that go 20 people out the door and the employees here continue to bust butt to get drink orders out. unfortunately, the ua has set them up for failure by also having an advanced ordering app (not the regular starbucks app--they don't do that at this location) where while there are 30 people in line, there are then 30 college students in classes also ordering drinks. so at certain times of the day the "in person" customers are getting angry because "tap and go" customers are getting drinks before the "in person" orders. but imagine how hard it is for these few employees to keep up with the 30 people in line and the 30+ mobile orders happening at the same time. really, ua has set them up for failure because people get cranky and take it out on these poor baristas who are doing amazing work. unfortunately, i have to deduct a star for the long, long wait times and for the fact that i think its a horrible business practice to have all of these different ordering systems slamming people. but again, still 5 stars for the employees! they don't let you redeem your stars for a free beverage here. that's frustrating. you can, however, still get happy hour deals and other perks here. i mainly earn my stars here and then cash them out at "regular" locations. what i --really-- hate about this location is how they nickle and dime you. i switch out the milk for soy or coconut in all of my beverages. the upcharge for this is fine, as i appreciate them offering soy, and i realize that costs them a bit more to make. i have zero issues with this. but what irks me is i used to order drinks with "no ice" and i'd have no issues. the ice makes things too cold for me to drink, but sometimes i want something that isn't hot either. "no ice" is a great way to get something closer to room temp. again, i've ordered like this several times with no issues (and i've never had an issue at any other location ever). but suddenly i'm told there is a 50 cent upcharge here for "no ice". they're actually trying to charge you to take ice out of your drink. i've just saved you some water, some ice, the power it took to make the ice, etc., and the coffee i'm purchasing is around $6-7 and i usually purchase here 3x a week, 2x a day (that's 6 drinks a week, a loyal customer). and you really, really want to charge someone to take ice out of a drink?! this "new rule" comes and goes. sometimes they have it. other times they don't. but basically, they need to get over all of their extra charges that don't exist anywhere else. seriously, this doesn't exist at any other "regular" or "franchise" location i've ever been to. at some point, you need to realize you're making plenty of money and you don't need to charge to remove ice. after all, i don't get a discount if i take whipped cream off of my drink... employees: 5 stars long waits and weird mysterious extra charges: 3 stars greed of this location: no stars
got a vanilla iced latte with oat milk, a chai tea late, and a caramel brûlée latte. they gave me an extremely small cup for the latte and it was still about $4. the cups were also sticky.
very disappointed. order was incorrect & no one seemed interested in helping to correct the problem by refunding the overcharge. will avoid this location in the future.

out of stock of all food treat items; one single pack of madeleines were crumbly. unappetizing. ordered cold drink, not asked for drink size. i got what i got. unfriendly and not clean either. disappointed.
really great location - one of the faster drive thrus i've been through. people who work there are friendly and i normally have a great drink.

the black guy who makes my drinks every morning who writes the cute messages on the cups deserves a gold star and a raise. i don't usually write about specific employees but it being corporate he probably doesn't get enough recognition for the little kindness he puts in customers mornings

the service at this location is sooo slow. the barista that took my drink order was friendly. then i had to wait about 15 minutes for my drink. there were 7 people working and only 1 person on bar. whoever did deployment for that shift needs to have a rethink. one of the baristas was moving breakfast pastries from one tray to another. she could have assisted on bar, but instead the gal on bar gets a newbie to help her. she has to tell newbie everything from how to read the sticker to how to make a frappucino. why would you put your newbie on bar during the busiest time of the day? like every other drive thru location, the focus is on drive thru customers as opposed to walk-ins.
i came in yesterday in a rotted mood. but-- steven was super friendly, kind, and took great care of me. it really made my day.

this is the best starbucks around. warrington consistently messes up orders and huntingdon valley shopping center location runs a little slow. i loveee the baristas at this location. they're all incredibly friendly and make the best drinks. if you aren't 100% satisfied, they go out of their way to correct the order and make up for any errors. great starbucks, indeed!

i recently started going here vs the regular starbucks where i was going. for the most part, i have been very pleased. the baristas and managers have been very pleasant and friendly. it's not as big of a store compared to where i was going before, which is more relaxed, the music is softer and the loud music was a real gripe of mine at another starbucks. the only downside about the smaller shop is it feels like sometimes the large amount of people waiting for their food and beverages feels like they're on top of the people who are in the limited seating. it would be nice if they had more seating but they're a smaller shop. for the most part, i really like this starbucks, friendly baristas. there was an issue recently with an angry customer but the manager and customer service addressed it. there are a fair amount of homeless in the area too. again, i like this starbucks for the most part, these are my thoughts.
i go to this location every morning before work. i find the baristas friendly and engaging. they always remember my drink and are cheerful at the ungodly hour of 4:30 am.

maybe i need to get out more, but can't say that i've ever been to a starbucks that closes at 2pm. granted, it was a sunday, but still. i was hoping to get my coffee fix, but had to settle by making some hotel room coffee. sad.

the baristas were extremely friendly. they even recommended me certain drinks but didn't recommend the most expensive ones. the service was quick but i wasn't cut off or anything. great coffee experience

i am a daily weekday customer of this starbucks location, so if this review were anything other than five stars you'd better believe i'd be a walking contradiction. the success of this particular starbucks may seem to be due to its centralized south tampa location, but that is only partially true. the rest of its success can be attributed to its excellent management and staff. the manager ian and all of his baristas make a solid effort to get to know you - and not just how to spell your name (an effort that i have come to appreciate given the unique spelling of mine), but they also make a considerable effort to get to know who you are as a person. i would love to report that this is just my experience, but i have personally overheard more than one barista ask various customers, "hey (insert any name you can think of), the usual?" sometimes, they're already preparing the drink as the person walks in. this type of familiarity is hardly achievable at most modern-day bars, let alone a "chain" coffee shop! on a recent occasion, a newer barista forgot my breakfast sandwich order. upon realizing her mistake, she offered me a $5 gift card for my wait and trouble (which i graciously accepted...my daily visits make for an expensive habit!) the aforementioned is only one of many examples that support my assertion of a five star experience. one last thing - re: the bathroom code; i am honestly shocked that it took starbucks so long to implement a secured bathroom system. while i lived in nyc, starbucks was referred to as "the public restroom of the city." this is no joke and it is no surprise - heck, there was one on nearly every corner! if you are actually a customer and not just popping by in a drunken stupor post-gasparilla or after your generic walk/run on bayshore, then asking for the code shouldn't be that hard now, should it?

i love that this starbucks is soo close to work! i get a grande hot chocolate and everything bagel with cream cheese almost every morning (i know super unhealthy, right?) anyway the point is - this place gives me my breakfast and i like the baristas here.. they are super friendly and nice :)

i usually enjoy starbucks a lot. that is if they are made as its supposed to. this location always seem to have the least friendly and the drinks always taste very weak. it is some what frustrating when i drive through the starbucks to get my favorite drink to help me start the day.. and the ladies are not friendly and drinks are not just meeting the expectation. . .. not a ideal way to start the day . i will be taking extra time and trip to avoid this location and go try other location.

walked in this past week to the new starbucks out of morbid curiosity. i was not acknowledged at all while the store was basically empty minus one guy on his laptop. i waited a minute and a half to get service at the counter where i was rudely served. my regular drip coffee was horrid and the service was even worse...no actually check that. the worst part about this whole thing is that there is another coffee shop in the same building! the guy over there was super nice, helpful and his coffee was outstanding! apparently, he has been in the building for 4 years when $tarbuck$ moved in...what a bunch of greedy people. not only am i never coming here again for my daily coffee, i'll just walk an extra 50 steps to get much better coffee and much better service! p.s. if you don;t like the other little guy in the building, there are literally 3 other places within a 100 foot radius. this was a poor job in picking out locations...shame ::ding ding::
quick service and pleasant staff. coffee and food were good and served quickly! got a double chocolate chip frap and bacon & gouda sandwich.

the customer service is at it's finest at this starbucks! the manager, paul, is one of the kindest people that i have met in philadelphia. his service goes above and beyond. you definitely leave with a smile!
this place is easy to get to in center city. the staffs are friendly and drinks are well made. i was able to get in and out quickly, not bad!

excellent branch. not too crowded, decent amount of seating and lots of parking. thankfully they were not overrun by tens of inconsiderate people doing work all day and taking up every single seat, as often happens in yardley and newtown.

they have recently had a remodel and it looks great! everything is so clean!! i ordered my usual complicated drink and it was perfect!! target starbucks was a great idea!! coffee and shopping for the win!!
never disappointed. the baristas know customer service and are always happy to make your day better with the excellent brews.

starbucks is starbucks. always pretty good. i went through the drive-thru, and was not busy too busy unlike what someone said previously about always being busy. i guess it just depends on the time and day you go. i went on sunday around 2:30. i'm not sure what the one-way issue was from the previous review, but everything i saw was 2-ways (in parking lot, the streets) so this place was easy to find and i didn't have a problems going in and out.
barista molly has a lot of attitude for someone working in the hospitality industry. while chummy with select customers, she is cold, impersonal and hostile to others. get rid of molly!

8/10. totally fine starbucks. not too busy when i went. drinks were well made and egg bites were good. they were out of a few things like lemonades and cranberry bliss bar dessert for the season, but probably not this location's fault.

if you are using the drive through be prepared to wait in line for long time since this seems to be a very popular drive-through location. and unfortunately this location has really bad and sloooooow service. i have been inside many times and 90% of the time had to just wait for someone to actually take my order. there are always employees walking around but dis-interested in actually taking an order. they only know what they do walking around. since i have to wait to get my drink for a long time as well. this location really needs to improve on the service.

not my favorite venue, but was in a hurry and needed a caffeine fix. 1 starbucks, 2 starbucks, i never see any differences except, location & service, even the staff seems the same. expensive, but good coffee. what's a girl to do? caffeine fixes can reach critical levels!

when you place your order online and people that have ordered after you in line get their order first, something isn't working. i ordered online and 5 people, who ordered after me, got their drinks before i did. apparently, the barista never checked for the online order. wtf?! hey starbucks! don't push online ordering unless your people are trained to check for the orders. again!!! wtf?!

what a terrible place. they're rude and dismissive. i had an issue with my order and after 25 minutes of waiting i finally asked and they basically blamed it on another customer taking my order. i wouldn't of been so upset if they were apologetic. do yourself a favor and go to dunkin'. the food may be trashy but at least you don't have rude employees. do better starbucks.

though the line does go for days it seems before that first cup of coffee, the staff totally makes up for it! they are super friendly and a great way to start the day - thanks for being amazing this morning!!

updated review. only three stars today as the pastry display case was pitiful, to say the least. the barista explained to me that they are having great difficulty getting their pastry orders, especially the pumpkin scone order. the starbucks advertising for the season talks up pumpkin, pumpkin, pumpkin, and yet they have nothing in their stores. someone has obviously dropped the ball major big-time on this one! nice sb's. clean with friendly baristas. ample parking out front. only drawback is it has no drive-thru. conveniently located in shopping center with a grocery store.

waited in the drive thru line for over 30 minutes at this location which is by far the longest i have ever waited in a starbucks line. this was in the afternoon on a monday... not even what i would consider to be "rush hour". extremely frustrating!!
slowest starbucks i've ever been to. read the rest of the reviews this is not only once it's all the time.

i was on my way home from work. i was singing 80s music-with gusto-and suddenly had to have a starbucks spinach and feta wrap. warm and cozy, savory and tangy, ooey and gooey-it had to be done. so i turned down my "totally awesome" throwback tunes long enough to hit the nearest drive-thru... right there in the 1600 block of west kennedy. ohhh, i was not disappointed. the wait was short, the service super friendly and the wrap, so tasty and satisfying. it really is a nice combination-perfect for morning. it's six hours later and i'm still getting energy from it (and still humming "total eclipse of the heart.") thanks starbucks!

very rarely do i order from this starbucks just because of how long they normally take. however, this is perfectly understandable since they're located on city ave and when you consider the amount of traffic they get into their store. but when i do order, i am always displeased with quality of the drinks they make. as a former barista at starbucks, i understand the pressure to make drinks fast and correct. however this also means it's hard not to notice when they haven't been made right. i ordered a grande vanilla sweet cream cold brew, a pretty simple iced drink. the cold brew flavor wasn't bold enough, so you can assume they cut it with too much water or got lazy and used ice coffee. they also used regular cream most likely instead of vanilla sweet cream, or simply didn't put any pumps of vanilla. i'm always disappointed when i order from here. so if you're not eager to wait 10+ minutes to receive a sub par drink, don't order from here. on a slightly more positive note, when they're normally open, they're often very friendly, both in store and at the drive thru. they'd also be willing to remake your drink if you're not satisfied
what a great staff!! i always get greeted and most of the time they have my drink ready before i get to the counter.

this stores is very inconsistent. i purchased the clear double-insulated cup that last year ice tea refills were always 50 cents each. this year, this specific store will charge 50 cents for ice tea refills one day and then the next they try to charge full price less 10 cents. they need to come up with a store policy and stick to it. ironically, they actually sell the clear double-insulated cups at this location, so it's not like they are unaware of the cup and the concept behind it. in the future, i'll visit the many other locations around tucson, which are much more customer service friendly and consistent.

i love starbucks and have been a devoted fan since the early 90's, however this particular starbucks definitely does not live up to my expectations. it's always crowded, you can never find seating (even though they have a nice upstairs area), and the music is a little louder than i'd prefer. the baristas are friendly and i've never had a problem with them making the wrong drink, however they do consistently make the drinks at a lower volume than expected and very, very slowly. case in point: my grande caramel apple spice today was a good two inches below the rim of the cup. which makes it a tall. and it took 10 minutes to make even though there wasn't anyone in front of me because the barista had to first look up the recipe in a menu book and then ask another barista where the apple juice was and then go digging in a supply closet to find it. and it wasn't made nearly as well as the one i'd had a couple days ago at the other starbucks a block away on chestnut. while it is convenient, i'd prefer to hit a different coffee shop, like the nursing school's jazz & java or walk the extra block to the larger, quieter, and nicer starbucks on chestnut.

it is 7:30pm on thanksgiving day, and these folks will be here till midnight. you have no idea how grateful we were to call and be told your store would be the sole source of quality caffiene in the area still open at this holiday hour. after a day filled with food, family, and frustration, chris and his bling-y pimp headset, and the rest of his team, were a lifesaver. thank you starbucks crew at this location, you rock our coffee colored socks.

i frequent this starbucks as it's closest to my job, however the staff at this particular store are not very knowledgeable when it comes to specific drinks (even though i had the drink with the ingredients pulled up on my phone. candy cane frappuccino: grande= vanilla bean frappuccino w/ 1.5 pumps of peppermint). i also ordered a puppaccino for my dog and both of them looked at me with this look of pure loss as to what on earth i was talking about. then when i explained to them what a puppaccino is they looked at me like i totally inconvenienced them although it was just myself and one other customer in there. pretty sad that i had to explain what both items i ordered contained and i'm not even a barista. and to top it off, the store was out of peppermint syrup so i had to order something different.
i swear, i saw a homeless man on a laptop playing games here.

we pulled up at 8:59pm having no idea what time they closed and walked in to see two guys cleaning up the counter area. when i asked if they closed at 9pm fully prepared to walk away, they invited us in to serve us and reassured us it was no problem at all that it was 9pm. wow... this kind of service rarely exists and these two young men are true stars! thanks zachary and carlos!!! merry christmas

my favorite starbucks location. they always make my green tea latte on ice just right, other places make it too milky. they were out of the tomato mozzarella panini but they offered to remove the meat out of any other panini to make it a vegetarian option for me. the customer service is excellent !! they were very nice and made a good conversation while i waited for my drinks :)
the service was amazing and the girl with the blue hair was so nice and make my drink perfectly

if we had a zero rating i would give it here. the way you treat all patrons is crucial. the disrespect for our local law enforcement 4 days ago was uncalled for. why can't businesses stick to their specialty? why must everything be political? from this day forward i will not visit a starbucks. i will not buy gift cards etc as i often do for my staff. instead i will go elsewhere. this community is in support of our local police. you might consider that the next time your staff behaves rudely to them. oh, and maybe take a class in service recovery because your manager just failed!

you will never want for coffee in this area of philly!!!! there are three starbucks right here around me. this one just happens to be facing my hotel so its the easiest to access. when you walk in the people are friendly. they acknowledge you as you walk in. yesterday the service was a little slow but this morning i had my coffee right away.

this has got to be the slowest starbucks i have ever been to. there was a group of three girls in front of me who ordered iced teas already made in the blender and it's took them 20 minutes to get there order. it took me 25 minutes to get my tall caramell machiatto. ridiculous. i won't be returning to this place again.

i went into this starbucks last evening, to use the restroom. the doors to the restroom were open but i was told the restrooms were closed. i asked if i purchased an item, as a customer, i could use the restrooms and was told i could not. i asked how it was determined that the restrooms were closed, and the barista said he or she determines whether the restrooms were closed and they were closed to me. i went out the door, but promptly returned to ask another question and there was a child coming from the restroom. my only guess, since the store was still open and still serving customers, and a child went in after i was told i could not, is that my brown skin played a part in the decision to deny my use of the restrooms. i intend to follow this up to the highest corporate management. i won't use the barrista's name here but i will use it in every letter and email i write and every phone call i make. this type of response to anyone who walks in a starbucks is humiliating, and is completely unacceptable.

came in for the 1/2 off frap monday. the place is packed, but still got great service, and the place is clean. other than that, it's a starbucks. pretty much it's all the same as the others. knowledgable nice people, what more do you need?

i was the only person in line. i watched the bug on the crumby counter while i waited to be served. "someone will be with you" who "can't wait to have tomorrow off" and "can't wait to go home" finally took my order. i had a 5 minute expectation to be served. i had 15 min more to wait and look around at how filthy the place was and wait for my cup of coffee. i kept reminding myself that this is metairie. not bourbon street.

i live in pinellas county but come to this area quite often for dinner. i have had nothing but great service here!!! the staff are really nice. i popped in literally before closing on a sunday night not realizing how late it was and the two young ladies working were super sweet! they cheerfully made my drink and gave me a piece lemon cake! they made me feel important and i really appreciate them!!! thanks for making my night ladies! i will be back!!!

in the middle and late 1980s, this particular location was a café paradiso. i was more of a tea drinker than a coffee drinker then, but every once in awhile, i went to café paradiso for their snacks, baked goods and an occasional cup or two of coffee. it was a nice place and i liked it a lot. then, one day when i was working and going to classes at a nearby university, café paradiso was gone; it was converted to a starbucks. and it's still a starbucks. my café paradiso was gone! quel horreur! so now, it has continued to be a starbucks ever since then, and it's not too much different than all the other starbucks locations. some have outside seating, and others don't have outside seating, this one has outside seating. the interior must have been remodeled because it's dark like everything is mahogany wood or made to look like that and depressing inside. i'm not sure what they were trying to accomplish with that, but for me, it doesn't work. it doesn't feel inviting and i didn't want to stay there very long. they offer typical starbucks stuff. they were limited on the starbucks merchandise like cups and mugs. they had food offerings like made up sandwiches and what seemed like limited baked goods. parking is a problem now because the formerly free spaces along wydown are not free anymore. they have an electronic pay station where you have to pay for the parking now. i don't know why they changed it, but it is now really inconvenient to park there now. those electronic pay stations are a major pain in the ass because they take coins or cards, and you have to put in your plate number in addition to paying with either the card or coins. i hate them. around the corner are the old fashioned parking meters which i prefer over that damn pay station. so just beware of that and you will get a ticket if you don't pay. i won't be back anytime soon.

8am mobile ordered that was prepared promptly! that's incredible considering my order & how busy they were. i ordered something in error & they happily corrected my mistake & no cost. great staff & very pleasant!!!! items ordered: 2 cheese danishes one banana nut bread grande white chocolate mocha tall americano
these guys are great!! never gotten bad service. i've been going here for years and most of the people know me on a first name basis at this point.

the employees there are great and very respectful. very down to earth and serves the customers well. newtown is a very high maintenance area, and i give them props for how they deal with complaints!!!
horrible. slow and poor service. it seems like the baristas are not trained properly so they are very inefficient and don't make the drinks the same. never will come here again.

oh dear god ... if you go to a "regular" starbucks (i.e. not on the u of a campus), you'll have an awesome visit and it'll be everything starbucks is suppose to be. if you go to a starbucks on the u of a campus, you'll probably have a terrible experience, especially at this location, right outside the cameron library. first off, the servers only speak spanish. why? i have no idea. they also never smile or strike up a normal, polite conversation with you. the one server who must be a manager of sorts always looks like she's ready to kill someone. i actually don't think she's capable of a smile or even standard conversation. the drinks and food are the same as any other starbucks, and you early points on your starbucks membership at this location, but you can't use your points at this location. this is stupid and i think has something to do with how this location is franchised compared to others. i worked at the library and in the summer this location didn't open until 8am, the same time the library opened. for students, this is fine, but for staff it sucked. if they even opened 15 minutes earlier it would have made life so much easier. and in the summer their sign says they close at 3:30pm. personally i think that's ridiculously early for a coffee shop. and for awhile their sign said 4pm, but they still closed at 3:30pm! they're also incredibly slow. in fact, i think i could be faster moving backwards. they always seem to have a line, but it's mainly because they serve so slowly! they have two cash registers, but i've only ever seen the one open. they also seem to have a hard time keeping the condiments refilled. most mornings the milk isn't out right away, and most afternoons there's no sweetener available. they're definitely letting the starbucks reputation down.

this starbucks is always busy. same people out front with their pets ... a lot of people on laptops ... ultra thin starbucks very little standing room. not much of a place to go for the ambiance, the employees and location makes up for the lack of interior flare. i do like when i complained about some food they gave me another sandwich on the house. amazing customer service.
this was a typical starbucks. service was prompt. staff was pleasant.
the baristas are extremely helpful and friendly! drive thru is always efficient and the cafe is enormous! my favorite starbucks in indianapolis without question!

this is open for a while now and one thing they need to work on is maintaining the condiments counter. i'm there often and almost always its out of supplies. i'm also somewhat disappointed that they don't fill the coffee to the top... why should i get 3/4 cup of coffee? happened too many times to not say anything about ...
no drive-thru available and limited parking. staff is friendly and location is well kept.

parking is a challenge because it's so busy. outlets to work off of are not easily accessible like at other locations. i don't think i'll be back here anytime soon. i don't like the layout here. havertown is so much better in that regard.

[out-of-towner review] thank you and you suck...are how i feel! given that you are in a convention center, you'd think although it was the weekend, you'd have better hours of operation. conventions run 7 days a week. my convention happened to run sunday - tuesday. so opening day (sunday), everyone was jonsing for coffee all day, but after 2pm you were screwed! it was closed...you could endure the gross humidity and/or pouring rain and walk a few blocks to the marriott starbucks, but theirs closes early too! annoyed...to say the least... but, for the two remaining days was their saving grace. although the line was continuously 40+ people deep, these automons kept it movin'! no lie, they were quick and efficient for a very small set-up. they weren't the friendliest, but they were damn good and efficient with their time...you can't complain about that... if i walk the length of this convention center, you better reward me with a caffeinated beverage. your wimpy hours just won't cut it for all the various people who saunter through here...step up your game, but keep up the good work! it's a catch 22, sorry guys! keepin' convention'er's caffeinated on their timetable...

double blended with an extra dose of love. this was my second time at a starbucks today (shhhh) the wonderful erynne assisted us via the drive-thru. she was super accurate with our orders and asked if i still wanted whipped cream since my base option was non dairy - so many assume i don't, don't give me any and i actually always want extra lol . we stan a lovely accurate barista. you know your employee is great when they impress during a short visit! :) .

starbucks ahhh classic! love a good frapp every once in a while. it doesnt happen very often because this location always has a wait.early morning i have no time for slow pace between traffic and always running behind this location for me would have me in search of a new job. parking is impossible at times i mean they are connected to the bank and also a very busy mall....always a drive thru line unless some random hour of the day.cake pops a guilty overpriced pleasure i adore. do not care much for there breakfast only a fan of the coffee. i am a dunkin donut kind of gal so this is 3 stars for me.

though this is my neighborhood location, i've never ventured here until this past week. my favorite drink was made to perfection (green tea latte) and there wasn't an issue with anybody inside. do wish they had more seating or atleast outlets. no other issues.

still need much faster drive-thru service but they always make my ice coffee taste amazing.. i stop in 1 time a day and it's always delicious!!! they have 2 gentlemen that are always at the drive-thru both are delightful and always smiling

quick access, good drinks, a few indoor seats, and consistently good drinks! i like this location for it's convenience because in the mornings it's a little slower than the location down the street. while it doesn't have a large indoor area to sit down it does have a few seats where there are a couple of regulars that offer a kind a greeting. if you feel like chatting, it is never a challenge to find someone to talk with. usually, i need to get some grad school work done (or catch up on yelp reviews) and i hide out behind my computer. the seating outside the store is plentiful. the baristas are always helpful and it seems that they could use a couple of extra hands, but they are good at what they do and always kind.

compared to other starbucks this one always has the same issue - really dirty, gross restrooms. i stop here on occasion to work before appointments downtown. nothing makes me lose my appetite more for a delicious cup of joe than smelling urine while washing my hands. please fix!!! begging you because you have good wifi. you are kind of my office starbucks.... most recent visit was yesterday 6/28.
